overview: The Academy of Mary Immaculate offers financial hardship assistance to families experiencing financial difficulties. The College considers alternative payment options and fee reduction options on a case-by-case basis for eligible families.
description: Families who experience financial hardship may apply for consideration of alternative payment options to assist them in meeting their financial obligations. The College offers fee rebates that are limited to a maximum of 50% of the annual tuition fee. A holding deposit of $750 is requested from families and is non-refundable and non-transferable, but will be credited towards any future fees once the student returns to the College. All requests for fee reduction due to financial hardship must be made in writing to the Principal. Consideration for financial assistance is made on an individual basis by the College Business Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Must submit written request to the Principal; Individual assessment of circumstances required
special_conditions: Maximum fee rebate limited to 50% of annual tuition fee. Holding deposit of $750 is non-refundable and non-transferable but will be credited towards future fees. All requests must be made in writing to the Principal. Consideration is made on an individual basis by the College Business Manager.

overview: Aitken College offers the College Board Foundation Scholarship to recognise and support exceptional Year 12 students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement, leadership qualities, and active participation in college life. The scholarship rewards students who exemplify the College's values including academic excellence, leadership, service and commitment to the College community.
description: The College Board Foundation Scholarship provides Full Fee Remission for the recipient's Year 12 year and is awarded to a Year 12 student demonstrating exceptional academic achievement, leadership, participation in the life of the College, and service to the community. The scholarship recognises students who embody the qualities the College seeks to develop in its students. Selection is based on a comprehensive assessment of the student's academic performance, leadership contributions, involvement in co-curricular and service activities, and alignment with the College's Christian values and ethos. Recipients are selected through an application and review process conducted by the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Demonstrate academic achievement; Show leadership qualities; Participate actively in College life; Demonstrate commitment to service and College values
special_conditions: Scholarship covers annual tuition fees for Year 12. Recipients must demonstrate and maintain commitment to College values and expectations. Selection emphasises academic achievement, leadership, participation in the life of the College, and service.

overview: Aitken College offers the Rachael Woodhams Music Scholarship to recognise and support musically talented students in Year 8 or Year 9. The scholarship enables students to pursue advanced musical education and development through masterclasses or specialised music courses in composition or performance.
description: The Rachael Woodhams Music Scholarship is awarded to a student entering Year 8 or Year 9 who demonstrates exceptional interest and ability in music. The scholarship supports the student in completing a masterclass or course in music composition or music performance. The financial value and specific award amount are not publicly detailed on the College website but represent meaningful support for music education. Selection is based on demonstrated musical talent, commitment to music study, and potential for growth in the discipline. The scholarship recognises the importance of nurturing musical excellence within the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8 or Year 9; Demonstrated interest in music; Musical talent and ability in composition or performance; Currently enrolled or enrolling at Aitken College
special_conditions: Scholarship enables completion of a masterclass or course in music composition or music performance. Applications advertised through student bulletins with online application form completion required. Recipient uses award to support advanced musical study.

overview: Aitken College offers the Parents and Friends Arts Scholarship to support and recognise artistically talented Year 12 students. The scholarship enables students to pursue advanced study in any area of the arts, including visual arts and performing arts, through masterclasses and specialised courses.
description: The Parents and Friends Arts Scholarship is awarded to a Year 12 student who demonstrates exceptional talent and commitment in any area of the arts, encompassing both visual arts and performing arts disciplines. The scholarship allows the recipient to complete a masterclass or course in an area of artistic interest. The specific financial value is not detailed publicly but provides meaningful support for advanced arts education. Selection is based on demonstrated artistic talent, creativity, commitment to arts study, and potential for further development. The scholarship recognises the importance of fostering creativity and artistic expression at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Demonstrated talent in visual arts or performing arts; Commitment to arts study; Currently enrolled at Aitken College; Interest in completing a masterclass or specialised arts course
special_conditions: Scholarship applies to visual arts or performing arts. Recipients use award to complete a masterclass or course in an area of artistic interest. Applications advertised through student bulletins with online application form completion required. Award recipients recognised on the College's 2024 Recognition of Students page.

overview: Al Iman College offers the Annual Scholarship Program Tier 1 to recognize outstanding academic achievement in students from Year 5 and above. This scholarship provides full tuition fee remission to students who maintain exceptional academic performance, demonstrating commitment to academic excellence.
description: The Annual Scholarship Program Tier 1 is awarded to students from Year 5 and above who achieve an average grade of 95% or above across both semesters for all subjects. The scholarship provides 100% tuition fee reduction, representing full fee remission for the entire year. Selection is based on academic merit determined through semester grade averages. Students must maintain a minimum of 90% attendance throughout the year and avoid receiving any orange or red card disciplinary notices during their enrolment. The discount is applied quarterly to tuition fees in the following year after meeting all eligibility criteria. This scholarship recognises sustained academic excellence and supports students demonstrating outstanding performance.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 and above; Average grades of 95% or above across both semesters for all subjects; Minimum 90% attendance throughout the year; No orange card or red card disciplinary issues throughout the year
special_conditions: Discount will be applied quarterly to tuition fees the following year after meeting all criteria. Scholarship is renewable annually based on maintaining required academic performance and attendance standards. Discipline record must remain clear of orange and red cards throughout the year.

overview: Al Iman College offers the Annual Scholarship Program Tier 2 to support students from Year 5 and above demonstrating strong academic achievement. This scholarship recognises very good academic performance with a 50% reduction in tuition fees.
description: The Annual Scholarship Program Tier 2 is available to students from Year 5 and above who achieve an average grade between 90% and 94% across both semesters for all subjects. The scholarship provides 50% reduction of tuition fees, significantly reducing the financial burden on families. Selection is based on objective academic performance data collected from both semester results. Students must maintain a minimum of 90% attendance throughout the year and avoid receiving any orange or red card disciplinary notices. The discount is applied quarterly to tuition fees in the following year after all criteria are met and verified. This program recognises strong academic achievement across all subject areas.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 and above; Average grades between 90% and 94% across both semesters for all subjects; Minimum 90% attendance throughout the year; No orange card or red card disciplinary issues throughout the year
special_conditions: Discount will be applied quarterly to tuition fees the following year after meeting all criteria. Scholarship is renewable annually based on maintaining required academic performance and attendance standards. Discipline record must remain clear of orange and red cards throughout the year.

overview: Al Iman College provides a concession card discount on extracurricular levy fees for families holding valid and eligible concession cards. This financial assistance program helps reduce educational costs for families facing financial hardship.
description: Al Iman College offers an extracurricular levy discount for parents and guardians who hold a valid and eligible concession card. The discount provides relief from extracurricular activity fees for eligible families. Qualification is determined through validation of concession card documentation provided by parents. The discount is applied after the details provided are verified and validated by the school administration. This program is designed to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent students from participating in extracurricular activities and enrichment opportunities. Concession cards recognised include various government-issued financial assistance cards indicating eligible status.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian holds valid and eligible concession card; Concession card must be current and valid during the specified period; Details provided must be validated by school administration
special_conditions: Discount applies only to extracurricular levy. Applicable between January and April of each school year. Discount will be applied after the details provided are validated. Parents must provide valid concession card documentation.

overview: Al Siraat College offers LEAP, a challenging gifted and talented program designed for academically strong, highly motivated students with excellent work ethics. The program provides rigorous and accelerated content in English, Mathematics, Science and Inquiry to extend and enrich exceptional learners.
description: LEAP is a selective entry program focused on providing rigorous and accelerated content for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. The program aims to extend and enrich students in key learning areas including English, Mathematics, Science and Inquiry. Students are selected through a competitive process involving the ACER High Ability Selection Test (HAST), followed by a formal interview to assess motivation, thinking skills, work ethic and positive attitudes. The program is available on a co-educational basis for each year level. Students selected for LEAP are required to commit to the program from Year 7 through Year 9. The program is open to both current Al Siraat students and external students currently enrolled in Year 6. No specific monetary scholarship value is publicly stated for this selective entry program.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 (both Al Siraat and external students); Must score above threshold on ACER High Ability Selection Test; Must demonstrate academically strong performance; Must show high motivation and excellent work ethic; Must demonstrate positive attitudes and behaviours; Must commit to program from Year 7 to Year 9
special_conditions: Acceptance into LEAP is based on performance on the external ACER High Ability Selection Test. Successful applicants will be contacted to participate in a formal interview assessing motivation, thinking skills, work ethic and positive attitudes. Students selected in LEAP are required to be part of the program from Year 7 to Year 9.

overview: Al Siraat College supports eligible students through the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a government-funded financial assistance program. CSEF provides annual support to students from families holding valid concession cards to participate in camps, excursions, sporting activities and outdoor education programs.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a means-tested financial assistance program administered through the College for eligible families. The fund provides annual payments of $400 per eligible student (both Primary and Secondary), up from previous annual amounts. Eligibility is based on holding a valid, means-tested concession card such as a Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card, or being a foster parent as validated by Centrelink on specific dates in 2026. Applications open on 29 January 2026 and must be submitted to the College by 26 June 2026. The program is designed to support participation in school camps, excursions, sporting activities, and outdoor education programs. Funding is provided on an annual basis and requires reapplication each year.
eligibility_criteria: Holding a valid Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card validated by Centrelink; OR being a foster parent validated by Centrelink; Application must be submitted between 29 January 2026 and 26 June 2026
special_conditions: Eligibility based on holding a valid, means-tested concession card (Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card) or being a foster parent, as validated by Centrelink on specific dates in 2026. Applications must be submitted within the stated timeframe. Annual reapplication required.

overview: Alia College offers the General Excellence Scholarship (Bursary) to students who have concerns about their ability to pay school fees. The scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance to students who wish to attend the school but face financial hardship.
description: Alia College welcomes applications from students with financial concerns. Applicants are required to write a letter or email explaining why they would be a good fit for the school and why the school would be good for them, including their personal story or circumstances. The school reviews applications considering how well the student would fit with the currently enrolled student group, the authenticity of the application, and subjective factors. The bursary provides financial assistance in the form of fee reduction or partial fee remission, though the exact value is not specified on the website. The selection process is based on a written application (letter/email) and subjective assessment by the school. Renewal and continuation of support is assessed based on the student's fit within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Must be keen to attend Alia College; Must have concerns about ability to pay fees; Must submit letter or email application; Application reviewed on holistic basis including student fit
special_conditions: Applications are considered holistically. The school reviews applications based on how the student would fit into the currently enrolled group, authenticity of the application, and subjective factors including timing (how nice or awful a day staff have had). Other people may submit supporting letters on behalf of the applicant. The exact fee reduction or remission amount is negotiable and determined on a case-by-case basis.

overview: Al-Taqwa College offers the Select Entry Enrichment Program (SEEP) to address the learning needs of students who display a high degree of learning potential. The program provides curriculum enrichment in English, Mathematics and Science, with eligible students receiving a 100% scholarship for the remainder of their schooling upon maintenance of high academic standards.
description: The Select Entry Enrichment Program (SEEP) is designed for gifted and talented students in Grades 3 to Year 9. Eligible students will receive Full Fee Remission for the remainder of their schooling provided they maintain a score of 90% or above in all their assessments throughout the year. Students also receive FREE Tuition support throughout VCE, and a $2000 reward is offered towards university fees, a holiday or Umrah upon successful completion of Year 12 studies. Selection occurs through teacher nomination and internal assessment for Grades 3-4, with the Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST) required for Grades 5-8. Current SEEP students must sit the HAST Test annually to assess eligibility to remain in the program. Students in Years 5-8 are also eligible for additional scholarships based on HAST results, with scholarship testing via the ACER High Ability Selection Test occurring during Term 3.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grades 3 to Year 9; Teacher nomination required; For Grades 3-4 must pass internal assessment; For Grades 5-8 must sit HAST test; Current SEEP students must sit HAST annually for renewal; Must maintain 90% or above in all assessments to retain scholarship; Students intending to enroll must follow general enrolment process
year_levels: Grade 3, Grade 4, Grade 5, Grade 6, Grade 7, Grade 8, Year 9
special_conditions: Eligible students must maintain a score of 90% or above in all their assessments throughout the year to retain the scholarship. Students currently in the SEEP Program must sit the HAST Test annually to assess eligibility to remain in the program for the following year. The scholarship is provided for the remainder of schooling provided conditions are maintained. Students intending on enrolling at Al-Taqwa College will still be required to follow the general enrolment process.

overview: Al-Taqwa College's Select Entry Enrichment Program (SEEP) provides a 100% scholarship to high-achieving gifted students. The program offers curriculum enrichment in English, Mathematics, and Science, recognising students with high learning potential and commitment to independent learning.
description: SEEP offers eligible students a 100% scholarship for the duration of their remaining schooling, provided they maintain a score of 90% or above in all assessments throughout each year. Recipients receive free tuition support throughout the VCE (Years 11-12) and a $2,000 reward towards university fees, a holiday, or Umrah upon successful completion of Year 12. The program is offered to students in Grades 3 to Year 9 each year. Selection is based on internal assessment for Grades 3-4, and the ACER Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST) for students in Grades 5-8. Current SEEP students must sit the HAST test annually to assess continued eligibility for the following year. All students must maintain the school's Code of Conduct standards and complete the general enrolment process.
eligibility_criteria: Nominated by teachers or based on prior assessment; High learning potential and commitment to independent learning; Grades 3 to Year 9 at point of entry; Maintain 90% or above in all assessments annually to retain scholarship; Must pass internal assessment (Grades 3-4) or ACER HAST test (Grades 5-8); Current SEEP students must pass annual HAST reassessment; Must follow standard enrolment process for the college
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional upon maintaining 90% or above in all assessments throughout each year. Eligible students receive free tuition throughout VCE. A $2,000 reward is offered towards university fees, a holiday, or Umrah upon successful completion of Year 12. Scholarship applies for the duration of remaining schooling at the college. Annual HAST reassessment required for current SEEP students to maintain program eligibility.

overview: Al-Taqwa College offers Level 1 ACER Scholarships to recognise high-achieving students entering Year 7 or Year 8. These scholarships reward exceptional academic merit and are available to students not already enrolled in the SEEP program.
description: Al-Taqwa College offers ACER Scholarship opportunities at three levels to recognise and reward high achievers. Level 1 ACER Scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 or Year 8. The scholarship assessment involves the ACER Scholarship Test administered through the official ACER platform. Applications are submitted online through the ACER website at scholarships.acer.org, with an application fee of $110. Students currently in the SEEP program are not eligible to apply. The exact financial value and duration of these scholarships are not specified on the school's published materials, though students who meet selection criteria will receive offers by mail following successful test performance.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Not currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP program; Open to high-achieving students; Application through ACER platform required
special_conditions: Students currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP Program are not eligible to apply. Application must be submitted online via ACER website. School application form download required. Successful applicants will be contacted by mail with offers.

overview: Al-Taqwa College's Level 2 ACER Scholarship recognises high-achieving students entering Year 9 or Year 10. These scholarships reward exceptional academic achievement and are available to high-performing students.
description: Level 2 ACER Scholarships are available to students entering Year 9 or Year 10 who demonstrate high academic achievement. The scholarship assessment involves the ACER Scholarship Test administered through the official ACER platform. Applications are submitted online through the ACER website at scholarships.acer.org, with a non-refundable application fee of $110 that includes access to a practice test with marked answers. Students currently in the SEEP program are not eligible to apply. The exact financial value and duration of these scholarships are not specified on published materials, though successful applicants will be notified by mail.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 9 or Year 10; Not currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP program; High academic achievement required; Application through ACER platform required
special_conditions: Students currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP Program are not eligible to apply. Application must be submitted online via ACER website. School application form download required. Non-refundable application fee of $110 includes practice test access. Successful applicants will be contacted by mail.

overview: Al-Taqwa College offers Level 3 ACER Scholarships to recognise high-achieving students entering Year 11 (VCE). This scholarship supports senior students demonstrating exceptional academic merit and commitment to excellence.
description: Level 3 ACER Scholarships are available to students entering Year 11 who demonstrate high academic achievement and merit. The scholarship assessment involves the ACER Scholarship Test administered through the official ACER platform. Applications are submitted online through the ACER website at scholarships.acer.org, with a non-refundable application fee of $110 that includes access to a practice test with marked answers. Students currently in the SEEP program are not eligible to apply. The exact financial value and duration of these scholarships are not specified on published materials. Successful applicants will be notified by mail with details of their offers.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 11; Not currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP program; High academic achievement required; Application through ACER platform required
special_conditions: Students currently enrolled in Al-Taqwa College SEEP Program are not eligible to apply. Application must be submitted online via ACER website. School application form download required. Non-refundable application fee of $110 includes practice test access. Successful applicants will be contacted by mail.

program_name: Elevate Program for Gifted and Talented Students
overview: Altona College offers the Elevate Program, a three-year selective entry program designed for highly capable learners entering Year 7. The program provides a supported and challenging environment where students engage in deep, complex learning experiences across multiple aspects of the Victorian Curriculum.
description: Elevate at Altona College is a three-year program for families seeking selective entry into a heavily supported and vastly challenging environment for highly capable learners. In this program, students have access to fewer teachers for longer periods of time, allowing for integration across learning areas wherever possible. Experienced and innovative staff support students to engage in deep, complex learning experiences across multiple aspects of the Victorian Curriculum. Elevate students are challenged to use deep and logical thinking to solve problems focused on key global issues and contexts. The program has no direct monetary scholarship value; it is an accelerated academic program rather than a fee-reducing scholarship. Selection is based on entrance examination (EduTest) followed by an interview, with approximately 30 students shortlisted for interview and a maximum of 25 offered places in the Elevate class per year level.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 at a government primary school; Must apply for placement at Altona College via state schools transition form in Term 2; Must sit the Elevate entrance exam (EduTest); Must pass the entrance examination; Must participate in Stage 2 interview if shortlisted; Students who do not apply for placement at Altona College may not be eligible for placement in the Elevate program
special_conditions: Elevate is a three-year continuous program. Students with an appeal pending from initial placement may still register for EduTest to avoid disadvantage or delay. Students registering for EduTest at multiple schools will only sit the first available test. Completion of the entrance examination does not guarantee entry. Offers for placement in the Elevate program are made as soon as possible after interviews are completed (generally within 7 days).

overview: Ararat College recognises student excellence at the Annual Presentation Evening by awarding scholarships to acknowledge personal development and achievement. The scholarships recognise students across academic, attitude, effort, citizenship and special accomplishments.
description: At the end of each school year, Ararat College awards scholarships to recognise student personal development including academic achievement, attitude, effort, citizenship and special accomplishments. These annual awards are presented at the College's Annual Presentation Evening as part of the formal recognition of student achievement across multiple dimensions of college life. The scholarships serve to celebrate and encourage continued excellence in all areas of school involvement. Specific financial value is not disclosed in available school documentation. The awards are determined by school assessment of student achievement across the academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Current Ararat College students; Demonstrated achievement in academic, attitude, effort, citizenship or special accomplishments; Selection determined by school staff and leadership
special_conditions: Awards are presented at the Annual Presentation Evening. Determined by school assessment of student achievement and contribution during the academic year.

school_name: Armstrong Creek School - Specialist P-12 Campus

overview: Aquinas College offers bursaries to support students with demonstrated financial need. The bursary program is designed to ensure that all deserving students can access a Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances. Academic ability is not a criterion for receiving a bursary.
description: Aquinas College's Bursary Fund provides financial assistance to families with a demonstrated means-tested inability to afford a Catholic Education. The bursaries support access to essential items such as textbooks and uniforms. Academic ability is not a criterion for receiving a bursary; the College's focus is solely on financial need. The College invites eligible families to contact the Registrar at registrar@aquinas.vic.edu.au to apply for assistance. Donations to the Aquinas Bursary Fund are tax deductible and help make Catholic education accessible to families in need.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated means-tested inability to afford Catholic Education; Not based on academic ability; All year levels eligible; Preference given to deserving students with genuine financial hardship
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded based on demonstrated means-tested inability to afford a Catholic Education. Families must contact the Registrar directly to apply for assistance. Support is provided specifically for essential educational items including textbooks and uniforms.

overview: Ardeer South Primary School participates in the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) program to assist eligible families in covering the costs of school trips, camps, and sporting activities. The fund is provided to families who hold a valid means-tested concession card or are temporary foster parents.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government financial assistance program that provides eligible families with funds to support their children's participation in school camps, excursions, and sporting activities. The allowance is paid directly to the school to be used towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the child. Families who hold a valid means-tested concession card or are temporary foster parents may be eligible for CSEF. The fund provides non-monetary support to participating families. For information on how to apply or eligibility details, families can contact the school office to obtain a CSEF application form or download information from www.education.vic.gov.au/csef.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card; OR be a temporary foster parent
special_conditions: Allowance is paid to the school to use towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the child. Application form must be obtained from school office or downloaded from www.education.vic.gov.au/csef.

overview: Assumption College offers the Academic Boarding Scholarship to support academically strong students pursuing boarding education. The scholarship provides financial assistance towards annual boarding fees for students who demonstrate academic excellence.
description: The Academic Boarding Scholarship is valued at $10,000.00 per annum towards the annual boarding fees. This scholarship is available for secondary students (Years 7-12) and is offered on an annual basis. The selection process involves assessment of academic merit and suitability for boarding education. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and commitment to the college community during their tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to boarding students; Secondary school students (Years 7-12); Academic excellence required; Demonstration of commitment to boarding school values
special_conditions: Scholarship is for one year but may be renewable subject to satisfactory academic progress. Application fee of $350 non-refundable applies to all enrolment applications.

overview: Ashwood High School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement. All applicants of the Accelerated Curriculum and Enrichment (ACE) Program are eligible and automatically considered for this scholarship based on their entrance examination performance and primary school recommendation.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is a full scholarship providing for all materials, books and levies as per the School Resource List. Voluntary fees, IT equipment costs and optional items are not covered by this scholarship. Selection is based upon the most successful result from the ACE entrance examinations and tests, along with recommendation from the student's primary school. The scholarship is ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning as well as extra-curricular programs is essential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must be applicant of the Accelerated Curriculum and Enrichment (ACE) Program; Selection based on ACE entrance examination results; Primary school recommendation required; Must maintain high level of commitment to scholarship program ideals; Must demonstrate preparedness to embrace school values of respect, participation and excellence
special_conditions: All scholarships are ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in the area of the scholarship granted. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate preparedness to embrace the school's values of respect, participation and excellence.

overview: Ashwood High School offers the Sporting Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in sport. The scholarship is open to all Grade 6 students demonstrating a strong affiliation with sport both in the community and within their primary school.
description: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate exceptional sporting achievement and commitment to excellence in physical activity. Students must demonstrate a strong affiliation with sport both in the community and within their primary school. The scholarship is awarded for entry to Year 7 and is ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), provided the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in sporting endeavours.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate strong affiliation with sport in community and primary school; Must maintain high level of commitment to scholarship program ideals; Active participation in extra-curricular programs essential; Must embrace school values of respect, participation and excellence
special_conditions: All scholarships are ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in sporting endeavours.

overview: Ashwood High School offers the Leaders and Community Spirit Scholarship to recognise outstanding leadership and community engagement. The scholarship is open to all Grade 6 students demonstrating sound academic achievement, a positive work ethic, leadership and peer mentoring skills.
description: The Leaders and Community Spirit Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate sound academic achievement, a positive work ethic, leadership qualities and peer mentoring skills. Applicants must have participated in a variety of school and community based activities during their time at primary school. The scholarship is awarded for entry to Year 7 and is ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), provided the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in leadership and community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate sound academic achievement; Must demonstrate positive work ethic; Must demonstrate leadership and peer mentoring skills; Must have participated in variety of school and community based activities; Must maintain high level of commitment to scholarship program ideals; Must embrace school values of respect, participation and excellence
special_conditions: All scholarships are ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in leadership and community service areas.

overview: Ashwood High School offers the Instrumental Music Scholarship to recognise outstanding musical achievement. The scholarship is available to Grade 6 students demonstrating high instrumental music achievement and a commitment to music performance.
description: The Instrumental Music Scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate exceptional instrumental music achievement and a strong commitment to music performance. Applicants must have participated in a range of school or community based music performances during their time at primary school. The scholarship is awarded for entry to Year 7 and is ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), provided the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in musical endeavours and extra-curricular programs is essential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate high instrumental music achievement; Must demonstrate commitment to music performance; Must have participated in range of school or community based music performances; Must maintain high level of commitment to scholarship program ideals; Must embrace school values of respect, participation and excellence
special_conditions: All scholarships are ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in music performance.

overview: Ashwood High School offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in performing arts. The scholarship is available to Grade 6 students demonstrating performing arts achievement in drama, music or dance and a commitment to performance.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in drama, music or dance and a strong commitment to performance. The scholarship encompasses multiple performing arts disciplines including drama, music and dance. The scholarship is awarded for entry to Year 7 and is ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), provided the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in performing arts and extra-curricular programs is essential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate performing arts achievement in drama, music or dance; Must demonstrate commitment to performance; Must have participated in school or community based performances; Must maintain high level of commitment to scholarship program ideals; Must embrace school values of respect, participation and excellence
special_conditions: All scholarships are ongoing for the three middle years of secondary schooling (Years 7-9 inclusive), providing the student maintains a high level of commitment to the ideals of the scholarship program. Active participation in learning and extra-curricular programs is essential, particularly in performing arts.

overview: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholarship through The University of Melbourne is a prestigious award recognising academic excellence and leadership potential. This scholarship is aligned with Ashwood High School's commitment to fostering academic excellence and pathways toward highly regarded academic opportunities.
description: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholarship is a prestigious academic enrichment program established in partnership with The University of Melbourne. It recognises high-achieving students who demonstrate both academic excellence and leadership potential. The program supports Year 10 students in achieving their personal best through a two-year program of events and activities that expand academic and personal horizons to prepare for success at university and beyond. This scholarship symbolises commitment to fostering academic excellence and represents an investment in students' futures. Participants gain access to personal development workshops, overnight campus adventures and form close bonds with peers from across Victoria and select border regions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must demonstrate leadership potential; Must be from Victoria or select NSW/South Australian border schools; School endorsement required; Academic performance in top tier required
special_conditions: School endorsement required; Program runs for two years; Participants engage in academic and social enrichment activities including workshops and on-campus overnight adventures; Successful completion supports preparation for university entry with guaranteed ATAR benefits at University of Melbourne; Regional students may receive $2,500 relocation allowance.

school_name: Ashwood School-Garfield North Outdoor Education Centre

overview: Auburn High School offers a SEAL program accredited by TAAS for highly capable students. The program enables students to complete Year 7-10 Victorian Curriculum standards in three years rather than four, providing acceleration and enrichment for high-achieving learners.
description: The SEAL program at Auburn High School brings together like-minded, high-achieving students and provides acceleration opportunities through condensed curriculum delivery. Students complete Year 7-10 curriculum in three years and can then undertake an enriched VCE sequence through the Accelerate VCE Program (AVP) with the option of university enhancement subjects in Year 12, equivalent to first-year university subjects. Entry requires attaining the high benchmark measured by Edutest, and students must first be offered a place at the school via the Year 6-7 Application Form process before taking the selective entry test. Shortlisted students are invited for an interview with their parent/guardian. The program is one of only three accredited TAAS programs in the area.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students seeking entry to Year 7; Must attain high benchmark score on Edutest; Must first be offered place at school via Year 6-7 Application Form; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Students must complete Year 7-10 Victorian Curriculum standards in three years rather than four. SEAL students undertake core subjects of English, Maths, Science and Humanities in their SEAL group and the remainder of their classes with peers in general and French Binational programs to support social connections and sense of community.

overview: Auburn High School is the only Victorian government secondary school offering a Binational French program accredited by the French Ministry of Education. The program provides select-entry, internationally recognised language learning that merges French and Australian pedagogy.
description: Auburn High School's French Binational Program enables students to learn half their subjects in English and half in French, ensuring high levels of skill and confidence in both languages. Students follow a dual accredited curriculum combining Instructions officielles françaises for Cycle 4 (Year 7-9) along with Victorian Curriculum requirements. Students sit the same end-of-cycle examination (Diplôme National du Brevet) as in France. From Year 10 onward, students join the Auburn High senior school (Lycée) and continue studies through VCE or IB with opportunity to accelerate. Participation in the program attracts an additional specialist fee of $2,940 per year in 2025. Year 6 students must record Auburn High School as first preference on the placement information form with their primary school. Candidates are invited for a workshop and interview with Auburn High School French staff in Term 2.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students from Victorian Government schools only; Must record Auburn High School as first preference on primary school placement form; Must participate in workshop and interview with French staff; Candidates must demonstrate ability in French language
special_conditions: Participation attracts additional specialist fee of $2,940 per year. Students from only Victorian Government schools are eligible. Workshop and interview required in Term 2 of 2025. Students study half their subjects in English and half in French. Program runs from Year 7 (5eme) to Year 9 (3eme) with continuation through VCE or IB from Year 10 onward.

overview: Auburn High School's French Partial Immersion Program is designed for students with advanced skills in the French language. The program focuses on the Victorian Curriculum while supporting accelerated French language acquisition through immersive approaches.
description: The French Partial Immersion Program at Auburn High School supports students with advanced French language skills who wish to continue their French studies at an accelerated pace and prepare for their senior years. The program focuses on Victorian Curriculum delivery with seven hours per week of French (French advanced) plus one additional subject (Science) taught with 18 hours per week in English. Year 6 students must record Auburn High School as first preference on the placement information form with their primary school. Candidates are invited to participate in a workshop and interview with Auburn High School French staff in Term 2. The program runs from Year 7 to Year 9, with students joining the Auburn High senior school (Lycée) from Year 10 onward to continue studies through VCE or IB. Participation in the program attracts an additional specialist fee of $580 per year in 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students from Victorian Government schools only; Must have advanced skills in French language; Must record Auburn High School as first preference on primary school placement form; Must participate in workshop and interview with French staff
special_conditions: Participation attracts additional specialist fee of $580 per year. Students from only Victorian Government schools are eligible. Workshop and interview required in Term 2. Students must demonstrate advanced French language skills. Seven hours per week in French plus one subject in Science. Program runs Year 7-9 with continuation through VCE or IB from Year 10.

overview: Auburn High School offers Transition Scholarships to students starting Year 7 who are selected in partnership with their primary school. These scholarships identify students eligible based on attendance, engagement, and access to means-tested concession cards.
description: Transition Scholarships are available to students starting Year 7 at Auburn High School and are selected in partnership with the young person's primary school. Students are identified as eligible scholars based on their attendance, engagement in education or a specific passion, and their access to means-tested concessions cards. The program provides financial assistance to support students' transition to secondary school. For further information about eligibility and the application process, families should contact Leah Rintoule at the school. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate strong engagement with their education and provides support during their transition to high school.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to Year 7; Must be eligible for means-tested concessions cards; Selection in partnership with primary school; Demonstrates attendance and engagement in education or specific passion
special_conditions: Selection is made in partnership with the student's primary school. Eligibility based on access to means-tested concessions cards. Families experiencing financial hardship may be eligible.

overview: The Education CHANCES Scholarship Program provides financial assistance to students who live or study in the Boroondara area and whose families are experiencing financial hardship. The program identifies eligible scholars based on attendance, engagement, and access to means-tested concessions cards.
description: The Education CHANCES Scholarship Program is an external support initiative providing financial assistance to students who live or study in Boroondara and whose families are experiencing financial hardship. Students are identified as eligible scholars based on their attendance, engagement in education or a specific passion, and their access to means-tested concessions cards. This program provides a pathway to educational support for families facing financial barriers. The program complements other forms of financial assistance available through Auburn High School, including transition scholarships and support from the Hawthorn Community Chest. Further details about eligibility and application procedures can be obtained by contacting the school.
eligibility_criteria: Live or study in Boroondara area; Family experiencing financial hardship; Access to means-tested concessions cards; Demonstrated attendance and engagement in education or specific passion
special_conditions: Specific to students in the Boroondara area. Families must demonstrate financial hardship. Eligibility based on access to Victorian Government means-tested concessions cards.

overview: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Department of Education and Training funded program that provides yearly payments to eligible students to support participation in school camps, excursions, and sports and outdoor education programs.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a government-funded program providing yearly payments of $225 for eligible students to attend school camps, excursions, and sports and outdoor education programs. Eligibility is based on access to means-tested concession cards as recognised by the Victorian Government. Auburn High School administers this program in partnership with the Department of Education and Training. The program ensures that financial barriers do not prevent eligible students from participating in valuable educational and sporting experiences. Students with access to qualifying means-tested concessions cards are automatically eligible. A full list of eligible means-tested concession cards can be found on the Victorian Government website.
eligibility_criteria: Access to eligible means-tested concession cards; Enrolled as student at Auburn High School; Eligible cards include Victorian Government recognised concession categories
special_conditions: Annual payment of $225 for eligible students. Administered by Department of Education and Training. Requires access to Victorian Government means-tested concession cards. Eligible cards list available on vic.gov.au website.

overview: ACC Victoria Online provides fee discounts to families who hold a health care card. This financial assistance program supports families experiencing financial hardship by reducing tuition costs for eligible students.
description: Families who hold a health care card are eligible for further discounts on tuition fees. Eligible families should enquire at the school prior to submitting their enrolment application to confirm the discount amount and ensure they understand the process for applying. The health care card must be current and valid at the time of application. Families need to contact the school directly to discuss the specific discount available to them and how to provide documentation of their health care card status. This is means-tested financial assistance based on government recognition of financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Current valid health care card holder; Must enquire prior to submitting enrolment application; Victorian resident
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Families must enquire at the school prior to submitting their enrolment application. Documentation of health care card status will be required. Discount amount varies based on family circumstances.

overview: ACC Victoria Online offers a full tuition fee discount for families with four or more children enrolled at the school. The 4th and subsequent children receive 100% discount on tuition fees, making the school more accessible to larger families.
description: Families with four or more children enrolled at ACC Victoria Online will receive a 100% discount on tuition fees for the 4th child and each subsequent child enrolled. This significant discount applies to the base tuition fees charged on a per-term basis. The discount helps families with multiple children manage the cost of education and recognises the financial commitment required when educating several children simultaneously. Resource levy fees still apply per student. This automatic discount applies once families meet the four-child threshold.
eligibility_criteria: Must have four or more children enrolled at ACC Victoria Online; Children must be enrolled at the same school; Discount applies from 4th child onwards
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Discount applies automatically once fourth child is enrolled. Resource levy fees of $100 per year (prorated per term) still apply per student. Children must be currently enrolled in the school to qualify for this discount.

overview: ACC Victoria Online offers a ministry discount to families with paid Christian pastoral employment. This program supports Christian ministry workers by reducing tuition costs for their children's education.
description: Families with paid Christian pastoral employment may be eligible for a ministry discount on tuition fees. Documentation of pastoral employment status may be required. The discount applies to tuition fees charged on a per-term basis.

year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Must have paid employment in a Christian pastoral role. Families should contact school to confirm eligibility and discount amount. Documentation of pastoral employment status will be required.

overview: Australian International Academy of Education offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding achievement across all year levels. The scholarship programme recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, with scholarships available for both primary and secondary students.
description: The Academic Scholarship is one of six scholarship categories offered by AIA across the Melbourne Senior Campus, King Khalid Coburg Campus, and Caroline Springs Campus. Scholarships are available in Full, Half, and Quarter forms, providing fee reduction of 100%, 50%, and 25% respectively. The Academy offers a total of 35 scholarships (Full, Half, and Quarter combined) across all campuses and scholarship categories. Academic Scholarship examinations are administered by EduTest, an organisation that conducts testing for almost 100 schools across Australia. Testing occurs in August (2025 testing date: Tuesday, August 12, 2025). Scholarships are open to both local and overseas students. Applications are submitted via online registration, with application deadlines typically in late June.

special_conditions: Scholarships open to both local and overseas students. A non-refundable application fee is required to cover administrative costs. Scholarship testing is conducted by EduTest for primary level exams and internally for secondary level exams.

overview: Australian International Academy of Education offers Qur'aan Kareem Scholarships to recognise students who have achieved excellence in Qur'an memorisation and tajweed (correct recitation). The scholarship is designed for students across primary and secondary year levels who demonstrate dedication to Quranic studies.

Eligibility is structured by year level: Foundation to Year 2 students must memorise One Part (Juzz); Year 3 to Year 5 students must memorise Two Parts (Ajza); Year 6 to Year 8 students must memorise Three Parts (Ajza); and All year levels can compete for memorisation of Ten Parts (Ajza). Scholarships are available in Full, Half, and Quarter forms (100%, 50%, and 25% fee reduction respectively). The scholarship is offered across the Melbourne Senior Campus, King Khalid Coburg Campus, and Caroline Springs Campus. Testing takes place in August (2025 testing date: Wednesday, August 13, 2025). The Academy offers these scholarships to both current and prospective students, local and overseas.
eligibility_criteria: Open to primary and secondary students; Year levels determined by memorisation level required; Foundation to Year 2 - Memorisation of One Part/Juzz; Year 3 to Year 5 - Memorisation of Two Parts/Ajza; Year 6 to Year 8 - Memorisation of Three Parts/Ajza; All levels - Memorisation of Ten Parts/Ajza; Open to local and overseas students; Prospective and current students eligible
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
number_awarded: Multiple (part of 35 total across all categories)

overview: Ave Maria College offers the Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through competitive examination and is open to those who have accepted enrolment at the College.
description: The 2026 Excellence Scholarship is awarded on the basis of academic performance demonstrated through a competitive scholarship examination held on Saturday 26 July 2025. Excellence Scholarships contribute between 50% to 80% of tuition fees based on the year level. At Year 7, scholarships are open to students who have accepted their 2026 enrolment; at Year 9 and Year 11, scholarships are only open to current students at Ave Maria College. Scholarships are awarded for the duration of the student's secondary education and are subject to an annual review of excellence performance. The selection process requires completion of a full application, onsite examination, and interview.

special_conditions: All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Applications for Year 9 and Year 11 will only be available to current students at the College.

description: The Arts Scholarship at AIA recognises students with outstanding talent and achievement in the performing and visual arts. The scholarship offers Full Fee Remission, Half Scholarship (50% Fee Reduction), or Quarter Scholarship (25% Fee Reduction). The selection process includes evaluation of artistic portfolios and demonstrated achievement in arts disciplines. Information regarding the Arts Scholarship criteria and requirements is available for download from the scholarships page. Total of 35 scholarships available across all campuses. Applicants are encouraged to demonstrate their artistic excellence through portfolio presentation and achievement records.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with demonstrated excellence in arts; Must submit artistic portfolio or evidence of achievement; Open to local and overseas students; Artistic merit and talent required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Selection based on artistic portfolio and demonstrated excellence in arts disciplines. Information document available for download detailing full Arts Scholarship criteria. Scholarship holders expected to contribute to school arts programs.

school_name: Bacchus Marsh Grammar - Staughton Vale Farm Campus
Ballarat Specialist School - Midlands Farm Campus
Bass Coast College - San Remo Campus
Bass Coast College-Community VCAL Program Campus

overview: Bayside P-12 College offers the Select Entry Academic Achievers Program for highly motivated and academically able students in Years 7 to 9. The program provides a challenging and rewarding learning environment tailored to meet the needs of high-achieving students at both Altona North and Williamstown junior campuses.
description: The Select Entry Academic Achievers Program is a comprehensive academic initiative spanning from Year 7 to Year 9, specifically created for students with a passion for learning. Students participate in a curriculum that exceeds the expected learning level, with higher-order thinking tasks to foster critical and creative problem-solving skills. The program offers opportunities for potential acceleration into VCE courses as early as Year 10 and a wide variety of engaging excursions and learning experiences. There is no additional fee charged for program participation as it is offered within the government school system. Selection is based on demonstrated academic merit through an entrance examination in Mathematics and English. Students are supported with enriched curriculum delivery and advanced academic challenges throughout their three years in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Must reside within Bayside P-12 College school enrolment zone; Must be enrolling in Year 7; Must pass the entrance examination in Mathematics and English; Available at Altona North Campus (Prep-9) and Williamstown Campus (Years 7-9)
special_conditions: Entrance examination scheduled for Friday, 5th September 2025, from 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM. Following finalisation of general enrolments in late July, all applicants will be contacted with testing details. Contact: Camilla Bishop, Academic Achievers Coordinator.

overview: Bayside P-12 College, in partnership with Tennis Victoria, offers an Elite Tennis Program for secondary students from Year 7 to Year 12. The program combines intensive on-court training with the Academic Achievers Program, allowing students to pursue their tennis aspirations while maintaining rigorous academic standards.
description: The Elite Tennis Program is located on the Williamstown Campus and provides a unique opportunity for students to develop their tennis potential under the direction of John Trickey (Program Director). Students in Years 7 to 10 spend approximately 22 hours per fortnight in the Tennis Program, including on-court training, competition, tactical sessions, strength and conditioning, nutrition, and sports psychology, alongside 28 hours in the Academic Achievers Program. VCE students spend approximately 11 hours per fortnight in the Tennis Program. The program is designed for students who demonstrate strong tennis ability and academic commitment. Enrolment is selective and requires an assessment process including an on-court evaluation with program coaches, or alternatively a comprehensive video submission demonstrating tennis competency. There is no additional tuition fee charged for participation in this government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate tennis ability suitable for elite program level; Must pass suitability assessment including on-court session or video evaluation; Suitable candidates must also participate in the Academic Achievers Program; Located at Williamstown Campus
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applicants must complete Tennis Enrolment Enquiry form. Assessment session will be arranged following form submission, including on-court evaluation with coaches. School accepts comprehensive video from players in place of on-court session. Program operates at Williamstown Campus only. Ongoing participation requires maintaining academic standards in the Academic Achievers Program component.

overview: Bayside P-12 College offers the Academic Achievers Program as a select entry initiative for highly motivated and academically able students. The program spans Years 7 to 9 and provides an enriched, challenging curriculum designed for students with a passion for learning and exceptional academic ability.
description: The Academic Achievers Program is a comprehensive academic initiative spanning Years 7 to 9, specifically created for students with a passion for learning. The program is built around an explicit instructional model that challenges and extends students while utilizing an evidence-based enriched curriculum. Students engage with content at a metacognitive level, fostering deeper understanding and connection across various key learning areas. They are encouraged to make interdisciplinary links, apply real-world problem-solving, and participate in the school-wide wellbeing program. No financial benefit is provided as this is a government school program. Students remain in the program for the duration of their enrolment in Years 7-9 unless circumstances change. Selection is based on performance in an entrance examination testing Mathematics and English.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must sit an entrance exam in Mathematics and English; Must reside within the school's enrolment zone; Open to students from both Altona North and Williamstown junior campuses
special_conditions: Following the finalisation of enrolments in late July, all applicants will be contacted with details for the Academic Achievers select entry test. The 2025 Year 7 Academic Achievers entry test for 2026 entry takes place in early September. Parents must ensure their child resides within the school's enrolment zone to be eligible.

overview: Bayside P-12 College offers an Elite Tennis Program in partnership with Tennis Victoria for secondary school students in Years 7 to 12. The program is designed to develop young athletes' tennis potential while maintaining academic excellence through placement in the Academic Achievers Program.
description: In partnership with Tennis Victoria, Bayside P-12 College offers a unique Elite Tennis Program for secondary school students from Year 7 to Year 12, located on the Williamstown Campus. The program is designed to nurture young athletes, providing access to world-class coaching and elite-level training in a supportive environment. Students in Years 7-10 spend approximately 22 hours per fortnight in the Tennis Program (on-court training, competition, tactical sessions, strength and conditioning, nutrition, and sports psychology) and 28 hours in the Academic Achievers Program. Students in VCE (Years 11-12) spend approximately 11 hours per fortnight in the Tennis Program. No direct financial benefit is provided, though elite coaching is included as part of the program structure. The program operates for the duration of a student's enrolment at the school. Selection is based on demonstrated tennis ability and potential, combined with academic capability for placement in the Academic Achievers Program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students in Years 7 to 12; Must demonstrate elite-level tennis ability and potential; Must be capable of meeting academic standards for the Academic Achievers Program; Located on Williamstown Campus
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Tennis students are placed in the Academic Achievers Program, which requires successful completion of an entrance examination in Mathematics and English. Program is exclusive to Williamstown Campus. Students must maintain the commitment to both the tennis training hours and academic program requirements.

overview: Bayswater Secondary College offers the SEAL Program to allow talented students to undertake their Years 7-10 curriculum in three years. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and enables completion of VCE education and university entrance courses across the remaining three years.
description: The Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program at Bayswater Secondary College is designed for gifted and talented students who wish to fast-track their secondary education. Students accepted into the program complete their Years 7-10 curriculum in three years rather than four, allowing them to begin VCE studies and university entrance courses in Year 10. The program utilises assessment through an EduTest exam format that evaluates mathematical and literacy skills as well as problem-solving ability. Selection is highly competitive, with students required to demonstrate exceptional academic performance across all areas. Students in the SEAL program attend special classes with other SEAL students to ensure their learning is appropriately accelerated and challenged. The program is available to students entering from Year 6 and continues through to Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must complete EduTest selective entry exam; Academic performance demonstrating exceptional ability; Application through school-based process
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: SEAL students attend special accelerated classes within the school. The program allows completion of VCE and university entrance courses in Years 11-12 due to the accelerated pace in Years 7-10.

overview: Bayswater West Primary School participates in the Victorian High Ability Program (VHAP), which provides extension and enrichment opportunities for students who have demonstrated high ability in their learning. The program is designed to challenge and support academically advanced students within the school community.
description: The Victorian High Ability Program (VHAP) is aimed at students from Years 5 to 8 and provides extension and enrichment opportunities for students who have demonstrated high ability in their learning. Selected students participate in virtual classes delivered by specially trained teachers using web-based Learning Management System and Cisco Webex technology. The program focuses on tasks that stimulate students' critical thinking, problem solving and creativity, enriching their understanding of English and Mathematics. Students participate in one virtual class per week over a 10-week period. This program is non-monetary and involves no direct cost to families. Selected students will only be offered a place in up to one course each term to balance the load on students and minimise the impact of missing more than ten regular classes per term.
eligibility_criteria: Students from Years 5 to 8; Must be identified by the Department of Education and Training using student achievement data; High ability students typically work significantly above the expected level for their age group in one or more areas of the curriculum
special_conditions: Only students who have been selected by the Department of Education and Training are able to participate in the program. Students will be invited to participate across multiple years if they are deemed eligible. However, students will only be able to participate in mathematics or English once in primary and once in secondary. Selected students only be offered a place in up to one course each term.

overview: Bayswater West Primary School participates in the Victorian Challenge and Enrichment Series (VCES), which provides enrichment opportunities and activities across the curriculum to extend high-ability students. The program is open to all high-ability students from Prep to Year 12 in Victorian government schools.
description: The Victorian Challenge and Enrichment Series (VCES) provides enrichment opportunities and activities across the curriculum to extend high-ability students from Prep to Year 12 in Victorian government schools. The Department partners with more than 20 expert education and not-for-profit organisations, universities, tech schools, teacher associations, and science and mathematics specialist centres to deliver diverse enrichment activities. All activities are free for participating students and often combine content from more than one learning area within the Victorian Curriculum. Activities run in a small group format, and the school aims to offer places to different students throughout the year. Courses and activities offered vary from term to term according to what providers offer. All activities are facilitated by teaching staff from Bayswater West PS who provide any support students require to engage with the activities.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as high ability students working significantly above the expected level for their age group in one or more areas of the curriculum; Open to students from Prep to Year 12
special_conditions: All activities are facilitated by teaching staff from Bayswater West PS and include support as needed. The school aims to offer places to different students throughout the year to ensure broad participation across the student cohort.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Academic Scholarship to talented students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and strong commitment to their studies.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed for students with outstanding academic performance entering Year 10 or Year 11. Applicants must register and sit an Edutest examination scheduled at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. All Edutest results and portfolios are reviewed by a College assessment panel. Shortlisted applicants will be notified of outcomes by the end of Term 2, 2026. The scholarship provides financial support to help talented students access Beaconhills' educational program. Applicants must submit school reports from the last two years, latest NAPLAN results, a student statement, awards and achievements documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must submit last two years of school reports; Must submit latest NAPLAN results; Must provide student statement explaining motivation; Must submit supporting awards and achievements
special_conditions: All applicants must register and sit the Edutest examination. Shortlisted applicants will be notified by end of Term 2, 2026. Recipients must maintain academic standards and alignment with College values.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to well-rounded students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence across academic, co-curricular, and leadership pursuits.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding achievement across multiple dimensions, including academic performance, co-curricular activities, and leadership qualities. Applicants must register and sit the Edutest examination at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. The application process requires submission of school reports from the last two years, latest NAPLAN results, a student statement, supporting awards and achievements. All Edutest results and portfolios are reviewed by a College assessment panel, with outcomes notified by end of Term 2, 2026. The scholarship provides financial assistance to support talented, well-rounded students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must submit last two years of school reports; Must submit latest NAPLAN results; Must provide student statement; Must demonstrate excellence in multiple areas including academics, co-curricular and leadership
special_conditions: All applicants must register and sit the Edutest examination. Shortlisted applicants will be notified by end of Term 2, 2026. Recipients must maintain standards across academic and co-curricular pursuits.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to talented performing artists entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional ability in music, drama, or dance.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship is designed for students with demonstrated excellence in music, drama, dance, or other performing arts. Applicants must register and sit the Edutest examination at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. Shortlisted Performing Arts applicants will be invited to an audition at the Berwick Campus on Monday 23 March 2026. The application requires submission of school reports from the last two years, latest NAPLAN results, a student statement, and supporting media files of performances or achievements. Media files must be of high quality. All Edutest results and portfolios are reviewed by a College assessment panel, with outcomes notified by end of Term 2, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must attend audition at Berwick Campus on 23 March 2026 if shortlisted; Must submit last two years of school reports; Must submit latest NAPLAN results; Must provide high-quality media files of performances
special_conditions: Shortlisted Performing Arts applicants must attend audition at Berwick Campus on 23 March 2026. High-quality media files of performances are essential for evaluation. Recipients must maintain performing arts involvement and alignment with College values.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Indigenous (Wominjeka) Scholarship to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous students pursuing their education at the College.
description: The Indigenous (Wominjeka) Scholarship is designed specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2027. Applicants must register separately for this scholarship and sit the Edutest examination at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. The application requires submission of school reports from the last two years, latest NAPLAN results, a student statement outlining motivation and contribution to the College, and supporting awards and achievements. All Edutest results and portfolios are reviewed by a College assessment panel, with applicants notified of outcomes by the end of Term 2, 2026. The scholarship provides financial support to facilitate Indigenous student access to independent education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Must be entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must submit last two years of school reports; Must submit latest NAPLAN results; Must provide student statement; Must provide supporting awards and achievements
special_conditions: Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent. Recipients must maintain academic standards and contribute to College life. The scholarship supports Indigenous students aligned with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity.
