overview: Beaconhills College offers the Sliding Doors Scholarship to students from socially or financially disadvantaged backgrounds entering Year 9 in 2026. The scholarship is means-tested and recognises students with demonstrated interest in leadership and positive community contribution.
description: The Sliding Doors Scholarship is a means-tested financial assistance program designed for students from socially or financially disadvantaged backgrounds entering Year 9 in 2026 at either the Pakenham or Berwick Campus. The scholarship, made possible through partnership with The Sliding Doors Foundation and the College's Community Education Support Fund (CESF), will cover the majority of each year's school fees from Years 9-12. Recipient selection prioritises students demonstrating demonstrated interest in leadership pursuits and creating positive impact in their community, who are motivated, and aligned to the College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity. Only shortlisted applicants are required to submit a Financial Application form. Outcomes are notified by end of Term 2, 2026. Registrations for the 2026 Sliding Doors Scholarship have now closed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 9 in 2026; From socially or financially disadvantaged backgrounds; Demonstrated interest in leadership pursuits; Commitment to creating positive community impact; Aligned to College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity; Will actively contribute to College life through extra-curricular participation, leadership and community service
special_conditions: Registrations for 2026 Sliding Doors Scholarship have now closed. Only shortlisted applicants required to submit Financial Application form. Recipients must maintain demonstrated commitment to leadership and community service. The CESF was established for the charitable purposes of advancing education.

overview: The Community Education Support Fund (CESF) is part of Beaconhills College's Beacon of Hope Foundation, established to financially support families in need who are unable to access a Beaconhills College education. The fund provides targeted financial assistance to eligible families.
description: The Community Education Support Fund (CESF) is a dedicated fund within the Beacon of Hope Foundation, established specifically for the charitable purpose of advancing education at Beaconhills College. The fund is designed to financially support families in need or those who are unable to access a Beaconhills education due to financial constraints. Recipients are determined through an application process that assesses demonstrated financial need and commitment to College values. The CESF supports the delivery of scholarships and bursaries including the Sliding Doors Scholarship program. Families can apply through the College's enrolment office for consideration of financial assistance under this fund.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families unable to access a Beaconhills College education; Financial need assessment required; Application process required; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Recipients determined via application process. Fund was established for the charitable purposes of advancing education. Applicants should contact the College's enrolment office for more details on the application process.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 10 and 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning excellence.
description: Beaconhills College's Academic Scholarship is awarded to high-achieving students entering Years 10 and 11. Applicants must sit an Edutest examination held at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction based on merit. Recipients are selected through a rigorous assessment process that includes written testing, portfolio review, and submission of supporting documents including the last two years of school reports and latest NAPLAN results. Shortlisted applicants may be interviewed by the College assessment panel. Results are notified by the end of term 2, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or current students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must be currently enrolled in Year 9 or equivalent; Last two years of school reports required; Latest NAPLAN assessment results required
special_conditions: All applicants must register online and sit Edutest examination. Selection is based on academic merit and demonstrated commitment to learning. Recipients' scholarships are subject to ongoing academic performance and continued alignment with College values.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise well-rounded students entering Years 10 and 11. The scholarship rewards students who demonstrate excellence across academic, cultural, sporting or leadership pursuits.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship at Beaconhills recognises talented all-rounder students entering Years 10 and 11 who demonstrate outstanding achievement across multiple disciplines. Applicants must sit an Edutest examination held at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction based on demonstrated excellence. Assessment includes written testing, portfolio submission, and supporting documentation including school reports and NAPLAN results. The College assessment panel reviews all submissions and notifies outcomes by end of term 2, 2026. Strong academic results and high attendance are required, along with evidence of involvement in co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or current students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must demonstrate excellence in multiple areas (academic, arts, sport, leadership); Last two years of school reports required; Latest NAPLAN assessment required; Strong attendance record required
special_conditions: All applicants must register online and sit Edutest examination. Recipients must demonstrate alignment with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity. Scholarships subject to annual review and continued co-curricular participation and academic progress.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to reward students with exceptional talent and achievement in performing arts. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 10 and 11 and recognises excellence in music, drama, dance or theatre.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship recognises students with outstanding talent in music, drama, dance or other performance areas entering Years 10 and 11. Applicants must sit an Edutest examination at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. Shortlisted Performing Arts applicants are invited to an audition at the Berwick Campus on Monday 23 March 2026. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction based on artistic merit and potential. Selection considers Edutest results, audition performance, portfolio materials, and supporting documentation. All applicants must submit school reports from the last two years, latest NAPLAN results, a student statement, and evidence of awards or achievements in performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or current students entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Demonstrated excellence or exceptional talent in performing arts (music, drama, dance, theatre); Must attend audition if shortlisted on 23 March 2026; Last two years of school reports required; Latest NAPLAN assessment required; Portfolio of performing arts work required
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants only will be invited for audition at Berwick Campus on 23 March 2026. High quality media files of performing arts work must be submitted. Recipients must maintain active involvement in Performing Arts at the College. Scholarship subject to annual review based on continued artistic participation and academic progress.

overview: Beaconhills College offers the Indigenous (Wominjeka) Scholarship to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 10 and 11 and provides opportunities for Indigenous students to access quality secondary education.
description: The Indigenous (Wominjeka) Scholarship at Beaconhills College supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 10 and 11. Applicants must register separately for this scholarship and sit an Edutest examination at the Pakenham Campus on Saturday 14 March 2026. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction to assist Indigenous students in accessing a Beaconhills education. Selection is based on academic merit, demonstrated potential, and alignment with College values. Applicants must submit last two years of school reports, latest NAPLAN results, a personal statement, and supporting awards or achievements. The College assessment panel reviews all applications and notifies outcomes by end of term 2, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students; Entering Years 10 and 11 in 2027; Must sit Edutest examination on 14 March 2026; Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Last two years of school reports required; Latest NAPLAN assessment required; Personal statement outlining contribution to College community required
special_conditions: Separate registration required for Indigenous scholarship. Applicants must provide evidence of Indigenous heritage. Recipients must maintain academic standards and be aligned with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity. Scholarship subject to annual review.

overview: Beaconhills College, in partnership with The Sliding Doors Foundation, offers the Sliding Doors Scholarship to support students from socially or financially disadvantaged backgrounds. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 9 and recognises students with demonstrated leadership potential and commitment to community impact.
description: The Sliding Doors Scholarship is a new initiative designed to support students from socially or financially disadvantaged backgrounds who wish to access independent education. Available for students entering Year 9 in 2026, this means-tested scholarship covers the majority of each year's tuition fees from Years 9 to 12. The scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating interest in leadership pursuits and creating positive community impact. Selection is based on evidence of strong academic results, high attendance, alignment with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity, and demonstrated capacity to contribute to College life. Following initial assessment, shortlisted applicants must submit a Financial Application Form and attend an interview with the Executive Principal and Head of Engagement. This is a needs-based scholarship covering approximately the majority of annual tuition costs across four years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 9 in 2026 at either Pakenham or Berwick Campus; Families unable to cover the costs of a Beaconhills College education; Demonstrated interest in leadership and creating positive community impact; Strong academic results and high attendance required; Evidence of latest school report and NAPLAN assessment required; Alignment with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity; Must demonstrate how scholarship would enable them to benefit from Beaconhills education
special_conditions: Registrations for 2026 Sliding Doors Scholarship have now closed (closed 17 October 2025). This is a means-tested scholarship; only shortlisted applicants required to submit Financial Application Form. Recipients must maintain active involvement in College life through extra-curricular participation, leadership and community service. Scholarship covers the majority of tuition fees from Years 9-12. Recipient must be aligned with College values of Respect, Compassion and Integrity.

overview: Beaconhills College's Community Education Support Fund (CESF) provides financial assistance to families in need or those who are unable to access a Beaconhills education. The fund is administered through the Beacon of Hope Foundation and operates on an application basis.
description: The Community Education Support Fund (CESF) is part of the Beacon of Hope Foundation's charitable programs and provides ongoing financial support to families who face barriers to accessing Beaconhills education. The fund financially supports families in need through an application process. Recipients are determined through assessment of demonstrated financial hardship and inability to meet school fees. The CESF awards scholarships and bursaries to eligible families and is funded through the Foundation's charitable giving and community contributions. Families interested in this support should contact the College directly to discuss their circumstances and learn about the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Families unable to access a Beaconhills education; Demonstrated financial hardship; Current or prospective Beaconhills students; All year levels eligible; Application process required; Assessment based on financial need
special_conditions: Application-based assessment. Recipients determined through formal application process. Fund is administered by the Beacon of Hope Foundation. Available to families facing genuine financial barriers to access. May include both scholarships and bursaries. All donations to the fund receive tax benefits.

overview: Beit Hillel Community School participates in the Jewish Education Foundation's Fee Assessment Board (FAB) program, which provides financial assistance to families. The FAB assesses and assists with school fees for eligible families enrolling children at participating Jewish schools.
description: The Fee Assessment Board (FAB) is a program administered by the Jewish Education Foundation that provides financial fee assistance to families enrolling at participating Jewish schools including Beit Hillel Community School. The program can assess fees for children commencing in Prep if families do not have older children attending a Jewish school or independent school. Financial assistance is provided to help make Jewish education more accessible. The exact financial benefits, application process, and eligibility criteria are determined by the Jewish Education Foundation's assessment and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Families without older children attending a Jewish school or independent school; Open to students commencing in Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, or Year 4 (depending on year and school availability); Must be applying to one of the participating schools
special_conditions: Families must apply through the Jewish Education Foundation. The program is means-tested and based on assessed financial need. Participating schools include Beit Hillel Community School, Bialik College, King David School, Leibler Yavneh College, Mount Scopus Memorial College, Sholem Aleichem College and Yeshivah Beth Rivkah Colleges.

overview: Bellarine Secondary College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEAL), one of only 35 accredited programs in Victoria. The program provides like-minded students with the opportunity to be accelerated and challenged through a demanding and rewarding learning environment tailored to academically gifted students.
description: The SEAL program allows students to progress through their secondary education, particularly Years 7-10, at a faster rate without omitting content. Students experience greater emphasis on higher-order thinking, learning skills, independent learning and research. The course content of a typical Year 7-10 program is completed more rapidly with less repetition than the mainstream program. Enrichment is provided by broadening content, understanding, experience and skills to appropriate levels, with extension leading to in-depth study and integration of knowledge at deeper levels. Students are accelerated across core subjects including English, Mathematics, Humanities, Science, Indonesian Language, The Arts, Digital and Design Technologies, Health and Physical Education. The selection process involves formal testing using the Edutest Scholarship Test. No specific financial benefit is listed for this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have solid and positive self-concept; Must demonstrate high levels of creativity; Must have high level of maturity and commitment; Must be motivated and capable of independent learning; Must sit formal entrance testing (Edutest Scholarship Test)
special_conditions: The program is designed to accommodate the learning needs of particularly talented students. Application process begins in March of the preceding year and closes the last week of Term 1. Students must be capable of handling a greater workload and deeper learning in the early years of secondary schooling.

overview: Bellarine Secondary College offers a scholarship to a Grade 6 student from each of its Partnership Primary Schools. The scholarship recognises students who have consistently demonstrated academic effort, participation in school activities, and respect for others.
description: Bellarine Secondary College awards a scholarship to one Grade 6 student from each partnership primary school to support their transition to secondary education. Recipients are selected based on consistent display of the following qualities: academic effort, participation in school activities, and respect for others. The scholarship acknowledges students who embody the College's values and are ready for secondary education. These scholarships are intended to support and recognise excellence across multiple dimensions of school life, not solely academic achievement. Specific financial details regarding the value or duration of this scholarship were not disclosed in available sources.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students; Must be enrolled in a Partnership Primary School; Must have consistently demonstrated academic effort; Must have participated in school activities; Must have demonstrated respect for others
special_conditions: One scholarship is awarded per partnership primary school. Selection is based on demonstration of academic effort, participation in school activities, and respect for others. Partnership primary schools may include Drysdale Primary School and other local feeder schools.

overview: Bellarine Secondary College offers a scholarship to one Grade 6 student from each of its partnership primary schools. These scholarships recognise students who have demonstrated exceptional academic effort, participation in school activities, and respect for others.
description: Bellarine Secondary College provides scholarships to Grade 6 students from partnership primary schools as part of its commitment to supporting high-achieving students transitioning to secondary education. Recipients are selected based on their consistent demonstration of academic effort, active participation in school activities, and respect for others. The scholarship provides recognition and support for students entering Year 7. The selection process involves nomination by partnership primary schools based on demonstrated qualities across academic, co-curricular, and behavioural domains. Students who receive this scholarship benefit from the College's comprehensive curriculum, excellent facilities, and supportive learning environment as they commence their secondary education journey.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 6 at a Bellarine Secondary College partnership primary school; Must demonstrate academic effort; Must show active participation in school activities; Must demonstrate respect for others; Nominated by partnership primary school
special_conditions: Scholarship nomination is made by the student's primary school. Awards are based on demonstrated consistency across academic, behavioural and co-curricular domains.

overview: The Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEAL) at Bellarine Secondary College is one of only 35 accredited programs in Victoria. It provides a specialised learning environment for students who have demonstrated they can progress through their learning faster than their peers, with greater emphasis on higher-order thinking and independent research.
description: Bellarine Secondary College's Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEAL) is an accredited selective entry program designed for students with exceptional academic ability who can progress through secondary education at an accelerated pace. The program operates across Years 7-10, allowing students to complete standard secondary curriculum content more rapidly without omitting any learning outcomes. The SEAL program provides enhanced learning experiences with emphasis on higher-order thinking skills, independent learning, and advanced research. Selection involves submission of information from the applicant's primary school, student attendance records from Grades 5 and 6, parental information, NAPLAN results, and performance on the Edutest Scholarship Test. Students in the SEAL program continue to achieve outstanding VCE results, with many earning high ATAR scores (96.75-99.55 for College Dux over recent years) and accounting for a large share of top college results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Demonstrated ability to progress through learning faster than peers; Strong NAPLAN performance; Minimum attendance expectations based on Grade 5 and 6 records; All school sectors eligible
special_conditions: The Edutest Scholarship Test is conducted at Drysdale Campus on Tuesday, 22nd July 2026 from 9:15am to 12:30pm for 2026 entry. Registration must be completed at least one week before the test date. Students must continue through Year 10 as the program is structured for Years 7-10. The program is one of only 35 accredited Accelerated Learning Programs in Victoria.

overview: Belmont High School offers an accredited Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program for gifted and high potential students in Years 7 to 9. The program provides curriculum acceleration with faster-paced learning, reduced repetition, and in-depth study of abstract and complex concepts. Students are challenged by their intellectual peers and develop advanced investigative and problem-solving skills.
description: The SEALP is an accredited program offered by Belmont High School, one of 37 Victorian Government secondary schools providing accelerated learning for gifted students. Students accepted into the program complete Year 7-10 curriculum in three years instead of the usual four, allowing them to begin VCE subjects in Year 9. Curriculum acceleration in the SEAL Program differs from mainstream classes by providing faster-paced curriculum with less repetition, supporting study of abstract and complex concepts, and developing high-level investigative, problem-solving and creative thinking skills. At Belmont High School, students undertaking this program are accelerated in English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities and Languages, while studying other subjects (Arts, Technology, Health and Physical Education) with mainstream classes. The program offers no direct monetary benefit. There is no application fee. Selection is based on a general aptitude test evaluating mathematics, problem-solving, comprehension and writing abilities, Grade 5 NAPLAN results, and Semester 2 school reports.
eligibility_criteria: Must reside in Belmont High School catchment zone; Must complete Application for Secondary School Placement; Entrance by examination and assessment; Grade 5 NAPLAN and Semester 2 reports required; Proof of residential address required; Eligible for Year 7 entry 2027 with selection process in 2026
special_conditions: Students must reside in the Belmont High School catchment zone. All applicants must provide proof of residential address. Belmont High School must adhere to the Department of Education Priority Order for Placement policy for enrolment. Information Session held Wednesday 25th March 2026. Year 7 Placement forms available Week 1, Term 2 2026 (April 20-24).

overview: Belmont High School offers a growing International Student Program that welcomes overseas students on a long-term basis as well as short-term study tours. The school provides an on-site English Language Centre with intensive language support for international students, facilitating successful integration and academic achievement.
description: Belmont High School welcomes international students through its accredited International Student Program managed by the International Education Division (IED) of the Victorian Department of Education and Early Childhood Development. Applications for places in the International Student Program are managed centrally by the IED. The school provides long-term enrolment opportunities for overseas students as well as short-term study tours. An on-site English Language Centre provides intensive English language instruction to support international students' learning. The program has no direct monetary scholarship value. International students apply through the centralized IED application process rather than direct school application. The school is accredited to accept international students holding appropriate Student visas.
eligibility_criteria: International student visa holders; Application through International Education Division (IED); Application process managed centrally by Victorian Department of Education and Early Childhood Development; Eligible for long-term or short-term study
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications managed centrally by International Education Division (IED) at www.study.vic.gov.au. Simone Buchanan-Huhn is Director of International Student Program (buchanan-huhn.simone.s@edumail.vic.gov.au). Michael Caccamo is Assistant Principal involved in the program (caccamo.michael.d@edumail.vic.gov.au).

school_name: Bemin Secondary College - Everton Road Campus

overview: Belmont High School offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEALP) to provide highly talented and motivated students with a program that meets their specific learning needs. The program allows gifted and high potential students to complete their Year 7-10 curriculum in three years instead of the usual four, enabling earlier tertiary education entry.
description: The Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEALP) at Belmont High School is designed to challenge intellectually gifted students through an accelerated, fast-paced curriculum with less repetition and greater study of abstract, complex and in-depth concepts. Students undertaking this program are accelerated in English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities and Languages, with other subjects studied with mainstream classes including Arts, Technology and Health and Physical Education. The program fosters a learning environment where students are challenged by their intellectual peers and develop academically, socially and emotionally. Students in this program complete the school's Year 7-10 curriculum in three years instead of the usual four, and have options including completing VCE in two years and entering tertiary education earlier, including additional subjects over 3 years (including VCE units, first year university subjects and/or VET subjects), or consolidating Year 10 subjects and selecting limited VCE subjects in Year 4 to extend themselves in areas of special interest. There is no monetary benefit to this program, as it is a free government school.
eligibility_criteria: Must reside in the Belmont High School catchment zone; Proof of residential address required; Successful performance on general aptitude test evaluating mathematics, problem solving, comprehension and writing abilities; Grade 5 NAPLAN results and Semester 2 reports considered; Application for Secondary School Placement must be completed
special_conditions: Students must reside in the Belmont High School catchment zone and proof of address will be requested. Belmont High School must adhere to the Department of Education Priority Order for Placement policy for enrolment. The SEALP class is part of this priority order. All applicants must complete an Application for Secondary School Placement through the standard Victorian government school placement process.

overview: The Mason Family Trust provides former students of Bendigo Senior Secondary College with support in the form of bursaries to pursue a career and further study in the Arts. The bursary recognises students committed to developing careers in performing arts, visual arts, music, dance, drama, fashion, photography, media and related fields.
description: The Mason Family Trust was established in 2012 to provide former students of Bendigo Senior Secondary College with substantial financial support through bursaries to pursue tertiary study in the traditional and contemporary arts. The Trust makes available $30,000 per annum which will be distributed among a number of successful applicants. Funds can be used for course materials, specialist courses or lessons, and international travel costs related to the area of study, but are not available for local travel and living costs. Recipients must be enrolled in a tertiary course (University or TAFE) in the visual or performing arts, including Visual Arts (any art form including Graphic Design), Dance, Drama, Music, Fashion, Photography, Media and Stained Glass or Art Glass. Successful applicants are selected based on demonstrated interest and passion in developing a career within the Arts, and applications are competitively reviewed. The Trust requires applicants to provide proof of enrolment in the specified course and to present a written report outlining how funds were spent by September of the application year.
eligibility_criteria: Former students of Bendigo Senior Secondary College; Wish to undertake further education and training in the Visual or Performing Arts; Need financial assistance to complete a traditional Arts course already enrolled in; Not currently in receipt of another Mason Family Trust Bursary; Studying at tertiary level (University or TAFE); Able to provide evidence of enrolment
special_conditions: The Mason Family Trust was established in 2012 to support former students of BSSC. Ms. Mason was a supporter of Traditional Arts and Traditional Music. All applicants are assessed by the selection panel according to the conditions of the Trust. Funds must be spent exclusively on course-related items as specified in the approval. Recipients must provide a written report by September outlining expenditure. The number of bursaries awarded and their final value is at the discretion of the selection panel. Successful applicants may reapply in subsequent years.

overview: The Margaret Standen Bursary, established in 2018 in memory of Margaret Standen, a valued art teacher at Bendigo Senior Secondary College, provides Year 12 visual arts students with financial support to pursue their passion and career within the Visual Arts. The bursary assists students to purchase art materials and equipment needed for their final year VCE studies.
description: The Margaret Standen Bursary was established in 2018 by Peter Standen in memory of his wife, Margaret, a much-admired art teacher at Bendigo Senior Secondary College from 1977 to 2013. The bursary provides Year 12 students studying Art Making and Exhibiting (Painting, Drawing, Printmaking; Ceramics and Sculpture or Photography) and/or Art Creative Practice with financial support. Bursary amounts are up to $250 per application for items the school does not provide, such as canvases, brush sets, beads, sequins, and other specialist materials. Fashion students may apply only for items containing sculpture elements (wearable art), not standard garments. The bursary enables students to further their art studies and produce the best quality work possible. Applications are assessed on demonstrated interest and passion in the visual arts, with references from at least one BSSC teacher required.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students at BSSC; Currently studying Art Making and Exhibiting (Units 3 and 4) or Art Creative Practice; Studying Painting, Drawing, Printmaking; Ceramics and Sculpture; or Photography; Art Creative Practice Fashion students may apply for textile-based soft sculpture items only; Can provide references from at least one BSSC teacher
special_conditions: This bursary honours Margaret Standen, a valued art teacher at BSSC. Fashion students may only apply for items containing sculpture elements (wearable art), not standard garments. Applications that do not fit within the scope of the bursary may be requested to provide further documentation before consideration by the Standen family. Applicants must be studying Units 3 and 4 Studio Arts and/or Art General. All Year 12 students studying Studio Arts or Art General are eligible to apply.

overview: Bendigo South East College offers the Academy of Creative Arts (ACA), an audition-entry specialist arts program designed to help student-artists pursue excellence in their chosen field. The ACA nurtures the creativity, artistry and scholarship of students across five disciplines: drama, vocals, visual arts, music and dance.
description: The Academy of Creative Arts is an audition-entry specialist Arts Academy designed to help student-artists pursue excellence in their chosen field. The program offers successful auditionees access to specialist extracurricular coaching from leading industry professionals, alongside timetabled subject offerings. Students can apply to be in one of five fields: drama, vocals, visual arts, music or dance. The program includes flexible learning environments where students work in specialist facilities, attend high-performance workshops, are mentored by elite professionals, and access competitions, excursions, and creative work experience. There is a financial contribution of $1,300.00 per year for the specialist ACA program or $520.00 for Band only participants. Audition-based selection is required for entry into all fields. The program is open to students from Year 6 through to Year 12 alumni.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and beyond; Audition-based entry required; Students must demonstrate artistic ability and commitment to their chosen discipline; Open to all genders; Five fields available: drama, vocals, visual arts, music or dance
special_conditions: Successful auditionees must maintain behavioral and academic standards to remain in the program. The Academy promotes core values of Readiness, Resourcefulness, Resilience, Reflectiveness, and Reciprocity. Students participate in performances and exhibitions to showcase their talents.

overview: Bendigo South East College offers the Athlete Development Program (ADP), a select-entry program dedicated to improving educational and sporting outcomes for elite student-athletes. The ADP runs Technical Squad Sessions for AFL, Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cycling, Netball and Soccer, providing long-term athletic development training within a secondary college environment.
description: The Athlete Development Program is a select-entry program dedicated to improving educational and sporting outcomes for student-athletes. The ADP provides an opportunity for student-athletes to pursue their passion in a holistic academic and sport-based partnership. Students participate in two technical squad sessions per week focused on skill acquisition, tactical development and game understanding, two general physical preparation sessions targeting flexibility, mobility, strength, speed, agility, endurance and injury prevention, and one theoretical session per week covering sports psychology, nutrition, physiotherapy and exercise physiology. The financial contribution is $1,350.00 per year for the specialist ADP program or $520.00 for the ADP Associate Program, and $1,350.00 for the Athlete Development Associate Program with Squad Sessions. Sports offered include AFL, Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cycling, Netball and Soccer. Selection is based on application and assessment through the centralised ADP Victoria website.
eligibility_criteria: Select-entry based on sporting ability and potential; Must demonstrate commitment to academic and behavioral standards; Eligible sports include AFL, Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cycling, Netball and Soccer; Open to all genders; Students must meet requirements outlined in the Student-Athlete Agreement upon acceptance
special_conditions: Student-athletes are held to certain requirements to retain their position based on academic and behavioral standards, attendance and effort. All requirements are tied into the Student-Athlete Agreement which every athlete signs when accepting a place in the Program. Annual review is conducted. Applications are made via the ADP Victoria website at https://www.adp.vic.edu.au/. ADP Information Sessions are held in early May with open training sessions following.

overview: Bentleigh Secondary College offers a Gifted and Talented Program designed to nurture students identified as having exceptional educational ability. The program provides advanced learning opportunities in greater depth across the curriculum, with a clear focus on maximising the academic potential of each student. Students develop high-level written and oral skills while engaging in learning that encourages divergent thinking.
description: The Gifted and Talented Program is for high-achieving students in Years 7-9 who wish to develop their full potential in an advanced academic environment. Students gain entry through a selection process including a written entrance test, assessment of school performance reports, and Year 5 NAPLAN results. All GAT students participate in the Music Instrumental Program from Year 7 to 9. When students graduate to senior school, they have developed strong foundations to maximise their ATAR scores and increase opportunities for tertiary study. The program offers specialized curriculum with emphasis on academic excellence, creative thinking, and lifelong learning. Entry is selective and placement is by test performance only.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 (test taken before Year 7 entry); Outstanding performance on Gifted and Talented entrance test; Strong school performance reports from Year 5-6; Satisfactory Year 5 NAPLAN results; Students may also be recommended after Year 7 based on high performance; Must demonstrate commitment to academic excellence
special_conditions: All GAT students must participate in the Music Instrumental Program from Year 7 to 9. Participation is mandatory. Program consists of two classes per year level. Students must maintain satisfactory academic progress to remain in the program.

overview: Bentleigh Secondary College's Sports Academy has been an integral part of the school since 2017, offering selected students in Years 7-10 the opportunity to boost their athletic skills and overall development in a high-performance training environment. The program provides dedicated class time to develop sport-specific skills alongside comprehensive academic study.
description: The Sports Academy Program is an elite sporting program available to students in Years 7-10 who wish to pursue advanced athletic development. Selected students receive dedicated class time to hone their sport-specific skills, with access to Strength & Conditioning and Pilates sessions to develop well-rounded athletes. Sports available include Basketball, AFL/AFLW, Soccer, and Netball. Students are released from class once per week for Years 7-8, while Years 9-10 can select Sports Academy as an elective. The program is a full-year commitment with participation fee of $1,200.00 per year (2025). Selection is competitive and based on demonstrated athletic ability and commitment. The program includes sports-specific coaching and access to professional physiotherapy services.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Demonstrated athletic ability and sport-specific skills; Commitment to full academic program; Adherence to school code of conduct; Non-compliance may lead to program termination; Application does not guarantee placement at the college; Full year participation commitment required
special_conditions: Participation fee for 2025 Year 7 Sports Academy is $1,200.00. Participation is a full year commitment and payment is required. Non-payment results in removal from the program. Students must adhere to college code of conduct; non-compliance may lead to program termination. For Semester 1 withdrawal, only Semester 2 charges are refunded. For Semester 2 withdrawal, no refund is provided. Program is extra-curricular and optional. Contact: Matthew.burke3@education.vic.gov.au

overview: Bentleigh Secondary College offers a Music Academy for students passionate about developing their musical potential. The program provides specialized music instruction from expert teachers and access to state-of-the-art facilities currently under construction. Students collaborate with fellow musicians in a supportive environment designed to unleash their musical talents.
description: The Music Academy at Bentleigh Secondary College is designed for students passionate about refining their musical skills and collaborating with like-minded peers. The program offers specialized instruction from experienced music teachers, access to state-of-the-art instrumental music facilities including a new Performing Arts Precinct built in 2024 with six instrumental rooms, and opportunities to perform in over 13 ensembles including Concert Bands, String Ensembles, Stage Bands, Woodwind, Brass & Percussion Ensembles, and Choirs. Students can learn a wide range of instruments including Woodwind, Brass, Percussion, Strings, and Voice. Weekly lessons are scheduled on a rotated timetable to minimize impact on classroom time. The college participates in prestigious music festivals including Mt Gambier Jazz Festival and the Victorian Schools Music Festival.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated passion and interest in music; Proven ability in music or willingness to develop new skills; Participation in instrumental music lessons; Commitment to participate in at least one ensemble or band; Students interested in pursuing music to senior level; Can enroll from Year 7 onwards
special_conditions: Students enrolled in the Music Instrumental Program attend weekly 30-minute lessons on a rotated timetable. Participation in at least one band or ensemble is required. Tuition is for a full academic year. Any request to discontinue participation should be made to the head of program; if during Semester One, only Semester Two charges are refunded; if during Semester Two, no refund is provided. Contact: tim.rex@education.vic.gov.au

overview: Bentleigh Secondary College has a well-established Instrumental Music Program that offers numerous opportunities for student development through ensemble participation. The program features over 13 ensembles meeting weekly with rehearsals conducted before school, after school, or at lunch. Lessons are offered on various instruments with rotated timetables to minimize classroom disruption.
description: The Instrumental Music Program at Bentleigh Secondary College provides comprehensive music education and performance opportunities. Students can participate in over 13 ensembles including Concert Bands, String Ensembles, Stage Bands, VCE Ensemble, Woodwind/Brass/Percussion Ensembles, and Choirs. Weekly lessons are provided on a rotated timetable covering Woodwind, Brass, Percussion, Strings, and Voice instruction. Rehearsals are conducted before school, after school, or at lunch times. The college maintains a strong musical culture, encouraging all abilities to participate. Students participate in prestigious festivals including Mt Gambier Jazz Festival and Victorian Schools Music Festival. Participation in Instrumental Music is mandatory for all Gifted and Talented Program students in Years 7-9.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students interested in learning an instrument; Demonstrated interest in music; Commitment to weekly lessons and ensemble participation; Students must attend a 30-minute class each week; Participation in at least one band or ensemble is required; Prior musical experience beneficial but not required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Weekly 30-minute lessons are scheduled on a rotated timetable. Participation in at least one ensemble or band is expected. Mandatory for all GAT students Years 7-9. Tuition is for a full academic year; withdrawal during Semester 1 allows refund of Semester 2 charges only; withdrawal during Semester 2 incurs no refund. Students learn maintaining positive participation in college's musical culture.

school_name: Berry Street School - Narre Warren Campus

scholarship_url: Not publicly listed - contact school directly
program_name: Financial Assistance and Bursary Support Program
overview: Berengarra School provides financial assistance and bursary support to families to enable education for their students. The school is committed to supporting students facing financial and situational challenges through grants and bursaries.
description: Berengarra School offers financial assistance through grants and bursaries to support families who may face financial barriers to education. As a non-profit specialist independent secondary school, the school's mission explicitly includes supporting families through grants and bursaries to enable education for their students. The school recognises that some students have intersecting identities or additional needs that require financial support. Eligibility and specific financial values are determined on a case-by-case basis through direct contact with the school. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to discuss payment plans and bursary options with the school's administration.
eligibility_criteria: Students from families facing financial and situational challenges; students with intersecting identities or additional needs; families demonstrating financial hardship; enrolled or prospective students
special_conditions: Applications and enquiries should be made directly to the school. Bursaries are reviewed on individual circumstances. Final approval is at the discretion of the school leadership team.

overview: Berwick College Dance Academy (BCDA) is a full-time academic and dance program that provides an integrated learning pathway for students passionate about dance and performing arts. The program enables young dancers to develop their talents while completing their secondary education, preparing them for VCE Dance, VET Dance and professional careers in dance.
description: The Berwick College Dance Academy (BCDA) is a selective full-time academic and dance program available from Year 7 to Year 12. Students participate in a comprehensive dance curriculum covering multiple styles including Classical Ballet, Contemporary, Lyrical, Jazz, Tap, Musical Theatre and Hip-hop, while also studying core subjects including Mathematics, English, Humanities, Physical Education, Science and LOTE. The program has no direct financial value as it is a government school program, though there is a course fee for specialised dance instruction and materials. Selection is through audition process managed by the Dance Academy coordinator. Successful applicants are required to sign a Dance Academy Behaviour Contract and must maintain satisfactory academic performance in all core subjects or face warnings and potential removal from the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry; Audition required; Currently enrolled in primary school (Year 6) or transferring to secondary; Maximum 25 students per year level; No prior formal dance training required but dance experience preferred; Academic achievement at satisfactory level required; Behaviour contract signed; Attendance at all rehearsals, competitions and assessments mandatory
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful applicants must sign a Dance Academy Behaviour Contract outlining commitment to the academy. If academic achievement in all core subjects is not at satisfactory level, students will be placed on a warning and may be asked to exit the BCDA. Attendance at all rehearsals, competitions and assessments is mandatory with prior communication required if unable to attend. Students required to travel to weekend dance competitions in May/June and potentially interstate competitions. Compulsory entry into annual Berwick College Choreographic Competition. Period Zero classes (8am-8:50am) run for some academy classes. Dancing outside school is encouraged but not required. Study tour to USA available for Year 9 and above (approximately $5,000 per student, bi-annual). Full school uniform required at all times; BCDA top provided by academy.

overview: Berwick College Basketball Academy is an elite program providing an integrated academic pathway for student athletes passionate about basketball. The Academy allows young athletes to develop their sporting talent while receiving secondary education, with strong emphasis on academic excellence alongside athletic performance.
description: The Berwick College Basketball Academy is a selective elite sports program for Years 7 to 10 allowing student athletes to enhance and develop their basketball skills while completing their secondary education. The program provides specialised coaching and training as part of the curriculum with no direct financial scholarship value as it is a government school program. Selection is through application and evaluation of basketball ability and commitment. The Academy emphasises that academic pursuits remain the highest priority, with students' sporting aspirations supporting high academic results both athletically and academically. The program has operated since 2012/2013 and is recognised as the strongest school-based basketball academy in Australia. Students participate in multiple tournaments including Victorian College Championships, School Sports Victoria, Champions Cup, and Australian Schools Championships (Nationals). Year 10 and 11 students have opportunity to travel to USA bi-annually for matches against other high schools.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 7 to 10; Entry by application and basketball skills assessment; Currently enrolled in primary school (Year 6) or already at secondary; Must demonstrate outstanding basketball ability and commitment; Academic performance monitored; Attendance at all training sessions and competitions mandatory; Behaviour expectations consistent with school discipline policy
special_conditions: Academic pursuits are highest priority; constant monitoring of academic performance. USA tour opportunity for Year 10 and 11 students bi-annually. Students participate in various tournaments throughout the year. You will only be considered for Basketball Academy once you are accepted into Berwick College. Information sessions held at Grade 6 Information Evening.

overview: Berwick College High Achievers' Program (HAP) is an enrichment program designed to foster 21st Century learners through extension and acceleration. The program is highly sought after and enables students to expand their thinking in creative and innovative ways, ensuring confidence, engagement and academic extension throughout their secondary education.
description: The High Achievers' Program (HAP) at Berwick College is a selective enrichment and acceleration program for gifted students operating from Year 7 onwards. The program provides challenging curriculum and extension learning opportunities allowing students to progress with their peers while receiving differentiated instruction tailored to their abilities. No direct financial scholarship is offered as this is a government school program. Selection is through application and testing process to identify academically gifted students. The program emphasises development of independent learning, creative thinking, and 21st Century skills. Students benefit from extension learning opportunities, challenging curriculum design, and opportunities to work independently on projects and develop innovative solutions. The program creates an environment where students can test their ability to work and learn independently while thinking creatively.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Application and selection testing required; Demonstrated academic ability and potential for extension; Interest in creative and independent learning; Ability to work on challenging projects; Academic excellence expected
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students progress through Berwick College with their peers while receiving extension learning. Information sessions held at Grade 6 Information Evening. You will only be considered for HAP once you are accepted into Berwick College. Selection testing is administered by the school to identify gifted learners.

school_name: Bindjiroo Yaluk Community School-The Island Campus
Bright P-12 College Dinner Plain Annexe Secondary
Bright P-12 College-Dinner Plain Annexe Primary
Brighton Primary School-Brighton Primary School Deaf Facility

overview: Brighton Secondary College's Music Department offers instrumental music scholarships to reward students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to instrumental study. The scholarships provide financial support for group music lessons and incentivise continued involvement in the school's music community.
description: The Music Department offers four $700 scholarships for group music lessons. These scholarships provide an incentive to music students to further their instrumental studies and continue their involvement in the music community at Brighton Secondary College. Scholarships are available on a variety of instruments including brass (French Horn, Trombone, Trumpet), woodwind (Saxophone, Clarinet, Flute), strings (Violin, Viola, Cello, Double Bass), and contemporary instruments (Voice, Drums/Percussion, Guitar, Bass Guitar). The selection process involves an initial written application, shortlisting, and audition in mid-December where applicants must perform one piece that best demonstrates their ability. The piece can be performed solo or with accompaniment (live or backing track). The scholarship value is $700 per recipient for group music lessons.
eligibility_criteria: Current students enrolled at Brighton Secondary College; Demonstrated instrumental music ability; Available on specified instruments: Brass (French Horn, Trombone, Trumpet), Woodwind (Saxophone, Clarinet, Flute), Strings (Violin, Viola, Cello, Double Bass), Contemporary (Voice, Drums/Percussion, Guitar, Bass Guitar)
special_conditions: Applications are assessed and shortlisted for an audition process held mid-December. Scholars are expected to continue their involvement in the music community and group music lesson participation.

school_name: Broadmeadows Special Developmental School-Sorrento Street Campus
Broadmeadows Valley Primary School Meadowfair North Campus
overview: Broadmeadows Valley Primary School participates in The Smith Family's Learning for Life program, which provides financial support to eligible families. The program offers scholarship money to help with schooling costs, along with dedicated family partnership support and educational programs for the whole family.
description: The Learning for Life program provides eligible families with financial assistance in the form of scholarship money to help with the costs of schooling. Support includes access to a dedicated Family Partnership Coordinator who provides guidance and assistance. The program also offers access to educational programs designed for the whole family to participate in. Families must meet strict eligibility criteria and commit to regular school attendance, yearly updates for each child, providing school reports annually, and maintaining contact with The Smith Family. The financial benefit varies based on individual family circumstances and specific support needs.
eligibility_criteria: Must have a Health Care or Pension Concession Card; Must have a child enrolled in Broadmeadows Valley Primary School; Must commit to supporting child's regular school attendance; Must complete a yearly update for each child; Must provide a school report yearly for each child; Must maintain contact with The Smith Family
special_conditions: This is a partnership program between Broadmeadows Valley Primary School and The Smith Family. As a parent, guardian, or caregiver, you need to commit to regular school attendance, completing a yearly update for each child, providing a school report yearly for each child, and maintaining contact with The Smith Family. Interested families must complete the form provided by the school to express interest in the program.

overview: Buckley Park College offers the STEAM Academy program to Year 7 students who are passionate about Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics. The program provides an accelerated learning environment where students solve real-world problems through innovation, curiosity, collaboration and community engagement.
description: The STEAM Academy is open to Year 7 students who demonstrate a strong passion for Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics and a desire to solve real world, everyday problems. Students must possess a love of learning through experimentation, resilience in learning and not be afraid of failures. Acceptance into the STEAM Academy is highly competitive and students are expected to engage in the program throughout the academic year. Students must uphold Buckley Park College's core values of Aspiration, Kindness, Resilience and Community, demonstrate strong commitment to active participation in all STEAM Academy projects, and actively participate in all incursions and excursions as required. A minimum of one year enrolment is required. Students are assessed against the Victorian Curriculum 2.0 achievement standards in Science, Digital Technology, English, Art, Humanities and Mathematics. No monetary value or financial remission is provided with this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 and accepted into Buckley Park College through Year 6 to 7 Enrolment process; Must complete Expression of Interest form after acceptance into college; Passion for STEAM disciplines required; Commitment to active participation in all projects and excursions mandatory; Must uphold school values of Aspiration, Kindness, Resilience and Community
special_conditions: Acceptance into the STEAM Academy is highly competitive. Students are expected to engage in the program throughout the academic year. Students must demonstrate a strong commitment to active participation in all STEAM Academy projects undertaken in the program. Students must actively participate in all incursions and excursions as required for the projects. Students in the STEAM Academy who choose to exit the program will be placed into a mainstream class within the College. Minimum of one year required.

overview: The Benjamin Inverno Memorial Scholarship is awarded to Buckley Park College students in Years 10-12 who exhibit character strengths and values including courage, integrity, gratitude, learning agility, passion, humility, composure, enthusiasm, teamwork, foresight and empathy. This scholarship honours the memory of Benjamin Inverno and provides financial support to students demonstrating exceptional character and values.
description: The Benjamin Inverno Memorial Scholarship is awarded annually to a Buckley Park College student in Years 10, 11 or 12 who demonstrates the values and character strengths exemplified by Benjamin Inverno. These values include courage, integrity, gratitude, learning agility, passion, humility, composure, enthusiasm, team-player mindset, foresight and empathy. Students may self-nominate, be nominated by another student, or be nominated by a staff member. The scholarship provides $1,000 as a disbursement towards the cost of the recipient's education. The application must be completed by the nominee and submitted to the Assistant Principal of the Later Years. The recipient is announced at the BPC Awards Ceremony in December. This scholarship was established by Simone Andrewartha in memory of her son Benjamin, who attended Buckley Park College and was DUX in Physics and Further Mathematics in 2016. The initiative aims to remove stigma around mental health issues and support families in educating young people.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 10, 11 or 12; Must be currently enrolled at Buckley Park College; Must exhibit character strengths and values including courage, integrity, gratitude, learning agility, passion, humility, composure, enthusiasm, team-player qualities, foresight and empathy; May self-nominate or be nominated by students or staff members
special_conditions: Recipient is announced at the BPC Awards Ceremony on Tuesday 16th December at the Clocktower, Moonee Ponds. Application deadline is Friday 31 October. Nomination can be submitted by the student, another student, or a staff member. Contact Assistant Principal James Stevens at st@buckleyparkco.vic.edu.au for questions or enquiries.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the Music Scholarship to provide an extension opportunity for students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and commitment to music performance. The scholarship recognises students selected for the specialised music program in grades 4-6.
description: The Music Scholarship is one of four specialist scholarship programs offered by Buninyong Primary School for students in grades 4-6. This program provides access to specialised music instruction and performance opportunities. Selected scholarship recipients receive access to the school's comprehensive music program, including instruction in various instruments such as guitar, keyboard, drums and trumpet, performance opportunities at school assemblies and events, and participation in the Junior and Senior Choir. The program aims to develop students' understanding and appreciation of music through singing, movement and performing. Students are encouraged to work cooperatively and incorporate the school values of optimism, commitment, respect, compassion and honesty in all music activities. Selection involves identification of students with demonstrated musical talent and interest. The scholarship is available for the duration of the student's enrolment in the eligible year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in grades 4, 5, or 6; Students must demonstrate musical ability and interest in music performance; Selection based on demonstrated talent and school assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship provides access to extension program and specialist music instruction. No monetary remission stated. Students must remain actively engaged in the program for duration of enrolment.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the Visual Arts Scholarship to recognise students with exceptional artistic ability and creative talent. The scholarship provides extension opportunities for selected students demonstrating excellence in visual arts in grades 4-6.
description: The Visual Arts Scholarship is one of four specialist scholarship programs at Buninyong Primary School for grades 4-6. This program provides access to a stimulating visual arts program that encourages creative and imaginative ways of thinking. Selected scholarship recipients participate in specialised art classes led by specialist art teacher Michelle Noyce, experiencing a range of visual arts activities including painting, printmaking, collage, textiles, sculpture and ceramics. Students have the opportunity to express their knowledge, ideas and feelings in an individual and expressive way. The program develops strong aesthetic appreciation and skills in a range of media. Recipients also participate in the Grade 6 Art Team, designing and completing artworks displayed around the school. Selection is based on demonstrated visual arts ability and creative potential. The scholarship is available for the duration of the student's enrolment in the eligible year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in grades 4, 5, or 6; Students must demonstrate visual arts ability and creative talent; Selection based on demonstrated artistic potential and school assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship provides access to extension program and specialist visual arts instruction. No monetary remission stated. Recipients selected for Grade 6 Art Team. Students must remain actively engaged in the program for duration of enrolment.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the Photography Scholarship to students demonstrating interest and talent in photography. The scholarship provides access to specialised photography instruction and opportunities to develop photographic skills in grades 4-6.
description: The Photography Scholarship is one of four specialist scholarship programs offered by Buninyong Primary School for students in grades 4-6. This program provides access to specialised photography instruction and develops students' technical and creative skills in photography. Selected scholarship recipients take photography lessons throughout the year and are given responsibility for taking photographs at school events and activities. This practical experience allows students to develop their photographic eye and understanding of visual composition and storytelling through images. The program integrates photography skills with creative and technical learning. Recipients also contribute to the school's visual documentation through their event photography work. Selection is based on demonstrated interest in photography and potential for developing photographic skills. The scholarship is available for the duration of the student's enrolment in the eligible year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in grades 4, 5, or 6; Students must demonstrate interest in photography; Selection based on demonstrated potential and school assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship provides access to specialised photography instruction. No monetary remission stated. Recipients given responsibility for event photography at school. Students must remain actively engaged in the program for duration of enrolment.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the Master Chef Scholarship to students demonstrating exceptional interest and ability in culinary arts and cooking. The scholarship provides access to specialised cooking instruction and kitchen-based learning experiences in grades 4-6.
description: The Master Chef Scholarship is one of four specialist scholarship programs at Buninyong Primary School for students in grades 4-6. This program provides access to specialised cooking and culinary instruction, developing students' skills in food preparation, nutrition understanding, and culinary creativity. Selected scholarship recipients participate in hands-on cooking and food preparation activities, learning fundamental culinary techniques and food science concepts. The program emphasises practical skills, creativity in food preparation, and understanding of ingredients and cooking methods. Students develop knowledge of nutrition, food safety, and meal planning. The Master Chef program also integrates with the school's environmental focus, particularly at the Scotsburn Campus where students use produce from the vegetable garden in cooking activities. Selection is based on demonstrated interest in cooking and culinary arts. The scholarship is available for the duration of the student's enrolment in the eligible year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in grades 4, 5, or 6; Students must demonstrate interest in cooking and culinary arts; Selection based on demonstrated interest and school assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship provides access to specialised cooking instruction and hands-on culinary experience. No monetary remission stated. Scotsburn Campus students benefit from connection to school vegetable garden. Students must remain actively engaged in the program for duration of enrolment.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the High Achievers Program to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and learning excellence. The program provides extension learning opportunities for identified high-achieving students in grades 5-6.
description: The High Achievers Program is an enrichment and extension program at Buninyong Primary School for students in grades 5 and 6 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and learning potential. This program provides opportunity for high-achieving students to extend their learning beyond the regular curriculum, accessing challenge and extension activities across curriculum areas. The program recognises students who consistently demonstrate above-standard achievement in literacy and numeracy, as well as strong conceptual understanding and critical thinking skills. Through the High Achievers Program, students engage with advanced learning content, enriched problem-solving tasks, and extended independent research opportunities. The program supports the school's commitment to personalised learning and catering to individual student needs and abilities. Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance and learning achievements throughout grades 5 and 6.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in grades 5 and 6; Must demonstrate exceptional academic achievement; Identified based on school assessment of learning outcomes
special_conditions: Program provides extension learning opportunities for identified high-achieving students. No financial remission stated. Selection based on demonstrated academic achievement and learning outcomes.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers a Music Scholarship Program for students in grades 4-6, providing an extension opportunity for selected students with demonstrated musical ability and interest.
description: The Music Scholarship Program is designed for students in grades 4, 5, and 6 who demonstrate exceptional interest and ability in music. This program provides an amazing extension opportunity for selected students. Students gain access to specialized music instruction and performance opportunities. Specific financial benefits, duration, and selection process details should be obtained by contacting the school directly, as comprehensive program details are not published on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 4, 5, or 6; Demonstrated interest or ability in music; Selection based on school assessment
special_conditions: Program details and specific conditions should be confirmed with school. Selection is competitive and based on demonstrated musical interest or ability.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers a Visual Arts Scholarship Program for students in grades 4-6, providing an extension opportunity for selected students with demonstrated ability in visual arts.
description: The Visual Arts Scholarship Program is available for students in grades 4, 5, and 6 who demonstrate exceptional ability and interest in visual arts. This program provides an amazing extension opportunity for selected students to develop their artistic skills and explore diverse art forms. Students receive specialized instruction in various art media and techniques. Financial benefits, program duration, and detailed selection criteria are not published online and should be obtained directly from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 4, 5, or 6; Demonstrated interest or ability in visual arts; Selection based on school assessment
special_conditions: Program details and conditions should be confirmed with school. Selection is competitive based on demonstrated artistic ability.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers a Photography Scholarship Program for students in grades 4-6, providing specialized instruction and extension opportunities for selected students interested in photography.
description: The Photography Scholarship Program is designed for students in grades 4, 5, and 6 who demonstrate interest in photography and visual documentation. Selected students participate in photography lessons and are responsible for taking photos at school events, providing both skill development and meaningful involvement in school activities. The program offers hands-on experience in photography techniques and creative expression. Specific program value, duration, and formal selection criteria are not detailed on the school website and should be confirmed by contacting the school directly.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 4, 5, or 6; Demonstrated interest in photography; Selection based on school assessment
special_conditions: Selected students take photography lessons and are responsible for photographing school events. Program details should be confirmed with school office.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers a Master Chef Scholarship Program for students in grades 4-6, providing culinary skills development and extension opportunities for selected students.
description: The Master Chef Scholarship Program is available for students in grades 4, 5, and 6 who demonstrate interest in cooking and food preparation. This program provides an amazing extension opportunity for selected students to develop culinary skills and food knowledge. Participants gain practical experience in food preparation and cooking techniques through specialized instruction. The school also offers Friday rotation cooking sessions as part of their broader curriculum. Specific program value, duration, and detailed selection procedures are not published on the website and should be obtained by contacting the school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 4, 5, or 6; Demonstrated interest in cooking and culinary arts; Selection based on school assessment
special_conditions: Program details should be confirmed with school. School also offers cooking as part of Friday rotation programs for broader student participation.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the Ready Set Grow Program for grade 3 students interested in gardening, providing hands-on experience in horticulture and environmental education.
description: The Ready Set Grow Program is designed for grade 3 students who demonstrate interest in gardening and horticulture. This program provides students with practical gardening experience and environmental knowledge through hands-on learning in the school's gardens. Participants develop skills in planting, growing, and caring for plants while learning about sustainability and nature. The school has invested in garden infrastructure including a Japanese garden project on the Buninyong campus. This is an extension opportunity that fosters environmental stewardship and hands-on learning. No financial support or scholarships are associated with this program.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 3; Demonstrated interest in gardening and horticulture; Open to all students at the school
special_conditions: This is an open program for interested Grade 3 students. Informal expression of interest process. School features gardening as part of Friday rotation activities for broader participation.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers the High Achievers Program for students in grades 5 and 6, providing extension opportunities for students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement.
description: The High Achievers Program is designed for students in grades 5 and 6 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and capability. This program provides extension opportunities for high-achieving students to develop their skills further and engage in enriched learning experiences. The program recognizes and supports academic excellence across the curriculum. Specific program details including selection criteria, application procedures, financial benefits if any, and program components are not detailed on the school website and should be confirmed by contacting the school directly.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 5 or 6; Demonstrated outstanding academic achievement; Selection based on school assessment
special_conditions: Program details and selection criteria should be confirmed with school. This is an extension program for academically high-achieving students.

overview: Buninyong Primary School offers Grade 6 Leadership Teams that provide students with genuine opportunities to shape and contribute to school leadership, decision-making, and community engagement.
description: The Grade 6 Leadership Program provides students in their final year of primary school with meaningful opportunities to develop leadership skills and contribute to school decision-making and governance. Grade 6 leadership teams give students genuine opportunities to shape school culture, lead initiatives, and contribute to positive school outcomes. The program includes development of communication, collaboration, and strategic thinking skills. Students typically participate in school assemblies, run school announcements, attend leadership conferences such as the GRIP Leadership Conference at the Melbourne Convention Centre, and develop projects that benefit the school community. No specific financial value is associated with this program, though some activities may involve costs.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Demonstrated leadership potential; Selection by school staff
special_conditions: Grade 6 leadership teams participate in school announcements, leadership conferences, and decision-making. Large group of Grade 6 students attend GRIP Leadership Conference at Melbourne Convention Centre each year.

overview: Bundoora Primary School awards Scholarships of Excellence Awards to students who demonstrate exemplary effort and excellence in relation to school values. These awards recognise students across the school who exemplify the values of respect, excellence, resilience, cooperation and friendship.
description: The Scholarships of Excellence Awards recognise students who demonstrate exemplary effort and excellence in relation to one or more of the school's core values: respect, excellence, resilience, cooperation and friendship. These awards are presented to students from all year levels at Bundoora Primary School who consistently display these valued qualities. Awards are presented at school assemblies and special ceremonies throughout the year. The program recognises all-rounder excellence across academic, personal and social domains.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolled at Bundoora Primary School; Must demonstrate exemplary effort and excellence in one or more school values: respect, excellence, resilience, cooperation and friendship
