special_conditions: These are recognition awards presented throughout the year at school assemblies and special ceremonies. Award recipients are selected by teaching staff based on demonstrated exemplary effort and alignment with school values.

school_name: Cairnlea Park Primary School-Western Special Assistance Unit - Language

overview: Camberwell Grammar School offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in academically gifted students entering Year 7 and Year 9. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, with selection based on competitive examination performance.
description: The Academic Scholarship at Camberwell Grammar School recognises exceptional academic achievement in incoming students. Applicants undergo a comprehensive assessment process including a written scholarship examination, interview with school leadership, review of school reports and NAPLAN results. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction for the duration of the student's enrolment, conditional upon satisfactory academic progress and conduct maintained year to year. Recipients are expected to remain at the school until the end of Year 12; premature withdrawal may require repayment of scholarship value already received. The selection process is highly competitive, with the number and percentage of fee remission varying according to the calibre of candidates.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 9; Demonstrate exceptional academic performance; Australian or New Zealand citizen or permanent resident; Age and year level appropriate; Current school reports and NAPLAN results required
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional upon satisfactory academic progress and conduct from year to year. Recipient must remain at Camberwell Grammar School until end of Year 12 except in unusual circumstances. Should a student leave the school prematurely, the value of scholarship already received would normally have to be repaid to the school.

overview: Camberwell Grammar School offers the Music Scholarship to talented young musicians entering Years 7 to 10. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated musical excellence and commitment to furthering their musical journey within the school's vibrant music program.
description: The Music Scholarship at Camberwell Grammar School is designed to support talented musicians in Years 7 to 10. The scholarship provides tuition free of fees on one instrument to be learnt at the school, along with a concession on tuition fees. Selection is based on a recorded music audition submission, written scholarship examination results, participation in assessment activities, and interview. Applicants must demonstrate proficiency at Grade 7 or Grade 8 AMEB level or equivalent in their primary instrument. Music Scholars are expected to be active participants in the school's co-curricular Music Academy programs, which include masterclasses and performance opportunities. The scholarship is tenable for the duration of enrolment subject to maintained academic and musical standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Proficiency at Grade 7 or Grade 8 AMEB level or equivalent; Instruments considered include Strings, Woodwind, Brass, Piano or Percussion; Demonstrated commitment to musical excellence; Must be prepared to participate actively in Music Academy programs
special_conditions: Music Scholars are expected to be active participants in co-curricular Music programs through the Music Academy. Scholarship holders must maintain high standards of academic achievement, participate actively in the life of the school, and be a positive member of the school community. Performance is reviewed annually. Applicants must provide recorded audition submission with pieces from specified ABRSM or equivalent grade lists.

overview: Camberwell Grammar School offers the General Excellence Scholarship to students entering Years 7 to 10 who demonstrate excellence across multiple co-curricular and community activities. The scholarship recognises all-round achievement in academic, sporting, cultural and leadership pursuits.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional achievement and commitment beyond academics, encompassing sporting excellence, cultural participation, leadership, and community service. Applicants are assessed on demonstrated excellence in at least three of the following areas: academic achievement, sporting achievement (at state/national level), cultural activities including music, drama or visual arts, leadership and community service. Selection involves a competitive examination, interview assessment, and evaluation of a comprehensive portfolio demonstrating excellence across multiple disciplines. The scholarship provides partial fee remission for the duration of enrolment, with the value determined at the school's discretion based on calibre of candidates. Information for 2027 entry was to be made available shortly at the time of this research.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Open to new students not currently attending an independent school; Demonstrate excellence in at least three of: academics, sport, arts/culture, leadership, community service; Financial disadvantage may be considered; Age and year level appropriate
special_conditions: Information for 2027 entry was to be made available shortly. Applicants must demonstrate excellence across multiple disciplines. Selection based on school's discretion and calibre of candidates.

overview: Camberwell Grammar School offers the Indigenous Scholarship to enable students from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander backgrounds to enter the school at Years 7 to 10. The scholarship is part of the school's commitment to reconciliation and supporting diverse students in the community.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship Program at Camberwell Grammar School seeks to enable students from an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander background to access education at the school, entering in Years 7 to 10. These are non-boarding scholarships designed to support Indigenous students' full participation in all aspects of school life. The school works closely with Wurundjeri elders, Worawa Aboriginal College and other Indigenous partners to support students' cultural connection and success. Selection is based on demonstrated connection to cultural heritage, alignment with school values, and commitment to community. Applicants must provide evidence of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, recent school reports, NAPLAN results, and identification documents. The school has enrolled Indigenous students annually since 2011, with these students enriching the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Current students from independent schools not eligible; Must provide evidence of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage (birth certificate or passport); Recent school report and NAPLAN required; Non-boarding scholarship only
special_conditions: Non-boarding scholarship. Applicants must provide documentation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. The school is committed to supporting cultural connection through partnerships with Wurundjeri elders and Indigenous organisations. Selection based on demonstrated cultural heritage connection and alignment with school values of integrity, courage, learning, respect and optimism.

overview: Camberwell Grammar School offers the David de Kretser Scholarship to enable students who would otherwise be unable to access a Camberwell Grammar School education due to financial circumstances. The scholarship is part of the school's commitment to providing equity and access to students of merit.
description: The David de Kretser Scholarship is a recently launched initiative at Camberwell Grammar School designed to support students experiencing financial hardship who have demonstrated academic or co-curricular merit. Named in honour of David de Kretser AC, the former Governor of Victoria and distinguished Camberwell Grammar alumnus, the scholarship provides financial support to enable talented students to access the school's education. The scholarship is funded through community donations and is allocated at the school's discretion to students entering Years 7 to 10. Applicants are assessed on demonstrated ability and potential, with consideration of financial need and alignment with school values. The value and specific terms of the scholarship are determined based on individual circumstances and funding availability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Demonstrated academic or co-curricular merit; Evidence of financial hardship or disadvantage; Australian or New Zealand citizen or permanent resident; Alignment with school values; Open to new and current students
special_conditions: Scholarship value and allocation determined at school's discretion based on individual circumstances and funding availability. Named in honour of David de Kretser AC, former Governor of Victoria and Camberwell Grammar alumnus. Funded through community donations to the CGS Foundation Scholarship Fund.

overview: Camberwell High School offers the Learning Enrichment and Extension Program (LEEP) to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to advanced learning. The program is available for Year 7, Year 8, and Year 9 students selected through a competitive entry process. LEEP students achieve at an exceptionally high level within a structured enrichment environment.
description: The Learning Enrichment and Extension Program (LEEP) is a select entry program designed for academically gifted students at Camberwell High School. The program is available to current students in Years 7, 8, and 9 who have demonstrated exceptional academic capability. Students in LEEP participate in enriched curriculum and accelerated learning opportunities that extend beyond the standard curriculum. Selection for the program is competitive and based on demonstrated academic performance. The program allows gifted students to work at their level of ability with peers of similar aptitude, fostering both academic advancement and collaborative learning experiences.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 7, Year 8, or Year 9 student at Camberwell High School; demonstrated exceptional academic ability; selection through competitive entry process; must maintain academic standards to remain in program
special_conditions: Students must be enrolled at Camberwell High School to access the program. There is an additional cost to families for participation in LEEP activities and materials as indicated in the school's Parent Payment Arrangements.

overview: Camberwell High School offers the Launch Program, a select entry STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics) initiative for Year 9 and Year 10 students. The program is designed for students who have demonstrated a passion for mathematics and science and wish to engage in advanced project-based learning with industry and tertiary partners.
description: The Launch Program is a select entry initiative available for Year 9 and Year 10 students who have demonstrated exceptional passion and aptitude in Mathematics and Science. The program is supported by industry and tertiary education partners and provides students with the opportunity to work on mini-thesis projects guided and mentored by specialists. Students engage in authentic, real-world learning experiences that connect academic content with practical applications in STEAM fields. The program emphasizes problem-solving, critical thinking, and collaboration with external partners from higher education institutions and industry sectors. Selection is competitive based on demonstrated interest and achievement in mathematics and science.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 or Year 10 student; demonstrated passion for Mathematics and Science; enrolled at Camberwell High School; selection through competitive entry process; must meet academic performance standards
special_conditions: Student must be enrolled at Camberwell High School. There is an additional cost to families for participation in Launch Program activities as indicated in the school's Parent Payment Arrangements. Students are expected to engage in mentored mini-thesis work and participate in tertiary and industry partnerships.

overview: Camberwell High School acknowledges that some families may experience financial difficulty and offers the Boroondara Chances Scholarship as part of its financial assistance options. This program provides support to eligible families facing financial hardship to ensure student access to school programs and services.
description: The Boroondara Chances Scholarship is a financial assistance program available to Camberwell High School families experiencing financial difficulty. The school recognizes that economic circumstances may impact a student's ability to participate fully in the school program and offers this scholarship as part of its commitment to equity and access. The school also provides additional support services including access to secondhand book sales, secondhand uniform programs, and connections to external support organizations including Camcare Camberwell, Anglicare Victoria, and the Salvation Army. For families requiring financial assistance, confidential discussions can be held with the school's Business Manager to discuss payment arrangements and access to these support programs. The value and terms of assistance are determined based on individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Student enrolled at Camberwell High School; family experiencing financial difficulty; Australian citizen or permanent resident (for government school access); no income threshold specified
special_conditions: Families seeking financial assistance are encouraged to contact the school's Business Manager for confidential discussion about their circumstances and available options. The school offers flexible payment arrangements and access to secondhand resources to reduce costs. Assistance is provided based on individual family needs and circumstances.

school_name: Camberwell Girls Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability, strong performance in examinations and commitment to learning.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to new and current students entering Years 5 and 7 to 11, and is tenable until the end of Year 12. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission at a percentage determined at the Principal's discretion, with the percentage varying according to the calibre of candidates. Selection is based on scholarship examination results (administered by Edutest), participation in the mandatory Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report, and a scholarship interview. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not cover composite fees, private music lessons, specialist camps, exchanges, school uniform, textbooks, resources or IT requirements. Recipients are expected to maintain a high standard of academic achievement, participate in school life, and be positive members of the school community. Performance is reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: New or current CGGS students; Entering Years 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11; Australian, New Zealand citizen, or Australian Permanent Resident; Age and year level appropriate; Photo ID required on examination day; Students may apply for only one scholarship type per year of entry
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. The number of scholarships offered and the percentage of tuition fee remission will vary according to the calibre of candidates. Scholarship holders are expected to complete their education at CGGS and maintain high academic standards. In the event of withdrawal prior to completing their education, the full value of the scholarship up to that time will be repayable to the school. No scholarship holder may hold more than one scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply to a scholarship holder.

school_name: Camberwell Girls Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Music / Academic Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating outstanding musical achievements and strong academic performance. The scholarship recognises students who excel in music while maintaining academic excellence.
description: The Music / Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to new and current students entering Years 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11, and is tenable until the end of Year 12. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission at a percentage determined at the Principal's discretion, plus private music tuition (30-minute lessons over 16 lessons per semester) on the student's primary instrument. Selection is based on a music audition, performance in the scholarship examination, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report, and a scholarship interview. The scholarship applies to tuition fees and music tuition only and does not cover composite fees, specialist camps, exchanges, school uniform, textbooks, resources or IT requirements. Recipients must be active participants in co-curricular music programs.
eligibility_criteria: New or current CGGS students; Entering Years 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11; Australian, New Zealand citizen, or Australian Permanent Resident; Age and year level appropriate; Proficient at required AMEB standard or equivalent for year level; Candidates of Brass, Double Reed and Double Bass instruments may apply regardless of grade level; Voice auditions accepted for students entering Years 9, 10 and 11 only; Photo ID required on examination day; Students may apply for only one scholarship type per year of entry
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. The number of scholarships offered and the percentage of tuition fee remission will vary according to the calibre of candidates. Music auditions are conducted by the CGGS Music Department on either Friday 14 February or Monday 17 February 2026. Applicants who apply for Music/Academic Excellence Scholarship are also considered for the Academic Excellence Scholarship. Scholarship holders must complete their education at CGGS and maintain high standards. In the event of withdrawal prior to completion of education, the full value of the scholarship up to that time will be repayable to the school. No scholarship holder may hold more than one scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply.

school_name: Camberwell Girls Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Principal's Scholarship for Service to students who demonstrate the school's core values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope, and Courage through co-curricular activities and community service. The scholarship recognises outstanding character and service leadership.
description: The Principal's Scholarship for Service is available to new and current students entering Years 7 to 11, and is tenable until the end of Year 12. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission at a percentage determined at the Principal's discretion, with the percentage varying according to the calibre of candidates. Selection is based on the Principal's Scholarship for Service application, scholarship examination results, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report, and a scholarship interview. Applicants must demonstrate sustained and meaningful engagement within co-curricular activities and meaningful character and contributions to others at home or in the broader community. The scholarship applies to tuition fees only and does not cover composite fees, private music lessons, specialist camps, exchanges, school uniform, textbooks, resources or IT requirements.
eligibility_criteria: New or current CGGS students; Entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11; Australian, New Zealand citizen, or Australian Permanent Resident; Age and year level appropriate; Demonstrated commitment to community service and co-curricular activities; Demonstrated alignment with school values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope and Courage; Photo ID required on examination day; Students may apply for only one scholarship type per year of entry
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. The number of scholarships offered and the percentage of tuition fee remission will vary according to the calibre of candidates. Applicants must complete a separate Principal's Scholarship for Service application form online. Applicants who apply for the Principal's Scholarship for Service are also considered for the Academic Excellence Scholarship. Scholarship holders must complete their education at CGGS and maintain high standards. In the event of withdrawal prior to completion of education, the full value of the scholarship up to that time will be repayable to the school. No scholarship holder may hold more than one scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply.

school_name: Camberwell Girls Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Means-Tested Scholarship to support students from families with demonstrated financial need. The scholarship recognises academic potential and provides financial assistance to enable access to a CGGS education.
description: A limited number of Means-Tested Scholarships are available to new and current students entering Years 7 to 11, and are tenable until the end of Year 12. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission at a percentage determined at the Principal's discretion, with the percentage varying according to the calibre of candidates and extent of demonstrated financial need. Selection is based on scholarship examination results, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report, a scholarship interview, and demonstrated financial need. Applicants must submit a Confidential Statement of Financial Circumstances Form with supporting financial documentation including income tax returns and financial statements. The scholarship applies to tuition fees only and does not cover composite fees, private music lessons, specialist camps, exchanges, school uniform, textbooks, resources or IT requirements.
eligibility_criteria: New or current CGGS students; Entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11; Australian, New Zealand citizen, or Australian Permanent Resident; Age and year level appropriate; Demonstrated financial need supported by financial documentation; Photo ID required on examination day; Financial information must accompany the application; Students may apply for only one scholarship type per year of entry
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. The number of scholarships offered and the percentage of tuition fee remission will vary according to the calibre of candidates and demonstrated financial need. Applicants must complete a Confidential Statement of Financial Circumstances Form available from the Admissions Office and return with supporting financial documentation. The school assesses demonstrated financial need as part of the selection process. Scholarship holders must complete their education at CGGS and maintain high standards. In the event of withdrawal prior to completion of education, the full value of the scholarship up to that time will be repayable to the school. No scholarship holder may hold more than one scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply.

school_name: Camberwell Girls Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the First Nations Scholarship to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The scholarship recognises students who identify as being from Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander backgrounds and have demonstrated connection to their cultural heritage.
description: The First Nations Scholarship is open all year round for secondary school students who identify as being from Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander backgrounds. The scholarship is awarded based on a candidate's demonstrated connection to their cultural heritage and strong alignment with the school's values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope, and Courage. Selection considers the applicant's cultural engagement and community involvement. The school works closely with Murrundindi, ngurungaeta of the Wurundjeri people, and partners with Worawa Aboriginal College in Healesville and the Melbourne Indigenous Transition School in Richmond to support Indigenous students. The school is committed to ongoing learning, recognition and participation in Indigenous culture, with Indigenous culture being a major focus of the Year 8 Service Learning program.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary school students; Identifies as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Demonstrated connection to cultural heritage; Alignment with school values; Open all year round

The school works in partnership with Indigenous organisations including Murrundindi, Worawa Aboriginal College and the Melbourne Indigenous Transition School to support Indigenous students. The school is committed to cultural learning and recognition as demonstrated through the Year 8 Service Learning program and participation in initiatives such as the Mungo Youth Project and Living Culture Immersion Program with Indigenous elders.

overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and outstanding achievement. Scholarships are available for entry at Years 5 and 7 to 11, with awards made based on scholarship examination results, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, school reports and NAPLAN performance.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship provides tuition fee remission at the Principal's discretion, with the percentage varying according to the calibre of candidates and generally awarded at around 50% reduction in tuition fees for the duration of the student's time at the school. Scholarships are tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on scholarship examination results, participation in the compulsory Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report and an interview with members of the scholarship panel. Academic scholarships apply to tuition fees and do not cover composite fees, private music lessons, activities such as specialist camps and exchanges, school uniform, textbooks or resources. Scholarship holders are expected to complete their education at CGGS and maintain a high standard of academic achievement, participate in the life of the school and be a positive member of the school community. The performance of each student is reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current Camberwell Girls Grammar School students; Entry at Years 5 and 7 to 11; Must be Australian or New Zealand Citizens, or Permanent Residents in Australia; Temporary Residents are not eligible; Students must be age and year level appropriate; Photo ID required on examination day
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: No Scholarship holder may hold more than one Scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply to a Scholarship holder. In the event of a student's withdrawal prior to completing their education, the full value of the Scholarship up to that time will be repayable to the school.

overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Music and Academic Excellence Scholarship to students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in both music and academic studies. The scholarship recognises exceptional musical talent alongside strong academic performance and is available for entry at Years 5 and 7 to 11.

Scholarships are tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on a music audition, scholarship examination results, a written application addressing both musical and academic excellence, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, the applicant's most recent school report and NAPLAN report. Music and Academic Excellence Scholarships apply to tuition fees and music tuition (private music lessons x 30-minute tuition over 16 lessons per semester) on the student's primary instrument. They do not cover composite fees, activities such as specialist camps and exchanges, school uniform, textbooks or resources. Recipients are expected to be active participants in co-curricular Music programs and maintain high academic and music achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current Camberwell Girls Grammar School students; Entry at Years 5 and 7 to 11; Must be Australian or New Zealand Citizens, or Permanent Residents in Australia; Temporary Residents are not eligible; Students must be age and year level appropriate; Candidates should be proficient in their instrument(s) at the required AMEB or equivalent level for their year; Photo ID required on examination day
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11

A sibling discount does not apply to a Scholarship holder. overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the Principal's Scholarship for Service to recognise students who demonstrate the school's core values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope and Courage through their co-curricular and community activities. The scholarship is available for entry at Years 7 to 11 and rewards students with exceptional character and contributions to others.
description: The Principal's Scholarship for Service provides tuition fee remission at the Principal's discretion, with the percentage varying according to the calibre of candidates and generally awarded at around 50% reduction in tuition fees for the duration of the student's time at the school. Scholarships are tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on scholarship examination results, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, the applicant's most recent school report, demonstrated connection to school values, examples of character and contributions to others at home or in the broader community, and evidence of sustained and meaningful engagement within co-curricular activities. Recipients are expected to complete their education at CGGS, maintain a high standard of academic achievement, participate in the life of the school and be a positive member of the school community. Performance is reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current Camberwell Girls Grammar School students; Entry at Years 7 to 11; Must demonstrate the school's values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope and Courage in co-curricular and community activities; Must be Australian or New Zealand Citizens, or Permanent Residents in Australia; Temporary Residents are not eligible; Students must be age and year level appropriate; Photo ID required on examination day
special_conditions: A separate online application form is required beyond the standard scholarship exam registration. overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers a limited number of Means-Tested Scholarships to eligible families on a limited income who demonstrate financial need. Scholarships are available for entry at Years 7 to 11 and recognise deserving families who would benefit from financial assistance to access a CGGS education.

Scholarships are generally awarded at around 50% reduction in tuition fees for the duration of the student's time at the school and are tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on scholarship examination results, participation in the Scholarship Interactive Session, latest school report, NAPLAN report, a scholarship interview and demonstrated financial need verified through a Confidential Statement of Financial Circumstances. Means-Tested Scholarships apply to tuition fees and do not cover composite fees, private music lessons, activities such as specialist camps and exchanges, school uniform, textbooks or resources. Scholarship holders are expected to complete their education at CGGS and maintain academic achievement.

special_conditions: Confidential Statement of Financial Circumstances form must be completed and returned to the school with supporting financial documentation. Financial information is treated confidentially and only used for the scholarship assessment process. No Scholarship holder may hold more than one Scholarship. A sibling discount does not apply to a Scholarship holder.

overview: Camberwell Girls Grammar School offers the First Nations Scholarship to support secondary school students who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated connection to their cultural heritage and strong alignment with the school's core values. Applications are open all year round.
description: The First Nations Scholarship is available to secondary school students (Year 7 to 11) who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander and wish to join the Camberwell Girls community. The scholarship is awarded based on demonstrated connection to cultural heritage and strong alignment with the school's values of Integrity, Commitment, Respect, Hope and Courage. The school partners with Indigenous organisations including Murrundindi (ngurungaeta of the Wurundjeri people), Worawa Aboriginal College in Healesville and the Melbourne Indigenous Transition School in Richmond to support Indigenous student access. The school values learning about Indigenous culture as a major focus of the Year 8 Service Learning program and has developed partnerships for cultural immersion programs. Applications are accepted on an ongoing basis by contacting the school's Admissions office directly by email.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary school students (Years 7 to 11); Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must demonstrate connection to cultural heritage; Strong alignment with school values required; Open year-round applications; Contact Admissions office for application pack and process details

Applicants must contact the Admissions office at admissions@cggs.vic.edu.au to request the application pack. The school works closely with Indigenous community partners to enable student access. overview: Carey offers Academic Scholarships each year to students in Years 7 and 10. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and ability. Current and prospective students can apply.
description: Carey's Academic Scholarships reward outstanding academic achievement for students entering Year 7 or Year 10. Scholarships usually cover 50% remission of tuition fees only. They do not cover the cost of application and entry fees, books, uniform, digital devices or consolidated charges (which include compulsory camps and excursions). The scholarship is awarded based on performance in a written examination conducted by Edutest, combined with assessment of the latest school report and an interview. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by the relevant School Section Office to arrange an interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Current and prospective students can apply; Must submit latest school report to Admissions Office; Shortlisted candidates must participate in interview
special_conditions: Scholarship testing will take place on Saturday 7 February 2026. Year 7 examination at 9.00am; Year 10 examination at 2.00pm. Late applications incur fee of $150.00 additional. Supplementary exam service incurs $150.00 fee in addition to standard registration fee. Unsuccessful candidates will receive notification once the selection process has been finalised, along with a copy of the test results.

school_name: Carey Baptist Grammar School - Donvale Campus
overview: Carey offers General Excellence Scholarships to reward excellence in sport for students in Years 7, 10 and 11. The scholarship recognises students performing at an exceptional level in an APS sport at representative, State or National level. Current and prospective students can apply.
description: Carey's General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in APS sports including Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cross Country, Football (AFL), Hockey, Netball, Rowing, Soccer, Softball, Swimming, Tennis and Volleyball. Scholarships usually cover 50% remission of tuition fees. They do not cover the cost of application and entry fees, books, uniforms, computers or the consolidated fee (which covers camps and excursions). Selection involves an Expression of Interest form submission, interview and tour of the school with the Head of Sport and Activities, assessment of school reports and NAPLAN results, and an academic test (Edutest) for shortlisted candidates. Applicants must maintain consistent academic performance and high involvement in co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10 or Year 11; Must be performing at exceptional level in an APS sport; Must be competing at representative, State or National level; Current and prospective students can apply; Must demonstrate leadership potential; Must maintain high academic achievement and co-curricular involvement; Must uphold school values and demonstrate good citizenship
special_conditions: Scholarship will be tenable to the completion of Year 12 provided that the applicant's academic achievement, behaviour and involvement in the co-curricular program are met in line with the expectations of the scholarship. Carey will review any scholarship awarded on an annual basis. Should conditions not be maintained, the School reserves the right to withdraw scholarships. Scholarship recipient must be available for Carey fixtures and training commitments wherever possible; exemptions may be made for State and National representative matches and will be assessed on a case-by-case basis. In the event of extended injury or illness, recipient is expected to volunteer in a coaching and/or mentoring capacity in the Carey sports program.

school_name: Carey Baptist Grammar School - Donvale Campus
overview: Carey offers Access and Equity Scholarships to students commencing Year 7 or Year 10 who are experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship. The scholarship is subject to financial assessment and supports students who demonstrate the potential to successfully complete Year 12.
description: Access and Equity Scholarships provide full tuition fee remission from commencement in Year 7 or Year 10 through to and including Year 12. Successful applicants must be experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship as determined by financial assessment. Applicants must also demonstrate enthusiasm about attending school, likelihood to complete Year 12, and have parents or family who have shown support and encouragement for the scholarship application and enrolment. The scholarship represents a life-changing opportunity for students who would otherwise not be able to access a Carey education. Applications close at the end of Term 1 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship; Subject to financial assessment; Must be enthusiastic about attending school; Must demonstrate likelihood to complete Year 12; Must have parental or family support and encouragement for scholarship application
special_conditions: Applications close at the end of Term 1 each year. Scholarship covers tuition fees from commencement in Year 7 or Year 10, up to and including Year 12. Financial assessment is required to determine eligibility. School may refer interested students who could benefit from this scholarship to the Principal.

overview: Carey offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship rewards students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through a rigorous entrance examination.

Scholarships usually cover 50% remission of tuition fees only and do not cover application and entry fees, books, uniform, digital devices or consolidated charges (which include compulsory camps and excursions). The selection process includes an online application, submission of the latest school report, an EduTest examination on Saturday 7 February 2026 (with supplementary sitting on Wednesday 11 February 2026), and an interview with the relevant School Section Office. The scholarship is tenable to the completion of Year 12 provided that the applicant's academic achievement, behaviour and involvement in the co-curricular program are maintained in line with the expectations of the scholarship. Carey will review any scholarship awarded on an annual basis and reserves the right to withdraw scholarships if conditions are not maintained.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be currently in Year 6 or Year 9 respectively; Current and prospective students may apply; Must submit latest school report to Admissions Office; Must sit EduTest entrance examination; Interview with School Section Office required
special_conditions: Scholarships cover 50% remission of tuition fees only. They do not cover the cost of application and entry fees, books, uniform, digital devices or consolidated charges (which include compulsory camps and excursions). overview: Carey offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in sport at the representative, State or National level. The scholarship is awarded to students in Years 7, 10 and 11 who demonstrate exceptional athletic ability and commitment to sporting excellence.
description: Carey's General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7, Year 10 or Year 11 who are performing at an exceptional level in an APS sport and competing at a representative, State or National level. Scholarships usually cover 50% remission of tuition fees and do not cover application and entry fees, books, uniforms, computers or the consolidated fee (which covers camps and excursions). The selection process includes an Expression of Interest form, interview and tour with the Head of Sport and Activities, submission of school reports and NAPLAN results, an academic test by EduTest, and a final interview with the Principal. Eligible sports are Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cross Country, Football (AFL), Hockey, Netball, Rowing, Soccer, Softball, Swimming, Tennis and Volleyball. The scholarship is tenable to the completion of Year 12 provided that the applicant's academic achievement, behaviour and involvement in the co-curricular program are maintained. Carey will review any scholarship awarded on an annual basis and reserves the right to withdraw scholarships if conditions are not maintained.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10 or Year 11; Must be performing at exceptional level in an APS sport; Must be competing at representative, State or National level; Current and prospective students may apply; Must maintain high level of commitment to whole school program; Must complete full commitment to summer and winter sport program
special_conditions: Scholarships usually cover 50% remission of tuition fees. They do not cover the cost of application and entry fees, books, uniforms, computers or the consolidated fee (which covers camps and excursions). The scholarship recipient will act as a role model at all times, including when representing Carey. The scholarship recipient will use talents to the best of ability and will provide support and encouragement to other Carey students. Scholarship recipient will not apply for scholarships at any other school and if they leave Carey on that basis, they must pay Carey back all money provided by the scholarship. Scholarship recipient will complete full commitment to summer and winter sport program, as well as spring season (athletics or water polo). In the event of extended injury or illness, scholarship recipient is expected to volunteer in coaching and/or mentoring capacity. The scholarship recipient is expected to maintain high level of commitment to whole school program including school attendance, being positive influence in classes, completion of work, excellent citizenship, wearing uniform correctly, attendance at school functions, active contribution to co-curricular house and voluntary school activities. The Head of School and Head of Sport will review the scholarship recipient's award on an annual basis and may discontinue the award or vary its provisions with one month's notice if conditions are not maintained.

overview: Carey's Access and Equity Scholarship is offered to students demonstrating financial disadvantage or hardship and supports the school's commitment to social justice. The scholarship enables students with considerable potential but without the financial means to be part of the Carey community.
description: The Access and Equity Scholarship is offered to applicants commencing Year 7 or Year 10 who are experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship, subject to a financial assessment. The successful applicant must be enthusiastic about attending school, likely to complete Year 12, and have parents or family that have demonstrated support and encouragement for the Scholarship application and enrolment. The scholarship covers tuition fees from commencement in Year 7 or Year 10 through to the completion of Year 12. The selection process involves submission of an Expression of Interest form and a financial assessment to determine need and eligibility. Applications close at the end of Term 1 each year (approximately April). This scholarship supports Carey's strong commitment to social justice and contributes to the diversity and inclusiveness of the school's student body. Donations are allocated exclusively to needs-based scholarships and are not used for any other purpose.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students commencing Year 7 or Year 10; Must be experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship; Must be subject to financial assessment; Must be enthusiastic about attending school; Must be likely to complete Year 12; Must have parents or family that have demonstrated support and encouragement for scholarship application and enrolment

Strict confidentiality applies to all applications. All donations received towards scholarships at Carey are allocated exclusively for needs-based scholarships and are not used for any other purpose.

overview: Carey offers Humanitarian Scholarships to students from refugee families across all year levels. The scholarship supports the school's strong commitment to social justice and provides educational opportunities for refugee students.
description: Carey Baptist Grammar School offers Humanitarian Scholarships which include a full remission of tuition fees and are offered across all year levels from ELC to Year 12. The scholarship is available to students from refugee families and supports Carey's strong commitment to social justice. The scholarship contributes to the diversity and inclusiveness of Carey's student body by enabling refugee students to access a Carey education. These scholarships have been instrumental in supporting refugee students to build confidence, form friendships, and take advantage of educational and co-curricular opportunities they would not otherwise have had access to. Strict confidentiality applies to all applications.
eligibility_criteria: Must be from a refugee family; Open to students across all year levels; Selection is by invitation or referral from the Principal
special_conditions: Humanitarian Scholarships include a full remission of tuition fees and are offered across all year levels. Strict confidentiality applies to all applications. Families who could benefit from the support a Carey Humanitarian Scholarship offers should contact the Principal, Mr Jonathan Walter, at principal@carey.com.au.

overview: Carlo Acutis Catholic Primary School offers concessional fee assistance to families experiencing financial difficulties. The program provides fee concessions to eligible families who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education, helping them meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay.
description: Under the MACS Concessional Fee Policy for primary schools, Carlo Acutis Catholic Primary School provides fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. Eligible families include those holding Department of Human Services Health Care Cards, Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Cards, Department of Home Affairs Proof of Identity cards (ImmiCard), or those of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. The level of fee concession varies based on family circumstances and capacity to pay. Families experiencing financial difficulties impacting the ability to pay school fees should contact the Office Manager to make an appointment with the Principal. The school acknowledges the need for families to meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay while maintaining a consistent approach to fee concessions across all MACS primary schools.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Holders of Department of Human Services Health Care Cards; Holders of Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Cards; Holders of Department of Home Affairs Proof of Identity card (ImmiCard); Students of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Open to all year levels Foundation to Year 6
special_conditions: Applications must be made by contacting the Office Manager to arrange an appointment with the Principal. Fee concessions are assessed based on individual family circumstances and capacity to pay. The policy applies to MACS primary schools and is based on principles of Catholic social teaching, including the dignity of the human person and preferential option for the poor.

overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College recognises and rewards outstanding student achievement in academic excellence. The Principal in collaboration with the Leadership Team awards these scholarships annually to celebrate curricular success among all eligible students across Years 7-12.

All students across Years 7-12 are eligible for consideration, and there is no formal application process required. Recipients are acknowledged at whole school assemblies where successes are celebrated together. overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers Music Scholarships to recognise exceptional musical talent and ability. The scholarship provides significant financial support for instrumental and vocal music tuition, enabling talented musicians to pursue their passion at the College.
description: The Year 7 Music Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to music. Applications are submitted through a formal application process and applicants must participate in an audition as part of the selection process. The full music scholarship includes free tuition, books, and instrument hire for a period of up to two years. This comprehensive support covers all costs associated with music study, enabling talented young musicians to develop their skills without financial barriers. The scholarship recognises both instrumental and vocal excellence. Interested applicants should contact the Director of Music for specific audition dates, repertoire requirements, and application deadlines.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants entering Year 7; Demonstrated exceptional musical ability; Must pass competitive audition; Application required; Open to students with instrumental and/or vocal experience; Commitment to music study required
special_conditions: Full music scholarship includes free tuition, books and instrument hire for up to two years. Selection requires competitive audition. Application must be submitted by published deadline. Scholarship subject to satisfactory progress in music studies.

overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers a Fees Assistance Program to support families experiencing financial hardship in meeting school fee obligations. The program provides confidential assessment and support to eligible families.
description: The Fees Assistance Program is designed to support families facing financial difficulties in paying school fees. Families wishing to apply must request and complete an application form from the College's Accounts Office, providing detailed information regarding their financial circumstances. All applications are considered in the strictest confidence by the College Fees Committee. The Committee reviews applications carefully, taking into account the family's attempts, commitment, and communication regarding previous debts when making decisions. Please note that assistance is only available for fees in the current school year, not for balances from previous years. If approved, families must complete a Fee Commitment Agreement, authorise CSEF (Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund) allowance payments to be transferred fully to the College, and agree to remit payments by direct debit authority.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Application to Accounts Office required; Must provide detailed financial information; Applications assessed in confidence by Fees Committee; Assistance available for current school year fees only; Must demonstrate commitment to fee payment; Prior debt history considered
special_conditions: Assistance only available for current school year fees. Recipients must complete Fee Commitment Agreement. CSEF allowance payments must be transferred fully to College. Payments must be made by direct debit authority. Applications considered confidentially by College Fees Committee. Family history of debt communication and commitment considered.

overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College awards the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate personal excellence in both curricular and co-curricular activities. Students demonstrating excellence in curricular and co-curricular activities are considered for this annual award. The Principal and Leadership Team assess candidates based on set criteria outlined in the Scholarship Guidelines document and award scholarships to successful students. All students across Years 7-12 are automatically considered - there is no formal application process. Recognition is provided at whole school assemblies to celebrate the recipients' achievements. For specific information about funding value and detailed renewal conditions, applicants should consult the Scholarship Guidelines document or contact scholarships@cccc.vic.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: All students across Years 7-12; Excellence demonstrated in curricular and/or co-curricular activities; No application process required - automatic consideration
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: No formal application required - recipients are identified by the Principal and Leadership Team. Awards recognise excellence across curricular and co-curricular activities. Specific criteria and funding are detailed in the Scholarship Guidelines document.

school_name: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College - Sacred Heart Campus
overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers a Year 7 Music Scholarship to talented young musicians entering Year 7. The scholarship provides comprehensive musical support including free tuition, books, and instrument hire for eligible students.
description: The Year 7 Music Scholarship is awarded through an application and audition process to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and commitment to music performance. The full music scholarship includes free tuition, books, and instrument hire for a period of up to two years. This scholarship is designed to support talented musicians and enable them to pursue their musical education while enrolled at the College. Students interested in applying for this scholarship should complete the Year 7 Music Scholarship Application form and participate in a musical audition to demonstrate their skill level and potential. For detailed application dates, audition requirements, and further information, applicants should contact the Director of Music at music@cccc.vic.edu.au or call the College on 03 9296 5311.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Demonstrated musical ability; Ability to participate in audition process; Application submission required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Tuition fee reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship includes free tuition, books, and instrument hire for up to two years. Recipients must participate in ensembles and maintain commitment to music program. Application and audition required.

school_name: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College - Sacred Heart Campus
overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers the Encore Classroom Music Program for experienced young musicians in Years 7-8. Students in this specialist program receive free orchestral instrument allocation and expert tuition in small groups.
description: The Encore Classroom Music Program is a specialist music opportunity for students who already play a musical instrument and wish to develop their instrumental skills further. Students selected for this program are allocated orchestral instruments for two years at no extra cost and receive expert small group tuition during their semester of Encore in Years 7 and 8. The program runs for one semester in Year 7 and one semester in Year 8, allowing students to develop proficiency on orchestral instruments. Students enrolled in Encore are expected to participate in the concert band and string ensemble programs throughout the year. This program provides a pathway for talented young musicians to develop their skills within the College's musical community. Interested students should complete an application form available through the Music Program section of the website.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 7 or Year 8; Prior instrumental music experience required; Selection through application process; Expectation to participate in ensemble programs
special_conditions: Runs for one semester in Year 7 and one semester in Year 8. Free orchestral instrument allocation for two years. Mandatory participation in concert band and string ensemble. Expert tuition provided in small groups. Application required.

school_name: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College - Sacred Heart Campus
overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers a Fees Assistance Program to support families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The program provides fee reduction or remission to eligible families to ensure no child is disadvantaged due to financial circumstances.
description: The Fees Assistance Program is a confidential financial support scheme for families requiring assistance with school fees at Caroline Chisholm Catholic College. Families seeking assistance must request and complete an application form from the College's Accounts Office, providing detailed information regarding their financial circumstances. Applications are reviewed in the strictest confidence by the College Fees Committee, which also considers the family's previous attempts, commitment to payment, and communication regarding any outstanding balances. Assistance is only available for fees in the current school year and does not apply to balances from previous years. If approved, families must complete and sign a Fee Commitment Agreement and authorise any Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) allowance payments to be transferred fully to the College. This program demonstrates the College's commitment to providing accessible Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Financial hardship demonstrated; Application form completion required; Genuine financial circumstances; Willingness to sign Fee Commitment Agreement; CSEF authorisation required
special_conditions: Assistance available for current school year fees only, not previous balances. Family must sign Fee Commitment Agreement. CSEF allowance must be transferred to College. Applications considered in strictest confidence. Committee notes family's attempts and commitment regarding previous debts.

school_name: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College - Sacred Heart Campus
overview: Caroline Chisholm Catholic College offers the Aspire Program, an accelerated learning opportunity for gifted and talented students in Years 7-9. The program allows exceptional students to work at an advanced pace and explore content in greater depth.
description: Aspire is an accelerated educational enrichment program designed for Caroline Chisholm Catholic College students in Years 7 to 9 who demonstrate exceptional academic abilities and a strong work ethic. Students accepted into Aspire have the opportunity to work through the core curriculum at a faster pace while exploring aspects of it in greater depth. The program applies to core subjects and some electives, with students potentially working six to twelve months ahead of their year level cohort. Aspire is an invitation-only program, with eligibility typically determined based on results from student testing. Students demonstrating outstanding academic performance in assessments are invited to participate. After Year 10, accelerated learning opportunities continue to be offered, where students can undertake VCE Unit 1 and 2 in Year 10. This program provides academically gifted students with appropriate challenge and extension opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Years 7-9 students; Exceptional academic abilities; Strong work ethic; Invitation-only based on test results; High performance in assessments required
special_conditions: Invitation-only program based on student testing results. Students work 6-12 months ahead in core subjects and electives. After Year 10, students can undertake VCE Unit 1 and 2 in Year 10. Requires strong academic commitment.

program_name: SEE High Achievers Program (Select Entry Excellence)
overview: Carwatha College offers the SEE High Achievers Program to address the needs of high-achieving students who demonstrate academic excellence, strong task commitment, and outstanding creativity. The program recognises students who are willing to take on challenges, take risks, and set more challenging goals for themselves.
description: The SEE High Achievers Program is designed for high-achieving Year 7 students at Carwatha College P-12 who demonstrate academic excellence, high levels of task commitment, and outstanding creativity. Students in the program are provided with enhanced curriculum opportunities and are supported by carefully selected, high-quality teaching staff to ensure their specific needs are met. The program aims to develop deep understanding and critical thinking skills, enhance students' ability to work with more abstract and in-depth course material, and allow students to explore future pathways and career opportunities through goal-setting and study enhancement programs. Students participate in extra-curricular activities and full immersion in college life. Year 7 students in the SEE program demonstrate mathematical skill development at more than double the expected annual growth rate and reading comprehension skills that develop at more than four times the expected annual growth. The program continues through Years 7-9 until students transition into the V3 Program in Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students entering from Year 6; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must show high levels of task commitment and outstanding creativity; Must be willing to take on challenges and risks; Entrance exam required
special_conditions: Students must be prepared to take on challenges offered, be willing to take risks, and set more challenging goals. The program has limited places available. Entrance exam held in April for Year 7 entry. Selection is based on high-achieving student profile demonstrating academic excellence, task commitment, and creativity.

overview: Carwatha College's V3 Program is an innovative three-year VCE pathway that enables students to undertake additional VCE subjects, increasing their chances of academic success and improving ATAR outcomes. The program allows Year 10 students to study VCE Unit 1 and 2 subjects alongside their regular coursework.
description: The V3 Program at Carwatha College P-12 provides an innovative three-year Victorian Certificate of Education pathway beginning in Year 10 with no traditional Year 10 studies. All Year 10 students undertake VCE Unit 1 and 2 subjects, allowing them an extra year to study more VCE subjects and develop deeper subject expertise. Students receive teacher-supported study and mentoring sessions timetabled weekly where they receive one-on-one support to set goals, review performance, and work on areas of need. The program includes special interest electives that allow students to balance the demands of a rigorous VCE program with their chosen interest areas such as fitness, strategic games, and primary leadership. Students have the ability to retake Unit 3 & 4 subjects to improve ATAR results. Supported study sessions are offered three periods per week with senior staff members for students undertaking Unit 3 & 4 subjects. The program has demonstrated success with students achieving strong ATAR outcomes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Students selected from SEE Program typically transition into V3 at Year 10; High academic achievement required; Willingness to undertake rigorous study program required
special_conditions: Students must maintain good academic standing. Weekly mentoring sessions and study support are provided. Students can retake Unit 3 & 4 subjects to improve results. The program is designed to maximize ATAR outcomes through an additional year of VCE study.

overview: Carwatha College offers the Primary Maths Enrichment Program to students in Years 5 and 6 who demonstrate high mathematical aptitude. The program provides opportunities for students to work with senior secondary mathematics teachers, extending their learning through more abstract and in-depth material.
description: The Primary Maths Enrichment Program at Carwatha College P-12 is designed to extend students in Years 5 and 6 who demonstrate high mathematical aptitude. The program provides students the opportunity to work directly with senior secondary mathematics teachers who specialize in advanced mathematics instruction. Students engage with more abstract and in-depth mathematical material beyond their year level, developing deeper conceptual understanding and problem-solving skills. The program recognises and nurtures mathematical talent in younger students, preparing them for academic success in mathematics throughout their secondary education. By working with experienced secondary teachers, students gain exposure to higher-level mathematical thinking and applications.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Must demonstrate high mathematical aptitude; Must be currently enrolled at Carwatha College P-12
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated high mathematical aptitude. Students work with senior secondary mathematics teachers. Program extends learning through abstract and in-depth material.

overview: Carwatha Football College offers an exciting football program with full scholarships available for new students in Years 5-12. The program integrates academic and athletic pathways and includes Certificate III Sport and Recreation qualifications for senior students.
description: The Carwatha Football College Academy Program delivers a comprehensive football program for students from Prep to Year 12 as part of the student curriculum. The program combines classroom academic subjects with football-specific content and includes on-field practice, training matches, games, and sports science activities. Full scholarships are available for new Carwatha College P-12 students with spots limited. The program provides three training sessions per week where students learn the latest football training methods, including skill acquisition, conditioning, game intelligence, and mental preparation. Students in Years 10-12 have the opportunity to be part of the Certificate III Sport and Recreation (Football Model) program. Students undertaking VCE are accredited units 1 & 2 towards their VCE program in the first year and units 3 & 4 in the second year. Students completing VCAL also qualify for Certificate III in Sports & Recreation. The program is led by experienced coaches with professional football backgrounds and emphasizes technical development, team work, and player well-being. The college fosters a positive and disciplined environment supporting excellence both on the field and in the classroom.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Prep to Year 12; External students welcome; Limited places available; Must demonstrate football interest and commitment; Medical condition management plan required if applicable; Asthma management required if applicable
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a limited basis - spots are limited. Full scholarships available for new Carwatha College P-12 students. Any players with medical conditions must provide updated management plan to school nurse prior to commencing. Students with asthma must bring asthma pump to every session in a named bag. International students welcome and offered opportunity to study English while playing football. Students in Years 10-12 can pursue Certificate III Sport & Recreation. Attendance and participation requirements must be met. The program emphasizes excellence on field and in classroom with positive and disciplined environment.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten offers partly or fully subsidised scholarship places to eligible new families who would otherwise be unable to enrol due to financial disadvantage. These scholarships are available year-round for Kindergarten to Class 7 students, helping to foster an inclusive and diverse socioeconomic community.
description: CSSK is offering a number of partly or fully subsidised places to eligible applicants of new families to the school, who would otherwise not have the opportunity to enrol due to financial disadvantage. The scholarship provides a subsidy of up to 100 percent of tuition fees for eligible students for the duration of their enrolment at CSSK. Applications can be made at any time following a successful enrolment process. Assessment is conducted by a panel drawn from the School's Leadership team and Board, with applicants notified within four weeks of application submission. Scholarships are reviewed every 2 years, and in most cases ancillary fees such as camps and excursions remain the responsibility of parents/guardians.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Have successfully completed the enrolment process at CSSK; Provide evidence of financial disadvantage (must be named as cardholder or dependent child on valid government concession card: Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card); Demonstrated commitment to the principles of Steiner education and CSSK (expected from both student and parents/guardians); Open to new families to the school only
year_levels: Kindergarten, Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7
special_conditions: Applications run year-round and can be made at any time following successful enrolment process. All applications are strictly confidential. The scholarship is subject to conditions and reviewed every 2 years. Ancillary fees and charges such as camps and excursions are the responsibility of parents/guardians. Assessment takes into consideration the quantity of applications across different year levels and the ability of the class and teachers to meet the students' educational needs. Scholarships are offered to new families to the school only.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten offers Class 1 Inclusion Scholarships that are not contingent on financial background. These scholarships are designed to support selected students entering Class 1 based on inclusion principles rather than demonstrated financial hardship.
description: Class 1 Inclusion Scholarships are available at CSSK and are not contingent on financial background, making them distinct from the financial disadvantage scholarships. The specific criteria, financial value, and number of awards for these scholarships are not detailed on the publicly available scholarship page. Prospective families interested in this program are directed to contact the Enrolments Officer directly for comprehensive information regarding eligibility requirements, application procedures, and scholarship value. These scholarships represent the school's commitment to fostering inclusive access to Steiner education.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to Class 1; Not contingent on financial background; Contact school for specific eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Further information and specific details must be obtained by contacting the Enrolments Officer. These scholarships are not dependent on demonstrated financial disadvantage and represent a separate program from the financial disadvantage scholarships.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College provides access to the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), an annual government payment available to eligible families. This program supports secondary students with costs related to camps, sports and excursion activities by providing direct financial assistance.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is an annual payment of $250 per secondary student made directly to the college for eligible families. The payment is used towards camps, sports and excursion expenses for the benefit of the eligible student. Eligible families must be holders of a Veterans Affairs Hold Card, or be holders of an eligible Centrelink Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The college processes applications through the CSEF tab on their Finance & Assistance page. Families experiencing financial difficulty with textbooks, uniforms, laptop computers or other school costs are encouraged to contact Julie Westwood, Business Manager, on 9788 9100 or julie.westwood@education.vic.gov.au for confidential discussion about alternative payment arrangements.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible families must hold a Veterans Affairs Hold Card; or be eligible Centrelink Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card holder; Secondary students only
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Payment is made directly to the school for camps, sports and excursion expenses only. Non-government school students who received CSEF in 2024 are also eligible. Families can use CSEF bonus towards school costs including uniforms, textbooks, camps, trips and excursions.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College offers the Scholars Program to identify and support students who are excelling in specific academic areas. The program recognizes individual student talents across different disciplines and fosters their continued excellence through specialized opportunities and mentorship.
description: The Scholars Program offers students who are excelling in specific academic areas the opportunity to work with other high-performing students, engage with higher education and employment pathways (such as visiting universities, CSIRO, Parliament), and participate in academic competitions and events (debating, Westpac Maths Competition, short story competitions). Students gain support from an academic mentor, can access subjects in earlier year levels (including VCE subjects), attend tailored Scholars camps, excursions and incursions, and work with distinguished alumni, academics and community leaders to develop their skills and knowledge in specific areas of interest. The program differs from traditional Select Entry Programs because it recognizes that individual students will excel in different areas. The Scholars Program is open to all students across the college with a range of opportunities offered across all year levels. Non-monetary benefits only.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be excelling in a particular academic area; Open to all students across the college; Range of opportunities offered across all year levels
special_conditions: Participation requires active engagement with other high-performing students and willingness to participate in academic competitions and enrichment activities. Specific selection criteria determined by learning area leadership.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College offers the Aspire Basketball Program, providing students with specialist basketball coaching and competitive opportunities. The program is designed to develop students' basketball skills and provides structured pathways for elite-level participation.
description: The Aspire Basketball Program provides students with high-level specialist basketball coaching and the opportunity to compete in school-based competitions and against other schools. Students undertaking this program can complete the FDBA Referees Course (for students turning 14 in the year of the course). The program runs one morning per week in both Term 1 and Term 4. Students develop technical basketball skills, game strategy, and leadership abilities. The program utilizes specialist staff and community expertise to support student learning, enhancement and engagement. Non-monetary benefits include coaching, competition opportunities, and referee certification.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with interest and ability in basketball; Students must be able to commit to one morning per week in Term 1 and Term 4; Students turning 14 in the year of the course are eligible for FDBA Referees Course component
special_conditions: Applications and Expressions of Interest will be available from mid Term 3. All 2026 Year 7 students will receive an invitation to apply. College PE uniform is required for participation. Program availability may vary by year level.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College offers the Aspire Mountain Biking Program, providing specialist coaching in mountain biking for interested students. The program caters to all abilities and is designed to engage students through structured pathways in the sport.
description: The Aspire Mountain Biking Program is focused on engaging students through the sport of mountain biking. The program caters to all abilities commencing with the junior program which encompasses the basics of safe riding and bike maintenance. In Year 9, 10 and beyond, students tackle a senior program which is offered as a co-curricular elective in Year 9-10 and segways into senior pathways such as VET Sport and Recreation and VCE Outdoor Education. The program commenced for 2026, with students traveling for training sessions at Red Hill trails and plans underway for the college to have its own track.
