Non-monetary benefits include specialist coaching, bike maintenance training, and outdoor education pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with interest in mountain biking; Junior program available to all abilities; Senior program available to Year 9 and 10 students as a co-curricular elective; Pathway into VET Sport and Recreation and VCE Outdoor Education
special_conditions: Applications and Expressions of Interest available from mid Term 3. All 2026 Year 7 students will receive an invitation to apply. College PE uniform is required for participation. Program offers structured ongoing pathway from junior to senior levels.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College offers the Aspire Volleyball Program, providing specialist volleyball coaching and competitive opportunities for students. The program supports student development in volleyball with participation in school and inter-school competitions.
description: The Aspire Volleyball Program provides students with specialist volleyball coaching and competitive opportunities. Students have the opportunity to compete in school-based competitions and against other schools at regional and state levels. The college has competed at national levels, with Year 8 and Year 11 boys volleyball teams finishing runners-up at the 2024 National Volleyball Championships in Queensland. The program utilizes specialist staff and community expertise to support student learning, enhancement and engagement. It is run before or after school as an opt-in program. Non-monetary benefits include specialist coaching, competition opportunities, and pathways to elite-level participation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with interest and ability in volleyball; Students can compete at school, regional, state and national levels; Participation requires commitment to training and competition schedule
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications and Expressions of Interest available from mid Term 3. All 2026 Year 7 students will receive an invitation to apply to any of the Aspire programs. Program runs before or after school. Participation at national championships available to high-performing teams.

overview: Carrum Downs Secondary College offers the Aspire Instrumental Music Program to develop students' musical abilities and foster participation in ensembles. The program emphasizes inclusivity, teamwork and performance opportunities for students of all levels.
description: The Aspire Instrumental Music Program provides instruction and performance opportunities for students learning instruments. The college has a range of instruments available for tuition with specialist instrumental teachers covering Brass (Trumpet, Trombone, Tuba, French Horn, Euphonium) and other instruments. There are a range of ensembles that rehearse and perform regularly throughout the year. Students gain support from an academic mentor and participate in multiple performance opportunities including college assemblies, college open night, semester progress music concerts, Royal South Street Bands Competition, college awards night, and visits to local primary schools. Research demonstrates that effective music education lifts student self-confidence and performance in general, with some research indicating accelerated learning in other subjects. Non-monetary benefits include music tuition, ensemble participation, and performance opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students who want to learn an instrument; Focus on inclusivity and teamwork; Students interested in participating in ensembles; Students capable of commitment to regular rehearsals and performances
special_conditions: Applications and Expressions of Interest available from mid Term 3. All 2026 Year 7 students will receive an invitation to apply. Multiple ensemble options available. Performance opportunities throughout the year including external competitions and community events. Contact Greig Douglas (Music Coordinator) for further information.

overview: Catholic Ladies' College offers financial assistance through its Family Financial Assistance Policy to support families experiencing demonstrated financial hardship. Fee assistance and bursaries are available to eligible families to improve access to education at the College.
description: The College recognises that some families may experience financial hardship and offers fee assistance and bursaries as part of its commitment to providing broad access to education. Students receiving bursaries or fee assistance are noted in the school's fee schedule and policy documentation. The exact level of financial support varies based on individual family circumstances and demonstrated need. The College directs families to its Family Financial Assistance Policy for specific details about eligibility criteria, application processes, and the amount of assistance available. This support is designed to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent deserving students from accessing a Catholic Ladies' College education.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship; Families seeking fee assistance or bursary support; Current or prospective students at the College
special_conditions: Students in receipt of Bursaries or fee assistance should not be enrolled in non-compulsory activities that incur additional costs. The College's Family Financial Assistance Policy contains full details of the application process and conditions. Contact the school directly for a copy of the policy and application information.

school_name: Catholic College Sale - Cathedral Hall Campus
overview: Catholic College Sale offers scholarships to Year 7 students entering in 2026. The scholarships balance both merit and means, recognising students' existing talents, achievements, academic performance, and demonstration of strength of character.
description: Catholic College Sale is committed to nurturing the growth of exceptional students who are eager to learn, excel, and contribute to the vibrant school community. Scholarships are available for Year 7 entry in 2026 and typically provide a reduction in tuition fees. Scholarships last for three years from entry. The number and value of scholarships are determined by the applicant's ability to meet or exceed the scholarship criteria, assessment of the means component, and the availability of places. All scholarships balance both merit and means, recognising students' existing talents, achievements, academic performance, and demonstration of strength of character.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Currently in Year 6 or equivalent; Must attend testing morning; Scholarships balance merit and means components
special_conditions: Scholarships last for three years. Testing morning required on Saturday 29 March 2025. The number and value of scholarships are determined by the applicant's ability to meet or exceed the scholarship criteria, assessment of the means component, and the availability of places. Scholarships are currently not offered at any other year level.

overview: Catherine McAuley College offers the Young Carers Scholarship Program to support students who have caring responsibilities. The scholarship provides financial assistance to help students access school or community-based activities and programmes that they might otherwise be unable to participate in.
description: The Young Carers Scholarship Program provides financial support of up to $500 to eligible students. Scholarships are awarded to support students to access school or community-based classes, activities such as camps and excursions, school trips or sporting club participation fees. The program recognises the additional financial pressures that young carers face and aims to ensure their participation in co-curricular and community activities. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated caring responsibilities and financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be a young carer with caring responsibilities; Must demonstrate financial need; Must be enrolled at Catherine McAuley College Bendigo
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate caring responsibilities and provide evidence of financial need. Funding is limited and awarded based on application assessment.

overview: Catholic College Wodonga offers the Bishop Joe Grech Scholarship to recognise outstanding student achievement and leadership. Recipients are selected based on their demonstrated excellence and contributions to the college community.
description: The Bishop Joe Grech Scholarship is awarded to exceptional students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and leadership qualities at Catholic College Wodonga. Recipients are recognised in an official presentation ceremony hosted by Catholic Education Sandhurst. The scholarship acknowledges students who exemplify the values of the college and make significant contributions to the school community. Recipients of this award have demonstrated excellence across their studies and involvement in college life. The exact financial value, selection process details, and duration have not been publicly disclosed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at Catholic College Wodonga; Demonstrated academic excellence; Leadership qualities; Active contribution to college community
special_conditions: Award is presented by Catholic Education Sandhurst. Specific eligibility criteria and application procedures not published on school website.

overview: Catholic College Wodonga presents the Mercy Education Award to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to youth mental health support and community service. This award celebrates students who embody the Mercy values of compassion and justice.
description: The Mercy Education Award recognises students who display outstanding dedication to supporting their peers, particularly in the area of youth mental health. Recipients demonstrate exceptional commitment to community service and witness to the dignity of all people. The award recipient is recognised for planning and leading significant initiatives such as mental health awareness days. The scholarship celebrates students who live out the Mercy tradition through selfless, life-giving relationships with their peers. Specific financial details and formal application procedures are not disclosed on the school's public website.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at Catholic College Wodonga; Demonstrated commitment to youth mental health support; Active community service; Embodiment of Mercy values
special_conditions: Award recognises exceptional work in promoting youth mental health support. Recipients must demonstrate leadership in community service initiatives.

overview: Catholic College Wodonga awards the Fr Les Ring Scholarship to recognise students of outstanding ability and achievement. This scholarship honours the legacy of Father Les Ring and supports exceptional students.
description: The Fr Les Ring Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating outstanding academic ability and excellence at Catholic College Wodonga. This scholarship honours the memory and contributions of Father Les Ring to the college community. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated academic achievement and engagement in college life. The scholarship is presented as part of the college's recognition of student leadership opportunities and development. Financial details regarding the value and specific selection criteria are not publicly detailed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at Catholic College Wodonga; Outstanding academic ability; Demonstrated excellence in studies; Active engagement in college community
special_conditions: Scholarship honours the legacy of Father Les Ring. Award presented as part of college recognition programs.

program_name: Financial Assistance for Families in Financial Hardship
overview: Catholic College Wodonga provides financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The college encourages families to communicate early with the finance team to discuss fee payment concerns and available support options.
description: Catholic College Wodonga offers financial assistance to support families who anticipate difficulty paying tuition fees. The college recognises that some families face financial challenges and provides confidential support through its finance team. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the Business Manager and Finance Team immediately to discuss their circumstances and explore available options. All discussions are strictly private and confidential, with information retained only by the Finance Team and Principal. The college's approach prioritises early communication to help families gain clarity around fee payments and address concerns. This is not a formal scholarship but rather an individualised support program for families in need.
eligibility_criteria: Families with genuine financial hardship; Ability to demonstrate financial need; Willingness to communicate early with finance team; Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment
special_conditions: All discussions strictly confidential. Information retained only with Finance Team and Principal. Early communication strongly encouraged. Families must demonstrate genuine financial hardship to be considered for assistance.

program_name: Catholic College Sale Year 7 Merit-Based Scholarship
overview: Catholic College Sale offers merit-based scholarships to Year 7 students in 2026 who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and strength of character. The scholarship recognises and rewards students who show outstanding potential and commitment to learning while balancing both merit and financial means assessment.
description: Catholic College Sale Year 7 scholarships are merit-based awards that balance both merit and means, recognising students' existing talents, achievements, academic performance, and demonstration of strength of character. Scholarships typically provide a reduction in tuition fees and are awarded for three years (Year 7 through Year 9). The selection process involves a written assessment conducted during a testing morning at the College. The number and value of scholarships awarded are determined by each applicant's ability to meet or exceed the scholarship criteria, assessment of the family's financial means, and the availability of scholarship places. Renewal and continuation of the award beyond the initial three-year period depends on maintaining academic standards and satisfying any special conditions outlined at the time of award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must be enrolled in Year 6 at time of application; Must demonstrate strength of character; Family financial means assessed as part of selection process; Merit-based selection determined by standardised testing assessment
special_conditions: Scholarships last for three years. The number and value of scholarships are determined by the applicant's ability to meet or exceed the scholarship criteria, assessment of the means component, and the availability of places. Students are required to attend a testing morning at Catholic College Sale on Saturday 29 March 2025. Scholarships currently offered only for Year 7 entry; no other year levels.

overview: Catherine McAuley College offers the Young Carers Scholarship Program to support students who are caring for family members. The program provides financial assistance to help students access school activities, community-based classes, camps and excursions, and sporting club participation.
description: The Young Carers Scholarship Program provides financial assistance of up to $500 per student to support young carers in accessing educational and community activities. The scholarship is designed to remove financial barriers for students who have caring responsibilities outside of school. Funds can be used for school or community-based classes and activities such as camps and excursions, as well as sporting club participation fees. The program recognizes that young carers often face additional financial pressures and aims to enable their full participation in school and community life. Applications and eligibility assessment are managed through the college's student support services.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be a young carer with family caring responsibilities; Open to all year levels; Demonstrated financial need related to caring responsibilities
special_conditions: Funds designated for school or community-based classes and activities including camps, excursions, and sporting club participation fees.

overview: The Bendigo Sports Star Awards Scholarship program is a community-funded initiative launched in 2016-2017 to support elite young athletes. Catherine McAuley College students have been selected for multiple scholarships across various sports to assist them on their sporting journey.
description: The Bendigo Sports Star Awards Scholarship program was launched in 2016-2017 and is funded by the generous support of participating businesses from across the Bendigo region. The program aims to provide financial support to select elite athletes and assist them on their sporting journey. Catherine McAuley College has participated in this program, with students selected for awards across multiple sports including canoeing, netball, AFL, basketball and other sporting disciplines. The scholarship recognizes outstanding sporting achievement and potential at regional and state levels. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in competitive sport at state or national level.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding sporting achievement at regional, state or national level; Current participation in competitive sport; Demonstrated excellence and potential in chosen sport; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Funded by participating businesses across the Bendigo region; Awards made at various times throughout the year; Selection based on demonstrated elite sporting performance.

overview: The Spirit of Catherine Awards recognize outstanding members of the Catherine McAuley College community who embody the Mercy values and demonstrate Living Mercy in their words and actions. Two students from each year level are selected for these prestigious awards.
description: The Spirit of Catherine Awards are prestigious college awards that recognize members of the Catherine McAuley College Bendigo community who exemplify the Mercy values of Compassion, Justice, Respect, Hospitality, Service and Courage. Awards are presented multiple times throughout the year, including Term 1, Term 4 and other occasions. Two students from each year level are selected based on their demonstrated commitment to the Mercy values and their contributions to college community life. Recipients are recognized for embodying Catherine McAuley's words: 'we should be shining lamps, giving light to all around us'. Selection recognizes leadership, service, kindness and positive community engagement. This is a non-monetary award recognizing excellence in character and community values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Demonstrated embodiment of Mercy values; Active contribution to college community; Exemplary conduct and character; Recognition by staff and peers
special_conditions: Awards presented throughout the year at various college occasions including assemblies and special events; Selection based on staff observation and community recognition; Recognition focuses on character and service rather than academic achievement.

overview: Catherine McAuley College's Musician of the Year Award recognizes outstanding musical achievement and performance excellence. The annual competition celebrates student musicians across both classical and contemporary music styles.
description: The Musician of the Year Award is an annual recognition for outstanding musical achievement at Catherine McAuley College Bendigo. The award includes two categories: Classical and Contemporary music. The competition is held at the Marian Theatre at St Mary's campus and is a highlight of the college's performing arts calendar. Student musicians perform various instruments and styles, demonstrating their development and engagement with music studies. All students who participate gain valuable experience performing in a competitive environment before expert adjudicators. There is also an Emerging Artist Award for younger or developing musicians. The awards recognize the work of students and dedicated music staff in preparing performers. This is a non-monetary award recognizing musical excellence and performance achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to student musicians at all year levels; Participation in college music programs; Willing to perform at concert event; Performance in either Classical or Contemporary music category
special_conditions: Competition held annually at Marian Theatre St Mary's campus; Judged by expert adjudicators including music educators and performers; All performers receive feedback from adjudicators; Awards in Classical and Contemporary categories plus Emerging Artist Award.

overview: The Bishop Noel Daly Memorial Award is the most esteemed college community award, recognizing Year 12 students who represent the best all-round student and member of the college community. The award honors those whose conduct is exemplary and who have given distinguished service.
description: The Bishop Noel Daly Memorial Award is the most prestigious award at Catherine McAuley College Bendigo, presented to Year 12 students who demonstrate excellence as all-round community members. Recipients are recognized as students whose 'conduct is best fit to be imitated and who has given distinguished service to the College.' The award represents the highest standard of personal character, leadership and community contribution. Recipients exemplify the college's values through their actions and are recognized for their positive influence on the broader college community. This is a non-monetary award focusing on character, conduct and service. The award is presented at the annual Presentation Evening or Laureate Assembly celebrating academic and community achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students only; Exemplary conduct and personal character; Distinguished service to the college; Positive influence on college community; Recognition by college leadership
special_conditions: Awarded annually to one Year 12 student; Selection by college leadership; Recognition of conduct and service; Named after Bishop Noel Daly; Presented at formal college event.

overview: The Mercy Scholarship is awarded to students who foster leadership in the Mercy tradition. The scholarship recognizes students who exercise genuine concern for others less fortunate, promote school spirit and take active part in fundraising activities.
description: The Mercy Scholarship is a special award at Catherine McAuley College that recognizes students who embody the Mercy tradition of service and leadership. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated genuine concern for others who are less fortunate, active promotion of school spirit and engagement, and meaningful participation in fundraising and community service activities. The scholarship honors the legacy of Catherine McAuley and the Sisters of Mercy, recognizing students who live out these values through action. The award is presented annually to students who show exceptional commitment to service and compassion. This appears to be a non-monetary recognition award celebrating service and leadership in the Mercy tradition.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated genuine concern for others less fortunate; Active promotion of school spirit; Meaningful participation in fundraising activities; Service-oriented attitude; Embodiment of Mercy values
special_conditions: Recognition of service and leadership in Mercy tradition; Awards students showing exceptional community engagement and concern for others; Presented at formal college event.

overview: The annual College Dux Award is presented to the highest achieving Year 10 student across all subject areas. This award recognises outstanding academic excellence and achievement in the College.
description: The Dux Award is the College's highest academic recognition, presented annually to the Year 10 student who has achieved the highest academic standing across all subject areas. This is a non-monetary honour award that recognises exceptional academic performance and commitment to learning at Catholic Regional College North Keilor. The recipient is recognised at the annual College Awards evening in Term 4. Selection is based on overall academic achievement across the entire curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; highest academic achievement across all subject areas
special_conditions: Award is presented at the annual College Awards Evening held in Term 4.

overview: The John Arthurson Award is sponsored by the College Parents and Friends Association in recognition of Mr John Arthurson, College Principal from 1995-2005. It is presented to one student in each year level who demonstrates exceptional qualities.
description: The John Arthurson Award is presented annually to one student in each year level at Catholic Regional College North Keilor. This non-monetary honour award recognises students who demonstrate persistence, consistent effort, resilience, striving to achieve, and positive relationships with peers and staff. The award is sponsored by the College Parents and Friends Association in recognition of the former College Principal's contributions. Selection criteria emphasise both personal qualities and commitment to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: One student per year level (Years 7-10); Persistence, consistent effort, resilience, striving to achieve; Positive relationships with peers and staff
special_conditions: One award is presented to a student in each year level. Award is presented at the annual College Awards Evening in Term 4.

program_name: University of Melbourne Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program
overview: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program is sponsored by the University of Melbourne in recognition of former Vice-Chancellor Kwong Lee Dow. It is a three-year academic enrichment scholarship designed to assist high achieving students in realising their full potential.
description: This is a three-year academic enrichment scholarship offered by the University of Melbourne to high achieving students at Catholic Regional College North Keilor. The program is designed to create a network and community of scholars who receive support in realising their full potential. While the specific financial value is not detailed on the College website, it provides academic enrichment opportunities and mentoring from the University. Participants benefit from being part of an exclusive cohort of high-performing students. The scholarship duration spans three years, providing sustained support throughout secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: High achieving students; Three-year commitment to the program
special_conditions: Three-year academic enrichment program. Participants form a network and community of scholars. Sponsored by University of Melbourne.

overview: Catholic Regional College North Keilor recognises financial pressures on families and offers assistance to those experiencing financial hardship. Parents and guardians experiencing difficulty with school fees are encouraged to contact the College Business Manager.
description: The College provides financial assistance to families experiencing hardship or unexpected financial difficulties. Parents and guardians are encouraged to communicate directly with the College Business Manager to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options. The College is committed to assisting families in times of need and maintains an open line of communication to help find practical solutions. This is a discretionary assistance program where support is tailored to individual family circumstances. The College understands that financial situations can change and is willing to work with families to support their children's education.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship; Contact required with College Business Manager; Clear communication between family and College
special_conditions: Individual assessment of circumstances required. Direct contact with College Business Manager needed. College emphasises clear communication between family and school.

overview: Catholic Regional College North Keilor offers a $400 discount for families with multiple children enrolled at any Catholic Regional College Federation secondary school. This discount applies to each CRCNK enrolled student.
description: Families with more than one child enrolled at any of the Catholic Regional College Federation secondary schools (including CRC St Albans, Caroline Springs, Melton, or Sydenham) receive a $400 discount applied to their CRC North Keilor tuition fees for each enrolled student. This discount is applied automatically when multiple siblings are enrolled and helps reduce the financial burden on families with children at CRC schools. The discount applies per student at CRCNK and represents a 7.5% reduction on the 2025 tuition fee of $5,340. The discount is credited directly to the school fee account.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child enrolled at any Catholic Regional College Federation secondary school; Automatic application to CRCNK enrolled students
special_conditions: Applicable to families with more than one child enrolled at any Catholic Regional College Federation secondary school. Discount is per CRCNK enrolled student.

overview: Catholic Regional College Sydenham administers the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) to support eligible families with costs for camps, sports and excursions. The program provides financial assistance to students whose parents or guardians hold eligible concession cards or are temporary foster parents.
description: The CSEF program provides $250 per student per year in funding to eligible families. Eligible recipients include parents or guardians who hold a current Veterans Affairs Gold card, Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC), or Pensioner Concession Card (PCC). The funding is applied directly to reduce the family's account balance at the school. Temporary foster parents are also eligible. Families must complete an application form and provide concession card documentation. Applications are processed on a rolling basis throughout the year, with a final deadline set annually (typically around June). The funding is means-tested and eligibility is verified through the Australian Government Department of Families, Fairness and Housing.
eligibility_criteria: Current holder of Veterans Affairs Gold card, Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC), or Pensioner Concession Card (PCC); Temporary foster parent status; Student must be enrolled at a registered Victorian secondary school; Application must be submitted by annual closing date
special_conditions: Funding is applied to the student's account to reduce fees. Eligibility is assessed annually. Families must hold valid concession cards or appropriate documentation proving eligibility status. The school will verify eligibility through the Australian Government.

overview: Catholic Regional College Sydenham offers tuition fee discounts to families with multiple children enrolled across the Catholic Regional College Federation campuses. The discount applies directly to tuition fees and increases with each additional sibling.
description: The sibling discount program provides progressive reductions on tuition fees for families with two or more children attending Catholic Regional College Federation schools simultaneously. Families with 2 children receive a 10% discount on tuition fees; families with 3 children receive a 20% discount; and families with 4 or more children receive a 30% discount. These discounts apply to the tuition component of fees only and are automatically calculated when families complete their fee payment plans. The discount applies to each eligible child's tuition fees across all CRC Federation campuses (Caroline Springs, North Keilor, St Albans, Melton, and Sydenham). Families must complete the tuition fee payment plan agreement form and indicate the number of siblings attending other CRC Federation schools.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at Catholic Regional College Federation schools in the same year; Must be attending different campuses within the CRC Federation or same campus; Discount applies to tuition fees only; Families must complete fee payment arrangement with the school
special_conditions: Discounts are calculated automatically on tuition fees when families complete fee payment forms. Second child: 10% discount; Third child: 20% discount; Fourth or more children: 30% discount. Discount applies across CRC Federation campuses only. Subject levies and other fees are not included in the discount.

overview: Catholic Regional College Sydenham's Sports Academy is a selective program designed for high-performance athletes seeking excellence in sport while completing Year 11 and Year 12. The program provides specialized coaching, strength and conditioning facilities, and structured athletic development alongside academic studies.
description: The Sports Academy is a specialized Year 11 and 12 program for students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability and commitment to excellence in sport. Applicants must be currently enrolled at CRC Federation feeder campuses (St Albans, North Keilor, Caroline Springs, or Melton) and must have attended a mandatory Sports Academy Information Seminar. Entry to the program requires completion of an application form and participation in a shortlisted interview at CRC Sydenham. The program includes mandatory participation in three 70-minute coaching blocks per week: recovery and rehabilitation sessions, personalized strength and conditioning training at the onsite CRCFIT facility with qualified personal trainers, and sport-specific technical skills coaching. Program costs include a $110 levy covering coaching tuition and excursions, plus approximately $55 for academy uniforms purchased through the school supplier. Students must provide reference letters from their Year 10 Coordinator and coaching or employment references, and sign a contract outlining program standards and expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Year 11 for 2026 academic year at Catholic Regional College Sydenham; Currently attending CRC Federation feeder campus (St Albans, North Keilor, Caroline Springs, or Melton); Must have attended CRC Sydenham Sports Academy Information Seminar; Must have discussed program with current Year 10 Coordinator; Year 10 Semester 1 and Interim Reports required; Shortlisted applicants required to attend interview
special_conditions: Participation is competitive and selective. Students must sign a contract outlining standards to maintain placement in the program. Mandatory attendance at three weekly coaching sessions totaling 210 minutes per week. Students expected to represent the College at SACCSS events, cluster sports days, and the CRC Sydenham Athletics Carnival. Minimum 90% attendance is expected. Students must purchase academy training top and uniform from designated school supplier ($55). Program levy is $110 per year. Reference letters from Year 10 Coordinator and coaches/employers required. This is a specialised program requiring significant time commitment alongside academic studies.

school_name: Caulfield Grammar School - Wheelers Hill Campus

overview: Chaffey Secondary College offers the General Excellence William Birks Hawson Scholarship to Year 7 students commencing in 2026. This merit-based scholarship has been funded by the college since 1994 and recognises students demonstrating overall excellence across academic achievement, leadership, arts, and sports.
description: The Year 7 William Birks Hawson Scholarship is awarded to students enrolling in Year 7 at Chaffey Secondary College demonstrating general excellence across multiple domains. Recipients receive a $100 Totally Workwear voucher (redeemable for Chaffey Secondary College uniform), $100 in direct funds, and a book and stationery pack in December 2025. The scholarship is awarded based on a comprehensive application form completed by the student and a confidential teacher reference from the student's Year 6 teacher. Selection criteria assess academic achievement, leadership qualities and experience, arts talent and experience, sports talent and experience, and hobbies and interests. The college reserves the right not to award scholarships if applications do not demonstrate the high standards expected. Scholarship certificates are presented at the first official whole-school assembly in Term 1, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 at Chaffey Secondary College in 2026; Must be enrolled at Chaffey Secondary College in Year 7 for the 2025 school year by closing date; Application must be completed by 3:40pm Friday 14 November 2025; Students may only apply using the scholarship application form supplied by the college; Scholarship applications must be handwritten by the student; Late applications will not be accepted
special_conditions: Chaffey Secondary College reserves the right not to award scholarships if the standard of the applications does not demonstrate the high standards expected. The college may contact a second referee if additional information is required in the event there are more than ten applicants at equal ranking. Scholarship applications must be handwritten by the student. Successful candidates will be advised of the outcome by phone and mail in December 2025. Recipients must accept and confirm their place by attending the college and completing their scholarship agreement.

overview: Chaffey Secondary College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program for incoming Year 7 students. This selective program provides an educational opportunity for students capable of completing a more comprehensive and challenging secondary curriculum alongside like-minded peers.
description: The SEAL program is a selective entry opportunity for high-ability Year 7 students introduced at Chaffey Secondary College in 2020. The program provides no direct financial award but offers enriched educational benefits including accelerated learning in English, Mathematics, Humanities and Science where students complete four years of content within three years. Students gain access to broader and more flexible learning programs and have opportunities to commence VCE studies in Year 10. Selection involves completion of a written application by the student and parent/guardian with sections completed by the student, a parent/guardian, and the student's Year 6 teacher. Short-listed applicants complete online registration with Edutest by Friday 24 October 2025 and sit standardised testing at Chaffey Secondary College on Friday 31 October 2025 (testing cost is covered by the college). Students must provide a copy of their Year 5 NAPLAN results and most recent school report. Applications must be returned to Chaffey Secondary College by 4pm Monday 1 September 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must be enrolled at Chaffey Secondary College to apply; Students capable of completing a more comprehensive and challenging secondary curriculum; Students and parent/guardian must complete written application; Year 6 teacher reference required
special_conditions: Testing is completed at Chaffey Secondary College commencing at 8:45am on Friday 31 October 2025 and the cost of testing is covered by Chaffey Secondary College. Short-listed applicants must complete online registration with Edutest by Friday 24 October 2025. Students must attend the college and complete their SEAL agreement to accept and confirm their place by Tuesday 9 December 2025. Orientation day is Tuesday 9 December 2025 with students in their 2026 SEAL class. Successful applicants will be notified and offered a place by Friday 28 November 2025. Research has shown that students participating in this program achieve higher ATAR scores, with many attributing their success at VCE and beyond to the skills learned in the SEAL program.

school_name: Caulfield Grammar School - Yarra Junction Campus

overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to support families who demonstrate genuine financial need in accessing Christian education. The bursary program offers assistance in the provision of school fees to families with identifiable financial hardship.
description: The school maintains a General Bursary Fund and offers assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. More information and application forms are available from the Finance Department. The program is designed to ensure that families facing financial difficulties can still access education at the school. Specific details regarding the amount of assistance, selection criteria, and application procedures are available directly from the school's Finance Department.
eligibility_criteria: Families must demonstrate identifiable financial need; Application through Finance Department required
special_conditions: More information and application forms available from the Finance Department. Individual assessment of financial circumstances required.

overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to provide financial assistance to families with an identifiable financial need. The bursary supports families seeking to access Christian education.
description: Chairo Christian School operates a General Bursary Fund that offers assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. This program recognises that tuition costs can be a barrier to accessing quality Christian education. The school works directly with families to understand their financial circumstances and provide appropriate support. More information and application forms are available from the Finance Department. Families interested in this assistance should contact the Finance Department directly to discuss their individual circumstances and eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Families with an identifiable financial need; Current or prospective students enrolled at the school
special_conditions: Applications and additional information available from the Finance Department. Assessment is based on demonstrated financial need.

overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to provide financial assistance to families with an identifiable financial need. The school is committed to making Christian education accessible to families across a broad income range by offering bursary support for tuition fees.
description: The General Bursary Fund is administered by Chairo Christian School to assist families with an identifiable financial need in accessing Christian education at the school. Applicants must demonstrate financial hardship and meet the school's assessment criteria. The bursary provides partial financial assistance towards tuition fees, with the exact amount varying based on individual family circumstances and fund availability. More information and application forms are available directly from the school's Finance Department, which assesses all applications in accordance with the school's financial assistance policy.
eligibility_criteria: Families must demonstrate identifiable financial need; Must be enrolled or applying for enrolment at the school; Financial assessment required; School maintains discretion in awarding assistance
special_conditions: Application forms and further details are available from the Finance Department. Bursary awards are subject to the school's financial assistance policy and the availability of funds. The level of financial assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and financial need demonstrated in the application.

school_name: Chairo Christian School - Drouin East Campus
overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to assist families with identifiable financial need. The fund provides financial assistance to help families access Christian education at the school. Information and application forms are available through the Finance Department.
description: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund and offers assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. The bursary is designed to help eligible families manage tuition fees by providing financial relief. The level and duration of bursary assistance varies depending on individual family circumstances and the availability of funds. Recipients must demonstrate financial need through the application process. Families should contact the school's Finance Department for detailed information about application procedures, eligibility criteria, and the amount of assistance that may be available. Applications are processed on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students and families; Families must demonstrate identifiable financial need; Application required through Finance Department
special_conditions: Assistance is offered to families with identifiable financial need. More information and application forms are available from the Finance Department. Contact schoolfees@chairo.vic.edu.au for further details.

overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to offer assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. The bursary is designed to support families who wish to access quality Christian education but face financial constraints.
description: The school maintains a General Bursary Fund and offers assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. The fund provides financial assistance through fee reductions or remission for eligible families. Application is on a case-by-case basis and families are assessed according to their financial circumstances. More information and application forms are available from the Finance Department. The school operates flexible payment plans including quarterly, monthly, fortnightly, and weekly payment options to further assist families managing school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Families with identifiable financial need; Open to students across all year levels and campuses
special_conditions: Families are assessed on a case-by-case basis according to identified financial need. The school reserves discretion in determining bursary amounts and support. Additional fee discounts are available: early payment discount of 2-3% if full annual fees are paid by early March; generous sibling discounts apply where two or more children attend the school from one family.

overview: Charles La Trobe P-12 College offers the Year 7 Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in prospective Year 7 students. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability as they transition to secondary school.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship worth $1,000. The scholarship is awarded annually to recognise academic excellence in students entering Year 7. Applications close in August each year. The selection process involves an academic assessment to identify high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent; Prospective Year 7 applicants
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: The Rotary Club of Rosanna provides generous scholarships for students across secondary year levels. Charles La Trobe P-12 College awards one scholarship to one student in each year level from Years 7 to 11, recognising academic achievement and student potential.
description: Rotary Club of Rosanna provides generous scholarships for one student in each year level from Years 7 to 11 at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. Successful students receive a $1,000 bursary for the purchase of uniforms or textbooks. Students must apply for the scholarship. The award recognises academic excellence and provides financial support to assist with school-related costs, including uniform purchases and educational materials required throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 11; Must apply for the scholarship; Students selected by the school
special_conditions: Successful students are provided with a $1,000 bursary for the purchase of uniform or text books.

overview: The Lions Club provides a bursary to support Year 10 students whose families experience financial disadvantage. The bursary assists with the costs associated with undertaking the Victorian Certificate of Education (VCE) in Years 11 and 12.
description: Lions Club provides a $1,000 bursary for a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of VCE preparation and enrolment. The recipient is selected by the secondary school principal based on demonstrated financial need and family circumstances. This bursary aims to ensure that students from disadvantaged backgrounds have access to quality senior secondary education and can progress to VCE studies without financial hardship. The award is made to one student per year in Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Family must experience financial disadvantage; Student selected by secondary school principal; Student must be enrolled at the college
special_conditions: Recipient selected by secondary school principal based on financial disadvantage. Award intended to assist with costs of VCE.

overview: The Masonic Club provides a bursary to support Year 11 students whose families experience financial disadvantage. The bursary assists with the costs of completing VCE in Year 12 and ensures equitable access to senior secondary education.
description: Masonic Club provides a $500 bursary for a Year 11 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of Year 12 studies. The recipient is selected by the secondary school principal based on demonstrated financial need and family circumstances. This bursary aims to provide financial assistance to students from disadvantaged backgrounds during their final year of secondary education. The award is made to one student per year in Year 11, helping reduce financial barriers to completing VCE.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 students; Family must experience financial disadvantage; Student selected by secondary school principal; Student must be enrolled at the college
special_conditions: Recipient selected by secondary school principal based on financial disadvantage. Award intended to assist with costs of Year 12.

overview: Access Ministries provides encouragement awards to recognise Year 6 students at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. The awards acknowledge students demonstrating positive attributes as they prepare for transition to secondary school.
description: Access Ministries provides two encouragement awards for Year 6 students at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. These awards recognise and encourage students who demonstrate positive qualities and character as they approach their transition to secondary schooling. The awards are designed to provide positive recognition and encouragement to selected Year 6 students. While specific monetary values are not detailed, these awards serve as acknowledgment of student achievement and potential as students prepare for their secondary education journey.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students; Students must be enrolled at Charles La Trobe P-12 College
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-The Pavilion Campus - Preston East
Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
overview: Charles La Trobe P-12 College offers an Academic Scholarship to prospective Year 7 students who demonstrate excellence in learning. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and commitment to academic studies. One scholarship is awarded each year for each year level from Year 7 to Year 11.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship worth $1000. The scholarship provides a one-time payment of $1000 to be used for the purchase of uniforms or textbooks. This scholarship is open to students currently enrolled in Year 6 who are transitioning to secondary education at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. Applications are submitted by students and close in August of each year. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence in learning and commitment to their academic studies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Must apply by August deadline; Open to all genders and backgrounds
special_conditions: Applications close in August of each year. Scholarship is awarded once to support the purchase of uniforms or textbooks.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
overview: The Rotary Club of Rosanna provides generous scholarships to secondary students at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. The scholarship programme awards one student in each year level from Years 7 to 11 who demonstrates academic excellence. Recipients receive a $1000 bursary for the purchase of uniforms or textbooks.
description: The Rotary Club of Rosanna provides generous scholarships for one student in each year level from Years 7 to 11. Students must apply for the scholarship. The successful students are provided with a $1000 bursary for the purchase of uniform or text books. The scholarship recognises secondary students who demonstrate academic achievement and excellence in learning. This is an annual award with applications required from interested students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11; Must apply for the scholarship; Students across all backgrounds eligible
special_conditions: One scholarship awarded per year level (Years 7-11). Students must apply to be considered. Funds are for purchase of uniform or textbooks.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
program_name: Lions Club Financial Hardship Bursary - Year 10
overview: Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary to support Year 10 students whose families experience financial disadvantage. The bursary assists with the costs of transitioning to Year 11 VCE studies. Recipients are selected by the secondary school principal.
description: Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary for a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of VCE. The secondary school principal selects the recipient based on demonstrated financial need and academic engagement. This is an annual award targeting one student per year. The bursary provides direct financial assistance to support students from disadvantaged backgrounds to continue their secondary education and access VCE pathways without financial barriers.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students only; Family must experience financial disadvantage; Selected by secondary school principal; One bursary awarded annually
special_conditions: Recipient selected by secondary school principal based on financial disadvantage. One bursary awarded per year for Year 10 students only. Intended to assist with VCE costs.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
program_name: Masonic Club Financial Hardship Bursary - Year 11
overview: Masonic Club provides a $500 bursary to support Year 11 students whose families experience financial disadvantage. The bursary assists with the costs of Year 12 VCE studies. Recipients are selected by the secondary school principal.
description: Masonic Club provides a $500 bursary for a Year 11 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of Year 12. The secondary school principal selects the recipient based on demonstrated financial need and academic engagement. This is an annual award targeting one student per year. The bursary provides direct financial support to students from disadvantaged backgrounds to enable them to successfully transition to and complete their final year of secondary education with reduced financial stress.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students only; Family must experience financial disadvantage; Selected by secondary school principal; One bursary awarded annually
special_conditions: Recipient selected by secondary school principal based on financial disadvantage. One bursary awarded per year for Year 11 students only. Intended to assist with Year 12 costs.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
overview: Access Ministries provides encouragement awards to Year 6 students at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. Two bursaries of $200 each are awarded annually to students transitioning to secondary education. Recipients are selected by the College chaplain in partnership with the primary school principal.
description: Access Ministries provides two encouragement awards for Year 6 students. These bursaries are $200 each and are for the purchase of uniform or text books. Students are selected by the College chaplain in partnership with the primary school principal. This program recognises and supports students as they transition from primary to secondary education at the college. The awards provide practical financial assistance to help families with the costs of uniforms and textbooks for students entering secondary school.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students only; Students transitioning to secondary education; Selected by College chaplain and primary school principal; Two awards per year
special_conditions: Two awards per year. Recipients selected by College chaplain in partnership with primary school principal. Funds for purchase of uniform or textbooks. Awarded to Year 6 students making transition to secondary school.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Deaf Facility
overview: Charles La Trobe College offers an Academic Scholarship to prospective Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students of exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning upon entry to secondary school.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship of $1000. Applications close in August of each year. The scholarship provides $1000 in financial assistance for eligible students. Selection is based on the application process outlined by the college. This is a merit-based award recognising academic excellence at entry to secondary school. Successful applicants receive recognition of their outstanding academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective Year 7 students; Must apply for the scholarship; Academic merit-based selection

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Deaf Facility
overview: Charles La Trobe College awards a scholarship at each secondary year level to recognise high-achieving students who demonstrate excellence in learning. The College awards five scholarships each year—one for each year level from Year 7 to Year 11.
description: At each secondary year level, a high achieving student who demonstrates excellence in learning is awarded with a scholarship in recognition of their commitment to their academic studies. The College awards five scholarships each year – one for each year level (from Year 7 to 11). The value and specific conditions of these awards are determined by the college annually. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated academic excellence throughout the school year. These scholarships recognise sustained commitment to learning across all year levels of secondary schooling.

overview: Chelsea Primary School participates in the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) administered by the Victorian Government. This program provides financial assistance to eligible families to help cover costs associated with school camps, sporting activities, excursions and incursions. CSEF enables all students to participate in these essential educational experiences regardless of family financial circumstances.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative designed to assist eligible families with the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. From 1 January 2026, eligible families receive $400 per year for primary school students. The fund is paid directly to the school to use towards expenses relating to camps, excursions or sporting activities for the benefit of eligible students. Families holding a valid means-tested concession card (such as Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card) or temporary foster parents may apply. Additionally, Prep and Year 7 students who receive CSEF are eligible to receive financial help with the cost of school uniforms through State Schools' Relief. A special consideration category also exists for asylum seeker and refugee families who do not have access to Centrelink or a means-tested Health Care Card.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or carer must hold a valid means-tested concession card (Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card) on first student day of Term 1 or Term 2; OR be a temporary foster parent; OR qualify under special consideration category (asylum seeker or refugee families); Student must be enrolled in a registered Victorian government or non-government primary school; Student must not be attending preschool, kindergarten, TAFE or homeschooled
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted to the school by 26 June 2026. Schools must process applications by 3 July 2026. Funds are paid directly to the school and allocated by the school towards camps, sports and excursion costs for eligible students. School requires permission from families before allocating CSEF funds. A credit note remains on the family account until the family advises how they want the funds allocated. Payments are for the application year only and cannot be claimed for previous years. Prep and Year 7 students receiving CSEF automatically qualify for uniform assistance through State Schools' Relief.

overview: Christ the King School Newcomb offers concessional fees to families who meet system requirements. The college works with families to explore assistance options and develop sustainable solutions. This program complements the scholarship offerings and demonstrates the college's commitment to supporting worthy students irrespective of financial means or social circumstances.

overview: Christian College Geelong offers Academic Excellence Scholarships each year to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. special_conditions: Scholarship recipients strive to uphold the Christian values of Truth, Love, Hope, Grace and Faith and pursue their education at Christian College Geelong, wherever possible, through to Year 12. special_conditions: Scholarships awarded according to availability at one of the College's campuses across Geelong, Surf Coast and Bellarine Peninsula. school_name: Christian College Geelong - Senior School – Waurn Ponds

The Christian College Geelong Foundation Board supports this program as a major project, seeking to source and increase ongoing financial contributions to expand these opportunities. The program provides pathways for passionate and talented students to access an exceptional quality education, irrespective of financial means or social circumstances, and who might not otherwise have the opportunity to access the College. The specific financial support and selection criteria are managed through the Foundation's scholarship fund.

The scholarship provides access to the College's exceptional educational program and supportive community environment.

special_conditions: Applications are facilitated through partnerships with local churches. The College funds a Resource and Community Liaison Officer dedicated to supporting cultural scholarship recipients. Support includes breaking down cultural barriers and providing ongoing pastoral care.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: The Year 3 Learning That Matters (LTM) Scholarship at Christian College Geelong supports outstanding students who would not otherwise be able to access the College due to financial circumstances. The scholarship recognises exceptional citizens with a thirst for learning and commitment to making a positive difference.
description: Scholarships are awarded to a small number of Year 3 students at each of the Junior School Campuses (Belmont, Drysdale, and Torquay) for the duration of their educational journey at Christian College. The LTM Scholarship provides opportunities for children to further develop their passion for learning and guide them towards a flourishing future. The College seeks students who are outstanding citizens, have a thirst for learning, are curious and keen to make a positive difference in the lives of others, but due to financial circumstances, have been unable to consider or take up an educational opportunity at Christian College. The application process requires students to submit a piece of work—an artefact—that demonstrates their understanding and captures their own ideas about making the world a better place for others.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to Year 3; Currently enrolled in Year 2 or equivalent; Outstanding citizenship qualities; Demonstrated thirst for learning and curiosity; Commitment to making positive difference in others' lives; Financial need and inability to otherwise access Christian College; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarships awarded for the duration of educational journey at Christian College. Applicants must submit an artefact (poster, short story, video, multimedia production, song, artwork or other demonstration) showing understanding of making the world better for others. Application for Enrolment form and LTM Scholarship information available on the College website.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: The Salvation Army Music Scholarship at Christian College Geelong, supported by the College Foundation, partners with the Salvation Army and their music programs such as 'Just Brass' to identify talented young musicians. This scholarship recognises that students from disadvantaged backgrounds can benefit enormously from Christian College's excellent music programme and pastoral care.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: The Sponsored Student Placements bursary program at Christian College Geelong Foundation assists students in maintaining a stable educational experience when life presents unexpected financial challenges. The program supports families facing temporary hardship or changed circumstances.
description: The Sponsored Student Placements is a bursary program managed by Christian College Geelong Foundation that helps families maintain their children's educational experience at the College during times of financial difficulty or unexpected hardship. This program recognises that even enrolled families may face changed circumstances that affect their ability to continue paying full fees. The Foundation seeks to source ongoing financial contributions to support this essential program. For many years, Christian College Geelong has made provision to support students from disadvantaged circumstances by providing them with enrolment opportunities and continuing support. The bursary programme works in partnership with the Foundation to ensure worthy students can complete their education without interruption.

special_conditions: Administered through Christian College Geelong Foundation. Requires financial means assessment and evidence of changed circumstances. Supported by tax-deductible donations. Case-by-case consideration based on individual circumstances. College Leadership approval required.

overview: Christian College Geelong offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in prospective students entering Years 5, 7 and 10. Students must demonstrate high levels of academic excellence. A successful scholarship application means the student's tuition fees are reduced by up to 50% annually, and this continues through Middle School to Year 12 at Senior School. The scholarship selection process involves consideration of application responses from the student, a written test administered by Edutest, and interviews for shortlisted applicants. Testing usually takes place in October each year, with applications open for the duration of Term 3.

The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction or remission based on the level of musical excellence demonstrated. These scholarships are available to prospective students entering Years 5 through 12 in 2026, creating pathways for musically talented students from diverse backgrounds. overview: Christian College Geelong offers Financially Supported Places for current College families who may face financial hardship or challenging circumstances. These places provide tuition fee reduction or remission to support families in maintaining their child's enrolment.

The College offers confidential conversations and individually tailored support to explore ways to assist affected families. Financial support is provided through fee reduction or remission based on family financial circumstances and need. This program is distinct from scholarship programs and is designed to provide continuity of education for current students when families face temporary or ongoing financial challenges. Applications and discussions are handled confidentially through the College administration.

special_conditions: Available to current Christian College families only; Confidential application and assessment process; Individually determined based on family circumstances; Not subject to formal competitive selection; College reserves discretion on all awards.

overview: Christway College offers the Christian Leadership Scholarship to recognise and encourage students who demonstrate their commitment to God through strong faith, integrity and a commitment to a local church community. This scholarship supports the college's mission of developing students of strong character and faith.

Scholarships provide a discount of up to 50% off tuition fees, as determined by the Executive Principal. Scholarship recipients are required to uphold the Christway Five Foundations of a Whole Child and pursue their education at the College with enthusiasm and commitment. The scholarship recognises applicants who demonstrate commitment to the Christian faith, demonstrate servant leadership, maintain regular and active involvement in a local church, and commitment to personal integrity and Christian character. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an interview with the Head of Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Commitment to the Christian faith; Demonstrates servant leadership; Regular and active involvement in a local church; Commitment to personal integrity and Christian character; Shortlisted applicants only invited for interview
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are required to uphold the Christway Five Foundations of a Whole Child and pursue their education at the College with enthusiasm and commitment. A letter of recommendation from the applicant's Church pastor or minister must be attached to the application.

This internal award honours students who have demonstrated consistent high achievement and commitment to their studies throughout the academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in Year 6-11; Highest academic achiever in respective year level; Current enrolment at Christway College at time of award

overview: Christway College awards an academic scholarship annually to the highest achieving student in Years 6-11. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic performance and commitment to learning.

year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11

overview: The Timothy Award is presented annually to the outstanding Primary and Secondary student of the year at Christway College. The award recognises overall excellence across academic, personal, and character dimensions.
description: The Timothy Award is the College's highest honour, presented annually to one outstanding Primary student and one outstanding Secondary student. The award recognises students who have demonstrated exceptional achievement and character development across multiple dimensions of college life. Recipients are selected based on overall excellence. Specific selection criteria, monetary value, and renewal conditions were not detailed on the publicly available school website.

special_conditions: Award is presented annually. Students are selected by the College based on demonstrated excellence. Specific renewal conditions and any associated monetary value were not publicly disclosed.

overview: Cire Community School offers eligible Year 11 students the opportunity to compete for the Cowey-Selman Kokoda Award, a prestigious external scholarship coordinated by the Monbulk, Mount Evelyn, and Lilydale RSL sub-branches. The award recognises students who demonstrate exceptional understanding of Australian military history and leadership potential.
description: The Cowey-Selman Kokoda Award is an annual competition open to Year 11 students enrolled at secondary schools and colleges within the Shire of Yarra Ranges, including Cire Community School. Winners receive a fully sponsored, escorted trek to walk the Kokoda Trail at no cost to themselves, other than personal medications, spending money, and gifts for Papua New Guinea locals. The award recognises service and sacrifice by honouring the memory of James Cowey MC and Harold Geoffrey Selman, both WWII servicemen from the 39th Battalion. Students submit entries in any medium (PowerPoint, art, poetry, or essay up to 600 words) addressing two questions about the Kokoda Campaign's importance to Australia and why they wish to trek the trail. Selection involves school nomination, with shortlisted entries reviewed by the Scholarship Committee. The scholarship aims to inspire leadership and historical awareness in young Australians.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 students; Currently enrolled at secondary school or college within the Shire of Yarra Ranges; Must submit entry addressing two specified Kokoda Campaign questions; Must obtain parental permission and school principal's confidential reference
special_conditions: External award administered by RSL sub-branches, not the school. Entries submitted to school, then school submits up to two shortlisted entries to Mount Evelyn RSL. Award includes fully sponsored, escorted Kokoda trek experience with no costs except personal spending and gifts.

overview: Cire Community School students are eligible for scholarships awarded by the Wandin Rotary Club, which has sponsored disadvantaged youths to attend the school. The club provides direct financial support to students and mentoring to help them succeed in their education.
description: The Wandin Rotary Club offers financial scholarships to disadvantaged students attending Cire Community School. In recent years, awards have ranged from $500 to $2,000 per recipient. For example, in 2020, the club awarded a $2,000 scholarship distributed equally among four Cire students (approximately $500 each). The Wandin Rotary Club specifically sponsors disadvantaged youths and provides not only financial assistance but also mentoring support to help students complete their curriculum at their own pace. The scholarship recognises the importance of alternative education options for young people who need positive support. Recipients have been highlighted as exemplars of resilience and determination, with the club emphasising the life-changing impact of their investment in community education.
eligibility_criteria: Attending Cire Community School; Identified as disadvantaged or experiencing financial hardship; Subject to Wandin Rotary Club selection criteria; Mentoring support also provided alongside financial aid
special_conditions: External award administered by Wandin Rotary Club in partnership with Cire. Recipients also receive mentoring support from club members. Award amounts vary based on club's available funds and number of recipients in each year. Rotary Club is one of the school's first organisational members.

overview: Cire Community School students are eligible for the SwinLocal program, a partnership with Swinburne University of Technology that awards scholarships for VET qualification study. The program supports students from Learn Local and community schools pursuing vocational education pathways.
description: SwinLocal is a community outreach initiative by Swinburne University of Technology established in 2016 to support students studying at Learn Local and community schools including Cire Community School. The program covers the outer-east region including the Yarra Ranges. Scholarship recipients receive funding to undertake VET courses at Swinburne University, providing quality vocational training and a safe, supported introduction to a larger educational institution. As of 2020, the program had awarded 23 scholarships across all participating Learn Local providers. Cire Community School students have received multiple SwinLocal scholarships. The scholarship recognises vocational pathways and provides students with access to higher-level training and potential career advancement in their chosen fields.
eligibility_criteria: Studying at Cire Community School; Enrolled in or planning to pursue VET qualification study; Based in outer-east Melbourne region including Yarra Ranges; Demonstrating commitment to vocational education pathway
special_conditions: External partnership program with Swinburne University of Technology. Provides both scholarship funding and supported entry pathway to university VET programs. Learn Local providers and community schools like Cire nominate eligible students. Cire Training was awarded Community Training Provider of the Year for 2019 in recognition of excellence.

school_name: Clyde Creek Primary School - Specialist Campus

overview: Clyde Secondary College offers a learning pathway for highly able and academic Year 7 students. This program is designed to provide students with a challenging and diverse curriculum that will enhance each student's ability to achieve success at school and beyond.
description: The High Achievers Program is a selective entry program for Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential. Students will benefit from the opportunity to expand their knowledge and skills beyond the mainstream classroom environment, enabling them to participate in learning based on their own performance and skills.
