They are provided with open-ended activities and questions inviting curiosity, exploration and manipulation of ideas and materials at a deeper and broader level than might otherwise occur. Students are encouraged to focus on creativity, initiative, critical thinking, responsibility, and leadership. In some cases, pre-testing will establish the opportunity to skip ahead to new material and in others, students will learn both the mainstream material and additional, more complex, material. Students have the opportunity to engage in individual, self-directed learning that specifically addresses both their abilities and their interests. This is not a scholarship with financial value, but an accelerated learning program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must demonstrate high academic ability; Must be enrolled at Clyde Secondary College; All students who have accepted a place at the College for Year 7 will be forwarded information on how to apply
special_conditions: This is a learning pathway program, not a scholarship. Enrollment is based on demonstrated academic ability and performance. Pre-testing may be conducted to establish learning opportunities.

overview: Cobram Anglican Grammar School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement in existing students. The scholarship recognises students with the best academic records who demonstrate exceptional commitment to learning as they progress through the school.
description: Cobram AGS has introduced a new scholarship program to recognise and reward the academic achievements of existing students as they progress through the school. Students with the best academic record from Year 6 through to Year 11 will be awarded a 50% remission on tuition fees for the following year. These annual scholarships are a significant reward for high achieving students and will be formally recognised at the Presentation Day ceremony that concludes the academic year. The scholarship provides substantial financial support through a 50% fee reduction, recognising academic excellence across multiple year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled Year 6 through Year 11 students; Students must have the best academic record in their year level; Open to all students regardless of gender
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Awarded based on academic performance throughout the year. Scholarships are awarded annually and provide a 50% remission on tuition fees for the following year. Recipients are formally recognised at the Presentation Day ceremony.

overview: Cobram AGS Bursaries have been established to provide educational opportunities for students who have the potential to lead within the community but are facing financial hardship. The bursary program enables access to quality education for families experiencing financial difficulty.
description: Cobram AGS Bursaries have been established to provide educational opportunities for students who have the potential to lead within the community but are facing financial hardship. These bursaries support families who face financial challenges in meeting school fees while their children demonstrate leadership potential and character. The program is designed to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent capable students from accessing the school's educational programs. Interested families should contact the Business Manager to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Students facing financial hardship; Demonstrated potential to lead within the community; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Bursary amount varies based on individual family circumstances and financial need. Applicants should contact the Business Manager, Mrs. Narelle Schreenan, to discuss eligibility and application requirements.

school_name: Collingwood English Language School-South Morang Campus
Croydon Community School-Woori Yallock Farm School Campus
Crusoe 7-10 Secondary College Bendigo Teaching Unit

overview: Dandenong Ranges Steiner School offers the Scholarship Program to facilitate an inclusive and diverse community and make Steiner education more accessible across the community. The program offers a limited number of subsidised places to eligible applicants who would otherwise not have the opportunity to enrol in the school due to financial disadvantages.
description: Each scholarship provides a subsidy on tuition fees for eligible students for the duration of their enrolment at Dandenong Ranges Steiner School while their family is experiencing financial disadvantage. The exact financial value of the subsidy is determined on a case-by-case basis and is not specified as a fixed percentage or amount. In most cases, sundry charges such as camps and excursions will remain the responsibility of parents/guardians; however, holders of Pension/Concession Cards can access additional government funding support for costs associated with these activities. The total number of scholarships awarded each year depends on the number of applications at each year level, the financial impact, and the social and educational impact on individual classes and the school. Scholarships are subject to annual review, and while it is expected that scholarships are awarded until the completion of Class 6, DRSS may choose to award a scholarship for a shorter period.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be eligible for enrolment at Dandenong Ranges Steiner School; Provide evidence of financial disadvantage (e.g., a valid government concession card such as Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card, or other evidence of financial disadvantage); A commitment to the principles of Steiner education and Dandenong Ranges Steiner School is expected from parents/guardians of applicants; Open to all year levels (Prep through Class 6)
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to review each year. It is expected that scholarships are awarded until the completion of Class 6, however, DRSS may choose to award a scholarship for a shorter period. Holders of scholarships will be bound by the same rules and responsibilities and awarded the same rights as other students at DRSS. Assessment of eligibility and offer of a scholarship is solely at the discretion of DRSS. All applications will be treated strictly confidentially. Applicants meeting the eligibility criteria will be invited to meet with the Class Teacher to learn more about the school, and for the school to determine if it can meet the needs of the child.

school_name: Dandenong Ranges Steiner School - School Road Campus
overview: Dandenong Ranges Steiner School offers a Scholarship Program to facilitate an inclusive and diverse community and make Steiner education more available across the community. The program offers limited subsidised places to eligible applicants who would otherwise not have the opportunity to enrol due to financial disadvantage.
description: The Scholarship Program provides a subsidy on tuition fees for eligible students for the duration of their enrolment at Dandenong Ranges Steiner School while their family is experiencing financial disadvantage. The financial benefit is tuition fee subsidy, with the amount varying depending on individual circumstances and school assessment. Applicants must provide evidence of financial disadvantage through valid government concession cards (Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card) or other evidence. The selection process includes submission of the DRSS Scholarship Application Form, confidential assessment by the Finance Committee, and an interview with the Class Teacher to determine if the school can meet the child's needs. Scholarships are subject to annual review and are expected to be awarded until the completion of Class 6, although DRSS may choose to award for a shorter period. In most cases, sundry charges such as camps and excursions remain the responsibility of parents/guardians; however, holders of Pension/Concession Cards can access additional government funding support for these costs.
eligibility_criteria: Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Must be eligible for enrolment at Dandenong Ranges Steiner School; Must provide evidence of financial disadvantage via valid government concession card (Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card) or other evidence of financial disadvantage; Must demonstrate commitment to principles of Steiner education and DRSS from parents/guardians; Applies to Australian citizens and permanent residents only
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to review each year. It is expected that scholarships are awarded until the completion of Class 6, however, DRSS may choose to award a scholarship for a shorter period. Holders of scholarships will be bound by the same rules and responsibilities and awarded the same rights as other students at DRSS. Assessment of eligibility and offer of a scholarship is solely at the discretion of DRSS. A commitment to the principles of Steiner education and to DRSS is expected from parents/carers of applicants. All applications will be strictly confidential. The total number of scholarships awarded depends on the number of applications at each year level, the financial impact, and the social and educational impact on individual classes and the school.

school_name: Dandenong High School-Myuna Secondary Teaching Unit
Dandenong High School Burremah Grassmere Annexe

overview: Daylesford Dharma School offers a limited number of full tuition scholarships for the 2026 school year. This newly established scholarship program is designed to support families seeking an education based on Buddhist principles and the Australian Curriculum.
description: Daylesford Dharma School has a limited amount of full tuition scholarships available for the 2026 school year through its newly established scholarship program. The scholarship provides Full Fee Remission and covers the complete tuition cost for the duration of enrolment. Applications are reviewed by the Principal through a confidential discussion process where families can inquire about scholarship eligibility and options. Recipients must demonstrate alignment with the school's Buddhist philosophy and commitment to the school's core values of kindness, compassion, and wisdom. The selection process involves direct contact with the Principal to discuss individual circumstances and educational goals.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families seeking enrolment at Daylesford Dharma School; Families must contact the Principal to discuss eligibility; Demonstrated commitment to the school's Buddhist philosophy and values; Open to all year levels from Foundation to Year 6
special_conditions: Limited scholarships available. Applicants must contact the Principal for a confidential discussion about scholarship options. Scholarship subject to ongoing alignment with school values and satisfactory engagement with the school community.

overview: Daylesford Dharma School offers bursaries for families experiencing financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring accessibility for all families whose values align with the school's Buddhist educational philosophy.
description: Daylesford Dharma School provides flexible bursary arrangements for families experiencing financial hardship who wish to enrol their child. The bursary program offers Partial Fee Remission and fee support assistance based on individual family circumstances. The school conducts a confidential assessment process where families can discuss their financial situation and explore flexible arrangements with the Principal. As an independent, not-for-profit institution, the school is dedicated to ensuring that families can access its unique curriculum regardless of economic circumstances. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and genuine commitment to the school's educational approach.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial hardship; Must demonstrate genuine commitment to school values and Buddhist philosophy; Families must contact Principal for confidential discussion; Open to all year levels from Foundation to Year 6; Income assessment may be required
special_conditions: Bursaries arranged on case-by-case basis. Families must contact the Principal for a confidential discussion about financial support options. School reserves the right to review and adjust support based on circumstances. Not-for-profit institution committed to accessibility.

overview: Daylesford Dharma School offers a concession fee level for families holding a low-income concession card. This program is part of the school's commitment to ensuring educational access for all families.
description: The Dharma School maintains a two-tier fee structure with a concession fee level available to all families holding a valid low-income concession card. The concession fee represents a significant fee reduction on the standard full fee and applies for the duration of enrolment. Families are required to provide documentation of their concession card status during enrolment. This fee option is available across all year levels and operates alongside the school's other financial support programs. The concession rate reflects the school's not-for-profit status and commitment to keeping education accessible to families from diverse socioeconomic backgrounds.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid low-income concession card; Available to all families meeting concession card criteria; Open to all year levels from Foundation to Year 6; Must provide proof of concession card status
special_conditions: Requires valid low-income concession card documentation. Fee applies for duration of enrolment. School may request updated documentation as required.

overview: Daylesford Dharma School offers a 10% sibling discount to families with multiple children enrolled at the school, with additional discounts available for families with three or more children.
description: The school provides a 10% fee discount on tuition for the second and subsequent siblings enrolled at Dharma School. Families with three or more children receive further sibling discounts beyond the standard 10% reduction. The discount applies for each child's entire duration of enrolment at the school. This program recognises the financial commitment of families with multiple children and helps reduce the overall cost of education. Discounts are applied automatically at enrolment and continue throughout the student's schooling, making education more accessible for larger families.
eligibility_criteria: Second and subsequent siblings must be enrolled at Daylesford Dharma School; 10% discount applies per eligible sibling; Families with three or more children receive additional discounts; Available for all year levels from Foundation to Year 6
special_conditions: Discount applies per additional sibling enrolled. Families with three or more children receive further discounts beyond 10%. Automatically applied upon enrolment.

school_name: De La Salle College - Kinnoull Campus
David Scott School - High Street Campus
Darul Ulum College of Victoria - Darul Ulum Academy
Diamond Valley Special Developmental School - Whittlesea Campus

overview: Dimboola Memorial Secondary College supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students through the Marrung Education Scholarship program. The scholarship recognises students who have shown outstanding excellence in their commitment to their education, Aboriginal community and forging the path of their future.
description: The Marrung Education Scholarship program provides financial support to Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students to complete Years 11 and 12. The program offers 30 scholarships across Victoria and recognises students who demonstrate a high potential to succeed in their chosen pathway. Recipients are selected based on their commitment to education, involvement in their Aboriginal community, and dedication to forging their future pathway. The scholarship is designed to provide assistance to support students with their studies through to the end of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Completing Years 11 and 12; Demonstrated commitment to education; Active involvement in Aboriginal community; High potential to succeed in chosen pathway
special_conditions: Recipients must remain enrolled at school for completion of Years 11 and 12. Scholarship is awarded through external selection process.

overview: Dimboola Memorial Secondary College students can access the MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Scholarship. This scholarship supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students with their studies through to the completion of Year 12.
description: The MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Scholarship is administered by MADEC, a Mildura-based organisation, and provides financial assistance to Indigenous students. Eligible students must demonstrate excellent attendance, strong work ethic at school, good academic results, and an important desire to complete Year 12. Selection involves a written application and interview with a MADEC representative. The scholarship provides support for students to continue their studies through to the completion of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Excellent attendance record; Strong work ethic at school; Good academic results; Commitment to complete Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on written application and interview with MADEC representative. Scholarship provides support until completion of Year 12. At least five DMSC students have been recipients of this scholarship.

overview: Dimboola Memorial Secondary College offers German exchange and scholarship opportunities to students studying German at middle and senior school levels. Students are encouraged to participate in exchange programs including a ten-week exchange program.
description: Students studying German in middle and senior school are encouraged to take advantage of scholarship and exchange opportunities available through the school's German program. The program includes a ten-week exchange program opportunity, allowing students to engage in cultural exchange and international learning. While specific financial details are not explicitly stated on the school website, the exchange program represents a significant educational opportunity for German language students.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in German program; Middle or senior school year level; Academic standing in German studies
special_conditions: Participation in ten-week exchange program. Program details available through the school's German language department.

school_name: Djerriwarrh Community & Education Services - Djerriwarrh Community College

overview: Doncaster Secondary College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program as a pathway for high-ability students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The program is designed to challenge gifted learners and provide opportunities for them to achieve their full potential through an enriched and accelerated curriculum.
description: The SEAL program at Doncaster Secondary College is designed to meet the needs of gifted and high-ability learners in Years 7 to Year 9. In Junior school, Year 7 and Year 8 SEAL classes explore the Victorian Curriculum while engaging in high-order thinking skills, inquiry-based learning, community-based projects and leadership opportunities. In Year 9, SEAL students complete core subjects together but mix with mainstream students in their electives. In Year 10, SEAL students with consistently high results are considered for Extension Math and/or Extension English classes, and have the opportunity to engage in VCE advancement through enrolment in one or more VCE subjects. SEAL students undertake an enhanced curriculum containing extended tasks, activities and content aimed at providing students with the opportunity to explore material in considerable depth. The curriculum covers content at a faster rate than in mainstream classes, and includes different range of texts in English and tasks that focus on stimulating critical thinking, problem-solving and creativity. No tuition fee reduction or remission is offered as this is a government school. The program is offered for the duration of Years 7 to Year 9 (or potential extension to Year 10). Selection is through an entrance examination based on aptitude testing.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 7 for entry; Must have received confirmed enrolment offer at Doncaster Secondary College; Must be currently residing in Victoria; May only continue in program following Term 3 2026 applications
special_conditions: Applications for the Doncaster Secondary College Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program for 2027 will open at the beginning of Term 3 2026. Further information regarding the SEAL application process for Year 7 2027 families will be provided in the Year 7 Transition Book which will be emailed to families once a student's enrolment is confirmed. A SEAL Program Information Night is hosted in August for prospective families. This is a selective entry program, not a financial scholarship. No fee remission or financial benefit is offered.

school_name: Donvale Christian College - Hall Road Campus
overview: Donvale Christian College offers a Sibling Discount Program to families with multiple children attending the college concurrently. This program provides tiered tuition fee reductions as an alternative to traditional scholarships, with no tuition charged for the fourth or subsequent children.
description: Donvale Christian College provides sibling discounts to all families with multiple children attending the college at the same time. The discount structure reduces tuition fees progressively for each additional child enrolled. No tuition fee is charged for the fourth or subsequent children attending concurrently. This program is designed to make multi-child education more affordable and is the primary financial assistance offered by the college, as the school explicitly does not run a traditional scholarship program. The discount applies to tuition fees only and is automatically applied to families with eligible children. The maximum tuition fees payable per family for 2026 is $29,905 (excluding other charges), with further reductions available for families with four or more children enrolled.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple children attending college concurrently; Open to all families; Applies to second child onwards; No tuition charged for fourth or subsequent children
special_conditions: This is the only financial assistance program offered by the college. The school explicitly states it does not run a scholarship program. Sibling discounts apply automatically to eligible families with multiple children enrolled concurrently. No application process is required.

overview: Donvale Christian College provides sibling discounts to all families with multiple children attending the College concurrently. The program recognises the commitment of families by progressively reducing tuition fees for additional children in the family.
description: Donvale Christian College offers a structured sibling discount system for families with multiple children enrolled concurrently. The 1st child receives 0% discount on tuition fees. The 2nd child receives 20% discount on tuition fees. The 3rd child receives 40% discount on tuition fees. The 4th and all subsequent children receive 100% discount, meaning no tuition fee is charged. These discounts apply across all year levels (Prep to Year 12) and reduce the maximum tuition fees payable per family. The discount structure is designed to make education more accessible for families with multiple siblings at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Families with multiple children attending the College concurrently; Children must be biological siblings (discretion available for step-siblings, foster children or other family members); All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Sibling discounts are offered for biological siblings, and the College has discretion to extend similar discounts to step-siblings, foster children or other family members. The discount is subject to annual review and may be amended as required by the College.

overview: Donvale Christian College may provide financial assistance to families engaged in paid ministry work. This discretionary program recognizes families serving in Christian ministry contexts and offers fee support on a case-by-case basis.
description: Donvale Christian College provides discretionary financial assistance for families engaged in paid ministry work to help pay for school fees. Such arrangements are subject to review and amendment on an annual basis or more frequently if required by the College. The fact that financial assistance has been provided in the past does not guarantee ongoing financial assistance or the repeat of any arrangements. Applications are assessed individually and approved at the College's discretion. The amount and terms of assistance may vary depending on individual circumstances and the College's capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Families engaged in paid ministry work; Subject to College discretion and individual assessment; Arrangements subject to annual review
special_conditions: Financial assistance is provided at the sole discretion of the College. Arrangements are subject to review and amendment on an annual basis or more frequently as required. Previous financial assistance does not guarantee continued or repeated assistance. All assistance is conditional upon the College's financial capacity and strategic priorities.

overview: Drouin Secondary College offers the Year 7 Accelerated Pathways Program for Grade 6 students with demonstrated academic aptitude and a passion for learning. The program provides accelerated learning opportunities for high-achieving students transitioning into Year 7.
description: The Accelerated Pathways Program (APP) is designed for Grade 6 students with strong academic ability who wish to pursue an accelerated learning pathway at Drouin Secondary College. This is a selective program for students demonstrating exceptional academic aptitude and commitment to their studies. Students in the APP undertake an enriched and accelerated curriculum that challenges high-achieving learners. The program continues from Year 7 onwards, providing ongoing academic extension and support. Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance, learning motivation, and suitability for accelerated study. The program is offered for the duration of enrolment from Year 7 through Year 12, providing students with extended learning opportunities aligned with their high academic abilities.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate academic aptitude; Must demonstrate passion for learning; Selection based on academic performance and learning motivation
special_conditions: This is a selective program requiring students to meet academic criteria and demonstrate high levels of learning commitment.

overview: Drouin Secondary College offers VET programs as accelerated learning pathways for students in Years 10, 11 and 12. VET enables students to combine vocational qualifications with their secondary studies while gaining nationally recognised qualifications.
description: VET is an accelerated learning program offered at Drouin Secondary College for Year 10, 11 and 12 students who demonstrate maturity and high expectations in their learning and behaviour. Students undertake vocational qualifications through registered training organisations such as TAFE while completing their secondary studies. The program allows students to gain both a VCE and a nationally recognised VET qualification, with Certificate III courses being equivalent to VCE subjects and Certificate II courses equivalent to Year 10 level studies. Students must display maturity and maintain high academic and behavioural standards to remain in the program. VET units have equal status with other VCE units and some courses offer scored assessment contributing to ATAR. The program is designed for students managing significant additional workload while maintaining their mainstream schoolwork.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 10, 11 or 12; Must demonstrate maturity; Must maintain high expectations with learning and behaviour; Must be able to manage additional workload alongside regular schoolwork
special_conditions: VET is not an easy course to complete. Students will miss one lesson of three different subjects per week and must keep up to date with both VET and regular schoolwork. Year 11 VCE VM students must complete minimum 180 hours and one day of work placement per week is compulsory. Work placement is required and Department of Education paperwork must be completed.

overview: Drouin Secondary College's Blackwood Annexe is a twelve-month engagement program based in Hallora for students at risk of disengaging from their education. The program provides alternative pathways for Year 7-10 students from the Gippsland area experiencing educational challenges.
description: The Blackwood Annexe program is a unique, dynamic and positive small school environment designed for students with complex learning needs to re-engage with their education. Enrolment is available to Year 7-10 students from the Gippsland area who are at risk of dropping out of school or experiencing disengagement in their learning. The program is a twelve-month engagement program based in Hallora with a maximum group size of 20 peers, offering smaller class sizes and differentiated learning tailored to individual student needs. Blackwood's structure, curriculum and purpose focus first on building each student's capacity to engage and then nurturing their willingness to learn. The program provides literacy and numeracy support aligned with Victorian Curriculum outcomes, focusing on core skills to support student transition back into mainstream programs or movement into applied pathways. Students in the program develop personal growth and capacity to be key contributors in their own learning.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 7-10; Must be from Gippsland area; Must be at risk of disengagement or dropping out of school; Selection through referral process based on complex learning needs
special_conditions: This is a selective program based on referral. Students are selected for the program through identified need. Maximum group size is 20 peers per year to ensure differentiated learning and personal support.

overview: Dromana Secondary College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement in their chosen subject. The scholarship supports talented students with a strong record of academic performance and who make meaningful contributions to their school or wider community.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded to incoming Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding academic performance and strong commitment to learning in their chosen subject. Recipients receive either a $350 Laptop Voucher or assistance with a chosen Enhancement Program or Instrumental Music Tuition in Year 7. The selection process requires an interview where applicants present their current school report, teacher endorsement, and a portfolio demonstrating broader school and community involvement. Applicants must submit a half-page written summary showing how they have excelled in their chosen subject and a one-page response explaining how they demonstrate the school's values (Respect, Responsibility, Integrity, Personal Best) in their everyday life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be enrolled in Dromana Secondary College for Year 7 2025; Strong record of academic performance in chosen subject; Demonstrated contributions to school or wider community; Must have received acceptance letter from Dromana College
special_conditions: Recipients have the choice of either a $350 Laptop Voucher, or assistance with a chosen Enhancement program or Instrumental Music Tuition in Year 7. Applicants must bring current school report, teacher endorsement, and portfolio to interview. Interview can be rescheduled by phoning 5987 2805.

overview: Dromana Secondary College offers the Curriculum Enhancement Scholarship to recognise students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding academic performance and contribute significantly to the wider school and local community. This scholarship supports all-round excellence across academic and co-curricular endeavours.
description: The Curriculum Enhancement Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding academic performance and active contributions to the wider school and local community. Recipients receive assistance with either a chosen Enhancement Program or Instrumental Music Tuition in Year 7. Selection is based on an interview process where applicants must present their current school report, teacher endorsement, and a portfolio showing broader school and community involvement. Applicants must submit a written summary demonstrating performance and excellence in their chosen area and a one-page response describing how they understand and live the school's values (Respect, Responsibility, Integrity, Personal Best) in their daily life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be enrolled in Dromana Secondary College for Year 7 2025; Outstanding academic performance; Active contribution to school and local community involvement; Must have received acceptance letter from Dromana College
special_conditions: Recipients have the choice of assistance with either a chosen Enhancement program or Instrumental Music Tuition in Year 7. Interview format includes presentation of school report, teacher endorsement, and portfolio. Interview can be rescheduled by contacting school on 5987 2805.

overview: Dromana Secondary College's LEAP program provides advanced accelerated learning opportunities for a select group of high-ability students in Years 7-12. The program upwardly differentiates the curriculum to support students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential and commitment to their learning.
description: LEAP is an accelerated learning program designed to provide both challenges and opportunities for high-ability students through upwardly differentiated curriculum. Students in LEAP undertake enriched curriculum with assessments specifically targeted to high-ability learners, including undertaking Year 8 work in Year 7. LEAP students engage in cross-curricular projects, participate in two LEAP Challenge Days annually, access Year 10/VCE studies within the Wednesday F Block, study two languages, and receive mentorship from Year 10 LEAP students. In VCE, LEAP students complete six or more Unit 3 & 4 subjects and access university enhancement subjects through Monash University (Accounting, Biology, Biomedical Science, Computational Thinking, Chemistry, Mathematics, Mobile Applications Development, Physics). Registration opens March 11, 2025 and closes August 8, 2025; entrance exam held online August 23, 2025. An examination fee of $137.50 is required after registration.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-ability students; Demonstrated exceptional academic potential; Level of maturity and commitment to learning; Must sit entrance exam for selection; Based on exam results and registration, students offered place in LEAP for 2026; Registered applicants only; Current residence in Victoria recommended
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: LEAP students must maintain outstanding academic work ethic and challenge themselves to improve areas of weakness. Students must exemplify school values: Respect, Responsibility, Integrity, Personal Best. Participation in enrichment opportunities expected. Students receive Year 10 LEAP mentors in Year 7. Continuation into Year 9 requires invitation to access Extension Maths and/or Extension English. All Year 7-9 LEAP students must study at least one language (Indonesian or Japanese). VCE students expected to complete minimum six Unit 3 & 4 VCE subjects. Students encouraged to pursue university scholarships and bursaries.

overview: Drouin Secondary College offers the Accelerated Pathways Program (APP) for Year 7 students who demonstrate advanced academic ability. The program is designed for very capable, committed, enthusiastic students with academic skills at least six months ahead of their expected level. Students receive a cohesive, accelerated curriculum in Year 7 with ongoing flexible acceleration pathways through Year 8-12.
description: The Year 7 Accelerated Pathways Program (7APP) is designed to offer a cohesive program for learners who have been identified as very capable, committed, enthusiastic students with academic skills at least six months ahead of their expected level. The APP provides a complete and flexible pathway for selected students beginning in Year 7. After completing Year 7 APP, students will have a managed individual pathway with access to acceleration in their individual areas of strength from Year 8 to Year 12. The program is non-monetary, offering educational acceleration rather than financial assistance. Selection requires teacher recommendation and identification of advanced academic ability and demonstrated commitment to learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students entering Year 7; Academic skills at least six months ahead of expected level; Identified as very capable learner; Demonstrated commitment and enthusiasm for learning; Application required
special_conditions: After Year 7 APP completion, students have access to managed individual pathways with acceleration in areas of individual strength from Year 8 to Year 12. Participation is based on identification of advanced capability and commitment to learning.

overview: Drouin Secondary College provides access to the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) for eligible families. This government-funded program assists eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. CSEF is available to families holding valid means-tested concession cards or temporary foster parents.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government program that provides financial assistance to eligible families to cover costs of school camps, sports activities, excursions, and uniform expenses. Eligible families include those holding a valid Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Gold Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or temporary foster parents. From 2026, secondary students receive $400 per year to be applied to school accounts for eligible expenses. Applications must be completed annually. The funding is credited directly to the student's school account and allocated towards camps, sports, excursions and uniform costs.
eligibility_criteria: Hold valid means-tested concession card (Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Gold Card, or Pensioner Concession Card); OR be temporary foster parent; Application required annually; Concession card details must match Centrelink records
special_conditions: Applications must be completed and returned to the school office annually with a copy of concession card. CSEF is not payable to students attending pre-school, kindergarten, home schooled, or TAFE. Families are required to apply each year to be eligible. From January 1, 2026, the secondary student rate increased to $400 per year. Applications can be emailed with clear photo or scan of completed application and concession card to drouin.sc@education.vic.gov.au.

overview: Drouin Secondary College offers a Sustainable School Shop program providing access to second-hand uniforms and school materials at reduced cost. This initiative supports families seeking to manage school expenses through access to pre-owned uniforms and books via a dedicated online platform.
description: The Sustainable School Shop provides families with an opportunity to purchase second-hand uniforms and books at reduced prices through an online subscription service. The school-funded initiative at Drouin Secondary College makes the Sustainable School Shop subscription available to families seeking cost-effective options for school uniforms and materials. Year 7 CSEF recipients are eligible for additional uniform vouchers through the Victorian Government School Support Register (SSR) Uniform Package. Access to the Sustainable School Shop is available online through the dedicated website, offering sustainability and cost-saving benefits for families.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Year 7 CSEF recipients eligible for additional SSR Uniform Package; Online access available to purchase second-hand uniforms and books
special_conditions: Access through online subscription at sustainableschoolshop.com.au. Year 7 students who are CSEF recipients are also eligible for uniform vouchers through the School Support Register (SSR). Families register interest for CSEF and SSR programs at the school office.

school_name: Dunkeld Consolidated School Glenthompson Primary School

overview: Eaglehawk North Primary School presents the Peter Krenz Scholarship each year to two Grade 5 students who have demonstrated excellence in all of their school endeavours. The scholarship recognises students who display qualities of excellence and participation across their academic and co-curricular pursuits.
description: The Peter Krenz Scholarship is presented annually to two Grade 5 students who have demonstrated excellence in all aspects of their school endeavours. Peter Krenz was Area Manager of the Ministry of Housing and was better known as CEO of the various Bendigo District YMCA's, with the Peter Krenz Leisure Centre named in his honour. The scholarship was established following Peter's passing in 2007, aged 51, by his wife Kerry who remains dedicated to supporting the community. Recipients are selected based on their demonstration of excellence across academic, co-curricular, and community participation. The awards are presented at the end-of-year Grade 5 Graduation Ceremony. No specific monetary value is disclosed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 5; Demonstrated excellence in all school endeavours; Display qualities of excellence and participation in school community
special_conditions: Presented annually at end-of-year Grade 5 Graduation Ceremony. Award is presented by the scholarship founder's family member.

overview: Eaglehawk North Primary School presents the A.D Ferguson Award each year to two Grade 6 students who have demonstrated academic excellence and active participation in the school community in a variety of ways. The award was established through a bequest by the Ferguson family to commemorate Andrew David Ferguson.
description: The A.D Ferguson Award was bequeathed by the Ferguson family to Eaglehawk North Primary School to commemorate Andrew David Ferguson. Andrew was born in Scotland and came to Australia in the 1950s, where he became a teacher working in north-eastern Victoria and at Golden Square High School, teaching Woodwork and Music. He passed away in 1997 and left a legacy to two schools that were important to his family: Golden Square High School and Eaglehawk North Primary School. The award is presented each year to two Grade 6 students who have demonstrated academic excellence and active participation in the school community in a variety of ways. Andrew's son Scott attends the end-of-year Grade 6 Graduation Ceremony to present the award. No specific monetary value is disclosed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Demonstrated academic excellence; Demonstrated active participation in school community in a variety of ways
special_conditions: Presented annually at end-of-year Grade 6 Graduation Ceremony. Award is presented by family member of the award founder (Andrew's son Scott).

program_name: ALPHA (Advanced Learning Program for High Achievers)
overview: East Doncaster Secondary College offers the ALPHA program to recognise and cater for high potential learners and gifted and talented students. The program enables like-minded, highly motivated students to study one year ahead of their peers in a challenging and supportive learning environment from Year 7 to Year 9.
description: ALPHA (Advanced Learning Program for High Achievers) is an accelerated program designed for students who demonstrate advanced academic development, a capacity for fast-paced learning and higher level achievement across academic disciplines. Students in ALPHA study one year ahead of other students until the end of Year 9. The curriculum is delivered at a fast pace with topics covered in greater depth across all subjects. Selection involves a rigorous assessment process during Grade 6 testing in numeracy, literacy, writing, logic and reasoning, complemented by a student profile developed with the cooperation of the student's primary school teacher. The ALPHA curriculum is an enhancement of the Victorian Essential Learning Standards, maximising the individual's ability to utilise their thinking skills. Students who are selected will be enrolled in ALPHA classes for Years 7, 8 and 9. This program is offered to Year 6 students transitioning to Year 7 only. As a government school, East Doncaster Secondary College does not charge tuition fees for Australian residents, so there is no financial cost associated with ALPHA participation beyond standard parent contribution payments.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students transitioning to Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Grade 6; Must have submitted enrolment application and been offered enrolment at East Doncaster Secondary College; Student's legal name and residential address must match enrolling name and address; Must meet school's general enrolment criteria; High performance capability in specific academic disciplines or general high achievement across numeracy, literacy and logic and reasoning
special_conditions: Students must apply for, be offered an enrolment, accept the enrolment and meet the enrolment criteria at East Doncaster Secondary College before being eligible to sit the ALPHA test. Late applications will not be accepted. No refunds will be issued should an applicant be deemed ineligible to apply or ineligible to enrol at the College. Test registrations close Friday 5 September 2025, with the test held on Saturday 13 September 2025. Families will be contacted late October regarding ALPHA placement. Students accepted into ALPHA will study one year ahead of their peers until the end of Year 9. ALPHA classes are supplemented by opportunities for students to become involved in other co-curricular programs including sports teams, drama, the Music Program, debating and student leadership. Students are invited to undertake assessment in numeracy, literacy, writing, logic and reasoning. After Year 9, ALPHA students continue in the regular secondary program but have access to advanced English and Maths courses in Year 10 and the full range of VCE subjects in Years 11 and 12.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers a scholarship program to support students who aspire to further their education. The program provides financial assistance to eligible students to cover school fees, camps, excursions, school uniforms, and other educational materials. The scholarship program is funded by community donations and enables students to access educational opportunities that might otherwise be unavailable due to financial barriers.
description: ECG Secondary College's scholarship program provides financial assistance to eligible students for a range of educational expenses including school fees, camps, excursions, school jumpers, and stationery. The scholarship recognizes that not every student has the resources to complete their studies or progress to further education. The program is designed to remove financial barriers that prevent students from engaging fully in their education. The exact financial value varies depending on individual student need and available funding. The selection process involves assessment of individual circumstances and demonstrated financial need. The scholarship funding is contributed towards eligible students throughout their enrollment at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to ECG Secondary College students; Demonstrated financial need; Currently enrolled at the college; Application required through college
special_conditions: Scholarship funding is contributed towards eligible students for school fees, camps, excursions, school jumpers, stationery and related educational expenses. Students must maintain enrollment at ECG Secondary College to retain the scholarship.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers Start-Up Kits to provide direct financial assistance to students. These kits provide $500 to support students in meeting their individual goals, including facilitating their transition from school into the workforce, overcoming social anxiety, and supporting overall student wellbeing.
description: Start-Up kits are designed to provide students with direct financial assistance of $500 to support them in meeting their specific goals and challenges. The assistance is intended to facilitate a student's transition from school into the workforce, help overcome social anxiety and enable students to connect meaningfully with their community, and support overall student wellbeing and personal development. The funding can be applied to costs that directly support these outcomes. The selection process involves identifying students with demonstrated need in these areas. Kits are awarded based on individual assessment and available funding.
eligibility_criteria: Open to ECG Secondary College students; Demonstrated need in one or more target areas (transition to workforce, social anxiety support, wellbeing); Currently enrolled at the college
special_conditions: Start-Up kits provide $500 in direct financial assistance. The assistance is designed to address specific student needs including workforce transition, social connection and wellbeing support.

overview: ECG Secondary College provides transport assistance to students who face barriers to education due to location or financial constraints. The program aims to provide reliable and safe transport options to enable students to access their study location. This is particularly important for students whose educational journey is hindered by lack of access to affordable transport.
description: The Transport Assistance Program addresses a significant barrier to education by providing reliable and safe transport options for students to reach their study location. The program recognizes that for some students, particularly those living out of home or without family support, a lack of access and affordability to transport can prevent school attendance and engagement. The program is designed to ensure that students can access their education regardless of their location or financial circumstances. Funding is contributed to eligible students based on individual need assessment. The selection process considers student circumstances and demonstrated transport barriers.
eligibility_criteria: Open to ECG Secondary College students; Demonstrated transport-related barriers to education; Location or financial constraints affecting access to school; Currently enrolled at the college
special_conditions: Transport assistance is designed to provide reliable and safe transport options. The goal is to ensure students can access their study location consistently. Assistance is provided to students facing location or financial barriers to education.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers a financial hardship application process for students experiencing exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. This program provides direct support to students who are unable to meet essential educational costs. The application process allows students and families to access assistance when facing unexpected financial challenges.
description: The Financial Hardship Application program is designed for students currently living with exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. The program recognizes that some students face unexpected or severe financial challenges that prevent them from continuing their education. The application process allows for individual assessment of circumstances and consideration of appropriate financial support. Assistance can be directed towards essential educational costs including school fees, materials, and other necessary expenses. The selection process involves submission of an application form with details of the financial hardship situation. Funding is allocated based on available resources and demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to ECG Secondary College students; Currently experiencing exceptional circumstances resulting in genuine financial hardship; Must provide documentation of hardship; Currently enrolled at the college
special_conditions: Students must be currently experiencing exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. Applications require documentation and assessment of the hardship situation. The college reserves discretion in determining the level of assistance provided based on available funding.

overview: ECG Secondary College recognizes student achievement through a donor awards program. The college presents annual donor awards across its multiple campuses to students who have demonstrated hard work, determination, and excellence. These awards are made possible through generous community donations and support from sponsors.
description: ECG Secondary College presents annual donor awards to recognize student achievement and excellence. The awards program includes the presentation of 18 donor awards across the college's Warragul, Pakenham, Leongatha, and Dandenong campuses. The awards reflect the hard work and determination of students within the ECG community. Donors and sponsors contribute to these awards, which recognize various forms of student achievement. The awards are presented at the annual CCG Awards Night event alongside scholarships and other recognitions. The selection process involves identification of students who have demonstrated meritorious performance during the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to ECG Secondary College students across all campuses; Demonstrated hard work and commitment to studies; Determination and perseverance; Assessment by college staff
special_conditions: Donor awards are presented annually at the CCG Awards Night. Awards are made possible through generous donations from community members and organizations. Each award reflects the hard work and determination of individual students within the college community.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers financial hardship assistance to currently enrolled students experiencing exceptional financial circumstances. The application form allows students to apply for support when facing genuine financial difficulties that impact their ability to access education.
description: ECG Secondary College provides financial assistance to students living with exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. Eligible students can access support via a dedicated application form available through the school's documents page. The assistance is designed to help students overcome barriers to education caused by financial constraints, enabling them to remain engaged in their studies. Assessment of eligibility is conducted on a case-by-case basis, with support determined by the severity and nature of the financial hardship claimed.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students; Experiencing exceptional circumstances resulting in genuine financial hardship; Must complete and submit the Financial Hardship Assistance application form
special_conditions: Application must demonstrate genuine financial hardship with exceptional circumstances. Applicants must be currently enrolled students at ECG Secondary College.

overview: ECG Secondary College provides Start-Up Kit funding of $500 to eligible students to support them in achieving their goals in career transition, social connection, and wellbeing. The program is designed to empower students and facilitate their transition from school into the workforce.
description: The Start-Up Kit Program provides financial assistance of $500 to eligible ECG Secondary College students designed to support multiple student needs and goals. The funding is allocated to support student transitions from school into the workforce, helping students facilitate career readiness and job placement. The program also assists students to overcome social anxiety and enable them to connect meaningfully with their community, grow their confidence and connections. Additionally, the program empowers students to take charge of their wellbeing through tailored support. Start-Up Kit funding can support various student aspirations and life goals aligned with the school's commitment to holistic student development.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students at ECG Secondary College; Students seeking support for career transition and workforce readiness; Students wishing to overcome social anxiety and build community connections; Students focused on personal wellbeing development
special_conditions: Each Start-Up Kit is allocated $500 and is designed to support specific student goals in career transition, social development, and wellbeing. Funding is provided by generous community donors.

overview: ECG Secondary College provides transport assistance to students for whom access and affordability to transport is a barrier to education. The program aims to provide reliable and safe transport options for students to reach the Dandenong campus and other study locations.
description: ECG Secondary College recognises that lack of access and affordability to transport can be a significant barrier to accessing education. The Transport Assistance Program is designed to provide reliable and safe transport options for students to reach their study location. This program specifically addresses transportation challenges faced by students, particularly those in the Dandenong and Melbourne's southeast area who rely on alternative transport solutions. The program offers solutions for students who can access public transport to campus, ensuring equitable access to education regardless of financial constraints or location.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students at ECG Secondary College; Students for whom transport is identified as a barrier to education; Students located in areas with limited transport infrastructure; Students facing financial hardship related to transport costs
special_conditions: Program is designed to support students for whom transport is a documented barrier to accessing education. Support is tailored to individual student circumstances and needs.

program_name: Scholarship Program - School Fees and Educational Costs
overview: ECG Secondary College offers scholarship funding to eligible students to support access to education and reduce financial barriers. Scholarship funds are contributed towards eligible students for school fees, camps, excursions, school uniforms, and stationery.
description: ECG Secondary College's Scholarship Program provides funding to eligible students designed to help them aspire to further their career goals and complete their education. Scholarship funding is specifically allocated to cover educational costs including school fees, camps, excursions, school jumpers, and stationery. The program recognises that not every student has the resources to complete their studies or progress to further education. By funding these essential educational expenses, the scholarship program removes financial barriers and enables students to maintain focus on their learning. The program is supported by community donors and provides non-repayable assistance to eligible students. Scholarships are awarded based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to education.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students at ECG Secondary College; Students demonstrating financial need; Students aspiring to further education and career development; Students requiring support for school fees, camps, excursions, and educational materials
special_conditions: Scholarships are provided as non-repayable grants funded by community donors. Awards are based on demonstrated financial need and educational commitment. Recipients must maintain good standing at ECG Secondary College.

overview: ECG Secondary College recognises student achievement through annual Donor Awards presented across all four campuses. These awards, funded by generous community donors and sponsors, celebrate students who have demonstrated meritorious performance and exceptional dedication during the academic year.
description: ECG Secondary College presents Donor Awards annually to recognise and celebrate student achievement across all four campuses including Dandenong, Pakenham, Leongatha, and Warragul. The 2024 awards ceremony presented 18 donor awards alongside three scholarships, sponsorships, RTO awards, Learn Local Awards, Industry Partner Award, and Community Builder Award. Each award reflects hard work, determination, and commitment demonstrated by students throughout the academic year. Awards are funded by generous community sponsors including Gippsland Forest Lodge, Freemasons Foundation Victoria, South East LLEN, Commonwealth Bank, and other local businesses. The awards are presented at an annual Awards Night ceremony that celebrates the achievements of the broader CCG community.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students at ECG Secondary College; Students demonstrating meritorious performance during the academic year; Students showing exceptional dedication and commitment to their studies and personal development
special_conditions: Awards are presented annually at the CCG Awards Night ceremony. Eligibility is based on staff and sponsor nomination of students demonstrating exceptional achievement and dedication. Awards are funded by community donors and sponsors.

overview: Echuca College offers scholarships to high achieving students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. This scholarship recognises students with outstanding performance across academic subjects.
description: Echuca College provides academic scholarships for Year 7 entry to support high-performing students. The scholarship programme aims to support students who excel in their academic studies and show commitment to learning. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement. Specific financial value and duration details are referenced in the 2020 Scholarship Booklet. Scholarship holders are expected to maintain high academic standards throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated academic excellence; High achieving students from Year 6
special_conditions: Scholarship information is contained in the Scholarship Booklet available from the school. Interested families should contact the school for current scholarship details and application procedures.

overview: Echuca College offers scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and achievement. The scholarship recognises excellence on the sporting field and commitment to athletic development.
description: Echuca College provides sporting scholarships for students who excel on the sporting field and demonstrate exceptional athletic skills. These scholarships are available to Year 7 students and recognise outstanding sporting achievement and dedication. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence and participation. Recipients are expected to maintain high standards of athletic performance and sportsmanship. Specific financial value and selection criteria are detailed in the Scholarship Booklet.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated sporting excellence; Outstanding athletic achievement; Participation in school sports
special_conditions: Scholarship information is contained in the Scholarship Booklet available from the school. Sporting excellence scholarships require demonstrated commitment to athletic pursuits and participation in college sports programs.

overview: Echuca College offers scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding ability in the performing arts. The scholarship recognises excellence in music, drama, dance and other performing arts disciplines.
description: Echuca College provides performing arts scholarships for Year 7 students who show exceptional talent and commitment in music, drama, dance or other performing arts. These scholarships support students who demonstrate outstanding creative and performance abilities. Selection is based on demonstrated performing arts excellence through audition or portfolio assessment. Recipients are expected to participate actively in the college's performing arts programs and maintain high artistic standards throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated performing arts excellence; Outstanding ability in music, drama, dance or theatre; Auditioned or portfolio-based selection
special_conditions: Scholarship information is contained in the Scholarship Booklet available from the school. Applicants are required to demonstrate performing arts excellence through audition and/or portfolio presentation.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers Start-Up Kit assistance to help students transition from school into the workforce and overcome barriers to educational access. The program provides direct financial support to students facing genuine financial hardship.
description: Start-Up kits are designed to provide students with financial assistance of $500 to support them to meet their goals in facilitating a student's transition from school into the workforce, supporting a student to overcome social anxiety and enable them to connect meaningfully with their community, and to empower a student to take charge of their wellbeing. Funding assists students with essential resources needed to succeed in their studies including transport, classroom materials, trade equipment, books, excursions and fees. The assistance is available to all enrolled students who qualify based on demonstrated need. Applications are assessed on individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at ECG Secondary College; Demonstrated financial hardship or genuine need; Years 9-12
special_conditions: Funds are allocated based on individual student circumstances and demonstrated financial need. Applicants must be currently enrolled students experiencing genuine financial hardship.

overview: ECG Secondary College offers scholarships funded by generous community donors including organisations such as Gippsland Forest Lodge, Club 88, and Baw Baw Shire Council. These scholarships support eligible students to complete their studies and pursue further education.
description: Donor-funded scholarships are contributed towards eligible students for school fees, camps, excursions, school jumpers, stationery and other educational costs. Funding helps students who lack resources to complete their studies or progress to further education, assisting them to aspire to and achieve their career goals. The annual awards are presented at the CCG Awards Night to recognise students who have achieved meritorious performance during the year. In 2024, three scholarships were awarded across the Warragul, Pakenham, Leongatha, and Dandenong campuses. Funds are allocated based on student achievement and demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at ECG Secondary College; Demonstrated achievement and commitment to learning; Assessed financial need; Years 9-12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually at the CCG Awards Night. Recipients are selected based on meritorious performance and demonstrated need. Donors include Gippsland Forest Lodge, Freemasons Foundation Victoria, Club 88, and Baw Baw Shire Council.

overview: ECG Secondary College provides financial hardship assistance to students living with exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial difficulty. The school recognises that some students face challenging times and offers support to help them navigate adversity.
description: The Financial Hardship Assistance Application is available to students currently living with exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. The application form is provided to enable students and families to formally request assistance. The school assesses applications on individual merit and circumstances, recognising that students need access to various resources to succeed in their studies including transport, classroom materials, equipment, books, excursions and fees. All costs cannot be met by many students, particularly those living out of home or without family support. Assistance is allocated based on demonstrated need and individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at ECG Secondary College; Currently experiencing exceptional circumstances resulting in genuine financial hardship; Years 9-12
special_conditions: Applications require demonstrated financial hardship. A formal application form is required, available from the school. Assistance is assessed on individual circumstances and availability of funds.

overview: ECG Secondary College recognises that a lack of access and affordability to transport can be a barrier to accessing education. The school offers transport support to enable students to reach their study location.
description: The Transport Support Program is designed to provide students with reliable and safe transport options to get to their study location. Education is a powerful tool with potential to transform lives, but for some students the journey to knowledge is hindered by their location or lack of financial resources for transport. The school's goal is to provide dependable transport support, particularly for students who can catch public transport to campus or those living in areas with limited accessibility. Funding assists with transport costs that would otherwise prevent students from attending classes. Support is allocated based on individual need and circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at ECG Secondary College; Demonstrated transport access barriers; Years 9-12
special_conditions: Support is targeted at students with demonstrated barriers to transport access. Applications are assessed based on individual circumstances and location relative to campus.

school_name: ECG Secondary College - Pakenham Senior Campus
overview: ECG Secondary College presents annual donor awards across its campuses to recognise student achievement and provide financial support. These awards are funded through generous community donations and sponsorships from local organisations and benefactors.
description: ECG Secondary College presented 18 donor awards across the Warragul, Pakenham, Leongatha, and Dandenong campuses in 2024. These awards are funded through partnerships with community organisations and individual donors including Gippsland Forest Lodge, Fonterra, and various local sponsors. The awards recognise student achievement and contribution to the college community. Recipients are celebrated at the annual Awards Night. The specific financial value varies depending on the donor and award category. Award selection is based on demonstrated merit, participation, and contribution to college life.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at ECG Secondary College; Demonstrated achievement and contribution to college community; Recognition of meritorious performance during the academic year
special_conditions: Recipients are recognised at the annual CCG Awards Night. Awards are presented across all campuses. Specific donor award criteria vary by sponsoring organisation.

school_name: ECG Secondary College - Pakenham Senior Campus
overview: ECG Secondary College offers Start-Up Kits to provide students with financial assistance to support their transition from school into the workforce. These kits are worth $500 and are designed to help students achieve their employment and personal development goals.
description: Start-Up Kits are designed to provide students with financial assistance of $500 to support them to meet their goals. The program aims to facilitate a student's transition from school into the workforce by providing essential resources and support. The $500 financial assistance can be used for employment preparation needs such as work equipment, uniforms, interview clothing, trade tools, or related transition expenses. The program is part of ECG's commitment to supporting student wellbeing and successful transition to employment. Eligibility is based on application and demonstrated need for support with transition to work.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at ECG Secondary College; Transitioning from school into workforce; Demonstrated need for financial support; Exceptional circumstances or genuine financial hardship
special_conditions: Designed specifically to facilitate transition from school to workforce; Funds may be allocated for work-related materials, equipment, uniforms or similar employment preparation items; Application requires evidence of genuine financial hardship or exceptional circumstances.

school_name: ECG Secondary College - Pakenham Senior Campus
overview: ECG Secondary College provides financial assistance to students experiencing genuine financial hardship due to exceptional circumstances. This program is designed to remove barriers to education and ensure students can access the support they need to continue their studies.
description: ECG Secondary College offers a formal Financial Hardship Assistance application process for students living with exceptional circumstances that have resulted in genuine financial hardship. The college recognises that not all students have the resources to complete their studies and provides support to eligible applicants. Students can apply using the dedicated Financial Assistance Form available on the college's documents page. The program aims to provide assistance with school fees, camps, excursions, school uniforms, stationery, and other educational necessities. Support includes various forms of assistance ranging from fee reductions to provision of essential learning materials and resources. The application requires documentation of circumstances and financial information.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at ECG Secondary College; Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Exceptional circumstances affecting family finances; Demonstrated need for support with school costs
special_conditions: Applications must demonstrate genuine financial hardship resulting from exceptional circumstances; Requires supporting documentation and financial information; Handled with strict confidentiality; Funds may support school fees, camps, excursions, uniforms, stationery and materials; Recipients are part of broader donation-funded scholarship program.

school_name: ECG Secondary College - Pakenham Senior Campus
overview: ECG Secondary College offers scholarship funding supported by community donations and the Freemasons Foundation Victoria. These scholarships help eligible students aspire to further their career goals and remove barriers to education through financial support.
description: ECG Secondary College provides scholarship funding supported by generous community donations and sponsorships. The 2024 Awards ceremony highlighted scholarships funded by organisations including Gippsland Forest Masonic Lodge and Freemasons Foundation Victoria, with recognition that 'financial assistance translated into recognition for students who achieved meritorious performance during the year and helped a student in furthering their education with a scholarship grant.' Scholarship funding is contributed towards eligible students for school fees, camps, excursions, school uniforms, stationery, and other educational costs. The program aims to help students who would not otherwise have access to educational opportunities. Funding amounts vary depending on the specific donor scholarship and the student's circumstances. Awards recognise both merit and financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at ECG Secondary College; Demonstrated financial need; Meritorious academic or personal performance; Exceptional circumstances affecting access to education
special_conditions: Funded through community donations and specific donor organisations including Gippsland Forest Lodge and Freemasons Foundation; Recipients recognised at annual Awards Night; Awards acknowledge both merit and financial need; Donor contributions are tax-deductible (CCG is a Deductible Gift Recipient); Recipients expected to maintain good academic standing and conduct.

school_name: ECG Secondary College - Pakenham Senior Campus
overview: ECG Secondary College implements a healthy foods initiative to support students' wellbeing by removing barriers to accessing nutritious food. The program recognises the link between healthy eating and improved student wellbeing and is funded through donations and program partnerships.
description: ECG Secondary College implements a healthy foods initiative in partnership with generous program donors and individual supporters. The program recognises strong links between eating fresh foods and improved student wellbeing. The initiative provides support to students who may face barriers to accessing healthy food options, breaking down financial and logistical obstacles. The program is funded through community partnerships and donations. Implementation began in 2024 across all campuses. The initiative provides students with access to healthy food options that might otherwise be out of reach due to cost or availability. This complements other financial assistance programs including the Start-Up Kits and general hardship support.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at ECG Secondary College; Face barriers to accessing healthy food; Financial constraints affecting nutrition access
special_conditions: Program implemented across all ECG campuses; Funded through community partnerships and donations; Focuses on removing barriers to healthy food access; Supports student wellbeing as part of holistic student support approach; Donations welcome through friends@ccg.asn.au

overview: Edgars Creek Secondary College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program for Year 7 students who demonstrate high academic ability. The program allows students to work at an accelerated pace across all core subject areas consistent with their strong academic abilities, completing the Year 7-10 curriculum by the end of Year 9.
description: The SEAL Program is a selective entry program designed for students with exceptional academic ability.
