Entry is determined by the ACER High Ability Selection Test (HAST), which assesses Reading Comprehension, Written Expression, Mathematical Reasoning, and Abstract Reasoning. Students in the SEAL program work at an accelerated pace in all core subject areas including English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities, and Health. The program ensures that students complete the Year 7–10 curriculum by the end of Year 9, with enrichment provided through broadened content, understanding, experience and skills appropriate to students' zone of proximal development. The program is non-monetary in nature and provides accelerated learning opportunities rather than financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7; Must receive a High School Placement Notification for Edgars Creek Secondary College; Must pass the ACER High Ability Selection Test (HAST)
special_conditions: SEAL Test registrations and payments are now closed for incoming Year 7 in 2026. Only students who have received a High School Placement Notification for Edgars Creek Secondary College are allowed to register. The test will be conducted in August at the College.

overview: Edenbrook offers an Enrichment Program for very able students entering Year 7. This exceptional program aims to develop lifelong learners who are passionate, curious, adaptable, creative and collaborative, providing a different way of teaching and learning that enables students to expand their minds in creative and innovative ways.
description: The Enrichment Program is available to Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional educational performance and potential. All students enrolled for Year 7 at Edenbrook Secondary College undertake an academic testing program held during their Grade 6 year. Students shown to be in the upper range for educational performance and potential are invited to submit an application and attend an interview with the Principal and/or Senior Staff. Places in the program will be offered from these interviews. The program focuses on developing higher-order thinking skills, critical thinking, questioning strategies, multiple ways of solving problems, and offers an early VCE pathway where Year 10 students may undertake Units 1 and 2 VCE subjects subject to meeting academic requirements and subject availability.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 6 at primary school; Students in the upper range for educational performance and potential; Successful completion of academic testing program; Successful application and interview with Principal and/or Senior Staff
special_conditions: This is not an acceleration program; it is a different way of teaching and learning. Early VCE pathway access subject to meeting academic requirements and subject availability. Parents are encouraged to contact the Assistant Principal for Teaching and Learning – Mrs Zoë Carolan via email for further information.

overview: Edenbrook Secondary College understands that some families may experience financial difficulty and offers a range of support options to assist with educational costs and resources. The college provides access to various government and school-based financial assistance programs to ensure students can access education without financial barriers.
description: Edenbrook Secondary College offers multiple financial assistance options for families experiencing financial difficulty. These include: Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) to help eligible families access school camps, excursions, and sports activities; State Schools Relief assistance for eligible families; and personalised support options. Additionally, families unable to participate in the School Preferred Device Program due to financial hardship are encouraged to contact the Business Manager, Joanne Clifton, to discuss options that may include payment plans, loan devices, and other supports. All financial arrangements remain confidential and financial matters are not discussed with students. The college offers flexible payment options and assistance programs to ensure families can afford necessary school resources and participation in co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Demonstrated financial need or hardship; Eligible for government support programs (CSEF, State Schools Relief); All year levels
special_conditions: Financial arrangements remain confidential. Financial matters are not discussed with students. Families experiencing hardship are encouraged to contact Business Manager Joanne Clifton directly to discuss flexible payment options, loan devices, and personalised support options.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program Academic Pathway to students demonstrating strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and commitment to learning. This program recognises emerging scholars ready to challenge themselves and deepen their engagement with academics through mentorship and structured extension.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program is ELTHAM College's contemporary approach to scholarships, offering more than traditional fee reduction. The Academic Pathway supports students who demonstrate strong academic potential and intellectual curiosity. Successful applicants receive a tuition fee reduction along with mentoring and academic guidance, enrichment opportunities to extend learning, early VCE opportunities where appropriate, and support for relevant study opportunities. The program is designed to recognise potential early and nurture it deliberately through a combination of academic evidence and personal information review. Participants are current ELTHAM students or new applicants entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027. The application process includes submission of an introductory video and academic evidence, followed by review, shortlisting, and interview with the selection panel.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Australian citizens or permanent residents; Domestic students only; Demonstrated strong academic potential and intellectual curiosity; Commitment to learning; Alignment with College values
special_conditions: Program includes mentorship and structured extension as core components. Elevate is designed to create opportunity, not pressure. Successful applicants receive confirmation of tuition fee reduction component in their offer letter. The Principal reserves the right to make final decisions in all cases.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program Arts Pathway to students demonstrating exceptional talent and commitment to deepening their artistic craft. This program recognises students with emerging excellence in performance, visual arts, or creative disciplines.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program Arts Pathway supports students who are already demonstrating commitment to artistic disciplines and are ready to deepen their artistic craft. Successful applicants receive a tuition fee reduction along with mentoring and structured extension through enrichment experiences and opportunities to extend learning. The program provides tailored enrichment for each applicant and is designed around the principle that participation is supportive, not burdensome. Students are guided to grow and develop their artistic talents through authentic learning opportunities aligned with College values. Applications are reviewed by the selection panel considering pathway-specific strengths and alignment with College values. The program is open to both current ELTHAM students and new applicants entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Australian citizens or permanent residents; Domestic students only; Demonstrated commitment to artistic discipline; Evidence of artistic talent and potential; Alignment with College values
special_conditions: Program includes mentorship and structured extension as core components. Elevate is designed for enrichment, not pressure. Successful applicants receive confirmation of tuition fee reduction component in their offer letter. The Principal reserves the right to make final decisions in all cases. Students should submit evidence of artistic engagement, portfolio, or performance experience.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program Sport Pathway to students demonstrating exceptional athletic talent and potential in competitive sport. This program recognises emerging athletes ready to balance high-level sport with academic and personal growth.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program Sport Pathway supports students already demonstrating commitment to athletic pursuits at a high level and ready for additional challenge and support. Successful applicants receive a tuition fee reduction along with participation in the Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP), which provides sports-specific coaching, strength and conditioning testing, and comprehensive programs to assist students in achieving their personal best. The program aims to provide an environment and opportunities for students with athletic potential to thrive while maintaining balance with academic and personal development. Sport Pathway students are guaranteed entry to the ELTHAM College Elite and Emerging Athletes Program, which works with students during the school week while allowing continuation of evening and weekend sport activities for local and regional clubs. The application process includes submission of sporting achievements, academic evidence, and an introductory video, followed by review and interview.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Australian citizens or permanent residents; Domestic students only; Demonstrated exceptional athletic talent and potential in competitive sport; Evidence of sporting achievement or high-level participation; Alignment with College values
special_conditions: Sport Pathway students are guaranteed entry to the Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP). The program works with students during school week but does not conflict with evening and weekend sport activities for local and regional clubs. Students receive personal course mapping to support, challenge and motivate them to achieve their definition of sporting success. The Principal reserves the right to make final decisions in all cases.

overview: ELTHAM College provides hardship bursaries to families facing genuine financial difficulty who would otherwise be unable to afford tuition fees. These means-tested awards recognise talented students and support equal access to education regardless of financial background.
description: ELTHAM College continues to provide hardship bursaries to eligible families demonstrating genuine financial need. These are means-tested awards with financial value dependent on individual family circumstances and financial assessment. The College's commitment to access ensures talented students from across the community can benefit from an ELTHAM education. Bursary applications require completion of a detailed financial assessment process, and families must demonstrate genuine financial hardship. The specific financial value of bursaries varies based on family circumstances and is determined following thorough financial assessment. Families are encouraged to contact the College directly to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options. The College's bursary program is supported by the generosity of current and former parents and community members.
eligibility_criteria: All year levels; Demonstrated financial hardship; Valid reasons preventing family from affording full fees; Family willing to provide detailed financial documentation; Good academic standing; No disciplinary issues
special_conditions: Means-tested awards with value dependent on family circumstances and assets. Applications require separate detailed financial assessment. Families must provide documentary evidence of financial circumstances. The College reserves discretion to award based on available funds and assessed need.

overview: The Murdoch Program at Elisabeth Murdoch College provides strong academic pathways for students entering Year 7. This selective program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to challenging themselves across the curriculum. The program extends the regular curriculum to create an environment where students develop critical thinking, leadership, and advanced learning skills.
description: The Murdoch Program is a full-time integrated academic program available to Year 7-9 students who have been accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College. Students in this program engage with more challenging speciality content and often learn at a faster rate than their peers in the regular curriculum. The program develops unique academic bonds and friendships among students who support each other's success and drive high performance. Students enrolled in the Murdoch Program are required to pay a special program levy to help subsidise the cost of incursions and excursions throughout the year, with some events potentially incurring additional costs. Applications for Year 7 entry open around August, and only students who have been formally accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College can apply. The selection process involves written applications and may include interviews conducted by the program coordinators.
eligibility_criteria: Only students accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College for Year 7; Applications open around August each year; Must demonstrate academic interest and commitment to challenging coursework; Open to all students regardless of prior academic testing
special_conditions: Students enrolled in specialist programs are required to pay a special program levy. This levy assists with subsidising the cost of incursions and excursions throughout the year. Some events may include a small additional cost. Students must remain engaged with the program throughout their enrolment.

overview: The Performing Arts Collective at Elisabeth Murdoch College is a specialised program for students passionate about performing arts who seek to develop skills while receiving a high quality secondary education. This full-time, integrated academic program combines excellence in performing arts with strong academic development for Years 7-9. The college has outstanding performing arts facilities and a history of excellence in the performing arts.
description: The Performing Arts Collective is a full-time integrated academic program designed for students entering Year 7 who are passionate about performing arts. Students develop practical skills in performing disciplines including drama, dance, vocal performance, and musical theatre while concurrently receiving a high quality secondary education. Personal development, collaboration, organisation, critical thinking, and reflection are core components of the program. Students are exposed to positive education and benefit from strengthening a growth mindset approach. The program requires submission of a 3-minute video demonstration (maximum 15MB, MP4 format) showcasing two preferred specialist genres. An application fee of $50 (non-refundable) is payable at the time of application. Shortlisted applicants participate in a 10-minute online interview conducted via Zoom. Students enrolled in the program are required to pay a special program levy to subsidise incursions and excursions, with some events incurring additional costs. Students must wear full EMC uniform and PAC polo with black jazz pants during performance subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Only students accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College for Year 7; Applications open around August each year; Must demonstrate passion for performing arts; Must be able to participate in audition/interview process; Open to all students interested in drama, dance, vocal, musical theatre, or film and television
special_conditions: Application requires submission of a 3-minute video in MP4 format (no larger than 15MB) demonstrating two preferred specialist genres. Shortlisted candidates participate in a 10-minute online interview via Zoom. Special program levy applies. Students are required to wear full EMC uniform including PAC polo and black jazz pants during performance subjects. Winter rain jacket with PAC logo is also available for purchase. The College has a history of producing successful performers in film, television, dance, and musical theatre.

overview: Leaders in Sport at Elisabeth Murdoch College develops strong leadership within the community for Year 7-9 students with sporting talent and leadership potential. This selective program combines sport excellence with leadership development, preparing students to become role models and community leaders. The program extends the regular curriculum to challenge students and develop the leadership skills required for continued success.
description: Leaders in Sport is a specialised program for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and leadership potential. Students in this program engage with sport at an elite level while developing strong leadership and community engagement skills. The program develops unique bonds among like-minded students who support each other's success and drive high performance in sport. Students in the program often learn at a faster rate and engage with more challenging content focused on sports coaching, strategy, and community leadership. A special program levy applies to all students enrolled in specialist programs to help subsidise the cost of incursions, excursions, and sporting development opportunities. Some events may include additional costs. Applications open around August each year, and only students who have been accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College can apply. The selection process involves written applications and may include interviews and/or practical assessments of sporting ability.
eligibility_criteria: Only students accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College for Year 7; Applications open around August each year; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and potential; Must show evidence of community leadership or service; Open to students in any sport
special_conditions: Students enrolled in specialist programs are required to pay a special program levy to help subsidise costs of sporting development, incursions, and excursions. Some sporting events may include small additional costs. Students must maintain engagement with the program and demonstrate commitment to sporting and community leadership development throughout their enrolment.

overview: The Aspirant Leaders program, part of the THRIVE positive education network, empowers students to become change makers entering Year 7-9. This selective program develops leadership capabilities and community service orientation in students who demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to positive change. The program extends the regular curriculum to build the behaviours and leadership skills for continued success.
description: The Aspirant Leaders (THRIVE) program is designed for students entering Year 7 who demonstrate leadership potential and a commitment to becoming positive change makers within their school and broader community. This full-time integrated program develops student leadership capabilities through authentic community-based activities and initiatives. Students develop unique bonds with fellow leaders as they support each other's success and drive positive impact. The program emphasises personal development, collaboration, critical thinking, and reflection on leadership practice. Students in this program often learn at a faster rate and engage with more challenging content focused on community service, social responsibility, and leadership development. A special program levy applies to all students in specialist programs to subsidise the costs of leadership development activities, incursions, and excursions, with some events potentially incurring additional costs. The college has partnered with local primary schools to create the THRIVE positive education network. Applications open around August each year for Year 7 entry, with selection based on demonstration of leadership qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Only students accepted into Elisabeth Murdoch College for Year 7; Applications open around August each year; Must demonstrate leadership potential and vision; Must show commitment to community service and positive change; Open to students from all backgrounds
special_conditions: Students enrolled in specialist programs are required to pay a special program levy to help subsidise the cost of leadership development activities, incursions, and excursions. Some events may include a small additional cost. The program is part of the THRIVE positive education network in partnership with local primary schools. Students must maintain engagement with the program and demonstrate commitment to leadership and community service development throughout their enrolment.

overview: Elwood Primary School provides access to the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) to assist eligible families cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. Eligible families with valid means-tested concession cards may apply for this annual assistance program.
description: CSEF is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. The program provides an annual payment of $125 per primary school student, paid directly to the school to be allocated towards camps, sports and excursion expenses. To be eligible, a parent or legal guardian must hold a valid means-tested concession card (Veterans Affairs Gold Card, Centrelink Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card) or be a temporary foster parent. Applicants must submit an application to the school by the specified due date. Special consideration eligibility categories also exist for families experiencing hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or legal guardian must hold a valid means-tested concession card (Veterans Affairs Gold Card, Centrelink Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card) or be a temporary foster parent; Student must be enrolled in a registered Victorian primary or secondary school; Student must be aged between 5 and 18 years inclusive; Application must be submitted to the school by the due date; Concession card must be successfully validated with Centrelink on the first day of Term 1 or Term 2
special_conditions: CSEF is not payable to students attending pre-school, kindergarten, home schooling, or TAFE. The fund is paid directly to the school and allocated by the school towards eligible expenses. Eligibility is subject to parent/legal guardian's concession card being successfully validated with Centrelink on the first day of either Term 1 or Term 2.

overview: Emmanuel College offers the Brophy Scholarship to Year 8 students demonstrating outstanding academic performance. This award recognises exceptional achievement based on Grade 6 testing and Year 7 results and is presented at the College's first assembly of the year.
description: The Brophy Scholarship is awarded to a Year 8 student from each campus (St Paul's Altona North and Notre Dame Point Cook) and provides full payment of school fees for Year 8. Awardees are selected on the basis of performance in testing conducted with Grade 6 students prior to their commencement at the College and their Year 7 achievement data. Additionally, the Brophy Scholarship is awarded to the Dux of the College as determined by the ATAR score for the relevant Year 12 cohort. This scholarship was historically supported for many years by BJ Brophy, with funds provided to assist scholarship winners with $1,000.00 per annum for three years of undergraduate study.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8 students; Strong performance in Grade 6 entrance testing; Excellent Year 7 achievement data; Current enrolment at Emmanuel College; OR Year 12 Dux award based on highest ATAR score
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded on the basis of performance in testing conducted with Grade 6 students prior to their commencement at the College and their Year 7 achievement data. Year 12 Dux award is determined by ATAR score for the relevant cohort.

overview: Emmanuel College presents the Adele Trenquelleon Leadership Award to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding leadership potential. This award recognises students who embody the qualities of Adele Trenquelleon and the Characteristics of a Marianist Educated person.
description: The Adele Trenquelleon Leadership Award is presented to a Year 7 student from each campus (St Paul's Altona North and Notre Dame Point Cook) at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Award recipients are selected based on demonstrated outstanding leadership potential and alignment with the qualities of Adele Trenquelleon and the Characteristics of Marianist Education. The award recognises and celebrates students who show exceptional promise as leaders within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Demonstrated outstanding leadership potential; Alignment with Characteristics of Marianist Educated person; Current enrolment at Emmanuel College
special_conditions: Award presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Selection based on leadership qualities and embodiment of Marianist characteristics.

overview: Emmanuel College presents the VET Excellence Award to final year students pursuing careers in Vocational Education and Training. This award celebrates excellence in VET pathway choices and provides support for further studies in this field.
description: The VET Excellence Award is presented to a final year student who is pursuing a career pathway in a VET field. This award celebrates excellence in Vocational Education and Training and recognises the important contribution of VET pathways to student achievement. The award is presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening and provides support and recognition for a student who has excelled in their VET studies and demonstrates commitment to this career pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 (final year) students; Pursuing a career pathway in a VET field; Demonstrated excellence in VET studies; Current enrolment at Emmanuel College
special_conditions: Award presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Selection based on excellence and commitment in VET pathway.

overview: Emmanuel College awards the Performing Arts Excellence Award to students who have demonstrated exceptional achievement in Performing Arts. This award provides support for students pursuing further studies in this creative field.
description: The Performing Arts Excellence Award is presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening to a student who has displayed excellence in Performing Arts and wishes to pursue further studies in this field. This award recognises and supports students with exceptional talent and commitment in drama, music, dance, or related performing arts disciplines. The award provides encouragement and recognition for students pursuing performing arts pathways beyond secondary school.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating exceptional achievement in Performing Arts; Intent to pursue further studies in Performing Arts; Current enrolment at Emmanuel College
special_conditions: Award presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Selection based on demonstrated excellence in Performing Arts and intent to pursue further studies.

overview: Emmanuel College presents the Social Justice Award to students who have demonstrated outstanding contributions to social justice. This award was established by the Marianist founders and recognises students embodying Service, Justice, Peace and Integrity of Creation.
description: The Social Justice Award recognises students who have demonstrated outstanding contributions to Social Justice through the embodiment of the Characteristics of Marianist Education - Service, Justice, Peace and Integrity of Creation. Award recipients have been actively involved in the college's social justice club, promoted and attended key events, and acted for a more just, peaceful and sustainable Emmanuel community and society. This award is presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening and celebrates student commitment to social justice values.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated outstanding contributions to Social Justice; Active involvement in social justice club; Promoted and attended key social justice events; Acted for a more just, peaceful and sustainable Emmanuel community and society; Embodiment of Characteristics of Marianist Education - Service, Justice, Peace, Integrity of Creation
special_conditions: Award presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Selection based on demonstrated outstanding contributions to Social Justice and embodiment of Marianist characteristics.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the Leadership/Community Spirit Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional leadership skills and a strong sense of community spirit. This scholarship is awarded to incoming Year 7 students who show evidence of involvement in school or community activities and their ability to inspire and lead others.
description: This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional leadership skills and a strong sense of community spirit. Applicants should provide evidence of their involvement in school or community activities and their ability to inspire and lead others. The scholarship award presentation is held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Students are invited to apply for this scholarship, and the selection process involves reviewing evidence of leadership involvement in school or community settings. The scholarship aims to nurture student talent and leadership excellence at Emerald Secondary College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must demonstrate exceptional leadership skills; Must show strong sense of community spirit; Evidence of involvement in school or community activities required
special_conditions: Scholarship award presentation held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application submission via email, in person, or by post to Tiana Clayworth, Emerald Secondary College, P.O. Box 264, Emerald 3782.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the SEAL (Select Entry Accelerated Learning) Program Scholarship, which is awarded automatically to the highest performers on the SEAL entry test. This program caters to high-achieving students looking to accelerate their learning in a supportive environment.
description: The SEAL (Select Entry Accelerated Learning) scholarship is awarded automatically to the highest performers on the SEAL entry test. This program caters to high-achieving students looking to accelerate their learning in a supportive environment. Students interested in the SEAL program must complete an entrance examination, and top performers may be invited to an interview prior to acceptance into the program. The college has offered the SEAL program since 2008 and provides specialised instruction and enrichment opportunities for gifted students. The SEAL program includes orientation days and specialised learning challenges such as STEM activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must perform in the highest tier on the SEAL entry test; Completion of entrance examination required; May require interview prior to acceptance
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded automatically to highest performers on SEAL entry test. No separate application required for SEAL scholarship; entry is determined by performance on the entrance examination. Successful SEAL students receive invitation to special orientation day featuring accelerated learning opportunities.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the Visual Arts Scholarship to students with outstanding artistic passion and talent. This scholarship recognises and nurtures students who demonstrate exceptional ability in visual arts disciplines.
description: This scholarship is awarded to students with outstanding artistic passion and talent. Applicants should submit a portfolio of their work showcasing their creativity and technical skills in various mediums. The selection process involves review of the submitted portfolio demonstrating artistic achievement and potential. The scholarship aims to recognise and nurture student talent in visual arts at Emerald Secondary College. Award presentation is held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application should be submitted to Tiana Clayworth via email, in person, or by post with portfolio materials demonstrating artistic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must demonstrate outstanding artistic passion and talent; Portfolio of work required showcasing creativity and technical skills in various mediums
special_conditions: Portfolio submission required as part of application. Scholarship award presentation held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application submission via email, in person, or by post to Tiana Clayworth, Emerald Secondary College, P.O. Box 264, Emerald 3782.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the Music Scholarship to students with exceptional musical abilities. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding talent in instrumental, vocal, or composition disciplines.
description: The music scholarship recognises students with exceptional musical abilities. Whether in instrumental, vocal, or composition, applicants should submit a portfolio that showcases their musical talent. The selection process involves review of the submitted portfolio demonstrating musical achievement and technical ability. Emerald Secondary College offers a strong music program which includes a wide range of musical instruments, a variety of bands and ensembles, and twice yearly concerts. The college features the Emerald Performing Arts Centre providing a great venue for music performance. Award presentation is held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application should be submitted with portfolio materials demonstrating musical excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must demonstrate exceptional musical abilities; Portfolio required showcasing instrumental, vocal, or composition talent
special_conditions: Portfolio submission required demonstrating instrumental, vocal, or composition ability. Scholarship award presentation held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application submission via email, in person, or by post to Tiana Clayworth, Emerald Secondary College, P.O. Box 264, Emerald 3782.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to students who have consistently excelled academically across a range of subjects. This scholarship recognises and rewards outstanding academic achievement in incoming Year 7 students.
description: This scholarship is offered to students who have consistently excelled academically across a range of subjects. Applicants should provide evidence of high achievement, including school reports and any academic awards or recognitions. The selection process involves review of academic records, school reports, and evidence of consistent high achievement. The scholarship aims to recognise student academic excellence and nurture high-achieving students at Emerald Secondary College. Award presentation is held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Emerald Secondary College has a strong history of academic success with a high proportion of students being accepted into university or other tertiary institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must demonstrate consistent academic excellence; Evidence of high achievement required including school reports; Academic awards or recognitions must be documented
special_conditions: School reports and evidence of academic achievement required. Scholarship award presentation held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application submission via email, in person, or by post to Tiana Clayworth, Emerald Secondary College, P.O. Box 264, Emerald 3782.

overview: Emerald Secondary College offers the Sport Scholarship to students who excel in one or more sporting areas. This scholarship recognises and nurtures students who demonstrate exceptional athletic achievement and commitment to sporting excellence.
description: The sports scholarship is awarded to students who excel in one or more sporting areas. Applicants should provide a record of their sporting achievements, including team participation, awards, and references from coaches or sports teachers. The selection process involves review of sporting achievements, team participation records, awards received, and references from coaches or sports teachers. Emerald Secondary College supports students through interschool sports competition across many different sports. The scholarship aims to recognise student sporting talent and excellence. Award presentation is held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application should be submitted with documentation of sporting achievements and references.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must demonstrate excellence in one or more sporting areas; Record of sporting achievements required; Team participation and awards documentation required; References from coaches or sports teachers required
special_conditions: Documentation of sporting achievements, team participation, and awards required. References from coaches or sports teachers must be submitted. Scholarship award presentation held at the first Year 7 celebration assembly for 2026. Application submission via email, in person, or by post to Tiana Clayworth, Emerald Secondary College, P.O. Box 264, Emerald 3782.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program - Academic Pathway to support students who demonstrate strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to learning. This pathway provides tailored mentorship and structured extension opportunities for academically gifted students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The Academic Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program is designed for students who demonstrate strong academic potential and intellectual curiosity. Participants receive mentoring and academic guidance, enrichment opportunities to extend learning, early VCE opportunities where appropriate, and support for relevant study opportunities, qualifications or industry experiences. The program includes tuition fee reduction for successful applicants. This pathway is suited to students who are motivated by learning and keen to engage deeply with their academic interests. The program is focused on creating opportunity through mentorship and structured extension rather than traditional scholarship models. Selection is based on a combination of academic evidence, recent school reports, standardised testing such as NAPLAN where available, and a standardised academic assessment administered by Edutest. All applicants are required to submit an introductory video, academic evidence, and supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students only (Australian citizens or permanent residents); Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Current ELTHAM students or new applicants welcome; Demonstrated strong academic potential and intellectual curiosity; Commitment to learning
special_conditions: Participation is supportive, not burdensome - students are guided to grow, not pressured to perform. While the program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants, Elevate is much broader than traditional scholarships, focusing on mentoring, enrichment and authentic learning opportunities. Applications require submission of an introductory video (up to three minutes), academic evidence including recent school reports and standardised testing, supporting documentation including certificates and records of achievement, and completion of a standardised academic assessment.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program - Arts Pathway to support students who demonstrate creative potential, commitment to their artistic development, and willingness to engage deeply in their chosen art form. This pathway provides mentorship and enrichment opportunities for artistically talented students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The Arts Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program supports students who demonstrate creative potential and are committed to their artistic development. Participants receive mentoring and guidance aligned to their artistic discipline, enrichment opportunities aligned to their artistic discipline, early access to senior Arts pathways where appropriate, and support for relevant study opportunities, qualifications or experiences. The program includes tuition fee reduction for successful applicants. This pathway is suited to students who are passionate about the Arts and keen to further develop their skills within a supportive learning environment. Selection is based on a combination of academic evidence, school reports, standardised testing, completion of a standardised academic assessment by Edutest, and pathway-specific materials. Arts pathway applicants must submit an introductory video, academic evidence, and supporting documentation. The portfolio provides insight into each student's creative thinking, technical skill and approach to their chosen art form, with all application components considered together holistically.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students only (Australian citizens or permanent residents); Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Current ELTHAM students or new applicants welcome; Demonstrated creative potential and commitment to artistic development; Willingness to engage deeply in chosen art form
special_conditions: Participation is supportive, not burdensome - students are guided to grow, not pressured to perform. While the program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants, Elevate is much broader than traditional scholarships, focusing on mentoring, enrichment and authentic learning opportunities. Applications require submission of an introductory video (up to three minutes), academic evidence including recent school reports and standardised testing, supporting documentation including certificates and records of achievement, pathway-specific materials such as art portfolios, and completion of a standardised academic assessment. All application components are considered together - no single element is assessed in isolation.

overview: ELTHAM College offers the Elevate Pathways Program - Sport Pathway to support students who demonstrate athletic potential, commitment to their sport, and willingness to balance performance with learning and wellbeing. This pathway provides mentorship and training opportunities for athletically talented students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The Sport Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program supports students who demonstrate athletic potential and are committed to their sport. Participants are guaranteed entry to the ELTHAM College Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP). Participants receive mentoring and performance guidance, training and development opportunities aligned to their sport, support for relevant sporting opportunities, qualifications or experiences, and tuition fee reduction. The program is open to athletes from any sport and is designed to complement their specific training for that discipline. The Elite and Emerging Athletes Program works with students during the school week, allowing them to continue with their evening and weekend sport activities for local and regional clubs. A personal course is mapped to support, challenge and motivate each student to achieve their definition of sporting success. Selection is based on academic performance, school reports, standardised testing, a standardised academic assessment by Edutest, and relevant sporting achievement and coach references.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students only (Australian citizens or permanent residents); Entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Current ELTHAM students or new applicants welcome; Demonstrated athletic potential and commitment to sport; Willingness to balance performance with learning and wellbeing; Open to athletes from any sport
special_conditions: Sport Pathway students are guaranteed entry to the ELTHAM College Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP). Participation is supportive, not burdensome - students are guided to grow, not pressured to perform. While the program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants, Elevate is much broader than traditional scholarships, focusing on mentoring, enrichment and authentic learning opportunities. The Elite and Emerging Athletes Program is designed to complement their specific training, allowing students to continue with evening and weekend sport activities for local and regional clubs during the school week. Applications require submission of an introductory video (up to three minutes), academic evidence including recent school reports and standardised testing, supporting documentation including sporting achievements and coach references, and completion of a standardised academic assessment.

overview: ELTHAM College provides hardship bursaries and financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial difficulty who would not otherwise be able to afford school fees. The College remains committed to ensuring talented students from across the community can access an ELTHAM education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: ELTHAM College continues to provide hardship bursaries and financial assistance to eligible families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. Unlike the traditional scholarship model which ended in 2023, the College maintains dedicated funding for families who would not otherwise be able to afford school fees. These bursaries are part of the College's commitment to ensuring equitable access to quality education and supporting talented students from across the community. The College recognises that financial barriers should not prevent capable students from accessing an excellent education at ELTHAM. Families interested in applying for hardship support should contact the College's admissions team directly. The application process involves assessment of family financial circumstances, and successful bursary recipients benefit from the College's commitment to supporting their full educational experience.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial hardship or inability to afford full school fees; Acceptance to ELTHAM College; All year levels; Domestic and international students may be eligible; Subject to individual assessment of financial circumstances
special_conditions: Applications for hardship bursaries must be made directly to the College's admissions team. Families should contact the Head of Admissions on 03 9433 9935 or admissions@elthamcollege.vic.edu.au to inquire about hardship support. Assessment involves careful evaluation of family income and circumstances. Hardship bursaries are awarded at the College's discretion to families demonstrating genuine financial need. The College maintains flexibility in providing support to ensure no talented student is prevented from attending ELTHAM due to financial barriers.

overview: Emmaus Catholic Primary School offers fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from accessing Catholic education. The school implements the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) concessional fee policy to support eligible families in paying school fees.
description: To assist school families experiencing financial hardship in accessing a Catholic primary education, Emmaus Catholic Primary School has implemented a fee concession policy in line with MACS guidelines. Families meeting specific eligibility criteria are entitled to concessional school fees at a reduced rate. The financial benefit is provided as a reduction in annual school fees, with the exact amount determined by the family's circumstances and assessed financial capacity. Eligible families are required to submit a MACS Concessional Fee Application Form along with an annual statutory declaration confirming their eligibility. The concession is provided on a pro-rata basis for part-year attendance. Applications should be emailed to accounts@emsydenham.catholic.edu.au or delivered to the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels in school; Families with current Health Care Card or Pension Card eligible for CSEF rebate; Any family of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Any family experiencing severe financial hardship; Any family holding a DVA Gold Card; Any family identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard; Families required to relocate child for long-term medical treatment at Royal Children's Hospital; Home schooled students attending on part-time basis meeting above criteria; Annual statutory declaration required to confirm eligibility
special_conditions: Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility each year. The school works in partnership with families to meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay. Concessional fees provide a consistent approach across all MACS primary schools based on principles of Catholic social teaching and preferential option for the poor.

overview: Emmaus Catholic Primary School offers the Family Assistance Scheme provided by the Diocese of Ballarat to support families experiencing financial hardship. This scheme assists eligible families with school fees through a means-tested application process.
description: The Family Assistance Scheme is a diocesan financial aid program offered by the Diocese of Ballarat to help families who are experiencing genuine financial difficulty with school fees at Emmaus Catholic Primary School. Families who are current holders of a Health Care or Pensioner Card are encouraged to apply. The exact financial benefit varies based on individual circumstances and financial assessment. Eligibility requires completion of an application form and provision of current health care card documentation. The school operates on a confidential basis to support families during times of financial need, with no student denied enrolment due to inability to pay fees. The scheme operates on an ongoing basis for the duration of enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial hardship; Current holder of Health Care Card or Pensioner Card; Must complete application form; Must provide copy of current health care card; No child will be denied enrolment due to financial hardship
special_conditions: Application form available from school office. Families must provide evidence of financial hardship and current health care card. The school treats all applications with confidentiality.

overview: Emmaus Catholic Primary School participates in the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a Victoria State Government exemption program. This provides financial assistance to eligible families to help cover costs of school camps, sports events, and excursions.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a state government program offered through Emmaus Catholic Primary School to assist families with the cost of school camps, sports programs, and excursion activities. The program provides eligible families with financial support to offset these curriculum-related costs. Families who hold a current Health Care Card or Pensioner Card are eligible to apply. The CSEF application form is available from the school office or via the Victorian government website. Financial support is allocated per eligible student on an annual basis. The scheme operates throughout the student's enrolment at the school, with applications typically required each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families holding current Health Care Card or Pensioner Card; Must complete CSEF application form; Forms available from school office; Must provide evidence of eligible concession card status
special_conditions: Application form available from school office. CSEF is a Victoria State Government program administered through the school. Funds are specifically designated for camps, sports, and excursion costs.

overview: Emmaus Catholic Primary School supports eligible families with the Conveyance Allowance, a Victorian government program that assists with the cost of transporting children to and from school for families living in remote areas.
description: The Conveyance Allowance is a Victorian government program administered by Emmaus Catholic Primary School to support families with the cost of transporting their children to and from school. Eligibility is based on distance from the school and availability of public transport options. Students must be aged between five years (by 30 April in the year the allowance is sought) and 21 years to qualify. Eligible families must live more than 4.8 kilometres from the nearest Catholic school attended or more than 4.8 kilometres from the nearest bus service. Application forms are available from the school office. The allowance is calculated based on distance and transport costs, operating on an annual basis for eligible students.
eligibility_criteria: Aged between 5 years (by 30 April) and 21 years; Live more than 4.8 km by shortest practicable route from nearest Catholic school attended; OR live more than 4.8 km from nearest bus service; Must complete application form available from school office
special_conditions: Application forms available from school office. Eligibility based on residence distance from school. This is a Victorian government program with specific distance thresholds that must be met.

overview: Emmaus Catholic Primary School supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families through the Opening the Doors Foundation, which provides education funding to help remove financial barriers to schooling.
description: The Opening the Doors Foundation is a specialized financial assistance program that supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families in Victoria with education funding. The foundation works to ensure that Indigenous students have access to quality education regardless of financial circumstances. Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage can apply for support to cover school-related costs including fees, uniforms, and educational materials. The application process is managed through the foundation's website at https://www.openingthedoors.org.au/. Financial support is tailored to individual family circumstances and needs. The program operates on an ongoing basis for eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families throughout the student's enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Family of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Student attending school in Victoria; Must apply through Opening the Doors Foundation website; Families must demonstrate connection to Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander community
special_conditions: Applications made directly through Opening the Doors Foundation website (https://www.openingthedoors.org.au/). This is an external organization providing support specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families. School acknowledges Wadawurrung people whose land the school occupies.

overview: Emmanuel College awards a Year 8 Merit Scholarship to one student from each campus who demonstrates exceptional academic performance. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement in testing conducted with Grade 6 students and strong academic results in Year 7.
description: This scholarship is awarded to a Year 8 student from each campus (Notre Dame and St Paul's) and provides full payment of school fees for Year 8. Awardees are selected on the basis of performance in the testing conducted with Grade 6 students prior to their commencement at the College and their Year 7 achievement data. The selection process involves assessment of test performance at entry and Year 7 academic achievement. This is a significant award recognising students who have demonstrated sustained academic excellence from their first year at the College through to Year 8.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8 students; Students who have completed Grade 6 testing; Strong Year 7 academic achievement data
special_conditions: One scholarship awarded to each campus (Notre Dame Campus and St Paul's Campus). Selection based on Grade 6 entry testing and Year 7 academic performance.

overview: The Brophy Scholarship is awarded to the Dux of Emmanuel College, the student with the highest ATAR score in the Year 12 cohort. This prestigious award was established to support tertiary education for the top-performing student and recognises outstanding academic excellence.
description: The Brophy Dux Scholarship is awarded to the student who achieves the highest ATAR score in the Year 12 cohort. This scholarship was supported for many years by BJ Brophy and provides funds to assist the scholarship winner with $1,000.00 per annum for three years of undergraduate study at university. The award is presented at the first assembly of the year. This award recognises not only exceptional academic achievement but also the Marianist values of excellence and dedication embodied by the recipient.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Highest ATAR score in the cohort; Completion of VCE or equivalent qualification
special_conditions: Award is for three years of undergraduate study. Presented at the first assembly of the school year. Requires continuation in tertiary undergraduate studies.

overview: The Adele Trenquelleon Leadership Award recognises a Year 7 student from each campus who has demonstrated outstanding leadership potential. The award honours students who embody the characteristics of Marianist education and reflect the qualities of Adele Trenquelleon.
description: This award is presented to a Year 7 student from each campus (Notre Dame Campus and St Paul's Campus) at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Recipients are selected for demonstrating outstanding leadership potential and embodying the qualities associated with Adele Trenquelleon and the Characteristics of a Marianist Educated person. The award recognises students who show promise as future leaders and who live the values of the Marianist tradition including service, justice, peace and integrity. Students are evaluated on demonstrated leadership qualities, character, and contribution to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Demonstrated outstanding leadership potential; Exemplify Marianist Education characteristics; Students from each campus eligible
special_conditions: One award presented to each campus. Presented at the annual Celebration of Excellence evening. Recognition of leadership potential and Marianist values.

overview: The Jessica Wagner Memorial Mathematics Award recognises a student who demonstrates exceptional passion for mathematics and shows growth in mathematical achievement. The award honours the legacy of Jessica Wagner and celebrates students who approach learning with enthusiasm and personal best achievement.
description: This award is presented annually to a student who shows a passion for mathematics, a positive approach to life, and demonstrates growth and/or achievement in mathematics through persistence and a commitment to personal best achievement and enthusiasm in all aspects of school life, particularly in mathematics. The award recognises not just achievement but also the character qualities of dedication and positive attitude toward the subject. Recipients are selected for showing sustained engagement with mathematics, resilience in problem-solving, and an infectious enthusiasm that inspires others. The award is presented at the College's annual Celebration of Excellence evening.
eligibility_criteria: Any year level; Demonstrated passion for mathematics; Evidence of growth or achievement in mathematics; Positive approach and enthusiasm; Commitment to personal best in mathematics and school life
special_conditions: Memorial award honouring Jessica Wagner. Recognition is based on demonstrated passion, growth, and positive approach to mathematics and school life.

program_name: Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program
overview: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program is an academic enrichment program for high achieving students run in partnership with the University of Melbourne. This program supports gifted and talented students in their intellectual development through university-level engagement.
description: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program is an academic enrichment initiative for high achieving students, coordinated and run by the University of Melbourne. Students selected for this program gain access to specialised academic enrichment opportunities designed to develop their intellectual capabilities beyond the standard secondary curriculum. The program provides exposure to university-level thinking and learning while students remain in secondary school. Participation offers students the opportunity to engage with university academics and researchers, expanding their understanding of advanced academic disciplines and preparing them for tertiary-level study.
eligibility_criteria: High achieving students; Selection by Emmanuel College and University of Melbourne; Demonstrated academic excellence; Potential for advanced tertiary-level study
special_conditions: Partnership with University of Melbourne. Selection based on demonstrated academic excellence. Provides academic enrichment rather than financial support.

overview: The Marianist Social Justice Award recognises students who have demonstrated outstanding contributions to social justice and embodied the Marianist values of service, justice, peace and integrity of creation. This award celebrates students committed to creating a more just and peaceful world.
description: This award is presented to students who have demonstrated outstanding contributions to Social Justice through the embodiment of the Characteristic of Marianist Education - Service, Justice, Peace & Integrity of Creation. Recipients must have been actively involved in the social justice club, promoted and attended key social justice events, and acted to create a more just, peaceful and sustainable Emmanuel community and society. The award recognises sustained commitment to social justice causes, active engagement in advocacy and service activities, and living out the Catholic and Marianist values of the College. Students are evaluated on their demonstrated leadership in justice initiatives and their impact on the broader community.
eligibility_criteria: Any year level; Active involvement in social justice club; Attendance and promotion of key justice events; Demonstrated action for justice, peace and sustainability; Embodiment of Marianist characteristics
special_conditions: Recognition based on active involvement in social justice initiatives. Must demonstrate embodiment of Marianist values. Recognises contribution to creating just and peaceful community.

overview: The Ampol Best All Rounder Award recognises Year 12 students who have achieved strong academic results, demonstrated leadership, and contributed meaningfully across multiple areas of school life including sport, community service and other activities.
description: The Ampol Best All Rounder Award is aimed at Year 12 students who have achieved strong academic achievements, display strong leadership, lead by example and serve as role models, contribute in service, participate in other school activities such as civic affairs, debating, or school magazine, as well as represent the school in sport. The award celebrates students who exemplify all-round excellence across academic, sporting, leadership and community service dimensions. Recipients must demonstrate sustained commitment across multiple areas of school life and show capacity to balance excellence in diverse pursuits. The award recognises students who embody the holistic development philosophy of Emmanuel College and the Marianist tradition.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Strong academic achievement; Demonstrated leadership; Active participation in service; Involvement in civic affairs, debating, or school magazine; Sporting representation; Role modelling behaviour
special_conditions: Year 12 only. Recognition of all-round excellence across academic, leadership, service and sporting dimensions. Must demonstrate commitment to multiple areas of school life.

overview: The VET Excellence Award celebrates excellence in Vocational Education and Training pathways, recognising a final year student pursuing a career in a VET field. This award acknowledges the value and importance of vocational qualifications.
description: Celebrating excellence in Vocational Education and Training, this award is presented to a final year student who is pursuing a career pathway in a VET field. The award recognises students who have chosen the vocational education pathway and demonstrated commitment to excellence in their chosen VET discipline. VET students may undertake programs such as VCAL or VCDA qualifications alongside or instead of traditional VCE studies. The award acknowledges the rigour and relevance of vocational training and celebrates students who are preparing for direct entry into skilled trades and vocations. Recipients are recognised for their dedication to practical, skills-based learning and their contribution to the College's commitment to supporting diverse pathways to success.
eligibility_criteria: Final year students; Enrolled in VET program (VCAL or equivalent); Pursuing vocational career pathway; Demonstrated commitment to excellence in VET studies
special_conditions: For final year students in VET pathways. Recognition of vocational education excellence. Acknowledges non-traditional academic pathways.

overview: Epping Secondary College's ACE program is a cluster grouped acceleration strategy designed for gifted and talented students. The program ensures students can access challenging curriculum within a supportive environment of similarly-aged peers, allowing them to accelerate through Years 7-10 curriculum at a faster rate than typical and access VCE studies as part of Year 10.
description: The Accelerated Cluster Education (ACE) Program is a selective program for students who demonstrate very high cognitive ability and readiness for acceleration. Students in the ACE program complete Years 7 to 10 curriculum in some subject areas within three years rather than four, allowing them to access VCE studies as part of their Year 10 studies. The program is designed for students who perform at very high levels and feel unchallenged in regular classrooms, or for high-ability students who may be disengaged from the regular curriculum. Admission is based on student achievement levels and performance on independent AAS (Australian Assessment System) testing conducted by the school. Students enrolled in a cohort are eligible to be considered. The program provides no direct financial benefit but offers educational acceleration and enhanced learning opportunities. Selection occurs through cognitive assessment testing, with results typically communicated during the Year 6-7 transition period. Students remain in the program throughout their secondary schooling subject to maintaining appropriate engagement and progress.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in a cohort at Epping Secondary College; Demonstrated very high cognitive ability; Excellent performance on independent AAS testing; Academic performance indicating readiness for acceleration; Suitable for Year 7 entry and continuing through secondary years
special_conditions: Only students enrolled in a cohort at Epping Secondary College are eligible to be offered a place in the ACE Program. Cognitive readiness for acceleration is required. Students benefit from being in class with peers of similar ability while developing social and life skills through collaborative problem-solving.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers the Select Entry Accelerated High Achievers Program to students demonstrating exceptional academic talent and potential. The program provides accelerated learning pathways for gifted students in Years 7-9 across the college's middle years campuses.
description: The High Achievers Program is a select entry program designed for students identified as gifted and talented. Students in this program participate in accelerated learning, challenge classes and enrichment activities tailored to advanced learners. The program operates across Years 7-9 at the Niddrie and East Keilor campuses. Entry is by application and selection, with families receiving application forms as part of their enrolment welcome pack following successful placement at the college. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual curiosity across multiple disciplines. Successful applicants will be interviewed and positions confirmed before the commencement of Year 7. No specific financial benefit is publicly stated as this is a government school; however, students gain access to accelerated curriculum and advanced learning opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Selection through application and interview process; Accepted into Essendon Keilor College; Open to all genders at Niddrie and East Keilor campuses
special_conditions: Applications for the High Achievers Program are sent to families as part of the welcome pack following successful placement. Shortlisted applicants are invited for interview. Positions are confirmed prior to Year 7 commencement.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers an elite AFL Football Academy for students demonstrating exceptional talent and commitment to Australian Rules Football. The program is now proudly aligned with the Essendon Football Club through a Memorandum of Understanding.
description: The elite AFL Football Academy provides structured pathways for students from Years 7-12 to develop football skills at competitive levels. For Years 7-9, students receive weekly academy sessions tailored to developing fundamental skills and nurturing passion for the sport. At Year 9, students can enrol in a semester-based AFL elective subject with deeper theoretical study. Year 10 students engage in an intensive one-year football program preparing them for senior competition. Years 11-12 students can enrol in VET Sport and Recreation Football program to earn industry qualifications. The college's football program has produced 28 AFL draftees. Students access exclusive opportunities including visits to the Essendon Football Club and participation in SSV Premier Team competition. Premier coaching is provided through partnership with Essendon Football Club. There is no explicit fee reduction mentioned; however, participation provides elite-level coaching and professional development opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Years 7-12; Must demonstrate football talent and ability; Selection through academy participation; Must meet attendance and commitment requirements; Available at all three campuses with primary focus at Essendon senior campus
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Partnership with Essendon Football Club ensures premier coaching and resources. Students in later years have opportunity to participate in SSV Premier Team competition. College has strong tradition producing AFL-drafted players.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers an elite Basketball Academy providing top-tier coaching and resources to develop student athletes both on and off the court. The academy is led by head coach Mike Spears with professional international experience.
description: The EKC Basketball Academy provides weekly coaching sessions for students at all year levels, emphasizing skill development, game knowledge, and athletic growth. The program is led by head coach Mike Spears, who has extensive professional playing and coaching experience across over 8 countries. Students participate in regular inter-school competition, exhibition academy matches, and curriculum-based basketball education covering technical skills, game theory, and physical development. The academy partners with Keilor Basketball Association to enhance coaching quality and community connection. The program welcomes both experienced players seeking to elevate their performance and newcomers eager to develop foundational skills. Non-monetary benefits include elite coaching access, competition opportunities, and professional development pathway. No explicit fee reduction is provided as this is a government school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Years 7-12; Previous basketball experience helpful but not required; Demonstrated interest in basketball development; Available across all three campuses; Selection based on skill level and commitment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Weekly coaching sessions led by professional coach Mike Spears. Partnership with Keilor Basketball Association. Students engage in inter-school competition and exhibition matches. Program accommodates players at all skill levels.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers an elite Netball Academy delivering top-tier coaching and resources to nurture athlete growth on the court. The academy partners with the Essendon Thunder program under head rep coach Fiona Maher.
description: The EKC Netball Academy provides structured coaching and development pathways for netball players at all year levels. The program is led by head rep coach Fiona Maher with extensive expertise in netball development. Students benefit from exclusive sessions with Vixens players, providing invaluable insights and inspiration from professional athletes. The academy emphasises skill development, tactical knowledge, game awareness and leadership. Like the football and basketball programs, the netball academy provides elite-level coaching and exclusive professional exposure opportunities. Academy members gain access to specialized training sessions and inter-school competition. There is no explicit fee remission; however, students access professional-level coaching and player mentorship opportunities unique to the academy program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Years 7-12; Must demonstrate netball talent and commitment; Selection through academy participation; Available at all three campuses; Previous netball experience beneficial
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Partnership with Essendon Thunder program under head coach Fiona Maher. Exclusive access to Vixels player sessions for mentorship and professional insights. Students participate in specialized training and inter-school competition.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers an Instrumental Music Program with tuition on a range of instruments and participation in college bands and ensembles. The program reflects the college's long tradition of excellence in music and performing arts.
description: The Instrumental Music Program provides weekly music lessons on selected instruments, with students able to choose from a variety of instruments including guitar, electric bass, drums/percussion, voice, and piano/keyboard. Lessons are conducted in groups of two to five students, typically on a weekly basis. Participation includes automatic membership in college bands and ensembles including Senior Choir, Jazz Ensemble, Rock Groups at Essendon Campus, or Junior Choir and Drum Groups at Niddrie and East Keilor Campuses. A mandatory instrumental music levy of $320 per year is payable at enrolment, supporting instrument maintenance and program development. Direct Debit payment plans are available on a term ($80) or semester ($160) basis. Fees must be paid by the third week of each term. Students are expected to participate in regular ensemble rehearsals and performance opportunities, and to practice regularly outside lessons. The college has a long tradition of music excellence and encourages performance skill development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students Years 7-12; Places limited for some instruments; Must commit to regular practice and ensemble participation; Students expected to attend rehearsals and performances; Instrumental music levy of $320 per year required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Instrumental music levy of $320 per year payable at enrolment. Direct Debit payment available on term ($80) or semester ($160) basis. Fees due by third week of each term. Regular personal practice and ensemble rehearsal expected. Work missed through lessons must be caught up before next class. Places limited for some instruments.

overview: Essendon Keilor College partners with the University of Melbourne and the Victorian Government on a STEM Centre of Excellence investigating the collision of art and science. This enrichment program integrates advanced science, technology, engineering and mathematics education.
description: Delivered in partnership with the University of Melbourne and the Victorian Government, the STEM Centre of Excellence (SCoE) at Science Gallery Melbourne provides advanced learning opportunities for students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. The college offers a STEM club and opportunities for students to engage with cutting-edge research and creative problem-solving approaches combining art and science. The Niddrie campus has a purpose-built new STEM learning centre providing integrated teaching and learning spaces with flexible design supporting 21st-century learning approaches. The partnership provides access to university-level resources, expert guidance and modern learning environments designed to support student achievement in STEM disciplines. No explicit fee reduction or scholarship value is stated; however, students gain access to university partnership opportunities and specialized facilities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Years 7-12 with interest in STEM; Available at Niddrie campus with Essendon campus access; STEM club participation available; University of Melbourne partnership provides advanced learning opportunities
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Partnership with University of Melbourne and Victorian Government. Dedicated STEM Centre of Excellence at Niddrie campus. Modern learning environments designed for flexible, student-centered education. STEM club provides additional enrichment opportunity.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers the High Achievers Program as a select entry accelerated program for talented and motivated students in Years 7–9. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning across multiple subjects.
