description: The High Achievers Program provides an accelerated curriculum for students in Years 7-9, with a focus on enrichment across core subjects including English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities, Health and Physical Education, and electives such as STEM, Horticulture, Art, and Performance. Students in the program are placed in dedicated classes alongside like-minded peers and benefit from collaborative learning environments. Participation requires attendance at one or more extracurricular activities, such as leadership, sports, or music, to develop global citizenship and interpersonal skills. The program fosters a sense of belonging and prepares students for advanced VCE studies and potential university extension units in senior years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Select entry based on demonstrated academic ability; Required participation in at least one extracurricular activity; Located at Keilor East and Niddrie campuses for Years 7-9
special_conditions: Applications sent to families as part of welcome pack upon placement offer. Applicants are interviewed and positions confirmed. Integration with statewide Victorian High-Ability Program offers additional networking and enrichment opportunities.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers the Instrumental Music Program to students who wish to develop performance skills and musical excellence. The program provides weekly lessons on selected instruments with opportunities to participate in ensembles and performance events throughout the year.
description: The Instrumental Music Program provides weekly tuition in one or more instruments with flexible scheduling to minimise missed class time. At the Niddrie and East Keilor campuses, lessons are conducted in groups of 2-5 students and scheduled on rotation. At the Essendon campus, VCE music lessons are conducted individually or in small groups depending on the VCE music stream, with VCE Music Performance Solo students receiving 45-minute individual lessons weekly. The annual levy is $320 ($80 per term; $160 per semester), payable at the campus office on enrolment. Students are expected to engage in regular personal music practice, group or ensemble rehearsals, and performance opportunities. Scholarships are offered at each campus to support talented musicians.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Expression of Interest Form required for consideration; Available at all three campuses (Niddrie, East Keilor, and Essendon); Students required to participate in ensemble rehearsals and performances
special_conditions: Annual levy of $320 required. Regular personal music practice and ensemble rehearsals expected as part of weekly schedule. Work missed through attending music lessons must be caught up before next class. Applications sent to families as part of welcome pack for Year 7 entry. Scholarships available; contact campus principal for details.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers elite AFL and AFLW Football Academy programs aligned with the Essendon Football Club. The program provides premier coaching and resources to develop students' football skills and knowledge for potential professional pathways.
description: The Football Academy is an elite sporting program with partnerships with the Essendon Football Club through a Memorandum of Understanding, ensuring premier coaching and resources. Students receive educational sessions covering various facets of the game including nutrition and game analysis, led by Essendon Football Academy Coaches and EKC coaches. Members enjoy exclusive opportunities including inner sanctum visits to the Essendon Football Club for professional development insights. Year 10 students can engage in an intensive one-year football program to prepare for senior-level competition. In Years 11 and 12, students have the opportunity to enrol in the VET Sport and Recreation Football program, acquiring industry qualifications and hands-on experience. Students are also selected for the SSV Premier Team to participate in shortened season competitions and trial matches against other government and independent schools with AFL academies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating football talent and commitment; Year 10 intensive one-year program available; VET Sport and Recreation program available for Years 11-12; Selection required for SSV Premier Team placement
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Elite program providing access to professional coaching and Essendon Football Club facilities. VET qualifications available in Years 11-12. Program has historically produced 28 AFL draftees. SSV Premier Team selection based on demonstrated ability and performance.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers the Basketball Academy providing top-tier coaching and resources to develop student athletes. The program includes partnerships with professional coaching organisations and provides access to high-level competitions.
description: The EKC Basketball Academy provides top-tier coaching led by head coach Mike Spears, who has extensive experience playing and coaching professionally across over 8 countries. Students receive weekly coaching sessions with a curriculum designed to enhance technical and tactical skills, fitness, and comprehensive basketball education. The program is supported by an MOU partnership with Keilor Basketball Association, ensuring premier coaching, resources, and experiences to create successful pathways. Members receive personalised training singlets and enjoy priority access to tournaments beyond inter-school sport, including Basketball Victoria competitions such as 3x3 basketball and the Victorian Championships Cup. Students participate in exhibition academy matches and regular inter-school sports competitions, gaining invaluable experience and exposure to high-level competition.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with basketball commitment and demonstrated ability; Selection required for academy membership; Access to all academy training sessions and competitions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Professional coaching with extensive experience. Partnership with Keilor Basketball Association supports training quality. Members receive personalised training singlets. Priority tournament access through Basketball Victoria. Regular inter-school competition and exhibition matches.

overview: Essendon Keilor College offers the Netball Academy providing elite coaching and development opportunities for student athletes. The program partners with the Essendon Thunder program and provides access to professional development pathways.
description: The EKC Netball Academy delivers top-tier coaching and resources through partnership with the Essendon Thunder program, led by head rep coach Fiona Maher who brings extensive experience and expertise. Academy members gain access to exclusive sessions with Vixels players, offering invaluable insights and inspiration from elite-level players. Students enjoy priority access to Netball Victoria tournaments, including the prestigious Victorian Schools Cup. Academy sessions are meticulously crafted to enhance all facets of the game, encompassing technical skills, tactical understanding, fitness, and mental strength development. Members participate in competitive competition opportunities that provide a platform to showcase talents on a competitive stage.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with netball commitment and demonstrated ability; Selection required for academy membership; Participation in coaching sessions and tournaments
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Partnership with Essendon Thunder program ensures high-quality coaching. Exclusive access to sessions with professional Vixels players. Priority access to Netball Victoria tournaments including Victorian Schools Cup. Holistic skill development program.

program_name: Warwick Brewster Science, Maths, and Technology Scholarship for girls
overview: Erasmus Primary School offers the Warwick Brewster Science, Maths, and Technology Scholarship to recognize and support female students with a demonstrable interest in STEM-related fields. The scholarship rewards girls in Year 1 to Year 6 who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment in science, mathematics, and technology.
description: The Warwick Brewster Science, Maths, and Technology Scholarship for girls is awarded to Year 1 to Year 6 female students with a demonstrable interest in Science, Maths, or Technology (STEM-related) who show At Standard or better academic performance. Recipients receive an offer ranging from 25% to 50% off tuition fees (excluding Additional Fees, levies and taxes). The scholarship selection process includes a face-to-face assessment and interviews with the scholarship committee to assess character and motivation. Applicants must demonstrate participation in activities such as arts, sports (local, regional, state, or national representation), and community service or leadership roles. Scholarships are valid from the year of award through to graduation at the completion of Year 6, subject to progress, performance, adherence to school values, and good behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Year 1 to Year 6 female students; Demonstrable interest in Science, Maths, or Technology; At Standard or better academic performance evidenced by recent school report records and academic awards; Consistent effort towards work tasks and completion of tasks to a high standard; Participation in activities such as arts (music, drama, visual arts), sports (local, regional, state, or national representation), community service or leadership roles
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Scholarships are valid from the year of award through to graduation, the completion of Year 6, subject to progress, performance, adherence to school values, and good behaviour. Annual review by the scholarship committee. Submission of updated academic records and a summary of extracurricular participation required. Scholarships may be revoked for reasons including academic probation, behavioural issues, or failure to fulfil participation requirements. Should a student's enrolment be withdrawn prior to graduating (Year 6) from Erasmus Primary School, 100% of monies paid as part of the scholarship may require to be repaid back to the school. Erasmus Primary School reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship due to consistent non-compliance with the agreement. Scholarships may be deferred upon written request for up to one (1) year, provided the reason is approved (e.g., medical or personal leave). Poor behaviour may result in probation or loss of the scholarship. Recipients must embrace and uphold the School's values: Courage, Wisdom, Temperance, and Justice, embrace the School's culture of mutual care and respect for others, make positive contributions to both the School and the wider community, and participate fully in the wide variety of programs provided by the School.

overview: Erasmus Primary School offers merit-based scholarships to recognize outstanding academic achievement and potential in students across all year levels. The scholarships support talented students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and commitment to learning.
description: The General Scholarship programme at Erasmus Primary School is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic merit across Year 1 to Year 6. Recipients receive an offer ranging from 25% to 50% off tuition fees (excluding Additional Fees, levies and taxes), depending on the type of scholarship awarded. Selection involves a face-to-face assessment and interviews with the scholarship committee to evaluate academic performance, character, and motivation. Applicants must maintain At Standard or better academic performance evidenced by recent school reports and academic awards. The scholarship is valid from the year of award through to graduation at Year 6, subject to annual review of academic progress and adherence to school values. Applicants must demonstrate involvement in co-curricular activities including arts, sports, and community service.
eligibility_criteria: Year 1 to Year 6 students; At Standard or better academic performance evidenced by recent school report records and academic awards; Consistent effort towards work tasks and completion of tasks to a high standard; Participation in activities such as arts (music, drama, visual arts), sports (local, regional, state, or national representation), community service or leadership roles
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Scholarships are valid from the year of award through to graduation, the completion of Year 6, subject to progress, performance, adherence to school values, and good behaviour. Annual review by the scholarship committee. Submission of updated academic records and a summary of extracurricular participation required. Scholarships may be revoked for reasons including academic probation, behavioural issues, or failure to fulfil participation requirements. Should a student's enrolment be withdrawn prior to graduating (Year 6) from Erasmus Primary School, 100% of monies paid as part of the scholarship may require to be repaid back to the school. Erasmus Primary School reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship due to consistent non-compliance with the agreement. Scholarships may be deferred upon written request for up to one (1) year, provided the reason is approved (e.g., medical or personal leave). Poor behaviour may result in probation or loss of the scholarship. Recipients must embrace and uphold the School's values: Courage, Wisdom, Temperance, and Justice, embrace the School's culture of mutual care and respect for others, make positive contributions to both the School and the wider community, and participate fully in the wide variety of programs provided by the School.

overview: Erasmus Primary School offers need-based scholarships to support families experiencing financial hardship. These scholarships recognize the importance of access to education and provide financial assistance to deserving students.
description: The Need-Based Scholarship programme at Erasmus Primary School provides financial assistance to families with demonstrated financial need. Recipients receive an offer ranging from 25% to 50% off tuition fees (excluding Additional Fees, levies and taxes), with possible inclusion of school-related expenses like uniforms or textbooks at the discretion of the Scholarship committee. The application process requires submission of proof of household income (tax returns, financial statements) and a completed financial aid form outlining expenses and income. Selection involves a face-to-face assessment and interviews with the scholarship committee. The scholarship is valid from the year of award through to graduation at Year 6, subject to annual review and notification to the school in the event of changed circumstances. Students must maintain At Standard or better academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Year 1 to Year 6 students; Demonstrated financial hardship or need; Proof of household income (tax returns, financial statements) required; Completed financial aid form outlining expenses and income; At Standard or better academic performance; Notification to school of any changed circumstances
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: For need-based scholarships, applicants must submit proof of household income (tax returns, financial statements) and a completed financial aid form outlining expenses and income. Notification to the school is required in the event of changed circumstances. Scholarships are valid from the year of award through to graduation, the completion of Year 6, subject to progress, performance, adherence to school values, and good behaviour. Annual review by the scholarship committee. Scholarships may be revoked for reasons including academic probation, behavioural issues, or failure to fulfil participation requirements. Should a student's enrolment be withdrawn prior to graduating (Year 6) from Erasmus Primary School, 100% of monies paid as part of the scholarship may require to be repaid back to the school. Erasmus Primary School reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship due to consistent non-compliance with the agreement. Scholarships may be deferred upon written request for up to one (1) year, provided the reason is approved (e.g., medical or personal leave). Recipients must embrace and uphold the School's values: Courage, Wisdom, Temperance, and Justice.

overview: Erasmus Primary School maintains a Bursary Trust to provide financial assistance to families in need. The bursary programme supports eligible families to access education at the school through means-tested support.
description: The Bursary Trust is a financial assistance programme available to families requiring support to afford tuition at Erasmus Primary School. The trust provides means-tested financial assistance to eligible families, with the amount of support determined by individual circumstances and financial need assessment. Families interested in bursary support should contact the school's enrolment office for information about application procedures and eligibility requirements. The programme aims to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent talented students from accessing the school's education. Support may be reviewed annually based on changing family circumstances and school performance.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial need; Means-tested assessment required; Families seeking bursary support should contact school enrolment office for details
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Means-tested assessment required. Annual review of circumstances and school performance. Support may vary based on individual family financial circumstances.

overview: Fintona offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The scholarship provides a 50% discount on tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is designed to attract talented students who demonstrate outstanding academic potential. Recipients receive a 50% discount on tuition fees only from point of entry until completion of Year 12. The selection process involves completion of the Edutest examination, submission of previous school reports, a student questionnaire, and an interview with the Principal. Selection is based on holistic review of each applicant's performance including Edutest and Fintona assigned examinations. The scholarship covers tuition fees only; uniform, books, external music exams, stationery, camps, incursions, excursions levy, and ICT levy are not covered. Scholarships are reviewed annually, and the Principal may withdraw a scholarship if the student does not maintain satisfactory academic progress or appropriate behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and enrolled students entering Years 5 to 11; Must be domestic student; Must complete Edutest examination; Selection based on exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Acceptance of scholarship required within seven days of offer. Scholarship results and offers are strictly confidential and must not be shared beyond immediate family. Ongoing progress reports required from staff each semester. Scholarship covers 50% remission on tuition fees only; does not cover uniform, books, external music exams, stationery, compulsory camps, incursions, excursions levy, ICT levy. Principal may withdraw scholarship without notice if satisfactory academic progress or behaviour not maintained. Student must remain at school until end of Year 12; if student leaves prior to Year 12 completion, scholarship is terminated.

overview: Fintona offers Music Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional musical skill and high level of academic achievement. Recipients receive a 25% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12 plus a weekly instrumental music lesson during term time with a member of the Music Department.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises students with exceptional musical ability who are committed to pursuing music at a high level. Recipients receive a 25% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12 plus a weekly instrumental music lesson (term time only) with a member of the Music Department. Applicants must complete the Edutest examination and submit an MP4 video recording of two contrasting pieces via dropbox or email to performingarts@fintona.vic.edu.au. Shortlisted applicants are invited to audition in person and will be tested on sight reading and aural exercises appropriate for their instrument and level. Candidates must be performing comfortably at minimum AMEB or equivalent level as specified (varying by instrument). Students currently in Year 4 or 5 may apply if at a level 1 AMEB grade lower than required. Music scholarship recipients must participate in two music ensembles on their audition instrument as determined by the Music Learning Leader and demonstrate commitment to the music program at Fintona.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and enrolled students entering Years 5 to 11; Must be domestic student; Must complete Edutest examination; Must submit MP4 video audition of two contrasting pieces; Must demonstrate minimum AMEB or equivalent level for instrument (specific levels vary by instrument); Students in Year 4 or 5 may apply if one AMEB grade lower than listed; Commitment to participate in two music ensembles required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Acceptance of scholarship required within seven days of offer. Scholarship results are strictly confidential and must not be shared beyond immediate family. Recipients must participate in two music ensembles as determined by Music Learning Leader. Candidates must make genuine commitment to music program at Fintona. Video audition via YouTube, Vimeo, or Dropbox. Not all candidates invited to audition will receive in-person audition. Scholarship covers tuition fees only; does not cover private music lessons beyond the weekly lesson provided, uniform, books, external music exams, stationery, camps, incursions, excursions, or ICT levy.

overview: Fintona offers the Music/Academic Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating both exceptional musical skill and outstanding academic achievement. Recipients receive a 50% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12 plus a weekly instrumental music lesson during term time.
description: The Music/Academic Excellence Scholarship recognises students who combine exceptional musical ability with outstanding academic performance. Recipients receive a 50% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12 and a weekly instrumental music lesson (term time only) with a member of the Music Department. Applicants must complete the Edutest examination and Fintona test component, plus submit an MP4 video recording of two contrasting pieces. Shortlisted applicants are invited to audition in person and will be tested on sight reading and aural exercises appropriate for their instrument and level. Candidates must be performing comfortably at minimum AMEB or equivalent level as specified. Students in Year 4 or 5 may apply if at one AMEB grade lower than required. Recipients must participate in two music ensembles as determined by the Music Learning Leader. Selection is based on Edutest and Fintona examination results, previous school reports, student questionnaire, and interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and enrolled students entering Years 5 to 11; Must be domestic student; Must complete Edutest examination and Fintona test component; Must submit MP4 video audition of two contrasting pieces; Must demonstrate minimum AMEB or equivalent level for instrument; Students in Year 4 or 5 may apply if one AMEB grade lower than listed; Commitment to participate in two music ensembles required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Acceptance of scholarship required within seven days of offer. Scholarship results are strictly confidential and must not be shared beyond immediate family. Recipients must participate in two music ensembles as determined by Music Learning Leader. Candidates must make genuine commitment to music program at Fintona. Video audition via YouTube, Vimeo, or Dropbox. Not all candidates will be invited to in-person audition. Scholarship covers 50% of tuition fees only; does not cover private music lessons beyond the weekly lesson provided, uniform, books, external music exams, stationery, camps, incursions, excursions, or ICT levy. Ongoing progress reports required from staff each semester.

overview: Fintona offers the Principal's All-Rounder Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional leadership, community service, academic excellence, and talent in sports, arts, or performing arts. Recipients receive up to a 30% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12.
description: The Principal's All-Rounder Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional qualities across multiple areas: leadership with confidence and service to community; striving for academic excellence; and excelling in sports, performing arts, or visual arts. Fintona considers all-rounders to be girls who make a difference by leading initiatives and supporting others, work hard to achieve outstanding results, and excel where their skills and passions combine. Recipients receive up to a 30% discount on school tuition fees from point of entry until completion of Year 12. Applicants must submit a Scholarship Application Form (limited to one single-sided typed page) along with supporting documentation, and attend a panel interview at Fintona Girls' School. Selection is based on the applicant's community involvement and achievements detailed in their application form, previous school reports, and panel interview at Fintona. Application forms must focus on the school motto 'Age Quod Agis' and outline how qualities and attributes support the student to do what they do well. There is no application fee for the All-Rounder Scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 8 to 11; Must be domestic student; Must submit Scholarship Application Form with supporting documentation; Must attend panel interview at Fintona; Must demonstrate leadership and community involvement; Must demonstrate academic excellence or talent in arts/sports; Focus on school motto 'Age Quod Agis' in application
special_conditions: No application fee for All-Rounder scholarship. Applicants must submit written application on one single-sided typed page focusing on school motto 'Age Quod Agis' explaining how qualities and attributes support student to do what they do well. Panel interview required at Fintona Girls' School. Acceptance of scholarship required within seven days of offer. Scholarship results are strictly confidential and must not be shared beyond immediate family. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and appropriate behaviour. Fintona reserves discretion to award up to 30% discount - actual award may be less.

school_name: Fish Creek and District Primary School

overview: Fitzroy Community School established a Scholarship Fund at the end of 2013 to aid families who cannot afford the school fees to enrol with a subsidised place. The fund becomes operational when the school receives sufficient donations to offer subsidised placements.
description: The Scholarship Fund was established with the aim of providing financial assistance to families unable to afford school fees. Fitzroy Community School operates as a small school with approximately forty families and does not levy school families to fund scholarships for other children. The scholarship fund becomes operational when donations are received that make it possible to offer subsidised place(s). Being a small, independent primary school, the financial assistance model relies on external donations rather than internal school levies. The school acknowledges that it does not expect to offer scholarships in the immediate future without sufficient donations to support the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families who cannot afford school fees; Financial need assessment required
special_conditions: Donations to the fund are generally anonymous unless the donor wishes to be acknowledged. The scholarship fund operates on a donation basis and the school does not expect to offer any scholarships in the immediate future. Families interested in supporting the fund should contact the principal (Timothy) or the bookkeeper (Jennifer) to make a donation.

school_name: Fitzroy High School - Wurun Senior Campus

overview: Flinders offers an Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability with a minimum average of 85% and above. This merit-based award is open to external applicants for either Carrum Downs or Tyabb Campuses.
description: The Academic Scholarship provides either full or half fee remission for the tuition component of school fees. Selected students applying for an Academic Scholarship will be required to participate in a Scholarship examination. The College evaluates all applications against the student's current academic performance and reports, NAPLAN results, character references, and the scholarship examination. Scholarships will be tenable until the end of Year 12. All successful scholarship students will still need to cover the cost of levies, camps, stationery, electronic devices (Secondary) and any other costs that do not form part of the annual tuition fees. A non-refundable $100 administration fee is required to complete the online application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external applicants not currently enrolled at the College; Minimum average of 85% and above; Must be willing to support the College's values and faith tenets; Must make a positive contribution to the community; Subject to availability of places within the College
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction

overview: Footscray High School provides access to the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), which assists eligible families with the costs of school camps, sporting activities, excursions and incursions. The fund is available to families holding valid concession cards and provides annual payments to support student participation in extracurricular activities.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative that provides financial support to eligible families to cover the costs of school camps, excursions, sporting activities, and outdoor education programs. From 2026, eligible families receive $400 per secondary school student per year. Payments are made directly to the school for the benefit of the eligible student. The fund is available to families who hold a valid Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card, or are temporary Foster Parents. Families with more than one child at the school can combine their CSEF to use on one child or for a specific activity such as a camp. Applications must be submitted to the school by 26 June 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or carer who is beneficiary of a financially means-tested card; Student over 16 who is a mature minor and holds valid means-tested concession card; Eligible concession cards include Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Gold Card, or temporary Foster Parent status; Must be assessed as valid on first student day of Term 1 (28 January 2026) or Term 2 (20 April 2026)
special_conditions: Applications cannot be claimed retrospectively for prior years. Schools must process all applications by 3 July 2026. Year 7 and Prep students in government schools who receive CSEF are also eligible to receive financial help with the cost of school uniforms. CSEF funds can only be used for the eligible student or for that student's siblings at the same school.

overview: Footscray High School offers the Victorian High-Ability Program, which is part of the Department of Education's Student Excellence Program. This program identifies high-performing students and offers them an intensive extension course online designed to target their academic needs in the early years of secondary school.
description: The Victorian High-Ability Program identifies students as high-performing based on their academic performance and offers an intensive extension course delivered online. This program is designed to target the academic needs of high-ability students in the early years of secondary school. After completing the virtual program, students participate in a face-to-face masterclass with other high-ability students from schools in their local area. All masterclasses incorporate appropriate safeguarding measures. Additionally, high-ability students from Year 7 to Year 12 benefit from the Victorian Challenge and Enrichment Series, which provides access to excursions, incursions, workshops and programs run by expert partners and specialist practitioners. These activities are fully funded through the Victorian Challenge and Enrichment Series and are delivered during school hours, after school, on weekends and during school holidays. The Department of Education centrally manages the selection process based on student academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Students identified as high-performing based on academic performance; Open to Year 7 to Year 12 students; Selection managed centrally by the Department of Education; No application required - schools nominate students according to their abilities and interests
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Selection is managed centrally by the Department of Education based on student academic performance. Schools nominate eligible students from Term 4 onwards. Students are withdrawn from normal classes on a rotation basis for activities. Pedagogical Learning Specialists at FHS identify and respond to the needs of high-ability students by facilitating differentiated, deep learning tasks.

overview: Footscray High School students are eligible to apply for the Western Chances Scholarship, an external scholarship program based in Melbourne's west. Western Chances provides scholarships to talented and motivated secondary school students who may not otherwise have an opportunity to pursue their education dreams, with financial support and ongoing assistance.
description: Western Chances is a scholarship program based in Melbourne's west that provides scholarships for up to $1,000 per year for secondary school students. Once a student receives a scholarship, they are eligible for renewal of their scholarship every year whilst engaged in an education pathway. The program supports talented and motivated students who may not otherwise have an opportunity to pursue their education dreams by providing scholarships, opportunity programs and ongoing support. Students from Footscray High School are nominated by the school for consideration. According to recent school announcements, in 2024 ten students from FHS won Western Chances Scholarships, receiving a combined total of $24,200 for 27 students. In 2025, 12 new students received scholarships totalling $10,750, with an additional $16,900 in renewals to 18 existing scholars. The school typically nominates new students in July. Recipients may also be eligible for additional recognition, such as the Lynne Wannan Award of general excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary school student at Footscray High School; Talented and motivated student; Selection through school nomination process; Eligible for annual renewal whilst engaged in education pathway
special_conditions: Students are nominated by Footscray High School rather than applying directly. Recipients are eligible for scholarship renewal every year whilst engaged in an education pathway. Scholarships may be awarded on a competitive basis. Highest awards such as the Lynne Wannan Award of general excellence recognise students with exceptional work in fields such as STEM.

program_name: Friends of FHS Small Grants Program
overview: The Friends of Footscray High School organisation provides an annual Small Grants Program offering grants to members of the school community. These grants are designed to enhance the learning environment and provide opportunities for students to engage with their peers, grow their knowledge and experience, and maintain their wellbeing.
description: The Friends of FHS annual Small Grants Program provides grants of up to $2,000 to members of the school community, including students, teachers and parents/carers. These grants are designed to assist with funding initiatives or projects that enhance the Footscray High School learning environment and provide opportunities for students to engage with their school community and peers, gain opportunities to grow their knowledge and experience, and maintain their mental, physical, social and emotional wellbeing throughout their school years and beyond. Grants are awarded to each campus and are assessed on their potential long-term benefit for as broad a range of students as possible. The program recognises that community support is essential to the school's success and provides a tangible way for stakeholders to contribute to student development and school enhancement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students; Open to teachers; Open to parents and carers; Project must enhance Footscray High School learning environment; Project must benefit a broad range of students; Annual application cycle
special_conditions: Grants are awarded to each campus separately. Assessment is based on potential long-term benefit for as broad a range of students as possible. Friends of FHS also provide community volunteering opportunities for parents, carers and community members to assist with school-based events such as musicals and film festivals.

school_name: Forest Hill College-Forest Hill Deaf Facility
Forest Street Primary School-Forest Street Deaf Facility
Foundation Learning Centre - Malcolm Court Narre Warren
Frankston Special Developmental School-Blackwood Special Schools Outdoor Education Centre
Furlong Park School For Deaf Children
Geelong English Language School - Corio Campus
Geelong English Language School - North Geelong Campus
Geelong Grammar School - Bostock House Campus
Geelong Lutheran College - Armstrong Creek Campus
Geelong Lutheran College - St John's Newtown Campus

overview: Glen Iris Primary School administers the CSEF, a Victorian Government financial assistance program supporting eligible families. The program helps cover costs of school camps, excursions, sports activities and outdoor education programs for students attending the school.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative administered by Glen Iris Primary School to assist eligible families with financial costs. From 2026, the fund provides $400 per year for eligible primary school students. Payments are made directly to the school and allocated towards camps, sports, excursions, and incursions. To be eligible, families must hold a valid means-tested concession card (such as a Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card) or be a temporary foster parent. Applications must be submitted to the school by 26 June 2026, with processing completed by 3 July 2026. Prep and Year 7 CSEF recipients are also eligible for uniform vouchers through State Schools' Relief.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or carer must hold a valid means-tested concession card (Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card); Student must attend Glen Iris Primary School; Parent/carer concession card must be assessed as valid on first day of Term 1 (28 January 2026) or Term 2 (20 April 2026); Student must be aged 5-18 years; Special consideration category available for families on bridging visa, temporary protection visa, community detention or asylum seeker families
special_conditions: CSEF is an annual payment that must be used for eligible students or their siblings at the same school. Funds must be used for camps, sports, excursions and incursions only—not for books, stationery, uniforms (except Prep/Year 7), before/after school care, or music lessons. Schools must seek family agreement before allocating funds. Families can pool CSEF for multiple children to use on one activity. Funds are held for 12 months if a student leaves the school system.

overview: Gladstone Park Secondary College offers the Jake Herd Memorial Scholarship to honour the legacy of Jake Michael Herd, a former student. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate the values and character strengths that Jake exemplified, including leadership, learning agility, passion, enthusiasm, integrity, curiosity, gratitude, humility, insightfulness, connection and empathy.
description: The Jake Herd Memorial Scholarship provides a financial disbursement of $1,000 annually (awarded until scholarship funds are exhausted) towards the cost of a student's education. The scholarship is open to current Years 7-11 students enrolled at Gladstone Park Secondary College. Recipients must submit a personal essay (maximum 500 words) demonstrating the values and character strengths that Jake exhibited, obtain endorsement from a minimum of 2 teachers, provide a copy of their most recent school report, and optionally include supporting portfolio samples, awards or certificates, and letters of recommendation. All application materials must be submitted by the end of Week 6 of Term 4 as a hard copy to the General Office. Scholarship funds are disbursed for the following academic year, and any unused portion is forfeited and returned to the fund. Recipients must provide proof of purchase before funds are released as reimbursement.
eligibility_criteria: Current Years 7-11 student enrolled at Gladstone Park Secondary College; Must demonstrate values and character strengths including leadership, learning agility, passion, enthusiasm, integrity, curiosity, gratitude, humility, insightfulness, connection and empathy; Must submit personal essay of maximum 500 words; Must obtain endorsement from minimum 2 teachers; Must provide copy of most recent school report; Applications must be submitted by end of Week 6 of Term 4
special_conditions: Scholarship funds will be disbursed for the following academic year. Any unused portion of the scholarship will be forfeited and returned to the scholarship fund. Recipient will be required to provide proof of purchase (e.g. tax invoice) to the school business manager before funds are released in the form of a reimbursement. Scholarship recipient will be notified via letter and invited to attend the College end of year awards night. The fund is managed by Jake's family.

overview: Gladstone Park Secondary College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program, designed to cater for the special educational needs of gifted and highly able students. The school is a member of The Association of Accredited SEAL Schools, providing a high-quality program that meets the needs of highly able students through curriculum acceleration.
description: The SEAL program uses curriculum acceleration, specifically a method called 'telescoping', where students complete several years of the school's curriculum in less time. For example, Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 are completed in three rather than four years. This acceleration means material is not skipped but students move more speedily through all curriculum content. Students experience the full breadth of curriculum offered at the college while moving through their learning at a faster pace. Entry into the SEAL program for Year 7 requires students to be currently in Grade 6 and sit the Edutest examination administered by Edutest. Historical records indicate testing has occurred on Saturday mornings in May (e.g., Saturday 18th May at 9:30am). Shortlisted applicants are invited for interviews with their parents/guardians. The program offers no direct monetary value but provides accelerated academic pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6 / Year 6; Must sit Edutest examination; Identified as gifted and highly able through testing process; Must be shortlisted to proceed to interview stage; Shortlisted applicants and their parents/guardians participate in interviews
special_conditions: Students in the SEAL program experience the full breadth of curriculum offered at the college, however they move through their learning at a faster pace through curriculum acceleration (telescoping). Only students currently in Grade 6 may apply. If a child has applied for other SEAL programs which use EduTest, it is only necessary to sit the test at one school. Test results from other providers (e.g., ACER) are not made available to Gladstone Park. Provisions can be made for students to sit the test at a later date if there are timing conflicts. Students continue in the program through Year 10 if they complete the accelerated curriculum successfully.

overview: Gladstone Park Secondary College offers a flagship Italian Immersion Program using Content Learning in Language (CLIL) methodology, where students learn History and Geography in Italian. This program develops students' Italian language skills while providing intellectual engagement and improved literacy and critical thinking skills.
description: The Italian Immersion Program uses the CLIL (Content Learning in Language) approach, where students learn History and Geography subjects in Italian rather than English. This method benefits students' Italian language learning through a more engaging and relevant process of language acquisition while giving them better retention of the content taught. Students' ability to focus improves dramatically when concentrating on understanding content delivered in Italian, resulting in better information retention than when learning in English. The higher intellectual engagement required in CLIL classes helps students improve their literacy skills, critical thinking, problem-solving and overall academic success. This program is highly regarded internationally and in the European Union, where it is compulsory for students to take at least one subject through CLIL in their final year of high school. The program is offered from Year 7 onwards and does not carry a direct monetary value but provides enhanced language and content learning pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all Year 7 students; Interest in learning History and Geography in Italian; Commitment to language immersion program; No specific academic threshold mentioned
special_conditions: Program begins at Year 7 and continues through secondary schooling. Students learn History and Geography content delivered entirely in Italian. The program is part of a broader curriculum offering and students participate alongside mainstream classes. An information brochure is available on the school website describing the program in detail.

overview: Glen Eira College offers College Council Academic Excellence Scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in Year 7, Year 9, and Year 11 students. These scholarships reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: The College Council Academic Excellence Scholarships are awarded annually to students entering Years 7, 9, and 11 who achieve outstanding academic results. If successful scholars maintain their excellent grades, the scholarship is automatically awarded for a second consecutive year, providing continuity of recognition. The selection process involves evaluation of student academic achievement and potential. These scholarships recognise students demonstrating the highest level of academic excellence across the year cohort. Awards are presented at the college's annual Presentation Night, celebrating student achievement publicly.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Outstanding academic achievement; Demonstrated commitment to learning; Academic performance at highest level in cohort
special_conditions: If successful scholars maintain excellent grades, the scholarship is automatically awarded for the second year. Awards are presented at the college's annual Presentation Night.

overview: Glen Eira College offers the Noel Parker Scholarship to support students facing financial difficulties. This scholarship encourages students to continue their education and helps bridge the financial gap that may otherwise prevent access to schooling.
description: The Noel Parker Scholarship is designed for students currently enrolled in Years 8 to 12 who are facing financial difficulties and at risk of not being able to continue their education. The scholarship values are not specified on the official page, but selection criteria focus on genuine financial need combined with demonstrated academic and citizenship qualities. Candidates must show a positive academic record, commitment to Glen Eira's Code of Conduct, strong citizenship qualities, and intention to undertake tertiary study upon completion of VCE. The scholarship supports students in maintaining their educational journey despite economic challenges.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Years 8 to 12; Facing financial difficulties; Positive academic record; Demonstrated commitment to Glen Eira's Code of Conduct; Strong citizenship qualities; Intention to undertake tertiary study at completion of VCE
special_conditions: Encourages students facing financial difficulties to continue their education. Selection criteria include positive academic record, commitment to school Code of Conduct, citizenship qualities, and intention to undertake tertiary study at completion of VCE.

overview: Glen Eira College offers the Mercia Sailah Scholarship to support Year 10 and Year 11 students with demonstrated financial need and strong commitment to completing their secondary education. The scholarship recognises students who intend to pursue tertiary education.
description: The Mercia Sailah Scholarship provides financial support valued at $500 per award to eligible Year 10 and Year 11 students. Applicants must have been students of Glen Eira College for a minimum of the previous 12 months, demonstrating long-term commitment to the school community. Students must intend to complete Year 12 at Glen Eira and continue to tertiary education. Selection criteria also include commitment to making promising academic progress and eligibility for Youth Assistance. Two school and one community referee must be provided as part of the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Years 10 and 11; Enrolled at Glen Eira College for minimum 12 months; Intend to complete Year 12 at Glen Eira; Intend to continue to tertiary education; Committed to making promising academic progress; Eligible for Youth Assistance
special_conditions: Minimum 12 months continuous enrolment at Glen Eira College required. Two school and one community referee must be provided. Recipients must be eligible for Youth Assistance and demonstrate commitment to promising academic progress.

overview: Glen Eira College offers the Instrumental Music Program Prize to recognise and encourage student excellence and commitment to instrumental music. These annual prizes support students pursuing their passion for instrumental music at the college.
description: The Instrumental Music Program Prize is an annual endowment consisting of two prizes, each valued at $250, awarded to deserving students entering Years 8 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional passion for and commitment to instrumental music. The prizes are designed to encourage continued pursuit of instrumental music studies. Eligible students are nominated directly by Glen Eira College music staff based on demonstrated excellence, dedication, and progress in their instrumental music studies. Recipients must be continuing their studies at Glen Eira College. Awards are presented at the college's annual Presentation Night.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 8 to 12; Continuing studies at Glen Eira College; Demonstrated passion for instrumental music; Commitment to instrumental music studies; Must be nominated by music staff
special_conditions: Eligible students are nominated by music staff. Two prizes of $250 each awarded annually. Recipients must continue studies at Glen Eira College. Awards presented at annual Presentation Night.

overview: Glen Eira College's Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program is designed for gifted and high-potential students in Years 7 to 9 who are motivated and academically advanced. The program offers enriched learning experiences that enhance intellectual potential and development.
description: The SEAL Program is an accredited selective entry program coordinated with The Academy of Accredited SEAL Schools (TAASS) for Years 7 to 9. Students involved in the SEAL Program receive an extended range of options in their final years of schooling, including additional VCE subjects and University Enhancement Studies. Participation in the program enhances potential to achieve greater Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR) scores and improves transition to university studies. Students in the SEAL Program are expected to learn at the edge of their ability, developing resilience and independent learning skills. A $100 application fee is required for Edutest registration. The SEAL entrance test is administered by Edutest and conducted on a specified date each year. Candidates must apply to Glen Eira College through the standard enrolment process and then register separately with Edutest.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Motivated and academically advanced; Demonstrated high potential; Eligible for standard Year 7 enrolment at Glen Eira College; Required to participate in Edutest entrance testing
special_conditions: Testing for 2027 Year 7 SEAL class conducted on Friday 31st July 2026. Application for Year 7 Placement forms due to government primary schools by Friday 8th May 2026. Participation in testing does not guarantee SEAL program placement. Students must be fully immersed in all aspects of college life including instrumental music, performing arts, sports, and co-curricular activities. Expectation of high challenge and failure as integral to learning at the edge of ability.

overview: The Margaret Schofield Memorial Scholarship is awarded annually to VCE Music: Solo Performance students from Victorian government schools who demonstrate commitment to music and aspire to careers in music performance. The scholarship supports tertiary music education.
description: The Margaret Schofield Memorial Scholarship provides an annual award of $4,000 to support tertiary education costs in music performance. The scholarship is offered to government school students currently studying VCE Music: Solo Performance. Selection panel members consider applicants' demonstrated commitment to music in recent years and future career aspirations in music performance. The student's VCE Music: Solo Performance study score is a key consideration in the selection process. The scholarship was established by the family of the late Margaret Schofield in recognition of her achievements as a musician and her commitment to music education. This scholarship is coordinated by the Victorian Curriculum and Assessment Authority (VCAA). Application forms are published in the VCAA Bulletin (September and October editions).
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in VCE Music: Solo Performance; Attending a Victorian government school; Demonstrated commitment to music in recent years; Aspiration toward future career in music performance; Study score in VCE Music: Solo Performance considered
special_conditions: Application form published in VCAA Bulletin (September and October editions). Coordinated by Victorian Curriculum and Assessment Authority (VCAA). Award contributes towards cost of tertiary education in music performance. Selection based on demonstrated commitment to music and career aspirations. VCE Music: Solo Performance study score taken into consideration.

overview: Gleneagles Secondary College offers Principal's Scholarships to Year 7 students who exemplify college values of Respect, Inclusion, Stamina and Excellence in the academic category. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate skills and knowledge above the expected level for a Year 6 student in one or more learning areas.
description: To be considered for an academic scholarship, students must demonstrate skills and knowledge above the expected level for a Year 6 student in one or more learning areas. They must be committed to ongoing learning and have an exemplary school report. Selection will be through an application and interview process. The scholarship is available for students entering Year 7 and provides recognition of exceptional academic ability upon entry to secondary education. Applications for Year 7 2026 Principal's Scholarships will open early in Term 3, 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate skills and knowledge above expected level for Year 6 in one or more learning areas; Must be committed to ongoing learning; Must have exemplary school report
special_conditions: Recipients must embody college values of Respect, Inclusion, Stamina and Excellence. Scholarships are reviewed based on ongoing academic performance and engagement.

overview: Gleneagles Secondary College offers Sports Scholarships to students who have demonstrated outstanding sporting ability in any team sport. This scholarship recognises talented athletes, sporting club members, coaches, and players who show ongoing commitment to school or community sporting programs.
description: Sports Scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated outstanding sporting ability in any team sport. Students who are talented athletes, sporting club members, coaches, players or have demonstrated ongoing commitment to a school or community sporting club or program may apply for the scholarship. Selection will be through an application and interview process. The school recognises the importance of well-rounded development including sporting excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating outstanding sporting ability in team sports; Must be a talented athlete, sporting club member, coach, or player; Must demonstrate ongoing commitment to school or community sporting programs; Open to any year level
special_conditions: Recipients must demonstrate ongoing commitment to sporting excellence and positive engagement with school values and community.

overview: Gleneagles Secondary College offers Arts Scholarships to talented students in expressive arts disciplines. The scholarship recognises singers, dancers, musicians, artists, designers, photographers and other performing and visual arts practitioners who demonstrate exceptional ability and passion.
description: If you are a talented singer, dancer, musician, artist, designer, photographer, or any other form of expressive arts you may apply for the scholarship. The Arts program provides students with the opportunity to excel in an area they are passionate about. Selection will be through an application and interview process. The school values the contribution of arts to student development and community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to talented students in any expressive arts discipline; Must demonstrate exceptional ability in singing, dancing, music, visual arts, design, photography or other performing/visual arts; Eligible from any year level; Must show passion and commitment to their chosen art form
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to participate actively in arts programs and contribute to school cultural life. Portfolio or performance audition may be required.

program_name: Principal's Scholarship - EAL (English as an Additional Language)
overview: Gleneagles Secondary College offers EAL Scholarships to students from any year level who have demonstrated resilience and commitment in language learning and cultural integration. This scholarship recognises students who show strength and persistence in developing English language skills.
description: EAL Scholarships are available to students from any year level who have demonstrated resilience and commitment at language school, primary school or in the community. The scholarship recognises the challenges and achievements of students learning English as an additional language. Selection will be through an application and interview process. The school is committed to supporting linguistically diverse learners and celebrating their contributions to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to any year level; Must have demonstrated resilience and commitment at language school, primary school or in the community; Must show strength in English language acquisition; EAL background required
special_conditions: Recipients must demonstrate continued commitment to language learning and positive engagement with school community. EAL support services are available to scholarship holders.

overview: Gleneagles Secondary College is one of only 34 SEAL schools in Victoria, offering a Select Entry Accelerated Learning program for gifted students. The SEAL program provides a focused educational environment for academically gifted students who demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability and potential.
description: SEAL (Select Entry Accelerated Learning) is a streaming program for academically gifted students entering Year 7. The program allows students to undertake Year 8 work in Year 7, with the option to complete secondary education in five years instead of six. Students develop complex investigative, problem-solving and creative thinking skills. The program supports the unique emotional, social and intellectual needs of gifted students. Testing is held on a scheduled date (2025: Saturday 9 August), consisting of a 5-part paper-based test. Shortlisted candidates are contacted for further consideration. Application and parent referral must be submitted through Edutest portal by Friday 1 August, with teacher referral forms due by Friday 1 August, 2025. The test fee is $99.00.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate natural giftedness in academic areas; Teacher referral required; Parent referral required; Registration and test fee payment required by application deadline
special_conditions: SEAL is an accelerated learning program, not a fee-waiving scholarship. Students complete Year 8 curriculum in Year 7. Gleneagles is accredited by The Academy of Accredited SEAL Schools (TAASS) and was re-accredited in 2024. Students may have access to first-year university subjects in Year 12 through approved providers.

overview: Glowrey Catholic Primary School offers concessional school fees to families experiencing financial difficulty. The school works with families to make education accessible by providing fee assistance to eligible families meeting specific criteria.
description: Glowrey Catholic Primary School provides concessional school fees for families experiencing financial hardship that impacts their ability to pay school fees. Families experiencing financial difficulties should contact the Office Manager (Elisa) or School Registrar (Emilia) to make an appointment with the Principal to discuss eligibility. The financial value varies based on individual family circumstances and assessment of need. The program is available to any family that meets the eligibility criteria determined through consultation with school administration. Support is ongoing for the duration of enrolment, subject to continued financial need assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship impacting ability to pay school fees; Must meet school-determined eligibility criteria; Contact required with Office Manager or School Registrar to arrange appointment with Principal
special_conditions: Families must contact the Office Manager (Elisa) or School Registrar (Emilia) to make an appointment with the Principal. Assessment of financial need is required to determine eligibility and the level of fee assistance available. Support is subject to ongoing assessment of family circumstances.

overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward students with outstanding academic achievement. The scholarships are available at the Tarneit Campus for entry to Year 7, Year 9 and Year 11, and at the Mambourin Campus for Year 7 entry only. The scholarship is open to all students, including current GNLC students and those not yet enrolled at the College.
description: Good News Lutheran College's Academic Scholarship programme provides fee reductions to high-achieving students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11 (Tarneit Campus) or Year 7 (Mambourin Campus). Scholarship recipients can receive a reduction of 20% to 50% on annual tuition fees for up to two years, provided they meet the Academic Scholarship conditions. Awards are based on an academic examination administered through the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER), which uses objective measures to assess student performance. Applicants can apply directly to the College or through the ACER website, with the scholarship examination held in early 2025 for 2026 entry. Scholarship benefits are renewable annually subject to meeting the Academic Scholarship conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; currently enrolled or not yet enrolled at Good News Lutheran College; applicable to Year 7 entry (both Tarneit and Mambourin campuses); Year 9 entry (Tarneit Campus only); Year 11 entry (Tarneit Campus only); successful performance on ACER scholarship assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must meet the Academic Scholarship conditions to receive the award. Fee reductions are available for up to two years, subject to annual renewal and continued satisfaction of Academic Scholarship conditions. Scholarships are offered through both direct application to the College and via the ACER website.

school_name: Good News Lutheran College - Mambourin Campus
overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability and is awarded based on objective measures provided by ACER. Scholarships are available at both Tarneit Campus (Year 7, 9, 11) and Mambourin Campus (Year 7 only).
description: Good News Lutheran College awards Academic Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement as measured by the ACER Scholarship Test. Scholarship recipients can receive a fee reduction of 20%-50% on annual tuition fees for up to two years, provided they meet the Academic Scholarship conditions. The scholarship recognises students with outstanding academic performance and commitment to learning. All students are eligible to apply, including current GNLC students and those not yet enrolled at the college. Selection is based entirely on academic examination performance using objective measures provided by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The scholarship duration is two years, contingent upon recipients continuing to meet the specified Academic Scholarship conditions throughout their tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11 in 2026 (Tarneit Campus); Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026 (Mambourin Campus); Open to all students including current GNLC students and those not yet enrolled at the college; Selection based on ACER Scholarship Test performance
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must meet the Academic Scholarship conditions to maintain their award for the full two-year duration. Mambourin Campus scholarships are available for Year 7 entry only in 2026, while Tarneit Campus offers scholarships for Year 7, Year 9, and Year 11 entry. Applications for 2026 entry require registration through the ACER website and participation in the scholarship examination held early in 2025.

overview: Good Samaritan Catholic Primary School participates in the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) School Fees Concession Program to assist families experiencing financial hardship. This program provides reduced school fees to eligible families who would otherwise be impeded from accessing a Catholic education, ensuring families can meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay.
description: The MACS School Fees Concession Program offers significantly reduced annual fees for eligible families. Concession rates range from $780 per year for one child ($15 per week or $195 per term), $1,196 per year for two children ($23 per week or $299 per term), to $1,560 per year for three or more children ($30 per week or $390 per term). These reduced fees cover all standard school fees and levies. The program is available for the full duration of enrolment, with pro-rata adjustments if a family becomes eligible mid-year. Selection is based on meeting specific eligibility criteria including holding approved means-tested concession cards (Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or DVA Gold Card), Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, or demonstrating severe financial hardship. To receive the concession, families must agree to a direct debit or Centrepay deduction arrangement and submit the MACS School Fees Concession Program application form along with proof of eligible card status.
eligibility_criteria: Family holding approved means-tested concession card (Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or DVA Gold Card) in the name of the fee payer; Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Severe financial hardship; Must agree to direct debit or Centrepay deduction arrangement; Application must be submitted prior to commencement of school year; Student must be enrolled at Good Samaritan Catholic Primary School
special_conditions: Approved applicants will be charged only the annual concessional fee which covers all standard school fees and levies, but does not cover Camp Levy ($320 for Years 5 and 6) or Water Safety Program Levy, which must be paid in addition. If applicant becomes eligible mid-year, pro-rata concession is granted. Default in payment may result in the concession being withdrawn. The concessional fee is fixed annually by Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS). All families receiving a fee concession must notify the school if their financial circumstances change sufficiently to affect the level of concession offered.

overview: Good Shepherd School provides concessional school fees to families experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. The policy ensures that families can meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay, while maintaining a consistent approach to fee concessions across all MACS primary schools.
description: Under the MACS Concessional Fee Policy, Good Shepherd School offers reduced tuition fees and levies to eligible families. Eligible categories include families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent, families holding a Health Care Card and eligible for the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card, families identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard, and families experiencing severe financial hardship. The policy operates on the principle of Catholic social teaching, prioritising the dignity of the human person and a preferential option for the poor. Pro-rata concessions are provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm ongoing eligibility. Applications for concessional school fees must be made on the school's approved form, and approved applicants have an automated payment plan established. The concession covers both tuition fees and levies.
eligibility_criteria: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Families holding a valid Health Care Card and eligible for CSEF; Families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card; Families identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard; Families experiencing severe financial hardship; Students of The Royal Children's Hospital, Melbourne required to relocate for long-term medical treatment; Enrolled as a student at Good Shepherd School (Years P-6)
special_conditions: Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility. Approved applicants must have an automated payment plan established. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to discuss alternate arrangements with the school office. Information provided in support of concessional school fees is treated in the strictest confidence in accordance with the school's Privacy Policy.

overview: Good Shepherd School supports families with Health Care Cards to access government funding to offset costs associated with camps, sports and excursions. The CSEF provides eligible families with a payment towards school fees to help reduce costs associated with co-curricular activities.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a government-funded program that provides financial assistance to eligible families to offset the cost of camps and excursions. Parents or carers/guardians holding a valid Health Care Card, Aged Pension Card, or other qualifying government benefit cards are encouraged to apply for the CSEF. The government provides $150.00 per student to eligible families to help cover expenses associated with school camps, sporting events, and excursions. Good Shepherd School facilitates applications for families to access this funding, which is paid to the school against school fees. Families who are eligible should contact the school office to apply for this assistance program, which helps to ensure all students can participate in school-based co-curricular activities regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families holding a valid Health Care Card; Families receiving Aged Pension; Families receiving other Australian Government financial assistance; Students enrolled at Good Shepherd School (Years P-6)
special_conditions: Families must hold a valid Health Care Card or other qualifying government benefit card to be eligible. The $150 per student per year is a government payment paid to the school against school fees. Families should contact the school office for application procedures and deadlines.

overview: Global Village Learning operates a sliding fee scale based on financial capacity. The school is committed to accessibility and ensures no family is turned away for financial reasons, offering a needs-based approach to tuition costs.
description: Global Village Learning employs a flexible sliding fee scale model based on individual family financial capacity to ensure education is accessible to all learners. The school explicitly states it is committed to accessibility and no family will be turned away due to financial hardship. This approach allows families to pay tuition at a level appropriate to their circumstances while maintaining the quality of educational delivery. Additionally, the school offers sibling discounts to support families with multiple children enrolled. The sliding scale is discussed during the personalised enrolment process with the school registrar. All-inclusive tuition fees cover specialist classes, excursions, healthy food programs, textbooks, stationery, and activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families enrolling at Global Village Learning; Assessment based on demonstrated financial capacity; No family turned away for financial reasons
special_conditions: Sliding fee scale is determined through personalised enrolment conversations with school registrar Beth. Sibling discounts are available to support families with multiple children. All-inclusive tuition covers specialist classes, excursions, food programs, textbooks, stationery, and activities; camps are invoiced separately and participation is optional. Community members can contribute financially to sponsor a learner as an alternative support mechanism.

school_name: Gowrie Street Primary School Shepparton-Manega Koorie Annexe

overview: Goulburn Valley Grammar School offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in an independent external academic achievement test. The scholarship is available for entry to Years 5, 7, 10 and 11, rewarding students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through competitive EduTest examinations.
description: The Academic Scholarship covers all compulsory school fees for Years 7 to 12. This includes Tuition Fee, Capital Charge and Fixed Charge. Parents are expected to pay for textbooks, school uniform, travel expenses and other optional items not covered by fees. The scholarship is awarded based on merit, with selection determined through an independent external academic achievement test conducted by EduTest Australia. Registrations open from December 2025 and registrations close on Friday 13th March 2026, with examinations held on Saturday 21st March 2026. The school will provide scholarship candidates with a report produced by EduTest with general information about their performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5, 7, 10 and 11; Must be a new enrolment at the School; Must achieve outstanding performance on EduTest Australia independent academic achievement test; Registration and payment online only
special_conditions: All decisions regarding scholarships are at the discretion of the School Principal. EduTest has indicated that actual scores (or order of merit) must not be communicated to parents. This is a condition of entering students to this examination. Scholarship covers compulsory fees only; students must pay for textbooks, uniform, travel and optional items.

overview: Goulburn Valley Grammar School offers the GVGS Foundation Social Justice Scholarship to provide exceptional educational opportunities for eligible students who are likely to engage positively in the full educational programme and thrive. These scholarships recognise outstanding achievement and demonstrate the school's commitment to promoting social justice in the local community through education.
description: The GVGS Foundation Social Justice Scholarships are established to provide exceptional educational opportunities for eligible students.
