These generous scholarships offer major fee reductions and cover all compulsory school fees for Years 7 to 12, including Tuition Fee, Capital Charge and Fixed Charge. Parents are expected to pay for textbooks, school uniform, travel expenses and other optional items not covered by fees. Social Justice Scholarships are awarded based on merit, including outstanding achievement and qualities demonstrated across a range of criteria. The domains considered include academic effort and achievement, the Arts, sport, leadership and community. Successful applicants will also need to demonstrate their potential to lead within the community. All decisions regarding scholarships are at the discretion of the School Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible student likely to engage positively in full educational programme; Must demonstrate exceptional qualities and potential to lead in community; Must demonstrate outstanding achievement across academic, arts, sport, leadership and/or community domains; Applicant must have potential to thrive; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident who arrived in Australia from a humanitarian refugee background may be eligible
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 entry have now closed. Scholarships and Priority Entry Programmes 2027 applications have closed. All decisions are at the discretion of the School Principal. It is anticipated that all Scholarship outcomes will be communicated to applicants in June 2026. Scholarship covers compulsory fees only; students must pay for textbooks, uniform, travel and optional items.

overview: The Priority Entry Programme – Academic provides an enrolment access pathway for families who might otherwise not receive admission due to extensive waiting lists. The programme is awarded on merit based on outstanding achievement in an independent external academic achievement test conducted by EduTest Australia.
description: The Priority Entry Programme – Academic is available only to new enrolments at the School. Successful candidates under this programme will receive priority enrolment at the school. Currently the school has extensive waiting lists at most year levels. This programme includes no fee remission or discount. Candidates are awarded on merit based on outstanding achievement in an independent external academic achievement test conducted by EduTest Australia. Registrations open from December 2025 and registrations close on Friday 13th March 2026, with examinations held on Saturday 21st March 2026. The examination venue is Goulburn Valley Grammar School, 455 Verney Road, Shepparton. It is anticipated that results will be shared with applicants in June 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new enrolments only; Must achieve outstanding performance on EduTest Australia independent academic achievement test; Available at major entry years 5 and 7; Priority entry does not include any fee remission or fee discount; Only a small number of places likely to be offered each year
special_conditions: Only available to new enrolments at the School. Applicants will be notified of the arrangements one week prior to the examination date. This is a priority entry programme only and does not include any fee remission or discount. Small number of places likely to be offered each year.

overview: The Priority Entry Programme – General Excellence provides an enrolment access pathway for families who might otherwise not receive admission. The programme is awarded on merit based on outstanding achievements and qualities demonstrated across a range of domains including Academic Achievement, The Arts, Sport, Leadership and Community.
description: The Priority Entry Programme – General Excellence is available only to new enrolments at the School and is generally awarded in Year 7 but may be available at other year levels. Successful candidates under this programme will receive priority enrolment at the school. Currently the school has extensive waiting lists at most year levels. This programme includes no fee discount. Candidates are awarded on merit based on outstanding achievements and qualities demonstrated across a range of domains. The areas considered include Academic Achievement, The Arts, Sport, Leadership and Community. The Principal considers each application and short listed candidates and their parents/caregivers are invited to attend an interview with the Principal. A second interview may be required. All decisions will be made by the Principal. It is anticipated that all Priority Entry Programme – General Excellence outcomes will be communicated to applicants in June 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new enrolments only; Must demonstrate outstanding achievements and qualities across multiple domains; Academic Achievement, The Arts, Sport, Leadership and/or Community involvement considered; Generally available at Year 7 entry; May be available at other year levels; Expression of Interest required; Priority entry does not include any fee discount
special_conditions: Only available to new enrolments at the School. Generally awarded in Year 7 but may be available at other year levels. The 2027 Priority Entry Programme – General Excellence Expression of Interest has now closed. All decisions made by the Principal. Short listed candidates and their parents/caregivers invited to attend interview; a second interview may be required. This is a priority entry programme only and does not include any fee discount.

school_name: Greater Shepparton Secondary College - Greater Shepparton College Deaf Facility

overview: Greater Shepparton Secondary College offers the Myra Jean Slee Music Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate commitment and achievement in music. The scholarship was established in 2022 through a generous donation to honour the memory of Myra Jean Slee, a long-time member of the Shepparton community with a life-long love of music and dance.
description: The Myra Jean Slee Music Scholarship provides annual financial support of $500 to eligible GSSC students who demonstrate outstanding aptitude and commitment in music. Two scholarships are awarded annually, with recipients selected based on their demonstrated excellence in instrumental music, vocal music, or other music disciplines. The scholarship is tenable for the duration of enrolment at GSSC and recipients are expected to actively participate in the music program and contribute to the school's musical life. The scholarships commenced in 2022 and continue for a ten-year period ending in 2032. A commemorative plaque bearing Myra Jean Slee's name, with the names of student recipients progressively added, is displayed in the vicinity of the school's state-of-the-art dance, music and performance spaces.
eligibility_criteria: Current GSSC students; Demonstrated outstanding aptitude, commitment and achievement in music; Active participation in school music programs or ensembles
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually for the next decade (2022-2032). Recipients are expected to maintain active participation in music at GSSC and contribute to the broader school music community. The award is presented as part of the school's recognition of achievement in the arts.

overview: Greensborough Primary School offers the Watsonia RSL School Captain Scholarship annually to recognise outstanding leadership. The scholarship is awarded to School Captains who display excellent leadership skills, demonstrate school values, and serve as excellent role models for their peers.
description: Each year, Watsonia RSL provides financial support to School Captains at Greensborough Primary School. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated excellent leadership skills, alignment with school values, and their ability to be positive role models within the student community. The scholarship recognises student leadership contributions to the school. The specific financial value and duration of the award are not disclosed in available school publications. Selection occurs annually, with winners announced to the broader school community. Recipients are invited to represent the school at community events, including Remembrance Day ceremonies at the Watsonia RSL.
eligibility_criteria: Must be appointed as a School Captain at Greensborough Primary School; Must display excellent leadership skills; Must demonstrate school values; Must be an excellent role model for peers
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to represent the school at community events and ceremonies. Award is based on demonstrated leadership throughout the school year.

overview: Greensborough College offers scholarships to young people who diligently seek to realise their potential in all pursuits undertaken whilst a student of the College. Applicants will demonstrate preparedness to embrace the College's values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality.
description: Greensborough College offers a Scholarship Program available to prospective and current students who demonstrate commitment to achieving their potential across all aspects of school life. The program recognises students who embody the college's core values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality. Applicants are expected to show commitment to academic excellence, co-curricular participation, and community contribution. Applications are assessed on merit and the school reviews candidates' overall achievement and potential. The specific financial benefit, duration and selection process details are managed by the school's scholarship committee. Interested students should contact the college directly for complete information regarding available awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 and above students; Must demonstrate commitment to the College's values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality; Academic performance and co-curricular involvement considered; Renewable based on annual review
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate preparedness to embrace the College's values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality. Recipients are expected to maintain academic standards and co-curricular involvement.

overview: The Athlete Development Program provides student athletes with the opportunity to upgrade their athletic performance whilst at school. Their involvement will help them achieve their personal best in sporting pursuits and in all facets of their schooling and life beyond Greensborough College.
description: The Athlete Development Program (ADP) at Greensborough College provides comprehensive athletic development for qualifying student athletes. The program includes access to a high-performance centre, sports-specific performance testing, comprehensive musculoskeletal screening, ongoing fitness assessments, goal-setting plans, and individualized strength and conditioning programs. Participants engage in two strength and conditioning sessions per week. Full scholarships are available covering all expenses except training uniform (additional cost). The program is subject to annual review and acceptance is conditional on progressive performance and compliance with school standards. Training occurs during normal school timetable and additional sessions before, during, or after school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students entering Greensborough College in 2026; Must demonstrate athletic potential and commitment to athletic development; Application based on school report and assessment; Participation in trials required; Subject to annual review and progressive assessment
special_conditions: Full Scholarships available cover all expenses except training uniform which is an additional cost. Acceptance into ADP is subject to progressive and annual reviews. A deposit of $200 is due by October 17, 2025 and balance of payments due by December 5, 2025. Most training is completed during normal school timetable. Minimum attendance standards and disciplinary conduct expected.

overview: Greensborough College offers a High Achievers Program for students in Years 7 and 8 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. Highly able students together with those with strong work ethic are urged to apply. Based on assessments undertaken during orientation, identified students are invited to join specialised high ability classes in Mathematics and/or English.
description: The High Achievers Program provides acceleration and enrichment for gifted and talented students at Greensborough College. Students in Year 7 are identified through orientation assessments and invited to participate in specialised high ability Mathematics and/or English classes. Students who consistently perform at a high level in Year 7 are invited to remain in high achievers classes in Year 8. The program includes extension subjects from Year 9 onwards to accelerate and challenge advanced learners. There are no additional costs for families associated with High Ability Programs. Students are supported by a dedicated High Ability Coordinator and selected specialist teachers to support their studies. The program allows students to participate in a range of internal and external sequences of learning tailored to their specific learning needs.
eligibility_criteria: Identified through assessment during Year 7 orientation; Top academic performers in Mathematics and/or English; Consistent high performance required for continuation into Year 8; Demonstrated strong work ethic; All year levels welcome to participate in external programs
special_conditions: There are no additional costs for families associated with High Ability Programs. Students are invited based on assessment results and teacher nomination. Internal identification through orientation assessments and ongoing teacher recommendation.

overview: The Victorian High-Ability Program is an enrichment program offered to high-ability students across Victorian government schools. It addresses the needs of high-ability students to engage with challenging material in English and Mathematics delivered through Virtual Schools Victoria.
description: The Victorian High-Ability Program (VHAP) is an enrichment initiative delivered through Virtual Schools Victoria for eligible students in Years 7 and 8. The program consists of ten 1-hour online synchronous lessons delivered by specialist teachers, with times alternating so students do not miss the same class every week. Each term, eligible students selected by the Department of Education participate in a 10-week program facilitated by the school's High Ability Practice Coordinator. Teachers can also nominate high-achieving students in English and/or Mathematics to participate. The program engages students in tasks involving critical thinking, problem solving and creativity, enriching their understanding of English and Mathematics. No cost to families.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible students in Year 7 and Year 8; Selected by Department of Education or teacher nomination; High-achieving in English and/or Mathematics; Demonstrated ability in critical thinking and problem-solving
special_conditions: Ten 1-hour online lessons per term run through Virtual Schools Victoria. Lessons conducted during school hours with alternating times. Upon completion, students receive a certificate of participation. Selection determined by Department of Education criteria.

overview: The Centre for Higher Education Studies (CHES) provides accelerated programs for high-achieving senior students from government secondary schools. CHES offers specialised electives for Year 10 students and VCE subjects for Year 11 and 12 students.
description: The Centre for Higher Education Studies (CHES) is a centre of excellence designed to improve educational outcomes for high-achieving and high-ability students across Victoria. Greensborough College supports applications for high-achieving students and assists them in submitting applications to CHES for selection to their chosen academic program. Year 10 students can undertake CHES specialised electives (one semester in length) via flexible study options, with one elective available per semester to replace a school-based elective. Students accepted into CHES receive a dedicated support teacher and study sessions at Greensborough College. Year 11 and 12 VCE students can undertake a VCE subject through CHES as part of their overall program, with dedicated support provided by the college. Successful completion of a CHES subject may add 3-5 points to ATAR.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students: High-achieving academic record; Selection by CHES through application process; Must be accepted to undertake elective; Year 11-12 students: VCE students; High academic achievement; Application and selection through CHES process; Dedicated support provided
special_conditions: Students accepted to CHES subjects receive dedicated support teacher at Greensborough College. Year 10 electives are one semester in length accessed via flexible study options. Successful completion of VCE through CHES may add 3-5 ATAR points.

overview: Greensborough College offers scholarships to young people who diligently seek to realise their potential in all pursuits undertaken whilst a student of the College. Applicants will demonstrate preparedness to embrace the College's values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality.
description: Scholarships offered by Greensborough College are available to young people who demonstrate commitment to academic excellence and well-rounded achievement. The scholarship program recognises students who show both academic merit and dedication to their personal development. Applicants must demonstrate preparedness to embrace the College's core values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality. The program is open to students entering Year 7 and selection is based on demonstrated potential and willingness to strive for excellence across all pursuits. Recipients are expected to maintain high standards of conduct and continue their commitment to the college community throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry students; Must demonstrate commitment to realising personal potential; Must embrace College values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality; Open to co-educational applicants
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate preparedness to embrace the College's values of Courage, Aspiration, Respect and Equality. Recipients are expected to maintain standards of conduct and continue commitment to the college community.

overview: Greensborough College offers the High Achievers Program to identify and support high-ability students at each stage of their secondary education. Students identified during orientation are invited to join specialised high-ability Maths and/or English classes to access enriched curriculum and targeted support.
description: The High Achievers Program is an internal program at Greensborough College designed to identify and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and consistent high-level performance. Based on assessments undertaken during orientation, identified students in Year 7 are invited to join either a specialised high ability Maths and/or English class. Students who consistently perform at an acknowledged high level are then invited to remain in the selected high achievers class in Year 8. The program continues for high-ability students across all year levels and provides access to a unique suite of enrichment programs. There are no additional costs for families associated with High Ability Programs, making this an inclusive opportunity for academically gifted students to extend their learning.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry - based on assessments undertaken during orientation; Demonstrated consistent high academic performance; Identified by school staff as high-ability student; Open to all genders
special_conditions: There are no additional costs for families associated with High Ability Programs. Selection is based on assessments undertaken during orientation and ongoing performance monitoring.

overview: Greensborough College offers the Athlete Development Program (ADP) to provide student athletes with the opportunity to upgrade their athletic performance whilst at school. The ADP provides access to strength and conditioning training, performance assessments, and sport-specific coaching to help aspiring athletes reach their full potential.
description: The Athlete Development Program (ADP) at Greensborough College is designed to empower student athletes to excel in their sports by providing individualised strength and conditioning programs, comprehensive performance testing, musculoskeletal screening, and sports-specific coaching. The program philosophy emphasises 'Students First, Athletes Second' and includes access to the College's High Performance Centre and surrounding sporting facilities. Participants receive individualised training programs, fitness assessments, goal-setting plans, and qualified expert supervision. The program includes sports coaching in three sports: AFL, Basketball and Tennis. Acceptance into the ADP is subject to progressive and annual reviews based on academic achievement, sporting progress, conduct, attitude, effort and participation. A deposit of $200 is due upon acceptance with balance payments due by December.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Year 7; Must complete online application form; Must provide Semester 1 Grade 6 report; Students will be selected based on application information and academic performance; Must participate in trials; Both academic achievement and sporting progress reviewed annually
special_conditions: Full Scholarships available covering all expenses except training uniform. Training uniform is an additional cost. Deposit of $200 due by Friday 17 October 2025 with balance due by 5 December 2025. Acceptance subject to progressive and annual reviews. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic achievement and sporting progress. Conduct, attitude, effort and participation are reviewed annually. Automatic acceptance does not carry forward to following years.

overview: The Victorian High-Ability Program is an enrichment program offered to high-ability students across Victorian government schools, selected by the Department of Education and Training. The program engages students in critical thinking, problem solving and creativity through online lessons focused on English and Mathematics.
description: The Victorian High-Ability Program (VHAP) is an enrichment program delivered through Virtual Schools Victoria as part of the Student Excellence Unit. Each term, eligible students in Year 7 and 8 are selected by the Department of Education to participate in a 10-week program facilitated by the school's High Ability Practice Co-Ordinator. The program consists of ten 1-hour online synchronous lessons conducted during school hours, with times alternating so students do not miss the same class weekly. Teachers can also nominate high-achieving students in English and/or Mathematics to participate. The program focuses on tasks that stimulate critical thinking, problem solving and creativity, enriching students' understanding of English and Mathematics. Upon completion, students receive a certificate of participation.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 and Year 8 students; Selected by Department of Education and Training; High-ability in English and/or Mathematics; Can be nominated by school teachers; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Selection is made each term by the Department of Education and Training. Lessons are conducted during school hours with alternating times. Teachers can nominate students for participation. Certificate of participation awarded upon completion of the program.

overview: Greensborough College offers the Year 7 English Skills Reading Program designed to assist and support students identified as needing intervention in the area of reading and literacy. The program provides targeted support to help students develop essential literacy skills.
description: The Year 7 English Skills Reading Program is a support program offered by Greensborough College for students identified as needing intervention in reading and literacy skills. The program is designed to assist and support students who require additional help to develop proficiency in reading and comprehension. This targeted intervention program helps students build confidence and competence in literacy, which is fundamental to success across all subject areas. The program is integrated within the college's commitment to delivering educational programs that meet the needs of students with a wide range of individual abilities and interests. No specific financial value or cost details are published, but this represents a non-monetary support and intervention service for eligible students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Identified as requiring intervention in reading and literacy; Referred by school assessment; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Students must be identified through school assessment as requiring reading and literacy intervention. Program is designed to assist students with demonstrated need in this area.

overview: Greenvale Secondary College offers the General Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students with strong academic backgrounds. The scholarship is available to students who wish to continue their pursuit of academic excellence in line with the college's values of Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect.
description: This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exemplary academic records with strong work habits and outstanding application and achievement in one or more of English, Mathematics or Science, along with strong performance in general studies and technology. Successful applicants display a joy for learning, outstanding work ethic and behaviour, and preparedness to participate fully in the life of Greenvale Secondary College. The scholarship encourages students to continue pursuing academic excellence throughout their enrolment. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement through school records and an interview process with shortlisted candidates. Specific financial value and duration are not published online and should be obtained directly from the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all prospective students; Exemplary academic record required; Strong work habits and outstanding application and achievement in English, Mathematics or Science demonstrated; Strong performance in general studies and technology required; Outstanding work ethic and behaviour required; Preparedness to participate fully in college life required; Must align with College Values
special_conditions: Scholarships open each year. Recipients must demonstrate College Values: Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. Selection involves shortlisting and interview process.

overview: Greenvale Secondary College offers the Leadership Scholarship to students who have demonstrated strong leadership qualities and wish to experience leadership opportunities through the Student Leadership program. The scholarship recognises students with personal maturity, confidence, and the ability to inspire and motivate others.
description: This scholarship is available to students who have demonstrated strong leadership qualities and wish to experience leadership opportunities through the Student Leadership program offered at Greenvale Secondary College. Successful applicants display personal maturity, confidence, outstanding work ethic and behaviour. They must be encouraging and supportive of fellow students and have the ability to inspire or motivate others. Recipients are selected through an interview process. Applicants must also demonstrate the college's core values of Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. The specific financial value and duration of the scholarship are not published online and should be obtained directly from the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all prospective students; Demonstrated strong leadership qualities required; Desire to experience leadership opportunities through Student Leadership program; Personal maturity required; Confidence in dealing with peers required; Outstanding work ethic and behaviour required; Must be encouraging and supportive of fellow students; Ability to inspire or motivate others required; Must demonstrate College Values
special_conditions: Scholarships open each year. Recipients must be able to demonstrate College Values: Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. Selection involves shortlisting and interview process.

overview: Greenvale Secondary College offers the Sporting Scholarship to promising young athletes who demonstrate the qualities of sportsmanship both on and off the field and exhibit skills of teamwork. The scholarship recognises students who display excellence in sport alongside the college's core values.
description: This scholarship is available to promising young athletes who demonstrate the qualities of sportsmanship both on and off the field and additionally exhibit skills of teamwork. Supporting documentation and/or references are required as evidence of the level of sporting achievement. Potential recipients must also be able to demonstrate the college's core values of Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. Selection involves submission of supporting documentation, references, and an interview process with shortlisted candidates. The specific financial value and duration of the scholarship are not published online and should be obtained directly from the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all prospective students; Promising young athlete status required; Demonstrated quality of sportsmanship both on and off the field; Evidence of teamwork skills required; Supporting documentation and/or references required; Must demonstrate College Values; Excellence in sport performance required
special_conditions: Scholarships open each year. Recipients must be able to demonstrate College Values: Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. Supporting documentation and references required as evidence of sporting achievement. Selection involves shortlisting and interview process.

overview: Greenvale Secondary College offers the Visual and Performing Arts Scholarship to students who demonstrate exceptional talent in one or more fields of music, drama, dance, and visual arts. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated excellence in performing and visual arts disciplines.
description: This scholarship is available to students who demonstrate exceptional talent in one or more of the fields of music, drama, dance, and visual arts. The college runs comprehensive instrumental music and performing arts programs, including annual school productions involving hundreds of students performing, working backstage, and playing in the band. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional talent, which may be evidenced through auditions, portfolios, or performance demonstrations. Potential recipients must also be able to demonstrate the college's core values of Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. The specific financial value and duration of the scholarship are not published online and should be obtained directly from the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all prospective students; Exceptional talent in music, drama, dance, or visual arts demonstrated; Evidence of talent through audition, portfolio, or performance demonstration required; Must demonstrate College Values; Readiness to participate in school productions or performance opportunities
special_conditions: Scholarships open each year. Recipients must be able to demonstrate College Values: Excellence, Teamwork, Leadership and Respect. Selection involves auditions or portfolio review and interview process with shortlisted candidates.

overview: Grovedale College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to students with an exemplary academic record who demonstrate outstanding application and achievement in Mathematics and English. The scholarship recognises students who combine exceptional academic ability with strong performance in general studies and learning technologies.
description: These scholarships are available to students with an exemplary academic record who have demonstrated outstanding application and achievement in Mathematics and English along with a strong performance in general studies and Learning Technologies. Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 whose academic performance and commitment demonstrate excellence in their core subjects and broader learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have exemplary academic record; Outstanding achievement in Mathematics and English; Strong performance in general studies and Learning Technologies
special_conditions: Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship.

overview: Grovedale College offers the General Entry Scholarship to students who demonstrate outstanding leadership skills and potential in a variety of settings. The scholarship recognises students with excellent work ethic, supportive relationships with peers, and full participation in school community life.
description: These General Entry Scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated leadership skills and potential in a variety of settings. Successful applicants will have an outstanding work ethic, have been supportive of fellow students and have fully participated in the life of their school. Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and community involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate leadership skills and potential; Outstanding work ethic required; Evidence of supporting fellow students; Full participation in school community life
special_conditions: Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship.

overview: Grovedale College offers Music Scholarships to students who have demonstrated musical ability in performance and a commitment to a vibrant music program. The scholarship recognises students with strong work ethic and sound academic skills in a music context.
description: Grovedale College Music Scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated musical ability in performance and a commitment to a vibrant music program. Successful applicants will have a strong work ethic and sound academic skills. Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. The College offers an extensive Instrumental Music Program with music instruction available for multiple instruments including flute, trumpet, clarinet, trombone, saxophone, French horn, guitar, bass guitar, keyboard, percussion, drums, and voice. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate musical ability in performance; Commitment to vibrant music program; Strong work ethic required; Sound academic skills
special_conditions: Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship.

overview: Grovedale College offers Sports Scholarships to students who have demonstrated outstanding sporting skills and potential. Five scholarship streams are available in Cricket, Football, Netball, Soccer and General Sports, with expectation of participation in the College Sports Academy Program.
description: These Scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated outstanding sporting skills and potential. Five scholarship streams are available – Cricket, Football, Netball, Soccer and General. The expectation is that successful applicants will apply for inclusion in the College Sports Academy Program. Scholarships are redeemable against College expenses including books and uniforms. The College provides specialist sports coaching, leadership and elite sport pathway opportunities through its unique Sports Academy. Applicants may apply for up to two classes of scholarship. The College's Sports Academy combines academic, sporting and leadership training within the college curriculum with specialist coaches and trained staff.
