Shortlisted students will be invited to attend an interview to discuss their application. The selection process evaluates students' academic promise and potential to contribute to and benefit from the Haileybury community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be of Australian Indigenous descent; Must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Must demonstrate academic and personal potential; Open to students entering Years 5, 7 or 10
special_conditions: Applicants must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. Scholarships are held from the year of entry until Year 12. Shortlisted students will be invited to attend an interview to discuss their application. Applications will be considered by Haileybury's Scholarships Committee.

overview: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships provide means-tested financial support to Victorian students who would not ordinarily be able to afford a Haileybury education. These scholarships are awarded based on demonstrated financial need and positive community contribution.
description: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships are means-tested and prioritise families who would not otherwise be able to access a Haileybury education. Scholarships offer between 50-100% remission of tuition fees for the duration of the student's schooling at Haileybury. The application process involves completing an online application form outlining students' past and current contribution to their community, submitting financial information including two most recent ATO tax returns, and attending an interview with Haileybury Senior Executive and Haileybury Foundation Board members. Students are assessed on strong academic promise, capability in sports, music, the arts, community service, demonstrated resilience, leadership, community-mindedness and character.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Victorian student; Transitioning into Year 7, Year 9 or Year 10; Would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury based on current financial situation; Must be a positive contributor to the community; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must satisfy equity requirements of the Haileybury Scholarship Fund Rules
special_conditions: Means-tested based on family financial circumstances. Recipients must engage fully in school life and participate in academic, co-curricular and community activities. Recipients must demonstrate personal effort and commitment, uphold Haileybury values of respect, integrity and leadership, and act as ambassadors for the Haileybury Foundation. Recipients must submit regular updates to benefactors and attend benefactor events. Scholarships are reviewed annually. Dependent upon circumstances, scholarships and bursaries can run concurrently.

overview: Haileybury Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who exhibit outstanding academic performance and commitment to their studies. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability across all subject areas and supports talented students to access a Haileybury education.
description: Haileybury offers partial Academic scholarships only. Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate strong academic performance from formal Edutest testing. The financial value is a partial fee reduction, awarded from the year of entry through until Year 12. Selection process includes initial academic testing administered by Edutest (covering Literacy, Numeracy and Comprehension), with invited students providing recent school reports and NAPLAN results, potentially completing second-round testing and interviews with the Head of Campus or delegate. Scholarship continuation is assessed annually against scholarship acceptance terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently in Years 3, 5 and 8 applying for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10 in 2028; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Year 3 students currently attending Haileybury are ineligible to apply; External students only for Year 5 entry; External and current students eligible for Year 7 and 10 entry
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are expected to remain a student at Haileybury until completion of the scholarship and participate fully in all school activities. Scholarship continuation is assessed on an annual basis against scholarship acceptance terms and conditions. Haileybury will not offer any changes to the test date, time or location.

overview: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships recognise outstanding achievement in academic, leadership, community service, sport and the arts. These scholarships support talented students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas and contribute to the school community.
description: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships are available for students entering Years 5, 7 and 10 in 2027 at our City campus. Financial value is determined by the Scholarships Committee with remission amount based on individual circumstances and achievement. Selection includes online application form, submission of academic reports, interview with Head of School or delegate, and potential presentation of additional achievement documentation. Awards are tenable from year of entry through Year 12 based on continued performance and involvement in school life and extra-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently enrolled in Year 4, 6 or 9 for consideration of entry into Year 5, 7 or 10 in 2027; Must be an Australian citizen or resident; External students only for Year 5 entry (current Year 4 students of Haileybury cannot apply); Open to new and current students for Year 7 and 10 entry; Must demonstrate commitment to academic excellence and achievement in leadership, community involvement, sport or culture
special_conditions: Applications have now closed for 2027 entry. Shortlisted students will be invited to attend an interview to discuss their application with Head of School. Students who are invited to interview are welcome to present further documentation of their achievements. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted. The Scholarship remission is determined by the Scholarships Committee.

overview: Haileybury Music Scholarships are awarded to talented musicians who exhibit advanced skill on Brass, Strings and Woodwind instruments. The scholarship recognises exceptional musical performance ability and supports music students at Years 7 and 10 entry.
description: Haileybury offers a limited number of Music Scholarships for students entering Years 7 and 10. Scholarships cover the music tuition fee for the instrument only (Brass, Strings, or Woodwind only). Financial benefit is tuition fee reduction for the specific instrument, awarded from year of entry through Year 12. Selection process includes demonstration of minimum music standard (AMEB Grade 4/5 for Year 7 entry; AMEB Grade 6/7 for Year 10 entry), online application submission, and interview with school staff. Continuation is subject to maintaining performance standards.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated advanced musical skill in Brass, Strings or Woodwind instruments; For Year 7 entry must have completed minimum AMEB Grade 4/5 or equivalent; For Year 10 entry must have completed minimum AMEB Grade 6/7 or equivalent; Open to external and current students; Application deadline to be confirmed
special_conditions: Music Scholarships are for Brass, Strings and Woodwind instruments only. The scholarship covers the music tuition fee for the instrument only, not general school fees. Recipients must maintain musical performance standards and continue participation in instrumental tuition and ensemble programs.

overview: Haileybury Indigenous Scholarships are open to talented students of Australian Indigenous descent. The scholarship recognises Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and supports them to access a Haileybury education.
description: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships include Indigenous Scholarships for qualified Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 5, 7 and 10. Financial value is determined by the Scholarships Committee based on individual circumstances. Students must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. Selection includes online application form, submission of supporting documentation including academic reports, and interview process. Scholarships are awarded from year of entry through Year 12, with annual assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Must be of Australian Indigenous descent (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander); Must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Currently enrolled in Year 4, 6 or 9 for consideration of entry into Year 5, 7 or 10 in 2027; Must be Australian citizen or resident; Open to external students for Year 5 entry; Open to external and current students for Year 7 and 10 entry
special_conditions: Confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage is required with application. Applications have now closed for 2027 entry. Shortlisted students will be invited to interview. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted. The Scholarship remission is determined by the Scholarships Committee.

overview: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships provide means-tested financial assistance to talented students who would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury due to financial circumstances. The scholarship recognises students who show strong academic promise and capability in sport, music, the arts or community service.
description: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships offer 50-100% remission of tuition fees for the duration of the student's schooling at Haileybury. These are means-tested scholarships open to Victorian students transitioning into Year 7, 9 or 10 at City campus. Selection includes completing online application form outlining community contribution and commitment, submitting copies of two most recent ATO tax statements as evidence of financial circumstances, providing academic reports, and attendance at interview with Haileybury Senior Executive and Foundation Board member. Scholarships are awarded based on financial need and ability to benefit from a Haileybury education.
eligibility_criteria: Transitioning into Year 7, 9 or 10; Would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury based on current financial situation; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must be positive contributor to their community; Means-tested with submission of two most recent ATO tax statements required; Applications for 2027 entry are now closed
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 Haileybury Foundation Scholarships are now closed. 2028 submissions will open in November 2026. Requires submission of financial documentation including ATO tax returns. Scholarship recipients are expected to engage fully in school life, participate in academic and co-curricular activities, uphold Haileybury values, and be available to attend benefactor events and submit updates to benefactors. Recipients must satisfy the equity requirement of the Rules of the Haileybury Scholarship Fund.

overview: Hazel Glen College's Athlete Development Academy has been established to identify and develop highly talented and emerging student athletes within their focus sport. The academy's holistic approach aims to develop the whole athlete, preparing individuals for success in elite sport while balancing academics and developing leadership skills.
description: The Athlete Development Academy (ADA) is available to Year 7 and Year 10 students and focuses on developing student athletes across Basketball, Netball, and AFL. The program offers two different pathways: the Focus Program provides sports-specific coaching sessions delivered by highly skilled and professional coaches, while the Tailored Program offers more individualized coaching targeted at improving specific skills. Students participate in timetabled classes as part of their core PE curriculum plus additional training sessions. Additional fees apply for enrollment in the ADA, which include the ADA training uniform, guest presenters, training and recovery aids, and excursions. The academy curriculum delivers a learning pathway that progresses each year from Year 7 to Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Open to Year 10 students; Demonstrated athletic ability in focus sport; Must be currently enrolled or applying to Hazel Glen College; Subject to ADA expectations regarding behaviour, uniform, attendance and academic commitment
special_conditions: Additional fees apply for ADA enrollment including training uniform, guest presenters, training and recovery aids, and excursions. ADA students must meet ADA expectations regarding behaviour, uniform, attendance and academic commitment. Open and frequent communication between ADA Teachers and families is essential.

overview: Hazel Glen College's Instrumental Music Academy has been established to identify and develop emerging student musicians across all facets and disciplines of instrumental music. The academy's holistic approach supports students to balance their academics, develop their leadership skills, and prepare for pathways into professional music settings.
description: The Instrumental Music Academy is a 2-year program for student musicians starting in Year 5 and Year 7. The academy develops student musicians through a high performance curriculum designed to support musical success both inside and outside the classroom. Students participate in 3 x 60-minute sessions per week that are a combination of timetabled, instrumental and after-school classes. The academy's holistic approach aims to develop student musicians to better prepare individuals for pathways into a professional setting by supporting them to balance their academics, develop their leadership skills and support them to become global citizens. The program focuses on deepening and supporting musical achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 students; Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated instrumental music ability; Must be selected through audition process; Currently enrolled or applying to Hazel Glen College
special_conditions: Academy is a 2-year program. Selection based on audition and demonstrated instrumental music ability. Academy application process details were posted on Compass school communication platform as of 2024.

overview: Hazel Glen College's Performing Arts Academy has been established to identify and develop highly talented student performers across dance, drama and singing. The academy's holistic approach aims to enhance, develop and immerse students in the performing arts while preparing them for pathways into professional settings.
description: The Performing Arts Academy (PAA) is a specialized program designed to develop highly talented student performers. The academy encompasses dance, drama, and singing disciplines and provides students with immersive experiences in the performing arts. The academy's holistic approach aims to enhance, develop and immerse students in the performing arts, and to better prepare individuals for pathways into professional settings by supporting them to balance their academics and develop their creativity and confidence. The program is available to students in Secondary Years and provides opportunities for students to develop advanced performance skills across multiple disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated talent and ability in performing arts (dance, drama, or singing); Must pass audition or portfolio review; Currently enrolled or applying to Hazel Glen College; Commitment to developing creativity and confidence
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated talent and audition process. Academy focuses on developing creativity, confidence, and professional pathways in performing arts disciplines.

overview: Hazel Glen College's Student Leadership Academy has been developed to provide students with the necessary skills, knowledge and experiences to become effective leaders in the College, communities and beyond. The academy helps students develop leadership qualities such as critical thinking, problem solving, communication, teamwork and decision making skills.
description: The Student Leadership Academy is a selective program available to Secondary Years students with demonstrated leadership potential. Academy students are offered a range of opportunities through teacher-led fortnightly classes, workshops and excursions. The selection process comprises two stages: first, students write an application explaining why they would like to be part of the academy; second, selected students are invited to attend a group interview where they present themselves and a school initiative they would like to develop. Once accepted, academy students work collaboratively to develop short-term and long-term goals, engage with Whittlesea Council members to develop and enhance aspects of the college, work with staff to develop programs and activities for yearly calendar events, and participate in student voice sessions with members of leadership. The program develops students who can contribute meaningfully to their communities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Secondary Years students; Demonstrated leadership interest and potential; Must complete written application; Must participate in group interview process; Commitment to developing leadership skills and contributing to school community
special_conditions: Two-stage selection process: written application followed by group interview. Students must present a school initiative they wish to develop as part of group interview. Academy offers fortnightly classes, workshops and excursions. Continued participation contingent on maintaining engagement and contributing to school initiatives.

overview: Healesville Primary School presents the Tricia O'Reilly Memorial Scholarship annually to a Year 6 student who embodies exceptional all-round qualities. The award recognises academic achievement, involvement in school activities, sporting ability, and personal qualities such as being cheerful, fun-loving and respectful.
description: The Tricia O'Reilly Memorial Scholarship is presented each year to a Year 6 student who demonstrates the qualities embodied by Tricia O'Reilly, a former student at Healesville Primary School who tragically died in 2001. Recipients are academically accomplished, interested in being involved in all aspects of school activity, conscientious in doing their best, sporty, cheerful, fun-loving and respectful. The award was established in 2001 in honour of Tricia and has been presented annually since that time. The scholarship recognises outstanding all-round achievement and personal qualities that exemplify school values. Recipients are acknowledged at a school assembly with the O'Reilly family in attendance to honour the award.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 student; Must demonstrate academic accomplishment; Must show involvement in school activities; Must display sporting ability; Must demonstrate cheerful, fun-loving and respectful demeanor
special_conditions: This is an honour award, not a financial scholarship. Recipients are selected by school staff based on demonstrated qualities. The award is presented at a school assembly and the O'Reilly family attends annually.

overview: Haileybury Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who exhibit outstanding academic performance and commitment to their studies. These scholarships reward excellence in literacy, numeracy and comprehension and recognise students demonstrating exceptional academic ability across all subjects.
description: Haileybury offers partial Academic scholarships only, which provide tuition fee reduction from the year of entry through until Year 12. Students are required to complete academic testing administered by Edutest covering literacy, numeracy and comprehension. Shortlisted students are invited to attend an interview with the Head of Campus or Head of Senior School. Testing is scheduled for Saturday, 20 June 2026 for Year 5 entry (at campus) and Year 7 entry (online), and Sunday, 21 June 2026 for Year 10 entry (online). Scholarship continuation is assessed on an annual basis against scholarship acceptance terms and conditions. Recipients are expected to maintain academic performance and participate fully in school activities.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently in Year 3, 5 or 8 in 2026; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Year 3 students currently attending Haileybury are ineligible; External students only for Year 5 entry; External and current Haileybury students eligible for Year 7 and Year 10 entry; Applicants must demonstrate positive attitude toward school and willingness to contribute to Haileybury community
special_conditions: Partial scholarships only. Scholarship awarded from year of entry through Year 12. Scholarship continuation assessed annually. Participating families must act in accordance with Haileybury's Code of Conduct. Instances where behaviour is below expected level will result in immediate cancellation of test registrations and exclusion from scholarship process in future years. Capacity restrictions apply; if applications exceed requirements, registrations will close prior to 11 July.

overview: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships recognise outstanding achievement and contribution in academics, leadership, community service, sport and the arts. These scholarships support talented students who demonstrate well-rounded excellence both within and beyond the school environment.
description: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships are available for students entering Years 5, 7 and 10 in 2027 at Berwick, Brighton, City and Keysborough campuses. Scholarship remission is determined by the Scholarships Committee and varies based on merit and financial circumstances. Applications involve completing an online form where students showcase skills, experiences and interests in study, leadership, community involvement, sport and culture. Shortlisted students are invited to attend an interview with the Head of School (from nominated campus) to discuss their application in detail. Selection criteria include academic achievement, leadership potential, community contribution, sporting achievements, artistic and performing abilities, and demonstrated character.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently in Year 4, 6 or 9 for entry into Year 5, 7 or 10 in 2027; Must be Australian citizen or resident; Must demonstrate commitment to academic excellence and achievement in leadership (within and external to school); Must demonstrate community service, sport and arts involvement; Year 5 entry open to external students only; Current Year 4 students of Haileybury cannot apply for Year 5 entry
special_conditions: Scholarship remission determined by Scholarships Committee. Applications closed for 2027 entry. Late or incomplete submissions not accepted. Feedback on individual submissions not provided. Multiple applications will not be considered. Haileybury Admissions cannot amend applications or accept documents not attached prior to submission.

overview: Haileybury Music Scholarships are designed to support talented musicians and cover the music tuition fee for brass, strings or woodwind instruments. These scholarships recognise students who exhibit advanced musical skill and commitment to performance.
description: Haileybury Music Scholarships are open to students entering Years 7 and 10 and are awarded to students who exhibit advanced skills in brass, strings or woodwind instruments only. The scholarships cover the music tuition fee for the student's instrument. Students applying for Year 7 entry must have completed a minimum of AMEB Grade 4 or 5 (or equivalent), and those applying for Year 10 must have completed a minimum of AMEB Grade 6 or 7 (or equivalent). Shortlisted students are invited to attend an audition at Haileybury's campus to present a piece on their instrument. Scholarships are awarded for the duration of the student's enrolment at Haileybury, subject to satisfactory progress and participation in school music programs.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must exhibit advanced skills in brass, strings or woodwind instruments only; Year 7 applicants must have completed minimum AMEB Grade 4 or 5 or equivalent; Year 10 applicants must have completed minimum AMEB Grade 6 or 7 or equivalent; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Open to external and current Haileybury students
special_conditions: Covers music tuition fee for instrument only. Awarded to students with advanced skills in brass, strings or woodwind instruments. Scholarships tenable for duration of enrolment at Haileybury subject to satisfactory progress. Recipients expected to participate fully in school music programs and activities.

overview: Haileybury Indigenous Scholarships are open to talented students of Australian Indigenous descent. These scholarships recognise Indigenous students and provide significant financial support to enable them to access a world-class Haileybury education.
description: Indigenous Scholarships are available for students of Australian Indigenous descent entering Years 5-7 and provide substantial financial support covering up to 90% of the school's tuition fees through to Year 12. When applying, students must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. Applications are considered by Haileybury's Scholarships Committee. Selection is based on demonstrated talent, academic potential, and commitment to contributing positively to the school community. Scholarships are awarded from the year of entry through Year 12. Recipients are expected to engage fully in school life and maintain satisfactory academic and behavioural standards.
eligibility_criteria: Students of Australian Indigenous descent; Must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Typically for students entering Years 5-7; Must be Australian citizen or resident; Talent and ability to benefit from Haileybury education; Demonstrated potential for academic and personal development
special_conditions: Covers up to 90% of tuition fees through to Year 12. Requires confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. Late or incomplete submissions not accepted. Feedback on individual submissions not provided. Multiple applications not considered. Recipients expected to maintain satisfactory academic performance and conduct.

overview: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships provide significant financial support to students who would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury based on their current financial situation. These means-tested scholarships offer between 50-100% remission of tuition fees and are generously provided by Old Haileyburians, parents, staff and friends.
description: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships offer between 50-100% remission of tuition fees for the duration of schooling at Haileybury. Scholarships are available for Victorian students transitioning into Year 7, 9 or 10 at any Victorian-based campus. Application process includes completing a short online form followed by submission of financial information and relevant academic reports to assess family's financial circumstances. Shortlisted students and families are invited to meet with Haileybury Senior Executive and Foundation Board member to discuss aspirations, interests and personal attributes. Scholarships typically cover 50-100% of school fees depending on individual circumstances. Recipients are expected to engage fully in school life, demonstrate personal effort and commitment, uphold Haileybury values, and be ambassadors for the Foundation.
eligibility_criteria: Victorian students transitioning into Year 7, 9 or 10 at any Victorian-based campus; Would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury based on current financial situation; Positive contributors to community in which student lives; Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Students who show strong academic promise and capability in sport, music, arts or community service encouraged to apply; Must demonstrate positive attitude toward school
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarships. Families required to submit copies of two most recent ATO tax statements. Scholarships typically 50-100% remission depending on circumstances. Recipients must engage fully in school life, participate in academic and co-curricular activities, demonstrate personal effort and commitment, uphold Haileybury values, and serve as ambassadors for Foundation. Annual review required. Applications for 2027 now closed; 2028 submissions open in November 2026.

overview: Heathmont College operates the Excellence and Enrichment Program (EEP) to support Year 7 students seeking additional extension and enrichment in academic fields. The program provides individualised coaching and tailored withdrawal sessions across multiple subject areas including Mathematics, Science, Humanities, English, Arts, and Sports to maximise personal and academic growth.
description: The Excellence and Enrichment Program is designed for Year 7 students who are seeking further extension or enrichment in academic areas, particularly Mathematics and/or English. Students in the EEP receive individual coaching by a trained member of the leadership team to help them set goals and work to achieve these goals across achievement, work habits and social/emotional wellbeing. A program is then designed to provide extension, enrichment and acceleration where needed. A range of withdrawal extension programs are offered across the year in areas such as Mathematics, Science, Humanities and English as well as additional activities and programs in other subject areas such as the Arts and Sports. The program can be combined with the Sports Academy, Language programs and Instrumental Music programs. Students are not required to purchase additional or separate texts; all resources required for the program are provided by the school as needed. No financial cost is specified for this program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Open to all students; Expression of interest application required; Student letter detailing interest and suitability required; Parent endorsement required; Testing data and demonstrated interests considered; All applications will be considered
special_conditions: Intake interviews will be conducted by coaching staff and will consider the student application, parental endorsement, testing data and student identified interests, passions and goals. The program emphasizes extension and enrichment, integrating with core curriculum elements while addressing students' strengths and areas for improvement.

overview: Heathmont College Sports Academy has been established to enhance students' sporting, academic and vocational opportunities through a holistic sporting approach. The program encourages student-athletes to be engaged in programs that aim to help them reach their potential in all areas of life including professional coaching, strength and conditioning training, and mentoring.
description: The Heathmont College Sports Academy is designed for student-athletes seeking enhanced sporting development alongside academic pursuits. The program offers professional technical skill development, professional tactical skill development, health and wellbeing sessions, competitions and inter-academy competitions, fitness testing and data analysis, peer analysis and assessment, and student-athlete development of coaching practices for leadership opportunities. Students receive strength and conditioning sessions, training recovery practices, and ongoing support to balance demanding schedules with education. The base uniform costs $167 and is added to the pro-rata program fee. A 'Term Taster' program is available allowing students to trial for one full term. Refunds are not issued where a student is withdrawn voluntarily, though consideration may be given at the principal's discretion with supporting documentation such as medical certificates.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary school enrollment or currently at secondary school level required; Expression of interest and application for enrolment required; May be interviewed by Assistant Principal and Director of Sports Academy; Currently enrolled students can participate in Term Taster program; Term Taster available once per athlete
special_conditions: Base uniform cost of $167 is added to pro-rata program fee. Term Taster program only available once per athlete. Payment plan options available; contact Sandra Nebl (Accounts Receivable) at Sandra.Nebl@education.vic.gov.au or phone (03) 9871 4888 for assistance with payment options. Program is designed and staffed based on confirmed student enrolments. Supporting documentation (e.g. medical certificate) may be required to accompany refund requests.

overview: Heritage College Knox offers the Year 7 Academic Scholarship to high achieving students entering Year 7. Full and Half scholarships are awarded to recognise exceptional academic ability and potential. The scholarship supports outstanding students for up to six years of enrolment.
description: Heritage College Knox provides Full and Half scholarships for students entering Year 7. The scholarship is awarded based on student achievement measured by the ACER scholarship test and an interview with the Principal. All scholarships cover general tuition fees and exclude excursions, camps, swimming and any extra-curricular activities. Successful scholarship recipients must be prepared to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance. The scholarship value includes either Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction, with awards made for up to six years. Applications open in September, and the ACER test is usually scheduled around February of the following year. Scholarships are offered on the basis of measured student achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate high academic achievement; Must perform well on ACER scholarship test; Must complete interview with the Principal
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Successful scholarship recipients must be prepared to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance. Scholarships cover general tuition fees only and exclude excursions, camps, swimming and any extra-curricular activities. Scholarships are offered for up to six years of enrolment.

overview: Heritage College Knox offers the Year 10 Academic Scholarship to high achieving students entering Year 10. Full and Half scholarships are awarded to recognise exceptional academic ability. The scholarship supports outstanding students for up to two years of enrolment.
description: Heritage College Knox provides Full and Half scholarships for students entering Year 10. The scholarship is awarded based on student achievement measured by the ACER scholarship test and an interview with the Principal. All scholarships cover general tuition fees and exclude excursions, camps, swimming and any extra-curricular activities. Successful scholarship recipients must be prepared to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance. The scholarship value includes either Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction, with awards made for up to two years. Applications open in September, and the ACER test is usually scheduled around February of the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10; Must demonstrate high academic achievement; Must perform well on ACER scholarship test; Must complete interview with the Principal
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Successful scholarship recipients must be prepared to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance. Scholarships cover general tuition fees only and exclude excursions, camps, swimming and any extra-curricular activities. Scholarships are offered for up to two years of enrolment.

overview: Heritage College Knox offers fee remissions to families experiencing financial hardship. The program provides discretionary support to ensure families can access the school despite financial difficulties. This is available at the discretion of the Principal once enrolment is confirmed.
description: In cases of hardship, fee remissions are available at the discretion of the Principal once enrolment is confirmed. The value of the remission varies depending on individual circumstances and demonstrated financial need. Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the Business Manager for a confidential discussion regarding fee remission options and the application process. This is a means-tested program designed to provide financial assistance to eligible families. The specific amount of any remission is determined on a case-by-case basis following discussion with the school leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing documented financial hardship; Must have confirmed enrolment at the school; Application assessed at discretion of the Principal; Confidential discussion with Business Manager required
special_conditions: Available at the discretion of the Principal once enrolment is confirmed. Applications are confidential. Families must contact the Business Manager directly to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options. Remissions are assessed on a case-by-case basis.

overview: Heritage College Knox offers a 15% discount on base tuition fees to families holding a valid healthcare card. This program recognises families with demonstrated financial need. The discount applies to tuition fees only.
description: A 15% discount is available for healthcare card holders on base tuition fees only. This discount provides ongoing support to eligible families to reduce the cost of education. The discount is automatically applied once a valid healthcare card is provided to the school. Base tuition fees cover general tuition costs and exclude uniforms, books, sporting equipment, excursions, camps and extra-curricular activities. Families eligible for this discount are also able to access CSEF (Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund) funds for eligible expenses. This is a simplified means-tested program that recognises families with demonstrated financial need based on government healthcare card eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold a valid healthcare card; Valid healthcare card documentation must be provided to the school; Discount applies to base tuition fees only
special_conditions: Applies to base tuition fees only and excludes uniforms, books, sporting equipment, excursions, camps and extra-curricular activities. CSEF funds may also be available for eligible expenses.

overview: Heritage College Knox offers substantial discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. Families with multiple siblings benefit from significantly reduced tuition fees, recognising the increased financial burden of educating more than one child.
description: Families with multiple children enrolled receive discounted rates on base tuition fees. The discount structure is progressive: 25% discount for the 2nd child, 40% discount for the 3rd child, and 65% discount for the 4th child. These discounts apply to base tuition fees only and exclude uniforms, books, sporting equipment, excursions, camps and extra-curricular activities. The program is designed to make education more accessible for families with multiple children. Discounts are applied automatically upon enrolment of additional siblings and continue for all years of enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Must have multiple children enrolled or enrolling at Heritage College Knox; Discounts apply to 2nd child onwards; Applies to base tuition fees only
value_aud: 25% Fee Reduction or 40% Fee Reduction or 65% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Discount structure: 25% for 2nd child, 40% for 3rd child, and 65% for 4th child. Applies to base tuition fees only. Discounts are applied automatically upon enrolment of additional siblings.

overview: Heritage College offers an Academic Scholarship awarded to students who excel academically. The scholarship recognises students with advanced academic standing who demonstrate exceptional achievement and commitment to learning.
description: Heritage College is offering an Academic Scholarship to prospective and current students entering Year 7 and Year 10. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate advanced academic standing. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Financial value and specific percentage reductions are not publicly disclosed on the website but are detailed in the official Scholarship Policy document. Recipients are awarded based on examination results, school reports, NAPLAN reports and interviews. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate commitment to supporting the college's Christian ethos and contributing to college life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026; Open to current students entering Year 10 in 2026; Must demonstrate advanced academic standing; Only families that have already put through an application and are not on a waitlist as of 2026 are eligible for Year 7 2026; Must commit to supporting the college's Christian ethos
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate commitment to tuition and supporting the college Christian ethos. Scholarship does not include School Levy, Uniform, Device and Bus fees.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students who excel in particular sporting disciplines. The scholarship recognises students with advanced athletic ability and commitment to representing the college in competition.
description: Heritage College recognises student excellence in sporting achievement and awards scholarships to those who excel in a particular sporting discipline. Students must demonstrate advanced sporting ability and be prepared to commit to representing the college in various sporting competitions. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Financial value and specific reductions are not publicly disclosed. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence and commitment to the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026; Open to current students entering Year 10 in 2026; Must excel in a particular sporting discipline; Must demonstrate advanced athletic ability; Only families that have already put through an application and are not on a waitlist as of 2026 are eligible for Year 7 2026
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Scholarship does not include School Levy, Uniform, Device and Bus fees. Recipients are expected to maintain commitment to representing the college in sporting competitions.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students who demonstrate leadership in community service. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to serving others.
description: Heritage College offers a Leadership Scholarship to students who demonstrate leadership in community service. Prospective students entering Year 7 who demonstrate leadership in community service are encouraged to apply. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional commitment to service and community leadership. Selection is based on demonstrated leadership qualities, commitment to service, and overall strength of application. Financial value is not publicly disclosed on the website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026; Open to current students entering Year 10 in 2026; Must demonstrate leadership in community service; Only families that have already put through an application and are not on a waitlist as of 2026 are eligible for Year 7 2026
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Scholarship does not include School Levy, Uniform, Device and Bus fees. Recipients must continue to demonstrate commitment to community service and college ethos.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students who excel in creative and performing arts. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional artistic ability and commitment to the creative disciplines.
description: Heritage College recognises creative excellence and awards scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional ability in the creative arts, including performing arts and visual arts. Students with advanced creative ability are encouraged to apply. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence across creative disciplines and commitment to contributing to the college's arts programs. Financial value is not disclosed on the public website. Selection is based on demonstrated creative excellence and commitment to the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026; Open to current students entering Year 10 in 2026; Must demonstrate creative excellence in performing or visual arts; Only families that have already put through an application and are not on a waitlist as of 2026 are eligible for Year 7 2026
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Scholarship does not include School Levy, Uniform, Device and Bus fees. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to participating in college creative arts programs and supporting college culture.

overview: Highview College offers a limited number of General Excellence Scholarships to students entering Year 7. These scholarships provide reduced tuition fees for the first year of enrolment and include an opportunity for students to participate in a mentoring program with the Principal.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises outstanding personal achievement across multiple dimensions including academic, sporting, cultural, leadership and community contributions. Applicants are current Year 6 students who must submit a hard copy application highlighting personal achievements across five key areas. The scholarship provides reduced tuition fees for the first year of enrolment and includes a mentoring program with the Principal. Applications may be presented in any format and do not require references or original documents. The Scholarship Panel reviews all applications and notifies successful applicants in early Term 4.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students; Applying for entry to Year 7; Must demonstrate personal achievements across five key areas; Hard copy application required; No references required
special_conditions: Applications should highlight personal achievements across five key areas. Applications may be presented in any format. Please do not include original documents, as these will not be returned. References are not required. Successful applicants will be notified in early Term 4. Applications may be brought to the enrolment interview with the Principal or delivered directly to Highview College Reception (hard copy only).

overview: Highview College provides bursaries to families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card. This financial assistance program is designed to support families requiring fee assistance to enable their children to attend the school.
description: Bursaries are available to families with a means-tested Health Care Card. This need-based financial assistance program helps eligible families access secondary education at Highview College. Families requiring financial support are welcome to apply for a bursary in addition to or instead of scholarship programs. For further information about bursary applications, eligibility, and the amount of assistance available, families should contact the Accounts Department at accounts@highview.vic.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Family holds a means-tested Health Care Card; Families requiring financial support
special_conditions: Means-tested assessment based on Health Care Card holding. Bursary availability and amounts may vary based on individual family circumstances and school funding availability.

overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers Access Awards to ensure families who would not otherwise have the financial means to afford a CREST education have access to leading Christian education. Access Awards are means-tested financial assistance programs available to Years 9-12 students seeking fee support.
description: Access Awards are designed to support families with demonstrated financial need to access Hillcrest Christian College's educational programs. All families of Years 9-12 students are invited to apply, including current students, students enrolled at CREST Colleges who have not yet commenced, and new students. Eligible families can receive up to 50% off tuition fees until the completion of Year 12. Access Awards are means tested, with shortlisted applicants being required to provide evidence of family financial circumstances as evidenced in income tax statements. Students who are not currently enrolled must complete an Expression of Interest or Enrolment Application Form prior to submitting an Access Awards application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 9-12 students; Open to current students, students enrolled at CREST Colleges (not yet commenced), and new students; Means-tested based on family financial circumstances; Must submit income tax statements as evidence of financial need; Non-enrolled students must complete Expression of Interest or Enrolment Application Form first
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants required to provide evidence of family financial circumstances (income tax statements). Students must be enrolled or have completed Expression of Interest or Enrolment Application prior to applying. Awards available until completion of Year 12.

overview: Hoa Nghiem Primary School offers a Financial Hardship Scholarship Program to support families facing financial difficulties in paying tuition fees. The program is designed to enable students to continue their education at the school and is subject to written approval from the Governance Committee (Temple Board).
description: The Financial Hardship Scholarship Program began operating from 2017 to provide assistance to families who are unable to meet the annual tuition fees. This program recognizes that financial circumstances can change and aims to remove barriers to education for qualified students. The scholarship provides fee assistance (exact amount varies based on individual family circumstances and Governance Committee approval). The selection process involves a written application detailing financial hardship circumstances and submission to the Temple Board for approval. Families must demonstrate genuine financial need through documentation of their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to enrolled students or prospective students; Family experiencing financial hardship; Written approval required from Governance Committee (Temple Board); Must be able to demonstrate genuine financial need
special_conditions: Scholarship is subject to written approval from the Governance Committee (Temple Board). Available from 2017 onwards for families facing financial hardship at any time during their child's enrollment.

overview: Heritage College offers an Academic Scholarship awarded to students who excel academically. The scholarship is available to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026 and current students entering Year 10 in 2026. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and is offered on a three-year basis commencing from the year of entry.
description: Heritage College is offering an Academic Scholarship for students demonstrating exceptional academic excellence. The scholarship is open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026, and current students entering Year 10 in 2026. The scholarship does not include School Levy, Uniform, Device and Bus fees. The scholarship is offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. To apply, families must have completed an application already and not be on a waitlist as of 2026. Applicants must read and understand the Scholarship Policy and submit an application through the college's portal. Selection is based on demonstrated academic standing and commitment to learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026; Open to current students entering Year 10 in 2026; Must have completed school application already; Must not be on waitlist as of 2026; Applicants must demonstrate advanced academic standing
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry. Only families that have put through an application already, and are not on a waitlist as of 2026, will be able to apply for the Year 7 2026 Scholarship Program. Limited availability for 2026 (Year 7 now at waitlist). Scholarships are subject to the terms outlined in the school's Scholarship Policy.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students who excel in particular sporting disciplines. The scholarship recognises outstanding sporting achievement and commitment to athletic excellence. Prospective Year 7 students and current students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10 who meet sporting excellence criteria are encouraged to apply.
description: Heritage College offers merit-based scholarships for students demonstrating excellence in sporting pursuits. Prospective students entering Year 7 who excel in a particular sporting discipline are encouraged to apply. Current enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10 who meet these criteria are also eligible to apply. The scholarship recognises students who combine sporting excellence with academic performance and leadership qualities. The exact financial benefit is determined by the college and varies based on individual applications. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7; Enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10; Must demonstrate excellence in a particular sporting discipline; Must show academic standing and commitment to the college community
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing from the year of entry. Applicants should provide evidence of sporting achievement and a commitment to participate in college sports programs. Selection considers overall contribution to college culture and values.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students who demonstrate leadership qualities and involvement in community service. The scholarship recognises students who show commitment to serving others and leading within their school and broader community. Prospective Year 7 students and continuing students are encouraged to apply.
description: Heritage College offers scholarships recognising demonstrated leadership in community service and engagement. Prospective students entering Year 7 who demonstrate leadership in community service are encouraged to apply. Current enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10 who meet these criteria are also eligible. The scholarship recognises students who combine leadership capabilities with academic standing and commitment to college values of Excellence, Resilience, Respect and Service. The financial value of the scholarship varies based on individual applications and college assessment. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7; Enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10; Must demonstrate leadership in community service; Must show commitment to college values and community engagement
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing from the year of entry. Applicants should provide evidence of leadership experience and community service involvement. Selection process considers overall demonstration of college values and personal character.

overview: Heritage College awards scholarships to students demonstrating creative excellence in artistic pursuits. The scholarship recognises students who excel in music, drama, visual arts and other creative disciplines. Prospective Year 7 students and continuing students are encouraged to apply.
description: Heritage College offers scholarships for students with demonstrated creative excellence in performing arts and visual arts. Prospective students entering Year 7 who demonstrate creative excellence are encouraged to apply, as are current enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10. The scholarship recognises students who combine artistic talent with academic performance and commitment to the college community. Creative excellence includes music, drama, dance, visual arts and multimedia pursuits. The financial value of the scholarship varies based on individual achievement and college assessment. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis, commencing on the year of entry.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7; Enrolled students continuing into Year 7 or Year 10; Must demonstrate creative excellence in arts or music; Must show commitment to furthering artistic pursuits
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing from the year of entry. Applicants should submit a portfolio demonstrating creative achievements and commitment to their artistic discipline. Evidence of participation in school or external arts programs and achievements is required.

school_name: Hillcrest Christian College - Rivercrest Christian College
overview: Hillcrest Christian College offers Access Awards to provide financial assistance to families who would not otherwise have the financial means to afford a Christian education. The program recognises families experiencing financial hardship and seeks to ensure all families have access to leading Christian education at the school.
description: Access Awards are offered through Hillcrest Christian College to eligible families in Years 9-12. The awards provide up to 50% off tuition fees until the completion of Year 12. The program is means-tested, with shortlisted applicants required to provide evidence of family financial circumstances as evidenced in income tax statements. Eligible families can benefit from substantial fee reductions, making education more accessible. Awards are available to current students, newly enrolled students, and new applicants to the college. Families not yet enrolled must complete an Expression of Interest or Enrolment Application Form prior to submitting an application for Access Awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 9-12 students; Current students at Hillcrest or Rivercrest Christian College; Students enrolled but not yet commenced; New students; Means-tested based on family financial circumstances; Evidence of family income required via income tax statements; Shortlisted applicants only
special_conditions: Access Awards are means tested and require submission of family financial documentation. Recipients must be enrolled or enrol at a CREST College. Families must support the Christian ethos of the college.

school_name: Hillcrest Christian College - Rivercrest Christian College
overview: Rivercrest Christian College offers a Fee Assistance Program to support families experiencing unexpected financial difficulties. The college is committed to assisting existing families to meet their financial commitments to school fees.
description: Rivercrest Christian College's Fee Assistance Program provides fee discounts to families who experience changes in circumstances or unexpected financial hardship. The program offers flexible fee assistance options to help families continue their child's education at the college. Assistance is provided to families demonstrating genuine financial need. The college works collaboratively with families to find solutions that make education accessible. Every effort is made to assist families to meet their commitment to pay college fees through this discretionary program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current enrolled family at Rivercrest Christian College; Demonstrate unexpected financial difficulties or change in circumstances; Financial assessment conducted on case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Applied on case-by-case basis for current families experiencing financial hardship. Program is discretionary and determined based on family circumstances and college capacity.

school_name: Hillcrest Christian College - Rivercrest Christian College
overview: Both Hillcrest Christian College and Rivercrest Christian College offer sibling discounts to families with multiple children attending the school. The discount reduces tuition fees for families with two or more students enrolled.
description: Hillcrest and Rivercrest Christian Colleges offer sibling discounts to recognise and support families with multiple children in attendance. The discount applies to tuition fees only and is calculated on the basis that the highest year level student is classified as the first child. Families with two or more children attending the school receive a reduction on the tuition component of their fees. This program aims to make education more accessible for larger families committed to the colleges' Christian education approach.
eligibility_criteria: Must have two or more children attending the college; Discount applies to tuition fees only; Highest year level student classified as first child
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only and does not extend to levies, charges, or other compulsory fees. Applied automatically upon enrolment of second and subsequent children.

school_name: Hillcrest Christian College - Rivercrest Christian College
overview: Both Hillcrest Christian College and Rivercrest Christian College offer early payment discounts to incentivise families to pay fees on time. The discount rewards families who pay their annual or bi-annual fees before specified due dates.
description: Hillcrest and Rivercrest offer early payment discounts to families who settle their tuition fees by scheduled deadlines. Annual fee payments made by 16th February receive a 3% discount off tuition fees. Bi-annual fee payments made by 16th February and 25th July receive a 2% discount off tuition fees. These discounts encourage prompt payment and help families reduce their overall education costs. The discounts apply to tuition fees only and are not available if accounts are in arrears or payments are made after the due date.
eligibility_criteria: All families enrolled at the college; Annual payment made by 16th February OR Bi-annual payments made by 16th February and 25th July; Account must not be in arrears; Payment must be made on or before due date
special_conditions: Discounts apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies, charges, or other compulsory fees. Discounts are forfeited if payment is made after the due date or if the account is in arrears.

Hume Central Secondary College-Blair Street Campus
