overview: The Islamic College of Sport offers scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional sporting excellence. The College awards up to five scholarships annually to students who display outstanding athletic ability and commitment to their chosen sports.
description: The Islamic College of Sport has up to five scholarships available for sporting excellence. These scholarships are awarded on a merit and case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. The selection process is based on demonstration of sporting excellence and athletic achievement. The value and duration of individual scholarships vary according to the College's assessment of each applicant's circumstances and the availability of funds.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating sporting excellence; Assessed on merit and case-by-case basis; Open to all students at the College
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depend on the number of applicants and the availability of places. All scholarships must be applied for during the formal enrolment process.

overview: The Islamic College of Sport provides scholarships to support students experiencing financial hardship. These scholarships aim to ensure that deserving students have the opportunity to access the College's programs regardless of their family's financial circumstances.
description: The Islamic College of Sport offers up to five scholarships available for students who are experiencing financial hardship. These scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial need through a formal assessment process. The duration and value of awards vary according to individual circumstances and annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students experiencing financial hardship; Assessed on demonstrated financial need and case-by-case basis; Must demonstrate genuine financial circumstances requiring support
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depend on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Financial circumstances are assessed during the enrolment process. All awards are subject to annual review.

school_name: Islamic College of Sport - Hoppers Crossing Campus
overview: Islamic College of Sport offers scholarships to students who demonstrate sporting excellence. The scholarship recognises exceptional athletic ability and commitment to high-performance sport, supporting student-athletes in achieving their sporting goals.
description: Islamic College of Sport has up to five scholarships available for students demonstrating sporting excellence. Scholarships are awarded on a merit and case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and availability of places. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. The College's High-Performance Program complements scholarship recipients' sporting development through personalised development plans designed by experts and professional athletes. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting ability and alignment with the College's high-performance sports pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated sporting excellence; Eligible for enrolment at Islamic College of Sport; Applicants assessed on merit and case-by-case basis; Commitment to sports-focused education pathway
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depend on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Recipients are expected to participate in the College's High-Performance Program.

school_name: Islamic College of Sport - Hoppers Crossing Campus
overview: Islamic College of Sport offers scholarships to support students experiencing financial hardship. This program enables access to the College's educational and sporting opportunities for eligible students regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Islamic College of Sport has up to five scholarships available for students experiencing financial hardship. The College recognises that financial barriers should not prevent capable students from accessing quality education and high-performance sporting pathways. Scholarships are awarded on a case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and availability of places. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to the College's educational and sporting programs.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship; Eligible for enrolment at Islamic College of Sport; Family financial assessment required; Case-by-case evaluation; Commitment to College's educational pathway
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depend on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Family financial circumstances are assessed as part of the application process.

overview: Iona College Geelong administers the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund to provide financial assistance to eligible families. The program delivers support for camps, sports activities and excursions to help all students participate in the full range of school experiences regardless of family income.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government financial assistance program administered by Iona College Geelong since 2020. Eligible families receive both government and college contributions totalling $500 per student per year ($250 government credit applied to school fee account plus an additional $250 provided by the College). The funds are specifically allocated for camps, sports and excursion costs to support student participation in co-curricular activities. Families holding a valid means-tested concession card or serving as temporary foster parents of school-age children are eligible to apply. Applications must be submitted with a copy of the Healthcare card. The program operates annually and allows families to list multiple students in a single application.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card (such as Healthcare card); OR be a temporary foster parent of school-age children; Must be enrolled at Iona College Geelong; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Payment made directly to the school and used towards camps, sports and excursion costs for the benefit of the student. Application must include copy of Healthcare card. Multiple students from the same family can be listed in one application.

overview: Iona College Geelong offers individual consideration of fee reductions for families experiencing financial hardship or difficult financial circumstances. Families can contact the College Business Manager to discuss their situation and seek support with tuition fees.
description: Iona College Geelong recognizes that financial circumstances vary for families and offers discretionary fee reduction support on a case-by-case basis. Families requiring fee reduction due to financial hardship or changed financial circumstances are invited to contact the College Business Manager, Tom Harriott, to discuss their individual situation. The college commits to considering requests for fee reduction and works with families to find solutions that ensure no student is denied education due to financial difficulty. This process is separate from the standard sibling discounts and government CSEF program, allowing families in genuine hardship to seek additional support.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or difficult financial circumstances; Individual assessment required; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Individual assessment and discussion required. Contact College Business Manager Tom Harriott at tom.harriott@iona.vic.edu.au to discuss specific circumstances. Decisions made on case-by-case basis.

overview: Iona College Geelong offers automatic fee discounts for families enrolling multiple children at the school. Students benefit from tiered discounts based on the number of siblings enrolled, reducing the overall cost of education.
description: Iona College Geelong provides automatic sibling discounts on all school fees for families with multiple students enrolled. The discount structure is 10% reduction on all fees for two students and 20% reduction on all fees for three or more students. These discounts apply to the base tuition fee of $7,235 (as of 2026) and are automatically applied at enrolment. The discounts do not require separate application and represent a commitment by the college to reduce the burden on families with multiple children attending the school.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more siblings enrolled at Iona College Geelong; Automatic application at enrolment; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Discount automatically applied for two students: 10% reduction on all fees for all students. For three or more students: 20% reduction on all fees for all students. Applies to all tuition and curriculum levies.

overview: Irymple Secondary College awards the All Rounder Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement across multiple areas of student endeavour. The scholarship acknowledges students who demonstrate excellence in academic, sporting, cultural, and community contributions.
description: The All Rounder Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional achievement across multiple domains of school life. Recipients are recognised for their well-rounded contribution to the college community through academic performance, co-curricular involvement, leadership qualities, and personal character. This award celebrates students who balance excellence in various aspects of their school experience. The exact financial value and duration of the scholarship are not publicly specified on the school's website. Selection is based on demonstrated all-round excellence across the college's academic and co-curricular programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated excellence across multiple areas of achievement; Strong academic performance; Active involvement in co-curricular activities
special_conditions: Award details are not publicly available. Interested students should contact the school directly for application information and eligibility requirements.

overview: Irymple Secondary College recognises students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in drama and performing arts. The Drama Scholarship awards students who show exceptional talent and commitment to theatrical performance.
description: The Drama Scholarship is awarded to students who have demonstrated exceptional talent and commitment in dramatic performance and theatre arts at Irymple Secondary College. This award recognises those with outstanding abilities in drama and a strong dedication to performing arts. Students have been recognised for performances at events such as the Mildura Eisteddfod Gala Concert. The exact financial value and specific selection criteria are not publicly detailed on the school's website. Interested applicants should contact the school for complete program details and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Irymple Secondary College students; Demonstrated exceptional talent in drama and performing arts; Commitment to theatrical performance
special_conditions: Recipients are recognised for participation in major performance opportunities such as the Mildura Eisteddfod. Specific renewal conditions and financial details are not publicly available.

overview: The Doris Price Memorial Scholarship provides financial assistance to Irymple Secondary College students. This scholarship award honours the memory of Doris Price and supports students at the school.
description: The Doris Price Memorial Scholarship is a named award providing financial assistance to Irymple Secondary College students. In 2018, a Year 8 student was awarded $200 from this scholarship, indicating it provides direct monetary support to recipients. The scholarship appears to recognise students based on merit and/or demonstrated need. The exact eligibility criteria, application process, and renewal conditions are not publicly specified on the school's website. For comprehensive information about this scholarship, families should contact the school directly.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Irymple Secondary College students; Specific eligibility criteria not publicly available
special_conditions: Named memorial scholarship. Selection and application procedures are not publicly documented.

school_name: Islamic College of Sport - North Campus
overview: The Islamic College of Sport offers Sporting Excellence Scholarships to recognise and support exceptional student-athletes. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding sporting achievement and potential to excel in their chosen sport while completing their VCE studies at the College.
description: The College has up to five scholarships available for sporting excellence. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. Awards are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places. The selection process involves assessment of sporting achievement, potential, and commitment to the College's programs. This scholarship is available for students entering the senior secondary program and aims to support talented athletes in pursuing their passion for sport whilst completing their education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; Demonstration of sporting excellence; Merit-based assessment on case-by-case basis; Subject to availability of places
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places.

school_name: Islamic College of Sport - North Campus
overview: The Islamic College of Sport offers Financial Hardship Scholarships to support students experiencing financial difficulties. The scholarship recognises that financial circumstances should not prevent deserving students from accessing the College's unique education and sports programs.
description: The College has up to five scholarships available for students who are experiencing financial hardship. Scholarships are awarded between 50–100 per cent reduction in tuition fees. Awards are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis, with the number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Eligible students must demonstrate genuine financial need and commitment to the College's educational mission. The selection process involves assessment of financial circumstances and student merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; Demonstration of financial hardship; Merit-based assessment on case-by-case basis; Subject to availability of places
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided on merit and a case-by-case basis. The number and value of scholarships depending on the number of applicants and the availability of places. Combined pool with Sporting Excellence Scholarship (total of up to 5 across both categories).

school_name: International Maarif Schools of Australia – Melbourne Campus
overview: International Maarif Schools of Australia offers ACER Academic Scholarships to recognise and support students with exceptional academic achievement entering secondary school. The scholarship is assessed through the ACER Scholarship Test and provides pathways for high-achieving students in Years 7-10.
description: International Maarif Schools of Australia is currently offering ACER Academic Scholarships for 2026 entry to students with outstanding academic ability. Level 1 ACER Scholarship Test is available for students entering Year 7 or Year 8 in 2026, while Level 2 ACER Scholarship Test is available for students entering Year 9 or Year 10 in 2026. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence and merit-based achievement. The scholarships provide tuition fee reductions as determined by the school. The application process involves completing an online application through the ACER Scholarships portal, with students required to sit the appropriate level ACER assessment test. Selection is based on performance in the ACER test and school assessment records.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10 in 2026; Application through ACER Scholarships portal; No permanent residency requirement stated; Completion of ACER Scholarship Test (Level 1 or Level 2 depending on year of entry)
special_conditions: Applications are made directly through the ACER Scholarships portal. Select International Maarif Schools of Australia - ACER Scholarship Test from the school list. Specific scholarship amounts vary based on academic merit and performance in the ACER assessment.

school_name: International Maarif Schools of Australia – Melbourne Campus
overview: International Maarif Schools of Australia offers comprehensive scholarships recognising students with academic excellence, sporting prowess, or musical talent. These merit-based scholarships support students demonstrating outstanding ability across academic, sports, and performing arts disciplines.
description: International Maarif Schools of Australia offers Maarif Academic, Sports & Music Scholarships for secondary students. This unified application allows students to apply across three distinct categories: academic merit, sports excellence, or music performance. The school recognises that talented students may excel in different areas and provides pathways for all types of achievement. Applicants complete the Maarif Academic, Sports & Music Application form. The selection process varies by category - academic applicants may undertake testing or assessment of academic records, sports candidates may be assessed on sporting ability and achievement, and music candidates may present evidence of musical performance or achievement. Exact fee remission amounts are determined by the school following assessment of each application.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering secondary school (Years 7-10); Open to all school sectors; Current enrolment or intention to enrol at International Maarif Schools of Australia; Demonstrated excellence in at least one of three areas: academic, sports, or music
special_conditions: Applications submitted using the Maarif Academic, Sports & Music Application form. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in the nominated discipline (academic performance, sporting achievement, or musical ability). Scholarship value and specific conditions determined by school following application review.

school_name: International Maarif Schools of Australia – Melbourne Campus
overview: International Maarif Schools of Australia offers Indigenous Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in accessing quality education. This culturally responsive scholarship recognises the importance of supporting Indigenous students in their educational journey.
description: International Maarif Schools of Australia is committed to supporting Indigenous students through the Maarif Indigenous Scholarship program. The scholarship is available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students seeking to enrol at the school. Applications are made using the dedicated Maarif Indigenous Scholarship Application form. The selection process considers students' circumstances, academic potential, and commitment to pursuing their education. The scholarship provides tuition fee reductions to support eligible Indigenous students who may face barriers to accessing independent school education. The school aims to foster cultural inclusivity and provide meaningful educational opportunities for Indigenous learners.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander ancestry; Intention to enrol at International Maarif Schools of Australia; Students from all year levels welcome to apply; Demonstration of commitment to educational pursuits
special_conditions: Applications submitted using the Maarif Indigenous Scholarship Application form. Specific eligibility requirements and scholarship amounts determined by the school. overview: Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School offers Music Scholarships to students entering Years 5 through to Year 11. description: Music Scholarships are awarded on the basis of an audition conducted similar to an AMEB exam. Applicants must meet minimum AMEB grade standards that vary by year level and instrument. For students in Years 4-6 at the time of audition, minimum Grade 1 standard is required for Bassoon, French Horn, Oboe, and Percussion; Grade 2 for Double Bass, Clarinet, Trombone, Trumpet, and Drum Kit. For students in Years 7-9, minimum Grade 2 standard applies, and for Years 8-10, Grade 3 minimum is required. All Piano students applying for a Music Scholarship in Years 9-10 must also learn a second instrument at an appropriate grade standard. Singers with a second orchestral instrument are given preference. The audition process includes a video screening followed by a live audition if shortlisted. Recipients must perform solo or with accompaniment, presenting contrasting pieces if applying with one instrument.

Selection involves written application, past school reports, and assessment of suitability for scholarship. Shortlisted candidates are invited for interview with the Principal or Head of Junior/Senior School.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027; Open to external students and currently enrolled Ivanhoe Grammar School students; International students with student visa (subclass 500) ineligible; Domestic candidates only; Demonstration of high academic potential, leadership, citizenship, and talent in visual arts, music, performing arts, or sports
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 entry are now closed. International students with student visa subclass 500 are ineligible. Application fee is $150 and non-refundable. Holistic assessment including written application, school reports, and interview. Shortlisted candidates invited to interview with Principal or Head of Junior/Senior School to explore suitability and alignment with school values.

overview: In celebration of the opening of the Coonawarra Willam Aquatic Centre at Plenty Campus in 2025, Ivanhoe Grammar School offers the Peter Rule Sports Scholarship to elite athletes with strong academic credentials. The scholarship is designed for students entering Year 10 or Year 11 who demonstrate excellence in sport combined with academic achievement.
description: The Peter Rule Sports Scholarship was established to celebrate the opening of the Coonawarra Willam Aquatic Centre and extended sports precinct at Plenty Campus in 2025. The scholarship is available for elite sporting students with strong academic credentials entering either Year 10 or Year 11 in 2026. This prestigious award is initially offered for students currently attending Ivanhoe Grammar School or new students looking to attend Ivanhoe Grammar School's Plenty Campus. The scholarship recognises elite athlete status combined with strong academic performance. Financial value and specific selection criteria are to be confirmed through direct contact with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to elite sporting students entering Year 10 or Year 11 in 2026; Strong academic credentials required; Current Ivanhoe students or new students; Initially for Plenty Campus only; Students must demonstrate elite athlete status at recognised level
special_conditions: Applications are currently closed. Initially offered for Plenty Campus only for entry in 2026. Prestigious award for elite athletes with strong academic credentials. Students currently attending Ivanhoe or new external students may apply. Direct contact with school needed for specific details on financial value and selection process.

overview: Ivanhoe Grammar School offers the First Nations Scholarship to provide educational opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Established in partnership with the Aborigines Advancement League, the scholarship covers tuition fees and supports students residing at the Gladys Nicholls Hostel in Northcote.
description: From 2022, the Ivanhoe Grammar School First Nations Scholarship provides opportunities for Indigenous students to attend the school each year. This unique program was developed over three years in partnership with the Aborigines Advancement League. The scholarship covers tuition fees for First Nations students allowing them to attend Ivanhoe Grammar School. As Ivanhoe Grammar School is not a boarding school, First Nations Scholarship recipients reside at the Gladys Nicholls Hostel in Northcote under the direct care of Aboriginal hostel parents. The scholarship covers tuition fees, approved co-curricular activities and camp fees, education materials, and school uniforms. Available to secondary years students with scholarships awarded at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students; Secondary years students; Must be eligible for ABSTUDY living away from home allowance; Must reside at Gladys Nicholls Hostel in Northcote; Demonstrated commitment to education and community values
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must reside at Gladys Nicholls Hostel in Northcote under care of Aboriginal hostel parents. Tuition fees covered. Approved co-curricular activities, camp fees, education materials, and school uniforms covered. ABSTUDY living away from home allowance eligibility required. Scholarships available to secondary years students at Principal's discretion. Unique program emphasising family environment and cultural safety.

overview: Ivanhoe Grammar School offered the Exceptional Scholars Award for entry into Year 9, specifically for the University Campus in Macleod. This award was designed for external applicants demonstrating exceptional academic ability and potential for secondary education success.
description: Ivanhoe Grammar School offered the Exceptional Scholars Award for entry in Year 9 (2024), specifically for the University Campus located in Macleod. This scholarship opportunity was open to external applicants only. If successful, the Exceptional Scholars Award offered a reduction of up to 50% of the tuition fees for the duration of the student's secondary education. All other fees and charges were payable in full. The application required completion of an online application form including a personal statement and submission of two most recent school reports. International students in receipt of a student visa (subclass 500) or requiring such visa were ineligible.
eligibility_criteria: External applicants only; Entry into Year 9 (2024); University Campus in Macleod; International students with student visa (subclass 500) ineligible; Domestic candidates only; Personal statement and two recent school reports required
special_conditions: Open to external applicants only. International students with student visa subclass 500 ineligible. All other fees and charges payable in full. Application required online form with personal statement and two most recent school reports (mid-year 2023 and end of year 2022). Specific to University Campus in Macleod, providing year-long university-based learning experience.

overview: Kamaruka offers bursary assistance for families experiencing financial hardship. Single low-income parents may qualify for funding to reduce school fees below the standard rate.
description: Kamaruka is a non-profit independent school committed to supporting families facing financial difficulties. Single low-income parents may qualify for funding to reduce fees below 50%. The school recognises that families may experience difficulty in meeting payment deadlines and provides special arrangements, such as payment plans, in consultation with the Principal. Families are encouraged to contact the Principal as soon as possible to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options. The school reviews all bursary applications on a case-by-case basis and works collaboratively with families to find sustainable payment solutions.
eligibility_criteria: Single parent household; Low-income status; Open to families experiencing financial hardship; Families must contact Principal to discuss circumstances
special_conditions: Applications assessed on a case-by-case basis. Families experiencing difficulty in meeting payment deadlines should contact the principal as soon as possible to make special arrangements, such as a payment plan. Fees are reviewed annually by the Committee of Management.

overview: John Paul College offers Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarships to students entering Year 7. These scholarships recognise outstanding academic achievement and engagement within the student's primary school community, providing percentage remission of academic tuition fees.
description: Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7 at John Paul College. These scholarships provide a percentage remission of academic tuition fees. Selection is based on performance in a scholarship examination, along with demonstrated engagement and contribution within the student's primary school community. Students who have secured a place for the upcoming academic year will receive an email invitation to apply. In the year prior to commencement, families will receive further information, including the Scholarship Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be enrolled to enter John Paul College; Must have secured a place for the upcoming academic year; Academic performance demonstrated in scholarship examination
special_conditions: Students who have secured a place for the upcoming academic year will receive an email invitation to apply. Selection based on scholarship examination performance and demonstrated engagement and contribution within primary school community.

overview: John Paul College offers Year 8-12 Academic Excellence Scholarships to current students automatically based on academic performance. No application is required, and the scholarship covers a percentage of the academic tuition fee based on the student's current academic record and contributions to the College community.
description: Year 8-12 Academic Excellence Scholarships are available to all current students with no application required. The scholarship covers a percentage of the academic tuition fee. The student's current academic record will be the major criterion for selection, as well as their contributions to the College community. Recipients are automatically considered based on their ongoing academic performance and engagement within the College.
eligibility_criteria: Current students in Years 8-12; Strong academic record; Active contributions to College community; No application required
special_conditions: No application required. Automatic selection based on current academic record and contributions to College community. Subject to annual review.

overview: John Paul College offers Year 8-12 Academic Excellence Scholarships to external prospective students from Years 8 to 12. The scholarship covers up to 50% of the student's tuition fee and recognises strong academic records and involvement in co-curricular and extra-curricular activities.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are open to external students from Years 8 to 12. The scholarship will cover up to 50% of the student's tuition fee. To be successful, the student must demonstrate a strong academic record and involvement in co-curricular and extra-curricular activities at their current school and/or in the wider community. Applicants must also submit an Application for Enrolment along with their scholarship application, ensuring comprehensive review of academic achievement and community involvement.
eligibility_criteria: External students entering Years 8-12; Strong academic record required; Demonstrated involvement in co-curricular and extra-curricular activities; Must submit Application for Enrolment
special_conditions: Applicants must submit an Application for Enrolment. Scholarship covers up to 50% of tuition fee only. Subject to annual review.

overview: John Paul College annually offers Instrumental Music Scholarships to high performing students who show outstanding potential on a musical instrument of their choice. The College recognises musical excellence across Voice, Piano/Keyboard, Guitar, Bass Guitar and Drumkit.
description: John Paul College annually offers music scholarships to high performing students who show outstanding potential on a musical instrument of their choice. Instruments offered are Voice (Singing), Piano/Keyboard, Guitar (Electric and Acoustic), Bass Guitar and Drumkit. In order to be considered for a music scholarship, families must complete an Instrumental Music Scholarship Application, prepare one piece of music on their chosen instrument, and attend an audition at the start of the school year. The scholarship provides percentage remission of academic tuition fees for successful applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Years 7-12; Outstanding potential and high performance on chosen instrument; Must complete Instrumental Music Scholarship Application; Must prepare and perform one piece of music; Must attend audition at start of school year
special_conditions: Families must complete Instrumental Music Scholarship Application, prepare one piece of music on chosen instrument, and attend audition at start of school year. Instruments offered: Voice (Singing), Piano/Keyboard, Guitar (Electric and Acoustic), Bass Guitar and Drumkit.

overview: Kardinia International College offers Music Scholarships to students entering Years 7-11. These scholarships recognise outstanding musical ability and performance at an advanced level, supporting students who demonstrate exceptional talent on their primary instrument.
description: Music Scholarships at Kardinia International College are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and technical proficiency. Successful Music Scholarship recipients will be entitled to a reduction in academic and/or music tuition fees, as determined by the Principal. Students must be playing at a recommended AMEB grade level or higher, with lower levels acceptable for brass and vocal students. The selection process involves a video audition submission showing two contrasting pieces, followed by a shortlist process where students attend an in-person audition including two performance works, aural tests, sight-reading test, and interview with College Leadership Team. Music Scholarships will be reviewed biennially. All responsibility for instrumental music fees will rest with the recipient.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 7-11; Must have application for enrolment at the College; Playing at recommended AMEB grade level or higher for most instruments; Lower levels acceptable for brass and vocal students; Vocal auditions available for Years 9-11 only; Students must participate in an instrumental and/or vocal ensemble as deemed appropriate
special_conditions: Successful applicants are required as a condition of the award to participate in an instrumental and/or vocal ensemble as deemed appropriate. Music Scholarships will be reviewed biennially. All responsibility for instrumental music fees will rest with the recipient.

overview: From 2027, Kardinia International College offers Bridges to the Future Scholarships to new students entering Years 7-11 who could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. These scholarships recognise general excellence combining academic achievement with high achievement in arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice.
description: The Bridges to the Future (Bridges) Scholarships are offered to new students entering Years 7-11 from 2027 who could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. The Scholarships are awarded for general excellence with an emphasis on academic achievement combined with high achievement in at least one of the following: the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice. Winners must demonstrate a strong connection to the College's cornerstones, in particular international mindedness. The financial value is determined individually and represents fee reduction as determined by the College Principal. The awarding of scholarships is based on an initial application followed by a panel interview for shortlisted students. Students from indigenous backgrounds are encouraged to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 7-11 from 2027 onwards; Must have application for enrolment at the College; Could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons; Must demonstrate general excellence with emphasis on academic achievement; Must show high achievement in at least one area: arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice; Must demonstrate strong connection to College's cornerstones, particularly international mindedness; Indigenous background encouraged
special_conditions: From 2027 entry onwards only. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate ongoing strong connection to College's cornerstones and international mindedness. Initial application followed by panel interview for shortlisted students. Students from indigenous backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

school_name: Kardinia International College - Kardinia Grove Campus
overview: Kardinia International College offers Music Scholarships to reward outstanding musical achievement in students entering Years 7-11. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to performance.
description: Music Scholarships are awarded to students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate exceptional musical performance. Recipients will receive a reduction in academic and/or music tuition fees as determined by the Principal. Students must be playing at a recommended AMEB grade level or higher (lower levels acceptable for brass and vocal students). The selection process includes a video audition of two contrasting pieces on the primary instrument, shortlisted live auditions with aural tests and sight-reading, school reports, and an interview with College Leadership. Vocal auditions are available for Years 9-11 students only. Music Scholarship recipients are required as a condition of the award to participate in an instrumental and/or vocal ensemble as deemed appropriate, and all instrumental music fees remain the responsibility of the recipient.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11; Must be currently enrolled in primary or secondary school; Playing at recommended AMEB grade level or higher; Lower levels acceptable for brass and vocal students; Vocal auditions available for Years 9-11 only; Must have application for enrolment at the College; Students from indigenous backgrounds encouraged to apply
special_conditions: Successful recipients are required to participate in an instrumental and/or vocal ensemble as deemed appropriate. Scholarships will be reviewed biennially. All responsibility for instrumental music fees rests with the recipient. Video audition format must be MP4 or similar, maximum 10 minutes total duration.

school_name: Kardinia International College - Kardinia Grove Campus
overview: Kardinia International College offers the Bridges to the Future Scholarships from 2027 to support new students entering Years 7-11 who could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. The scholarships recognise general excellence with emphasis on academic achievement combined with high achievement in the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice.
description: From 2027, the Bridges to the Future Scholarships provide financial support to new students entering Years 7-11 who could not otherwise afford to attend the College. Scholarships are awarded for general excellence with emphasis on academic achievement combined with high achievement in at least one of the following: the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice. Winners must demonstrate a strong connection to the College's cornerstones, particularly international mindedness. The selection process involves an initial written application (maximum two-page summary of achievements, two references, maximum 500 words on why attending Kardinia, maximum 500 words on international mindedness) followed by panel interviews for shortlisted students. Financial value is determined by individual circumstances and need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Must demonstrate financial hardship; Could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons; Demonstrated general excellence with emphasis on academic achievement; High achievement in at least one area: arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice; Must show strong connection to College's cornerstones, particularly international mindedness; Must have application for enrolment at the College; Students from indigenous backgrounds encouraged to apply
special_conditions: Available from 2027 onwards only. Scholarships are awarded based on financial need combined with merit in specific areas. Financial value determined individually by the Principal based on circumstances. Recipients should demonstrate strong commitment to College's international mindedness ethos.

school_name: Kardinia International College - Kardinia Grove Campus
overview: Kardinia International College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic performance. The selection process involves sitting a standardised academic assessment examination at the College. The exam evaluates students' academic abilities across key subject areas. Successful applicants will receive a reduction in tuition fees as determined by the Principal. Registration for the 2026 academic scholarship exam closes on 10 February 2025, with the exam held on 22 February 2025. These scholarships provide financial support to high-achieving Year 7 students and recognise academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated strong academic performance; Must sit the academic scholarship examination; Must have application for enrolment at the College
special_conditions: Registration closes 10 February 2025. Examination held 22 February 2025 at the College. Financial value of scholarship determined by the Principal based on academic performance.

program_name: McDonald House Trust Financial Support Program
overview: The McDonald House Trust provides financial support to families of Deaf and Hard of Hearing students enrolled in the Bendigo Deaf Facility. The program assists eligible families by offering partial reimbursement of educational expenses, supporting access to education for students with hearing disabilities.
description: The McDonald House Trust was established to financially support eligible families of Deaf and Hard of Hearing students within the Bendigo Deaf Facility. The program offers partial reimbursement of school fees, school uniforms, school camp costs, and other educational expenses related to a child's enrolment in the Bendigo Deaf Facility. Eligible families can claim a percentage of their student's educational expenses through this Trust. The support is available for students from Foundation through Year 12 across the three schools hosting the Deaf Facility: Kennington Primary School (Foundation to Grade 6), Bendigo South East Secondary College (Years 7-10), and Bendigo Senior Secondary College (Years 11-12). The program operates on a means-tested basis, prioritising families demonstrating financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be enrolled in the Bendigo Deaf Facility; Student must be Deaf or Hard of Hearing; Student must be attending one of the three host schools (Kennington Primary School, Bendigo South East College, or Bendigo Senior Secondary College); Family must demonstrate financial need; Eligible families determined through McDonald House Trust assessment process
special_conditions: Partial reimbursement is calculated based on eligible family circumstances and the Trust's assessment. The Trust determines the percentage of expenses that will be reimbursed. Applications and enquiries should be directed to the Bendigo Deaf Facility Coordinator at Kennington Primary School.

overview: Keilor Downs College offers the William Turner Scholarship to recognise students who have demonstrated consistent academic achievement and made significant contributions to their Primary School community. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 with confirmed enrolment at the college.
description: The William Turner Scholarship recognises students transitioning to Year 7 who have shown consistent academic achievement and have made a significant contribution to their Primary School community. Applicants are required to complete an application form with support from their Grade 6 teacher. The application process includes an assessment of academic merit and community contribution. The scholarship offers unspecified financial benefits (exact value not publicly disclosed on the website). Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance in primary school and evidence of meaningful contribution to the primary school community. Recipients are expected to maintain the college's standards and values during their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 with confirmed enrolment at Keilor Downs College; Currently enrolled in Grade 6 at a primary school; Demonstrated consistent academic achievement; Evidence of significant contribution to Primary School community
special_conditions: Applications must be completed with the assistance of the Grade 6 teacher. Late applications will not be considered. Scholarship is awarded annually on a competitive basis to qualifying Year 7 students.

program_name: SEAL - Select Entry & Accelerated Learning Program
overview: Keilor Downs College offers the Select Entry & Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program for gifted students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential. The program has delivered excellent outcomes for over 15 years and provides accelerated curriculum and enrichment for high-achieving students in Years 7 and 9.
description: The SEAL Program is designed to identify and support academically gifted students through rigorous testing and accelerated learning opportunities. The program accepts applications for entry in Year 7 and Year 9 after students have been offered mainstream places at the college. Selection is determined through the ACER HAST (Highly Able Student Test) which assesses reading comprehension, mathematical reasoning, abstract reasoning, and written expression. The test evaluates innate ability and academic potential rather than curriculum-based knowledge. Year 7 testing occurs in mid-August (Term 3), while Year 9 testing occurs in Term 4. The application fee is $110 for each test attempt. Students must meet specific academic benchmarks on the HAST assessment to be selected for the program.
eligibility_criteria: Must have received an offer of mainstream place at Keilor Downs College; Students entering Year 7 or Year 9; Must be willing to commit to accelerated learning program requirements; Open to all school sectors (government, private, Catholic)
special_conditions: Students can only apply for SEAL AFTER they have been offered a mainstream place at Keilor Downs College. Registration and payment for testing must be completed at the school office. Testing venue is Keilor Downs College. Social distancing protocols strictly enforced. Number of positions may be limited based on available spaces.

overview: The Camps, Sports & Excursions Fund (CSEF) provides annual financial assistance to eligible students at Keilor Downs College to support participation in school camps, sports programs, and educational excursions. This government-funded support program removes financial barriers to student participation in enrichment activities.
description: CSEF is an annual government payment made directly to Keilor Downs College to assist eligible students with costs associated with camps, sports programs, and excursion activities. The fund aims to ensure all students can participate in valuable co-curricular experiences regardless of family financial circumstances. Eligibility is based on family income and access to specific government payments or services. The CSEF funds are allocated per eligible student and used to offset participation costs throughout the school year. The specific dollar amount per student is determined by the Victorian government annually. This is a non-competitive, means-tested program with eligibility assessed through family income documentation and receipt of eligible government assistance payments.
eligibility_criteria: Family must meet income threshold; Must be a recipient of eligible government payments; Victorian residency required; Student must be enrolled at Keilor Downs College
special_conditions: Eligibility must be verified through government income assessment. Payments are made to the school, not directly to families. Assistance is limited to eligible students based on annual government criteria.

overview: The State School Relief Fund provides emergency financial assistance to students experiencing hardship to support access to essential school items such as uniforms, clothing, and footwear. This support ensures students can participate fully in school life regardless of family financial difficulties.
description: The State School Relief Fund is a Victoria-based assistance program that provides eligible students with support for essential school items including uniforms, clothing, and footwear. Assistance is provided in the form of items supplied from the school's own relief stock, delivered to school at no cost, or through authorised purchasing arrangements where families contribute on a co-payment basis. The program is designed for students facing genuine financial hardship who cannot afford essential school items required for participation and engagement in the school community. Eligibility is assessed on a case-by-case basis, with preference given to families experiencing temporary financial difficulty. The program operates on a confidential basis to protect student privacy and dignity.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate financial hardship; Essential school items (uniform, clothing, footwear) must be required; Student must be enrolled at Keilor Downs College; Referral or application through school administration required
special_conditions: Assistance is subject to availability of relief stock or approved supplier relationships. Families may be required to contribute on a co-payment basis for some items. Applications treated confidentially. Not a direct cash payment program; items provided in kind or through authorised purchasing.

overview: Keysborough Secondary College offers the Keysborough Extension and Enrichment Program (KEEP) as a select entry program for eligible Year 7 students who demonstrate high potential to excel in English and Mathematics. The program is designed for ambitious students and provides enrichment opportunities in other learning domains throughout the year.
description: KEEP is a selective entry program available for Year 7 students who possess high potential to excel in English and Mathematics at Keysborough Secondary College. The program is designed to provide students with the skills and knowledge they need to excel in their future endeavours. Enrichment opportunities are offered in other Learning Domains throughout the year. There is no cost associated with the Keysborough College Extension and Enrichment program, however students must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College for the 2026 school year to be eligible to undertake the EduTest exam, which is a requirement that will determine their suitability for the program. The program aims to challenge students working ahead of age-expected levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College for 2026; Must pass EduTest exam; High potential in English and Mathematics
special_conditions: Registration required via QR code or Keysborough College website. Exam conducted on Saturday 2 August 2025 from 9:00am to 11:45am at Banksia Campus, 8-20 Janine Road, Springvale South 3172. Applicants must arrive by 8:45am for prompt start.

program_name: Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) Select Entry Program
overview: Keysborough Secondary College offers a select entry STEM program for Year 10 students who demonstrate exceptional proclivity for Mathematics and Science. The program provides a dedicated pipeline to university studies in Science, Technology, Mathematics and Engineering, with both Acacia and Banksia campuses offering specialized classes for approximately 20 students each.
description: The STEM program is a Keysborough Secondary College select entry program involving technology in schools. Students completing Year 9 who show a proclivity for Mathematics and Science must successfully complete the entrance exam. The STEM program offers a pipeline to University studies in Science, Technology, Mathematics and Engineering. Both the Keysborough Secondary College Acacia and Banksia campuses provide a STEM program, with dedicated classes for around 20 students at each campus. The curriculum is geared towards achieving better VCE results and providing practical and tangible experiences to help senior students identify and work towards university courses in their areas of interest. The program can make a real difference to the future of many students from middle to lower socio-economic communities in the south-east of Melbourne.
eligibility_criteria: Completed Year 9; Must show proclivity for Mathematics and Science; Must pass entrance exam
special_conditions: Dedicated classes of approximately 20 students at each campus. Keysborough Secondary College has established links with leading international STEM programs in the UK including York University. Locally, the college is working with Monash University on reciprocal arrangements to enhance the program.

overview: Keysborough Secondary College participates in the Victorian High Abilities Program (VHAP), a state-based gifted and talented initiative designed to support students working ahead of age-expected levels. VHAP is part of the school's comprehensive Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) strategy.
description: The Victorian High Abilities Program (VHAP) is a state-based selective entry gifted and talented education program at Keysborough Secondary College that ensures students working ahead of the age-expected level are challenged in their learning. This program is one component of the school's comprehensive Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) initiative. VHAP provides extension and enrichment opportunities throughout the year, including incursions, excursions, guest speakers, and participation in community competitions. The program demonstrates a commitment to supporting high-achieving students and fostering their academic growth. Keysborough Secondary College's GATE programs take great pride in their ongoing partnerships with Melbourne's leading universities.
eligibility_criteria: Must be identified as working ahead of age-expected levels; Selection based on demonstrated high ability
special_conditions: Program includes access to extension and enrichment opportunities such as incursions, excursions, guest speakers, and participation in community competitions. Partnerships with Melbourne's leading universities provide additional benefits and opportunities.

overview: Keysborough Secondary College provides access to the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a government-funded program that provides payments for eligible students to attend camps, sports and excursions. Students under 16 years of age whose parents hold a valid Health Care Card or Pension Card may be eligible.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a government financial assistance program available to eligible students at Keysborough Secondary College. Students under 16 years of age whose parents hold a valid Health Care Card or Pension Card may be eligible for CSEF support. The fund provides payments to assist in the costs associated with education, including camps, sports activities, and excursions. Instructions and application forms are available from the school administration office. The program commenced in 2015 and continues to provide valuable financial support to families who meet the eligibility criteria, helping to ensure all students can participate in co-curricular activities regardless of family financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be under 16 years of age; Parent or guardian must hold a valid Health Care Card or Pension Card; Students must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College
special_conditions: Application forms and instructions are available from the school administration office. Fund is managed through government and has been in place since 2015.

program_name: Monash University Partnership - Exam Revision Program
overview: Keysborough Secondary College has established a partnership with Monash University that supports the educational aspirations of senior students from the City of Greater Dandenong. The program provides free exam revision lectures for Year 11 and Year 12 students.
description: A partnership arrangement between Monash University and Keysborough Secondary College supports the educational aspirations of students from the City of Greater Dandenong. As part of this agreement, senior students are provided with free exam revision lectures designed to assist with VCE preparation and success. The program demonstrates the school's commitment to providing additional academic support resources to help senior students achieve their best outcomes. This partnership provides tangible support through expert-led revision sessions that prepare students for their final examinations. The collaboration represents a significant resource advantage for eligible students at Keysborough Secondary College.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College as a senior student; Students in Year 11 or Year 12 from the City of Greater Dandenong area
special_conditions: Free exam revision lectures provided by Monash University. Part of formal partnership agreement between Monash University and Keysborough Secondary College.

overview: Keysborough Secondary College offers the 'Hands On Health' program, sponsored by the Monash University Health Sciences faculty. The program provides support and practical assistance for students interested in pursuing a career in Medicine and Health Sciences.
description: The 'Hands On Health' program is sponsored by the Monash University Health Sciences faculty and offers support and practical assistance for students interested in pursuing a career in Medicine and Health Sciences at Keysborough Secondary College. The program provides hands-on learning opportunities and expert guidance from health sciences professionals. Students gain exposure to health sciences pathways and receive mentoring to help them develop their interests and skills in this field. The program supports students in Years 10, 11 and 12 who are considering health-related university courses and careers. Access to this specialized program provides students with valuable insights into medical and health sciences education and career opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College; Must demonstrate interest in Medicine or Health Sciences careers; Generally available to Year 10, Year 11 and Year 12 students
special_conditions: Sponsored by Monash University Health Sciences faculty. Provides practical assistance and support specifically targeted at students considering health science career pathways.

overview: Keysborough College offers the Keysborough Extension and Enrichment Program (KEEP) as a selective entry program for high-potential Year 7 students. The program is designed for students who demonstrate high potential to excel in English and Mathematics and provides advanced academic opportunities and enrichment activities.
description: The Keysborough Extension and Enrichment Program (KEEP) is a comprehensive selective entry initiative designed for high-ability students. Students must successfully complete an entrance examination in English and Mathematics to gain entry. The program recognizes and nurtures the natural gifts of learners across intellectual, physical, creative and social domains. KEEP students participate in a tailored curriculum that fosters critical thinking, problem-solving and creative deep thinking, with emphasis on intellectually stimulating activities. The program includes participation in extension and enrichment opportunities such as incursions, excursions, guest speakers and community competitions. There is no cost associated with the program; however, students must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College. The program runs for the duration of Year 7 onwards.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible Year 7 students entering Keysborough College; Demonstrate high potential to excel in English and Mathematics; Must complete and pass the entrance examination successfully; Must be enrolled at Keysborough Secondary College for the 2026 school year
special_conditions: No cost associated with the program. Registrations closed 28 July 2025 for the August 2 2025 examination. Examination held on Saturday 2nd August 2025 from 9.00am-11.45am at Keysborough College Banksia Campus. Top performers in English and Maths will be invited to join KEEP classes.

overview: Keysborough College offers a selective entry STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) program for Year 10 students. The program involves technology in schools and provides pathways to university studies in STEM fields.
description: The STEM program is a selective entry Keysborough Secondary College initiative involving advanced technology in schools. Students completing Year 9 who show a proclivity for Mathematics and Science must successfully complete the entrance examination. Approximately 20 students per campus are selected for dedicated STEM classes at both Acacia and Banksia campuses. The curriculum is geared toward achieving better VCE results and providing practical and tangible experiences to help senior students identify and pursue University courses in their areas of interest. The program offers a pipeline to University studies in Science, Technology, Mathematics and Engineering. The college provides state-of-the-art Science teaching labs, Technology and Engineering design and build facilities, and a team of dedicated specialist teachers. Practical hands-on projects involve VIC Roads Civil Engineering, National Science competitions and Mathematics Technology competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Students completing Year 9; Must demonstrate proclivity for Mathematics and Science; Must complete and pass the entrance examination successfully; Approximately 20 students selected per campus
special_conditions: Keysborough Secondary College has established links with leading international STEM programs in the UK including York University. Locally, Keysborough College works with Monash University on reciprocal arrangements to enhance the program.

overview: Keysborough College participates in the Victorian High-Ability Program (VHAP), a state-wide selective program offered to high-ability students. The program provides 10-week online enrichment courses in Mathematics and English for eligible students in Years 5-8.
description: The Victorian High-Ability Program (VHAP) is an online enrichment program for high-ability students in Years 5-8 at government schools including Keysborough College. The program consists of ten-week online courses in either Mathematics or English delivered through Virtual School Victoria. Students engage in synchronous Webex classes with trained teachers and can also access course material on the VSV Online platform outside of class time. The program focuses on extending and developing students' mathematical and English abilities through intellectually stimulating tasks that develop critical thinking, problem-solving and creativity. Participation is free with no associated costs. Upon completion, students participate in a face-to-face masterclass with other high-ability students from their local area. Selection is conducted centrally by the Department of Education based on NAPLAN and teacher judgement data, with priority given to students experiencing socio-economic disadvantage, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students, or those from regional/rural areas.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled students in Years 5-8; Selected by Department of Education based on NAPLAN and teacher judgement data; Priority to high-ability students experiencing socio-economic disadvantage; Priority to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Priority to students from regional or rural areas
special_conditions: Free participation in the ten-week online course. No fees for participation in virtual classes. Schools may request parents pay for transportation to masterclasses as per department parent payments policy. Department provides Student Excellence Program funding for masterclass costs and other associated expenses. Selection lists uploaded each term to VSV portal. Schools must obtain parental/carer consent for participation. Students can only participate in each course once as material repeats each term.

overview: Kilvington offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 5, 7, 9 and 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through an independent academic examination and interview assessment.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship at Kilvington is awarded based on performance in an independent academic examination and a formal interview. Successful applicants receive scholarships valued from 25% to 50% fee reduction, with the exact financial benefit determined at the Principal's discretion and varying by year level. Scholarships are offered for the duration of enrolment until the completion of Year 12. The selection process involves sitting the ACER scholarship test (Primary level for Year 5, Level 1 for Year 7, Level 2 for Year 9, and Level 3 for Year 11) followed by an interview. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12, and must repay the full scholarship value if they choose to leave before completing Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Successful applicants must fully participate in school life until end of Year 12; If scholarship holder leaves school before Year 12 completion, scholarship value must be repaid in full; Acceptance of scholarship offer required within two business days.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered at the Principal's discretion. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12. If a scholarship student chooses to leave the School before the completion of Year 12, the value of the scholarships must be repaid in full. If a scholarship offer is made, acceptance and fees payable are required within two business days.

overview: Kilvington offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate academic achievement combined with active participation in co-curricular activities. The scholarship rewards well-rounded students entering Year 5, 7, 9 and 11 who contribute meaningfully to school life beyond the classroom.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who combine strong academic performance with active involvement in school activities and programs. Awards are granted based on performance in an independent academic examination, a formal interview, and evidence of active participation in co-curricular activities. The financial benefit typically ranges from 25% to 50% fee reduction, determined at the Principal's discretion. Scholarships are offered for the duration of enrolment until completion of Year 12. The selection process includes the ACER scholarship test appropriate to the year level, an interview, and assessment of the applicant's co-curricular engagement. Recipients must maintain full participation in school life through Year 12 and must repay scholarship value if they leave before completing Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Must demonstrate strong academic performance; Must provide evidence of active participation in range of activities outside school or within school co-curriculum; Successful applicants must fully participate in school life until end of Year 12; If scholarship holder leaves school before Year 12 completion, scholarship value must be repaid in full; Acceptance of scholarship offer required within two business days.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered at the Principal's discretion. Successful applicants must demonstrate evidence of active participation in a range of activities outside school. Selection assesses academic achievement combined with co-curricular engagement. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12. If a scholarship student chooses to leave the School before the completion of Year 12, the value of the scholarships must be repaid in full. If a scholarship offer is made, acceptance and fees payable are required within two business days.

overview: Kilvington offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support students with demonstrated musical excellence and commitment. The scholarship is available for entry at Year 5, 7, and 9, and rewards students who combine strong academic performance with exceptional musical ability.
description: The Music Scholarship at Kilvington recognises students who demonstrate outstanding musical ability and commitment to music study. Awards are determined based on an independent academic examination, a formal interview, and a musical audition. Financial benefits typically range from 25% to 50% fee reduction, determined at the Principal's discretion. Scholarships are offered for the duration of enrolment until completion of Year 12. Applicants must meet minimum AMEB levels: Grade 4 for Year 5 entry, Grade 5 for Year 7 entry, and Grade 6 for Year 9 entry. Recipients are required to participate in choir and a minimum of two ensembles through until Year 12. Kilvington prides itself on its music programs with dedicated facilities including the Patsy Venn Music Centre.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, or Year 9; Minimum AMEB Grade 4 level required for Year 5 entry; Minimum AMEB Grade 5 level required for Year 7 entry; Minimum AMEB Grade 6 level required for Year 9 entry; Must demonstrate strong academic performance; Recipients must participate in choir and minimum of two ensembles through until Year 12; Successful applicants must fully participate in school life until end of Year 12; If scholarship holder leaves school before Year 12 completion, scholarship value must be repaid in full; Acceptance of scholarship offer required within two business days.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered at the Principal's discretion. Applicants must meet specific AMEB music examination levels depending on year of entry. Recipients are required to participate in choir as well as a minimum of two ensembles through until Year 12. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12. If a scholarship student chooses to leave the School before the completion of Year 12, the value of the scholarships must be repaid in full. If a scholarship offer is made, acceptance and fees payable are required within two business days.

overview: Kilvington offers the Caroline Barrett Scholarship to honour the school's founder and recognise students who demonstrate exceptional character and commitment to the school's values. The scholarship rewards students who embody qualities of integrity, service, and leadership as exemplified by Caroline Barrett's vision for Kilvington.
description: The Caroline Barrett Scholarship, named after the school's founder, recognises students who demonstrate outstanding character traits aligned with Kilvington's values and motto 'Non Nobis Sed Omnibus - Not for our own, but for others' good'. Successful applicants receive a scholarship of up to 50% of tuition fees each year until completion of Year 12. The selection process includes submission of a letter demonstrating why the applicant would be a good fit for the Kilvington community, a character reference from someone other than a relative, review of most recent NAPLAN results and school reports, and documentation of awards, achievements and participation in programs demonstrating character. The scholarship is awarded at the Principal's discretion to applicants who demonstrate kindness, service to others, and a commitment to embodying the school's Christian and community values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students seeking entry to Kilvington; Must demonstrate outstanding character traits including kindness, integrity, and commitment to service; Must provide evidence of community engagement and positive impact; Must submit letter explaining fit for Kilvington community; Must provide character reference not written by relative; Successful applicants must fully participate in school life until end of Year 12; If scholarship holder leaves school before Year 12 completion, scholarship value must be repaid in full; Acceptance of scholarship offer required within two business days.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered at the Principal's discretion. Named after school founder Caroline Barrett who established Kilvington in 1923. Award recognises students who live out the school motto and demonstrate character traits such as kindness, integrity, service to others, and commitment to making positive difference. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12. If a scholarship student chooses to leave the School before the completion of Year 12, the value of the scholarships must be repaid in full.

overview: Kolbe Catholic College offers the Academic Scholarship to outstanding Year 7 students from priority parishes. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability and potential to contribute to the College community through academic excellence and co-curricular involvement.
description: The Academic Scholarship is offered annually to two Year 7 students whose families reside in one of the College's priority parishes (Our Lady's Parish Craigieburn/Roxburgh Park, Good Shepherd Parish Gladstone Park/Greenvale, and Holy Child Parish Meadow Heights/Dallas). The scholarship covers annual tuition fees for the duration of the student's enrolment. Applicants must be current Year 6 students and be enrolled at Kolbe for the following year. Selection is based on performance on a formal Scholarship Test and a subsequent interview, with candidates assessed on academic performance and ability to contribute to the College through liturgy, social justice, sport, creative arts or leadership. The scholarship is renewable for six years (Year 7 to 12) subject to maintaining academic diligence and upholding College values.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 student; Must reside in one of the designated priority parishes (Our Lady's Parish Craigieburn/Roxburgh Park, Good Shepherd Parish Gladstone Park/Greenvale, or Holy Child Parish Meadow Heights/Dallas); Must be enrolled at Kolbe Catholic College in order to be considered; Enrolment confirmation required prior to scholarship consideration
special_conditions: The Academic Scholarship would be in effect for the student's six years at Kolbe Catholic College. It is expected that successful recipients will uphold the values of the College and maintain a diligent approach to their academic endeavours. Scholarship selection is based on academic performance and the ability to contribute to the life of the College through liturgy, social justice, sport, creative arts or leadership. Applicants who are shortlisted after the Scholarship Test will be required to attend a formal interview.

overview: Kingswood College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding intellectual ability and academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance through a comprehensive examination and interview process. Selected students benefit from part tuition fee remission for the duration of their education at Kingswood.
description: Academic scholarships are awarded on the basis of the academic performance of the applicant in the scholarship examination, to be held on 14 February 2026. Applicants are also assessed on their submitted portfolio. Short-listed candidates will be interviewed by the Principal. These scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion and offer a part tuition fee remission for the duration of the student's education. Applicants must affirm their commitment to Kingswood College and remain part of the community for the longer term. The College does not provide the scholarship examination results to applicants or parents. Applications require evidence of Australian citizenship or permanent residency, copies of two most recent school reports, and a submitted portfolio.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 3–11 in 2027; Open to students entering Year 7 in 2028; Applicants must be a current Year 2 – 10 student (in 2026); Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Open to new and current Kingswood College students
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship examination to be held on 14 February 2026.
