By applying for this scholarship, students are affirming their commitment to Kingswood College and undertaking to remain part of our community for the longer term. The College does not provide the scholarship examination results to applicants/parents.

overview: Kingswood College offers the Choral Scholarship to students with significant experience singing in choirs. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate commitment to and enthusiasm for choral performance. Successful applicants will participate fully in the College's Choral Program and adopt leadership positions within the Kingswood choirs when requested.
description: Choral scholarships are designed for students who have significant experience singing in choirs, either within their current schools or externally. Students should possess experience of singing in high-level performance choirs and demonstrate commitment to and enthusiasm for choral performance, as well as vocal technical ability. Choral scholarships are awarded on the basis of an applicant's submitted portfolio, an audition and interview. Auditions will be held in February 2026 and applicants will be provided with their audition time prior to then. Applicants must be able to attend the audition as scheduled. These scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion and offer a part tuition fee remission for the duration of the student's secondary education. Successful applicants must participate fully in the College's Choral Program and adopt leadership positions within the Kingswood choirs when requested.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5–11 in 2027; Open to students entering Year 7 in 2028; Applicants must be a current Year 4 – 10 student (in 2026); Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Open to new and current Kingswood College students; Must have significant experience singing in high-level performance choirs
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Auditions will be held in February 2026. Applicants must be able to attend the audition as scheduled and provide audition portfolio. Successful applicants must participate fully in the College's Choral Program and adopt leadership positions within the Kingswood choirs when requested. Must perform their respective part of two choral works, accompanied by a recording of a full choir singing the chosen work.

overview: Kingswood College offers the Instrumental Scholarship to students demonstrating excellence in Brass, Percussion, Piano, Strings, Woodwind or Voice. The scholarship recognises high-level musicianship and commitment to instrumental performance. Successful applicants receive part tuition fee remission for the duration of their secondary education.
description: Instrumental scholarships are awarded on the basis of an applicant's audition and interview. Applicants are also assessed on their submitted portfolio. For students in Years 5 - 11 in 2027 and in Year 7 2028. These scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion and offer a part tuition fee remission for the duration of the student's secondary education. Students must be granted in Brass, Percussion, Piano, Strings, Woodwind or Voice. Applicants must affirm their commitment to Kingswood College and undertaking to remain part of our community for the longer term. Applications require evidence of Australian citizenship or permanent residency, copies of two most recent school reports, and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5–11 in 2027; Open to students entering Year 7 in 2028; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Demonstrated excellence in Brass, Percussion, Piano, Strings, Woodwind or Voice; Open to new and current Kingswood College students
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Awarded at the Principal's discretion. By applying for this scholarship, students are affirming their commitment to Kingswood College and undertaking to remain part of our community for the longer term.

overview: Kingswood College offers the Visual Arts Scholarship to students who demonstrate strong skills in the Visual Arts and a desire to lead and inspire others in this area. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional artistic ability and leadership potential. Successful applicants receive part tuition fee remission for the duration of their secondary education.
description: Visual Arts Scholarships are designed for students who have strong skills in the Visual Arts and a desire to lead and inspire others in this area. For students in Years 5 - 11 in 2027 and Year 7 2028. Applicants will be shortlisted on the basis of their folio interview. Shortlisted applicants will be interviewed by the Principal. These scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion and offer a part tuition fee remission for the duration of the student's secondary education. Applicants for Visual Arts Scholarships are required to attend an interview to present their portfolio. Applications require evidence of Australian citizenship or permanent residency, copies of two most recent school reports, and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5–11 in 2027; Open to students entering Year 7 in 2028; Applicants must be a current Year 6 – 10 student (in 2026); Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Demonstrated strong skills in Visual Arts; Must have desire to lead and inspire others in this area; Open to new and current Kingswood College students
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Applicants are required to attend an interview to present their portfolio. Awarded at the Principal's discretion. By applying for this scholarship, students are affirming their commitment to Kingswood College and undertaking to remain part of our community for the longer term.

overview: Kingswood College offers the Wyvern Young Women Scholarship to female students who demonstrate academic performance and excellence in one or more cocurricular areas such as art, music, performance or sport. The scholarship recognises well-rounded achievement and leadership potential. Successful applicants receive part tuition fee remission.
description: Wyvern Young Women Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic performance and demonstrated excellence in one or more cocurricular areas such as art, music, performance or sport. Shortlisted applicants will be interviewed by the Principal. Successful applicants must also have a positive attitude toward learning and school life and be of respectful, resilient, and motivated character. These scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion and offer a part tuition fee remission. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional talent and leadership potential across academic and cocurricular disciplines. Applicants must affirm their commitment to Kingswood College and remain part of the community for the longer term.
eligibility_criteria: For female students only; Demonstrated academic performance; Demonstrated excellence in one or more cocurricular areas (art, music, performance or sport); Positive attitude toward learning and school life; Respectful, resilient, and motivated character; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Open to new and current Kingswood College students
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants will be interviewed by the Principal. Successful applicants must have a positive attitude toward learning and school life and be of respectful, resilient, and motivated character. Awarded at the Principal's discretion. By applying for this scholarship, students are affirming their commitment to Kingswood College and undertaking to remain part of our community for the longer term.

overview: Koo Wee Rup Secondary College offers the High Ability Program (HAP), formerly known as SEAL, to provide enriched learning opportunities for academically gifted students. The program caters to students with above-average academic ability and a high level of commitment to their schoolwork, placing them in accelerated classes with like-minded peers.
description: The High Ability Program (HAP) focuses on maximising opportunities for academically talented students to excel by placing them in separate HAP classes where they are surrounded by like-minded peers and encouraged to continue making growth in their studies. The curriculum is delivered at a faster pace and more sophisticated level so that students can move ahead to work beyond their expected level. HAP students are placed into the class based on their academic achievement and are expected to maintain a level of achievement to remain in the program. At Year 7, HAP students receive separate instruction in English and Mathematics, with the program expanding in Year 8 and beyond. HAP students are included in annual programs designed to encourage their creativity and competitive nature, including guest speakers, excursions and enrolment in extension-based academic competitions. Progress and achievement is monitored regularly through College progress reports and semester reports, as well as teacher feedback. An Academic Excellence and Extension Learning Specialist exists to monitor and support HAP students and to liaise between students, their teachers and home.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Above-average academic ability required; High level of commitment to schoolwork; Year 5 NAPLAN results provided; Year 6 semester one report required; Written reference from Year 6 teacher required; Available through application process
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted by Thursday 15th August 2025. Students will be required to complete an application form online, which must include a written reference from their Year 6 teacher, Year 5 NAPLAN results, and their Year 6 semester one report. HAP students are expected to maintain a level of achievement to remain in the program. Progress is monitored regularly through College progress reports and semester reports, as well as teacher feedback. The program runs from Year 7 to Year 12, with expansion of subjects beyond English and Mathematics from Year 8 onwards.

overview: Kolbe Catholic College offers annual Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement and contribution to college life. The scholarships are offered to two Year 7 students from families who reside in priority parishes, recognising exceptional academic talent and holistic contribution to the school community.
description: The Kolbe Catholic College Academic Scholarship is offered annually to two Year 7 students of families who reside in one of the college's priority parishes. Academically talented students are selected on the basis of academic performance and their ability to contribute to the life of the College through liturgy, social justice, sport, creative arts or leadership. The scholarship is in effect for the student's entire six years at Kolbe Catholic College (Year 7 to Year 12). Successful recipients are expected to uphold the values of the College and maintain a diligent approach to their academic endeavours. The selection process involves sitting a scholarship examination and attending a formal interview. Recipients must demonstrate sustained academic performance and positive contribution to the college community throughout their tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must be enrolled at Kolbe Catholic College; Families must reside in one of the college's priority parishes; Academic performance evaluation required; Ability to contribute to college life through various activities (liturgy, social justice, sport, creative arts, leadership); Uphold college values; Maintain diligent academic approach
special_conditions: The Academic Scholarship is in effect for the student's six years at Kolbe Catholic College. Once enrolments for Year 7 2027 have been confirmed, an email inviting those students to apply for a Scholarship will be sent. Successful recipients must uphold the values of the College and maintain a diligent approach to their academic endeavours. Scholarship examinations are held at Kolbe Catholic College, 37-101 Lysterfield Drive, Greenvale. The examination date for Year 7 2027 is Saturday 30 May 2026.

overview: Koonung Secondary College offers the Select Entry Enhancement @ Koonung (SEE@K) program designed to accelerate highly able students in Mathematics and English throughout Years 7 to 9. The program enriches learning across all subjects including Science, Humanities, Health, PE, Arts, Performing Arts, and Technologies through differentiated teaching and a focus on critical thinking, problem-solving, and inquiry-based learning.
description: SEE@K is an academic enrichment program (not acceleration) designed for highly able students entering Years 7 and continuing through Year 9. The program provides differentiated courses in all core subjects reflecting self-directed inquiry learning, with enhanced opportunities in Mathematics for selected Year 7 and 8 students. Students in SEE@K progress alongside their age peers and participate in enrichment activities including open-ended projects, real-world applications, and thought-provoking tasks across multiple disciplines. The program encourages critical and creative thinking while developing problem-solving skills and fostering a global, community-focused outlook. Selection is based on general aptitude testing administered by EduTest. The program emphasises developing future leaders who are confident, open to learning, and committed to making meaningful impact. At Year 9, identified students may be offered accelerated learning pathways. Places in the SEE@K program are limited and competitive.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must first apply for and secure enrolment at Koonung Secondary College; Must pass general aptitude testing (EduTest); Ideal for students who ask insightful questions; Students who demonstrate strong work ethic and responsibility for own learning; Students who show persistence and resilience with challenges; Students who employ reasoning and mathematical skills to solve complex problems
special_conditions: Places in the SEE@K program are limited. Students must first apply for enrolment at Koonung Secondary College through standard Department of Education processes. Selection for the SEE@K program involves general aptitude testing (EduTest). The EduTest for entry into the 2026 Year 7 program will be held on Saturday 13 September 2025. SEE@K is not an accelerated learning program in Years 7-9, rather a differentiated course reflecting self-directed inquiry learning. SEE@K students progress alongside their age peers, allowing them to fully participate in valuable developmental programs such as Live Life. At Year 9, identified students may be offered an accelerated learning pathway.

school_name: Kurnai College - KYPP Campus (Kurnai Young Parents Program Campus)
program_name: Kurnai Young Parents Program (KYPP) - Free Tuition and Childcare
overview: Kurnai College offers the Kurnai Young Parents Program (KYPP) at its Morwell Flexible Learning Option campus, providing free tuition and free childcare to eligible young parents. The program is designed to support young parents under age 21 to complete their secondary schooling while balancing parenting responsibilities.
description: KYPP is a free program providing tuition and childcare support for young parents aged 14-21 to complete their Victorian Pathways Certificate (VPC) alongside VET modules and other qualifications. The program is completely free for eligible students, including free childcare (conditions apply for free childcare eligibility). Students receive a safe, supportive and flexible learning environment while completing their schooling. The program includes Certificate II in Hospitality components (food handling, barista training), first aid certificates, RSA, and other qualifications. KYPP provides numeracy, literacy, personal development and life skills training, and connects students with various services and community support systems.
eligibility_criteria: Young people under age 21 with a child or children; able to attend flexible learning program; conditions apply for free childcare qualification
special_conditions: Schooling is free. Childcare is free (conditions apply to qualify for free childcare). Please refer to the KYPP Enrolment Policy for the program enrolment process. Program delivers a flexible learning environment with ongoing personal and childcare support. Enrollment into these programs is subject to applicant qualifications and program availability.

school_name: Kurnai College - KYPP Campus (Kurnai Young Parents Program Campus)
overview: Kurnai College has formed a partnership with the Smith Family through its Learning for Life Scholarship program to support students to remain at school. This program provides financial and educational support to students at risk.
description: Through its partnership with the Smith Family, Kurnai College offers the Learning for Life Scholarship program designed to support vulnerable students and help them remain at school and continue their education. The program recognizes that many students face barriers to completing secondary education. While specific financial values are not publicly detailed, the program provides targeted support through the Smith Family's established learning support services. The program is part of Kurnai College's commitment to creating an inclusive learning community where every student has access to opportunities to succeed, regardless of their background or circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Students identified as at-risk; enrolled at Kurnai College; meeting criteria as determined by the Smith Family partnership
special_conditions: Partnership with the Smith Family. Designed to support students to remain at school. Support is provided through the Smith Family's learning support services framework.

overview: Kurnai College has partnered with the Smith Family through its Learning for Life Scholarship program to support students to remain at school. The program provides financial assistance to students experiencing hardship who are at risk of disengaging from education.
description: Kurnai College works in partnership with the Smith Family to provide Learning for Life Scholarships to eligible students. This program targets students from disadvantaged backgrounds who require financial support to continue their education and remain engaged at school. The scholarship provides financial assistance to eligible families. Students are identified and supported through the school's inclusive learning community framework. The exact financial benefit varies depending on individual circumstances and assessment by the Smith Family. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and risk of educational disengagement.
eligibility_criteria: Students experiencing financial hardship; At risk of disengaging from school; Identified through school assessment process; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Partnership with the Smith Family. Students must demonstrate financial need and commitment to remaining engaged at school.

overview: Kurnai College offers the Advanced Learning Program to students in Years 7-10 at both Churchill and Morwell campuses. The program caters for students with a strong work ethic and capability to achieve excellent academic results.
description: The Advanced Learning Program is designed to extend students' depth and breadth of thinking and learning in core subjects including Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities. Students in the ALP develop an excellent work ethic while being challenged with new ideas and practices. Teachers in ALP classes focus on promoting independent thinking and developing comprehensive understanding of concepts explored in class. The program does not seek to accelerate students through to tertiary education but rather to challenge high-achieving students. Core subjects are taught by committed teachers who develop strong relationships with students to maximize learning potential. Students also participate in mixed-ability classes for other subjects including Health & PE, Technology, Art and Languages, allowing friendships with mainstream students.
eligibility_criteria: Strong work ethic; Capable of achieving excellent results; Demonstrated academic excellence; Students from feeder primary schools automatically tested; Non-feeder school students can apply for testing; Year 6 students entering Year 7
special_conditions: Testing conducted for Grade 6 students from feeder schools at their primary schools. Non-feeder students must book testing at their relevant campus. Bookings essential. Students remain in mainstream school for non-core subjects to maintain peer relationships.

program_name: Smith Family Learning for Life Partnership
overview: Kurnai College works hard to connect with its local community and has formed a partnership with the Smith Family through its Learning for Life Scholarship program to support students to remain at school. The program provides financial and educational support to disadvantaged students and their families.
description: The Smith Family Learning for Life program provides comprehensive support to eligible students from disadvantaged backgrounds. This partnership assists with educational expenses including school fees (where applicable), uniforms, textbooks, school camps, and excursions. The program also offers tutoring support and connects students with mentors. Students receive individualised case management from a Learning for Life worker who works closely with the student, family, and school to support educational engagement and achievement. The program operates on a needs assessment basis and provides support for the duration of the student's schooling at Kurnai College.
eligibility_criteria: Students from disadvantaged backgrounds; demonstrated financial hardship; school referral or family application; commitment to engaging in education
special_conditions: Recipients must demonstrate commitment to education and engagement with the program. Regular review of support allocation. Partnership with Smith Family for case management and support coordination.

overview: The Aunty Dot Peters Award supports Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students to complete Years 9 and 10 at a Victorian secondary school. The inaugural award was presented by the Minister for Aboriginal Affairs in recognition of supporting Indigenous student completion and success.
description: The Aunty Dot Peters Award is a $5,000 scholarship for Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students completing Years 9 and 10 at a Victorian secondary school. The scholarship is provided as $2,500 per year over the two years. Students can use the funds to pay for schooling-related expenses such as tutoring, study supplies, IT and home office equipment, uniforms, excursions and extra-curricular activities. Four scholarships are awarded annually. The award honours the work of Aunty Dorothy Peters AM, a much-loved community member, educator and former Chairperson of the Victorian Aboriginal Remembrance Committee. Students are nominated by their school for exemplary attendance, dedication to schooling, positive attitude towards learning, and contribution to their school community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; enrolled in Year 9 or Year 10 at Victorian secondary school; exemplary attendance; dedication to education; positive attitude to learning; teacher/school nomination
special_conditions: Scholarship provided over two years ($2,500 per year) for Years 9 and 10 only. Students nominated by their school based on demonstrated attendance, dedication, and contribution to school community. Subject to annual review. Victorian Indigenous students at participating secondary schools.

program_name: Rotary Club of Morwell Educational Scholarship
overview: Kurnai College awards the Rotary Club of Morwell Educational Scholarship to recognise academic achievement and educational excellence. This award is presented at the annual College Awards Ceremony to a deserving student demonstrating outstanding academic performance.
description: The Rotary Club of Morwell Educational Scholarship is presented through Kurnai College's annual College Awards Ceremony. This award recognises students demonstrating excellence in their academic pursuits and contributions to the college community. The scholarship is awarded to a student enrolled at the Latrobe Valley Flexible Learning Option (LV FLO) Campus or the Kurnai Young Parents Program (KYPP). Recipients are selected based on their academic achievement, commitment to learning, and positive contribution to their educational pathway. The award provides recognition and support for students engaged in alternative education programs.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at LV FLO Campus or KYPP; demonstrated academic achievement; positive contribution to school community; commitment to education pathway; selected through annual awards process
special_conditions: Presented at annual College Awards Ceremony held in December. Award specifically targets students in alternative education programs (LV FLO and KYPP). Non-monetary award recognising achievement. Recipients announced publicly at formal ceremony.

overview: Kurnai College awards the Bill Walker Memorial Award to recognise students demonstrating all-rounder achievements and making significant contributions to the college community. The award is presented annually at the College Awards Ceremony.
description: The Bill Walker Memorial Award is presented annually to a student at Kurnai College's University Campus demonstrating exceptional all-rounder qualities including academic achievement, character, leadership, and community contribution. The award recognises students who balance academic success with active participation in co-curricular activities and positive community engagement. Recipients are selected through a holistic review of student achievement across multiple domains including academic performance, sporting or cultural participation, leadership roles, and positive impact on school community. The award is presented at the annual December awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: University Campus student; demonstrated academic achievement; active co-curricular participation; positive character and conduct; community or school contribution; all-rounder excellence
special_conditions: Presented at annual College Awards Ceremony held in December. Award specific to University Campus (Years 11-12). Non-monetary award recognising all-rounder achievement. Recipients announced publicly at formal ceremony.

overview: Kurnai College presents the Darren Chester Civics & Citizenship Award to recognise students demonstrating outstanding commitment to citizenship, civic responsibility, and community engagement. The award celebrates students who exemplify values of active citizenship.
description: The Darren Chester Civics & Citizenship Award is presented annually to a student at Kurnai College's University Campus who demonstrates exceptional commitment to civics education, citizenship values, and community engagement. The award recognises students who actively participate in promoting democratic values, community service, and social responsibility. Recipients show leadership in supporting others, engagement with community issues, and a commitment to positive social contribution. Students may demonstrate achievement through school leadership roles, community service involvement, or active participation in civics and citizenship programs. The award is presented at the annual College Awards Ceremony held in December.
eligibility_criteria: University Campus student; demonstrated commitment to citizenship values; community service involvement or civic participation; leadership in community engagement; positive character and conduct; active school or community contribution
special_conditions: Presented at annual College Awards Ceremony held in December. Award specific to University Campus (Years 11-12). Non-monetary award recognising civic contribution and citizenship values. Recipients announced publicly at formal ceremony.

overview: Korowa offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability, intellectual curiosity and a commitment to learning. This scholarship is available to new and existing students entering Years 5 to 11 and reflects the school's belief in nurturing excellence.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students displaying outstanding academic ability and intellectual curiosity with a strong commitment to learning. The scholarship covers a fee remission on tuition fees only, ranging from 20% to 50% as determined by the Principal at the time of offer. The selection process involves the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test, submission of student reports and NAPLAN results in an online portfolio, and an interview with the Principal if required. The scholarship is subject to ongoing conditions and renewal, with scholarship holders expected to demonstrate strong commitment to Korowa with funds to be repaid to the school if the student leaves before the end of Year 12, unless the Principal approves otherwise.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students entering Years 5 to 11; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test; Must have attended a school tour, open day or Try Us for a Day program; Student reports and most recent NAPLAN results must be submitted
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Fee remission applies to tuition fees only; Holding fee of $2,500 per scholarship place is required upon acceptance (credited against Term 2 tuition fees); School application fee ($150) and enrolment fee ($1,400) also required; Acceptance must occur within two business days of offer; Korowa does not offer 100% scholarships; Scholarships are not negotiable; Recipients must demonstrate strong commitment to Korowa; All scholarship funds must be repaid if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves otherwise; Subject to ongoing conditions and annual review.

overview: Korowa offers the Music Scholarship to talented musicians who demonstrate passion, discipline and potential for growth. This scholarship recognises and supports students entering Years 7 to 11 who show strong musical ability across instrumental and vocal performance.
description: The Music Scholarship supports talented musicians demonstrating passion, discipline and potential for growth. The scholarship covers either music tuition fees for an instrument taught at the school or is combined with academic or general excellence scholarships. Applicants must be studying at least one instrument at minimum AMEB Grade 3 standard (or equivalent) for most instruments, with special preference for viola, double bass, oboe and lower brass players. Voice is highly valued with proof of strong choral training required. The selection process includes audition with two pieces differing in style and tempo for first instrument, aural testing, interview, submission of music examination reports and teacher reports. Applicants must provide evidence of involvement in performing arts. Fee remission ranges from 20% to 50% of tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 in 2026; Must be studying at least one instrument at minimum AMEB Grade 3 standard (or equivalent) for most instruments; Special preference for viola, double bass, oboe and lower brass; Voice scholarships available with proof of strong choral training; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test; Must have attended a school tour, open day or Try Us for a Day program
special_conditions: Scholarships awarded as music tuition fees for school-taught instruments or in combination with academic or general excellence scholarships; Holding fee of $2,500 per scholarship place required upon acceptance; School application fee ($150) and enrolment fee ($1,400) required; Acceptance within two business days of offer; Applicants must provide music teacher contact details; Accompanist arrangements must be made by applicant; Korowa does not offer 100% scholarships; All scholarship funds must be repaid if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves otherwise.

overview: Korowa offers the General Excellence Scholarship to celebrate well-rounded students who combine academic achievement, leadership and community engagement. This scholarship recognises students entering Years 5 to 11 who demonstrate exceptional contribution to school life beyond academics.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to girls who will make a significant contribution to the life of Korowa through participation in sport, performing arts, community engagement or similar activities. The scholarship recognises well-rounded students combining achievement, leadership and community engagement. The selection process includes the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test, submission of an online portfolio with student reports and a handwritten letter to the Principal outlining why the student wishes to attend Korowa, and an interview with the Principal if required. Shortlisted candidates are required to attend an activity morning at Korowa and undertake further testing. Fee remission ranges from 20% to 50% of tuition fees as determined by the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students entering Years 5 to 11; Must make significant contribution to school life through sport, performing arts, community engagement or similar; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test; Must have attended a school tour, open day or Try Us for a Day program; Student reports must be submitted with online portfolio; Handwritten letter to Principal required as part of portfolio
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates required to attend activity morning at Korowa and undertake further testing; Holding fee of $2,500 per scholarship place required upon acceptance; School application fee ($150) and enrolment fee ($1,400) required; Acceptance within two business days of offer; Korowa does not offer 100% scholarships; All scholarship funds must be repaid if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves otherwise; Subject to ongoing conditions.

overview: Korowa offers Sport Scholarships recognising athletic excellence, teamwork and perseverance. This scholarship supports talented athletes entering specified year levels who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and commitment to their chosen sport.
description: The Sport Scholarship recognises athletic excellence, teamwork and perseverance in talented athletes. The scholarship is available for entry into key year levels (typically Year 7 onwards) and covers fee remission on tuition fees ranging from 20% to 50% as determined by the Principal at time of offer. The selection process includes the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test, submission of student reports and evidence of sporting achievement in an online portfolio, and an interview with the Principal if required. Athletes must demonstrate high-level sporting ability and commitment. Fee remission applies to tuition fees only, with all other school costs charged as per school business regulations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and existing students entering key year levels; Must demonstrate outstanding athletic ability and commitment to sport; Must demonstrate teamwork and perseverance; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) scholarship test; Must have attended a school tour, open day or Try Us for a Day program; Evidence of sporting achievement required in portfolio
special_conditions: Holding fee of $2,500 per scholarship place required upon acceptance (credited against Term 2 tuition fees); School application fee ($150) and enrolment fee ($1,400) required; Acceptance within two business days of offer; Korowa does not offer 100% scholarships; All scholarship funds must be repaid if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves otherwise; Subject to ongoing conditions and annual review.

overview: The Jean Barnfather Memorial Scholarship is a means-tested bursary funded by donors to the 2025 Giving Day that covers 100 percent of school fees for an exceptional student who, due to external circumstances, would otherwise not be able to attend Korowa. This scholarship honours the extraordinary contribution of Jean Barnfather to the school.
description: The Jean Barnfather Memorial Scholarship is a means-tested scholarship covering 100 percent of school fees for an exceptional student who would otherwise be unable to afford a Korowa education. The student must combine academic achievement, leadership and community engagement. Established in 2022 and first awarded in 2023, this scholarship was created to honour Jean Barnfather's more than 40 years of service to the school. It is funded by the generosity of donors to the 2025 Giving Day and provides full fee remission covering all tuition fees. Recipients are expected to support the school's values of integrity, curiosity and service. The scholarship is subject to ongoing conditions and review, with funds to be repaid if the student leaves before the end of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Exceptional students experiencing financial hardship or external circumstances preventing school attendance; Must demonstrate academic achievement, leadership and community engagement; Must be able to benefit fully from a Korowa education; Subject to means-testing and Principal approval; Must support school values of integrity, curiosity and service
special_conditions: Means-tested bursary; Funded by donor contributions to Annual Giving and Giving Day; Covers 100% of school fees; Recipients must support Korowa's Christian Anglican ethos and values of integrity, curiosity and service; All funds must be repaid if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves; Subject to annual review and ongoing conditions.

overview: Korowa is committed to supporting access to education for students experiencing financial hardship. The school offers financial assistance and bursary programs beyond formal scholarships to help families who are facing financial difficulties.
description: Korowa offers structured financial assistance and bursary support for families experiencing financial hardship or facing financial difficulties that might otherwise prevent their daughter from attending the school. The school is committed to supporting access to education where possible and encourages families to contact the school in confidence to discuss available options and support pathways. Financial assistance may include payment plan arrangements, bursary support, and other tailored solutions. The school works with families to develop individual support packages. Korowa also offers structured payment options to help families with planning and budgeting of school fees, which are invoiced quarterly one term in advance.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families facing financial difficulties preventing school attendance; Open to all families regardless of background; Contact school directly to discuss individual circumstances; Confidential discussions available with enrolments team
special_conditions: Must contact school directly in confidence; Individual circumstances reviewed; Flexible payment arrangements available; Fees invoiced quarterly one term in advance; Structured payment options available; School reserves right to determine support based on individual need and circumstances.

program_name: Learning for Life Scholarship Program (Smith Family Partnership)
overview: Kurnai College partners with the Smith Family to offer the Learning for Life Scholarship program. This financial assistance initiative supports disadvantaged students to remain at school by providing direct support with educational expenses and family financial hardship.
description: The Learning for Life Scholarship Program is delivered through a partnership between Kurnai College and the Smith Family, a national not-for-profit organisation. The program supports students experiencing financial hardship to access schooling-related expenses including school fees, uniforms, camp costs, books, and educational supplies. The program is available to eligible students identified by the school as requiring financial assistance. Beyond direct financial support, the Learning for Life program connects families with a local team member who provides mentoring and advocacy. The program continues to support students through their post-school transitions, including assistance with tertiary education costs and vocational training pathways. Recipients benefit from ongoing support that extends beyond Year 12 completion.
eligibility_criteria: Students experiencing financial hardship; Identified by school as requiring assistance; Demonstrated commitment to schooling; Families assessed by Smith Family on financial need basis
special_conditions: Partnership program delivered by Smith Family. School identifies eligible students. Support extends to post-Year 12 pathways including university and vocational training assistance.

overview: The inaugural Aunty Dot Peters Award supports Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander students to complete Years 9 and 10 at a Victorian secondary school. This award recognises students demonstrating exemplary attendance and dedication to their schooling.
description: The Aunty Dot Peters Award is a $5,000 scholarship established by the Victorian government to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in their Year 9 and 10 studies at Victorian secondary schools. The scholarship can be used to pay for schooling-related expenses including tutoring, study supplies, IT and home office equipment, uniforms, excursions and extra-curricular activities distributed over two years. Award recipients are nominated by their school based on exemplary attendance records, dedication to schooling, and demonstrated attitude towards learning. The award honours Aunty Dorothy (Dot) Peters AM, a respected community member, educator and former Chairperson of the Victorian Aboriginal Remembrance Committee who passed away in 2019. Kurnai College has successfully supported recipients including Churchill Campus student Travis Colson who was presented with the award at Parliament House.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Enrolled at a Victorian secondary school; Completing Year 9 or 10; Nominated by school based on exemplary attendance; Demonstrated dedication to schooling; Positive attitude towards learning
special_conditions: School nomination required. Award distributed over two years of study. Recipients recognised at Parliament House awards ceremony. Award honours Aunty Dot Peters AM and celebrates Indigenous student achievement.

overview: Kurnai College offers the Advanced Learning Program to identify and extend students who have demonstrated strong academic ability and work ethic. The program aims to extend students' depth and breadth of thinking and learning in core subjects across Years 7-10.
description: The Advanced Learning Program (ALP) is designed to cater for students who have both a strong work ethic and are capable of achieving excellent results. ALP classes take place at both Morwell and Churchill campuses running from Years 7-10. ALP classes focus on extending students' depth and breadth of thinking and learning in Maths, English, Science and the Humanities by developing an excellent work ethic and challenging them with new ideas and practices. Teachers of ALP classes strive to instil in students confidence in their abilities, a strong desire to learn and to achieve high results. The program does not seek to accelerate students through to tertiary education. Selection is based on testing completed with all Grade 6 students from feeder schools, with additional testing sessions available for non-feeder school students. Parents receive five weekly reports and parent-teacher meetings throughout the program.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students from feeder schools; Demonstrated strong work ethic and academic ability; Capable of achieving excellent results; Open to both Morwell and Churchill feeder schools; Students from non-feeder schools may apply but must attend additional testing sessions
special_conditions: Students are closely monitored in the program and parents receive timely feedback on their child's progress through five weekly reports and parent-teacher meetings. For subjects such as Health & PE, Technology, Art and LOTE, students are in mixed classes with other year level students allowing them to form friendships with mainstream students.

overview: Kurnai College has partnered with The Smith Family to offer the Learning for Life Scholarship program. This program supports students to remain at school through financial assistance.
description: Kurnai College works hard to connect with its local community and has formed a partnership with the Smith Family through its Learning for Life Scholarship program to support students to remain at school. The Learning for Life Scholarship is designed to provide financial support to students experiencing hardship. Students eligible to apply must be experiencing genuine financial need or hardship. The program aims to ensure that financial barriers do not prevent students from accessing quality education at Kurnai College. Application requires demonstrating genuine financial need and commitment to remaining at school.
eligibility_criteria: Students experiencing financial hardship; Families holding a valid means-tested concession card; Temporary foster parents; Special consideration category available
special_conditions: Families holding a valid means-tested concession card or temporary foster parents are eligible to apply. A Special Consideration category also exists for students with extraordinary circumstances.

program_name: Rotary Club of Morwell Educational Scholarship
overview: The Rotary Club of Morwell provides educational scholarships to recognize academic merit and achievement. Recipients are selected from students demonstrating outstanding academic performance.
description: The Rotary Club of Morwell Educational Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and merit. This scholarship recognizes students who have excelled in their studies and contributed positively to the school community. Recipients are announced at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. The scholarship provides recognition and support for continued academic excellence. Specific details regarding financial value and selection process are managed by Rotary Club representatives in partnership with Kurnai College.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated exceptional academic achievement; Positive contribution to school community; Selected by Rotary Club of Morwell in partnership with Kurnai College; All year levels considered
special_conditions: Award recipients are announced at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. Selection is made by Rotary Club of Morwell representatives.

overview: The Bill Walker Memorial Award recognizes outstanding contribution and leadership at Kurnai College. The award honors the legacy of Bill Walker and celebrates students who exemplify community values.
description: The Bill Walker Memorial Award is presented to students who have demonstrated outstanding leadership, character, and contribution to the Kurnai College community. This award recognizes students who have made a significant positive impact on their peers and the broader school community. Recipients are celebrated at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. The award is named in memory of Bill Walker and represents his values of service, integrity, and community engagement. Selection criteria emphasize leadership qualities, character development, and active contribution to school culture.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated outstanding leadership qualities; Significant positive contribution to school community; Exemplary character and integrity; All year levels considered; University Campus preferred based on previous awards
special_conditions: Award recipients are announced at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. The award honors the legacy of Bill Walker and celebrates community leadership values.

overview: The Darren Chester Civics & Citizenship Award recognizes students who demonstrate outstanding commitment to civic responsibility and community citizenship. This award celebrates active engagement in democratic participation and community service.
description: The Darren Chester Civics & Citizenship Award is presented to students who have demonstrated outstanding commitment to civic responsibility and active citizenship in the school and broader community. This award recognizes students who engage meaningfully in community service, demonstrate understanding of democratic values, and contribute positively to society. Recipients are celebrated at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. The award is named after Darren Chester and represents values of civic engagement, community responsibility, and active participation in democratic processes. Selection criteria emphasize demonstrated understanding of citizenship, community service, and positive social contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated outstanding civic responsibility and citizenship; Active engagement in community service; Understanding of democratic values; All year levels considered; University Campus preferred based on previous awards
special_conditions: Award recipients are announced at the annual Kurnai College Awards Ceremony. The award honors civic engagement and community citizenship values.

school_name: Kyneton High School-Macedon Ranges Satellite VCAL
overview: Kyneton High School offers a Languages Scholarship sponsored by a School Council member, available to Year 10 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence in language studies and commitment to linguistic development.
description: This significant scholarship has been sponsored and will be available to Year 10 students for the next 25 years, providing ongoing support for language learners. The sponsorship demonstrates a strong commitment to advancing language education at the school. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and commitment to language learning. Students selected will benefit from this long-term funding commitment that extends across multiple student cohorts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Students must demonstrate excellence in language studies; Selected by school assessment of academic merit in language subjects
special_conditions: Scholarship available for 25 years from date of establishment. Sponsored by School Council member as demonstration of commitment to public education.

school_name: Kyneton High School-Macedon Ranges Satellite VCAL
overview: Kyneton High School offers a Performing Arts Scholarship sponsored by a School Council member, available to Year 10 students. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment to performing arts disciplines.
description: This significant scholarship has been sponsored and will be available to Year 10 students for the next 25 years, providing sustained support for performing arts development. The sponsorship reflects a strong commitment to advancing arts education at the school. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and dedication to performing arts. Students selected will benefit from this long-term funding commitment that supports multiple cohorts of performing artists.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Students must demonstrate excellence in performing arts; Selected by school assessment of artistic merit and talent
special_conditions: Scholarship available for 25 years from date of establishment. Sponsored by School Council member as demonstration of commitment to public education. Selection likely involves performance audition.

school_name: Kyneton High School-Macedon Ranges Satellite VCAL
overview: Kyneton High School offers financial hardship arrangements to support families who cannot pay for school items, activities, or services. The program ensures that all students can participate fully in school life regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Kyneton High School understands that some families may experience financial difficulty and provides a comprehensive range of support options. The school has put in place financial hardship arrangements to ensure that students do not miss out on educational activities and services. A nominated parent payment contact person is available for confidential discussions regarding financial hardship arrangements and alternative payment options. The school recognises the importance of supporting all families and works to facilitate student participation in the full curriculum and co-curricular program. State Schools Relief (SSR) assistance is also available for uniforms, and families can contact the school office to enquire about eligibility. Payment plans can be arranged and multiple payment methods are accepted.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Families experiencing financial difficulty; Demonstrated need for support with school payments
special_conditions: Confidential discussions available with nominated parent payment contact person. Support tailored to individual family circumstances. All contributions are voluntary and schools provide free instruction to fulfil standard curriculum requirements.

overview: Kyneton High School offers the Roberta DuBarry Languages Scholarship to Year 10 students who demonstrate excellence in language studies. This scholarship recognises students who show commitment to language learning and represents a 25-year commitment by the benefactor to support language education at the school.
description: The DuBarry Languages Scholarship is valued at $1,000 and is awarded annually to Year 10 students at Kyneton High School. The scholarship was established by Roberta DuBarry, a former French language teacher at the school who is a strong advocate for public education. The scholarship will be available to students in Year 10 for 25 years, recognising students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to language studies. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and commitment to language learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Demonstrated excellence in language studies; Commitment to language learning
special_conditions: The scholarship is offered on a 25-year basis. Benefactor Roberta DuBarry is a past staff member who taught French language at Kyneton High School.

overview: Kyneton High School offers the Roberta DuBarry Performing Arts Scholarship to Year 10 students who demonstrate excellence in performing arts. This scholarship recognises students who show exceptional talent and commitment to music, drama, or dance and represents a 25-year commitment by the benefactor.
description: The DuBarry Performing Arts Scholarship is valued at $1,000 and is awarded annually to Year 10 students at Kyneton High School. The scholarship was established by Roberta DuBarry, a former staff member at the school who is a strong advocate for public education. The scholarship will be available to students in Year 10 for 25 years, supporting students who demonstrate exceptional talent in performing arts including music, drama, and dance. Selection is based on demonstrated achievement and commitment to performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Demonstrated excellence in performing arts (music, drama, or dance); Commitment to performing arts
special_conditions: The scholarship is offered on a 25-year basis. Benefactor Roberta DuBarry is a past staff member and former French language teacher at Kyneton High School who is a strong advocate for public education.

overview: Kyneton High School provides music scholarships to students who demonstrate commitment to furthering their studies in music. The scholarships support students in pursuing instrumental or vocal music education through the purchase of instruments or access to lessons.
description: Kyneton High School offers music scholarships to support students' musical development and advancement. These scholarships have been awarded to students such as Lana Hughes and Rhiannon Winchester, enabling them to purchase instruments (such as a trumpet) or access a year's worth of lessons with qualified music instructors. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment to music performance and study. The school promotes music as a vital component of student development, noting that music benefits brain functioning, builds confidence, improves memory, and teaches discipline.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated commitment to music studies; Exceptional talent in instrumental or vocal music; Current enrolment at Kyneton High School
special_conditions: Scholarships support the purchase of musical instruments or access to professional music lessons. Selection is based on demonstrated talent and commitment to music education.

overview: Lalor Secondary College offers the High Potential Learning Program to students identified with high learning potential in Years 7 to 10. The program provides access to advanced learning and extended curriculum in English, Humanities, and Science, with ability-appropriate Mathematics placement.
description: The High Potential Learning Program was introduced at Lalor Secondary College in 2012 to support students identified with high learning potential. Students beginning Year 7 are tested during Term Four of their Grade 6 year on Literacy and Numeracy skills and through an Abstract Reasoning Test designed to identify academic potential regardless of educational background. Students selected for the program undertake an extended curriculum in English, Humanities, and Science, and are placed in ability-appropriate Mathematics classes. The curriculum allows for extension on a class and individual level, with content structured around the theme of 'Our World'. The program is designed to complement the cultural diversity of the school community. This selection approach recognises that academic potential exists across all student backgrounds and demographics.
eligibility_criteria: Beginning Year 7 students; Testing during Term Four of Grade 6 year; Assessed on Literacy and Numeracy skills; Assessed through Abstract Reasoning Test; Years 7 to 10 participation
special_conditions: Testing is conducted during Term Four of Grade 6 year. The Abstract Reasoning Test was specifically chosen to complement the cultural diversity of the school. Curriculum is structured to link English, Humanities and Science to the common theme of 'Our World'.

overview: Lalor North Secondary College offers academic excellence scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in incoming students. The scholarship is designed to support high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Lalor North Secondary College offers academic scholarships to support students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement. According to the Principal's message, the college offers very substantial scholarships in every area of achievement designed to maximise academic, social and emotional growth and opportunity for young people. Applicants are typically assessed on demonstrated academic excellence. The specific financial benefit, duration, and selection process details are not publicly available on the school website and require direct contact with the school for complete information. Interested families are encouraged to contact the school directly.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Academic performance in top academic range; Australian citizen or permanent resident status preferred for government school scholarships
special_conditions: Specific scholarship terms and conditions require direct contact with the school for detailed information.

overview: Lalor North Secondary College offers scholarships for students with exceptional musical talent and achievement. The scholarship supports students entering Year 7 who demonstrate commitment to music and musical performance.
description: Lalor North Secondary College has specifically mentioned new musical futures scholarships from Year 7 in 2023. The college has been a Champion School for the Musical Futures model since 2010 and offers extensive music programs including VET Music and Musical Futures training. The exact financial benefit, selection criteria through audition or assessment, duration of the scholarship, and specific renewal conditions are not detailed on publicly available sources. Students interested in this scholarship should contact the school directly for comprehensive information about application procedures, audition requirements, and financial support offered.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated musical ability and commitment to music performance; Participation in school music programs expected
special_conditions: Selection based on musical ability demonstrated through audition or portfolio. Exact requirements require direct contact with the school.

overview: Lalor North Secondary College offers scholarships for students demonstrating excellence in the visual and performing arts. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional achievement and commitment in arts programs including drama, dance, and visual arts.
description: Lalor North Secondary College has announced new arts scholarships from Year 7. The college provides well-equipped music and performing arts facilities including purpose-built drama and dance facilities, and a well-stocked visual art program. The college is committed to providing challenging opportunities for students with talents in the Arts. However, specific details regarding the financial value of scholarships, selection procedures (portfolio, interview, or audition), duration, and renewal conditions are not publicly available. Families interested in arts scholarships should contact the school directly to obtain complete scholarship information and application requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated excellence and commitment in visual or performing arts; May include drama, dance, visual arts, or other artistic disciplines
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated arts achievement through portfolio, interview, or audition. Exact selection criteria require direct contact with the school.

overview: Lakeside College offers financial assistance to families whose child would greatly benefit from a Lakeside College education but who, without financial support, would be unable to attend. The College demonstrates a strong commitment to supporting families on the basis of financial need rather than academic or performance merit.
description: Lakeside College does not offer student performance-based scholarships. Instead, the College maintains a comprehensive financial assistance program designed to support eligible families. Financial assistance is provided on a needs-based assessment. The College works with families to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent capable students from accessing a Lakeside College education. Applicants should contact the College directly for detailed information regarding eligibility criteria, application procedures, required documentation, and the assessment process used to determine financial need and assistance levels.
eligibility_criteria: Financial need demonstrated through formal assessment; Prospective and current students; All year levels considered
special_conditions: Families must demonstrate genuine financial need. The College reserves the right to conduct a thorough financial assessment of family circumstances. Contact the school directly for complete information regarding the financial assistance application process and to discuss individual circumstances.

school_name: Lavers Hill K-12 College - Barongarook Primary School
Lavers Hill K-12 College - Greater Otway Applied Learning School

program_name: Born to Soar - Gifted and Talented Program
overview: Le Page Primary School hosts the Born to Soar Centre for Gifted and Talented Education, which attracts highly able and gifted students from across the South Eastern suburbs. The program is open to students attending Le Page Primary School and students from surrounding schools. The program is designed to develop creative, innovative, and original thinking in highly able learners.
description: The Born to Soar program operates enrichment classes for gifted and talented students at Le Page Primary School. Entercelerate is offered for Prep to Year 2 students at $895 per student per term, consisting of one half day per week for eight weeks beginning in Term Two 2025. Entersoar is offered for Year 3 to Year 6 students at $1,245 per student per term, consisting of one day per week for eight weeks beginning in Term Two 2025. The program is aligned to the Australian Curriculum and provides a Centre for Gifted and Talented Education with enrichment classes. Enrolment priority is given to parents who register and attend the parent information night. The program launches in Term Two 2025 subject to minimum enrolments.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students attending Le Page Primary School; Open to students from surrounding schools; Prep to Year 6; Identified as gifted and talented; Minimum enrolments required for classes to run
year_levels: Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Classes run one half day per week for eight weeks (Prep-Year 2) or one full day per week for eight weeks (Year 3-6) beginning Term Two 2025. Enrolment priority given to parents attending information night. Program subject to minimum enrolments.

overview: Leibler Yavneh College offers three Nicholas Slezak Memorial Scholarships to current Yavneh students. The scholarships recognise academic and general excellence for students progressing to Years 7, 9 and 11. Scholarships cover 25% of tuition for two years.
description: The Nicholas Slezak Memorial Scholarship is awarded annually to three existing Yavneh students, one each entering Years 7, 9 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship covers 25% of the tuition for two years of study. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and general excellence through a competitive examination administered by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). Students must sit a formal entrance exam on 22 February 2026 covering abstract reasoning, verbal reasoning, numerical reasoning, reading comprehension, and mathematics. These scholarships are designed to assist families seeking a first-class Jewish education for their children and recognise outstanding achievement across both academic and co-curricular endeavours.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Leibler Yavneh College; Entering Years 7, 9 or 11 in 2027; Must complete AAS entrance examination; Successful applicants expected to participate fully in College life
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years. Recipients must continue to demonstrate satisfactory academic progress and conduct. Successful applicants are expected to participate fully in College life.

overview: The Leibler Yavneh College Academic Scholarship is offered to external students entering Years 7, 9 and 11. This scholarship recognises academic excellence and general achievement for students not currently enrolled at the College. It covers 50% of tuition for two years.
description: The Leibler Yavneh College Academic Scholarship is awarded to external students (those not currently enrolled at Yavneh) entering Years 7, 9 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship covers 50% of the tuition for two years. Applicants must complete an entrance examination administered by Academic Assessment Services (AAS) on 22 February 2026, which assesses abstract reasoning, problem solving, reading comprehension, and mathematics achievement. Selection is based on demonstrated academic and general excellence. Applications must be submitted by early February 2026. This scholarship is designed to assist families seeking a first-class Jewish education for their children.
eligibility_criteria: External student (not currently enrolled at Leibler Yavneh College); Entering Years 7, 9 or 11 in 2027; Must complete AAS entrance examination; Must apply by early February 2026
special_conditions: Scholarship covers tuition for two years. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct standards.

overview: The Don and Sonia Marejn Scholarship is a needs-based bursary offered to students from Prep to Year 12. The College offers five scholarships annually to assist families who would otherwise be unable to afford a Jewish education. Awards are based on demonstrated financial need and non-academic criteria.
description: The Don and Sonia Marejn Scholarship is Leibler Yavneh College's primary needs-based bursary program, offering five scholarships each year to students at all year levels from Prep to Year 12. This scholarship is designed to assist families who would otherwise not be able to afford a quality Jewish education. Applications are assessed on a range of non-academic criteria, focusing on demonstrable financial hardship and family circumstances. The scholarship value varies based on individual family circumstances and financial assessment. Recipients are supported to participate fully in the college community and benefit from a comprehensive Jewish and general education.
eligibility_criteria: Students from Prep to Year 12; Demonstrated financial need; Non-academic criteria assessed; Must be seeking access to Jewish education; Successful applicants expected to participate fully in College life
special_conditions: Scholarship assessment based on non-academic criteria and demonstrated financial hardship. Award value varies based on individual family financial circumstances. Recipients must participate fully in College life.

overview: The Ruben Fried Scholarship is a needs-based bursary for one Jewish student entering Year 7 in 2026 who is not currently enrolled at a Jewish school. This bursary assists families seeking affordable access to a quality Jewish education and recognises demonstrated financial need and personal circumstances.
description: The Ruben Fried Scholarship is a dedicated bursary for one Jewish student entering Year 7 in 2026 who is not currently enrolled in a Jewish school. This scholarship is awarded based on non-academic criteria and demonstrated financial need. The key criteria on which this bursary scholarship is awarded focus on the family's financial circumstances and commitment to Jewish education. Successful applicants are expected to participate fully in College life and engage actively in the school community. The scholarship supports access to a quality Jewish and general education for families facing financial challenges.
eligibility_criteria: Jewish student; Not currently enrolled at a Jewish school; Entering Year 7 in 2026; Demonstrated financial need; Commitment to participation in College life
special_conditions: Applicants must be Jewish students not currently enrolled in Jewish school. Successful applicants must participate fully in College life. Scholarship award value and duration determined based on financial assessment and individual circumstances.

overview: Lauriston offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering the school. The scholarship recognises girls who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and a commitment to intellectual excellence, along with their capacity to contribute meaningfully to the school community.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to girls with outstanding academic potential who demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability. Selection is based on above-average results achieved in the annual scholarship examination, school reports, and an interview. Recipients are required to articulate what a scholarship would mean to them and demonstrate how they would plan to meaningfully contribute to the school and broader community if successful. The scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic potential demonstrated through above-average results in scholarship examination; Satisfactory academic school reports; Willingness to contribute to school and broader community; Current or prospective student at Lauriston Girls' School
special_conditions: All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Failure to meet expectations in either academic performance or behaviour may result in withdrawal of scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000.

overview: Lauriston offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise well-rounded students who demonstrate academic proficiency alongside significant contributions across multiple areas including Music, Sport, Performing Arts, Creative Arts, Leadership or Community Service.

Selection is based on above-average results achieved in the annual scholarship examination, school reports, and an interview. Applicants must demonstrate their participation and achievements in the activities noted in their application. Recipients are required to articulate what a scholarship would mean to them and demonstrate how they would plan to meaningfully contribute to the school and broader community if successful. The scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated academic proficiency; Above-average results in scholarship examination; Significant achievements or participation in Music, Sport, Performing Arts, Creative Arts, Leadership or Community Service; Satisfactory academic school reports; Commitment to community contribution; Current or prospective student at Lauriston Girls' School
special_conditions: All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Failure to meet expectations in either academic performance or behaviour may result in withdrawal of scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. overview: Lauriston offers Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and a commitment to the school's vibrant music community. These scholarships recognise students who excel in music performance and contribution to the school's musical life.
description: Music Scholarships are offered to new students entering Years 5, 7, 8 and 10 who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and active contribution to the school's music community. Selection is based on an examination, school reports, an audition, and an interview. Candidates must meet minimum AMEB (Australian Music Examinations Board) level requirements. Students are expected to demonstrate their musical achievements and articulate their commitment to contributing to the school's music program. The scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees for the duration of enrolment. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school, including active participation in the music program.
