special_conditions: All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Failure to meet expectations in either academic performance or behaviour may result in withdrawal of scholarship. Recipients must maintain active participation in the school's music program. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000.

overview: The Foundation Scholarship provides needs-based financial assistance to students from disadvantaged backgrounds who demonstrate intellectual ability and good character. This scholarship removes financial barriers for talented girls from diverse backgrounds to access Lauriston's outstanding education.
description: Established in 2011 with the generous support of donors, the Foundation Scholarship provides financial assistance to students who would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education. The scholarship is means-tested and awarded based on above-average results achieved in the annual scholarship examination, school reports and an interview. Selection criteria includes intellectual ability, community-mindedness and good character. The Foundation Scholarship provides full remission of tuition fees. As this fund grows, the Foundation aims to extend the offer to more students. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. Currently, up to two new means-tested Foundation Scholarships are offered to support students until graduation.
eligibility_criteria: Entry into Year 7; Demonstrated intellectual ability through above-average scholarship examination results; Community-mindedness and good character; Demonstrated financial need through means-testing; Satisfactory school reports; Would not otherwise have opportunity to attend Lauriston; Applicants must be willing to attend the school until Year 12
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarship. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Failure to meet expectations in either academic performance or behaviour may result in withdrawal of scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. The scholarship was established to support girls who demonstrate intellectual ability, community-mindedness and good character and would not otherwise have the chance to experience the outstanding education and opportunities at Lauriston. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000. Not being offered for commencement in 2027.

overview: Lauriston offers an Arts Scholarship to Year 10 or 11 entry students with an affinity for visual arts. This scholarship provides means-tested financial assistance to talented students from disadvantaged backgrounds who have a passion for artistic practice.
description: This scholarship is offered to new students in Years 10 or 11 with an affinity for the Arts who would not otherwise be able to experience a Lauriston education. The scholarship is means-tested and the availability of this fund depends on donors' generosity through fundraising endeavours. Candidates are assessed based on artistic abilities, above-average results in the annual scholarship examination, school reports and an interview. The scholarship provides financial assistance towards tuition fees. Recipients are expected to demonstrate their artistic achievements and contribute actively to the school's arts community during their enrolment. The scholarship is awarded on the basis that the recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School.
eligibility_criteria: Entry into Year 10 or Year 11; Demonstrated affinity for Visual Arts; Above-average results in scholarship examination; Demonstrated financial need through means-testing; Satisfactory school reports; Would not otherwise have opportunity to attend Lauriston; Evidence of artistic abilities and achievements
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarship. The availability of this fund depends on donors' generosity through fundraising endeavours. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. program_name: Belinda Phillips Goldman Sachs Memorial Scholarship
overview: The Belinda Phillips Goldman Sachs Memorial Scholarship supports talented all-rounders who demonstrate excellence in both academic and sporting pursuits. Created in memory of Belinda Phillips, a passionate sports advocate, this scholarship provides means-tested financial assistance to students who would not otherwise have the opportunity to attend Lauriston.
description: This perpetual fund supports a talented all-rounder who would not otherwise have the chance to attend Lauriston and is means-tested. The scholarship is designed for students who have a passion and proficiency for both academic and sporting pursuits, embodying a balanced approach to education with exposure to a range of activities. The scholarship was created by Richard and Annabelle Phillips in memory of Richard's sister Belinda, who was a passionate sports advocate. The philosophy behind the scholarship is that participation in sport is crucial to the development of a young person and playing in a team is a wonderful way to learn teamwork, resilience and persistence. Selection is based on above-average academic results, sporting achievements, school reports and an interview. The scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees for the duration of enrolment until Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Passion and proficiency in both academic studies and sport; Above-average academic performance; Demonstrated sporting ability and commitment to team participation; Demonstrated financial need through means-testing; Satisfactory school reports; Would not otherwise have opportunity to attend Lauriston; Entry into Years 7, 8, 10 or 11
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarship. All scholarships are subject to recipients maintaining academic standards, behavioural standards and an overall contribution to the school. Failure to meet expectations in either academic performance or behaviour may result in withdrawal of scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. Recipients are expected to maintain active participation in sport throughout their enrolment. Not being offered for commencement in 2027. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement and commitment to Christian values. The scholarship program supports both new and current students who demonstrate exceptional ability and leadership potential.
description: Lighthouse Christian College offers up to six Academic Scholarships annually to new and current students. The scholarship provides up to 50% fee discount tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on demonstrated Christian commitment, leadership ability, community service involvement, written application letter to the Principal, and a panel interview with the Principal/Deputy Principal. Applicants must be committed Christians with a Pastor's reference attached. The scholarship recognises students who combine academic excellence with Christian values and community contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a committed Christian with Pastor's reference; Must demonstrate leadership ability; Must demonstrate community service; Must write a letter to the Principal; Must participate in a panel interview with Principal/Deputy Principal; All current and prospective students must pay non-refundable scholarship registration fee
special_conditions: Scholarship is tenable until the end of Year 12. All current and prospective students applying must pay a non-refundable scholarship registration fee. Selection requires demonstrated Christian commitment including a Pastor's reference, evidence of leadership ability, community service involvement, written letter to Principal, and completion of a panel interview with the Principal/Deputy Principal.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College provides bursaries and financial assistance programs to support eligible students. These programs may include support for university enhancement programs while students are in Year 12.
description: Lighthouse Christian College offers bursaries which may support a university enhancement program while students are in Year 12. The bursary program is designed to provide financial assistance to families who demonstrate need. The specific financial value and eligibility criteria for bursaries are not fully detailed on the current website, but they operate as a complementary program to the scholarship offerings. Interested families should contact the school directly for detailed information about bursary applications, eligibility criteria, and available support amounts.
eligibility_criteria: Financial need demonstration required; Open to current and prospective students; May be available for Year 12 students seeking university enhancement program support
special_conditions: May support university enhancement programs while in Year 12. Specific conditions and eligibility details available directly from the school. Non-refundable application fee of $150 (GST inclusive) required.

overview: Lauriston offers Academic Scholarships to students with outstanding academic potential. These scholarships recognise exceptional intellectual ability and commitment to academic excellence at the highest level.
description: Academic Scholarships at Lauriston Girls' School are awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic potential and exceptional achievement across their studies. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7, 8, 10 or 11, and selection is based on performance in the annual scholarship examination (conducted by Academic Assessment Services - AAS), school reports, and an interview with the Principal. Academic Scholarships are partial scholarships and are not funded by philanthropic support. All recipients must complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. The exact financial benefit varies and is determined on a case-by-case basis as a partial remission of academic tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic potential; Excellent academic ability; Must demonstrate qualities consistent with the school ethos; Open to students entering Year 7, 8, 10 or 11; Not eligible if currently holding another Lauriston scholarship; Overseas students on full fee-paying student visas not eligible; Applicant must sit the scholarship examination; Applicants must maintain academic standards and behavioural standards; Recipient must complete education to Year 12 at Lauriston
special_conditions: All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. If a student is withdrawn from the school prior to the completion of Year 12, all scholarship funds awarded must be repaid in full. All scholarships are subject to annual review to confirm that high academic and behaviour standards expected of a scholarship student have been maintained. The school reserves the right to request financial information from families during the scholarship process. No student will receive more than one concession and the greater discount (whether a sibling discount or a scholarship) will apply. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000 (credited to second tuition fee instalment after commencement).

overview: Lauriston offers General Excellence Scholarships to well-rounded students who demonstrate academic proficiency and make significant contributions across multiple areas including music, sport, performing arts, creative arts, leadership or community service. These scholarships are awarded to students with broader talents and community mindedness.

The scholarship recognises students who are 'all-rounders' with a passion for both academic and co-curricular activities. Selection is based on performance in the annual scholarship examination (Academic Assessment Services - AAS), school reports, interview with the Principal, and provision of a portfolio of work demonstrating achievements in activities the student is involved in. Candidates must articulate what a scholarship would mean to them and demonstrate how they would meaningfully contribute to the school and broader community. General Excellence Scholarships are not funded by philanthropic support and provide partial fee remission determined on a case-by-case basis.

special_conditions: All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. If a student is withdrawn from the school prior to the completion of Year 12, all scholarship funds awarded must be repaid in full. All scholarships are subject to annual review to confirm that high academic and behaviour standards expected of a scholarship student have been maintained. Students are expected to participate in workshops to evaluate collaboration skills. No student will receive more than one concession and the greater discount will apply. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000 (credited to second tuition fee instalment).

overview: Lauriston offers Foundation Scholarships to provide financial assistance to students who would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education. These are means-tested scholarships established in 2011 with support from donors, providing substantial fee relief to deserving students.
description: The Foundation Scholarship at Lauriston Girls' School is a means-tested scholarship established in 2011 with the generous support of donors. It provides significant financial assistance to a student who would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education. The scholarship provides full remission for tuition fees and is awarded based on above-average results achieved in the annual scholarship examination, school reports, and an interview with the Principal. In most cases, the scholarship will cover 80% of a student's fees, determined on a case-by-case basis, with the Principal having discretion to increase the amount depending on circumstances. May include additional support such as the provision of a laptop. The scholarship supports students from Year 7 until graduation in Year 12, including participation in the unique Year 9 Howqua Program. Currently, up to two means-tested Foundation Scholarships are available. Candidates who have been shortlisted are required to complete a financial questionnaire.
eligibility_criteria: Means-tested; Demonstrate intellectual ability; Demonstrate community-mindedness and good character; Would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education; Above-average results in annual scholarship examination; Satisfactory academic school reports and NAPLAN results; New students only entering Year 7 until graduation in Year 12; Not currently holding another Lauriston scholarship; Overseas students on full fee-paying student visas not eligible; Must complete financial questionnaire if shortlisted; Applicants must maintain academic and behavioural standards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12

If a student is withdrawn from the school prior to the completion of Year 12, all scholarship funds awarded must be repaid in full. All scholarships are subject to annual review to confirm that high academic and behaviour standards expected of a scholarship student have been maintained. The Principal may discretionarily increase the scholarship amount depending on circumstances. Upon acceptance of a Scholarship, families are required to pay an Admission Fee of $1250 (non-refundable) and a Tuition Deposit of $5000 (credited to second tuition fee instalment). Provides full remission for tuition fees; may include additional support such as laptop provision.

program_name: Belinda Phillips Goldman Sachs Memorial Scholarship
overview: The Belinda Phillips Goldman Sachs Memorial Scholarship is a perpetual fund supporting talented all-rounders who demonstrate both academic and sporting proficiency. Created in memory of Belinda Phillips, a passionate sports advocate, this means-tested scholarship is for students who value balanced education with exposure to a range of activities.
description: The Belinda Phillips Goldman Sachs Memorial Scholarship is for girls entering Lauriston in Years 7, 8, 10 or 11 and is designed for a student who has a passion and proficiency for both academic and sporting pursuits. This perpetual fund supports a talented all-rounder who would not otherwise have the chance to attend Lauriston and is means-tested. The scholarship was created by Richard and Annabelle Phillips in memory of Richard's sister Belinda, a passionate sports advocate, who died tragically with her daughter Olivia in a car accident. Richard and Annabelle's philosophy is that an educational experience should be a balanced approach with exposure to a range of activities. They see participation in sport as crucial to the development of a young person and playing in a team as a wonderful way to learn teamwork, resilience and persistence. The scholarship provides a significant contribution towards tuition fees. It is expected that the recipient will complete three seasons (Year 10 - 2025) or two seasons (Year 11 - 2024) of Girls Sport Victoria sport with Lauriston.

special_conditions: Not being offered for commencement in 2027. Means-tested scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. If a student is withdrawn from the school prior to the completion of Year 12, all scholarship funds awarded must be repaid in full. All scholarships are subject to annual review. It is expected that recipient will complete three seasons (Year 10 entrants) or two seasons (Year 11 entrants) of Girls Sport Victoria sport with Lauriston. Perpetual fund created to honour a passionate sports advocate.

overview: Lauriston offers an Arts Scholarship to students with an affinity for the Arts who would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education. This means-tested scholarship recognises artistic abilities across performing arts, visual arts, music and creative arts disciplines.
description: The Arts Scholarship at Lauriston Girls' School is offered to a new student in Years 10 or 11 with an affinity for the Arts who would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education and is means-tested. As the Foundation's newest scholarship, the availability of this fund is dependent on the generosity of donors through fundraising endeavours. Candidates will be assessed based on artistic abilities as well as above-average results in the annual scholarship examination, school reports and an interview with the Principal. The scholarship recognises excellence in visual arts, performing arts including drama, music, dance and musical theatre. It is designed to support talented young artists from diverse backgrounds who demonstrate both artistic excellence and academic proficiency. The exact financial benefit is not specified but provides support towards tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Entering Year 10 or Year 11; Affinity for the Arts (visual arts, performing arts, music, drama, dance, musical theatre); Would otherwise be unable to experience a Lauriston education; Means-tested; Above-average results in annual scholarship examination; Satisfactory school reports; Artistic abilities demonstrated; Applicants must interview with the Principal; Applicants must maintain academic and behavioural standards; Recipient must complete education to Year 12; Not currently holding another Lauriston scholarship
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarship. Availability dependent on donor generosity through fundraising endeavours. Currently seeking donations for this scholarship. All scholarships are awarded on the basis that the scholarship recipient will complete their education to Year 12 at Lauriston Girls' School. If a student is withdrawn from the school prior to the completion of Year 12, all scholarship funds awarded must be repaid in full. Assessment based on artistic abilities, above-average examination results, school reports and Principal interview. Applicants must maintain academic and behavioural standards throughout enrolment.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in new and current students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. The scholarship recognises students who display commitment to learning and academic excellence within the college's Christ-centred environment.
description: Lighthouse Christian College offers up to six Academic Scholarships annually to new and current students for entry to the college. The scholarship provides up to 50% fee discount tenable until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, academic records, a letter to the Principal, demonstrated leadership ability, community service engagement, and a panel interview with the Principal and Deputy Principal. All applicants must be committed Christians with a Pastor's reference attached to their application. Prospective students must submit a complete enrolment application in addition to their scholarship registration form.
eligibility_criteria: New and current students; Be a committed Christian with Pastor's reference attached; Demonstrate leadership ability; Demonstrate community service; Must pass ACER Scholarship Test; Must participate in panel interview with Principal/Deputy Principal; Must submit letter to Principal
special_conditions: Scholarship is tenable until the end of Year 12. All scholarship applications must include a Pastor's reference. Selection criteria include demonstrated Christian commitment, leadership ability, community service, academic excellence, and successful performance on the ACER Scholarship Test. Prospective students must also submit a complete enrolment application.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College offers bursaries and financial assistance to support families experiencing financial hardship. The programme is designed to ensure no child is left behind due to financial constraints or unforeseen circumstances.
description: Lighthouse Christian College has established a Scholarship and Financial Assistance Fund to help families who may otherwise have to withdraw their children from school due to financial constraints or unforeseen circumstances. Bursaries may support a university enhancement program while students are in Year 12. The college allocated $250,000 to this fund in 2024, demonstrating commitment to providing accessible education for all students. Families interested in financial assistance should contact the school office or the Marketing Manager John Dawson directly for confidential discussion regarding support options and eligibility criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families at risk of having to withdraw children from school; Open to all current and prospective students; Financial circumstances assessed on individual basis
special_conditions: Bursaries may support a university enhancement program while student is in Year 12. Assessed on individual basis. Contact school office or Marketing Manager John Dawson for confidential enquiries. College has allocated significant funding annually to support this program.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in new and current students. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability, leadership potential, community service commitment, and a commitment to Christian faith.
description: Lighthouse Christian College offers up to six Academic Scholarships annually to new and current students. Recipients receive up to 50% fee discount tenable until the end of Year 12, and bursaries which may support a university enhancement program while in Year 12. The selection process requires completion of the ACER Scholarship Test, submission of a letter to the Principal, demonstration of leadership ability, evidence of community service, a Pastor's reference confirming Christian commitment, and participation in a panel interview with the Principal and Deputy Principal. Scholarships are awarded based on academic excellence, character, and alignment with the school's Christian values. All applicants must pay a non-refundable scholarship registration fee.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a committed Christian with Pastor's reference attached; Must demonstrate leadership ability; Must demonstrate community service; Must participate in ACER Scholarship Test; Must write a letter to the Principal; Must participate in panel interview with Principal/Deputy Principal; Open to both new and current students; Applicable to year levels that follow the school's standard entry points
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional on recipient maintaining a commitment to Christian faith and the school's values. Recipients are required to demonstrate ongoing leadership and community service. Bursaries may be available to support Year 12 students in a university enhancement program. All current and prospective students must submit a complete enrolment application in addition to the scholarship registration form. Scholarship registration fee of $95 (or $150 at Keysborough campus) is non-refundable.

overview: Little Yarra Steiner School offers scholarships to both new and current students to provide support to those who may face financial barriers to accessing an independent Steiner education. The scholarship programme recognises students who demonstrate enthusiasm in their studies, resilience in overcoming challenges, excellence in their areas of strength, and positive contributions to their class and school community.
description: The scholarship provides a discount on the student's annual tuition fee, which covers all essential aspects such as camps, excursions, swimming lessons for Classes 2-7, classroom materials, stationery (excluding Class 8 and above which have a booklist), and a weekly individual music lesson from Class 3–8. The scholarship is awarded for a duration of 3 years. Selection involves submission of school reports, a portfolio of work (for external applicants), a cover letter addressing selection criteria, and an interview. Scholarship recipients must maintain their eligibility by meeting the selection criteria each year, demonstrating continued commitment to the school's values and educational philosophy. The school reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship where the selection criteria is not being met.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Class 1 to Class 12; Available to both new and current students; Must demonstrate enthusiasm in studies and resilience in overcoming challenges; Must excel in areas of strength; Must contribute positively to class dynamics both academically and socially; Strong commitment from family to actively support student's educational journey within Steiner framework; Must display integrity, kindness and respect towards others; Must commit to upholding Steiner education principles inside and outside classroom; Must demonstrate demonstrated potential to excel in a Steiner educational environment; Must show genuine enthusiasm and curiosity towards learning; Current students do not require portfolio of work; External applicants must submit last two school reports and portfolio; Families with Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card eligible for need-based consideration
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. Continued eligibility is contingent upon meeting the selection criteria each year. The school will actively engage with families if there are concerns regarding retention of the scholarship. The school reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship where the selection criteria is not being met. Successful candidates will be notified by Friday, 31 October 2025. Scholarship will commence in Term 1, 2026. A separate capital levy charge is applicable and is not covered by the scholarship. New students progressing to interview stage must pay the normal application fee. Students already enrolled at LYSS do not need to supply a portfolio of work.

overview: Loch Sport Lions selects a graduating student to receive a very special scholarship voucher. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and contribution to the school community. The voucher is to be used at the student's chosen secondary college.
description: Loch Sport Lions presents this annual scholarship voucher to a graduating student who demonstrates exceptional leadership, character, and contribution to the school community. The scholarship takes the form of a voucher for financial assistance at the student's chosen secondary college. The award is presented at the school concert at the end of the year, recognising the selected student's commitment to excellence and service. This is a highly valued community-based award that celebrates the student's journey through primary school and supports their transition to secondary education. The specific financial value of the voucher is not publicly disclosed but represents a meaningful contribution towards secondary education costs.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a graduating student; Selected by Loch Sport Lions organisation; Demonstrated leadership and community contribution
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded by Loch Sport Lions. Presented at the school concert. Voucher must be used at a secondary college of the recipient's choice.

school_name: Lavalla Catholic College - St Paul's Campus

overview: Loreto College offers the Loreto Rise Up Scholarships to nurture individual students' talents and interests across all areas of the curriculum. These scholarships recognise well-rounded students who demonstrate excellence in academics, music, performing arts, or sport, and assist families with the costs associated with elite-level competition or performance through a remission of tuition fees.
description: The Loreto Rise Up Scholarships are available to both new and current students who demonstrate excellence in one of the following areas: academics, music, performing arts, and sport. These scholarships support the ongoing development of the student in their chosen field and assist families with the costs associated with competition or performance at an elite level through a remission of tuition fees. The financial benefit is provided as a remission (reduction) of tuition fees, with the exact percentage based on the outcome of the selection process. Recipients are expected to maintain high performance in their field and represent the College as requested. The scholarship is renewable annually subject to meeting conditions as outlined in the application form.
eligibility_criteria: Well-rounded students; Demonstrated excellence in one of the following areas: academics, music, performing arts or sport; Open to new and current students
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to new and current students. Recipients must meet the scholarship conditions as listed on the application form and support the College's commitment to excellence.

overview: Loreto College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to incoming Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability through formal testing and provides percentage-based remission of tuition fees through to the completion of Year 12.
description: The Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarship is offered to incoming Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance. Eligible students must currently be in Grade 6 at the time of application. The scholarship provides a percentage of tuition fees based on the outcome of academic testing and is awarded for the duration of the student's enrolment at Loreto College through to the end of Year 12. The exact financial benefit (percentage of tuition fees) is determined by performance in the academic testing assessment. Recipients must maintain the scholarship conditions as outlined in the application form, with annual review of scholarship status.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Must complete the Application for Enrolment form; Applications received prior to the closing date
special_conditions: Scholarship is provided on the basis of academic testing outcome and continues through to the end of Year 12 subject to recipient meeting scholarship conditions. Annual review of scholarship status is conducted.

overview: Loreto College offers the Eileen Macintyre Music Scholarship to incoming Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional talent and passion for music. This scholarship provides financial support through tuition fee remission plus weekly music lessons, recognising and nurturing musical excellence from the commencement of secondary education.
description: The Eileen Macintyre Music Scholarship is offered to students commencing Year 7 at Loreto College and provides up to 50% remission of tuition fees through to the end of Year 12, subject to the recipient meeting scholarship conditions as listed on the application form. Recipients also receive one half-hour music tuition lesson per week, with a maximum of 9 lessons per term. The scholarship is named in honour of past pupil Eileen Macintyre and is funded through her generous bequest. The financial benefit combines the tuition fee reduction (up to 50%) with the provision of weekly music lessons. Recipients must maintain the scholarship conditions throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Demonstrate musical talent and passion for music; Must complete the Application for Enrolment form; Applications received prior to the closing date
special_conditions: Scholarship provides up to 50% remission of tuition fees through to the end of Year 12 and includes one half-hour music tuition lesson per week (maximum 9 lessons per term). Recipients must meet scholarship conditions as outlined in the application form.

program_name: Past Pupils Association Music, Performance and Visual Arts Scholarship
overview: Loreto College awards the Past Pupils Association Music, Performance and Visual Arts Scholarship to current Year 8 students who demonstrate excellence in creative disciplines. These scholarships provide financial support for students pursuing music, drama, dance, and visual arts at a high level.
description: The Past Pupils Association Music, Performance and Visual Arts Scholarship is open to all current Year 8 Loreto College Ballarat students. A number of scholarships between $500 and $1,000 are offered to current students towards tuition and expenses associated with Music, Performing Arts, or Visual Arts (including Art, Dance, Drama, and Media). The scholarship supports students already enrolled at the College who demonstrate excellence and commitment in their chosen creative discipline. The financial benefit is provided as a monetary award ranging from $500 to $1,000, to be used towards tuition fees and expenses related to the student's artistic pursuits. Recipients are required to maintain their commitment to excellence in their chosen field.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Year 8 Loreto College Ballarat students; Demonstrate excellence in Music, Performing Arts (Drama, Dance, Musical Theatre) or Visual Arts (Art, Media); Current student at the College
special_conditions: Open to current Year 8 students only. Scholarships range from $500 to $1,000. For graduating or leaving students only.

overview: Loreto College offers the Loreto Aspiring Dream Award, which was established from proceeds of a 2019 fundraising event for the Michelle Payne movie 'Ride Like a Girl'. This award recognises and supports graduating students who demonstrate exceptional achievement across multiple fields of endeavour.
description: The Loreto Aspiring Dream Award was established in 2019 through fundraising efforts related to the regional launch of the Michelle Payne movie 'Ride Like a Girl'. All proceeds from ticket sales and donations from this event went towards establishing this award. The award recognises graduating or leaving students who demonstrate exceptional achievement and excellence across multiple areas of school life, reflecting the 'aspiring dream' values of the College. While specific financial values are not detailed on the main scholarship page, a PDF document titled 'Aspiring Dream Scholarship Information' provides comprehensive details about the award's purpose, eligibility criteria, and selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Graduating or leaving students only; Demonstrate exceptional achievement and excellence in multiple fields; Show commitment to the Loreto values and mission
special_conditions: For graduating or leaving students only. Award recognises exceptional achievement in multiple areas of school life.

overview: Loreto College offers the Dora O'Sullevan Bursary to support students from families experiencing financial difficulties who may otherwise be unable to attend the College. Named in honour of past pupil Dora O'Sullevan, this bursary embodies the generosity and commitment to education of the O'Sullevan and Coghlan families.
description: The Dora O'Sullevan Bursary is named in memory and honour of past pupil Dora O'Sullevan by her sister Rita Andre (nee O'Sullevan) and is administered by the Principal. The bursary supports students from families encountering financial difficulties which may otherwise prevent their daughter from attending Loreto College Ballarat. Eligible candidates may be current students or prospective students at any year level. Consideration for bursaries is reviewed annually on a case-by-case basis, and formal evidence of current financial situation is required to verify the need. The financial benefit varies depending on individual circumstances and family need, as determined by the Principal. Recipients must abide by the Loreto values and agreed code of conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Families encountering financial difficulties that may prevent attendance; Open to current or prospective students at any year level; Formal evidence of financial situation required; Personal application from family or supported by recommendation from Primary School Principal or Parish Priest; Applications must be received by the published closing date for Year 7 applications
special_conditions: Both ongoing and annual bursaries are subject to the student and family abiding by the Loreto values and agreed code of conduct as required by a student and/or parent/carer of the Loreto community. Bursaries are reviewed annually on a case-by-case basis. Recipients must provide formal letter to the Principal from parents/carers explaining request and current situation.

overview: Loreto College partners with the Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) and The Smith Family to offer scholarships funded by the Australian Government to Indigenous students. These scholarships support young Indigenous Australians from remote and regional communities to access secondary education and develop leadership capabilities.
description: Loreto College partners with the Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) to offer scholarships and leadership opportunities to young Indigenous Australians, with a focus on Indigenous youth from communities in very remote areas or regional communities. The IYLP is funded by the Department of Prime Minister and Cabinet's Children and Schooling Programme and The Smith Family was selected to be an IYLP Partnership Broker. The IYLP supports scholarships for Indigenous students to attend approved secondary schools including both day and boarding options, at government and non-government schools. In addition to helping Indigenous students achieve their educational aspirations, the IYLP supports the development of a pool of positive role models and future leaders. Recipients must be able to meet the requirements set out in the program guidelines in relation to receipt of other Commonwealth scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: Indigenous Australian students; Preference for students from very remote or regional communities; Students enrolling at Loreto College Ballarat; Able to meet program guidelines regarding other Commonwealth scholarships
special_conditions: Funded by the Australian Government through the Department of Prime Minister and Cabinet's Children and Schooling Programme. Recipients must meet requirements regarding other Commonwealth scholarships.

program_name: Health Care Card Fee Relief Program
overview: Lumen Christi School offers significant fee relief to families who hold a Health Care Card. This program is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship who meet the eligibility criteria for government health assistance.
description: Lumen Christi School, with support of the Catholic Education Office, offers a significant fee relief program to families who have a Health Care Card. The exact amount of relief is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on the family's documented financial circumstances. The school works directly with families to assess their situation and provide appropriate fee reduction. Families interested in accessing this program should contact the school office or Principal Sally Darby to discuss their circumstances and eligibility. The program operates on an ongoing basis throughout the school year for eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current Health Care Card; Open to all year levels at Lumen Christi School; Financial hardship must be demonstrated
special_conditions: Contact the school office or Principal Sally Darby to discuss eligibility and fee relief amounts.

program_name: CSEF Payment (Camps, Sports, Excursion Fund) Support
overview: Lumen Christi School assists eligible families in successfully applying for the CSEF payment from the Victorian State Government. The school provides guidance and support to help families access government funding for camps, sports, and excursion activities.
description: Lumen Christi School supports eligible families to successfully apply for the Camps, Sports, Excursion Fund (CSEF) payment from the State Government of Victoria. This is a government-funded program available to students from eligible families. The school provides assistance with the application process to ensure families understand the requirements and can submit their applications correctly. CSEF provides direct payment to schools for eligible students' participation in school camps, sports and excursion activities. The school provides an application form and works with families throughout the process. Eligible families should contact the school office to obtain the application form and receive assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Victorian school student; Eligible for government CSEF payments based on Victorian government criteria; All year levels welcome to apply
special_conditions: This is a Victorian State Government program. Application forms are available from the school office. School contact: 03 5122 2231

program_name: The Smith Family LEARNING FOR LIFE Scholarship Support
overview: Lumen Christi School assists eligible families in applying for The Smith Family LEARNING FOR LIFE scholarship, a national program that supports disadvantaged young Australians to stay engaged at school and reach their full potential.
description: Lumen Christi School provides support to eligible families to successfully apply for The Smith Family LEARNING FOR LIFE scholarship program. This is an external scholarship program administered by The Smith Family, a national youth support charity. The school helps families understand their eligibility and navigate the application process. The Learning for Life scholarship provides financial support and mentoring to disadvantaged young Australians to help them complete their education. The school acts as a liaison between families and The Smith Family to facilitate access to this program. Families experiencing financial hardship and who believe they may be eligible should contact the school office to discuss their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Must meet The Smith Family eligibility criteria for financial disadvantage; All year levels eligible; Families experiencing financial hardship
special_conditions: This is an external program administered by The Smith Family national charity. School provides support with application process.

program_name: OPENING DOORS Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander Support
overview: Lumen Christi School assists eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families in accessing the OPENING DOORS program, which provides educational and financial support to Indigenous students and their families.
description: Lumen Christi School provides support to eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families to apply for the OPENING DOORS program. This program is designed to assist Indigenous students and families with educational support and access to resources. The school helps families understand the program, determine their eligibility, and complete applications. OPENING DOORS provides financial assistance and support services to help Indigenous students succeed at school. The school coordinates with the program administrators to facilitate access. Families who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander should contact the school office to discuss available support and assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Student or family must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; All year levels eligible; Must meet OPENING DOORS program eligibility criteria
special_conditions: This is an external Indigenous support program. School provides assistance with application and eligibility assessment.

overview: Lumen Christi School works with families experiencing financial hardship to develop flexible payment plans that make school fees more manageable and accessible.
description: Lumen Christi School is able to discuss different payment plan options to assist families suffering from financial hardship. The school recognizes that families may face temporary or ongoing financial difficulties and is committed to working with them to find sustainable solutions. Payment plans are tailored to individual family circumstances and financial capacity. Families do not need to withdraw their children from school due to financial hardship. The school encourages families to contact the office or Principal Sally Darby early to discuss their situation and explore available options. Working together, the school and family can develop a payment arrangement that suits their circumstances while maintaining the child's enrollment.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship; All year levels eligible; Must contact school to discuss situation and arrange payment plan
special_conditions: Flexible payment plans arranged on case-by-case basis. Families must contact the school office or Principal Sally Darby directly to discuss their circumstances and arrange suitable payment options.

overview: Loyola College offers a Scholarship Program that recognises and acknowledges students who take responsibility for, and are proactive, in the development of their own education. The scholarship is available to students currently enrolled in Year 8 to Year 11 who meet the eligibility criteria.
description: The Loyola College Scholarship Program awards scholarships to the value of $1,000 as a deduction from school fees in the following years. Students currently in Year 8-11 interested in applying must follow the Scholarship Application Procedure. The scholarship recognises academic responsibility and proactive engagement in educational development. The award is applied to school fees over the recipient's enrollment period at the college. Specific selection criteria and the application procedure are managed by the college and prospective applicants should contact the school for detailed information about merit assessment and submission requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 8-11; Must follow the Scholarship Application Procedure; Open to all genders at the co-educational college
special_conditions: Award is applied as a deduction from school fees in subsequent years following selection. Scholarship recipients must maintain proactive engagement in their education throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Loyola College offers a Year 8 Music Subsidy program providing financial support for students commencing instrumental music tuition. The subsidy is available to Year 8 students who enrol in an approved ensemble alongside their music lessons, recognising the importance of ensemble participation in music education.
description: Year 8 students who select trumpet, trombone, flute, clarinet, saxophone, violin, viola, or cello and enrol in an approved ensemble for the year are eligible for a 50% subsidy on their music tuition fees. The subsidy applies only to music tuition fees and does not include instrument hire charges. To access the subsidy, students must complete the Music Program Application, Music Program Agreement, and the Year 8 Subsidy Agreement. The subsidy is only applicable to students who maintain active participation in their selected ensemble throughout the year. Withdrawal from an ensemble within the year of eligibility will result in reversal of the full subsidy and the student becomes liable for total music tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 8; Must select one of the approved instruments: trumpet, trombone, flute, clarinet, saxophone, violin, viola, or cello; Must enrol in and actively participate in an approved ensemble; Must complete all required application forms; Subsidy applies only to music tuition fees, not instrument hire
special_conditions: Subsidy applies only to music tuition fees and excludes instrument hire. Students must participate in an approved ensemble. Non-attendance at performances may result in reversal of the subsidy. Students who withdraw or are withdrawn from an ensemble within the year of eligibility will have the full subsidy reversed and will be liable for total music tuition fees. Minimum attendance and engagement requirements apply.

school_name: Loreto College - The Little Flower Campus

overview: Loreto Mandeville Hall offers the General Academic Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The scholarship supports students who are consistently high academic achievers and embody the Loreto values of commitment to learning and personal excellence.
description: The General Academic Scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student maintaining a high standard in her studies and a positive contribution to school life. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional results in the Edutest examination process. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview as part of the selection process. The scholarship is available to both current and prospective students. Applications without supporting documentation will not be considered.
eligibility_criteria: Students applying for a General Academic Scholarship must be consistently high academic achievers; Must demonstrate exceptional results in the Edutest examination process; Must be an Australian citizen or holder of a PERMANENT Resident's Visa at the time of application; Must satisfy standard Loreto Toorak entrance requirements
special_conditions: Scholarships apply to tuition fees only. Recipients are required to demonstrate commitment and willingness to participate fully in school activities. Scholarships are conditional on satisfactory progress, including diligent endeavour and achievement in studies. The school reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if non-compliance occurs.

overview: Loreto Mandeville Hall offers the Music Scholarship - Instrumental to recognise students with outstanding musical ability and strong academic achievement. The scholarship supports talented musicians who demonstrate excellence in instrumental performance.
description: The Music Scholarship - Instrumental provides partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, a 100% remission of music tuition fees on the offered instrument when lessons are taken in the School, and a 100% remission of AMEB Theory tuition up to Grade 5. Selection is based on an audition and demonstrated strong academic ability through the Edutest examination process. Candidates must meet minimum AMEB standards: Years 5 and 6 require AMEB Grade 4 for Piano or Grade 3 for other instruments; Years 7 and 8 require AMEB Grade 6 for Piano, Violin and Cello, or Grade 5 for other instruments. Applications must include two most recent school reports and relevant AMEB Music examination reports.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate strong academic ability through the Edutest examination process; Must achieve minimum AMEB music grades as specified; Must satisfy standard Loreto Toorak entrance requirements; Must be an Australian citizen or holder of a PERMANENT Resident's Visa at the time of application; Due to high volume of applications, not all candidates will be shortlisted for audition
special_conditions: Music scholarship recipients are encouraged to undertake Music as an elective subject and VCE Music Performance at Years 11 and/or 12. The Music Department reserves the right to offer auditions at its discretion based on the needs of the department. Candidates must provide their own accompanist. Candidates will perform two contrasting pieces up to 10 minutes total and sight read a piece at the appropriate level.

overview: The Sr Ruth Winship Scholarship honours the enduring commitment of Sr Ruth to Loreto Toorak and supports students with demonstrated academic ability and financial need. The scholarship recognises candidates who embody the Loreto values and commitment to learning.
description: The Sr Ruth Winship Scholarship is a means-tested scholarship that provides partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student maintaining a high standard in her studies and a positive contribution to school life. Candidates must demonstrate strong academic ability through the Edutest examination process and a solid academic standing. Supporting evidence of a student's ability to contribute to school life is required with the application. Families must provide copies of their most recent tax returns, complete a financial questionnaire and accompanying Statutory Declaration. The family income limit for this scholarship is $110,000 per annum. This scholarship is only available to prospective students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students only; Must demonstrate willingness to engage in opportunities a Loreto Toorak education offers; Must demonstrate commitment to academic studies; Must show solid academic standing through Edutest examination; Family combined income must not exceed $110,000 per annum; Must be an Australian citizen or holder of a PERMANENT Resident's Visa at the time of application; Supporting evidence of ability to contribute to school life required
special_conditions: This is a means-tested scholarship. Families are required to provide copies of most recent tax returns and complete financial questionnaire and Statutory Declaration. An interview will be conducted as part of the selection process for shortlisted candidates. Scholarship subject to annual review and satisfactory academic progress.

overview: The Open Circle Scholarship supports aspirational and talented candidates who seek a Loreto Toorak education but face financial constraints. The scholarship enables access to a Loreto education for students of merit whose financial circumstances would otherwise limit their opportunity to attend.
description: The Open Circle Scholarship is a means-tested scholarship awarded on merit to students with demonstrated academic ability and financial need. Each scholarship has specific criteria and is available only to prospective students. Applicants must outline in fewer than 500 words how they can contribute to the life of the school. As part of the selection process, families are required to provide copies of their most recent tax returns and complete the financial questionnaire and accompanying Statutory Declaration. Selection includes an interview for shortlisted candidates. Scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to maintaining high academic standards and positive contribution to school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students only; Must be aspirational and talented candidates; Must demonstrate financial need with family income assessment; Must demonstrate ability to contribute to school life; Must complete financial questionnaire and Statutory Declaration; Must satisfy standard Loreto Toorak entrance requirements; Must provide supporting evidence of academic ability and character
special_conditions: Means-tested scholarship. Families must provide tax returns and complete financial questionnaire and Statutory Declaration. Applicants must outline in less than 500 words how they can contribute to school life. Interview required for shortlisted candidates. Financial arrangement must be kept in strict confidence. Non-refundable once offered.

overview: The Margaret Pratt Science Scholarship recognises and supports outstanding achievement in science. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate high academic achievement and a keen interest in science with commitment to science pathways.
description: The Margaret Pratt Science Scholarship is awarded biennially to one student for their Year 11 and Year 12 tuition who demonstrates high academic achievement and keen interest in science. The student must be committed to undertaking Science subjects in VCE and aspiring to science-related tertiary studies and career pathways. A total of $20,000 per annum is awarded to the successful candidate for tuition fees in both Years 11 and 12. Applicants must provide a statement of no more than 300 words outlining their commitment to the field of science and demonstrate this desire in their career aspirations. Successful candidates will be shortlisted for an interview based on their statement, school reports, and science teacher reference. Current Loreto Toorak scholarship recipients are ineligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current student for Years 11 and 12; Must demonstrate high academic achievement in science as evidenced in reports and science teacher reference; Must show keen interest in science with commitment to science pathway in VCE studies and tertiary education; Must demonstrate evidence of participation and achievement in science programs and activities; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Current scholarship recipients ineligible
special_conditions: Awarded biennially. Payment of $20,000 annually to be used for tuition fees only. Applicants must complete Scholarship Application for Enrolment Form and attach school reports and 300-word article and science teacher reference. Shortlisted applicants will outline their motivation and suitability during interview. Scholarship subject to annual review and satisfactory progress.

overview: The Jean Elizabeth (Betty) Ryan Scholarship honours the memory of Betty Ryan and supports students with strong commitment to social justice and service to others. The scholarship recognises students who embody values of service and engagement in the broader community.
description: The Jean Elizabeth (Betty) Ryan Scholarship is offered in memory of Jean Elizabeth (Betty) Ryan, who graduated from Loreto Toorak in 1930 and held a deep affection for the school throughout her life. Betty and her sister Margaret remained committed to their family's legacy of service and philanthropy. The scholarship seeks to support a student who has a strong commitment to social justice and service to others. It supports recipients who are keen to engage wholeheartedly in the many opportunities that a Loreto education offers, to enhance academic development and deep personal formation committed to a lifetime of service and contribution. This scholarship is available to prospective students only. Scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to maintaining high academic standards and positive contribution to school life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students only; Must demonstrate strong commitment to social justice and service to others; Must show keen engagement in opportunities that Loreto education offers; Must demonstrate commitment to academic studies and personal formation; Must be willing to contribute to school community life; Must satisfy standard Loreto Toorak entrance requirements
special_conditions: Available to prospective students only. Selection based on demonstrated commitment to social justice and service to others. Recipients must maintain high academic standards and positive contribution to school life. Scholarships conditional on satisfactory progress and adherence to school terms and conditions.

overview: Lyndale Secondary College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward students demonstrating sound academic potential. The scholarship is awarded to Year 6 students intending to enrol at the college and recognises students who display exceptional achievement in their studies.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is one of four scholarships offered annually by Lyndale Secondary College to Year 6 students intending to enrol in Year 7. The scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate sound academic potential and commitment to learning. Recipients are selected through a competitive process involving a written application, a referee report, and an interview. The scholarship provides ongoing support to help encourage academic success throughout the student's enrolment at the college. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not publicly specified on the college website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College in Year 7; Demonstrate sound academic potential; Willing to participate in application, referee report, and interview process
special_conditions: Students may apply for more than one type of scholarship, but must indicate on the application for each type of scholarship they wish to be considered for. Scholarships are awarded based on an application, a referenced application, and an interview.

overview: Lyndale Secondary College offers the Leadership Scholarship (Ian Mitchell Scholarship) to students demonstrating exceptional leadership ability. The scholarship honours former Principal Ian Mitchell, who served from 1986-2004, and recognises students with strong leadership qualities and potential.
description: The Leadership Scholarship, named after Ian Mitchell, is one of four scholarships offered annually to Year 6 students intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College. Ian Mitchell was the longest serving principal, establishing himself as an outstanding leader and great role model to both staff and students. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate the ability to develop leadership skills and show commitment to the college community. Selection is based on a written application, a referee report, and an interview. The exact financial benefit of the scholarship is not specified on the college website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College in Year 7; Demonstrate leadership skills and potential; Willing to participate in application, referee report, and interview process
special_conditions: Students may apply for more than one type of scholarship, but must indicate on the application for each type of scholarship they wish to be considered for. Recipients are expected to serve as role models in the school community.

overview: Lyndale Secondary College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognise outstanding sporting talent and achievement. The scholarship, featuring the Basil Powell Memorial Scholarship, honours a former great supporter of sports at the college and supports gifted young athletes.
description: The Sports Scholarship is one of four scholarships offered annually to Year 6 students intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College. The late Councillor Basil Powell was a great supporter of Sports at the College, and the college is able to acknowledge his services in the form of this scholarship. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional talent in sports and commitment to athletic excellence. Recipients are selected through a competitive process involving a written application, a referee report, and an interview. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not publicly specified on the college website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College in Year 7; Demonstrate excellence in sports and athletic talent; Willing to participate in application, referee report, and interview process
special_conditions: Students may apply for more than one type of scholarship, but must indicate on the application for each type of scholarship they wish to be considered for. Recipients are expected to represent the college with distinction in sporting competition.

program_name: Performing Arts Scholarship (Gabrielle Blood Memorial Scholarship)
overview: Lyndale Secondary College offers a Performing Arts Scholarship to support talented students in music, drama, and dance. The scholarship honours former Principal Gabrielle Blood (1983-1985) and demonstrates the college's commitment to establishing itself as a performing arts school of choice in the region.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship is one of four scholarships offered annually to Year 6 students intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College. Gabrielle Blood was College Principal from 1983 to 1985 and was a great supporter of the Arts at the College. Lyndale Secondary College is dedicated to providing an environment which promotes Performing Arts as an essential element in life whereby respect, self-confidence, discipline, teamwork and commitment are fostered to the highest possible performance and personal standards. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional talent and potential in performing arts disciplines. Selection is based on a written application, a referee report, and an interview. The exact financial benefit is not specified on the website.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Intending to enrol at Lyndale Secondary College in Year 7; Demonstrate talent in performing arts (music, drama, dance); Willing to participate in application, referee report, and interview process
special_conditions: Students may apply for more than one type of scholarship, but must indicate on the application for each type of scholarship they wish to be considered for. The college is committed to developing students' performing arts abilities in a supportive, high-standard environment.

overview: Lyndhurst Secondary College offers the Academic Curriculum Enhancement (ACE) Program as a selective entry pathway for highly able and talented students entering Year 7. The program provides a challenging and diverse curriculum designed to enhance each student's academic achievement and success at school and beyond. A limited number of merit-based scholarships worth $1,000 per student are awarded annually to high-performing applicants based on academic testing results.
description: The ACE Program is a select-entry learning pathway for high-achievers who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. Students in the program access specialised core curriculum, individual research projects, extension English and mathematics classes in Year 10, and pathways focused on increasing ATAR scores with early start VCE options. The program runs from Year 7 through Year 12 and includes involvement in all practical subjects such as Woodwork and Performing Arts offered to mainstream classes. Each year, a limited number of scholarships totalling $1,000 per student are awarded to selected students based on the academic testing that is part of the application process. Selection is based on academic testing conducted at Lyndhurst Secondary College, and the College commits to awarding these scholarships fairly to students from local feeder primary schools based on performance results. The school provides further information and application details through the ACE Information Booklet.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Grade 6 (primary school) or exceptional students at other year levels; Must complete academic testing as part of the application process; Intended to continue through to Year 12; Selection based on academic merit and performance on entry assessment
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on academic testing results conducted at Lyndhurst Secondary College. The College is committed to supporting local feeder primary schools through this process and awards scholarships fairly based on results. For further information and application details, applicants must download the ACE Information Booklet. ACE testing date is to be confirmed annually.

overview: Lyndhurst Secondary College's Lyndhurst Legacy Awards System recognises students' long-term commitment and dedication to displaying college values of Commitment, Excellence, Integrity, High Expectations and Respect. The system also recognises student contributions to the college through participation in Arts, Clubs, Community, Leadership and Sports programs, with awards presented throughout the year.
description: The Lyndhurst Legacy Badge System is a comprehensive recognition system that acknowledges students' long-term commitment and dedication to displaying the LSC College Values across their time at the college. The badge system recognises a student's contribution to the college outside of the core values by acknowledging their efforts in Arts, Clubs, Community, Leadership and Sports programs, all represented under the umbrella of Lyndhurst Legacy Community Badge. The Lyndhurst Legacy is the school's approach to School-Wide Positive Behaviour Support practice designed to develop positive, safe and supportive learning cultures. Staff support students to understand the importance of each of the College's Legacy Values and unpack the associated positive behaviours through classroom instruction and Home Group and Mentor Group times. Recognition of positive student engagement with the Legacy Values happens both inside and outside the classroom through Lyndhurst Legacy Compass Chronicle entries, which all staff can use to recognise positive student behaviours. The system reinforces positive behaviours and ensures student efforts are noticed and celebrated with badge recognition and special semester-based awards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled students at Lyndhurst Secondary College; Recognition based on demonstrated commitment to college values; Participation in co-curricular programs including Arts, Clubs, Community, Leadership and Sports
special_conditions: Recognition is awarded on an ongoing basis throughout the year. Semester-based awards are presented at whole school assemblies. Students must demonstrate consistent positive behaviour and engagement with college values to maintain badge recognition. The system is founded on the college's five core values: Commitment, Excellence, High Expectations, Integrity and Respect.

overview: Lyndhurst Secondary College presents the Academic Excellence Award to recognise students who demonstrate high-quality academic work across all their classwork and assessments, and who act as model students in their subjects. The award is presented each semester to students who receive three or more teacher nominations.
description: The Academic Excellence Award is a semester-based recognition program launched by Lyndhurst Secondary College to celebrate students who display exceptional academic achievement and excellence. Recipients are students who demonstrate high-quality work in all their classwork and assessments, as well as acting as a model student in their subjects. The award process involves all teachers being invited to nominate up to three students per class for the Academic Excellence category. Students who receive three or more nominations under this category are presented with a certificate and congratulated on their achievement. Awards are presented at whole school assemblies with parent and carer attendance to recognise and celebrate the young person's academic achievement. The award is presented each semester, providing multiple opportunities throughout the year for students to be recognised for sustained excellence. This program complements the college's broader Lyndhurst Legacy recognition system and demonstrates the college's commitment to celebrating academic success.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolled at Lyndhurst Secondary College; Demonstrated high-quality academic work in classwork and assessments; Must receive minimum three teacher nominations per semester; Must act as a model student in their subject areas
special_conditions: Awards are presented each semester at whole school assemblies. Recognition is based on teacher nominations with students requiring minimum three nominations to receive the award. Certificates are awarded to recognised students. Families are invited to attend assemblies to celebrate their child's achievement.

overview: Lyndhurst Secondary College presents the Academic Endeavour Award to recognise students who demonstrate dedication to their subjects and show sustained effort to improve their skills and academic work over the semester. The award is presented each semester to students who receive three or more teacher nominations and show evidence of improvement.
description: The Academic Endeavour Award is a semester-based recognition program launched by Lyndhurst Secondary College to celebrate students who display strong commitment to their learning and demonstrate consistent effort to improve. Recipients are students who display dedication to the subject with a focus on improving their skills and work over the course of the semester, and who have demonstrated effort to improve as evidenced by improved academic results or work habits which has led to a greater amount of success in the subject. The award process involves all teachers being invited to nominate up to three students per class for the Academic Endeavour category. Students who receive three or more nominations under this category are presented with a certificate and congratulated on their achievement. Awards are presented at whole school assemblies with parent and carer attendance to recognise and celebrate the young person's improvement and dedication. The award is presented each semester, providing multiple opportunities throughout the year for students to be recognised for their effort and progress. This program recognises the value of dedication and improvement alongside academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolled at Lyndhurst Secondary College; Demonstrated dedication to their subject; Evidence of improvement in skills and work during the semester; Demonstrated effort and commitment to improving academic performance; Must receive minimum three teacher nominations per semester
special_conditions: Awards are presented each semester at whole school assemblies. Recognition is based on teacher nominations with students requiring minimum three nominations to receive the award. Certificates are awarded to recognised students. Families are invited to attend assemblies to celebrate their child's achievement. Award recognises both improvement and effort rather than absolute performance level.

overview: Lowther Hall offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 5 and Year 7. These scholarships support talented students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to academic excellence.
description: Academic scholarships are offered for entry into Year 5 and Year 7 only. A range of discounts of up to 100% may be offered on tuition fees. Scholarships cover fees to the end of VCE studies (a range of tuition fee remissions are offered each year to successful candidates). Students undergo academic testing conducted by Academic Assessment Services in February of the year prior to desired entry year. Examinations take place onsite at Lowther Hall. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of examination results, interviews, previous school reports, and other information. The offer of a Scholarship is made on the condition that the student remains enrolled at Lowther Hall until completion of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Must undergo Academic Assessment Services testing; Previous school reports required; No disciplinary issues allowed; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12; NAPLAN results considered
special_conditions: Scholarships cover tuition fees only; all other expenses such as composite fees, Building Fund contribution and enrolment fee are chargeable. If a student leaves before Year 12, parents or guardians must repay the amount of fee remission received up to that time. The Principal may waive this obligation in exceptional circumstances. Scholarships cannot be deferred. Scholarship is subject to annual review and continued satisfactory progress.

overview: Lowther Hall offers Piano Scholarships to recognise excellence in piano performance and musical ability. These scholarships are available for entry into Year 5 and provide significant fee remission for talented pianists.
description: Piano scholarships are offered for entry into Year 5 only and offer up to full remission on academic tuition fees. Students must undergo academic testing conducted by Academic Assessment Services in February of the year prior to desired entry.
