Auditions for Piano scholarships occur in February of the year prior to the desired entry year, including a small panel of staff including the Director of Music and Principal. Scholarships cover fees to the end of VCE studies. Piano scholarship recipients must be an active participant in the musical life of the School and perform within school ensembles. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of examination results, auditions, interviews, and previous school reports.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 only; Must demonstrate piano proficiency and musical ability; Academic testing required; Audition required; Must be active participant in school music programs; No disciplinary issues allowed; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships for music lessons must be conducted internally at the School with a Lowther Hall staff member. Funds will not be provided for music tuition outside of the School. Piano scholarship recipients must maintain active participation in the musical life of the School. Scholarships cover tuition fees only; composite fees and Building Fund contributions are chargeable. If student withdraws before Year 12, parents must repay fee remission received. Scholarships cannot be deferred.

overview: Lowther Hall offers Music Scholarships to recognise outstanding musical achievement and talent in instruments. These scholarships are available for entry into Year 7 and provide substantial fee remission for talented musicians.
description: Music scholarships are offered for entry into Year 7 and offer up to full remission on academic tuition fees and/or private music tuition fees. Students must undergo academic testing conducted by Academic Assessment Services in February of the year prior to desired entry. Auditions for Music scholarships occur in February of the year prior to the desired entry year with specific instrument needs of the School reviewed annually. The audition panel includes the Director of Music and Principal. Scholarships cover fees to the end of VCE studies. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of examination results, auditions, interviews, previous school reports, and other information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 only; Must demonstrate instrumental proficiency; Academic testing required; Audition required; Must be active participant in school music programs; No disciplinary issues allowed; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12; Minimum eligibility requirements posted annually
special_conditions: Scholarships for music lessons must be conducted internally at the School with a Lowther Hall staff member. Funds will not be provided for music tuition outside of the School. Scholarship value may include academic tuition fees and/or private music tuition fees. Scholarships cover tuition fees only; composite fees and Building Fund contributions are chargeable. If student withdraws before Year 12, parents must repay fee remission. Specific instrument needs reviewed annually. Scholarships cannot be deferred.

program_name: Girls' Voices of the Cathedral Choir (GVCC) Scholarship
overview: The GVCC Scholarship recognises vocal excellence and supports students who wish to participate in the prestigious Girls' Voices of the Cathedral Choir, which provides choristers for St Paul's Cathedral in Melbourne. This is the only cathedral in the Southern Hemisphere with a dedicated girls' choir.
description: Vocal scholarships are offered for entry into Years 5 or 7 for students wishing to be part of the Girls' Voices of the Cathedral Program. A range of discounts may be offered on academic tuition fees. Students must undergo academic testing conducted by Academic Assessment Services in February of the year prior to desired entry. Auditions for GVCC applicants occur in mid-February, with shortlisted candidates invited for a callback audition. The audition panel includes the Director of Music and Principal. GVCC scholarships convert to vocal scholarships in Years 11-12. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of examination results, auditions, interviews, and previous school reports. St Paul's Cathedral is the only cathedral in the Southern Hemisphere with a designated girls' choir, provided by Lowther Hall.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Must demonstrate vocal ability and musical proficiency; Academic testing required; Audition required; Must be willing to participate in School choirs; No disciplinary issues allowed; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12; Requirements for each year level outlined in school policy
special_conditions: GVCC scholarships convert to vocal scholarships in Years 11-12. Recipients must assist the Anglican Church by providing trained choristers for St Paul's Cathedral's weekly services. Must maintain active participation in school choirs. Scholarships for music lessons must be conducted internally at the School. Scholarships cover tuition fees only; composite fees and Building Fund contributions are chargeable. If student withdraws before Year 12, parents must repay fee remission. Scholarships cannot be deferred.

overview: Lowther Hall offers Vocal Scholarships to recognise excellence in singing and vocal performance. These scholarships support talented vocalists who wish to develop their musical abilities through participation in the School's music programs.
description: Vocal scholarships are offered for entry into Years 5 or 7 for students wishing to participate in the school's vocal music programs. A range of discounts may be offered on academic tuition fees. Students must undergo academic testing conducted by Academic Assessment Services in February of the year prior to desired entry. Auditions for vocal scholarships occur in February of the year prior to the desired entry year, with a small panel of staff including the Director of Music and Principal. Scholarships cover fees to the end of VCE studies. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of examination results, auditions, interviews, previous school reports, and other information. Students must demonstrate commitment to vocal excellence and active participation in school music programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Must demonstrate vocal proficiency and singing ability; Academic testing required; Audition required; Must be active participant in school vocal programs; No disciplinary issues allowed; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships for vocal lessons must be conducted internally at the School with a Lowther Hall staff member. Funds will not be provided for music tuition outside of the School. Vocal scholarship recipients must maintain active participation in the vocal life of the School. Scholarships cover tuition fees only; composite fees and Building Fund contributions are chargeable. If student withdraws before Year 12, parents must repay fee remission received. Scholarships cannot be deferred.

overview: The First Nations Centenary Scholarship provides significant support to students from First Nations backgrounds who wish to commence at Lowther Hall. Established in 2020 to celebrate the School's 100th anniversary, this scholarship provides full fee remission through to the end of Year 12.
description: The First Nations Centenary Scholarship provides the opportunity for a student from a First Nations background to commence at Lowther Hall in Year 7, receiving full fee remission through to the end of Year 12. Established in 2020 to celebrate the School's 100th anniversary, the Centenary Scholarships are funded through the Lowther Hall Scholarship Trust, made possible by the generosity of the school community. This scholarship is designed to support Indigenous students and provide access to a quality education at Lowther Hall. To apply, prospective students and families should express their interest to the Director of Admissions, Ms Sandy Nelson at admissions@lowtherhall.vic.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be from a First Nations background; Entry into Year 7; Direct application to Director of Admissions required; Must remain enrolled until end of Year 12
special_conditions: This scholarship is not subject to the standard scholarship policy conditions. Established to celebrate the School's 100th anniversary. Funded through the Lowther Hall Scholarship Trust. Families should contact the Director of Admissions to express interest and discuss the application process.

overview: Luther College offers a number of highly competitive scholarships for academic excellence each year. These scholarships recognise and encourage academic potential and promote the pursuit of excellence in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarships provide part remission of annual fees.
description: Luther College is pleased to offer a number of scholarships for academic excellence each year to recognise outstanding academic achievement. These highly competitive scholarships recognise and encourage academic potential and promote the pursuit of excellence. The scholarships provide partial fee remission of annual fees. Selection is based on performance in the Edutest Academic Scholarship Exam, interview with the Principal, and consideration of NAPLAN results and school reports. The scholarship is tenable for the duration of a student's time at Luther College. Scholarship recipients must maintain ongoing academic progress and support of the College ethos throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents; Scholarship testing is optional; Students with an accepted position at Luther College may apply without taking the test
special_conditions: Scholarship testing is optional for students with an accepted position at Luther College. Shortlisted candidates may be required to attend an interview with the Principal. All scholarships offered at Luther College are awarded at the discretion of the Principal and all application details are confidential. Scholarship holders are expected to complete their secondary education at Luther College. The scholarship does not extend to other fees or costs such as VET, camp fees, music tuition, materials, or uniform.

overview: Luther College awards the Harald and Dodie Ziemer Scholarship to a student who displays the values of the College (Excellence, Integrity, Creativity, Service and Community) and demonstrates high academic potential. The scholarship is awarded to students whose family circumstances would otherwise not enable them to afford to attend Luther College. It is available to students entering any year level.
description: The Harald and Dodie Ziemer Scholarship is awarded each year to a prospective student of Luther College who demonstrates high academic potential and whose family circumstances would otherwise not enable them to afford to attend the College. The scholarship recognises students who display the College values of Excellence, Integrity, Creativity, Service and Community. The scholarship will usually provide tuition fee remission for a specified duration which is advertised at the time of application. Selection is based on a formal application, interview, NAPLAN results, school reports, and a completed Family Financial Details Form. Scholarship recipients are required to exhibit continued academic progress and support of the College ethos through the course of their enrolment at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering any year level; Must demonstrate high academic potential; Family circumstances must otherwise prevent attendance at the College; Applicants must submit formal application with Family Financial Details Form; Current and prospective students may apply
special_conditions: The scholarship does not extend to other fees or costs that may be associated with the recipient's time at the college, such as VET, camp fees, music tuition, materials, or uniform. The scholarship is awarded subject to ongoing educational achievement and enrolment at the college. Scholarship holders are expected to complete their secondary education at Luther College.

overview: Luther College may offer general excellence scholarships to eligible students annually. These scholarships recognise well-rounded achievement across academic, co-curricular and personal dimensions. The scholarships act as a credit against fees and provide fee reduction.
description: Luther College may offer general excellence scholarships to eligible students annually. These scholarships are designed to recognise and encourage students who demonstrate excellence across multiple dimensions including academic achievement, co-curricular participation, and personal character development. The scholarships provide a reduction of annual fees and act as a credit against fees otherwise owing. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in multiple areas and consideration of overall contribution to the school community. The scholarship is tenable for the duration of enrolment at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated excellence in academic and co-curricular areas; Strong personal character; Contribution to school community
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must maintain ongoing academic progress and support of the College ethos throughout their enrolment.

overview: Luther College may offer music scholarships to eligible students annually. These scholarships recognise demonstrated musical ability and achievement. The scholarships provide a reduction of annual fees and act as a credit against fees otherwise owing.
description: Luther College may offer music scholarships to eligible students annually. These scholarships are designed to recognise and encourage students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and achievement. The scholarships provide a reduction of annual fees and act as a credit against fees otherwise owing. Selection is based on demonstrated musical ability, typically through audition or portfolio of musical achievement. Recipients must maintain involvement in the school's music program and continue to demonstrate musical excellence throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability; Participation in music program; Commitment to ongoing musical development
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain involvement in school music program and continue to demonstrate musical excellence. Scholarships are offered at the discretion of the Principal.

program_name: ASPIRE (Advanced Support Program and Individual Recognition of Excellence)
overview: The Advanced Support Program and Individual Recognition of Excellence (ASPIRE) identifies, supports and recognises students of all year levels who are pursuing performance excellence in the fields of Performing Arts or Sport. The program provides recognition, support and development opportunities for elite performers.
description: ASPIRE is designed to identify, support and recognise students who are pursuing performance excellence in the fields of Performing Arts or Sport. The program has been developed to provide a clear and formalised approach to identifying, supporting and recognising students pursuing elite pathways in these areas. ASPIRE students have access to personal development opportunities through participation in workshops designed to help build mindset skills, understanding of high-performance psychology and connections with other like-minded individuals. The program feeds identified students into the Pastoral Care team to provide support while they combine their performance or sporting commitments with their academic pursuits. The program celebrates and recognises student achievements and milestones and strengthens community connections and partnerships.
eligibility_criteria: Pursuing performance excellence in Performing Arts or Sport; All year levels from Year 7 to Year 12; Identified by school based on demonstrated or potential elite performance in discipline
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students are identified by the school based on demonstrated or potential elite performance in their discipline. The program includes approximately 50 students across Years 7-12 covering disciplines including singing, dance, gymnastics, athletics, football, soccer, martial arts, basketball, netball, tennis and ice hockey.

overview: Lyrebird College offers Needs Based Bursaries to support students whose families would otherwise be unable to afford a Lyrebird College education. The bursaries are designed to remove financial barriers and ensure equitable access to the college's specialised ABA-based program for children with Autism Spectrum Disorder.
description: Lyrebird College is pleased to offer a number of Needs Based Bursaries to students whose families could not otherwise afford a Lyrebird College education. Bursaries of up to 50% of the annual College fees are payable for a specified duration. In exceptional circumstances the College may be in a position to offer more than a 50% reduction in fees. Applications will be considered the year before the required commencement year with all bursaries being means tested. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Lyrebird College reserves the right to not award bursaries in a given year. The ongoing receipt of a Needs Based Bursary will be subject to an annual financial review process. Applicants must submit the Enrolment Application Form, Bursary Application Form and Financial Questionnaire to be considered for a Needs Based Bursary.
eligibility_criteria: Must be eligible for enrolment at Lyrebird College; Must demonstrate financial need through means testing; Application must be submitted the year before the required commencement year; Must be eligible for ASD assessment and acceptance by the college
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Lyrebird College reserves the right to not award bursaries in a given year. The ongoing receipt of a Needs Based Bursary will be subject to an annual financial review process. In exceptional circumstances the College may offer more than 50% reduction in fees. Applications are considered the year before the required commencement year.

school_name: MacKillop Specialist School - Flexible Learning Centre Maidstone
MacKillop Specialist School - MacKillop Education Caulfield

overview: Maffra Secondary College awards the Dux of the College to the Year 12 student who achieves the highest Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR) in the Victorian Certificate of Education. This honour recognises exceptional academic achievement across all VCE subjects.
description: The Dux of the College is awarded annually to the Year 12 student achieving the highest ATAR score in the VCE. This is a prestigious honour recognising outstanding academic performance and dedication to learning throughout the senior years. Recent recipients have achieved ATAR scores ranging from 85.1 to 96.45. While no specific monetary value is attached to the Dux award itself, recipients often gain access to university scholarships and recognition opportunities. The award is presented at the end of the academic year following VCE completion and serves as recognition of the highest academic achievement in the cohort.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Must be enrolled in VCE; Must achieve the highest ATAR score in the cohort
special_conditions: Selection is automatic based on final VCE ATAR results. No application required.

program_name: Jakob and Jeannie Malmo Tertiary Scholarship
overview: Maffra Secondary College supports graduates pursuing higher education through the Jakob and Jeannie Malmo Tertiary Scholarship. This scholarship, valued at $30,000, is awarded annually to assist high-achieving graduates with the costs of relocating and studying at tertiary institutions, particularly benefiting rural and regional youth.
description: The Jakob and Jeannie Malmo Tertiary Scholarship is valued at $30,000 and is awarded to recent graduates of Maffra Secondary College pursuing tertiary education. The scholarship provides financial assistance to support students relocating to study at universities or other tertiary institutions, with particular emphasis on supporting rural and regional youth accessing higher education. Recipients have used the scholarship to support studies in fields including paramedicine and psychology at Melbourne institutions. The scholarship recognises educational excellence and assists with the transition from secondary to tertiary education, removing financial barriers for deserving graduates. The award is presented to one recipient annually.
eligibility_criteria: Recent graduate of Maffra Secondary College; Enrolled in or accepted to a tertiary education program; Demonstrated academic achievement; Preference for students relocating for study
special_conditions: Award is established to honour exemplary school values. Preference given to students pursuing studies in regional Victoria or relocating to Melbourne for tertiary education.

school_name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
overview: Maharishi School offers the Frances Clarke Scholarship to support primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time. The scholarship is awarded to children of parents who actively practise Transcendental Meditation. The scholarship recognises commitment to Consciousness-Based education and the school's foundational philosophy.
description: The Frances Clarke Scholarship provides a percentage of the School Tuition Fee reduction applied to each year of the student's primary schooling from Year Foundation (Prep) through Year 6. The scholarship is designed to support families committed to the practice of Transcendental Meditation and the principles of Consciousness-Based Education. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to these practices, evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment, academic achievement, social and emotional skills, and participation in school life. Recipients must maintain ongoing commitment to Consciousness-Based education and Transcendental Meditation throughout their enrolment, and agreement to the terms and conditions of the scholarship is required.
eligibility_criteria: Children of parents who actively practise Transcendental Meditation; Open to primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Ongoing commitment by the student to Consciousness-Based education and Transcendental Meditation; Evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment; Capacity and willingness to participate in all aspects of school life to enhance academic, social and emotional development; Positive attitude towards school and maintaining timely and high attendance; Commitment to achieving their best academically; Social and emotional skills aligned with school values of kindness, respect, compassion and acceptance; Effective skills of communication and cooperation; Agreement to the terms and conditions of the scholarship
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the duration of primary schooling. A selection panel will review applications and assess them against selection criteria. The panel may choose to shortlist applicants and invite those shortlisted to an interview. Applicants will be notified in writing of the outcome. Scholarship subject to ongoing commitment by student to Consciousness-Based education and Transcendental Meditation. One or preferably both of the student's parents must learn the practice of Transcendental Meditation. Children must begin the practice of the TM technique prior to entry.

school_name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
overview: Maharishi School offers the Reg Blow Scholarship to provide support and encouragement for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous students in accessing Consciousness-Based Education.
description: The Reg Blow Scholarship provides a percentage of the School Tuition Fee reduction applied to each year of the student's primary schooling from Year Foundation (Prep) through Year 6. This scholarship is specifically designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and their families in accessing the school's unique alternative education approach. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to achieving their best academically, possess social and emotional skills aligned with school values, effective communication and cooperation skills, a positive attitude towards school with timely attendance, capacity to participate in all aspects of school life, and evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment. Selection is based on comprehensive assessment against established criteria by a selection panel.
eligibility_criteria: Identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Commitment to achieving their best academically; Social and emotional skills aligned with school values of kindness, respect, compassion and acceptance; Effective skills of communication and cooperation; Has been an outstanding ambassador for pre-school/primary school and role model for others; Evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment; Capacity and willingness to participate in all aspects of school life to enhance academic, social and emotional development; Positive attitude towards school and maintaining timely and high attendance; Agreement to the terms and conditions of the scholarship
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the duration of primary schooling. A selection panel will review applications and assess them against selection criteria. The selection panel includes representatives of the School's Senior Management Team and Board. The panel may choose to shortlist applicants and invite those shortlisted to an interview. Applicants will be notified in writing of the outcome. Scholarship subject to ongoing commitment to school ethos and conduct standards.

school_name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
overview: Maharishi School offers the Elsie Coates Scholarship to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time. The scholarship recognises Indigenous students seeking access to the school's Consciousness-Based Education approach.
description: The Elsie Coates Scholarship provides a percentage of the School Tuition Fee reduction applied to each year of the student's primary schooling from Year Foundation (Prep) through Year 6. This scholarship specifically targets Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and their families to facilitate access to Maharishi School's alternative education model. Recipients must demonstrate strong academic commitment, social and emotional development, positive school engagement, and willingness to participate fully in all aspects of school life. The selection process involves comprehensive review by a selection panel that includes school leadership and board representatives. Successful applicants will have shown evidence of transition readiness, communication skills, and community leadership potential.
eligibility_criteria: Identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Commitment to achieving their best academically; Social and emotional skills aligned with school values of kindness, respect, compassion and acceptance; Effective skills of communication and cooperation; Has been an outstanding ambassador for pre-school/primary school and role model for others; Evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment; Capacity and willingness to participate in all aspects of school life to enhance academic, social and emotional development; Positive attitude towards school and maintaining timely and high attendance; Agreement to the terms and conditions of the scholarship
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the duration of primary schooling. A selection panel will review applications and assess them against selection criteria. The selection panel includes representatives of the School's Senior Management Team and Board. The panel may choose to shortlist applicants and invite those shortlisted to an interview. Applicants will be notified in writing of the outcome. Scholarship conditional upon continued academic engagement and positive participation in school community.

school_name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
overview: Maharishi School offers the Financial Hardship Bursary to provide support and assistance for primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time whose families satisfy the school's criteria for financial support. The bursary is designed to remove financial barriers to accessing Consciousness-Based Education.
description: The Financial Hardship Bursary provides a percentage of the School Tuition Fee reduction applied to each year of the student's primary schooling from Year Foundation (Prep) through Year 6. Unlike scholarships, bursaries are conditional on an annual review of the family's financial position, requiring submission of supporting evidence to determine continuation of the bursary award. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to achieving their best academically, possess appropriate social and emotional skills, effective communication and cooperation skills, positive school attitude with good attendance, and evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment. The bursary is awarded to successful applicants who can demonstrate both financial need and potential to thrive in the school environment.
eligibility_criteria: Children of parents satisfying the school's criteria for financial support; Open to primary school students enrolling at the school for the first time; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Consideration given to applicants who can display potential to thrive in the primary school environment and who would benefit from financial support; Commitment to achieving their best academically; Social and emotional skills aligned with school values of kindness, respect, compassion and acceptance; Effective skills of communication and cooperation; Evidence of ability to successfully transition into the primary school environment; Capacity and willingness to participate in all aspects of school life to enhance academic, social and emotional development; Positive attitude towards school and maintaining timely and high attendance; Agreement to the terms and conditions of the bursary
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded for the duration of primary schooling but are conditional on annual review of the family's financial position. Recipients must submit supporting evidence annually to determine continuation of the bursary. Bursary may be withdrawn if family circumstances improve significantly or if student conduct or attendance standards are not maintained. Students must maintain good standing and positive participation in school life. Agreement to the terms and conditions of the bursary is required.

school_name: MacKillop Catholic Regional College - St Mary's Campus
Mahogany Rise Primary School-Mahogany Rise Student Development Centre

overview: Mansfield Rudolf Steiner School offers confidential financial assistance scholarships to families based on individual financial circumstances. These scholarships are designed to support families who would otherwise face barriers to enrolment at the school.
description: Mansfield Rudolf Steiner School provides financial assistance scholarships that are confidentially assessed based on the individual financial circumstances of families. The scholarships aim to make education accessible to families experiencing financial difficulties. Applications can be made by contacting the Operations Manager at the school. The assessment process is handled with strict confidentiality to respect the privacy of applicant families. As of 2025, the school has decided to phase out merit-based scholarships and is focusing on need-based financial assistance programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families with demonstrated financial need; Application by contacting Operations Manager; Confidential financial assessment required; Based on individual family circumstances
special_conditions: As of 2025, the school has decided to phase out merit-based scholarships and is transitioning to focus on need-based financial assistance. Assessments are handled confidentially.

overview: Mansfield Rudolf Steiner School offers short-term financial assistance to current families experiencing emergency or family crisis that impacts their capacity to meet expected school fee payments. This program provides immediate financial support during unexpected hardships.
description: Short-term financial assistance may be sought by current families in an emergency or family crisis that impacts their financial capacity to meet the expected school fee payments. This program is designed to provide temporary relief to existing students whose families face sudden financial difficulties or unexpected circumstances. The assistance is administered confidentially through the school's Operations Manager. This support is distinct from the longer-term need-based scholarships and is specifically tailored for emergency situations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current families only; Experiencing emergency or family crisis; Financial capacity impacted by unexpected circumstances; Must be enrolled at school
special_conditions: Available only to current families; Limited to emergency or crisis situations; Temporary/short-term assistance only

program_name: Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship
overview: Manor Lakes P-12 College offers the Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship program to help create better futures for children and families facing financial hardship. The scholarship provides financial assistance to help families afford the cost of their child's education and access to Smith Family educational programs.
description: The Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship program helps create better futures for children at Manor Lakes College by providing financial assistance to help families afford the cost of their child's education. The program also provides access to Smith Family educational programs to help children get the most out of their school years. Eligibility requires possession of a Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card and maintaining regular school attendance of 80% and above. The scholarship duration extends for the period of enrolment at Manor Lakes College.
eligibility_criteria: In possession of a Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; Have regular school attendance of 80% and above; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Requires financial documentation demonstrating financial hardship. School can submit applications on behalf of families.

overview: Manor Lakes P-12 College offers a selective entry Enrichment Program for high-ability learners in Years 7-9. The program creates a challenging and engaging curriculum for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and operate significantly above year level expectations.
description: The Enrichment Program at Manor Lakes P-12 College is a select-entry program designed for high-ability learners operating significantly above year level in Years 7-9. Only one homegroup of students in each year level is selected each year. The program provides an advanced curriculum tailored to individual needs, with students studying accelerated content across multiple subject areas. Eligibility requires strong academic performance across multiple learning areas, excellent behaviour, and outstanding attendance. Selection involves a multi-step process including teacher recommendations, school assessment data review (NAPLAN, reports, grades), submission of an Expression of Interest form with teacher references, and completion of the EduTest standardized assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at Manor Lakes College; Demonstrate strong academic performance (A+ to A grades) across multiple learning areas; Achieve results at or above state expectations on NAPLAN; Excellent school attendance; Outstanding behaviour and conduct; Pass the EduTest standardized assessment
special_conditions: Students must sign an Enrichment contract upon selection to secure their place. Participants are expected to be role models within the college. Students participate in alternative co-curricular learning including mandatory music component. Selection is reviewed if students do not maintain expectations. Students participate in leadership roles and represent the enrichment program within the college community.

overview: Manor Lakes P-12 College offers a Sports Academy program providing select-entry access to elite sporting pathways. The academy caters to students demonstrating exceptional athletic abilities and dedicates them to sport excellence across seven sports disciplines.
description: The Sports Academy at Manor Lakes P-12 College is an extracurricular sporting program launched in 2021 focusing on student athlete development and sporting career pathways. The program operates across seven sports: Rugby League, Australian Rules Football, Netball, Basketball, Soccer, Cricket, and Volleyball. Students must demonstrate excellent athletic abilities, outstanding behaviour, and dedication to academics to participate. The program provides access to elite-level coaching from professional coaches and partnerships with professional organizations including the Melbourne Storm and Werribee Football Club.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate excellent athletic abilities in one or more sports; Maintain outstanding behaviour and conduct; Demonstrate dedication to academics; Pass select entry process; Must be capable of balancing sporting commitments with academic requirements
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must operate under the Sports Academy motto: 'dream of a better future, believe in a pathway to it, achieve greatness through hard work.' Program may include opportunities for qualifications, coaching certifications, and aspirational career talks with professional players and development officers. Requires commitment to training sessions and team participation.

overview: Manor Lakes P-12 College offers an AFL Academy program in partnership with Werribee Football Club (VFL). The academy supports talented football students with elite-level coaching and pathways into professional player development programs.
description: The AFL Academy at Manor Lakes P-12 College operates in partnership with the Werribee Football Club (VFL) and is affiliated with the North Melbourne Next Generation Academy and Western Jets. Students receive elite-level coaching from ex-AFL players including Michael Barlow, access to brand new elite training facilities, and specialized strength and conditioning programs. The academy provides career development opportunities both on and off the field, including mentoring and player development pathways. Selection requires demonstrated football talent and capacity to participate in an intensive training program.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate exceptional Australian Rules Football talent; Maintain outstanding behaviour and conduct; Demonstrate academic commitment; Pass the select entry process for the academy
special_conditions: Partnership includes access to links with professional AFL development programs. Students participate in mentoring and professional coach sessions. May have opportunities for qualifications in coaching.

overview: Manor Lakes P-12 College offers a Rugby League Academy in partnership with the Melbourne Storm. The academy fosters rugby league talent through elite coaching and development opportunities for secondary students.
description: The Rugby League Academy at Manor Lakes P-12 College operates in partnership with the Melbourne Storm. The academy aims to foster and grow rugby league talent, knowledge, and skills. Students receive elite-level coaching from NRL Victoria coaches, female-specific training sessions, and opportunities to engage in aspirational career talks from development officers and high-profile players. The program provides students with opportunities to compete in NRL game development competitions and offers the chance to complete one or more qualifications in rugby league or related coaching disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate exceptional rugby league talent; Maintain outstanding behaviour and conduct; Show academic dedication; Pass the select entry process for the academy
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Partnership with Melbourne Storm provides professional coaching and development officer mentoring. Students complete qualifications in rugby league skills and coaching. Female-specific training sessions available. Participation requires commitment to training schedules and game development competitions.

overview: State Schools' Relief provides financial support to Victorian government school students facing hardship. Manor Lakes P-12 College assists eligible families by submitting applications on their behalf through the SSR online portal.
description: State Schools' Relief (SSR) is a government support program that provides assistance to any Victorian student attending a government school (primary, secondary or specialist) when there is evidence of financial need. The school can submit applications directly via the online portal whenever they identify a student whose family or carers are facing difficulty in providing appropriate uniforms, footwear, or educational items for attending school. Support covers essential school items including uniforms, footwear, and educational materials. Applications may be submitted for situations including unemployment, health issues/illness, financial difficulties, independent living and homelessness, natural disasters or house fires, bereavement, and domestic violence.
eligibility_criteria: Attending Manor Lakes P-12 College as an enrolled student; Family or carers facing financial difficulty; Need for uniform, footwear, or educational items; Evidence of hardship including unemployment, health issues, financial difficulty, or other specified hardship situations
special_conditions: School must identify and recommend eligible students. Applications submitted through school's SSR online portal. Assessment of genuine financial need required. Support is provided to help students access essential items for school attendance.

overview: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) provides financial assistance to eligible Victorian government school families to enable student participation in school camps, sports, and excursion activities.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian government program providing financial assistance to eligible families to support their children's participation in school-based camps, sports activities, and excursions. Manor Lakes P-12 College provides application forms to eligible families. Applications can be downloaded from www.education.vic.gov.au/csef or requested from the Burley Griffin office. To be eligible, families must hold a current Health Care Card, Pension Concession Card, or the student must have been approved for the Student Start-Up Scholarship or the Educational Maintenance Allowance. The fund aims to remove financial barriers to student participation in camp and sporting activities.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a current Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; Or have student approved for Student Start-Up Scholarship or Educational Maintenance Allowance; Enrolled at Manor Lakes P-12 College
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted through the CSEF online system. Assistance covers eligible camp and excursion fees. Must meet financial hardship eligibility criteria. Annual application required.

overview: The Jennifer Munyard Scholarship provides girls and boys who have experienced significant trauma the opportunity to be educated in a safe learning environment at Maranatha Christian School. The scholarship provides financial support to enable students who would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education to enrol at the school. It honours the legacy of Jennifer Munyard, a highly regarded Secondary School Teacher and Pastoral Care Leader at Maranatha from 2009 to 2017.
description: The Jennifer Munyard Scholarship is designed to support students who have experienced significant trauma and would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education at Maranatha Christian School. Applications are assessed by a selection panel according to criteria. The specific financial value of the scholarship is determined on a case-by-case basis and varies according to individual family circumstances and available fund balance. Recipients must demonstrate that they have experienced significant trauma and demonstrate financial hardship. The scholarship is funded through tax-deductible donations and contributions to the Jennifer Munyard Scholarship Fund. Applications are submitted via completed scholarship form with supporting documents to the Enrolments team.
eligibility_criteria: Students who have experienced significant trauma; Families demonstrating financial need unable to afford Christian education; Open to all year levels at Maranatha
special_conditions: Scholarship applications are assessed by a selection panel according to criteria. Recipients must provide evidence of significant trauma experienced. Selection is based on demonstrated financial hardship and need. The scholarship is funded through philanthropic contributions and tax-deductible donations. Enrolments team reviews all applications and supporting documentation.

overview: Marcellin College offers the St Marcellin Champagnat Bursary to assist families experiencing financial distress in meeting tuition costs. The bursary reflects the College's commitment to ensuring that financial circumstances do not prevent capable students from accessing a Catholic education at Marcellin.
description: The St Marcellin Champagnat Bursary is designed to assist families that may be experiencing financial distress to pay tuition fees in the absence of some form of assistance. Marcellin College acknowledges that parents and guardians make significant sacrifices to provide their sons with a Catholic education, and the College's policy is that it should never be a family's desire to reach the point of financial distress to meet the cost of enrolment. The bursary provides financial support to eligible families. Applicants must complete a formal application form, which is available from the College. All applications are treated confidentially. Families should contact the Finance Office on 0398511518 to discuss their situation and determine the best path forward for assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial distress; Open to all current and prospective students; Must complete formal application form; Applications treated confidentially
special_conditions: All applications are treated confidentially. The College encourages families to contact the Finance Office to discuss their situation prior to formal application.

overview: Maranatha Christian School offers the Jennifer Munyard Scholarship to provide financial support to students who have experienced significant trauma and who would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education. The scholarship is named after Jennifer Munyard, a highly regarded Secondary School Teacher and Pastoral Care Leader at Maranatha from 2009 to 2017, and provides girls and boys with the opportunity to be educated in a safe learning environment at the school.
description: The Jennifer Munyard Scholarship provides financial support to students who have experienced significant trauma and would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education. The exact financial benefit and duration are determined by the selection panel based on individual circumstances and the fund available. The selection process involves submitting a completed scholarship form with supporting documents to the Enrolment team for assessment according to established criteria. All reports are treated with the highest level of confidentiality throughout the application process. The scholarship is administered through the school's support mechanisms and may be reviewed periodically to ensure appropriate allocation of resources.
eligibility_criteria: Student has experienced significant trauma; Family cannot otherwise afford Christian education; Students of any year level may apply; Open to both boys and girls; Application requires submission of scholarship form and supporting documents
special_conditions: The scholarship provides financial support specifically for students who have experienced significant trauma. Recipient families are required to support the school's Christian ethos and educational mission. All applications and supporting documentation are treated with the highest level of confidentiality. Named in honour of Jennifer Munyard, who was remembered as a selfless educator with an incredible heart for vulnerable children. Donations to the fund are tax deductible.

school_name: Mansfield Secondary College-Mansfield Secondary Mt Buller Annexe

overview: Maribyrnong College offers 75 sports scholarships annually for Year 7 entry through the Maribyrnong Sports Academy, Victoria's only government-funded elite sports academy. The scholarship recognises talented athletes and provides a high-performance environment balancing sporting excellence with academic achievement.
description: MSA Year 7 scholarships fund the majority of costs through Department of Education and Training (DET) support, providing access to sport and training facilities, high-performance coaching, strength and conditioning, rehabilitation services, physiotherapy, sports nutrition, performance psychology and wellbeing support. Scholarship does not fund full program costs, with additional annual fees including Sport Academy Annual Fee ($450), MSA Uniform ($550) and school parent payments. Selection is based on existing sport performance, potential for development, academic achievement (B grade average or above), effort and behaviour. The application process commences when students are in Grade 6, with applications opening in Term 1 and closing by end of March. Selection trials occur in Term 2, with offers made by end of Term 2.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students; Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate current sporting talent and potential for high-performance development; Achieving B grade (very good) academic average; Academic effort and behavioural indicators must be high; Student-athletes NOT required to live in local area; Must be able to commit to high-performance training schedule
special_conditions: Student-athletes are reviewed every 12 months to determine whether they retain their Sports Academy scholarship for the following year. Consideration is based on sport performance pathway progression, academic progress (minimum C grade average), behavioural expectations, school engagement and MSA activity participation. Students failing to make satisfactory academic progress will be placed on an improvement program and may be suspended or removed if progress does not improve. Any student removed for agreement breaches will be permitted to continue education at Maribyrnong College. Students must maintain satisfactory rate of academic progress and a positive attitude to studies. Scholarship subject to annual review. MSA has a gender target of 50% male and 50% female for selections. MSA welcomes applications from students with eligible impairments for para-sport pathways.

overview: Maribyrnong College offers 26 sports scholarships annually for Year 9 entry through the Maribyrnong Sports Academy. The scholarship recognises talented student-athletes and provides a high-performance environment for those demonstrating both sporting potential and academic commitment.
description: MSA Year 9 scholarships provide funding through Department of Education and Training (DET) support covering majority of scholarship costs including sport and training facilities, high-performance coaching, strength and conditioning, rehabilitation services, physiotherapy, sports nutrition, performance psychology and wellbeing support. Scholarship does not fund full program costs, with additional annual fees including Sport Academy Annual Fee ($450), MSA Uniform ($550) and school parent payments. Entry into Year 9 is highly competitive with most successful applicants participating at junior state or national level. Selection is based on existing performance, potential for development in high-performance sport, balance between sport and academic performance, effort and behaviour. Application process commences when students are in Year 8, with applications opening in Term 2 and closing mid-June. Selection trials occur in Term 3, with offers made by end of Term 3.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8 students; Open to students entering Year 9; Must be participating at junior state or national level or equivalent in their sport; Current sport achievements and coach contact details required; Selection based on sporting potential and academic suitability; High academic effort and behavioural indicators required; May only apply for one sport due to limited places; Student-athletes NOT required to live in local area
special_conditions: MSA has a gender target of 50% male and 50% female for selections. As part of targeted focus on developing performance pathways for para-athletes, with support from Paralympics Australia, MSA welcomes applications from students with eligible impairments for para-sport. Entry is highly competitive; applicants generally participating at junior state or national level. Student-athletes are reviewed every 12 months to determine whether they retain their Sports Academy scholarship for the following year. Students failing to make satisfactory academic progress will be placed on improvement program and may be suspended or removed if progress does not improve. Selection is based not only on existing performance but potential for development in high-performance sport as well as suitability for balance between sport and academic performance, effort and behaviour.

overview: Maribyrnong College offers the Advanced Curriculum Enrichment (ACE) program to high-achieving students entering Year 7. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional intellectual, creative and critical thinking abilities and provides accelerated learning opportunities.
description: The ACE program is a selective entry program for high-achieving students that allows them to complete most of their Year 7-10 studies across the first three years of secondary schooling at Maribyrnong College. The program develops students' skills in independent learning, higher-order thinking and social and emotional wellbeing by exposing them to greater breadth and depth of curriculum. Students are selected primarily on their intellectual, creative and critical thinking abilities. The College encourages high-achieving ACE students to select a VCE subject as part of their Year 10 program, allowing them to accelerate in subjects where they achieve success and maximise opportunities for academic achievement across VCE. The selection process involves assessment of academic ability and potential, with specific details regarding application procedures and selection criteria available through the College Prospectus.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated exceptional intellectual, creative and critical thinking abilities; High academic potential; Students may apply for both Sports Academy and ACE program; Selection based on intellectual, creative and critical thinking abilities
special_conditions: This is an accelerated program allowing students to compress Years 7-10 curriculum into three years. Information on how to apply for ACE is provided to MSA scholarship offer recipients. Refer to College Prospectus page 4 for additional information. This program is for high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential.

overview: Maribyrnong College offers the ATAR Maximisation Program (AMP), a selective program for high-achieving Year 11 students. The program enables students to accelerate into two Unit 3+4 VCE subjects to maximise academic achievement and ATAR outcomes.
description: AMP is a selective program designed for high-achieving Year 11 students to accelerate their VCE studies by enrolling in two Unit 3+4 subjects. This allows students to demonstrate mastery in advanced curriculum areas and maximise their ATAR outcomes. The program supports students in achieving academic excellence by providing acceleration opportunities in subjects where they excel. Students enrolled in ACE program may transition to AMP in Year 11 as part of their accelerated pathway through the college curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; High academic achievement demonstrated in previous years; Selection as a high-achieving student; Recommendation or qualification through prior accelerated programs; Demonstrated ability to manage Unit 3+4 subject load
special_conditions: AMP is a selective program for students entering Year 11. Program allows acceleration into two Unit 3+4 subjects. Selection is based on demonstrated high academic achievement and suitability for advanced VCE study.

overview: Merbein P-10 College offers Scholarships for Academic Achievement to recognise and reward students entering Year 7 with strong academic capabilities. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of overall academic performance to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement.
description: The Scholarships for Academic Achievement program provides recognition to students with strong academic capabilities entering Year 7. Two scholarships are awarded annually under this category. Each scholarship is valued at $350 and is provided as a Merbein P-10 College voucher, which can be used to cover the cost of uniforms purchased from the school's Uniform Shop, camp costs, and/or school fees. The scholarship recognises overall academic performance and is designed to support students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in their studies. Applications are submitted online and are evaluated on the basis of academic merit and overall performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Strong academic capabilities and performance required
special_conditions: Scholarships are paid as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. Successful applicants are advised at the Year 7 transition day.

overview: Merbein P-10 College offers Scholarships for Excellence in Citizenship to recognise students who demonstrate high levels of participation in school and community activities. These scholarships reward students who are positive role models to their peers through their involvement and commitment.
description: The Scholarships for Excellence in Citizenship program recognises students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high levels of participation in a range of school and community activities. Two scholarships are awarded annually to students who serve as positive role models to their peers. Each scholarship is valued at $350 and is provided as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. The selection process evaluates the applicant's involvement in school and community activities, leadership qualities, and positive contribution to the school community. Applications are submitted online and assessed on demonstrated citizenship and community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated high-level participation in school and community activities; Positive role model to peers
special_conditions: Scholarships are paid as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. Selection based on demonstrated participation in school and community activities.

overview: Merbein P-10 College offers a Scholarship for Sporting Excellence to recognise students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high levels of sporting ability, potential, and commitment. This scholarship rewards students who show exceptional prowess and dedication to sports.
description: The Scholarship for Sporting Excellence program recognises students entering Year 7 who demonstrate a high level of sporting prowess, potential, and commitment to their chosen sport or sports. One scholarship is awarded annually to a student who exhibits outstanding sporting ability and dedication. The scholarship is valued at $350 and is provided as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. The selection process evaluates the student's sporting achievements, potential for further development, level of commitment to their sport, and demonstrated excellence in sporting pursuits. Applications are submitted online and assessed based on sporting merit and achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated high level of sporting prowess and potential; High level of sporting commitment required
special_conditions: Scholarships are paid as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. Selection based on demonstrated sporting excellence and commitment.

overview: Merbein P-10 College offers a Scholarship for Music/Performing Arts to recognise and support students entering Year 7 with talent and dedication in music or performing arts. This scholarship rewards students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to these disciplines.
description: The Scholarship for Music/Performing Arts program recognises students entering Year 7 who demonstrate talent and dedication in the areas of music or performing arts. One scholarship is awarded annually to a student who exhibits exceptional ability and commitment in music, drama, dance, or other performing arts disciplines. The scholarship is valued at $350 and is provided as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. The selection process evaluates the student's demonstrated talent, level of dedication to their chosen performing arts, artistic achievements, and potential for further development. Applications are submitted online and assessed based on demonstrated excellence and commitment to music or performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated talent and dedication in music or performing arts; Evidence of artistic achievement or potential required
special_conditions: Scholarships are paid as a Merbein P-10 College voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees. Selection based on demonstrated talent and dedication to music or performing arts.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers the Yanaya Watnanda 'Walking Together' Scholarship to support young people identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander who wish to join the MRC community. The scholarship is awarded to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12 and provides comprehensive financial and resource support.
description: The Yanaya Watnanda 'Walking Together' Scholarship supports any young person identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander wishing to join the Mercy Regional College community. The scholarship is awarded to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12. The scholarship ensures full fee and levy provision, and assistance with the provision of uniforms, textbooks, and laptop. This comprehensive support recognises the importance of removing financial barriers to education for Indigenous students. Students interested in applying should contact the College Registrar to express their interest and begin the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to students entering or currently enrolled Years 7 to 12; Students must be accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled at Mercy Regional College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship includes full fee and levy provision, uniform assistance, textbook provision, and laptop provision. Students must remain enrolled at Mercy Regional College.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers the Year 10 MacBook Scholarship to Year 9 students who have enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year. This scholarship recognises financial need and provides each recipient with a new MacBook for the remainder of their schooling at the college.
description: The Year 10 MacBook Scholarship is awarded to a Year 9 student who has enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year. This scholarship is based on financial need and provides the student with a new MacBook for the remainder of their schooling at Mercy Regional College. The MacBook is provided as technology support to enable full participation in the college's learning programs. The scholarship recognises the importance of equitable access to digital learning tools and supports students whose families may face financial barriers to purchasing necessary technology. Recipients benefit from this non-fee-based financial assistance throughout their remaining years at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 9 student; Must have enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year; Based on demonstrated financial need
special_conditions: Award provided as a new MacBook laptop for the remainder of the student's enrolment at Mercy Regional College. Technology provided rather than cash assistance.

overview: Mernda Hills offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship provides a tuition fee discount and is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability based on previous results, referrals, awards and testing.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is aimed at students entering Year 7 and Year 10, and provides a discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 and the end of Year 12 respectively. Successful applicants receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period at a value defined by the level of award, with this being determined by the scholarship committee. Recipients must demonstrate high level of academic achievement, as evidenced by previous academic results, referrals, awards and testing. Recipients must maintain an 'Above Standard' academic performance across all core subjects (English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science and Humanities) and must continuously display College values through their behaviour. Selection involves completion of an online application form, potential academic testing, and an interview at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be currently enrolled or have active application for future enrolment; Must demonstrate high level of academic achievement through previous results, referrals, awards and testing; Must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance across all core subjects; Must continuously display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients demonstrate excellence and commit to contributing to college life while supporting its Christian ethos. Recipients of a Mernda Hills Christian College scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Scholarships are awarded based on merit, judged by the strength of the candidate's application, references and school records.

overview: Mernda Hills offers the Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarship to recognise significant achievement in co-curricular and social justice activities for Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship provides a tuition fee discount for students who excel at elite level across a range of interests or contribute exceptional value to their school or church community.
description: The Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarship is aimed at students entering Year 7 and Year 10, and provides a discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 and the end of Year 12 respectively. Successful applicants receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period at a value defined by the level of award, with this being determined by the scholarship committee. Applicants must demonstrate a significant level of achievement in a particular co-curricular or social justice activity. They should excel in a wide range of interests and talents (normally at an elite level) or contribute exceptional value to their school or church community. Recipients must maintain an 'At Standard or Above' academic performance across all core subjects (English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science and Humanities). Selection involves completion of an online application form, potential testing, and an interview at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be currently enrolled or have active application for future enrolment; Must demonstrate significant achievement in co-curricular or social justice activity; Must excel at elite level or contribute exceptional value to school or church community; Must maintain 'At Standard or Above' academic performance across all core subjects; Must continuously display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients demonstrate excellence and commit to contributing to college life while supporting its Christian ethos. Recipients of a Mernda Hills Christian College scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Scholarships are awarded based on merit, judged by the strength of the candidate's application, references and school records.

overview: Mernda Hills offers the Leadership Excellence Scholarship to senior students who are passionate about leadership and seeking opportunities to grow their skills and understanding of leadership. The scholarship provides a tuition fee discount for up to three years.
description: The Leadership Excellence Scholarship is aimed at senior students who are passionate about leadership and are looking for opportunities to grow their skills and understanding of leadership. It provides a discount on tuition fees for up to three years. Successful applicants receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period at a value defined by the level of award, with this being determined by the scholarship committee. Applicants are expected to be actively leading in a club, project or initiative, and preferably will be currently involved in leadership roles within their school. Recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Selection involves completion of an online application form, potential testing, and an interview at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Senior year level students; Must be currently enrolled or have active application for future enrolment; Must be passionate about leadership; Must be actively leading in a club, project or initiative; Preferably currently involved in leadership roles within school; Must maintain consistently high academic efforts; Must display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients demonstrate excellence and commit to contributing to college life while supporting its Christian ethos. Recipients of a Mernda Hills Christian College scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Scholarship is offered for up to three years. Scholarships are awarded based on merit, judged by the strength of the candidate's application, references and school records.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers the Equity Scholarship to support students demonstrating financial need. This scholarship is awarded to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12 and provides assistance with fees and levies on a need basis.
description: The Equity Scholarship is awarded to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12. This scholarship is based on financial need and provides assistance with fees and levies, either partially or fully. The scholarship recognises families facing financial difficulty and ensures that no student is denied a Catholic education because of inability to pay fees. Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial hardship and submit a Fee Concession Application through the Business Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Must demonstrate financial need; Families experiencing financial hardship; Australian residents preferred
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship based on financial need. Fee Concession Application required. As a Catholic College, Mercy Regional College has a long-established policy that no family is denied a Catholic Education because of their inability to pay fees. Selection based on demonstrated financial hardship.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers the Yanaya Watnanda 'Walking Together' Scholarship to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students wishing to join the MRC community. This scholarship ensures comprehensive support including full fees, levies, uniforms, textbooks, and laptop provision for all years 7 to 12.
description: The Yanaya Watnanda 'Walking Together' Scholarship supports any young person identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander wishing to join the Mercy Regional College community. The scholarship is awarded to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12. The scholarship ensures full fee and levy provision, and the assistance of uniform, textbooks, and laptop provision. This comprehensive support demonstrates the College's commitment to closing the educational gap and creating an inclusive and culturally safe environment for Indigenous students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students identifying as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to students accepted for enrolment or currently enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Willingness to engage with MRC community; Australian Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander background required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship specifically for Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students. Includes full fees, levies, uniforms, textbooks, and laptop provision for duration of enrolment. Designed to support the educational achievement and cultural identity of Indigenous students at Mercy Regional College.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers the Year 10 MacBook Scholarship to support students entering Year 10 with a new computer device. This scholarship is based on financial need and is awarded to Year 9 students who have enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year.
description: The Year 10 MacBook Scholarship is awarded to a Year 9 student who has enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year. This scholarship is based on financial need and provides the student with a new MacBook for the remainder of their schooling at Mercy Regional College. The device supports digital learning and ensures equitable access to technology for students progressing through the senior years of secondary education. The MacBook provision recognises the essential role of technology in contemporary education and removes barriers to learning for students facing financial difficulty.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 9 student; Must have enrolled for Year 10 in the following school year; Must demonstrate financial need; Preference for students who would otherwise lack access to appropriate technology
special_conditions: Awarded to Year 9 students transitioning to Year 10 only. MacBook provided for remainder of schooling at Mercy Regional College (Years 10-12, approximately 3 years). Selection based on demonstrated financial need and lack of access to appropriate technology. Device remains property of the College.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers a Fee Concession Program to support families experiencing financial hardship in meeting their fee obligations. The College operates on the principle that no family should be denied a Catholic education because of their inability to pay fees.
description: The College understands that some families may be experiencing financial hardship and may find it difficult to meet their fee obligations. As a Catholic College, Mercy Regional College has a long-established policy that no family is denied a Catholic Education because of their inability to pay fees. Families are requested to contact the Business Manager to share their situation and depending on circumstances submit a Fee Concession Application. The program provides flexible fee arrangements and potential fee reductions or deferrals based on individual family circumstances. This is a rolling application process available throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled families; Must demonstrate financial hardship; Must contact Business Manager to initiate process; Assessment based on individual family circumstances; No specific income threshold but genuine hardship required
special_conditions: Rolling application process - families can apply at any time. Fee Concession Application must be submitted to Business Manager. Decision based on demonstrated financial hardship and individual family circumstances. College policy ensures no student is denied Catholic education due to inability to pay fees. Confidential assessment process.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers sibling discounts to families with multiple children enrolled at the College. This automatic fee discount recognises the financial impact of educating multiple children and provides fee relief from the third enrolled child onwards.
description: Mercy Regional College provides automatic fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled in the same year. For a 3rd child enrolled at the College in the year, $700 will be discounted against the fees. For a 4th and subsequent children enrolled at Mercy Regional College in the year, $3,240 will be discounted for each additional student. These discounts are automatically applied to fees and do not require a separate application. The sibling discount is a standard college policy designed to support families with multiple children in secondary education.
