eligibility_criteria: Must have 2 or more siblings enrolled at Mercy Regional College in the same year; Applied automatically to fees; No application required; Third child receives $700 discount; Fourth and subsequent children receive $3,240 discount per child
special_conditions: Automatically applied - no application required. Third child: $700 discount per year. Fourth and subsequent children: $3,240 discount per year per child. Discounts apply to year of concurrent enrolment only. Not cumulative with other discounts except early payment discount.

overview: Mercy Regional College offers an Early Payment Discount to families who complete their full fee payments by March 31st. This discount rewards prompt payment and provides immediate fee relief to supporting families.
description: Families completing their full fee payments by March 31st each year will receive a $150 discount on fees for each student. This early payment discount incentivises timely fee payment and provides immediate financial relief to families managing their school budget. The discount is automatically applied to accounts that meet the payment deadline and represents approximately 3-5% savings depending on total fees charged. This is a simple, transparent way to reduce overall annual education costs.
eligibility_criteria: Full fee payment must be made by 31 March of the school year; Discount applied automatically upon payment; Open to all enrolled families; No application required
special_conditions: Payment deadline is 31 March each school year. Discount automatically applied upon full payment. Applies to each student enrolled. Can be combined with sibling discounts but not other financial assistance. Encourages forward planning and budget management by families.

overview: Mercy Regional College facilitates access to the Victorian State Government's Camps, Sports & Excursions Fund (CSEF) for eligible families. CSEF provides annual grant funding to assist eligible families cover the cost of school camps and excursions.
description: The State Government provides the Camps, Sports & Excursions Fund (CSEF) which assists eligible families to cover the cost of school camps and excursions. If a family holds a valid means-tested concession card, they may be eligible for CSEF. The annual amount provided for secondary students is $400. Students with eligible family circumstances can access this government assistance to participate fully in school-based excursion and camp programs without financial barrier. Interested families should contact the College office to obtain an application form.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid means-tested concession card; Victorian State Government eligibility requirements must be met; Secondary student annual amount: $400; Families must contact school to obtain application form
special_conditions: State government program administered through the school. Requires valid means-tested concession card. Annual amount: $400 per secondary student. Families must apply through school with supporting documentation. Government eligibility criteria apply. Not a school scholarship but school-facilitated access to government assistance.

overview: Mildura Christian College offers scholarships to families struggling with financial hardship who cannot afford the school fees. The college is committed to ensuring that financial constraints do not prevent families from accessing Christian education.
description: The college offers scholarships to those families who are struggling with financial hardship and not able to afford the school fees. The college is committed to making sure Christian Education is available to all those who value it. The exact financial value varies based on individual circumstances, and further relief may be available upon application. Applicants should contact the college directly to discuss their specific situation and available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Demonstrable financial hardship; Must be enrolled or applying for enrolment at Mildura Christian College; Application required
special_conditions: Minimum 40% off tuition is offered to families in receipt of the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund subsidy (those with a low-income health care card). Additional fee relief (more than 40%) may be available for those facing substantial financial hardship upon application.

overview: Mildura Christian College offers tuition fee discounts to families who hold a low-income health care card. This program is designed to make Christian education more accessible to families experiencing financial disadvantage.
description: The college offers discounts on tuition fees to families with a low-income health care card. A minimum 40% reduction of tuition fees is provided to families holding this card. The discount is automatically applied to fees, helping reduce the financial burden for eligible families. Families may also qualify for additional fee relief beyond the standard 40% upon application to the college if facing substantial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a low-income health care card; Open to all year levels; Applies to tuition fees only; Families must register the card with the college
special_conditions: Minimum 40% off tuition is guaranteed for holders of low-income health care cards. The 5% early payment discount (paid in full by end of February) is not available to those receiving fee relief such as the health care card discount.

overview: Mildura Christian College offers tuition fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled. The discount recognises the financial burden that families face when sending several children to the school.
description: The college offers discounts on tuition fees for families with multiple children enrolled in the college. The discount is applied to the total annual tuition fees and helps reduce the financial burden for families with siblings attending. The exact percentage of discount varies and families should contact the college directly for specific information about the discount applicable to their family situation.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at Mildura Christian College; Open to all year levels; Applies to tuition fees; Applies only when multiple siblings are concurrently enrolled
special_conditions: Discount is applied to tuition fees only. The discount percentage should be confirmed with the college at the time of enrolment.

overview: Mildura Christian College facilitates the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) for eligible families. This provides annual payments to help with the costs of camps, excursions, and sporting events.
description: The Victorian government offers a CSEF payment for low-income families to assist with the costs of school camps, excursions, and sporting events. The annual payment is provided to families with a low-income health care card. Primary students receive $125 annually and secondary students receive $225 annually. Applications are made through the college, which then allocates the CSEF payment to the camps, sports and excursions portion of the levies charged to families.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a low-income health care card; Open to all students; Payment made annually; Applications made through the college
special_conditions: Primary students receive $125 per year; Secondary students receive $225 per year. Payment is provided annually and is allocated by the college to camps, sports and excursions levies.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College offers the Indigenous Leadership Scholarship to recognise and support Indigenous students completing Year 12. The scholarship is awarded to one male and one female student who have demonstrated commitment to academic success and leadership within the college community.
description: The Indigenous Leadership Scholarship provides $500 to each recipient. This scholarship is awarded to one male and one female student who have been motivated to complete Year 12 to the best of their potential. Recipients must have demonstrated commitment to succeeding, role modelling to others, demonstrated the values of the college, and have consolidated a pathway plan following their graduation. The scholarship was established in 2021 through the MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Program and recognises Indigenous students who exemplify leadership and academic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Must identify as Indigenous; Must be motivated to complete Year 12; Must demonstrate commitment to succeeding; Must role model to others; Must demonstrate college values; Must have consolidated pathway plan following graduation
special_conditions: This is a newly established scholarship made possible through the MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Program. Awards are presented at the end of Year 12.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College offers the Enid Borschmann Scholarship to support disadvantaged students progressing to university. This prestigious scholarship honours the legacy of a former principal and provides substantial financial assistance to Year 12 VCE students intending to pursue tertiary education.
description: The Enid Borschmann Scholarship provides $10,000 each year for three consecutive years for a total value of $30,000. This scholarship is aimed at supporting disadvantaged students to attend university. Eligible candidates must be currently studying Year 12 VCE at Mildura Senior College and intending to accept a university offer for commencement or deferment of tertiary studies in the following year. The scholarship was established in 2021 as a beneficiary of the estate of Enid Borschmann, who served as Principal of Mildura Senior College from 1980 to 1989. Applications open from August 1st for Year 12 students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 VCE students; Currently enrolled at Mildura Senior College; Intending to accept a university offer for commencement or deferment of tertiary studies the following year; Must be disadvantaged
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded for three consecutive years contingent on continued enrolment and academic progress. Recipient must maintain enrolment at the university they have accepted an offer from.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Dux Award to the student achieving the highest Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR) at the college. This award recognises exceptional academic achievement and excellence in the VCE.
description: The Dux Award provides $1,000.00 to the student with the top ATAR at the completion of Year 12 VCE. The award is named after either Slone or Chaffey, honouring distinguished alumni or benefactors of the college. This award recognises the student who has achieved the highest overall academic performance across all VCE subjects and assessments. The award is presented at the college's commencement ceremony or awards evening at the conclusion of the academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Must have completed VCE; Must achieve the highest ATAR in the cohort
special_conditions: Automatically awarded to the student with the top ATAR. No application required. Award is presented at the end-of-year ceremony.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
program_name: Mildura Senior College Outstanding Achievement Award
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Outstanding Achievement Award to recognise students who have achieved exceptional results and demonstrated outstanding commitment throughout the year. This award celebrates consistent excellence both academically and in attendance and participation.
description: The Mildura Senior College Outstanding Achievement Award provides $250.00 to students who have achieved A+ results, excellent attendance, and participation throughout the year. Multiple awards may be presented if more than one student meets the criteria. The award recognises not only academic excellence but also commitment to the college community through consistent participation in college activities and events. Selection is made by the Senior Management Team who review academic records and attendance data to identify students meeting the high standards set for this award. Recipients are announced and celebrated at the college's awards evening or commencement ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; Must achieve A+ results; Must demonstrate excellent attendance; Must demonstrate participation throughout the year
special_conditions: Award is presented at the end of year. Multiple recipients may be named if all candidates meet the established criteria. Senior Management Team review academic performance and attendance records to determine eligibility.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College offers the Sam Dichiera Memorial Scholarship to recognise exceptional achievement in Automotive Studies and commitment to pursuing a career in the automotive industry. This scholarship honours the memory of a dedicated Rotary member and automotive industry advocate.
description: The Sam Dichiera Memorial Scholarship provides $500.00 to recognise and support students who have achieved high results in Automotive Studies and demonstrated a commitment to further studies in the automotive field. The scholarship honours Sam Dichiera, a member of the Rotary Club of Irymple, who made an outstanding contribution to both Rotary Service and the local automotive industry. Recipients must demonstrate not only academic excellence in Automotive Studies but also commitment to pursuing further education and a potential career in the automotive industry. The award is presented at the college's awards evening or commencement ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Must have completed Automotive Studies; Must achieve high results in Automotive Studies; Must demonstrate commitment to further studies in automotive field; Must show interest in automotive industry career
special_conditions: Award is made in memory of Sam Dichiera. Preference is given to students pursuing automotive industry pathways. Award is presented at end-of-year ceremony.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Jason Gowers Sports Star Award to Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding talent and leadership in sporting pursuits. This award recognises excellence both on and off the sporting field.
description: The Jason Gowers Sports Star Award provides $250.00 to a Year 12 student who has shown outstanding talent and leadership in a range of sports offered within the college. The award recognises students who have not only excelled in their sporting endeavours but also demonstrated leadership qualities and commitment to representing the college across multiple sporting codes. Recipients are identified by the sports department and college leadership based on performance, participation, and conduct throughout the year. The award is presented at the college's awards evening or commencement ceremony to celebrate the recipient's sporting achievements and leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Must have participated in range of sports offered at college; Must demonstrate outstanding talent in sports; Must demonstrate leadership in sports
special_conditions: Award is presented at end-of-year ceremony. Recognition given to students demonstrating outstanding talent and leadership across a range of college sports. Selection made by sports faculty and college leadership.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Monash Rural Health Award to recognise outstanding achievement in Health & Human Development or Biology. This award is presented to students who demonstrate exceptional subject performance and active community engagement.
description: The Monash Rural Health Award provides $200.00 and is presented as a science faculty award. This award is presented to students who have achieved outstanding contributions in Health & Human Development or Biology and who show an interest in, or are active in community engagement. The award recognises students pursuing pathways in health-related fields and demonstrates the college's commitment to developing future healthcare professionals from the Mildura region. Recipients must show not only academic excellence in relevant subjects but also commitment to community service and engagement. The award is presented at the college's faculty awards evening or commencement ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; Must achieve high results in Health & Human Development or Biology; Must demonstrate interest in health field; Must show active participation in community engagement; Must demonstrate commitment to further studies in health-related fields
special_conditions: Award is presented in the science faculty awards section. Partnership with Monash University reflects rural health focus. Recipients recognised for both academic performance and community engagement.

school_name: Mildura Senior College-Mildura English Language Centre
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Helen Bowering Award to recognise excellence in English studies. This award is sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily and celebrates outstanding achievement in the English discipline.
description: The Helen Bowering Award provides $200.00 and is presented as an English Faculty award. This award recognises students who have demonstrated outstanding achievement, commitment, and excellence in English studies at the college. The award is sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily, a major local media organisation, reflecting the community's commitment to supporting education in the region. Recipients are selected by the English faculty based on academic performance, participation in class discussions, quality of written and analytical work, and contribution to the broader English program. The award is presented at the college's faculty awards evening or commencement ceremony to celebrate the recipient's linguistic and literary excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; Must achieve high results in English; Must demonstrate commitment to English studies; Must demonstrate excellence in written work and analysis
special_conditions: Award is presented in the English faculty awards section and is sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily. Selection made by English faculty based on academic performance and engagement with subject.

overview: MLC offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and commitment to learning. The scholarship is based on merit and potential, with successful candidates assessed through a competitive scholarship test and interview process.
description: The Academic Scholarship is available to both primary and secondary students seeking entry to MLC. Candidates must achieve strong academic performance and demonstrate commitment to the college values. The selection process includes a written scholarship test, school reports, and a formal interview. Successful candidates are assessed on the strength of their application, previous school reports, the scholarship test, and interview performance. The financial value and number of scholarships awarded annually may vary.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate strong academic achievement; Open to primary and secondary students; Must commit to college values and expectations; Subject to competitive assessment
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Applications have now closed. Applicants are assessed based on scholarship test results, previous school reports, and interview performance. Scholarship recipients are required to demonstrate commitment to MLC values.

overview: MLC offers Sports Performance Scholarships to students who demonstrate excellence in sport. These scholarships are designed to support talented athletes who combine sporting achievement with commitment to academic and college values.
description: The Sports Performance Scholarship recognises students with elite athletic ability. Candidates are assessed through trials, coaching evaluations, and demonstrated sporting excellence at regional or higher levels. Selection involves evaluation of athletic performance potential, previous sporting achievements, school reports, and a formal interview. The financial benefit and number of awards vary annually based on applications and candidate quality.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate elite or high-level sporting ability; Open to primary and secondary students; Must maintain academic standards; Must commit to college values
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Applications have now closed. Selection includes evaluation of sporting trials and coaching assessments. Scholarship recipients must maintain academic performance and college conduct standards.

overview: MLC offers Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate musical excellence and performance potential. The scholarship recognises talented musicians and provides support for their musical development alongside academic studies.
description: The Music Scholarship is available to students entering primary and secondary levels who show musical talent and commitment. Selection involves a musical audition where candidates perform prepared pieces and may be assessed on music theory knowledge and aural skills. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction with varying values and covers 100 percent of music tuition fees for one instrument. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated musical excellence, potential for growth, audition performance, and school reports.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate musical excellence and performance ability; Open to primary and secondary students; Must pass audition assessment; Must commit to college values
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Applications have now closed. Scholarship includes full coverage of music tuition fees for one instrument. Recipients must maintain musical participation and academic standards throughout enrolment.

overview: MLC offers Science Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional ability in science subjects. The scholarship supports students who show commitment to scientific excellence and innovation.
description: The Science Scholarship recognises students with outstanding achievement or potential in science disciplines. Candidates are assessed through school science results, the academic scholarship test, previous school reports, and a formal interview. The scholarship has a means-tested component where the financial value may be determined by evidence of financial need. Recipients demonstrate strong commitment to MLC values and scientific achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate strong performance or potential in science; Open to primary and secondary students; Financial need may be assessed; Must commit to college values
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Applications have now closed. Means-tested financial assessment may apply. Selection includes evaluation of science performance, academic test results, and interview.

overview: The Thomas Henry Scholarship is designed to support students who demonstrate commitment to MLC values but face economic barriers in accessing an MLC education. The scholarship recognises financial need as a selection criterion.
description: The Thomas Henry Scholarship is a means-tested financial assistance program based on demonstrated evidence of financial need. The scholarship aims to support students who demonstrate a commitment to the values and expectations of the College and who face economic barriers in accessing an MLC education. Selection involves assessment of financial circumstances, school reports, personal statement, and an interview. The financial value is determined by individual circumstances and evidence of need.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate financial need; Must show commitment to college values; Open to primary and secondary students; May require financial documentation
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Applications have now closed. Means-tested scholarship award determined by evidence of financial need. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to college values and academic engagement.

overview: MLC offers Boarding Scholarships to support students who require residential accommodation. The scholarship is particularly designed for students from regional, rural and remote communities to access boarding at MLC.
description: The Boarding Scholarship provides boarding fee reduction of varying values to eligible candidates. The scholarship is available to secondary students and supports residential accommodation. Selection includes evaluation of boarding need, academic performance, school reports, and commitment to college values. MLC works with external scholarship programs such as Yalari to support Indigenous girls from regional, rural and remote communities to board at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Must require residential boarding; Open to secondary students; Regional, rural or remote residence preferred; Must commit to college values; Indigenous candidates may apply through Yalari program
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Boarding Applications close on 1 May 2026. Successful candidates entering Years 7 to 10 hold their scholarship until graduation at the end of Year 12. MLC partners with Yalari scholarship programme to support Indigenous girls from regional and remote communities.

overview: MLC offers Indigenous Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls, particularly from regional, rural and remote communities. The scholarship recognises MLC's commitment to Indigenous education and cultural inclusion.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship provides educational and financial support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. MLC has established a partnership with the Yalari scholarship programme, a not-for-profit organisation that offers secondary education scholarships at leading Australian boarding schools. The scholarship supports Indigenous girls to study and board at MLC. Recipients benefit from educational facilities, cultural programs, and support services. Selection involves evaluation of academic potential, commitment to college values, and individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Preference for students from regional, rural or remote communities; Open to secondary students; Must demonstrate commitment to education
special_conditions: 2027 Entry Indigenous Applications close on 1 May 2026. MLC includes Indigenous issues in mainstream curriculum and maintains student Aboriginal Reconciliation Committee. Annual cultural exchanges held with Worowa Aboriginal College. Recipients are supported to excel through access to facilities and diverse co-curricular experiences.

overview: Mill Park Secondary College is an accredited provider of Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) programs for gifted and high ability students. Students are selected for this program prior to commencing Year 7 and participate in an accelerated program from Years 7 to 10 that recognises their high potential as learners.
description: The SEAL program at Mill Park Secondary College is designed for gifted and talented students demonstrating exceptional academic potential. Students accepted into this program progress through year level subject content at an accelerated pace, allowing for additional time to be spent on mastery of skills, deepening of thinking through inquiry projects and enrichment activities that expose students to real-world problems. Working in a setting with like-minded peers and skilled teachers, SEAL students are expected to demonstrate commitment to their learning beyond the classroom through participation in student leadership activities and extra-curricular programs. Students are expected to undertake a minimum of one early start VCE subject in Year 10, with many students undertaking two. There is no direct financial benefit in the form of fee remission; this is a selective accelerated learning program for Years 7-10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have been offered and accepted a placement at Mill Park Secondary College through the normal enrolment process; Must demonstrate high academic potential through testing and selection process; Open to all genders
special_conditions: SEAL Expression of interest must be completed first. Registration must occur in Term 3. Families must select Mill Park Secondary College as first choice on their enrolment preference form. Testing registration fee required with registration form. Students must report to Middle Years Campus General Office by 8:45 am on testing day (near end of August, 9:00 am - 12:00 pm). Selection process includes written testing, SEAL Connection Afternoon (information session and activities), and potential interview. Final decisions advised by mid-October. Scholarship students participate in leadership training days throughout the year.

overview: Mill Park Secondary College supports eligible families through the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF), a Victorian government program providing financial assistance for school activities. Students whose families hold a valid Concession Card or Health Care Card are eligible to apply for support.
description: CSEF is a Victorian government financial assistance program that helps eligible families with the costs of school camps, sports and excursion activities. Mill Park Secondary College administers this program for students whose families hold a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card. The amount of assistance varies depending on the student's year level and the specific activities undertaken. Families must submit the CSEF Application Form with a copy of their valid Concession Card or Health Care Card attached. The CSEF application can be returned to the General Office at either campus or submitted via email to mill.park.sc@education.vic.gov.au. This program is for all year levels (7-12) and is a means-tested financial support program specific to excursion and sporting activity costs.
eligibility_criteria: Families must hold a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card; Open to all students in Years 7-12; Student must be enrolled at Mill Park Secondary College
special_conditions: Valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card must be attached with application form. CSEF is a government-funded program with varying payment amounts per student per year level. Applications may have specific closing dates determined by the Victorian government calendar, typically announced in the Information Packs each year.

overview: Mill Park Secondary College offers Year 7 Scholarships to recognise academic excellence in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and potential.
description: Mill Park Secondary College provides Year 7 Scholarships as part of its commitment to supporting talented students. The scholarship program is offered annually to students commencing Year 7. Applications are typically received during the Year 6 to Year 7 transition period. The selection process involves assessment of academic performance. Specific financial value, duration, and selection methodology details are not publicly disclosed on the school website. For current information on eligibility criteria, financial benefits, application dates, and selection procedures, direct contact with the school is required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at a Victorian primary school; Academic excellence required
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 Scholarships have now closed. For information about future scholarship rounds, contact the Middle Years Campus directly.

overview: Mill Park Secondary College is an accredited provider of Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) programs for gifted and high ability students. Students are selected for this program prior to commencing Year 7 and participate in an accelerated program from Years 7 to 10 that recognises their high potential as learners.
description: SEAL students at Mill Park Secondary College progress through year level subject content at an accelerated pace, allowing for additional time to be spent on mastery of skills, deepening of thinking through inquiry projects and enrichment activities that expose students to real-world problems. Working in a setting with like-minded peers and skilled teachers, SEAL students are expected to demonstrate commitment to their learning beyond the classroom. Students are encouraged to participate in student leadership activities as well as extra-curricular programs that suit their interests including sport, public speaking, and community engagement. SEAL students are expected to undertake a minimum of one early start VCE subject in Year 10, with many students undertaking two. The selection process involves registration with the High Ability Selection Testing (HAST) program and includes written assessment, interview, and portfolio review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students who have been offered and accepted a placement at Mill Park Secondary College through the normal enrolment process; Students entering Year 7; High academic ability demonstrated through testing; Interview required for final selection
special_conditions: SEAL expression of interest is now closed for 2026, for urgent enquiries please feel free to contact the Middle Years Campus on (03) 9407 9700. Candidates are invited for interview with parents if referral forms and test results indicate suitability. Final decisions will consider all available information, and candidates will be advised of the outcome by mid October. SEAL Connection Afternoon is held prior to testing day where parents attend an information session and students participate in activities.

overview: Mill Park Secondary College administers the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) to assist eligible families with the costs of school camps, sports, and excursions. The program is available to students at all year levels whose families hold a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian government initiative designed to support eligible families with the costs associated with student participation in school camps, sports events, and excursions. Students whose families hold a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card are eligible to apply. Families must attach a copy of their eligible card with the CSEF Application Form and return it to the General Office or email it to mill.park.sc@education.vic.gov.au. The application process is administered annually, with application forms provided in the school's information packs for each year level. This financial assistance program aims to ensure all students can participate fully in school enrichment activities regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card; Copy of card must be attached to application form; Available to all year levels; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Eligibility requires holding a valid Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card with a copy provided. Applications are submitted annually. Refer to school information packs for specific application dates and procedures for each year level. Direct contact with the school is recommended for current application timelines and fund availability.

overview: Mildura Senior College offers the Enid Borschmann Scholarship to support disadvantaged students in their transition to university. The scholarship provides substantial financial assistance over three consecutive years, recognising the need to remove financial barriers to tertiary education.
description: The Enid Borschmann Scholarship is aimed to support disadvantaged students to attend university. The scholarship value is $10,000 each year for three consecutive years, providing a total of $30,000 over the scholarship period. The scholarship was established in 2021 as a beneficiary of Enid Borschmann's estate, who was Principal of Mildura Senior College from 1980 to 1989. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated financial disadvantage and commitment to pursuing tertiary studies. Applications open on 1st August annually.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled Year 12 VCE student at Mildura Senior College; Intending to accept a university offer for commencement or deferment of tertiary studies in the following year; Demonstrated financial disadvantage
special_conditions: Scholarship is provided for three consecutive years during tertiary studies.

overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Dux Award to the student who achieves the highest ATAR score in their graduating year. This award recognises outstanding academic excellence and achievement in the Victorian Certificate of Education.
description: The Dux Award - Slone or Chaffey is presented to the Year 12 student who achieves the highest Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR) in their cohort. The award value is $1,000.00, recognising top academic achievement. The award honours the legacy of the Slone and Chaffey families and is presented at the college's awards ceremony. Selection is based solely on ATAR performance, with the student achieving the highest ATAR automatically receiving this award.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Highest ATAR score in graduating cohort
special_conditions: Award is automatically presented to the student with the highest ATAR; no application required.

overview: Mildura Senior College awards the Sam Dichiera Memorial Scholarship to students who have demonstrated excellence in Automotive Studies and commitment to pursuing further studies in the automotive industry. The scholarship honours the memory and contributions of Sam Dichiera.
description: The Sam Dichiera Memorial Scholarship is awarded to a student who has achieved high results in Automotive Studies and has demonstrated a commitment to further studies with a possible outcome of a career in the automotive industry. The scholarship value is $500.00. Sam Dichiera was a member of the Rotary Club of Irymple and the award recognises his outstanding contribution to Rotary Service and the local Automotive Industry. Selection is based on academic performance in Automotive Studies and demonstrated intention to pursue automotive-related tertiary education or training.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; High results in Automotive Studies; Demonstrated commitment to further automotive studies or career in automotive industry
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Jason Gowers Sports Star Award to Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding talent and leadership across multiple sports offered by the college. The award honours the memory of Jason Gowers.
description: The Jason Gowers Sports Star Award is awarded to a Year 12 student who has shown outstanding talent and leadership in a range of sports that are offered within the college. The award value is $250.00. Jason Gowers was a student at Mildura Senior College in 2005/06, having previously attended Merbein Primary and Secondary Schools. He was studying a commerce degree in Adelaide until his tragic passing in November 2010. The award recognises excellence in multi-sport participation and demonstrated leadership qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Outstanding talent and leadership demonstrated across multiple college sports
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: Mildura Senior College awards the Indigenous Leadership Scholarship to Indigenous students who have demonstrated exceptional commitment to completing Year 12 and embodying college values. The scholarship recognises Indigenous students who serve as role models in their community.
description: The Indigenous Leadership Scholarship is awarded to a male and female student who has been motivated to complete Year 12 to the best of their potential. They have demonstrated commitment to succeeding, role modelling to others, demonstrated the values of our college and have consolidated a pathway plan following their graduation. The scholarship value is $500 for each recipient. This is the first year of the scholarship and is made possible through the MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Program. The award recognises Indigenous students who exemplify leadership and commitment to their community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander student; Completed Year 12; Demonstrated commitment to academic success; Role modelling and demonstrated college values; Consolidated pathway plan for post-graduation
special_conditions: Awarded to one male and one female student annually; Made possible through partnership with MADEC Indigenous Youth Leadership Program.

overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Monash Rural Health Award to recognise Year 12 students excelling in Health & Human Development or Biology who demonstrate active engagement in community health initiatives. The award is presented as a science faculty award.
description: The Monash Rural Health Award is a science faculty award with a value of $200.00 presented to Year 12 students who demonstrate outstanding contributions in Health & Human Development or Biology and show an interest in or are active in Community Engagement. The award recognises students who combine academic excellence in health sciences with a demonstrated commitment to community service. Selection criteria include exceptional performance in relevant subject areas and demonstrated involvement in health or community engagement activities.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Outstanding results in Health & Human Development or Biology; Demonstrated interest in or active participation in community engagement
special_conditions: Presented as part of science faculty awards ceremony.

overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Helen Bowering Award to Year 12 students who have demonstrated excellence in English. The award is presented as an English faculty award and is sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily.
description: The Helen Bowering Award is an English faculty award with a value of $200.00 presented to Year 12 students who have achieved excellence in English studies. The award is sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily, a local media organisation. Selection is based on outstanding academic performance in English subjects and demonstrated commitment to literary excellence. The award recognises the importance of English education in the college curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Outstanding performance in English
special_conditions: Presented as part of English faculty awards ceremony; Sponsored by the Sunraysia Daily.

overview: Mildura Senior College awards the McGlynn Hunt Funeral Scholarship to Year 12 students who have demonstrated exceptional commitment to community service through volunteering or who carry caring responsibilities. The scholarship is sponsored by McGlynn Hunt Funerals.
description: The McGlynn Hunt Funeral Scholarship is awarded to a student who has demonstrated commitment to the community through volunteering and or a student who has caring responsibilities, sponsored by McGlynn Hunt Funerals. The scholarship value is $1,000.00. The award recognises students who balance their education with meaningful contributions to their community or who maintain responsibilities for caring for family members. Selection is based on demonstrated community engagement and/or evidence of caring responsibilities alongside academic commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Demonstrated commitment to community volunteering; OR evidence of caring responsibilities alongside schooling
special_conditions: Sponsored by McGlynn Hunt Funerals.

program_name: Mildura Senior College Outstanding Achievement Award
overview: Mildura Senior College presents the Outstanding Achievement Award to recognise students who have achieved A+ results with excellent attendance and consistent participation throughout the year. The award celebrates all-round academic and engagement excellence.
description: The Mildura Senior College Outstanding Achievement Award is presented to students that have achieved A+ results, excellent attendance and participation throughout the year. The award value is $250.00. Multiple awards may be presented if multiple students meet the criteria. Selection is based on three key performance indicators: achieving A+ grades, maintaining excellent attendance, and demonstrating consistent participation in college activities. The award recognises excellence beyond academic performance, acknowledging commitment to college life.
eligibility_criteria: A+ results achieved throughout the year; Excellent attendance record; Consistent participation in college activities throughout the year
special_conditions: If more than one student meets criteria, all eligible students receive the award.

overview: Mirboo North Secondary College offers the James Harvey Trust Scholarship to support enrolled students seeking to advance their educational goals. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate commitment to academic achievement, leadership, community service, sporting excellence, and artistic achievement.
description: The James Harvey Trust was established in 2018 with funds bequeathed by James Harvey, a local farmer who believed firmly in the importance of education. The fund provides scholarships for educational purposes and may be granted for activities, resources, programs and educational subjects that assist students at the college. The Trust is managed by a committee comprising staff and school council members of MNSC. Scholarship awards are assessed and recommended based on academic performance, leadership and community service, sporting achievement, the arts, and overall college contribution. The financial value varies and is determined by the Trust based on the interests of individual applicants and available funds. Applications are submitted via an official form available from the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled students at Mirboo North Secondary College; Satisfactory completion of the previous year level studies; Adherence to the MNSC student code of conduct; Open to students with interest in achieving higher educational standards; Assessment considers academic achievement, leadership, community service, sporting, arts, and college contribution
special_conditions: Funding announcements made on 16th May 2025. The fund provides support for educational activities, resources and programs approved by the Education Department of Victoria. Awards may support participation in school and study-specific activities beyond tuition.

program_name: Victorian High Ability Program (VHAP) - English and Mathematics
overview: Mill Park Primary School offers the Victorian High Ability Program (VHAP) for gifted and high-achieving students in English and Mathematics. The program provides online learning modules, mentorship, and competitions to challenge students in their academic pursuits.
description: The VHAP program is designed for high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional ability in English and Mathematics. The program provides online learning modules that allow students to engage with advanced content at their own pace. Students receive mentorship from educators specializing in gifted education and have access to competitions that allow them to showcase their skills. The program challenges students to think critically and develop deeper understanding of curriculum concepts. Participation is based on teacher identification of students demonstrating high ability in their year level. The program operates throughout the school year with online delivery supporting flexible engagement with content.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as high-achieving or gifted in English and/or Mathematics; Teacher recommendation required; Currently enrolled at Mill Park Primary School
special_conditions: Selection is based on teacher identification of students working at higher levels than their peers. Program operates online throughout the school year.

overview: Mill Park Primary School provides an extension program through Mathematics Olympiad competition. This program allows high-achieving students to showcase their mathematical skills and develop critical thinking abilities through competitive mathematical problems.
description: The Mathematics Olympiad is an extension competition program designed for students demonstrating strength in mathematics. Through participation in this competition, students develop and showcase critical thinking abilities and advanced mathematical problem-solving skills. The program challenges students to apply mathematical concepts to novel and complex problems. Students gain experience in competitive mathematics while developing resilience and deeper engagement with mathematical thinking. The program supports students in achieving their full potential in mathematics and provides extension opportunities beyond the standard curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Strong mathematical ability; Teacher recommendation; Currently enrolled at Mill Park Primary School
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated mathematical ability and teacher recommendation. Competition format provides opportunity to develop critical thinking.

overview: Mill Park Primary School offers the Victorian Enrichment Series, which provides workshops and seminars on various topics designed to nurture student talents and abilities across a range of subjects and disciplines.
description: The Victorian Enrichment Series is an extension program consisting of workshops and seminars covering various curriculum and enrichment topics. These sessions are designed to nurture and develop student talents and abilities across a diverse range of subjects. The program provides opportunities for students to engage with content beyond the standard curriculum, allowing them to explore areas of particular interest and strength. Through participation in these workshops and seminars, students develop their understanding and skills in different subject areas while connecting with other high-ability learners. The program supports students in achieving their full academic and personal potential by providing enrichment experiences tailored to different areas of strength.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as high-achieving student; Interest in enrichment topics; Currently enrolled at Mill Park Primary School
special_conditions: Workshops and seminars are offered as part of the extension program throughout the school year. Selection based on demonstrated ability and interest.

program_name: Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program
overview: Monbulk College supports Year 10 students in applying for the Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program through the University of Melbourne. This academic enrichment program recognises and supports high-achieving students pursuing excellence in their chosen fields.
description: The Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars Program is an external academic enrichment program delivered by the University of Melbourne designed to support high-achieving secondary school students. Students complete a two-year program from Year 11 to Year 12 that includes academic workshops, personal development activities, and networking opportunities. Upon graduation, eligible Young Scholars receive financial support including guaranteed entry into University of Melbourne undergraduate courses at specified ATARs, a relocation allowance of up to $2,500 for regional/rural students, and a Melbourne Global Scholars Award of up to $2,500 for overseas exchange study. Monbulk College facilitates school endorsements and provides support for eligible Year 10 student applications.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students at Monbulk College; Must be identified as high-achieving; Must be endorsed by the school; Eligible for students in Victoria or approved border regions of New South Wales or South Australia; Application period typically July to September
special_conditions: Program is delivered by University of Melbourne, not Monbulk College directly. School endorsement required in addition to student application. Successful scholars must commit to two-year participation from Year 11 to Year 12. Regional/rural relocation allowance available for eligible students with permanent residence in designated areas.

overview: Mooroolbark College offers the ASPIRE Program to ambitious students entering Year 7 who have high aspirations and are dedicated to achieving challenging learning objectives. The program provides an enriched curriculum in Mathematics, Science, English and Humanities, recognising students who consistently pursue their personal best through increased effort and dedication.
description: The ASPIRE Program at Mooroolbark College is designed for ambitious students targeting Years 7-9 who set and strive to meet challenging learning goals. The program focuses on teamwork, collaborative learning and developing transformative problem-solving skills applied across all aspects of schooling. Students engage in tasks requiring higher-level complex thinking and participate in intellectual pursuits including externally conducted competitions and activities. ASPIRE is not an acceleration program; rather it emphasises personalised, stimulating curriculum designed to respond to students' abilities, interests and needs. Successful students benefit from an enriched learning experience across the four core subject areas throughout their junior school years. The program is limited to 25 students per year. Acceptance is contingent on examination results, supporting documents submission, and ability to collaborate effectively in team-based tasks.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; High aspirations and dedication to achieving challenging learning objectives; Must sit written examination and participate in Design Sprint Task; Students and parents/guardians must submit supporting documentation via Microsoft Forms; Demonstrated ability to work effectively in teams
special_conditions: Limited to 25 students per class due to space constraints. Testing occurs in Term 4 (typically October). Students must complete written examination and participate in Design Sprint Task on testing day. Supporting documentation must be submitted through Microsoft Forms link. Acceptance determined by examination results, supporting documents quality, and team collaboration effectiveness. Registration via TryBooking platform required. Programme encompasses English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities subjects. Not an acceleration program but rather enrichment-focused with emphasis on collaborative learning and problem-solving.

overview: Mordialloc College offers financial assistance scholarships to Grade 6 students enrolling at the College who demonstrate exceptional achievement. The College makes available a limited number of scholarships to outstanding students and their families by providing financial assistance during Years 7 and 8.
description: Each year Mordialloc College makes available a limited number of scholarships to outstanding students and their families by providing financial assistance during Years 7 and 8. Students who excel academically, in sport or performing arts, or in leadership or community service are encouraged to apply. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of an interview which is attended by a parent or guardian as well as the student. The financial assistance is provided for the duration of Years 7 and 8. The specific monetary value of the scholarships is not disclosed on the College website. Applicants must contact the College for further information regarding the application process and value of assistance.
eligibility_criteria: All Grade 6 students who are enrolling at the College are eligible to apply; Must excel academically, in sport or performing arts, or in leadership or community service; Interview required with parent or guardian and student
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded on the basis of an interview. Applications are open to Grade 6 students enrolling at the College during the specified transition period. Contact the College Office on 03 9580 1184 or email mordialloc.co@education.vic.gov.au for more information and to register.

overview: <cite index="26-1,26-2">The SEAL Program at Mordialloc College is designed for gifted and high achieving students who are looking to accelerate in their learning.</cite> <cite index="22-1">Through this special program, students are selected to complete the first four years of school in three years.</cite>
description: <cite index="23-4">The accredited SEAL Program has been running at Mordialloc College since 1997.</cite> <cite index="22-4,22-5">Mordialloc College provides the Select Entry Accelerated Learning program (SEAL) for motivated and academically advanced students. SEAL challenges gifted and high-achieving students in their studies, enriches their learning experience and helps them to fulfil their potential.</cite> There is no tuition fee reduction for SEAL as it is a government school program. <cite index="72-1,72-2">The Year 7 SEAL levy will be $65 in 2026, which covers the cost of entry into national competitions, additional learning opportunities and all excursions for the entire academic year.</cite> The selection process involves entrance exams via Edutest, submission of academic records, teacher reference, and interview. <cite index="27-1">The S.E.A.L program exists for years 7–10.</cite>
eligibility_criteria: Must be accepted into Mordialloc College as per DET placement policy; Must be currently in Year 6 and selected to enter Year 7; Identified as academically gifted and high-achieving; Must pass entrance exams; Must provide Grade 5 Semester 2 report, Grade 6 Semester 1 report, and Grade 5 NAPLAN scores; Current classroom teacher reference required
special_conditions: <cite index="26-5">Mordialloc College does not release exam results to parents as we triangulate all data sets to ensure we select the most suitable candidates for our SEAL Program.</cite> <cite index="72-3">A non-refundable deposit is required on acceptance of the offer.</cite> Students must maintain high academic standards throughout the program. The SEAL levy is non-refundable and payable annually.

overview: <cite index="62-1">Mordialloc College's Sports Excellence Program (SEP) is designed to develop student athletes into elite performers while maintaining academic success.</cite> <cite index="62-3">Specialized training in Netball, Basketball, and AFL.</cite>
description: <cite index="62-1">Mordialloc College's Sports Excellence Program (SEP) is designed to develop student athletes into elite performers while maintaining academic success.</cite> The program offers specialized coaching and competition opportunities in three sports: Australian Rules Football (AFL), Basketball, and Netball. <cite index="62-7">Costs for each sport for SEP 2026 will be: AFL/AFLW: $1250; Basketball: $1200; Netball: $1500.</cite> <cite index="62-7">Trials will commence August 20th 2025.</cite> <cite index="62-5">Each term students involved in competitive sport classes will have the opportunity to play against other schools in the district, with winners of the round-robin format invited to play in Southern Metro finals.</cite> The program develops technical skills, tactical awareness, and competitive experience.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 7 or above at Mordialloc College; Must demonstrate elite athletic ability in chosen sport; Must pass trials; Must maintain academic performance alongside sporting commitments; Selection based on tryout performance and talent assessment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must trial for acceptance into the program. Costs vary by sport: AFL/AFLW $1250, Basketball $1200, Netball $1500 per year. Students must balance elite sporting training with academic responsibilities and maintain acceptable academic standards.

overview: <cite index="36-9,36-10">Richard 'Dick' Williams was a Mordialloc College alum and an unsung local hero. He worked tirelessly for others, especially those in his beloved Mordialloc community.</cite> <cite index="32-2">It is an annual scholarship all students can apply for each year.</cite>
description: <cite index="36-11,36-12">After his passing in 2020, a few of his close mates got together to create a Scholarship Fund in his memory. This Fund is an important part of his legacy, keeping Dick's generosity of spirit alive.</cite> <cite index="32-1">This year we had six lucky students being awarded this scholarship, receiving $1000 each to purchase goods and equipment to support them to pursue their interests.</cite> The scholarship is awarded annually to Mordialloc College students who demonstrate commitment to community service, leadership, and helping others. Recipients may use the award to purchase equipment and goods to support their interests and community involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current student at Mordialloc College; Must demonstrate commitment to community service and leadership; Must show dedication to helping others; Open to students across all year levels; Selection based on character, community involvement, and service to others
special_conditions: Annual scholarship awarded to students demonstrating community service values aligned with Richard Williams' legacy. Applications typically open in December and close in March. Recipients are recognized at whole-school assemblies.

overview: Mortlake P-12 College offers the FasTrack Program to talented and gifted students who demonstrate capacity to work above their year level. The program is designed to allow students to accelerate through Years 5-10, completing a wider range of subjects at Years 8-10 and VCE level.
description: FasTrack is a program designed for talented students to allow them to accelerate through Years 5-10 so that they complete a wider range of subjects at years 8-10 and VCE level. It also allows gifted and talented students to accelerate their studies in their specific skill area. Students who have been assessed as capable of working at a higher level than their age group may be given the option of accelerating their learning to allow them to reach VCE earlier. This program gives students greater academic rigour earlier so that they are challenged and engaged in the curriculum. Students can complete VCE over 3 or 4 years and have the chance of taking a wider range of VCE subjects, which is especially attractive for students who enjoy both humanities and sciences. This may also include university studies in some cases. Students are able to maximize their chances of an excellent ATAR score because of the increased number of subjects completed. The program is administered by the Curriculum Coordinator who will mentor and monitor the academic pathways of these students from Year 5. The FasTrack Coordinator will continue to monitor and liaise with parents for the duration of the student's years at Mortlake College to give continuity and support to these accelerated students.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as capable of working above year level; Must complete M.Y.A.T. Gifted and Talented screening test routinely conducted at Year 5; Data triangulated with On Demand testing and NAPLAN data for potential in Literacy and Numeracy; Open to Years 5-12; Differing pathways discussed with parents and students prior to enrollment
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Once identified, the differing pathways will be discussed with parents and students. The FasTrack Coordinator will continue to monitor and liaise with parents for the duration of the student's years at Mortlake College to give continuity and support to these accelerated students. Students must maintain appropriate academic performance to continue in the program.

overview: Mornington Secondary College awards Dux recognition to students demonstrating the highest overall academic achievement across each year level and for the school overall. The Dux award is presented annually at the College's Awards night and recognises exceptional dedication and academic excellence.
description: The Dux Award is presented annually to students who achieve the highest overall academic performance. Each year level has a designated Dux, and a College Dux is also recognised, typically the highest-performing VCE student. Recipients are formally announced at the College's annual Awards Presentation evening. The award recognises students who have achieved outstanding academic results and exceptional commitment to their studies. Award details include certificate and trophy presentation. Recipients are identified based on academic achievement across semester 1 and 2 results at each year level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Recognition based on highest academic performance within each year level; VCE students eligible for College Dux based on ATAR results
special_conditions: Award presented at annual Awards night recognising overall academic achievement. Certificate and trophy awarded to recipients. VCE Dux selection based on final ATAR results.

overview: Mornington Secondary College presents Principal's Commendation Certificates to students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and personal excellence throughout the year. These certificates recognise sustained academic performance and achievement across the school.
description: Principal's Commendation Certificates are awarded to students who achieve sustained academic excellence and demonstrate outstanding personal qualities. Recipients are recognised at year-level assemblies and school award ceremonies. The certificates acknowledge academic achievement and contribution to the school community. These are presented to students meeting specific academic performance thresholds determined by school leadership. Award is non-monetary and presented as formal recognition of achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Achievement of specified academic performance threshold; Demonstrated commitment to learning
special_conditions: Award presented during year-level assemblies recognising academic achievement and personal excellence. Certificates distributed to recipients at assembly or award ceremonies.

overview: Mornington Secondary College awards Gold Stars to students achieving overall academic excellence within specific year levels. The award recognises sustained high performance across multiple subjects and commitment to academic achievement.
description: The Gold Star Award is presented to students who achieve overall academic excellence. Students receiving a gold star must meet specified academic performance criteria as determined by the school. The award is non-monetary and presented as formal recognition of sustained academic achievement throughout the year. Recipients are recognised at school assemblies and award ceremonies. The gold star designation indicates top academic performers within each year level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at all year levels; Must meet specified overall academic performance threshold; Demonstrated sustained academic excellence across subjects
special_conditions: Award presented to students meeting specified academic criteria. Recognition provided at school assemblies. No application required; selection based on school assessment of academic performance.

overview: Mossgiel Park Primary School works with families experiencing financial hardship to ensure students have adequate uniform items to wear. The school recognises that uniform costs can be a barrier to full participation and is committed to supporting equity of access.
description: The school provides support for families suffering financial hardship with uniform costs. This is a need-based assistance program that ensures students can access appropriate school uniforms without financial barrier to enrolment or attendance. Families experiencing difficulty meeting uniform costs should contact the school directly to discuss support options. The assistance is non-monetary but facilitates access to required uniform items. There are no formal application processes, eligibility thresholds, or predetermined number of awards—support is determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated need for uniform cost assistance; Enrolled or applying to enrol at Mossgiel Park Primary School
special_conditions: Support is determined on a case-by-case basis by the school in discussion with families. Families must contact the school directly to arrange uniform assistance.

program_name: ASPIRE (Advanced Student Program for Inquiry, Reasoning and Excellence)
overview: Mount Clear College offers the ASPIRE program to gifted and talented students entering Year 7. This selective program provides an enriched curriculum where students learn with like-minded peers while being extended across the eight key learning domains of the Victorian Curriculum.
description: The ASPIRE program provides a stimulating, enriched curriculum designed for academically gifted Year 7 and Year 8 students. Students complete Level 3 of the Compass Award in Year 7 and progress to Level 4 in Year 8. The program enhances personal development, fosters positive attitudes towards learning, and promotes engagement with the community. Selection is based on Edutest assessment conducted in August. The program allows students to develop critical thinking skills and participate in extension activities across multiple learning areas. Students remain in the program throughout their junior secondary years, with ongoing assessment of academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students applying for entry into Year 7; Must complete Edutest assessment; Top performance on selective entry assessment; Open to all students regardless of sector
special_conditions: Students must be registered with EduTest and complete required forms to participate in testing. Testing occurs on 30 August at Mount Clear College.

overview: Mount Clear College offers the SEAL Program, a select entry accelerated learning opportunity for gifted students. The program allows students to effectively complete Years 7–10 in three years instead of four, providing acceleration for high-achieving students.
description: The SEAL Program (formerly SEALP) is an accelerated learning pathway that enables eligible students to compress the Years 7-10 curriculum into a three-year timeframe rather than the standard four years. This program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and capacity for accelerated progress. Students participating in SEAL undertake enrichment opportunities and engagement in a range of academic and co-curricular activities. The program supports students to aspire to reach the pinnacle of their educational and personal development while encouraging success in a variety of areas beyond traditional academic subjects. The school is currently exploring expanded options for gifted and talented student participation including physical recreation, development of practical skills, and community service opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 9; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Selection through competitive assessment process; Must have capacity for accelerated learning
special_conditions: Program has been transitioning with an evolved version to be launched in Term 3. Schedule alterations incorporate successful completion of program goals.

overview: Mount Clear College offers the ASPIRE program as a select entry enrichment program designed for students with high academic aspirations. The program nurtures students' academic and personal development through a challenging, enriched curriculum that extends across all eight key learning domains of the Victorian Curriculum.
description: ASPIRE is the college's selective entry enrichment program for Year 6 students entering Year 7. The program provides a stimulating, enriched curriculum where students learn with like-minded peers and develop to their full potential. Students in the ASPIRE program undertake the Compass Award as a key component, which enhances personal development and fosters positive attitudes towards learning. The program aims to develop lifelong learners who are enthusiastic, adaptable, creative, collaborative, self-motivated and resilient. No direct financial benefit is listed, as this is a government school program. Selection is based on Edutest assessment. Applications are made through the Edutest Registration process, with forms available for parents/carers, students, and teachers.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; High academic aspirations and demonstrated commitment to challenging learning goals; Must pass Edutest selection assessment; Open to all students regardless of gender
special_conditions: Selective entry program. Students must demonstrate high aspirations and commitment to learning. Undertaking the Compass Award is a key aspect of the program.

overview: Mount Clear College offers the SEAL (Select Entry Accelerated Learning) program for gifted and talented students. The program allows students to effectively complete Years 7-10 in three years instead of four, providing academic acceleration and enrichment opportunities.
description: The SEAL program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and are ready for accelerated learning. The program allows students to complete Years 7 through 10 in three years rather than the standard four years, effectively advancing their progress through secondary education. The college is evolving this program to focus more on enrichment opportunities and development of the whole student rather than purely academic acceleration. The program emphasizes participation in physical recreation, development of personal interests and practical skills, and a strong sense of community spirit. No direct monetary benefit is provided as this is a government school. Selection and application details are provided through expressions of interest. The program structure and curriculum details were to be launched in Term 3 with an Information Evening planned.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional academic ability; Readiness for accelerated learning; Open to all genders; Commitment to balanced development including physical and community involvement
special_conditions: Program schedule will be altered to incorporate successful completion of program goals. Students participate in physical recreation improvement, personal skills development, and community responsibility activities alongside academic acceleration. Expression of Interest form required.

overview: Mount Clear College offers the Duke of Edinburgh Award to students as a leadership and personal development program. The Award empowers young people to explore their full potential through a structured program of personal development, service, and achievement.
description: The Duke of Edinburgh Award is a nationally recognized program available to Mount Clear College students. The Award is designed to enhance personal development and build leadership skills through structured activities and challenges. Students who complete the Award gain recognition that can support university applications and scholarship applications. The program is available to secondary students at the college. No direct financial benefit is provided as this is a non-monetary award program. The Duke of Edinburgh Award operates through the national framework and provides tiered levels of achievement. Participants work with an Award Coordinator at the college to manage their progress.
eligibility_criteria: Student of Mount Clear College; Secondary year level; Commitment to complete Award requirements; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Completion of the Duke of Edinburgh Award can support scholarship applications and university admissions. Award is recognized nationally. Program managed through college Award Coordinator.

overview: Mount Clear College incorporates the Compass Award into its programs to enhance student personal development and engagement. The Compass Award is a key component of the ASPIRE enrichment program and is designed to empower young people to explore their full potential.
description: The Compass Award is integrated into Mount Clear College's ASPIRE enrichment program as a core component of student development. The Award was created to enhance young people's personal development, foster positive attitudes towards learning, and promote engagement and interaction with the community. Students in the ASPIRE program undertake this award as part of their enriched curriculum experience. The Compass Award recognizes and develops leadership qualities, resilience, and community contribution. No direct financial benefit is associated with this award as it is a non-monetary recognition program. The award supports the broader goal of developing lifelong learners who are adaptable, creative, and self-motivated.
eligibility_criteria: Enrollment in ASPIRE program; Commitment to personal development and community engagement; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Undertaken as mandatory part of ASPIRE program. Designed to develop leadership, resilience, and community engagement skills.

overview: Mount Eliza Secondary College offers the Enhanced Performance Program (EPP) to recognise and challenge highly capable students across all aspects of school life. The EPP is an inclusive approach that allows all highly capable students to be challenged regardless of their area of strength. Successful EPP members can also be considered for scholarships in their area of strength.
description: The Enhanced Performance Program (EPP) at Mount Eliza Secondary College is designed to recognise the broad range of student talents across academic, sporting, artistic, and community engagement areas. Unlike traditional gifted programs that focus solely on academic excellence, the EPP allows all highly capable students to be challenged regardless of their area of strength. The program covers all areas where a student feels they excel, including English, Maths, Sport, Leadership, Art, Music, Personal Learning, and Community work. Students apply to become EPP members by providing evidence of their success in their chosen area. Successful students can also be considered for a scholarship in their area of strength. The EPP operates on an application basis requiring students to demonstrate achievement, excellence, or potential within their selected field.
eligibility_criteria: Open to any current Year 6 student considering enrolment at Mount Eliza Secondary College; Students must provide evidence of success in their chosen area of strength; Open to all genders; Application-based entry
