special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to successful EPP members in their area of strength. Financial value and renewal conditions are not publicly specified and should be confirmed directly with the school.

overview: Mount Eliza North Primary School offers an Instrumental Music Scholarship to assist families with the financial barriers to beginning instrumental music education. The scholarship provides 12 months of free instrument rental for a student's chosen instrument, recognising the importance of making music education accessible to interested students.
description: The Instrumental Music Scholarship provides 12 months of free rental for an instrument of the student's choice. Students must arrange and pay for instrumental lessons separately during this period at the parents' expense. The purpose of this scholarship is to assist with the financial output that may be a barrier to a family beginning an instrumental education for an interested child. Application is via written letter addressed to the Music Specialist, stating which instrument the student wishes to learn and why the letter must be signed by a parent/guardian and posted to the school office by the application deadline. Eligible instruments include flute, clarinet, saxophone, trumpet, trombone, and violin.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be interested in learning instrumental music; Parent/guardian signature required on application letter; Letter must specify chosen instrument and reasons for learning
special_conditions: Recipient must pay for instrumental lessons separately at parent/guardian expense. Instrument rental is provided for 12 months only. Application requires letter format with parent/guardian signature.

overview: Mount Lilydale Mercy College offers the Academic and General Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating outstanding achievement in their applications to enter the College. The scholarship is offered at multiple levels recognising both exceptional academic ability and students who aspire to academic success and a values-based Catholic education.
description: The Academic and General Excellence Scholarship is available to new students applying to enter the College at Years 10, 11 and 12. Scholarships are offered at 50%, 75% and 100% of tuition fees, depending on the level of achievement demonstrated by applicants. The selection process involves assessment of the student's academic performance and commitment to a values-based Catholic education. While Catholic children are the priority of the College, the College recognises that in the senior years of schooling the opportunity exists to help children who could not otherwise benefit from such an education. Applications are open from the start of Term 3 to 1 November each year.
eligibility_criteria: For NEW students only — those applying to enter the school; Students applying to enter at Year 10, Year 11 or Year 12; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction or 75% Fee Reduction or 100% Fee Remission
special_conditions: For new students only — those applying to enter the school. Catholic children are the priority of the College.

program_name: Archbishop Sir Frank Little Indigenous Scholarship
overview: Mount Lilydale Mercy College offers the Archbishop Sir Frank Little Indigenous Scholarship to recognise and support Indigenous students seeking a values-based Catholic education. The scholarship honours the dedicated service of the late Archbishop of Melbourne, Sir Frank Little, and his commitment to reconciliation and support of Australia's Indigenous people.
description: The Archbishop Sir Frank Little Indigenous Scholarship is available to Indigenous students applying to enter the College at all year levels. Scholarships start at $1000 towards college fees and support the College's commitment to serving the local community by offering opportunities to those who seek a values-based Catholic education and those who aspire to academic success. The scholarship honours the Archbishop's commitment to reconciliation and general support of Australia's Indigenous people in their quest for recognition and equality in life, and his general support of families in some need who desire a Catholic education for their child. Applications are open from the start of Term 3 to 1 November each year.
eligibility_criteria: For NEW students only — those applying to enter the school; Indigenous Australian student; Applying to enter at any year level; Must demonstrate aspiration for academic success and commitment to values-based Catholic education
special_conditions: For new students only — those applying to enter the school. Applicant must be of Indigenous Australian descent.

overview: Mount Lilydale Mercy College offers the Old Collegians' Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in current students. The scholarship awards 100% of tuition fees to an existing student progressing to Year 12 who demonstrates academic excellence, commitment to the Catholic faith, and embodiment of the Mercy ethos.
description: The Old Collegians' Scholarship is valued at 100% of tuition fees and is awarded to an existing student who is going into Year 12. The student must be able to demonstrate an academic standard to take full advantage of subjects offered, a commitment to the Catholic faith and embrace the Mercy ethos including participation in community events such as social justice fundraising and awareness, public speaking, music, sport or drama. This scholarship recognises students who combine academic excellence with active engagement in the College community and living out the College's values of justice, mercy, compassion, hospitality and hope.
eligibility_criteria: For CURRENT students only; Must be progressing to Year 12; Must demonstrate academic standard to take full advantage of VCE subjects offered; Must demonstrate commitment to the Catholic faith; Must embrace the Mercy ethos; Must participate in community events such as social justice, public speaking, music, sport or drama
special_conditions: For current students only. Awarded to an existing student going into Year 12. Selection based on demonstrated academic standard, commitment to Catholic faith, and active participation in Mercy community and co-curricular activities.

overview: Mount Lilydale Mercy College offers the Academic Scholarship to current students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship awards $1000 towards college fees and is awarded to two students from each year level in recognition of exceptional performance.
description: The Academic Scholarship is valued at $1000 towards college fees and is awarded to two existing students from each year level in recognition of outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship acknowledges students who have demonstrated excellence across their academic studies throughout the year. This award recognises the commitment and dedication of high-achieving students at the College, celebrating their success and encouraging continued academic excellence. The scholarship is awarded annually to eligible students in Years 7 through to Year 11.
eligibility_criteria: For CURRENT students only; Must be enrolled in Years 7, 8, 9, 10 or 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Two awards per year level
special_conditions: For current students only. Two scholarships awarded per year level (Years 7-11) in recognition of outstanding academic achievement throughout the year.

overview: Mount Lilydale Mercy College offers the Parents and Friends' Scholarship to current students demonstrating outstanding effort and engagement across their education. The scholarship awards $500 towards college fees and is funded by the Parents and Friends' Association to recognise students who show commitment and dedication.
description: The Parents and Friends' Scholarship is valued at $500 towards college fees and is awarded to two existing students from each year level in recognition of outstanding effort. This scholarship is funded through the Parents and Friends' Association, which raises funds through volunteer work and community events within the College. The scholarship recognises students who may demonstrate strong effort, commitment and engagement rather than purely academic excellence, celebrating the diverse contributions of students to the College community. Two awards are presented per year level to acknowledge sustained effort and dedication.
eligibility_criteria: For CURRENT students only; Must be enrolled in Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12; Must demonstrate outstanding effort and engagement; Two awards per year level
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: For current students only. Two scholarships awarded per year level in recognition of outstanding effort. Funded by the Parents and Friends' Association.

school_name: Mount Pleasant Road Nunawading Primary School

program_name: Mount Ridley P-12 College Academic Scholarship
overview: Mount Ridley P-12 College offers an Academic Scholarship to support families in covering essential educational costs. The scholarship is supported by business partners and is available to students across all year levels in the school.
description: Mount Ridley P-12 College invites families to apply for an academic scholarship to ensure equitable access to education. Successful recipients receive funds that may be applied towards educational items including textbooks, uniform costs, specialised programs, material charges, or special events such as graduation and excursions. The scholarship application process requires completion of an application form with two suitable references. A shortlisting process is conducted by members of the College leadership team to select recipients. The scholarship value and number of awards vary and are determined based on available business partner contributions and applicant merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolled at Mount Ridley P-12 College; Must complete application form with two suitable referees; Subject to College leadership team assessment
special_conditions: Successful recipients are determined through a shortlisting process by College leadership team. Funds must be applied towards eligible educational items only.

school_name: Mount Evelyn Christian School - Ranges Tec Campus
Mount Rowan Secondary College-LinkUp - Ballarat Learning Exchange Campus
overview: Mount Rowan Secondary College offers the Academic Scholarship Program to recognise students achieving a high standard of school work with exceptional achievement in literacy and numeracy. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate admirable attitude and leadership qualities.
description: Mount Rowan's Academic Scholarship Program awards two scholarships annually to students attending the college. Each scholarship is valued at $1,200.00 total, paid as a $200.00 annual credit to the student's school account over six years. The program recognises students achieving high standards in school work, particularly in literacy and numeracy, who also display admirable attitude and active leadership qualities. To maintain the scholarship in successive years, recipients must maintain high academic standards and demonstrate active membership of the school community through participation in co-curricular activities and community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Mount Rowan Secondary College; High standard of school work required, especially in literacy and numeracy; Must demonstrate admirable attitude; Must show leadership qualities; Must maintain active participation in school community activities for continuation; Nominations accepted from parents and primary school principals
special_conditions: Scholarships are paid as a credit to the student's school account at the rate of $200.00 each year over six years. To ensure continuation of the scholarship in successive years, recipients are expected to maintain a high standard of school work and to demonstrate active membership of the school community. Nominations can be made by parents and principals of primary schools.

overview: Mount Rowan Secondary College offers the Academic Scholarship Program to recognise and reward students who achieve a high standard of school work, particularly in literacy and numeracy. The program recognises students who demonstrate admirable attitude and leadership qualities within the school community.
description: The Academic Scholarship Program awards scholarships to two students annually who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and strong character. Each full scholarship is valued at $1,200.00 over six years, paid as a credit to the student's school account at the rate of $200.00 each year. Recipients are identified through nominations from primary school principals and parents. To ensure continuation of the scholarship in successive years, recipients are expected to maintain a high standard of school work and to demonstrate active membership of the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be nominated by a parent or primary school principal; Must demonstrate high standard of school work especially in literacy and numeracy; Must show admirable attitude and leadership qualities; Must maintain high academic standards and active school community membership for continuation
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a six-year basis. Recipients must maintain a high standard of school work and demonstrate active membership of the school community to retain the scholarship in successive years. Payment is made as an annual credit to the student's school account at $200.00 per year.

overview: Mount Rowan Secondary College offers the ACE TEAMS Program to aspirant and capable students seeking acceleration in STEAM disciplines. The program combines Technology, Engineering, Arts, Mathematics and Science through cross-curricular thematic inquiry activities that take students deeper into the curriculum.
description: ACE TEAMS is a selective acceleration program for Years 7 to 10 that combines Technology, Engineering, Arts, Mathematics and Science through cross-curricular thematic inquiry activities. Students in the Academy follow the Mount Rowan documented curriculum while undertaking extended investigations and immersive activities to explore integrated content in ways that allow them to build new knowledge. Each term, academy students undertake an exposition of their learning for parents, teachers and friends, which is a critical strategy for embedding student learning. Expressions of interest are accepted from eligible students who demonstrate capability in STEM disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 10; Must be aspirant and capable students in STEAM disciplines; Expression of interest required; Selection based on demonstrated capability and readiness for acceleration program
special_conditions: This is an acceleration program for selected students. Each term students participate in learning expositions. Program focuses on extended investigations and immersive learning activities in STEAM disciplines.

school_name: Mount Erin Secondary College-Mount Erin Deaf Facility
Mount View Primary School-Mount View Deaf Facility
Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Mount Scopus offers the Ruben Fried Scholarship to Jewish students entering Year 7 who are currently attending government or non-Jewish schools and would be unable to attend without significant financial assistance. This co-funded scholarship from the Spotlight Foundation and Mount Scopus enables access to an immersive Jewish and general education.
description: The Ruben Fried Scholarship provides a family contribution of $5,000 per year for tuition fees for each of the six years of secondary schooling (Years 7-12), with a significant contribution also made towards the cost of the Year 10 Ulpan program in Israel. The scholarship is means-tested and requires applicants to submit a Financial Assistance Application Package along with recent school and NAPLAN reports. Selection involves an interview with the College Principal, Head of Secondary School, and Director of the Alter School of Jewish Studies and Ivrit. Whilst only one Ruben Fried Scholarship is offered each year, additional awards at a reduced level of assistance may be available for other qualified applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Jewish students entering Year 7; Must be currently attending a government school or non-Jewish school; Must be unable to attend without significant financial assistance; Must complete Financial Assistance Application Package; Australian residents
special_conditions: Applicants required to meet with Bursary Committee. Scholarship is primarily for Jewish students not currently enrolled at a Jewish school. Enrolment and administrative fees of $400 are payable by scholarship recipient and are not covered by the scholarship. Non-refundable enrolment processes must be completed upon selection. Scholarship recipients must maintain satisfactory conduct and attendance.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Mount Scopus offers the Gesher Bursaries to support families in bridging the gap between the Child Care Subsidy received for kindergarten and school tuition fees from Prep to Year 6. These grants enable a broader range of families to provide their children with an immersive Jewish education during their formative years.
description: Gesher Bursaries provide grants of up to $44,000 in fee relief for Prep to Year 3 or up to $75,000 for Prep to Year 6. The bursaries are designed to assist families who have been assessed to receive the Child Care Subsidy for kinder and may not have previously felt eligible for assistance. Families can apply at any time by contacting the Finance Manager for a confidential discussion. The determination of assistance is based on each family's individual financial and personal circumstances, assessed annually by the College's Bursary Committee.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current or new students entering Prep; Must have been assessed to receive Child Care Subsidy for kinder; Jewish students; Families with financial need
special_conditions: Applications can be made at any time by new or current families. Fee assistance is available for entry in Prep and above. Assistance determination is made based on each family's individual circumstances based on their financial and other information. Annual reassessment required. College reserves right to adjust assistance based on family circumstances.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Mount Scopus offers a comprehensive range of financial bursaries and grants to assist Jewish students in all year levels from Prep to Year 12 in accessing a Scopus education. The College's needs-based assistance program aims to help families who otherwise would not be able to attend pay tuition fees.
description: Mount Scopus provides a range of financial bursaries and grants available to assist students in all year levels from Prep to Year 12 to help defray the cost of tuition fees. The program is strictly needs-based, distributed irrespective of academic ability and targeting financial help where it is most needed. A formal assessment of parents' financial and personal circumstances is undertaken annually. Between a fifth and a quarter of students attend Mount Scopus as a result of the College's fee assistance program. Applications can be made at any time by new or current families through the Fee Assistance Application Process.
eligibility_criteria: Jewish students; All year levels Prep to Year 12; Open to new or current families; Financial need demonstrated through formal assessment; Fee assistance available for entry in Prep and above
special_conditions: Formal assessment of parents' financial and personal circumstances undertaken annually by Bursary Committee. Annual reassessment required. College reserves right to adjust assistance. Determinations made based on individual family circumstances. High cost of living and property prices for Jewish families acknowledged in assessment process. Maximum anonymity maintained in assessment process.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Mount Scopus offers the Gusta Besen Scholarships to support talented musicians in Years 3-11. These scholarships are offered on a needs-basis to students who demonstrate musical ability and would benefit from financial assistance with music tuition costs.
description: Two Gusta Besen Scholarships are available annually for students in Years 3-11, including new students entering the College in Years 4 or above. Scholarship recipients will receive one year of music tuition at no cost. The scholarships are offered on a needs-basis, so applicants must demonstrate financial need. Parents who are recipients of formal fee assistance or who otherwise feel that they should qualify due to their financial circumstances are encouraged to contact the Credit Department for a confidential discussion about the application process. Applications close at the start of December for the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 3-11; Including new students entering in Years 4 or above; Demonstrated musical ability; Financial need required; Recipients of formal fee assistance or demonstrable financial circumstances
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients will receive one year of music tuition. Applications close at the start of December for the following year. Applicants must contact Credit Department for confidential discussion about application process.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Mount Scopus offers the Bernard McCrae Scholarship to support talented musicians in Years 3-11. This scholarship is offered on a needs-basis to a deserving student who demonstrates musical ability and would benefit from financial assistance with music tuition costs.
description: One Bernard McCrae Scholarship is available annually for a student in Years 3-11, including new students entering the College in Years 4 or above. Scholarship recipients will receive one year of music tuition at no cost. The scholarship is offered on a needs-basis, requiring applicants to demonstrate financial need. Parents who are recipients of formal fee assistance or who otherwise feel that they should qualify due to their financial circumstances are encouraged to contact the Credit Department for a confidential discussion about the application process. Applications close at the start of December for the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 3-11; Including new students entering in Years 4 or above; Demonstrated musical ability; Financial need required; Recipients of formal fee assistance or demonstrable financial circumstances
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship recipient will receive one year of music tuition. Applications close at the start of December for the following year. Applicants must contact Credit Department for confidential discussion about application process.

overview: Mount Waverley Secondary College offers the Community Bank Pinewood Scholarship to recognise and support Year 11 students who demonstrate outstanding commitment to community service. The scholarship aims to encourage and support community-focussed students with their studies and school-related expenses.
description: The Community Bank Pinewood Scholarship is valued at $1,500 and is awarded to a Year 11 student who demonstrates dedication to contributing to the community both inside and outside of school. The scholarship supports recipients with their studies and school-related expenses, including educational activities such as school band travel. The selection process involves application and review by Community Bank Pinewood and school staff. Recipients are recognised for their outstanding community work and citizenship, with the scholarship available on an annual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 enrollment; Demonstrated commitment to community service; Outstanding citizenship; School-based and community involvement
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded annually. Recipients must be Year 11 students with demonstrated leadership in music or community engagement.

overview: Mt Hira College offers secondary scholarships to students showing exceptional promise who will act as ambassadors for the college. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and potential to excel.
description: Mt Hira College provides secondary scholarships for students in Year 6 and above seeking entry to the secondary school. Applicants are required to sit an examination comprising two components: English and Mathematics. The selection process includes the written examination, an interview, and analysis of the applicant's academic performance. Further details regarding financial value, duration, and specific renewal conditions are available by contacting the college directly. Recipients are expected to act as ambassadors for the college and maintain high standards of academic achievement and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Grade 6 and above seeking secondary school entry; Must complete the scholarship examination; Selection based on examination results and interview performance; Academic performance analysis required
special_conditions: Scholarship examination includes English and Mathematics components. Selection process includes written examination, interview, and analysis of 2024 academic performance. Recipients expected to serve as ambassadors for the college.

overview: Mullauna Secondary College offers the Excel High Achievers Program as a selective program for high-performing students. The program operates from Years 7 to 10 and extends students' learning across all core subjects, with most participants operating 6-12 months ahead of expected level.
description: The Excel High Achievers Program is a selective enrichment program designed for high-performing students at Mullauna Secondary College. Students enrolled in the program benefit from advanced curriculum content and challenging learning experiences across all core subjects. The program operates across Years 7 to 10 and aims to extend and accelerate the learning of identified high achievers. Participants typically demonstrate academic performance 6-12 months ahead of expected level. Selection is based on demonstrated high achievement in primary school and identification of gifted and talented characteristics. The program provides differentiated and enriched learning opportunities to ensure these students are appropriately challenged and supported in their educational journey. The program is non-monetary and focuses on curriculum enrichment and acceleration.
eligibility_criteria: High-performing students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Selection typically based on primary school performance and teacher recommendation; Must meet school's gifted and talented identification criteria
special_conditions: Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and identification of high-performing student profile. Students operate at accelerated pace, typically 6-12 months ahead of expected level.

overview: Mullauna Secondary College offers the Challenge Program to all Year 7 students as a multi-disciplinary transition program. The program supports students' transition into secondary school with an understanding of the college's core values of Curiosity, Courage and Community.
description: The Challenge Program is an integrated, multi-disciplinary program offered to all Year 7 students at Mullauna Secondary College. The program is designed to support students' transition from primary to secondary education and develops understanding of the college's core values of Curiosity, Courage, and Community. The program includes several focus areas: Time Capsule where students create a personal record; My School Community where students explore what it means to be a great community member; This is Me where students develop self-awareness and explore their future goals; and Contributing to My Community which culminates in a community improvement project. The program operates throughout Year 7 and is non-monetary, focusing on social-emotional learning and whole-child development alongside academic curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all Year 7 students; Mandatory participation as part of core curriculum
special_conditions: Program is mandatory for all Year 7 students as part of the college's transition and curriculum experience. Focus areas include time capsule creation, school community exploration, personal goal-setting, and community contribution through projects.

overview: Mullauna Secondary College offers SkillZ@Mullauna as an inter-disciplinary program for all Year 9 students. The program combines learning across multiple disciplines and operates for 8 periods per cycle, allowing students to develop integrated knowledge and skills.
description: SkillZ@Mullauna is an innovative inter-disciplinary program offered to all Year 9 students at Mullauna Secondary College. The program operates for 8 periods per cycle and is designed to integrate learning across multiple subject areas. Students develop practical skills, knowledge and confidence through project-based and contextual learning experiences. The program combines elements from multiple disciplines including the Arts, English, Health and Physical Education, Science and Technology. Recent iterations have focused on themes such as 'It's My Life!' to support student wellbeing and decision-making. The program is non-monetary and forms a core component of the Year 9 curriculum experience. Completion of SkillZ@Mullauna is mandatory for all Year 9 students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all Year 9 students; Mandatory participation as part of core curriculum; All students participate in the program
special_conditions: Mandatory program for all Year 9 students. Program operates for 8 periods per cycle. Inter-disciplinary approach integrating multiple learning areas. Annual themes support student wellbeing and life skills development.

overview: Mullauna Secondary College offers acceleration (advanced placement) of Year 10 students into VCE Studies. High-achieving Year 10 students have the opportunity to undertake one or more VCE subjects ahead of the typical Year 11 start date.
description: Mullauna Secondary College provides acceleration opportunities for high-ability Year 10 students to undertake VCE (Victorian Certificate of Education) subjects. This allows capable students to progress at an accelerated pace and study at the senior secondary level while still in Year 10. The program is designed for students who have demonstrated exceptional academic ability and readiness for advanced study. Students may undertake VCE subjects in areas of particular expertise and interest, including access to both academic VCE and VET (Vocational Education and Training) subjects through the Mullum Cluster partnership. This is a non-monetary program that provides enhanced curriculum opportunities and advanced academic pathways. The school continues to trial access to VCE Advanced Placement subjects for high ability Year 10 students.
eligibility_criteria: High-ability Year 10 students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic performance; Must show readiness for senior secondary level study; Selection based on demonstrated achievement in Year 10 subjects
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated academic achievement and teacher recommendation. Students must demonstrate readiness for VCE-level study. Program provides access to full range of VCE and VET subjects through Mullum Cluster. Students who undertake VCE subjects in Year 10 progress to Unit 3 and 4 in Year 11.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Gandel Besen House Campus

overview: Nagle College offers the Neal Wilson Music Scholarship to recognise students actively involved in instrumental music who intend to study classroom music through their senior years. The scholarship provides financial support to encourage excellence in music performance and continued musical study.
description: The Neal Wilson Music Scholarship is valued at $500 and is awarded to a selected music student who is actively involved in instrumental music and intends to study classroom music up to and through their senior years. This scholarship is open to current Nagle music students in Years 7 to 11. The scholarship recognises sustained commitment to music learning and encourages students to continue their musical journey as they progress through secondary school. Selection is based on demonstrated instrumental ability, level of participation in school music programs, and commitment to continuing music study in senior years.
eligibility_criteria: Current Nagle music students in Years 7 to 11; Actively involved in instrumental music; Intend to study classroom music through senior years
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the school based on musical ability, involvement in school music programs, and commitment to continuing music study.

program_name: Music Bursary - Brass and Woodwind Instruments
overview: Nagle College offers a limited quantity of music bursaries for students who take up particular brass or woodwind instruments. The bursaries are designed to support students who wish to learn instruments essential for school ensembles and reduce financial barriers to instrument study.
description: Nagle College allocates a limited quantity of music bursaries for students who take up particular brass or woodwind instruments including Trombone, Saxophone, Flute, Clarinet, Trumpet, Euphonium, Bass Guitar, Guitar, Piano, Drum Kit and Percussion. The bursaries reduce lesson fees for eligible students. Students may choose to hire an instrument within the program, with brass and woodwind instruments currently available without hire fee. To be considered, students must tick the box 'I wish to apply for a Bursary' on the Expression of Interest Form and select their instrument preference(s). Group lessons on these instruments further lower the costs, with fee information provided once an Expression of Interest form is lodged to the College Music Department.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolling students in Year 7 or current students; Selecting to study brass or woodwind instrument; Completing Expression of Interest Form with bursary application checkbox
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited quantity available. Bursary reduces lesson fees but does not provide instruments. Students may hire brass and woodwind instruments without hire fee under the program. Group lesson options available which further reduce costs.

overview: The Arthur Grassby Scholarship provides funding for Year 11 East Gippsland students to walk the Kokoda Trail in Papua New Guinea. The scholarship recognises student commitment to understanding local history, honouring military sacrifice, and developing personal leadership through adventure and challenge.
description: The Arthur Grassby Scholarships, valued at $5,300, are available to Year 11 students at secondary colleges across the East Gippsland Shire including Nagle College. The scholarships provide funding to support participation in a Kokoda Trek with Adventure Kokoda in July, offering students insight into the Gippsland connection to the Kokoda Trail and the sacrifices made by men from the community. The application process begins with submission of answers to a short questionnaire and a short essay of not less than 400 words. Shortlisted applicants are then interviewed by current or ex-servicemen and women. Successful applicants receive training at designated locations and are accompanied on the trek by supporters of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students; East Gippsland resident; Selected for interview following essay submission and questionnaire; No mention of academic threshold; Selected applicants must complete required training
special_conditions: Applicants must write a short essay of not less than 400 words outlining their reasons for application. Shortlisted applicants are interviewed by current or ex-servicemen and women. Successful applicants must complete training and participate in the Kokoda Trek in July. Scholarships cover trek costs and meals; additional costs to winners are flights between Melbourne and Brisbane, trekking equipment, and personal spending money.

overview: Neerim District Secondary College offers Elevate 15, an accelerated enrichment program designed for a small cohort of 15 high-potential Year 7 students. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and provides a personalised learning journey tailored to each student's strengths, interests, and passions.
description: Elevate 15 is a selective enrichment program offering accelerated and differentiated curriculum delivery for gifted Year 7 students. The program features customised lessons adhering to Victorian curriculum standards while addressing individual student interests and learning levels. Students benefit from small class sizes and dedicated planning time enabling interdisciplinary learning across subject areas. The program is monitored and supported by the college's leading teacher of teaching and learning and is designed to be rigorous yet supportive, ensuring students can build confidence alongside their natural capabilities. The financial value and other specific conditions are not publicly detailed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: High-potential Year 7 students; Selection limited to small cohort of 15 students; Admission subject to school assessment process
special_conditions: Program is distinctive in curriculum design with interdisciplinary learning across various subject interests (video gaming, sport, fashion, physics, geography). Enrollment is limited to 15 students per year level.

overview: Nazareth College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise and reward students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and intellectual excellence. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 and continuing through secondary education who display exceptional scholastic ability across a range of subjects.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate an outstanding standard of scholastic ability across a range of subjects. Recipients are selected through a rigorous assessment process including the Edutest examination, NAPLAN results, school reports, and a formal interview with college leadership. The exact financial benefit value is not publicly specified on the official page but represents a significant tuition fee reduction. The scholarship is awarded for the duration of the student's enrolment at Nazareth College from Year 7 to Year 12, subject to annual review and maintenance of academic standards. Selection involves assessment of the Edutest examination results, recent school reports, NAPLAN test results, and an interview with the Principal and Deputy Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 from external schools; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent; Outstanding scholastic ability required across a range of subjects; Display exemplary personal conduct at all times; Adhere to all school expectations; Commit fully to all aspects of studies
special_conditions: All Scholarship holders are expected to complete their secondary education at Nazareth College. All Scholarship holders are expected to contribute positively and enthusiastically to school life. Each Scholarship holder's progress in the College and contribution to College life will be reviewed annually, subject to application. Ordinarily, no student will hold more than one Scholarship. Scholarships must be repaid to the College should a Scholarship holder leave to attend another school.

overview: Nazareth College offers the General & Sporting Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate excellence in sporting achievement, community contribution, and leadership. The scholarship rewards students who excel in attitude, sporting prowess, participation in cultural and artistic life, and service to the wider community.
description: The General & Sporting Excellence Scholarship recognises students who excel across multiple dimensions including athletic achievement, community service, and cultural participation. Recipients must demonstrate evidence of selfless contribution to the wider community, proven sporting excellence or merit, and enthusiastic participation in the cultural and artistic life of the school. The exact financial benefit is not publicly specified but represents tuition fee assistance. Selection is based on the Edutest examination result every two years, school reports, NAPLAN results, supporting documentation, and a formal interview with college leadership. External records of high achievements or performances must be provided as part of the interview process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 from external schools; Must demonstrate evidence of selfless contribution to wider community; Must exhibit sporting excellence or proven sporting merit; Must show enthusiastic participation in cultural and artistic life of school; Display exemplary personal conduct; Adhere to all school expectations; Commit fully to all aspects of studies; Complete Edutest examination every two years
special_conditions: All Scholarship holders are expected to complete their secondary education at Nazareth College. All Scholarship holders are expected to contribute positively and enthusiastically to school life. Each Scholarship holder's progress in the College and contribution to College life will be reviewed annually, subject to application. Ordinarily, no student will hold more than one Scholarship. Scholarships must be repaid to the College should a Scholarship holder leave to attend another school.

overview: Nazareth College offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise and support students with exceptional talent in performing arts including drama, dance, and instrumental music. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding performance ability and commitment to the performing arts program.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional talent and achievement in acting, dance, or instrumental music. Recipients must show evidence of external records of high achievements or performances such as AMEB levels, band performances, or stage experience. Students applying for this scholarship are not required to sit the Edutest if applying for Performing Arts only, but must complete auditions on their principal study area. The exact financial benefit is not publicly specified but represents tuition fee assistance. Selection includes preparing and presenting a short performance of their choice at interview, potential impromptu performance tasks, and formal audition in the Wheeler Auditorium where students must demonstrate their principal specialisation. All applicants must supply their own backing tracks for auditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 from external schools; Must demonstrate exceptional talent in acting, dance, or instrumental music; Must provide evidence of external high achievements or performances (AMEB levels, band or stage performance); Must be prepared to participate in, promote and support Performing Arts events; Must participate in college ensembles; Display exemplary personal conduct; Adhere to all school expectations; Commit fully to all aspects of studies
special_conditions: Students applying for Performing Arts Scholarship are not required to sit Edutest if applying for Performing Arts only, but if also applying for Academic and/or General & Sporting Excellence must sit Edutest every two years. All Performing Arts scholarship applicants must supply their own backing tracks. Auditions held in Nazareth College Wheeler Auditorium. All Scholarship holders are expected to complete their secondary education at Nazareth College. All Scholarship holders are expected to contribute positively and enthusiastically to school life. Each Scholarship holder's progress in the College and contribution to College life will be reviewed annually, subject to application. Ordinarily, no student will hold more than one Scholarship. Scholarships must be repaid to the College should a Scholarship holder leave to attend another school.

overview: Nazareth College awards a Monash University bursary to the top achieving student in Year 11 to assist with the cost of Year 12 studies. This bursary is designed to provide financial support to high-achieving students as they transition into their final year of secondary education.
description: The Monash University Year 12 Bursary is awarded to the highest-achieving student in Year 11 at Nazareth College to help pay for the cost of Year 12 studies. The bursary is provided through Monash University and represents financial assistance for the top student in the year level. The exact financial benefit value is not publicly specified on the college's official scholarship page. Selection is based on Year 11 academic performance and achievement. The bursary is awarded once annually and assists eligible students in managing the costs associated with their final year of secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be the top student in Year 11 at Nazareth College; Must be completing Year 11 at Nazareth College; Award based on academic achievement in Year 11
special_conditions: Award is provided in recognition of top Year 11 academic achievement. Bursary is intended to help offset Year 12 study costs.

overview: Nazareth College offers a Tuition Fee Concession program for families experiencing financial hardship. This program provides fee assistance to eligible families holding valid Centrelink concession cards, supporting access to quality Catholic education.
description: The Tuition Fee Concession is a financial assistance program designed to support families experiencing financial difficulty. Eligibility is determined through Centrelink concession card verification. Families holding a Family Health Care Card, Family Card, or Pensioner Concession Card (PPS only) may be eligible for fee relief. The application process requires completion of an official form with parent/legal guardian details and current Centrelink concession card information. The exact financial benefit varies based on family circumstances and is determined on a case-by-case basis. The college assesses applications and provides fee concessions to eligible families to ensure continued access to education.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold valid Centrelink concession card - Family Health Care Card, Family Card, or Pensioner Concession Card (PPS); Must provide current card details and expiry information; Must complete official tuition fee concession application form; Parent/legal guardian must provide full details; Application assessed on case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Requires verification of Centrelink concession card status. Applications assessed individually based on family circumstances. Fee relief amount determined on case-by-case basis by the college.

overview: Nelson Park School provides access to financial supports for school uniform to eligible families. The program assists families in meeting the cost of school uniforms, supporting equitable access to school resources.
description: Nelson Park School recognises that uniform costs can create barriers to participation for families experiencing financial difficulty. The school provides access to financial supports specifically designated for school uniform expenses. This support is administered through the Wellbeing Officers who coordinate assistance for families. The program operates as part of the school's broader commitment to equity and inclusion, ensuring that all students can access school resources regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Nelson Park School students; Families experiencing financial difficulty; Application through school wellbeing office
special_conditions: Contact the Student Wellbeing Officer in the relevant area of the school for further information and support. Contact the main office on (03) 52 783 620.

overview: Newcomb Secondary College offers an Accelerated Learning Program as part of its outstanding Middle Years offering. The program provides accelerated entry from Year 7 for students with high intellectual potential and exceptional academic ability.
description: The Accelerated Learning Program is an outstanding Middle Years program that includes the option of accelerated entry from Year 7. This program is designed to challenge and extend high-achieving students with high intellectual potential who would benefit from an accelerated and enriched learning curriculum. No specific financial value is mentioned as this is a curriculum acceleration program rather than a fee-based scholarship. The program aims to provide educational experiences of an appropriate type and quality for students identified as having exceptional academic ability. The selection process involves assessment of student aptitude in core subjects.
eligibility_criteria: High intellectual potential; Exceptional academic ability; Currently enrolled in Year 6 seeking Year 7 entry; Must have applied for mainstream enrolment at Newcomb Secondary College
special_conditions: Students may accelerate into Year 11 VCE subjects at Year 10, allowing them to begin transition to VCE early. Only students who have applied for and accepted a place in mainstream enrolment at Newcomb Secondary College are eligible to apply.

overview: Newhaven College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating outstanding abilities in academic, sporting, performing arts, leadership and community involvement. The scholarship is awarded to Secondary School applicants at any level who demonstrate exceptional aptitude across these broad areas. Recipients are selected based on application and interview process.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to applicants in Secondary School at any level who can demonstrate outstanding abilities in one or more of the following fields: academic achievement, sporting excellence, performing arts, leadership, and community involvement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional abilities across a broad range of areas. The exact financial benefit varies and is determined on a case-by-case basis following application and interview assessment. Recipients are selected through a rigorous application and interview process, with opportunities opening annually. The scholarship aims to provide access to the College's excellent teaching staff and facilities for talented students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Secondary School students at any level; Must demonstrate outstanding abilities in one or more of: academic achievement, sporting excellence, performing arts, leadership, or community involvement; Selection based on application and interview
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated outstanding abilities across broad range of areas. Scholarships awarded on basis of application and interview.

overview: Newhaven College offers Music Scholarships to talented, passionate and dedicated students who demonstrate strong musical potential. The scholarship is available to both new and existing students entering Years 5, 7, 9 and 11. Recipients gain access to individual music tuition and reductions in academic tuition fees.
description: Newhaven College is looking to award Music Scholarships to talented, passionate and dedicated new or existing students of music who demonstrate strong musical potential. The scholarship includes individual music tuition as well as reductions in academic tuition fees for the duration of the student's enrolment at Newhaven College, subject to satisfactory progress. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 5, 7, 9, and 11. Music is a vibrant part of life at Newhaven College, with students participating in musical productions, concerts, evening soirees and performances throughout the year. Selection is based on demonstrated musical potential and passion for music. The scholarship is subject to satisfactory academic and musical progress.
eligibility_criteria: Talented and passionate students demonstrating strong musical potential; Available to both new and existing students; Open to students entering Years 5, 7, 9, and 11; Subject to satisfactory progress during enrolment
special_conditions: Scholarship includes individual music tuition and reductions in academic tuition fees. Conditional upon satisfactory progress during enrolment at Newhaven College. For enquiries, contact Matthew Goss, Head of Music on 5956 7505.

overview: The Peter Reith Scholarship recognises students who strive to make a positive difference in the world through their actions, in honour of Peter Reith, founding father of Newhaven College. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 10 or 11 who demonstrate commitment to community and leadership. Exceptional academic performance is not required, but above-average academic performance is expected.
description: The Peter Reith Scholarship is awarded to a student who, like Peter Reith, strives to make a positive difference in the world through their actions. The scholarship is in recognition of Peter Reith's extraordinary contribution to Newhaven College as one of its founding fathers in 1980. The scholarship is open to all students entering Year 10 or 11. Whilst exceptional academic performance is not necessarily required, an above-average academic performance is expected. The scholarship value was established at $2,000 and is paid directly to the school to help cover school fees. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to making a positive difference through community engagement and personal actions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or 11; Above-average academic performance expected; Demonstrated commitment to making positive difference in world through actions; Community involvement and leadership qualities
special_conditions: Scholarship valued at $2,000 paid directly to school to help cover Year 12 fees. Above-average academic performance required but not necessarily exceptional. Awarded to student demonstrating commitment to positive community impact.

overview: Newhaven College offers Bursary Scholarships to provide financial assistance to students with demonstrated financial need. Thanks to the generosity of members of the school community, the College has a limited number of bursary places available for students wishing to enrol. Expressions of Interest are open at all times.
description: Newhaven College has a limited number of bursary places for students entering the College. The bursary scholarship provides an opportunity for students and families who have financial need but wish to enrol at Newhaven College. Financial need is a primary factor in Bursary Scholarship assessment. Applications are assessed on individual circumstances and family income. Applicants must provide copies of their latest Income Tax Assessment and descriptions of financial eligibility. School reports and supporting documentation are required. The exact financial benefit varies based on individual circumstances and the level of demonstrated financial need. Expressions of Interest for bursaries are open at all times, and applications are treated with absolute confidentiality.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students wishing to enrol at Newhaven College; Demonstrated financial need is primary consideration; Family income assessed; Any year level may apply
special_conditions: Financial need is primary factor in assessment. Applicants must provide Income Tax Assessment documentation and written description of eligibility. School reports and supporting documentation required. Absolute confidentiality assured. Applications can be submitted confidentially marked to Registrar at any time.

overview: Newmark Primary offers financial scholarships to children who would not ordinarily be able to attend the school but have the potential to thrive in our educational setting. Our scholarship program is designed to support families facing financial challenges while enabling access to our independent school education.
description: Newmark Primary offers a set number of financial scholarships based on the combined income and unique circumstances of individual families. The scholarship program includes both full fee and part fee support, with funding assessed on a case-by-case basis. Applications require submission of evidence of income (payment summary, business tax return, or recent payslips) and evidence of financial support such as Government Issued Health Care Card or Family Tax Benefit Part A eligibility. The selection process involves a comprehensive financial assessment of the family's circumstances to determine the level of support available. Recipients are welcomed as valued members of the school community and expected to thrive in our setting.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families demonstrating financial need; Evidence of combined family income required; Ability to demonstrate potential to thrive in school environment; Case-by-case assessment of unique family circumstances
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are assessed on a case-by-case basis. The number of scholarships available is set and limited. Families interested in applying should contact the admissions team at admissions@newmark.vic.edu.au. Annual review of financial circumstances may be required to maintain ongoing support.

Noble Park English Language School-Cranbourne Campus
Noble Park English Language School-Springvale Campus
Noble Park English Language School-Casey Campus

overview: Noble Park Secondary College offers the Class of 1976 Scholarship to students experiencing disadvantaged circumstances. The scholarship is funded by an anonymous benefactor who attended Noble Park Secondary College and studied at the University of Melbourne.
description: The Class of 1976 Scholarship provides a living allowance of $18,000 per year for three years, paid in half-yearly instalments of $9,000 in April and September. Eligible candidates are selected on the basis of academic merit and financial disadvantage demonstrated through VTAC equity schemes applications. Preference is given to students who completed Year 12 at Noble Park Secondary College. If no eligible students from Noble Park Secondary College qualify, preference will be given to students from other government schools within an incremental 5 km radius. Scholarship offers are made a few days after Victorian Year 12 ATAR results are released in December. No application is required — students are automatically considered if they have applied to the University of Melbourne via VTAC.
eligibility_criteria: Must have completed Year 12 at Noble Park Secondary College or nearby government schools within 5 km radius; Must be enrolling in an undergraduate degree at University of Melbourne via VTAC; Must be experiencing disadvantaged circumstances as demonstrated through VTAC Equity Schemes application; Selection based on highest overall level of disadvantage demonstrated
special_conditions: No formal application required. All students from Noble Park Secondary College who have applied to the University of Melbourne via VTAC will automatically be considered. Recipients must agree to give permission for the University to provide their name and course details to the donor and provide a brief report on the benefits of the scholarship if requested. Scholarship is subject to Coursework Scholarships Terms and Conditions and can be deferred to the following academic year.

overview: Northcote High School offers the Northcote Thrive Empower Fund to provide financial support to students and families experiencing hardship. The fund assists students in need by providing access to essential items required for school. This tax-deductible program was the first of its kind in Australia to receive tax-deductible status for purchasing essential school items for students in financial difficulty.
description: The Northcote Thrive Empower Fund is a financial assistance program designed to support students from families experiencing financial hardship. The fund provides assistance with the cost of essential items including school uniforms, digital devices required for learning, textbooks and books, and travel cards. Students and families can apply to this fund on a case-by-case basis. The school was the first in Australia to be granted tax-deductible status for donations that allow the school to purchase essential items for students in need through this Empower fund. Applications are processed by the school's Wellbeing and Support team. The program operates on an ongoing basis throughout the year, with families encouraged to contact the school to discuss their circumstances and eligibility. There are no formal renewal conditions, but applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial need and hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students currently enrolled at Northcote High School; Students and families must demonstrate financial hardship; Evidence of need required for approval; Open to all year levels; No residency restrictions beyond school enrolment
special_conditions: Northcote High School was the first school in Australia to be granted tax-deductible status for donations allowing the school to purchase essential items for students in need through the Empower fund. Applications must be made to the school to discuss individual circumstances. The fund is administered through the school's wellbeing and support services.

overview: Northcote High School's students from eligible families can access the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) administered through the Victorian Government. This fund provides financial assistance to cover costs associated with school camps, sporting activities, and excursions. Families with a health care card are eligible to apply for this government-funded support.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government program that provides financial assistance to eligible families to help cover the costs of camps, sporting activities, and excursions. Students are eligible if their families hold a valid health care card. The fund provides $400 per student in 2026. This is a government-administered program, not a school-specific scholarship. Eligible families can apply directly through the government application portal. The fund also provides access to information about other community and government assistance programs for items such as books and school uniforms. Applications are processed by the Victorian Government and funds are provided to families to offset costs of school-related activities.
eligibility_criteria: Families must hold a valid Victorian health care card; Open to students at all year levels; Must be enrolled at Northcote High School; Residency in Victoria required for eligibility; Income thresholds apply based on health care card eligibility
special_conditions: This is a Victorian Government program, not a school-operated scholarship. $400 per student provided in 2026. Families must hold a health care card to be eligible. Applications must be made through the government system, not directly to the school. Students may also be able to access other community and government assistance funds for items such as books and uniforms.

overview: Northcote High School offers the ACE (Advanced Curriculum Extension) Program, a selective entry program for highly able students in Years 7 and 8. The program caters to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential. Selection is based primarily on Educational Assessment placement test results, with NAPLAN considered as supporting evidence.
description: The ACE Program is a selective entry program at Northcote High School designed to extend and challenge highly able students in Years 7 and 8. All enrolled Year 7 students are welcome to apply and are automatically considered for placement based on their performance in the Educational Assessment placement test conducted at the school. Entry to the program is primarily determined by Educational Assessment data from the Year 7 placement test, with NAPLAN results considered as supporting information. Testing occurs in September and October each year. All enrolled students are considered for a place in the ACE program without needing to submit a separate application, as selection is made based on test results from the standard Educational Assessment testing program undertaken by all students entering Year 7. Places in ACE classes are offered in December prior to the Year 7 Orientation day. Year 8 students can apply if vacancies arise in the program, which is advertised internally towards the end of Year 7.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students entering Year 7 at Northcote High School; Must be currently enrolled for Year 7 entry; Participation in Educational Assessment placement test required; NAPLAN results considered as supporting evidence; No separate application required; Year 8 students eligible only if vacancies arise; Must demonstrate high ability through assessment results
special_conditions: Selection is made via the Educational Assessment testing program undertaken by all students entering Year 7. Testing results from other school programs are not considered in the ACE selection process. The school's decision is final. Places are offered in December prior to Year 7 Orientation day. Occasionally, places become available in the ACE program for the start of Year 8, advertised internally towards the end of Year 7. The assessment is made up of multiple-choice sections and written components. The school works with Academic Assessment in conducting these tests.

overview: The ACE Program is a select entry program offered by Northcote High School to cater for highly able students in Years 7 and 8. The program recognises students who have demonstrated academic aptitude in one or more specific curriculum areas and have high educational aspirations.
description: Northcote High School's ACE (Advanced Curriculum Extension) Program is designed for students who show academic ability beyond most of their peers. Students in the program are taught in similar groups for most subjects but receive alternate texts allowing for greater analysis and complexity, with generally more challenging and advanced work that encourages higher-level thinking skills. The program duration is for Years 7 and 8, with optional continuation into Year 9 and beyond through VCE advancement subjects. Selection is via application and the Educational Assessment placement test, with assessments normally conducted in September and October. The program provides students with access to additional extension and challenge programs such as the Philosophy Club.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or in Year 8; All enrolled Year 7 students are welcome to apply; Students must have a confirmed place at Northcote High for the following year; NAPLAN results are considered but selection is primarily based on Educational Assessment placement test results; Year 8 students can apply if vacancies arise
special_conditions: Selection is final and testing results from other school programs are not considered in the ACE selection process. Students in the program learn together in their Connect group for most subjects and follow the same curriculum as other students at their year level. Places in ACE classes are normally offered in December prior to Year 7 Orientation.

program_name: Northcote Thrive Empower Fund (Student in Need)
overview: The Northcote High School Thrive Empower Fund provides financial assistance to students facing hardship. The school is the first in Australia to have been granted tax-deductible status for donations allowing the purchase of essential items for students in need.
description: The Northcote Thrive Empower Fund is a purpose-directed fund governed by an approved charter and Australian Tax Office fund rules. Tax-deductible donations to the Empower fund allow the school to purchase essential items including uniforms, books, travel cards and other necessities for students from families experiencing financial hardship. The fund operates on an ongoing basis throughout the year. Applications for grants from the Thrive Empower fund are available to students currently enrolled at Northcote High School who are facing financial or other hardship. Families can contact the school to discuss individual applications and circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all current Northcote High School students; Students must be experiencing financial hardship; Consideration of personal and family circumstances; No specific year level restrictions
special_conditions: Applications and inquiries should be directed to Leanne Petroff on 9488 2300 or via email. The school also provides access to government assistance programs such as the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) for eligible families and State Schools' Relief for disadvantaged students.

overview: Northcote High School offers a music scholarship focused on the bassoon to support students in the school's vibrant music program. The scholarship aims to further develop the music program through supporting talented bassoon students.
description: Northcote High School, which has a renowned large music program with over 300 students participating in co-curricular music activities, offers a scholarship on the bassoon. The school's instrumental music program provides tuition in strings, woodwind, and limited places for percussion and voice, along with weekly lessons and ensemble rehearsals. Students in the instrumental program participate in core ensemble performances and have access to elective ensembles. The specific details regarding the financial value, duration, and selection criteria for the bassoon scholarship were not fully detailed in available sources but inquiries should be directed to the music department.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in bassoon performance; Currently enrolled at Northcote High School or prospective student; Demonstrated musical ability or potential
special_conditions: Contact Jackie Brogan, Director of Music, at brogan.jacqueline.j@edumail.vic.gov.au for further information on the bassoon scholarship and music program opportunities.

school_name: North Melbourne Primary School - Molesworth Street Campus
Northern Bay P-12 College-Staughton Vale Outdoor Education Centre
overview: Northern Bay College, through The NBC Challenge Committee, awards Year 7 & 8 Scholarships to recognise academic excellence. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in core curriculum including English, Mathematics, Languages Other than English and Humanities. Recipients receive tailored support to enhance their potential and prepare for progression to Year 9-12.
description: The Year 7 & 8 Scholarships under the Academic pillar recognise students demonstrating outstanding achievement across core curriculum subjects of English, Maths, Languages Other than English and Humanities, as well as other learning experiences. The scholarship provides benefits at the College valued at approximately $4,000. Recipients work with the College to identify areas of support that would assist them most, including specialist equipment, books and materials; specialised development opportunities such as leadership development, public speaking, coaching, mentoring, tutoring, and accelerated learning; and membership/registration support up to $200 per year. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and continues through Year 8, assisting students as they progress towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students at Northern Bay College; Outstanding academic achievement in core curriculum; Invited to apply in July of Year 6; Three-stage selection process including written application and interview
special_conditions: Scholarships are tailored for each scholar and scholarship pillar. Support includes access to specialist equipment, books, materials, leadership development, public speaking, coaching/mentoring/tutoring, accelerated learning, guest speakers, conditioning programs and excursions. Membership and registration support provided up to $200 per year.

school_name: Northern Bay P-12 College-Staughton Vale Outdoor Education Centre
program_name: Year 7 & 8 Scholarships - Sciences and Technologies Pillar
overview: Northern Bay College awards Year 7 & 8 Scholarships within the Sciences and Technologies pillar to support students with exceptional potential in science and technology disciplines. This scholarship recognises achievement in Science, STEM/STEAM, digital technologies and technical subjects including Wood, Food, Textiles, Robotics and Mechanical disciplines.
description: The Year 7 & 8 Scholarships under the Sciences and Technologies pillar recognise students demonstrating exceptional ability in Science (including chemistry, physics, biology), STEM/STEAM, digital technologies and Technologies (including Wood, Food, Textiles, Robotics and Mechanical). The scholarship provides benefits at the College valued at approximately $4,000. Recipients work with the College to identify areas of support including specialist equipment, books and materials; specialised development opportunities such as leadership development, public speaking, coaching, mentoring, tutoring and accelerated learning; and membership/registration support up to $200 per year. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and supports students through Year 8 as they progress towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students at Northern Bay College; Exceptional achievement or aptitude in sciences and technologies; Invited to apply in July of Year 6; Three-stage selection process including written application and interview
special_conditions: Scholarships are tailored for each scholar and scholarship pillar. Support includes access to specialist equipment, books, materials, leadership development, public speaking, coaching/mentoring/tutoring, accelerated learning, guest speakers, conditioning programs and excursions. Membership and registration support provided up to $200 per year.

school_name: Northern Bay P-12 College-Staughton Vale Outdoor Education Centre
program_name: Year 7 & 8 Scholarships - Sport, Physical Education and Health Pillar
overview: Northern Bay College offers Year 7 & 8 Scholarships within the Sport, Physical Education and Health pillar to recognise excellence in interschool and community sport, Physical Education and health. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to physical excellence.
description: The Year 7 & 8 Scholarships under the Sport, Physical Education and Health pillar recognise students demonstrating exceptional achievement and participation in interschool and community sport, Physical Education and health activities. The scholarship provides benefits at the College valued at approximately $4,000. Recipients work with the College to identify areas of support including specialist equipment, books and materials; specialised development opportunities such as leadership development, public speaking, coaching, mentoring, tutoring, accelerated learning, guest speakers and conditioning programs; and membership/registration support up to $200 per year. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and supports students through Year 8, assisting their transition and progression towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students at Northern Bay College; Exceptional achievement in interschool or community sport; Demonstrated excellence in Physical Education and health; Invited to apply in July of Year 6; Three-stage selection process including written application and interview
special_conditions: Scholarships are tailored for each scholar and scholarship pillar. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and supports students through Year 8 as they develop their artistic skills and progress towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus.

program_name: SEED Program Scholarship - Geelong Golf Club
overview: Through its SEED Program (Sport, Empowerment, Education and Development), Northern Bay College in partnership with Geelong Golf Club and Belgravia Foundation awards scholarships to students. The program recognizes and supports students of all abilities who excel in sport and demonstrate commitment to personal development.
description: Through its SEED Program (Sport, Empowerment, Education and Development), six students from years 7 to 10 have been awarded a scholarship from Geelong Golf Club with the support of the Belgravia Foundation. To support the scholarship program, Geelong Golf Club hosted fundraising events to raise vital funds to support the scholarship program. With over $2000 raised annually, Geelong Golf Club and Belgravia Foundation continue to foster support and guidance to the Northern Bay College scholarship students. Geelong Golf Club, Belgravia Foundation and Northern Bay College work together throughout the school year as the SEED Program continues to grow and nurture students of all abilities. Financial value: Varies based on fundraising and program needs.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 7 to Year 10; Enrolled in SEED Program; Demonstrated commitment to sport and personal development; All ability levels welcome
special_conditions: Partnership with Geelong Golf Club and Belgravia Foundation. Funding dependent on annual fundraising efforts. Program designed to nurture students of all abilities.
