program_name: NBC Challenge Committee Year 7 & 8 Scholarships - Academic Pillar
overview: Northern Bay College, through the NBC Challenge Committee, awards Year 7 & 8 Scholarships to recognise academic excellence in core curriculum including English, Mathematics, Languages Other than English, and Humanities. These scholarships support students as they transition through junior secondary education and prepare for progression to the senior campus.
description: The NBC Challenge Committee Year 7 & 8 Scholarships recognise exceptional academic achievement across core curriculum areas. Scholarships provide benefits at the College to the value of approximately $4,000 per year. Support is tailored for each scholar and includes access to specialised development opportunities such as leadership development, coaching, mentoring, and accelerated learning. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and continues through Year 8, assisting students as they progress towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus and prepare for completing Year 12. Selection occurs through a three-stage process beginning in July of the year prior to entry, comprising a written application, shortlisting for interview, and final scholarship award.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 at Northern Bay College; demonstrated academic excellence in core curriculum; open to all campuses of Northern Bay College; completion of written application and interview process in July
special_conditions: Scholars and parents/carers meet with the College to identify areas of support that assist them most. The scholarship commences when Year 7 studies begin and aims to assist students as they progress towards the Goldsworthy Years 9-12 Campus. Support includes materials, specialist equipment, books, leadership development, public speaking, coaching/mentoring/tutoring, accelerated learning, guest speakers, conditioning programs and excursions.

Selection occurs through a three-stage process beginning in July of the year prior to entry, comprising a written application, shortlisting for interview, and final scholarship award.

overview: Northern Bay College awards Year 7 & 8 Scholarships recognising excellence in The Arts including Music, Dance, Dramatic Arts, Visual Arts, Media and Graphics. overview: Northside Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement. These scholarships are available to current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10. The scholarship provides meaningful financial support through a discount on tuition fees, with the final percentage at the Principal's discretion.
description: The Academic Scholarship recognises high academic achievement measured through a scholarship examination, along with evidence of previous academic results including school reports, awards, and references. Recipients receive up to 70% off tuition fees, with the final percentage awarded at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship is awarded following completion of a scholarship examination and an interview with the Principal. Students may apply for multiple scholarships but can only hold one scholarship at any time. The scholarship continues for the duration of enrolment, subject to academic performance and conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on funded temporary VISA; Must demonstrate high academic achievement in scholarship examination; Must show evidence of previous academic results including school reports, awards, and references; Late applications not eligible
special_conditions: Final percentage awarded is at the discretion of the Principal. Students may apply for more than one scholarship but can only hold or be awarded one scholarship at any one time. Scholarship is subject to maintaining academic performance and conduct standards consistent with the College's expectations.

overview: Northside Christian College offers General Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate special talents or interests across a broad range of areas. These scholarships recognise achievements in sports, music, art, and other disciplines. The program is open to current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who display special talents or interests in a broad range of areas including the physical environment, sports, music, or art. Recipients receive up to 70% off tuition fees, with the final percentage at the Principal's discretion. Selection is based on the student's demonstrated talents, submission of supporting documents, at least one written reference from a school, mentor, community organisation or club, and an interview with the Principal. The scholarship continues for the duration of enrolment and is subject to maintaining appropriate standards of conduct and achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must be Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on funded temporary VISA; Must demonstrate special talents or interests in sports, music, art, or other broad areas; Must provide at least one written reference supporting the application; Late applications not eligible
special_conditions: Final percentage awarded is at the discretion of the Principal. Students may apply for more than one scholarship but can only hold or be awarded one scholarship at any one time. At least one written reference from the student's school, mentor, community organisation or club is required in support of the student's suitability.

overview: Northside Christian College offers Christian Leadership Scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities and commitment to Christian values. The scholarship is open to current and prospective students entering Year 10 and provides substantial financial assistance.
description: The Christian Leadership Scholarship recognises students who display exceptional leadership qualities aligned with Christian values and the College's mission. Recipients receive up to 70% off tuition fees, with the final percentage awarded at the Principal's discretion. Applicants must submit a completed application with supporting documents, a letter of recommendation from their Church pastor or minister detailing their involvement in their local church, and attend an interview with the Principal if shortlisted. The scholarship continues for the duration of enrolment and is subject to maintaining the College's standards of conduct and character.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students entering Year 10; Must be Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on funded temporary VISA; Must provide letter of recommendation from Church pastor or minister; Must demonstrate commitment to Christian values and leadership qualities; Late applications not eligible; Must support College Christian ethos
special_conditions: Final percentage awarded is at the discretion of the Principal. Requires letter of recommendation from Church pastor or minister confirming family's regular attendance and involvement in local Christian church. Students may apply for more than one scholarship but can only hold or be awarded one scholarship at any one time. Recipients must support the College Christian ethos and maintain conduct standards.

overview: Notre Dame College offers the Luigi Gigliotti Scholarship to support Year 12 students facing significant financial constraints for tertiary education. The scholarship was established from a bequest by a deceased member of the community and provides ongoing support from endowed investment funds to help students access university education.
description: The Luigi Gigliotti Scholarship was established through an estate bequest received by Notre Dame College from a deceased community member who held the college in high esteem. The enduring legacy of invested funds generates ongoing resources for Year 12 student scholarships to support tertiary study. The intent of this scholarship is to support students experiencing significant financial constraints that would prevent their family from sending their son/daughter to university, or who may face financial difficulty in being able to keep their son/daughter at university. Funds are presented privately to successful applicant(s) upon receipt by the college of confirmation of the applicant's enrolment into a university course. The scholarship is awarded after consideration of a range of criteria related to demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be able to demonstrate significant financial constraints preventing tertiary education access; Applications must be endorsed by the student's Head of House; Student must be enrolled in a university course to receive funds
special_conditions: Funds are presented privately to successful applicants. Recipients must provide confirmation of enrolment in a university course. The scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the college after consideration of demonstrated financial need and other specified criteria.

school_name: Northern Bay P-12 College-Hendy Street P-8 Campus

overview: Notre Dame College offers the Luigi Gigliotti Scholarship to Year 12 students facing significant financial constraints that would prevent their family from sending them to university or keeping them there. The scholarship was established from a bequest to the College and provides ongoing financial support for tertiary education.
description: The Luigi Gigliotti Scholarship came into being after Notre Dame College received a grant of money from the Estate of a deceased member of the community who held the College in high esteem. The enduring legacy of invested monies will continue to generate funds for Year 12 student scholarships for tertiary study long into the future. The intent of the Scholarship is to support students in regard to significant financial constraints that would prevent their family from being able to send their son/daughter to university, or who may face financial difficulty in being able to keep their son/daughter at university. Funds will be presented privately to successful applicant(s) upon receipt by the College of confirmation of the applicant's enrolment into a university course. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating financial need and commitment to pursuing tertiary education.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Demonstrates significant financial constraints preventing family ability to send student to university; Must be endorsed by current Head of House; Must provide confirmation of university course enrolment to receive funds
special_conditions: Scholarship Applications must be endorsed by the current Year 12 student's Head of House and are to be handed into Student Reception at the College or directly to Mr. John Cortese. Funds are presented privately to successful applicant(s) upon receipt by the College of confirmation of the applicant's enrolment into a university course.

overview: Notre Dame College offers the Sports Academy as a selective entry programme for talented athletes. The Sports Academy provides elite-level training and support for student-athletes in multiple sports including cricket, basketball, netball, AFL/W, tennis, soccer and other sports through comprehensive strength and conditioning sessions and skill development.
description: In 2026 the Notre Dame Sports Academy will be available for Year 7-12 students. Selected students will have access to weekly strength and conditioning sessions, fortnightly skill sessions in their chosen sport, sports dietician information and theoretical lessons in athlete-centred topics. The program includes partnerships with professional organisations such as Melbourne United (basketball), Melbourne Victory (soccer), and other elite coaching resources. Students receive specialised training tailored to their chosen sport, with field-based sports (AFL, Soccer, Hockey) and court-based sports (Netball, Basketball, Tennis) having different training models. The Academy aims to develop young athletes' skills while supporting their personal education and career development, with opportunities to represent regional and state pathway programs.
eligibility_criteria: Selection based on sporting ability and talent; Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10 entry points; Must be selected through talent identification process; Must maintain academic progress while participating
special_conditions: Year 10 participants: 2 strength and conditioning sessions per week. Students complete athlete-centred theory topics during theoretical lessons. Year 8 and 9 students may have different session frequency based on year level and sport. No disciplinary issues allowed while participating in the Academy.

overview: Notre Dame College offers eligible students the opportunity to accelerate a subject in Year 10, enabling them to begin their VCE studies early. This programme recognises high-achieving students and allows them to progress at an accelerated pace through their academic studies.
description: Notre Dame College provides eligible Year 10 students with the opportunity to accelerate a subject, enabling them to begin their VCE studies before Year 11. This programme is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and readiness to undertake advanced coursework. Eligible students have the opportunity to accelerate through selected subjects that can count toward their VCE certificate, allowing them to progress at an accelerated pace. Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance and teacher recommendation. Students who accelerate maintain their regular Year 10 timetable while beginning VCE studies in their accelerated subject.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Must be eligible based on academic performance; Subject eligibility may vary; Must have teacher recommendation; Demonstrated readiness for advanced coursework
special_conditions: Available to eligible students on a selective basis. Academic progress and conduct standards must be maintained. Subject selection must be made during regular Year 10 subject selection process.

overview: The McAuley Champagnat Programme is an innovative educational programme designed to meet the educational needs of disengaged and at-risk students. The programme focuses on holistic development and provides tailored educational pathways to enable maximum education, wellbeing and training outcomes for students who struggle to learn in a normal classroom environment.
description: The McAuley Champagnat Programme focuses on the holistic development of students in an effort to gain the maximum education, wellbeing and training outcomes. Notre Dame College tailors educational programmes to meet the individual needs of each student by drawing on a range of Victorian and National programmes including the Victorian Certificate of Applied Learning (VCAL), the Certificate in General Education for Adults (CGEA), and other vocational pathways. An Individual Learning Plan (ILP) is developed for each student as a core component of their success and educational achievement. Each student is allocated a welfare worker who is their primary point of support for all welfare, emotional and physical needs. Students at risk receive intensive case management. The programme is located at the Emmaus Campus and serves students who are disengaged or struggling in traditional classroom settings.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 students or older; Demonstrated disengagement from traditional schooling; Referral through school or community pathways; At-risk youth status; Requires assessment and placement through the College
special_conditions: Each student has an Individual Learning Plan (ILP) developed in collaboration with families and staff. Each student is allocated a designated welfare worker for ongoing one-on-one support. Intensive case management available for students at risk. Located at Emmaus Campus.

overview: Notre Dame College provides financial hardship support to ensure that no student whose parents are genuinely unable to afford to pay school fees in full or in part is denied admission to the College. The College works with families to provide flexible payment arrangements and fee assistance for those facing financial difficulty.
description: Notre Dame College has a clear commitment that no student whose parents are genuinely unable to afford to pay school fees in full or in part will be denied admission to the College. The College provides flexible financial arrangements to support families experiencing hardship. Payment options include direct debit/credit card instalment authorisation, payments made through participating banks, credit unions or building societies using a Biller Code and Reference Number, and pre-arranged payments taken directly from Centrelink benefits. Families experiencing changes in circumstances affecting their fee responsibility can complete Amendment to Fee Responsibility forms to adjust their contribution percentage or split billing arrangements. The Finance Department works directly with families to develop sustainable payment plans tailored to their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Parents/guardians genuinely unable to afford full or partial fee payment; Australian resident families; Requires application to Finance Department; Assessment of financial circumstance; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship
special_conditions: Application must be made to the Finance Department at the College. Assessment of financial circumstances required. Amendment to Fee Responsibility forms available for SPLIT BILLING (Child Basis) and SPLIT BILLING (% Basis) arrangements. Ongoing assessment may be required.

overview: Norwood Secondary College offers a selective Enrichment Program for students identified as highly motivated to achieve academically from Year 7 onwards. The program is designed for students who are intellectually curious, enjoy extra academic stimulation, and can work independently and collaboratively to achieve their goals.
description: The Enrichment Program (EP) operates from Year 7 to Year 10, emphasizing enrichment of the curriculum rather than acceleration. Students in EP classes are exposed to an enhanced curriculum with focus on building depth in their learning and developing critical thinking skills associated with academic excellence. Entry is by application only and there is no monetary value or fees associated with this government school program. The selection process involves an externally run achievement test, a copy of the student's most recent semester school report, and a written statement from both student and parent detailing their interest in and suitability for the program. Candidates are expected to demonstrate capacity to excel and be challenged across all academic areas, and are expected to participate significantly in all aspects of College life including national competitions, sports, performing arts and leadership roles.
eligibility_criteria: Must be identified as highly motivated to achieve academically; Must have received notification that their secondary school placement is Norwood Secondary College; Selection based on achievement testing, academic performance, student commitment to program, and parent endorsement; Intellectually curious and able to work independently and collaboratively
special_conditions: Entry is by application only. The selection process considers achievement testing, parent input and student academic performance, as well as strong student commitment to the program. EP class remains together as a cohort until the end of Year 9. Students are expected to involve themselves in all aspects of College life, such as national competitions, sports, performing arts and leadership. As a government school, this is a non-fee-paying program.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is available to Year 7 students for a one-year scholarship and Year 10 students for a three-year scholarship.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed to support the College's strong history of excellence and to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The scholarship includes a monetary value as well as mentoring sessions to further build on students' skills and offer ongoing support throughout their journey at NCC. For 2026, scholarships were available at Year 7 for a one-year scholarship and Year 10 for a three-year scholarship. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. The specific financial value and selection details are contained in downloadable scholarship information on the College website.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 or Year 10; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Must maintain consistently high academic efforts; Must display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: Year 7 scholarship is for one year; Year 10 scholarship is for three years. Recipients are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers the Leadership Scholarship for Years 9-11 students who demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities. The scholarship recognises students who excel in Business, Spiritual, or Community Service leadership categories.
description: The Leadership Scholarship is awarded to students in Years 9-11 who demonstrate exceptional leadership capabilities in one of three categories: Business, Spiritual, or Community Service. The scholarship includes a monetary value as well as mentoring sessions to further build on students' skills and offer ongoing support throughout their journey at NCC. This is a one-year scholarship that recognises students who show strong commitment to leading within the College community. Recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Years 9-11; Demonstrate leadership qualities in Business, Spiritual or Community Service; Maintain consistently high academic efforts; Display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: One-year scholarship. Recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Available in three categories: Business, Spiritual, or Community Service.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers the Sports Excellence Scholarship for Years 8-12 students who demonstrate elite performance in Basketball, Volleyball, or the NCC Fit program. The scholarship recognises students who excel in selected sports academies.
description: The Sports Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students in Years 8-12 who demonstrate exceptional athletic ability and commitment to one of the College's Sports Excellence programs: Basketball, Volleyball, or NCC Fit. The scholarship includes a monetary value as well as mentoring sessions to further build on students' skills and offer ongoing support throughout their journey at NCC. This is a one-year scholarship that supports students in the NCC Sentinels (the College's sports excellence teams). Recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Years 8-12; Must be part of the Academy program in Basketball, Volleyball, or NCC Fit; Demonstrate exceptional athletic ability; Maintain consistently high academic efforts; Display College values through behaviour
special_conditions: One-year scholarship. Recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Available in three sports/programs: Basketball Academy, Volleyball Academy, or NCC Fit.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers an Academic Scholarship for Year 7 students as a one-year award. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. It is part of the College's program to support excellence across multiple elements of student development.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 7 is offered as a one-year scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement. The financial value of this scholarship has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. The selection process involves assessment through standardised testing (ACER). Scholarship recipients are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Renewal is not automatic and will depend on continued academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be entering Year 7; Must have demonstrated exceptional academic ability in current schooling; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers an Academic Scholarship for Year 10 students as a three-year award. This scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and is designed to support students transitioning through Years 10, 11 and 12. It forms part of the College's commitment to nurturing overall student development.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 10 is offered as a three-year scholarship covering the student's enrollment from Year 10 through to Year 12. The financial value has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. Selection is conducted through ACER assessment. Scholarship recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and demonstrate College values through their behaviour. The three-year duration provides extended support for students achieving at the highest academic level.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be entering Year 10; Must have demonstrated exceptional academic ability in previous schooling; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: This is a three-year scholarship covering Years 10-12. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers Leadership Scholarships for Years 9-11 students in three categories: Business, Spiritual, and Community Service. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to contributing meaningfully to the school and broader community.
description: The Leadership Scholarship is offered as a one-year award for students in Years 9, 10, and 11, with three distinct categories recognising different forms of leadership excellence. Students may apply in the category of Business Leadership, Spiritual Leadership, or Community Service Leadership. The financial value has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. Selection criteria emphasise demonstrated leadership capabilities and contribution to the school community. Scholarship recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and demonstrate College values through their behaviour throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be in Years 9, 10, or 11; Must demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities in one of the three categories: Business, Spiritual, or Community Service; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: This is a one-year scholarship. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers a Sports Excellence Scholarship for Years 8-12 students excelling in Basketball through the school's Basketball Academy. The scholarship recognises outstanding athletic performance and commitment to the sport. It supports students in achieving excellence both on the court and in their academic studies.
description: The Sports Excellence Scholarship in Basketball is offered as a one-year award to students in Years 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12 who are members of the NCC Basketball Academy. The financial value has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. Selection is based on athletic ability, performance in Basketball Academy trials and assessments, and commitment to the sport. Recipients must participate in intensive training and competition through the Academy. Scholarship recipients are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour throughout the duration of the award.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be entering Year 8 to Year 12; Must demonstrate elite or advanced basketball ability; Must be accepted into the NCC Basketball Academy; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: This is a one-year scholarship. Participation in the NCC Basketball Academy is required. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers a Sports Excellence Scholarship for Years 8-12 students excelling in Volleyball through the school's Volleyball Academy. The scholarship recognises outstanding athletic achievement and commitment to the sport. It supports students in combining sporting excellence with academic success.
description: The Sports Excellence Scholarship in Volleyball is offered as a one-year award to students in Years 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12 who are members of the NCC Volleyball Academy. The financial value has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. Selection is based on athletic ability, performance in Volleyball Academy trials and assessments, and demonstrated commitment to the sport. Recipients participate in intensive training and competition through the Academy. Scholarship recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour throughout the duration of the award.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be entering Year 8 to Year 12; Must demonstrate elite or advanced volleyball ability; Must be accepted into the NCC Volleyball Academy; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: This is a one-year scholarship. Participation in the NCC Volleyball Academy is required. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

overview: Nunawading Christian College offers a Sports Excellence Scholarship for Years 8-12 students through the NCC Fit Academy, focusing on fitness, conditioning, and overall athletic development. The scholarship recognises students committed to health, fitness, and personal athletic development beyond traditional competitive sports.
description: The Sports Excellence Scholarship through NCC Fit is offered as a one-year award to students in Years 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12 who are members of the NCC Fit Academy. This program focuses on cardio, strength and conditioning training as part of the broader Sports Excellence Program launched in 2023. The financial value has not been publicly specified on the official scholarships page. Selection is based on commitment to fitness development and acceptance into the NCC Fit Academy. Recipients participate in structured fitness and conditioning programs. Scholarship recipients must maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour throughout the duration of the award.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be entering Year 8 to Year 12; Must demonstrate commitment to fitness and personal athletic development; Must be accepted into the NCC Fit Academy; Must be willing to accept the ethos and values of the College
special_conditions: This is a one-year scholarship. Participation in the NCC Fit Academy is required. Recipients of an NCC Scholarship are required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through their behaviour. Applications for 2026 scholarships have now closed.

school_name: Northern Bay P-12 College-Peacock Avenue P-8 Campus

overview: Oakleigh Grammar offers General and Academic Excellence Scholarships to talented, motivated and civic-minded students who demonstrate potential to make positive contributions in leadership and academic capacity. The scholarship recognises students with great academic standards and/or excellence in sport, music, leadership or community service.
description: Oakleigh Grammar, in conjunction with the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER), holds Scholarship exams for students entering Years 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10 in 2027. Successful applicants are curious learners, active sports participants, musically talented or community focused. To apply for a scholarship, a student must have a great academic standard and/or excellence in at least one of the following: sport, music, leadership, community service. All scholarship candidates must sit the ACER examination and provide school reports, NAPLAN test results, certificates of achievement, and evidence of sporting, musical, leadership and community service abilities. Students applying may include a reference from a teacher, principal, sporting organisation, church group or community service organisation, though this is not essential. Interviews will be held for shortlisted applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 6, 7, 8, 9 or 10 in 2027; Great academic standard and/or excellence in sport, music, leadership or community service; Display qualities aligned to School Values of Humility, Aspiration, Respect and Kindness; Must sit ACER examination; Provide school report and recent NAPLAN test results; Provide certificates of achievement; Both new and current Oakleigh Grammar students eligible
special_conditions: Exam will take place on Saturday 15 November 2025, with offers being sent to successful scholarship applicants in December prior to the end of the school year. In addition to scholarship application, a school application must be placed for the child. Discount code Y8FQNXZ available when submitting school application.

overview: Oberon High School awards the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in English, Mathematics or Science. This scholarship is for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and a thirst for knowledge combined with a passion for learning.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship rewards students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in one or more of English, Mathematics or Science. Students must demonstrate a thirst for knowledge and a passion for learning. The scholarship is offered for two years (Year 7 and Year 8) and includes full value of school fees for Years 7 and 8, an iPad purchased in Year 7, Year 7 Camp, Education Perfect package for Years 7 and 8, and all excursions for Years 7 and 8 (to the value of approximately $3,000). Recipients complete an application and interview process once their child's enrolment has been confirmed. Scholarships are redeemable against school expenses including school fees and textbooks.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding achievement in English, Mathematics or Science; Must demonstrate thirst for knowledge and passion for learning; Must complete application and interview; Awards made at school discretion
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years (Year 7 and Year 8). Continuing students receive the same benefits in Year 8. Recipients must maintain good standing and satisfactory academic progress throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Oberon High School offers the Leadership Scholarship to recognise exemplary leadership qualities. This scholarship is awarded to students who display outstanding communication, initiative, motivation and a passion to guide and direct their peers.
description: The Leadership Scholarship is awarded to students who display exemplary leadership skills including communication, initiative, motivation and a passion to guide and direct peers. This scholarship recognises that student leadership is the fundamental core of any successful school and Oberon prides itself on producing the leaders of tomorrow. The scholarship is offered for two years (Year 7 and Year 8) and includes full value of school fees for Years 7 and 8, an iPad purchased in Year 7, Year 7 Camp, Education Perfect package for Years 7 and 8, and all excursions for Years 7 and 8 (to the value of approximately $3,000). Recipients complete an application and interview process once their child's enrolment has been confirmed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must display exemplary leadership skills including communication, initiative, and motivation; Must demonstrate passion to guide and direct peers; Must complete application and interview; Awards made at school discretion
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years (Year 7 and Year 8). Continuing students receive the same benefits in Year 8. Recipients must maintain leadership standards and good conduct throughout the scholarship period.

overview: The Community (Good Citizenship) Scholarship is awarded to young citizens who have made significant contributions to their community, either at school or beyond. Recipients must demonstrate a strong social conscience and outstanding attitude towards their schoolwork.
description: The Community (Good Citizenship) Scholarship recognises Oberon's pride in the citizens it creates and is awarded to young citizens who have made a significant contribution in their community, be it school or other. The applicant needs to demonstrate a strong social conscience and an outstanding attitude towards their schoolwork. The scholarship is offered for two years (Year 7 and Year 8) and includes full value of school fees for Years 7 and 8, an iPad purchased in Year 7, Year 7 Camp, Education Perfect package for Years 7 and 8, and all excursions for Years 7 and 8 (to the value of approximately $3,000). The application and interview process begins once enrolment has been confirmed, and students may apply for multiple scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate significant contribution to community (school or other); Must demonstrate strong social conscience; Must maintain outstanding attitude towards schoolwork; Must complete application and interview; Awards made at school discretion
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years (Year 7 and Year 8). Continuing students receive the same benefits in Year 8. Recipients must maintain community engagement and good standing throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Oberon High School offers the Creative Arts Scholarship to students with outstanding experience and skills in either performing or visual arts. This scholarship recognises enthusiasm, participation and talent in creative expression.
description: The Creative Arts Scholarship is awarded to students who have outstanding experience and skills in either performing or visual arts. Oberon values creativity in all its forms and encourages students to express their individuality. Enthusiasm and participation, as well as talent will be highly regarded. The scholarship is offered for two years (Year 7 and Year 8) and includes full value of school fees for Years 7 and 8, an iPad purchased in Year 7, Year 7 Camp, Education Perfect package for Years 7 and 8, and all excursions for Years 7 and 8 (to the value of approximately $3,000). Recipients complete an application and interview process once their enrolment has been confirmed, and may apply for multiple scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding experience and skills in performing or visual arts; Must demonstrate enthusiasm and participation in creative activities; Talent in creative expression highly regarded; Must complete application and interview; Awards made at school discretion
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years (Year 7 and Year 8). Continuing students receive the same benefits in Year 8. Recipients must maintain participation in creative arts and good standing throughout the scholarship period.

overview: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students displaying high promise in their chosen sporting area. Continuing Oberon's history of producing exceptional sportspeople, this scholarship recognises both personal sporting achievements and exemplary sportsmanship.
description: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students displaying high promise in their chosen sporting area. With a history of producing exceptional sportspeople such as Patrick Dangerfield and Travis Boak, this scholarship recognises both personal sporting achievements and exemplary sportsmanship. The scholarship is offered for two years (Year 7 and Year 8) and includes full value of school fees for Years 7 and 8, an iPad purchased in Year 7, Year 7 Camp, Education Perfect package for Years 7 and 8, and all excursions for Years 7 and 8 (to the value of approximately $3,000). Recipients complete an application and interview process once their enrolment has been confirmed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate high promise in chosen sporting area; Must demonstrate personal sporting achievements; Must display exemplary sportsmanship; Must complete application and interview; Awards made at school discretion
special_conditions: Scholarship duration is two years (Year 7 and Year 8). Continuing students receive the same benefits in Year 8. Recipients must maintain sporting participation, exemplary sportsmanship and good standing throughout the scholarship period.

overview: The Year 7 Enhancement Program is a select entry program for dynamic students who are passionate about learning and ready to embrace opportunities for growth. Students demonstrate high ability in academic, leadership, citizenship and innovation.
description: The Year 7 Enhancement Program is a select entry class for students who demonstrate a high level of ability in a range of areas including academic, leadership, citizenship and innovation. The program is for dynamic students who are passionate about learning and are ready to embrace all the opportunities that Oberon High School has to offer. Students in the program are enthusiastic, collaborative team members, self-motivated learners, and community-minded leaders. Students access a range of engaging learning environments through various additional excursions, activities, competitions and opportunities. Students complete level 3 and 4 of the Compass Awards during Years 7 and 8, and have the opportunity to extend this in Year 9 by enrolling in the Duke of Edinburgh Award. All students from the enhancement program are encouraged to accelerate into VCE with at least one VCE subject being completed in Year 10. Applications are open in August and close on Friday 25th July (prior year). Applicants must complete an entrance test involving multiple-choice questions covering thinking and problem-solving, plus a creative writing task.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate high level of ability in academic, leadership, citizenship and innovation; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at feeder primary schools; Must complete entrance test; Must pass multiple-choice test and creative writing task; Students do not need to participate in scholarships to join enhancement program
special_conditions: This is not an academic acceleration program. All students at Oberon are catered to by teachers from their point of need. Students in the enhancement program receive additional opportunities through excursions, activities, competitions and special programs. The program emphasises personal development, positive attitudes towards learning and connection with community. Continuation in the program is subject to satisfactory engagement and progress.

overview: Officer Secondary College offers the High Achiever Program to students in Years 7-9 who exhibit high academic ability, exceptional talent, and a strong desire to excel. The program is designed to enhance learning capabilities by promoting problem-solving, critical thinking, and a keen sense of inquiry.
description: The High Achiever Program (HAP) is a selective entry program designed for students in Years 7-9 who demonstrate high academic ability, exceptional talent, and a strong desire to excel. The program aims to enhance learning capabilities by promoting problem-solving, critical thinking, and a keen sense of inquiry. Students address individual academic needs of high-achieving students through programs that foster 21st-century learning skills, develop deep understanding and critical thinking capabilities, enhance analytical and creative skills, and promote effective problem-solving and cooperative learning. Students receive close monitoring and support from a dedicated team including the Assistant Principal (Curriculum), Curriculum Leader, and Program Coordinator to ensure personalized attention to each student's needs and opportunities for exploring future career pathways. The program offers accelerated pathways for VCE and university enhancement options. No financial value is provided by the school as it is a government school without tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be in Year 6 at time of application; Expression of Interest required; Most recent Term 2 or Term 4 school report required; Latest available NAPLAN results required; Teacher's letter of recommendation required; Student's hand-written letter of application required; Must participate in High Ability Select Entry Test; Australian citizens and permanent residents only
special_conditions: Selection process involves Expression of Interest, submission of reports and NAPLAN results, teacher recommendation, and participation in written High Ability Select Entry Test. Shortlisted students receive invitation to sit the test on specified date and time. Successful candidates are notified in Term 3 of provisional acceptance. HAP students are encouraged to engage in a range of extension programs offered at the college including enrichment activities that further enhance their studies.

overview: Officer Secondary College offers the Performing Arts Masterclass to provide a select entry program for students interested in developing their Dance and Drama skills beyond the regular curriculum. The program offers a challenging and rewarding learning environment for like-minded students who wish to develop performing arts expertise while maintaining strong academic performance.
description: The Performing Arts Masterclass (PAM) is a select entry program designed to offer a challenging and rewarding learning environment for students interested in developing their Dance and Drama skills beyond the regular curriculum. The program aims to build skills in all academic learning areas with like-minded students while developing specialized skills in the Performing Arts fields. Key features include a full day of Performing Arts curriculum each week, skill development focus on choreography and performance, guest workshop incursions in dance and drama, excursion opportunities to professional theatre performances, community engagement and leadership opportunities, and participation in in-school and inter-school competitions. Students are expected to maintain balance between academic studies and performing arts commitments. No financial value is provided by the school as it is a government school without tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Strong interest in Dance and Drama; Commitment to both academic studies and performing arts; Ability to maintain balance between academic and co-curricular commitments; Selection based on application and audition process
special_conditions: Applications open 1 July. Parent Information Night held in March. Selection involves Expression of Interest and audition process. All students are expected to maintain a balance between their academic studies and Performing Arts commitments. Participation in extension programs and co-curricular activities is encouraged. Successful candidates are notified with details of program placement.

overview: OneSchool Global offers the OSG Fee Relief Program to support families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty meeting school fees. The program provides financial assistance to eligible students to help them continue their education at the school.
description: OneSchool Global provides the OSG Fee Relief Program for parents who are experiencing hardship or have difficulty meeting school fees in general. The program is designed to assist families with demonstrated financial need in accessing quality education at the school. The amount of fee relief varies based on individual family circumstances and financial assessment. Families interested in this program should contact the school directly for information about eligibility requirements, the application process, and available support amounts. The fee relief is administered through the school's central administration office.
eligibility_criteria: Parents or guardians experiencing financial hardship; Difficulty meeting school fee obligations; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Applications subject to financial assessment and verification of hardship circumstances. Amount of fee relief determined on case-by-case basis.

overview: OneSchool Global offers OSGAccelerate, a mandatory life-ready course for senior students in their final two years of school. The program is designed to empower students to develop essential workplace skills and leadership capabilities through partnership with FranklinCovey.
description: OSGAccelerate is OneSchool Global's life-ready course introduced in 2025 to replace the former Career Advantage Program (CAP). The program partners with FranklinCovey, the world leader in helping build exceptional leaders, teams, and cultures. The course is mandatory for all senior students and undertaken during Study periods, supported by teachers trained in Franklin Covey's 7 Habits of Highly Effective People. The program includes 120 hours of content delivered over two years, incorporating FranklinCovey's 7 Habits of Highly Effective Teens, Microsoft-certified courses (Excel and Word), and soft skills development. The curriculum emphasizes business fundamentals, communications, cross-cultural awareness, and workplace readiness. Students completing the program receive recognition for their development of life-ready skills.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Must be enrolled in senior secondary studies; Completion undertaken during Study periods
special_conditions: OSGAccelerate is a mandatory subject, not an extra-curricular scholarship program. No fee subsidy provided. No debenture offered. Work placement is not included in this program. Students with learning difficulties can be accommodated within the program structure.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy for Financial Hardship
overview: Our Lady of Fatima School, operating under Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS), provides fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The policy ensures that no Catholic family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy for primary schools provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty may be eligible for remission or exemption from school fees payable. The school requires families to contact the principal confidentially to discuss their financial circumstances. Fee concessions are determined based on demonstrated financial hardship and capacity to pay. The policy recognises the Catholic principle of preferential option for the poor and ensures accessibility to Catholic education regardless of family financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Students enrolled or seeking enrolment at Our Lady of Fatima School; Demonstrated inability to pay school fees; Confidential discussion with school principal required
special_conditions: Families must contact the school principal confidentially to discuss their financial circumstances. Fee concessions are determined on a case-by-case basis according to family capacity to pay. All confidential information is processed by the principal. Eligible families include those holding Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card or Health Card. The school also acknowledges government assistance programs available to families.

Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
program_name: MACS Concessional School Fees - Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Support
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School provides concessional school fees for families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage. This program recognises the school's commitment to reconciliation and closing the gap by removing financial barriers to Catholic education for Indigenous families.
description: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage are strongly encouraged to apply for concessional school fees as part of the MACS Enrolment Framework. The school provides fee remissions or reductions to eligible families through a confidential assessment process. The Principal of Our Lady of Mount Carmel has been delegated the authority to approve fee concessions for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families. The specific amount of fee reduction varies based on individual family circumstances and is determined following consultation with the Principal. This program applies to all year levels from Prep to Year 6 and is available ongoing for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Family has at least one member of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Open to all year levels (Prep to Year 6); Families must apply and demonstrate eligibility; Confidential assessment process applies
special_conditions: No child will be denied access to Catholic education due to genuine financial hardship. Applications are processed confidentially. Families are invited to discuss their circumstances with the Principal. The Principal has authority to determine appropriate fee concessions on a case-by-case basis.

school_name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
program_name: MACS Concessional School Fees - Health Care Card Holders
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School provides concessional school fees for families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for the Commonwealth Schools Flags Establishment (CSEF) funding. This program supports families experiencing financial difficulty in accessing quality Catholic education.
description: Families holding a current Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for CSEF are strongly encouraged to apply for concessional school fees under the MACS Enrolment Framework. The school provides fee remissions or reductions to eligible families through a confidential application and assessment process managed by the Principal. The specific amount of fee reduction is determined following consultation with the family and the Principal, and varies according to individual circumstances. This support is available for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school across all year levels from Prep to Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Currently holds a valid Health Care Card (HCC); Eligible for Commonwealth Schools Flags Establishment (CSEF); Open to all year levels (Prep to Year 6); Must apply for consideration; Confidential assessment applies
special_conditions: Policy states that no child will be denied access to Catholic education due to genuine financial hardship. Applications and family circumstances are treated confidentially. The Principal is delegated authority to approve appropriate fee concessions or remissions on a case-by-case basis.

school_name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
program_name: MACS Concessional School Fees - Severe Financial Hardship
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School provides concessional school fees for families experiencing severe financial hardship. This program ensures that financial circumstances do not prevent Catholic families from accessing quality education for their children.
description: Families experiencing severe financial hardship are strongly encouraged to apply for concessional school fees as detailed in the MACS Enrolment Framework. The school's Principal has been delegated authority by the MACS Board to provide fee remissions and/or concessions to families in genuine need. The application process is confidential and involves consultation between the family and the Principal to determine the appropriate level of fee assistance. The amount of fee reduction or remission is determined on a case-by-case basis, taking into account the family's specific circumstances. This assistance is available for all year levels from Prep to Year 6 and continues for the duration of the student's enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing severe financial hardship; Open to all year levels (Prep to Year 6); Must apply and demonstrate genuine financial need; Confidential assessment process required; No other specific conditions beyond demonstrated need
special_conditions: The school's policy explicitly states that no child will be denied access to a Catholic education merely because of financial restrictions. Applications are treated with complete confidentiality and discretion. Families are invited to have a confidential discussion with the Principal to explore available support options.

school_name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
program_name: MACS Concessional School Fees - Defence Force Families (DVA Gold Card)
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School provides concessional school fees for families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card. This program recognises the service of defence force members and supports their families in accessing Catholic education.
description: Families holding a valid DVA Gold Card are strongly encouraged to apply for concessional school fees under the MACS Enrolment Framework. The school provides fee remissions or reductions to eligible families who can demonstrate they hold the card. The application and assessment process is handled confidentially by the Principal, who has been delegated authority to approve appropriate fee concessions. The specific amount of fee reduction or remission is determined on an individual basis following consultation with the family. This support is available for all year levels from Prep to Year 6 and continues for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card; Open to all year levels (Prep to Year 6); Must apply for consideration; Application and assessment is confidential
special_conditions: Applicants must provide evidence of current DVA Gold Card status. Families are encouraged to have a confidential discussion with the Principal to determine the level of fee assistance available. The Principal has delegated authority to determine appropriate fee concessions on a case-by-case basis.

school_name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
program_name: MACS Concessional School Fees - Refugee Families
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Primary School provides concessional school fees for families who have been identified as refugees and hold an ImmiCard. This program supports refugee families in accessing quality Catholic education as they establish themselves in the Australian community.
description: Families identified as refugees and holding a current ImmiCard are strongly encouraged to apply for concessional school fees as part of the MACS Enrolment Framework. The school provides fee remissions or reductions to eligible refugee families through a confidential assessment process. The Principal has been delegated authority to approve appropriate fee concessions for refugee families. The specific amount of fee reduction or remission is determined on an individual basis following consultation with the family about their circumstances. This assistance is available for all year levels from Prep to Year 6 and continues for the duration of the student's enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as refugee family; Must hold a current ImmiCard; Open to all year levels (Prep to Year 6); Must apply for consideration; Confidential assessment process applies
special_conditions: Applicants must provide evidence of refugee status and hold a valid ImmiCard. Applications are handled with sensitivity and confidentiality. Families are invited to discuss their situation confidentially with the Principal to determine the appropriate level of fee assistance available.

Our Lady of the Way School
overview: Our Lady of the Way School offers concessional school fees to families experiencing financial hardship under the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) policy. The program ensures that all Catholic families, regardless of capacity to pay, can access Catholic education at the school.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Program provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing genuine financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. Eligible families receive concessional school fees covering all standard tuition fees and levies. Applications are assessed on individual circumstances and pro-rata concessions are granted if a family becomes eligible during the school year. The application process requires submission of an application form, a copy of a valid concession card (where applicable), and a CSEF application form if eligible. Approved applicants must enter into a direct debit or Centrepay deduction arrangement.
eligibility_criteria: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for CSEF; Families experiencing severe financial hardship; Families holding a DVA Gold Card; Families identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard; Families required to relocate their child due to long-term medical treatment at The Royal Children's Hospital Melbourne
special_conditions: Families receiving a fee concession must notify the school should their financial circumstances change sufficiently to affect the level of concession offered. Any offer of a concession is subject to the applicant entering into a Direct Debit or Centrepay deduction arrangement. Any default in payment may result in the concession being withdrawn. If an eligible concession card expires after the start of the year, the concessional fee rate will still be applied for the entire year. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility.

school_name: Our Lady Star of the Sea School
overview: Our Lady Star of the Sea provides fee concessions for families experiencing financial hardship. The school recognises that some families may struggle to meet their financial obligations and offers support through reduced fees.
description: Concessional school fees are available to families experiencing severe financial hardship, families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for the Camp, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF), families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card, families identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard, and families required to relocate their Australian resident child to St. Michael's Catholic Primary School, North Melbourne, for medical treatment at The Royal Children's Hospital, Melbourne. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility for the 2025 school year. Home schooling students (HSS) who attend a MACS primary school on a part-time basis and meet any of the criteria detailed above are also eligible. The reduction in fees is negotiated on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Any family experiencing severe financial hardship; Any family holding a Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for CSEF; Any family holding a DVA Gold Card; Any family identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard; Any family required to relocate their Australian resident child to St. Michael's Catholic Primary School, North Melbourne, for long-term medical treatment at The Royal Children's Hospital, Melbourne; Annual statutory declaration required to confirm eligibility
special_conditions: Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility. Families should contact the school principal confidentially to discuss their financial circumstances. Further information available from the Concessional Fee Policy for MACS primary schools and Application Form.

school_name: Our Lady Star of the Sea School
overview: Our Lady Star of the Sea participates in the government-funded Camp, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) program. This is a government payment to parents of eligible school-aged children to assist with school costs related to participation in camps, sports, and excursions.
description: The Camp, Sport and Excursion Fund is a government-funded payment to parents of school-aged children to assist with school costs specifically related to students' participation in camp, sport, or excursions. Eligible families include holders of a Health Care Card, concession card, or Veterans Affairs Card. Families must complete an CSEF application form and return it to the school office. The school provides information regarding the fund and eligibility criteria, as well as downloadable CSEF application forms. This program helps reduce the financial burden on families for participating in school camps (Years 3-6), sporting activities, and educational excursions.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of a Health Care Card; Holder of a concession card; Holder of a Veterans Affairs Card; School-aged child enrolled at Our Lady Star of the Sea School
special_conditions: Eligible families must complete an CSEF application form and return it to the school office. Information regarding the fund and eligibility can be downloaded from the school website. The fund covers camp, sport, and excursion costs.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart Primary
Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: OLSH College offers the Marie Louise Hartzer Bursary to provide financially supported education to students on the basis of financial need. The bursary is awarded at the discretion of the Principal and honours the legacy of Marie Louise Hartzer, first Congregational Leader of the Daughters of Our Lady of the Sacred Heart who founded the College in 1938.
description: The Marie Louise Hartzer Bursary has been established to provide financially supported education to students on the basis of financial need. Applicants will be assessed on the basis of personal and financial information supplied, interview with the student and parent(s)/caregiver and a character reference. Annual renewal of the Bursary is dependent upon the recipient's ongoing academic performance and contribution to the school community, and a review of the parents'/guardians' financial position. The Principal holds the final decision whether or not to award the Bursary and may consult with the OLSH College Board on this decision. The names of applicants and recipients of the Bursary will remain confidential. The application process includes completion of an application form and provision of required documentation, including financial information. Applicants must also complete the standard application for enrolment form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Financial need demonstrated; Character reference required; Must complete standard enrolment application form
special_conditions: Applications for the Year 7 2027 Bursary have now closed. Applications for the Year 7 2028 Bursary will open in February 2026. Annual renewal of the Bursary is dependent upon the recipient's ongoing academic performance and contribution to the school community, and a review of the parents'/guardians' financial position. A shortlist of applicants will then be interviewed and will be asked to provide a character reference. The final decision will be made by a panel from OLSH College including the Principal, Deputy Principals and a representative from the OLSH College Board.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Canowindra Campus
overview: Overnewton Anglican Community College offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 5, Year 7 and Year 9. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and continue through to the completion of Year 12, providing long-term support for students who demonstrate a strong commitment to learning.
description: The Academic Scholarship Program recognises and nurtures academic potential in students entering Year 5, Year 7, and Year 9. The College offers a select number of scholarships on a competitive basis. The scholarship value and duration varies, with the College reserving the right to set the number and value of scholarships on offer each year. Scholarships continue through to and include Year 12. Selection is based on performance in an external ACER examination administered by an independent national testing body, alongside consideration of school performance and other relevant factors. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted for an interview. If a student is awarded an Overnewton Scholarship, any bursary, financial discount or other financial support they currently receive will be replaced by the one that provides the most benefit to the student.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, or Year 9; Open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools; Must sit the external scholarship examination on the scheduled date; School performance and relevant factors considered in evaluation; Scholarship examination results from other schools cannot be transferred
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee of $140 includes access to a practice test with marked answers for self-correction. Applications are only considered from families whose children sit the examination on the scheduled date (Saturday 22 March 2025 for 2026 entry). Scholarships are offered on a long-term basis continuing through Year 12. Scholarship holders are bound by the College's Terms and Conditions of Enrolment and Scholarship Policy. If a scholarship awardee is withdrawn from the College prior to the conclusion of the scholarship term, the College requires the family to repay the amount of scholarship provided. The College reserves the right to set the number and value of scholarships on offer. The Principal's decision regarding scholarship awards is final with no disclosure of rationale. Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for an interview.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Yirramboi Campus
overview: Overnewton Anglican Community College offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 5. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, and continues through to the completion of Year 12.
description: Overnewton's Academic Scholarship Program for Year 5 entry reflects the College's commitment to supporting students who will make the most of the learning opportunities offered. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit as determined by performance on an external ACER assessment. The scholarship provides a reduction of annual tuition fees for the duration of enrolment from Year 5 through to Year 12. Selection includes a written ACER test (Primary Scholarship Test level) followed by an interview for shortlisted candidates. Recipients must maintain a visible and positive contribution to the culture of the College through conduct and behaviour, and demonstrate an ongoing commitment to academic progression. Scholarship holders must abide by the College's Scholarship Policy and Terms & Conditions of Enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5; Currently enrolled in Year 4 at any school; Open to both Overnewton students and those attending other schools; Must achieve satisfactory performance on the ACER Primary Scholarship Test; Must attend mandatory testing session on specified date
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee of $140 includes access to a practice test with marked answers. Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for an interview. Scholarship holders must contribute fully to the life of the College, demonstrating ongoing commitment to academic progression and consistently meeting all work and submission deadlines. Scholarship holders must maintain exemplary conduct at all times. Where a scholarship awardee is withdrawn from the College prior to the conclusion of the scholarship term, the family must repay the full value of scholarship funds awarded for the period up to withdrawal. The College reserves the right to set the number and value of scholarships on offer. The Principal's decision regarding scholarship awards is final with no disclosure of rationale.
