overview: Parkdale Primary School provides access to the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a Victorian Government financial assistance program. The fund assists eligible families to cover costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities, ensuring no student misses out on important educational experiences.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative that provides financial assistance to eligible families to cover the costs of school camps, excursions and sporting activities. The annual 2026 CSEF amount per student is $400. The allowance is paid directly to the school and used towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the student. Eligible families are those who hold a valid means-tested concession card or are temporary foster parents. Applications open in Term 1, and the application form must be submitted to the school by 26 June 2026. Only one CSEF application per student is allowed in the system.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid means-tested concession card; OR be a temporary foster parent; Students residing within the school zone are guaranteed a place at the school
special_conditions: The Victorian Government does not accept any late applications. CSEF system only allows one application per student. Parents/carers in shared custody situations who both hold a concession card must decide beforehand who applies for the CSEF on behalf of the student. If applied for CSEF at Parkdale Primary last year, reapplication is not required unless there has been a change in family circumstances. The allowance is provided by the Victorian Government to ensure no student misses out on educational and sporting activities that are part of a healthy curriculum.

overview: The Camps, Sports & Excursions Fund (CSEF) is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. This program supports students whose families demonstrate financial need.
description: CSEF is a Victorian Government-funded program designed to assist eligible families with the financial costs of camps, excursions, and sporting activities. The annual allowance is $150.00 per primary school student. Eligible families are those holding a valid means-tested concession card (Health Care Card, Low Income Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card) or families who are temporary foster parents. A special consideration category also exists for asylum seeker and refugee families. The allowance is paid directly to the school and used towards expenses relating to camps, excursions or sporting activities for the benefit of the student. Families may need to reapply annually unless there has been no change in circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card (Health Care Card, Low Income Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card); OR be a temporary foster parent; OR be part of an asylum seeker or refugee family; Income support demonstration required
special_conditions: Families who applied for CSEF in the previous year do not need to reapply unless there has been a change in family circumstances. New applicants should contact the school office to obtain an application form or download it from the relevant link.

overview: Parkdale Secondary College offers the Elite Sports Pathway (ESP) to provide a platform for students to follow their passion while undertaking a well-rounded and rigorous academic program. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to their chosen sport.
description: The Elite Sports Pathway provides opportunities for students to develop as life-long educated athletes. Parkdale currently has over 320 students enrolled in seven different sports: AFL, Basketball, Golf, Netball, Soccer and General Athletic Development. The program provides elite practices that allow students to reach their full potential as athletes, both physically and mentally. The program values challenge, respect, and resilience. Students participate in specialised training sessions and elite coaching while maintaining full academic requirements. The program is open to all students and there is no financial barrier to entry.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated sporting ability in one of the offered sports (AFL, Basketball, Golf, Netball, Soccer, General Athletic Development); Commitment to elite training and development; Willingness to undertake challenging athletic work
special_conditions: Students must balance elite sporting commitments with full academic curriculum. Program emphasises challenge, respect, and resilience as core values.

overview: Parkdale Secondary College offers the Arts Enrichment Pathway (AEP) to develop students' interests and skills in performance. The program engages specialised educators and industry professionals to provide opportunities to experience a variety of artistic styles including performance, composition, and wellbeing.
description: The Arts Enrichment Program is a vehicle for students to further develop their interest and skills in performance. The program provides opportunities to experience a variety of artistic styles, including performance, composition, and wellbeing. Students discover and enrich their love for dance, circus arts, musical theatre and acting, beyond attending technical classes. The program provides opportunities to perform in the musical production and Parky Does series. Students participate in two classes per week as well as the opportunity to engage with additional workshops, performances, competitions, and social events throughout the year.
eligibility_criteria: Interest and demonstrated ability in performing arts; Commitment to participate in two classes per week; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Students commit to two classes per week; Opportunities to perform in school productions and competitions; Engagement with industry professionals

overview: Parkdale Secondary College offers Science Technology Engineering Pathway (STEP) as an award-winning co-curricular program of innovation and excellence. The program recognises and supports students with demonstrated interest and ability in science, technology, engineering and mathematics.
description: STEP is an award-winning co-curricular program of innovation and excellence that is open to all students. In 2016, Parkdale was the proud recipient of the Graeme Clarke Award for Innovation in Science in Schools. The program has also been showcased on the Department of Education website. STEP offers a diverse range of highly engaging units that target specific year levels. The program empowers students to question, explore and engage in the world in which we live. Students are provided with access to a variety of stimulating, rich resources and learning opportunities including expert knowledge from professionals in their fields, opportunities to use various forms of technological equipment, and theoretical grounding in research and problem-solving.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in science, technology, engineering and mathematics; Demonstrated curiosity and problem-solving ability; Open to all year levels; Willingness to engage with stimulating learning opportunities
special_conditions: Access to stimulating resources and equipment; Engagement with STEM professionals; Award-winning program with government recognition

overview: Parkdale Secondary College offers the Writing Innovation Pathway (WIP) to support students with demonstrated interest and ability in creative and academic writing. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional writing skills and engagement with literary expression.
description: The Writing Innovation Pathway is an enrichment program designed for students who show strong interest and ability in writing. This program provides opportunities for students to develop and refine their writing skills across various genres and styles, including creative writing, academic writing, and performance-based writing. Students engage with stimulating writing tasks, receive feedback from experienced educators, and have opportunities to share their work through school publications and performances. The program fosters innovation, creativity, and a passion for the written word while building confidence in self-expression.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated writing ability and interest; Strong English language skills; Commitment to developing writing talents; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Focus on innovation and creativity in writing; Opportunities to publish and share work; Engagement with experienced writing educators

overview: Parkdale Secondary College offers an Instrumental Music Program with a longstanding history of providing students with diverse musical experiences both in and out of school. The program recognises students who develop self-confidence, responsibility, teamwork and musical appreciation through learning a musical instrument.
description: Parkdale Secondary College has a longstanding history of providing students with a large variety of musical experiences, both in and out of the school setting. Students who learn a musical instrument develop self-confidence, responsibility, teamwork, self-esteem, and above all, an enjoyment of lifelong musical appreciation. The program includes ensemble groups including contemporary rock band, jazz band, choir, stage band, percussion and guitar ensembles. The Year 7 and 8 classroom music program sets a strong foundation for students wishing to continue with either a performance pathway into VCE Music Performance or a technical pathway through VET Music Industry Certificate III. The program is supported by excellent facilities and experienced classroom and instrumental music staff who are both educators and musicians to help all students achieve excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in learning a musical instrument; Commitment to ensemble participation; Access to an instrument; Open to all year levels beginning at Year 7
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students develop musical skills with experienced staff; Opportunities to perform in school ensembles and community events; Pathways to VCE Music Performance and VET Music Industry; Engagement with both classroom and instrumental music instruction

overview: Puckapunyal Primary School awards Student Bursaries to recognise academic excellence and outstanding all-round achievement. The bursaries honour students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance, exemplary conduct, and strong involvement in co-curricular activities.
description: Student Bursaries are awarded annually to Year 6 students who best achieve academically and serve as great role models for their peers. Recipients are selected because they have achieved consistently throughout the year in all areas of the curriculum and in extra-curricular activities. The award includes a small plaque, an Office Works voucher valued at $500, and the recipient's name is inscribed on the school honour board to remain permanently. The bursaries have been presented for the past 30 years and are kindly donated by the RSL Social Club, Defence Bank and the school. Selection criteria include consistent academic achievement across all curriculum areas, exemplary behaviour and conduct, participation in extra-curricular activities, and demonstration of qualities that set the standard for other students to follow.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students; Academic achievement in all curriculum areas; Exemplary conduct and behaviour; Active participation in extra-curricular activities; Demonstrates strong role modelling for peers
special_conditions: Recipients must be nominated and selected by the school. Award recipients are recognised at the Year 6 Graduation Night ceremony. Their names remain permanently on the school honour board.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace offers fee exemptions for students experiencing financial hardship. This program provides access to educational opportunities for students who may otherwise face barriers due to financial constraints.
description: Fee exemptions are available for students experiencing financial hardship at Prace College. The exact financial benefit includes waiver of tuition fees for eligible students. Students may contact Prace directly for more information about eligibility and application procedures. The program is available for the duration of enrolment in accredited or pre-accredited courses. Selection is based on assessment of financial hardship circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Students experiencing financial hardship; Contact Prace for specific eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Fee exemptions are available for financial hardship. Contact Prace on 9462 6077 or email collegeadmin@prace.vic.edu.au for more information.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace provides complete fee waivers for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Victorians. This program ensures that Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students can access accredited qualifications and pre-accredited courses without cost of tuition fees.
description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Victorians can complete any accredited qualification under Skills First or pre-accredited course under Learn Local without the cost of tuition fees. This program provides full fee remission for eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The waiver covers all tuition costs for the duration of their enrolment. Selection is based on confirmation of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander status. Students must contact Prace for eligibility verification and enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Victorians; Must be eligible for Skills First accredited courses or Learn Local pre-accredited courses
special_conditions: Applicable to accredited qualifications under Skills First or pre-accredited courses under Learn Local. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Victorians can complete any course without tuition fees. Contact Prace for more information on eligibility.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace students may be eligible for government subsidy through the Skills First program for accredited courses. This program significantly reduces or eliminates tuition costs for eligible students undertaking nationally recognised qualifications.
description: Students may be eligible for a government subsidy through the Skills First program for accredited courses at Prace. The exact tuition fee reduction varies depending on individual circumstances and course selection, as determined by the Victorian Government and Victorian Skills Gateway eligibility criteria. The subsidy covers tuition fees for accredited courses for the duration of enrolment, subject to eligibility requirements. Selection is based on assessment of eligibility criteria through the Victorian Skills Gateway Eligibility Indicator. Students can enrol in a maximum of two government subsidised courses in any calendar year.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible students meeting Victorian Skills Gateway criteria; Must not hold a post-school qualification or be seeking a higher level qualification; Subject to individual circumstances at enrolment; Highest qualification must be below Diploma level for foundation skills courses
special_conditions: Government subsidy determined by Victorian Government. Accessing the Victorian Training Guarantee may impact future access to government subsidised training. Services and Amenities fee of $10 per class still applies. Fees subject to change given individual circumstances at enrolment.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace offers subsidised pre-accredited short courses through the Learn Local program. This program provides access to skill development opportunities at reduced cost for eligible community members.
description: Students may be eligible for government subsidy through the Learn Local program for pre-accredited short courses at Prace. The exact financial benefit and tuition reduction varies depending on eligibility and individual circumstances. The subsidy covers tuition fees for pre-accredited courses with no limit on the number of subsidised pre-accredited courses students can complete each year. Selection is based on assessment of eligibility criteria. Students should contact Prace for specific information about eligible courses and their eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible for Learn Local pre-accredited courses; Contact Prace for specific eligibility criteria; Services and Amenities fee still applies
special_conditions: No limit on number of subsidised pre-accredited courses in any year. Services and Amenities fee of $10 per class still applies. Contact Prace for eligibility information.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
program_name: Adult Migrant English Program (AMEP) - Free Classes
overview: Prace offers free English as an Additional Language (EAL) classes through the Commonwealth Government's Adult Migrant English Program (AMEP). This program provides English language instruction at no cost to eligible migrants and humanitarian entrants.
description: Eligible students can access free English as an Additional Language classes through the Commonwealth Government's Adult Migrant English Program (AMEP). The program provides full fee remission for tuition in accredited EAL courses. Classes are available for the duration needed to achieve English language learning goals. Selection is based on eligibility assessment for the AMEP program. AMEP participants who study for over 15 hours each week do not need to apply for a minimum number of jobs while engaged in this activity.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible migrants and humanitarian entrants; Must meet Commonwealth AMEP eligibility criteria; Cannot be currently enrolled at secondary school
special_conditions: Free English classes delivered through Commonwealth Government AMEP funding. All English as an Additional Language classes are delivered at Merrilands Community Centre in Reservoir. Must not be enrolled at secondary school. AMEP class hours can contribute to Workforce Australia mutual obligations.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
program_name: Skills for Education and Employment (SEE) Program - Free Classes
overview: Prace offers free English language and foundational skills classes through the Commonwealth Government's Skills for Education and Employment (SEE) program. This program provides essential language and numeracy support to eligible adult learners.
description: Eligible students can access free classes through the Commonwealth Government's Skills for Education and Employment (SEE) program. The program provides full fee remission for tuition in accredited foundation skills courses including English as an Additional Language. Classes are available for the duration of eligible participation in the program. Selection is based on eligibility assessment for the SEE program. Participants benefit from free instruction in essential literacy and numeracy skills.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible adult learners meeting SEE program criteria; Cannot be currently enrolled at secondary school; Must be over 17 or have formal exemption from school
special_conditions: Free English and foundational skills classes delivered through Commonwealth Government SEE funding. Must not be enrolled at secondary school. Students must be over 17 or have formal exemption from school.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace provides subsidised vocational training through the Asylum Seeker VET program for eligible asylum seekers. This program ensures access to accredited and pre-accredited training at no or reduced cost.
description: The Asylum Seeker VET program gives eligible asylum seekers access to accredited and pre-accredited training subsidised by the Victorian Government at Prace. The program provides reduced or full fee remission depending on the specific course and individual eligibility. Training covers various vocational areas for the duration of enrolment. Selection is based on confirmation of asylum seeker status and eligibility for the program. Students should contact Prace for more information about eligible courses and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Must be an eligible asylum seeker; Meet Victorian Government Asylum Seeker VET program eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Subsidised by Victorian Government for eligible asylum seekers. Access to both accredited and pre-accredited training. Contact Prace for eligibility and course information.

school_name: Preston Reservoir Adult Community Education - Mernda Campus
overview: Prace offers reduced tuition fees for students holding valid concession cards. This program ensures affordability for disadvantaged students across multiple course enrolments.
description: Students holding a valid concession card receive a maximum fee cap of $50 in tuition fees per calendar year for ACFE courses at Prace. If enroling in multiple ACFE courses, the student pays the $50 cap once, then only the Services and Amenities fee of $10 per additional course. For example, if a student enrols in one course they pay $50 tuition plus $10 amenities fee ($60 total). Any subsequent courses in the same year cost only $10 each. Selection is based on presentation of valid concession card documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid concession card; Applicable to ACFE courses; Services and Amenities fee of $10 per class still applies
special_conditions: Valid concession card required. $50 maximum tuition fee cap per calendar year for ACFE courses. Services and Amenities fee of $10 still applies to each course. If multiple courses, student pays $50 once then $10 per additional course.

overview: Quarry Hill Primary School awards the Florence Campbell Memorial Scholarship to recognise outstanding achievement in literary subjects with emphasis on written expression. The scholarship honours the memory of Florence Campbell, wife of former head teacher William J Campbell.
description: The Florence Campbell Memorial Scholarship was established by William J Campbell in memory of his wife Florence, who passed away on September 16, 1957. William Campbell served as head teacher at Quarry Hill State School from February 1950 until his retirement in June 1957. The award is presented annually to one boy and one girl for proficiency in literary subjects with an emphasis on written expression. This prestigious award recognises students who demonstrate exceptional skills and passion for English and literacy studies. The scholarship provides recognition and honour for academic excellence in the humanities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at Quarry Hill Primary School; Demonstrated proficiency in literary subjects; Emphasis on excellence in written expression
special_conditions: One award presented to a boy and one to a girl. Selection based on proficiency in literary subjects with emphasis on written expression.

overview: The Citizenship Award is presented by Quarry Hill Primary School to recognise outstanding citizenship and service to the school community. This award honours students who demonstrate exceptional dedication to their peers and the broader school community.
description: The Citizenship Award is presented annually to one male and one female student who have demonstrated outstanding citizenship and service to the school community. This award recognises students who go above and beyond in supporting their peers, contributing to a positive school environment, and actively participating in community service initiatives. Students selected for this award exemplify the school values of responsibility, respect, and community engagement. The award provides recognition and celebration of students who make meaningful contributions to the life of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at Quarry Hill Primary School; Demonstrated outstanding citizenship; Active service to school community; Positive contribution to school environment
special_conditions: One award presented to a male student and one to a female student. Selection based on demonstrated citizenship and service to school community.

overview: Quarry Hill Primary School presents the Music Award to recognise students who have demonstrated outstanding musical ability and genuine passion for studying music. This award celebrates students who excel in music and show commitment to their musical development.
description: The Music Award is presented annually to a student who has shown outstanding musical ability and demonstrated a genuine passion for studying music. This award recognises students who display exceptional talent, dedication, and enthusiasm in music both in class and through co-curricular musical activities. The award celebrates students who contribute to the school's musical culture through performances, participation in music ensembles, and commitment to developing their musical skills. This honour provides recognition for excellence in music and encourages continued artistic development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at Quarry Hill Primary School; Demonstrated outstanding musical ability; Genuine passion for studying music; Active participation in school music programs
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated outstanding musical ability and genuine passion for studying music.

overview: The Best All-Rounder Award is presented by Quarry Hill Primary School to recognise students who have demonstrated outstanding dedication, persistence, and resilience across the whole school curriculum. This award honours balanced excellence across all areas of learning.
description: The Best All-Rounder Award is presented annually to a student who has demonstrated outstanding dedication, persistence, and resilience across the whole school curriculum. This award recognises students who excel not only academically but also in co-curricular activities, demonstrating well-rounded development and commitment to all aspects of school life. Winners of this award display exceptional qualities including reliability, positive attitude, and continuous improvement across all learning areas. The award celebrates students who embody the school values of being Kind, Curious, and Brave in all their endeavours.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at Quarry Hill Primary School; Outstanding dedication across curriculum; Demonstrated persistence and resilience; Excellence in multiple areas of school life
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated outstanding dedication, persistence, and resilience across the whole school curriculum.

school_name: Red Cliffs Secondary College-FLO Connect Campus

program_name: Red Cliffs Secondary College Sports Academy
overview: Red Cliffs Secondary College Sports Academy is a full time integrated academic and sporting program for passionate sporting enthusiasts in Years 7-10. The program allows young male and female students to develop their sporting talent and aspirations concurrently to their secondary education.
description: The Red Cliffs Secondary College Sports Academy provides a selective, integrated academic and sporting program designed for students in Years 7-10 who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment. Students in the academy participate in a comprehensive academic curriculum while receiving specialized coaching and training to develop elite sporting performance. The program operates as part of the school's commitment to nurturing student talent across multiple dimensions. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting ability through trials and ongoing training. Students must maintain academic standards and demonstrate appropriate behaviour and sportsmanship reflecting college values. The program continues throughout Years 7-10 as part of regular enrolment at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and passion for sport; Selection based on trials and/or training sessions; Must maintain academic standards; Must demonstrate appropriate behaviour and sportsmanship; Subject to ongoing participation requirements
special_conditions: Integrated within the regular school program at no additional cost. Students continue with standard academic curriculum while receiving specialized sports training. Selection involves trials and training attendance. Students must maintain attendance and academic commitment to remain in the program.

overview: Red Cliffs Secondary College's Instrumental Music Program is an extracurricular program where students can learn an instrument or undertake singing lessons. The program provides individual attention from highly experienced instrumental music and voice teachers in the Sunraysia district.
description: The Instrumental Music Program at Red Cliffs Secondary College is an extracurricular program separate from general classroom music education. Students have the opportunity to learn an instrument of their choice or undertake singing lessons from specialized instructors. The program offers individual one-hour lessons scheduled on a rotation basis during the school day, ensuring students are not absent from the same class each week. A wide variety of instruments is available including flute, trombone, saxophone, guitar, bass guitar, clarinet, voice, trumpet, and percussion. The program is open to students from Year 7 to 12 and welcomes beginners through to advanced musicians. Students must pay for instrument hire and lesson fees as arranged with individual instructors. Opportunities to perform include college assemblies, lunchtime concerts, the college production, the Mildura Eisteddfod, and various community events.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in Years 7-12; No prior musical knowledge required; Open to beginners through to advanced musicians; Students can enrol at any time throughout the school year; Must complete booking form with parents; Subject to lesson and instrument hire fees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students are required to pay for instrument hire (if applicable) and instrumental music lessons as arranged with the specific instructor. Lessons occur during school time on a rotating schedule. Parents must complete a booking form and will be contacted by the Instrumental Music Coordinator (Mr Donald Mayne) for further information including timetables and required materials. Students can enrol at any time throughout the school year.

overview: Reservoir High School offers the Year 7 Scholarship Program to reward talented Grade 6 students who demonstrate leadership, respect, and excellent academic achievement throughout their primary school studies. This scholarship recognises students with exceptional academic records and potential for success at secondary level.
description: The Year 7 Scholarship Program is designed to support outstanding incoming Year 7 students entering from Grade 6. Candidates must have demonstrated excellent academic performance throughout their primary school studies and must display leadership qualities and respect for others. The school selects scholarship recipients based on academic achievement and character traits. Expression of Interest forms are required from Grade 6 students interested in applying. As a government school, specific financial benefits (such as fee remissions) are not applicable, as government schools do not charge tuition fees. The selection process prioritises students who have achieved at the highest levels academically and who show evidence of leadership and responsibility.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students entering Year 7; Demonstrated excellent academic record throughout primary school studies; Must display leadership qualities; Must demonstrate respect for others; Expression of Interest form must be submitted
special_conditions: Expression of Interest form to be returned to reservoir.hs@education.vic.gov.au by 13th July 2025. As this is a government school, scholarships do not provide fee remissions but rather recognition and potential priority placement or special support.

overview: Reservoir High School is one of a select group of secondary schools in Victoria accredited to offer the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program. This program provides engaging accelerated learning opportunities for committed and academically capable Year 7 students who can work at a significantly faster pace and in greater depth than their peers.
description: The SEAL program is designed for gifted and high potential students capable of working at a significantly faster pace and in greater depth than age peers. Each year, a limited number of places (25 co-educational places) are offered to Year 7 entrants. Selection involves three stages: online registration for the ACER test, undertaking the ACER assessment testing achievement in verbal and numerical reasoning, reading comprehension, mathematics, and written expression, and interviews conducted in June for suitable candidates. Information from the student's primary school is also sought. Parents and guardians are advised of outcomes by the end of July. As this is a selective entry program within a government school, there are no direct scholarship fee benefits, but students gain access to advanced accelerated curriculum and enriched learning experiences.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Grade 6 students entering Year 7; Must be capable of advanced academic work at a fast pace; Must achieve suitable test scores on the ACER test; Must satisfy interview assessment; Primary teacher evaluation forms required
special_conditions: Registrations for Round 1 SEAL Testing open Monday 27th April 2026 and close Wednesday 10th June 2026. There are no late registrations. SEAL testing occurs on a Saturday (June 2026). Payment by credit card is required at the time of registration. Students undertaking SEAL will be accelerated in core subjects (English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities) in Years 7 and 8 as a cohort, working at a fast pace with minimal repetition of course material. Unsuccessful applicants will be shortlisted on a reserve list.

overview: The Bruce Kingsbury Scholarship is offered to Year 10 students at Reservoir High School to recognise exceptional achievement and understanding of historical significance and human values. The scholarship, provided by the 2/14th Battalion Association since 2002, assists students in their pursuit of academic studies in the final years of secondary schooling.
description: The Bruce Kingsbury Scholarship is awarded by the 2/14th Battalion Association to a Year 10 student at Reservoir High School. The scholarship has a value of $1,000 AUD and is designed to assist students in the pursuit of their academic studies during the final two years of secondary schooling (Years 11 and 12). The application process requires students to research the deeds of Bruce Kingsbury (a Victoria Cross recipient) and the 2/14th Battalion soldiers, focusing on the human values displayed by these soldiers during the Battle of Isurava in 1942, and to develop a presentation reflecting their research. The scholarship has been awarded annually since 2002 and is managed in partnership with the school through a formal ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Must undertake research on Bruce Kingsbury and the 2/14th Battalion; Must develop and present a research-based presentation on human values displayed by these soldiers; Selection based on quality of research and presentation
special_conditions: Awarded by the 2/14th Battalion Association in partnership with Reservoir High School. The award ceremony is held annually in Term 3 and includes presentations by scholarship recipients. Recipients are recognised publicly at a formal school ceremony.

overview: The Dr Livesey Scholarship is offered to Year 12 students to support their tertiary education aspirations. The scholarship recognises academic achievement and is provided through the generous ongoing support of Doctor Livesey and his wife Kathy.
description: The Dr Livesey Scholarship is awarded to Year 12 students to assist them with their tertiary education pursuits. The scholarship recognises students who have demonstrated outstanding academic achievement during their secondary schooling. Multiple awards are presented annually to eligible Year 12 students. Recipients are formally recognised at school ceremonies and receive support for their further studies. The scholarship is provided through the continued generosity of Doctor Livesey and his wife Kathy, demonstrating ongoing community commitment to supporting student success beyond secondary school. While specific monetary values are not detailed in public documentation, the award assists recipients with their tertiary education costs and aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Outstanding academic achievement; Demonstrated commitment to learning; Selected by school leadership
special_conditions: Awarded to Year 12 students for tertiary education support. Recipients are selected and formally recognised by the school. Ongoing support from donors Doctor Livesey and his wife Kathy.

overview: Ringwood Secondary College offers the Heritage Scholarship to support deserving students who are entering VCE and need assistance to complete their secondary education. The scholarship was introduced by the College Heritage and Alumni Group in 2016 and recognises students who have surmounted considerable challenges in pursuing their education.
description: The Heritage Scholarship assists students entering VCE (Years 11 and 12) who are experiencing financial hardship or have overcome significant personal challenges in their educational journey. The scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance to help students complete their VCE studies through the critical final years of secondary school. Funding supports educational resources such as computers, books, musical instruments, and sporting equipment. The scholarship is managed by the Heritage and Alumni Group in partnership with the College. Selection involves submitting an application form and assessment by the Heritage Committee. The value and duration of the scholarship varies based on individual need and available funds. Recipients are selected annually, with applications typically closing in Term 4 (approximately October).
eligibility_criteria: Entering VCE (Year 11); Students experiencing financial hardship or who have overcome significant personal challenges; Demonstrable need for financial assistance; Application submission required
special_conditions: Applications are submitted in writing. The Heritage Committee of the College reviews applications. Contact the Heritage Committee Assistant Principal on 9870 2002 or [email protected] for application form and further details. Scholarship is offered on annual basis subject to available funds.

overview: Ringwood Secondary College maintains a Scholarship Fund established to provide financial assistance to students during their VCE years. The Fund supports students through the critical period of their senior secondary studies and is built through community donations and contributions from the Alumni network.
description: The Ringwood Secondary College Scholarship Fund is a donor-supported initiative that provides financial assistance to VCE students (Years 11 and 12) who require support. The Fund helps students cover costs of educational resources including computers, books, musical instruments, and sporting equipment. The scholarship supports students to achieve their potential during their senior years of secondary school. Funding is sourced through philanthropic contributions from alumni, parents, and community members. Students can apply or be nominated for assistance through the Fund, which is administered by the College. The value of assistance varies based on available funds and individual circumstances. Selection is based on demonstrated need and commitment to completing VCE studies.
eligibility_criteria: Studying VCE (Years 11 or 12); Demonstrated financial need; Commitment to completing secondary education
special_conditions: Funds are distributed based on available donations. Scholarships are intended to support educational costs during VCE. Contributions to the Scholarship Fund can be made through GiveNow platform or direct contact with the College.

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: Reservoir High School rewards talented Grade 6 students who display leadership and respect. Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who have demonstrated an excellent academic record throughout their primary school studies.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship recognises exceptional primary school achievement. Students eligible for this scholarship must have demonstrated an excellent academic record throughout their primary school studies and must also display leadership qualities and respect for others. The scholarship aims to reward high-achieving students transitioning to secondary school. Specific financial value, application deadline, and selection process details are not publicly detailed on the school website. Students should contact the school directly for complete information on application procedures and scholarship value.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6; Demonstrated excellent academic record throughout primary school studies; Display leadership qualities; Demonstrate respect
special_conditions: Expression of Interest Form required to be returned to reservoir.hs@education.vic.gov.au by 13th July (based on 2025 information).

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: Reservoir High School awards scholarships to Grade 6 students who display leadership qualities and serve as positive role models. Leadership Scholarships recognise students who have demonstrated strong leadership skills and been positive role models for their fellow students.
description: The Year 7 Leadership Scholarship is awarded to students who have demonstrated leadership skills and been positive role models for their fellow students throughout primary school. This scholarship recognises the importance of developing student leaders who contribute to a positive school culture. The scholarship supports the transition of leadership-focused students into secondary school. Specific financial value and application procedures are not fully detailed on the school website and families should contact Reservoir High School for complete application information.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6; Demonstrated leadership skills; Been a positive role model for fellow students; Display respect
special_conditions: Expression of Interest Form required to be returned to reservoir.hs@education.vic.gov.au by 13th July (based on 2025 information).

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: Reservoir High School awards $300 scholarships to Grade 6 students who demonstrate excellence in sports. Sport Excellence Scholarships are given to students who can demonstrate excellence across at least two sports (one in exceptional circumstances) and have evidence of potential leadership in sport.
description: The Year 7 Sport Excellence Scholarship provides $300 in financial support to students who demonstrate high levels of sporting achievement and potential leadership capabilities in sport. Students eligible must show excellence across at least two sports, or in exceptional circumstances, excellence in a single sport, combined with evidence of potential leadership in sport. Recipients are recognised for their athletic achievements and commitment to sporting excellence. This scholarship supports talented athletes as they transition to secondary school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6; Excellence demonstrated across at least two sports (or one in exceptional circumstances); Evidence of potential leadership in sport
special_conditions: Expression of Interest Form required to be returned to reservoir.hs@education.vic.gov.au by 13th July (based on 2025 information).

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: Reservoir High School awards $300 scholarships to Grade 6 students who demonstrate excellence in performing arts. Performing Arts Scholarships are given to students who can demonstrate excellence in performing arts such as dance, vocals, or instruments.
description: The Year 7 Performing Arts Scholarship provides $300 in financial recognition to students who demonstrate excellence in performing arts disciplines. Eligible students must show achievement and talent in areas such as dance, vocal performance, instrumental music or other performing arts activities. The scholarship recognises creative achievement and supports emerging performing artists as they continue their secondary education. Recipients demonstrate commitment and skill in their chosen performing arts discipline.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6; Demonstrated excellence in performing arts (dance, vocals, instruments or similar); Evidence of achievement and commitment
special_conditions: Expression of Interest Form required to be returned to reservoir.hs@education.vic.gov.au by 13th July (based on 2025 information).

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: The Bruce Kingsbury Scholarship is awarded by the 2/14th Battalion Association to Year 10 students at Reservoir High School. The scholarship, valued at $1,000, honours the legacy of Private Bruce Steel Kingsbury VC and supports students in their academic pursuits during their final two years of secondary school.
description: The Bruce Kingsbury Scholarship is a prestigious award valued at $1,000 per year, offered to Year 10 students to support their academic studies during Years 10, 11 and 12. The scholarship is provided by the 2/14th Battalion Association to recognise and honour the legacy of Private Bruce Steel Kingsbury VC, who was awarded the Victoria Cross for his heroism during World War II at the Battle of Isurava in 1942. Selection is based on a competitive research project and presentation about Bruce Kingsbury and the human values displayed by soldiers of the 2/14th Battalion. The award recognises both academic potential and understanding of historical significance and military service. Recipients are announced at an annual ceremony held in Term 3.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 10; Competitive research and presentation required; Understanding of Bruce Kingsbury's legacy and 2/14th Battalion history
special_conditions: Applicants must complete research on the deeds of Bruce Kingsbury and his comrades. Applicants must develop a presentation reflecting their research findings. Annual ceremony held in Term 3. Presented by the 2/14th Battalion Association to support Year 10 students through to Year 12.

school_name: Reservoir High School-Heidelberg Secondary Teaching Unit
overview: Reservoir High School offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program to academically capable prospective Year 7 students. The SEAL program provides accelerated and enriched learning experiences for gifted and talented students with rigorous academic content and high-order thinking opportunities.
description: The SEAL program is a selective academic program for gifted and talented students entering Year 7. A limited number of places (25 co-educational places annually) are offered to committed and academically capable students. The program focuses on providing skills and content that encourage students to develop their potential to achieve academic, personal and social success. Students in SEAL work at a fast pace with minimal repetition, engaging in exciting and challenging learning experiences with open-ended tasks involving multidisciplinary learning and high-order thinking skills. Selection involves application, standardised testing via ACER/EduTest (HAST), performance evaluation, and interview. Registrations for testing occur in April-June each year, with testing conducted in July.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7; Academically capable; Committed to accelerated learning program; Prospective student from Government, Catholic or Independent school sectors
special_conditions: Online application required. No late registrations accepted. Unsuccessful applicants placed on reserve list. Shortlisted candidates interviewed in June/July. Primary school information sought at interview stage. Parents/guardians notified of outcome by end of August. Registration link available from Monday 27th April 2026. Contact: Jodie Purches, Assistant Principal, 9466 0900.

overview: Rollins Primary School provides information about the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a Victorian Government program that assists eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. The fund ensures that no student will miss out on important educational and fun activities due to financial constraints.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. The allowance will be paid to the school to use towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the student. Eligible families are those who hold a valid means-tested concession card or are temporary foster parents. The financial assistance is non-monetary in the sense that funds are provided directly to the school for specific activities rather than as direct cash payments to families. Students do not need to apply separately; eligibility is determined based on family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card; OR Be a temporary foster parent; Must be enrolled at Rollins Primary School
special_conditions: Funds are paid directly to the school rather than to families. The allowance is used by the school for camps, excursions, or sporting activities.

overview: Rollins Primary School offers access to the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a Victorian Government financial assistance program. CSEF is designed to ensure all eligible students can participate in school trips, camps and sporting activities without missing out on important educational experiences.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. This financial support ensures no student will miss out on important, educational and fun activities beyond the classroom. The allowance is paid directly to Rollins Primary School and used towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the eligible student. Eligibility is based on holding a valid means-tested concession card or being a temporary foster parent. The program provides non-monetary support to help students access enrichment activities that support their educational development.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card; OR be a temporary foster parent
special_conditions: The Victorian Government does not accept late applications. The CSEF system only allows one application per student. If you applied for CSEF at a previous school, you do not need to reapply at Rollins Primary unless there has been a change in family circumstances (such as new student enrolments, change of custody, change of name, concession card number, or new siblings commencing). The allowance is paid to the school to use towards expenses relating to camps, excursions, or sporting activities for the benefit of the eligible student's participation.

school_name: Rosanna Golf Links Primary School-Rosanna Golf Links Deaf Facility

overview: STEP is Rosehill Secondary College's selective enrichment program for gifted and talented students in Years 7-9. The program provides advancement in the core subjects of English, Mathematics and Science for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and selective entry criteria.
description: STEP (Selective Talent Enrichment Program) runs from Years 7-9 and focuses on providing opportunities for enhancement across the core subjects of English, Mathematics and Science. STEP students at Years 7-9 study together as a group, with each subject's curriculum enriched, accelerated and compacted. Students push into further extension at Year 9 to prepare for advanced subjects and the opportunity for completion of a VCE subject at Year 10. STEP students undertake the balance of their program from a range of units offered by other Key Learning Areas including Humanities, The Arts, Languages, Technology and Physical Education and Health. The program seeks to develop well-rounded individuals with effective communication skills, commitment to learning, and the aspiration to live harmoniously and empathetically. Students whose families wish them to enter STEP will be required to participate in a selection process. The program is non-monetary and provides no financial benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Must participate in selection process; Open to students entering Year 7; Families must express desire to enter the program; Selection based on demonstrated academic ability and performance criteria
special_conditions: Students are provided with workshops throughout the year which aim to develop skills in resilience, collaboration and leadership. STEP students from Years 7-9 work in mixed year level groups for these initiatives. Non-monetary enrichment program with no financial support.

overview: RAMP is Rosehill Secondary College's accelerated mathematics program designed for high-achieving students across Years 7-9. The program is offered to students with an exceptional affinity for and ability in Mathematics, with teacher recommendations determining participation.
description: RAMP (Rosehill Accelerated Mathematics Program) is an alternative program offered by the College that is designed for high-achieving students who wish to specialise and enhance their mathematics skills. In RAMP, a small group of 10-15 students are taught advanced mathematics to prepare them for the more advanced VCE mathematics courses. Students are recommended for the program by their mathematics teacher based on demonstrated exceptional ability and performance in mathematics. The program provides acceleration and extension in mathematics content to ensure students are challenged appropriately. This is a non-monetary enrichment program that provides no financial benefit to families.
eligibility_criteria: Students with exceptional ability in Mathematics; Recommendation by mathematics teacher required; Open to Years 7-9; Must demonstrate advanced mathematical understanding and potential
special_conditions: Small group instruction with 10-15 students per cohort. Selection based on teacher recommendation and demonstrated mathematical ability. Non-monetary enrichment program.

overview: REAP is Rosehill Secondary College's accelerated English program for high-performing students in Years 7-9. The program is designed to enhance the literacy skills of students who demonstrate exceptional ability and high aptitude in English.
description: REAP (Rosehill English Accelerated Program) is an accelerated Literature program offered by the College that is designed to enhance the Literacy skills of high-performing students. The program targets students demonstrating high aptitude and exceptional ability in English across Years 7-9. REAP complements the long-standing STEP program and provides enrichment and acceleration in English content to extend high-achieving students. The program develops critical thinking, comprehension, and advanced literacy skills through engagement with sophisticated texts and literary analysis. Students are selected for the program based on their demonstrated English ability and performance. This is a non-monetary enrichment program that provides no financial benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Students with exceptional ability in English; High aptitude in literacy required; Open to Years 7-9; Demonstrated excellence in English performance and comprehension
special_conditions: Program targets students demonstrating high aptitude in Literacy. Students are individually selected for the program. Contact Ms Wasif or Ms Barbaro for further information or to express interest.

overview: VHAP is a state-wide program for high-ability Years 7 and 8 students across Victorian government schools. Rosehill Secondary College participates in this program, providing selected students with a 10-week virtual enrichment program focusing on critical thinking, problem solving and creativity in English and Mathematics.
description: The Victorian High-Ability Program (VHAP) is a virtual enrichment program that runs for 10 weeks during Term 1 and Term 3. The program focuses on tasks that stimulate students' critical thinking, problem-solving and creativity, enriching their understanding of English and Mathematics. VHAP does not focus on accelerating students through core Victorian Curriculum content, but rather pushes high-ability students out of their learning comfort zone. Research shows that highly able students thrive when surrounded by like-minded peers. In the virtual classroom, students connect with like-minded peers from across Victoria, enabling them to extend each other academically and personally. Students participate in masterclasses held on a face-to-face basis (may be delivered virtually if necessary) providing opportunities for students to connect with like-minded peers across the state. Only students selected by the Department of Education and Training can participate. This is a non-monetary enrichment program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be selected by the Department of Education; High-ability in English or Mathematics; Open to Years 7-8; Students selected by Department of Education criteria
special_conditions: Students must be selected by the Department of Education and Training to participate. Program runs for 10 weeks across Term 1 and Term 3. Virtual delivery with optional face-to-face masterclasses. Students from Rosehill are often selected by the Department of Education to participate every term.

overview: CSEF is a Victorian government financial assistance program available to eligible families at Rosehill Secondary College. The program provides funding that can be used towards excursions, camps and sporting activities that run throughout the year.
description: The Camps/Sport/Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian government financial assistance program available to families who hold a current Health Care Card or Pension Card. This funding can be used towards excursions, camps and sporting activities that run throughout the year at Rosehill Secondary College. CSEF payments are made to schools from March onwards. Application forms are available via the Department of Education website, at the College general office or online. Families must complete and return the application form along with a copy of their valid concession card. If families applied for CSEF in the previous year and are current students, they do not need to complete a new application form unless there has been a change in family circumstances. The program provides means-tested financial assistance for eligible families to support student participation in excursions, camps and sporting programs. Applications are accepted until the end of Term 2 each year.
eligibility_criteria: Must have a current Health Care Card or Pension Card; Australian resident; Eligible concession card holder; Change in family circumstances requires new application
special_conditions: Applicants must provide a copy of their valid Health Care Card or Pension Card with the application. CSEF payments are made to schools from March onwards. Applications can be submitted at any time but must be processed by the end of Term 2. The program is managed by the Victorian Department of Education. Families can use CSEF for consent/payment by calling the Administration Office.

overview: Riverwalk Primary School administers the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports & Excursion Fund (CSEF) for eligible students. This program provides financial assistance to support student participation in school excursions, incursions, and camps.
description: The Victorian Government provides Camps, Sports & Excursion funds (CSEF) to eligible students. These funds are paid to the school to use towards excursions, incursions and camps for eligible students. To be eligible for the funding, a parent or legal guardian must be the holder of a current Healthcare card or Pension card or be a temporary foster parent on the first day of Term 1 or Term 2. Parents who are eligible for CSEF will be required to complete and submit a CSEF application form before the end of Term 2. The financial value of the assistance varies depending on individual circumstances and school programs, but is specifically allocated for camps, sports, and excursion participation.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or legal guardian must hold a current Healthcare card, Pension card, or be a temporary foster parent; Eligibility determined on the first day of Term 1 or Term 2; Application must be submitted before the end of Term 2
special_conditions: Funds are used by the school specifically for excursions, incursions and camps. Eligible families must complete and submit the CSEF application form by the end of Term 2 each year.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers General Excellence Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence in their learning and are actively involved within their current school and out-of-school activities.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are offered to new students entering Years 5, 6, 7, 9, 10 and 11, recognising students who show excellence in their learning and active involvement in school and out-of-school activities. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student's continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. Selection involves sitting the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test and an interview with the Principal. Successful applicants must maintain satisfactory academic performance and involvement in school activities, with annual review of scholarship entitlements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students; Currently in Years 4 to 10 in 2026 to apply for entry in 2027; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Must demonstrate excellence in learning; Must be actively involved in school and out-of-school activities
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship testing will take place at Sacré Cœur on Saturday 21 February 2026 from 8:45am–12:45pm. Shortlisted applicants may be invited to an interview with the Principal. Scholarship recipients will be expected to support and be involved in school activities and events, wearing the Sacré Cœur uniform with pride. Performance and ongoing contribution to the life of Sacré Cœur will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is designed for students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 to 11 and are offered to those currently in Years 6 to 10 in 2026. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student's continuing academic performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. Applicants are required to sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test as part of the selection process. Students in Years 5-6 may be considered for Academic Scholarships at the discretion of the Principal. Annual review of scholarship performance and renewal conditions apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11; Currently in Years 6 to 10 in 2026; Students in Years 5-6 may be considered at Principal's discretion; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Strong academic performance required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship testing will take place at Sacré Cœur on Saturday 21 February 2026 from 8:45am–12:45pm. Students must first apply for and sit the Academic Scholarship test. During the online registration process, documentation will need to be uploaded including most recent NAPLAN results and last two school reports. Performance will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers Music Scholarships to recognise outstanding musical achievement and talent. The scholarship is designed for students demonstrating exceptional ability in music performance.
description: Music Scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 to 10 who are currently in Years 6 to 9 in 2026. Applicants must first apply for and sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student's continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. Short-listed candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions. Selection involves both the Academic Scholarship Test and a music audition. Annual review of performance and renewal conditions apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Currently in Years 6 to 9 in 2026; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Must demonstrate exceptional musical ability; Must audition with the Head of Music
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship testing will take place at Sacré Cœur on Saturday 21 February 2026 from 8:45am–12:45pm. All applicants must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test. Short-listed candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions. During the online registration process, documentation will need to be uploaded. Performance and ongoing contribution will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers bursaries to support students whose families may otherwise be unable to access a Sacré Cœur education. Bursaries are means-tested financial assistance available to both current and new families.
description: Each year, Sacré Cœur proudly offers a limited number of bursaries funded in part by the School, in conjunction with the Sacré Cœur Foundation, and supported by the generous contributions of the community. Bursaries are means-tested and may be offered to both current and new families entering Years 5 to 11. The bursary program is designed to provide up to 75% tuition fee remission to families who may not otherwise be able to afford a Sacré Cœur education. Bursary applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial need. Selection involves a financial assessment process. All bursaries and scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both current and new families; Students entering Years 5 to 11; Families must demonstrate financial need; Means-tested assessment required; Australian citizens and permanent residents only
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursaries are means-tested. A limited number of bursaries are offered each year. All applications require financial documentation and assessment. Bursaries may be available for new families entering in any year level from Years 5-11. The bursary program is supported by the Sacré Cœur Foundation. All bursaries and scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacred Heart College offers scholarships to one student from each of the six designated feeder Catholic Primary Schools. These scholarships recognize students demonstrating strong character and academic potential entering Year 7, selected by their primary school principals.
description: The College continues the tradition of offering scholarships to one student from each of the following feeder Catholic Primary Schools annually. Each scholarship is awarded to a Grade 6 student at each of the six feeder schools. The scholarship provides a $500 annual deduction from tuition fees for six years (Year 7–12). Recipients are selected by the Principal of each primary school using agreed criteria that evaluate academic achievement, character, leadership potential, and contribution to school community. The total value of each scholarship is $3,000 over the six-year period of enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 student at designated feeder Catholic Primary School; Recipient selected by primary school principal using defined selection criteria; Enrolled at one of the six designated feeder primary schools
special_conditions: Scholarship provides $500 annual deduction from tuition fees each year for six years (Year 7–12). Recipients are selected by the Principal of each designated feeder primary school rather than through a competitive application process.

overview: Sacred Heart College offers Music Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional musical talent and commitment. These scholarships recognize outstanding musicians across all instrumental and vocal specialties and are available to both current and prospective students.
description: Sacred Heart College awards Music Scholarships annually to recognize and support students with outstanding musical talent. Over 50 students typically apply for these scholarships, with auditions held in October each year. The audition process evaluates musical performance ability, technical skill, and potential for continued musical development. Recipients are announced at the College's Awards Ceremony following the Year 7-9 End of Year Mass. While specific financial value is not detailed in available documentation, scholarships are offered to encourage excellence in music performance and participation in the College's music program. Eligible students include current Year 7-11 students and prospective Grade 6 students entering the College.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 7–11 students at Sacred Heart College; Prospective Grade 6 students joining the College; Open to all students studying music; Applicants must submit application online
special_conditions: Auditions are held annually in October. Approximately 50 students apply each year. Shortlisted candidates are contacted for auditions. Scholarship recipients are announced at the College's Awards Ceremony following the Year 7-9 End of Year Mass. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to the College's music program.

program_name: CSEF (Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund) Assistance
overview: Sacred Heart College facilitates government CSEF assistance for eligible families. The College matches the government CSEF subsidy to provide additional financial support for camps, sports, and excursion activities.
description: Sacred Heart College supports eligible families in accessing the government Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF). Families must hold a means-tested concession card or be temporary foster parents to be eligible. When an application is successful, the government provides a $250.00 subsidy, which the College matches with an additional $250.00, resulting in a total credit of $500.00 per child applied to the family account. Both the government and College subsidies are credited directly to the family account. This program provides financial assistance to help reduce barriers to participation in camps, sporting events, and excursion activities.
eligibility_criteria: Family holds a means-tested concession card; Family is providing foster care on a temporary basis; Application must be successful; Applicant must be a current student at Sacred Heart College
special_conditions: College matches government CSEF subsidy of $250.00 with an additional $250.00, totaling $500.00 per child credited to family account. Both subsidies are credited directly to the family account. Families must hold valid means-tested concession card or be temporary foster parents.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate excellence across their learning and are actively involved in school and co-curricular activities. These scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to continuing academic performance and ongoing contribution to school life.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students who show excellence in their learning and are actively involved within their current school and out-of-school activities. Successful applicants will be positive communicators and hold a passion for learning and new experiences. Scholarship recipients will be expected to support and be involved in school activities and events, wearing the Sacré Cœur uniform with pride. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees for the duration of enrolment until the end of Year 12. Selection involves the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test held on Saturday 21 February 2026 from 8:45am–12:45pm, followed by shortlisting and interview with the Principal. The scholarship is subject to annual review and unsatisfactory performance may result in withdrawal of scholarship entitlements.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Years 4 to 10 in 2026 to apply for entry in 2027; Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Current and new students eligible; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test
year_levels: Year 5, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11

special_conditions: Scholarships are offered subject to the student's continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. This will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur's Academic Scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 7 to 11. Scholars receive full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, provided they maintain strong academic performance and demonstrate commitment to the school community.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed to reward and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and achievement. Applicants must be in Years 6 to 10 in 2026 to apply for entry in Years 7 to 11 in 2027. Students in Years 5-6 may be considered at the discretion of the Principal. Selection involves sitting the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test on Saturday 21 February 2026, with shortlisted candidates invited for interview with the Principal. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12. The scholarship is subject to annual performance review and unsatisfactory academic progress may result in withdrawal of entitlements.

This will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers Music Scholarships to talented musicians who demonstrate exceptional ability in musical performance and commitment to music within the school. Scholars receive full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, contingent on continued musical excellence and school contribution.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises and supports students with exceptional talent and ability in music. Applicants must be in Years 6 to 9 in 2026 to apply for entry in 2027 and must first sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test on Saturday 21 February 2026. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions. Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12. Selection is based on the academic test, musical audition performance, and interview with the Principal. The scholarship is subject to annual review and continuing musical excellence is expected.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Years 6 to 9 in 2026 for 2027 entry; Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Must first sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Current and new students eligible; Musical audition required for shortlisted candidates
