special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions. Scholarships are offered subject to the student's continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. This will be assessed on an annual basis and unsatisfactory performance may result in the withdrawal of Scholarship entitlements. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: The Kathleen McCarthy Bursary honours the legacy of Kathleen McCarthy, a former Deputy Principal and School Board Director, by providing substantial financial assistance to students entering Year 7 whose families would otherwise be unable to afford a Sacré Cœur education. The bursary funds 75% of tuition fees.
description: Established in June 2019, the Kathleen McCarthy Bursary provides significant financial support to deserving students entering Year 7. The bursary funds 75% of tuition fees to help families who may not otherwise be able to afford a Sacré Cœur education. The bursary is available to both new and current students. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and family circumstances. The support continues for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school, subject to the family's ongoing financial eligibility. This bursary is part of the Sacré Cœur Foundation's commitment to ensuring that excellence in education is accessible to students from all socio-economic backgrounds.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be entering Year 7; Open to both new and current students; Means-tested on basis of family financial circumstances; Family must demonstrate inability to afford full tuition fees; Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents
special_conditions: Bursary is means tested based on family financial circumstances. Available for both new and current students. The bursary is awarded at the Principal's discretion. Bursaries are funded through the School, the Sacré Cœur Foundation, and community contributions.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers a limited number of general bursaries each year to support students whose families may otherwise be unable to access a Sacré Cœur education. Bursaries are means tested and available to both current and new families across multiple year levels.
description: Each year, Sacré Cœur offers a limited number of bursaries to support students whose families may otherwise be unable to access a Sacré Cœur education. These bursaries are made possible through funding from the School, the Sacré Cœur Foundation, and generous contributions from the school community. Bursaries are means tested and may be offered to both current and new families. The amount of financial assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and availability of funds. Bursaries are awarded at the Principal's discretion and all families must complete a financial assessment to determine eligibility. The value of each bursary is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Year 5 through to Year 11; Means-tested on basis of family financial circumstances; Open to both current and new families; Australian citizens and permanent residents; Financial hardship must be demonstrated
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursaries are means tested and based on family financial circumstances. Available to both current and new students. Number and value of bursaries awarded varies year to year based on availability of funds. All bursaries are awarded at the Principal's discretion. Families must complete financial assessment. School and bursary concessions do not apply to certain fees and levies.

overview: Sacred Heart Primary School awards the Barbara Quinn Education Bursary to recognise academic progress and achievements in primary school students. The bursary encourages further academic success, builds confidence, and supports student transition into secondary education.
description: The Barbara Quinn Education Bursary is presented to students at Sacred Heart Primary School who demonstrate academic progress and achievement. The bursary recognises students' commitment to their studies and is designed to encourage continued academic success while building their confidence in their educational journey. The award supports student transition and progression into secondary education, providing both recognition and financial support to eligible recipients. While specific financial value is not publicly detailed, the bursary is offered annually to deserving Year 6 students transitioning to secondary school.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated academic progress and achievements; Typically awarded to Year 6 students; Evidence of commitment to academic studies
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded based on demonstrated academic progress and achievements. Recipients are recognised at school award ceremonies and publicly acknowledged in school communications.

overview: Ruyton Girls' School offers Excellence Scholarships to recognise outstanding talent, leadership, and commitment across various disciplines. The scholarship is available to exceptional students entering designated year levels and supports those demonstrating excellence in academic, musical, performing arts, sporting, visual arts, STEM, and leadership pursuits.
description: Ruyton's Excellence Scholarships recognise outstanding talent, leadership, and commitment across various disciplines. Applicants are assessed through a comprehensive process including online application submission, academic examination administered by Academic Assessment Services (AAS), and interview. The exact financial value varies based on merit assessment. Recipients must demonstrate exceptional ability in their chosen focus area, which may include STEM, music, performing arts, sport, visual arts, or leadership. All students sit the academic examination regardless of their focus area, with candidates required to complete an online application including personal statement and supporting evidence of achievements in their chosen discipline. Shortlisted candidates proceed to interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering designated year levels (Year 5, Year 7, Year 9); Competitive selection based on academic examination and submission of evidence of excellence; Focus area may include STEM, Music, Performing Arts, Sports, Visual Arts, or Leadership; All incomplete applications are ineligible; Must demonstrate exceptional ability in chosen discipline
special_conditions: The 2027 scholarship program is now closed. Applications for 2026 scholarships opened in Spring 2024. All students are required to sit the academic examination; registration for examination is required to ensure test paper is prepared. Incomplete applications are ineligible for consideration.

overview: Ruyton Girls' School offers Foundation Scholarships to support families requiring financial assistance. The means-tested scholarship recognises outstanding talent and commitment across various disciplines while providing financial support to eligible families, enabling girls to access a Ruyton education who may otherwise not be able to afford attendance.
description: The Foundation Scholarship is a means-tested scholarship that supports families requiring financial assistance. Similar to Excellence Scholarships, applicants complete an online application, sit an academic assessment examination conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS), and attend an interview. The financial value is determined by the school based on assessed family financial circumstances. This scholarship recognises outstanding talent, leadership, and commitment across various disciplines including STEM, music, performing arts, sports, visual arts, and leadership. Applicants may be considered under one or more focus categories and must provide supporting evidence of achievements and accomplishments in their chosen area.
eligibility_criteria: Families requiring financial assistance; Open to students entering designated year levels (Year 5, Year 7, Year 9); Must demonstrate outstanding talent and commitment in chosen discipline; Competitive selection based on academic examination, application submission, and financial means test; Demonstrated achievement in focus area (STEM, Music, Performing Arts, Sports, Visual Arts, or Leadership)
special_conditions: The 2027 scholarship program is now closed. Applications for 2026 scholarships opened in Spring 2024. Scholarship value is determined by the school's assessment of family financial circumstances. All students required to sit academic examination. Short-listed candidates undergo financial means test assessment and interview with Principal. Incomplete applications are ineligible.

overview: The Founder's Scholarship is a financially means-tested scholarship providing full financial support to eligible girls who could otherwise not afford to attend Ruyton. This transformational scholarship supports one student annually and aims to expand to provide six scholarships yearly.
description: The Founder's Scholarship is a financially means-tested scholarship that provides the gift of a Ruyton education to a young girl who could otherwise not afford to attend the school. The scholarship can provide full fee remission based on financial assessment. Currently offered to one girl per year, with plans through community fundraising to expand to six scholarships annually. The application process involves writing a letter to the Principal outlining passions and aspirations, completing a financial means test application, and attending an interview if shortlisted. Established through the generosity of the Ruyton community and supported by philanthropic donations, this scholarship is designed to open opportunities and potentially change lives for deserving students across Years 10, 11, and 12.
eligibility_criteria: Families in financial hardship; Girls who could otherwise not afford to attend Ruyton; Open to students in Years 10, 11, and 12; Must demonstrate financial need; Shortlisted candidates required to complete financial means test and attend interview
special_conditions: Applicants must write a letter to Principal (maximum 1000 words) outlining passions, aspirations, and what joining Ruyton would mean. Shortlisted candidates required to complete financial means test application. Interviews conducted with eligible candidates. Currently one scholarship awarded annually; community working to expand to six scholarships per year in perpetuity. Donations are tax deductible.

overview: Sacré Cœur offers the General Excellence Scholarship to students who demonstrate excellence in their learning and are actively involved in school and community activities. The scholarship recognises well-rounded students who show leadership potential and a passion for learning.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship provides full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student's continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. The scholarship is awarded to new students entering Years 5, 6 or 7 who show excellence in their learning and are actively involved within their current school and in out-of-school activities. Successful applicants will be positive communicators and hold a passion for learning and new experiences. Applicants must be in Years 4 to 10 in 2026 to apply for entry in 2027. Selection requires sitting the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test on Saturday 21 February 2026. Scholarship recipients are expected to support and be involved in school activities and events, wearing the Sacré Cœur uniform with pride. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 5, 6 or 7; Applicants must be in Years 4 to 10 in 2026; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Continuing performance and ongoing contribution to school life required for renewal
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on the basis of continuing performance and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. Scholarship recipients are expected to support and be involved in school activities and events, wearing the Sacré Cœur uniform with pride. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. Medical conditions and medication information must be disclosed to the School and Academic Assessment Services well before the examination. Electronic devices including watches, phones and fitbits are not permitted in the examination room. Unpaid applicants will not be allowed to sit the scholarship examination.

overview: Sacré Cœur's Academic Scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and potential. The scholarship provides financial support to talented students entering Years 7 to 11 who demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability.

The scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 11 in 2027. Applicants must be in Years 6 to 10 in 2026 and must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test on Saturday 21 February 2026. Students in Years 5-6 may be considered at the discretion of the Principal. Selection is based on performance in the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to their studies. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion.

overview: Sacré Cœur's Music Scholarship recognises exceptional musical ability and talent. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate high-level performance in music and a commitment to developing their musical skills.
description: The Music Scholarship provides full or partial remission of tuition fees until the end of Year 12, subject to the student's continuing performance in music and her ongoing contribution to the life of the School. Students entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027 are eligible to apply. Applicants must be in Years 6 to 9 in 2026 and must first sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test on Saturday 21 February 2026. Short-listed candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions, which provide an opportunity to demonstrate musical excellence in performance. The scholarship is awarded based on the combination of academic performance and musical ability. The selection process recognises both technical proficiency and artistic expression in music performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 10; Applicants must be in Years 6 to 9 in 2026; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services Scholarship Test; Must participate in music audition if short-listed; Australian citizens and permanent residents only; Continuing performance and ongoing contribution to school life required for renewal
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission

Short-listed candidates will be contacted by the Head of Music for auditions. All scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion. Medical conditions and medication information must be disclosed to the School and Academic Assessment Services well before the examination. overview: Sacré Cœur offers a limited number of means-tested bursaries annually to support students whose families may otherwise be unable to access a Sacré Cœur education. These bursaries are available to both current and new students.
description: Each year, Sacré Cœur offers a limited number of bursaries to support students whose families may otherwise be unable to access a Sacré Cœur education. These bursaries are means tested and the value of assistance varies based on the financial circumstances of the student's family. Bursaries are made possible through funding from the School, the Sacré Cœur Foundation, and the generous contributions of the community. Students entering Years 5 through to Year 11 may be considered for bursary support. The named Kathleen Bursary provides 75% of tuition fees to a student entering Year 7 whose family may not otherwise be able to afford a Sacré Cœur education. All bursaries are available to both new and current families and are awarded at the Principal's discretion following financial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 5 to 11; Open to both new and current families; Means tested on basis of family financial circumstances; Demonstrates financial hardship or inability to pay full fees; Principal discretion applied
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Bursaries are means tested and may be offered to both current and new families. The value of the bursary is related to the income and financial resources of the student's family. All bursaries are awarded at the Principal's discretion. Bursary and scholarship concessions do not apply to ancillary charges. Families should contact the Admissions and Community Manager to enquire about bursary support and financial circumstances assessment process.

overview: Saint Ignatius College Geelong offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement in Year 7 applicants. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential in their studies through performance on a standardised test.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship at Saint Ignatius College Geelong entitles successful applicants to a reduction of $1200 off the total school fees per year for Years 7 and 8. Scholarship recipients are assessed through the ACER (Australian Council for Educational Research) Scholarship test, which is administered and marked by ACER at the college. The test is not curriculum-based and assesses the ability to interpret, infer, deduce and think critically. The scholarship is reviewed at the end of Year 8 (the second year) based on academic and school performance. Scholarship applications are not processed through the College but directly through ACER's online registration system.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be entering in 2027 (applications now closed for 2026 and 2027); Must sit the ACER Scholarship test at the College; Year 7 2027 applications have closed
special_conditions: Scholarship entitles successful applicants to a reduction of $1200 off the total school fees per year for Years 7 and 8. The scholarship will be reviewed at the end of the second year, based on academic and school performance. Applications are not processed through the College but through ACER online. Students arrive at the college office by 3:45pm and are collected at 6:40pm on test day.

overview: Saint Ignatius College Geelong offers a limited number of bursaries for prospective students entering Years 7, 9 and 11 who would otherwise be unable to attend the College. These bursaries provide financial assistance as a contribution to the payment of school fees based on financial need and merit considerations.
description: Saint Ignatius College Geelong offers bursaries that provide a contribution to the payment of school fees for students entering Years 7, 9 and 11 who would otherwise be unable to attend the College due to financial circumstances. Bursaries may be available for up to 6 years of enrolment and apply to fees, not levies. All applications are assessed on their merits, and the amount varies based on individual circumstances, available funds, and assessment of need. As financial circumstances may change, all bursaries are reviewed during the second year of assistance. Selection of successful applicants considers academic performance, sporting talents, performing arts talents and community involvements. Priority is given to children who are baptised Catholic, attending Catholic schools, and whose parents are actively involved in a parish community. Applications are submitted directly to the College Registrar.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Open to students entering Year 9; Open to students entering Year 11; Must otherwise be unable to attend the College; Must demonstrate financial need; Priority given to baptised Catholic students; Priority given to families attending Catholic schools; Priority given to families actively involved in parish community
special_conditions: Bursaries may be available for up to 6 years. As financial circumstances may change, there is a requirement for the bursary to be reviewed during the second year of assistance. Bursary applies to fees and not to levies. Academic performance, sporting talents, performing arts talents and community involvements will be used to select successful applicants. Priority is given to children who are baptised Catholic, who may be attending a Catholic school and whose parents are actively involved in a parish community. Submit application to the College Registrar via email at registrar@ignatius.vic.edu.au.

overview: Sale College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program for Years 7-9 to provide a challenging program for academically able students. The program targets students identified as being in need of work presented at a significantly higher level and faster pace than the majority of students with the same chronological age.
description: The SEAL program is an accelerated learning pathway at Sale College's Guthridge Campus that runs for Years 7 through 9. Students undertake a compacted curriculum in five of the eight Key Learning Areas, with the aim to complete Years 7-10 in three years. Selection requires academic ability in approximately the top 20% of students their age, motivation, commitment to completing tasks, strong self-concept, and supportive parents or guardians. The program creates a stimulating learning environment that engages and inspires students and promotes independent and responsible study habits. Selection involves written assessment (based on Grade 5 NAPLAN results), parent evaluation, student submission ('The Famous You'), and an interview with students and parents. Students must pay a $30 application fee (subsidized by the school), with late applications incurring a $50 fee.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students entering Year 7 at Sale College; Must be identified as academically talented, approximately top 20% of age group; Must be motivated and interested with demonstrated commitment to completing set tasks; Must have strong and positive self-concept; Must have supportive parents or guardians; Must provide Grade 5 NAPLAN results; Must complete Parent Evaluation and 'The Famous You' submission
special_conditions: Late applications incur $50 fee. Payment required at time of application submission. Students must still follow the standard Year 7 enrolment process regardless of SEAL status if enrolling at Sale College. Compacted curriculum delivers Years 7-10 content in three years. Selection committee decision is final.

school_name: Saltwater P-9 College - Coaching Parade Campus

overview: Salesian College Chadstone awards scholarships on an annual basis to recognise personal excellence in curricular and co-curricular activities. The scholarship program aims to recognise, reinforce and reward high achieving students at all year levels from Year 7 to Year 11.
description: The College Scholarship Program, established in 2001, is designed to encourage students at each year level to achieve their personal best. Scholarships are awarded to recognise excellence in both academic and co-curricular pursuits. The program aims to identify and support high-achieving boys from Year 7 through to Year 11. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated excellence in their studies and participation in school activities. Since its inception, many scholarship recipients have gone on to achieve exceptional ATAR results and perform well in tertiary education. Scholarships are independent of financial circumstances and are purely merit-based awards. The college assesses applicants through a comprehensive selection process that evaluates overall achievement and contribution to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 to Year 11; Merit-based selection; Demonstrated excellence in curricular and co-curricular activities; Australian students eligible; Scholarships independent of financial circumstances
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded on an annual basis. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in curricular and co-curricular activities. The college reserves the right to determine scholarship values and number of awards annually.

school_name: School for Student Leadership-Gnurad Gundidj Campus
overview: The Student Equity Fund enables people who share the school's vision of transformative education to contribute to the School for Student Leadership program. The fund helps ensure the program is affordable and accessible for all students in the public education system.
description: The Student Equity Fund is designed to support students who may face financial barriers to participating in the School for Student Leadership residential program. This non-monetary program enables donors and supporters who share the vision of transformative education to contribute funding that helps ensure the unique residential leadership experience at Gnurad-Gundidj Campus is accessible to all Victorian Year 9 government school students regardless of financial circumstances. The specific financial benefit amount, application process, eligibility criteria, and renewal conditions are not detailed on the publicly available website. Interested families should contact the campus directly for detailed information about how the fund may assist with program costs.
eligibility_criteria: Victorian government Year 9 students; demonstrated financial need; students selected for the School for Student Leadership program
special_conditions: Fund is contributions-based and dependent on donor support. Availability and amount of assistance varies. Applicants should contact Gnurad-Gundidj Campus directly to discuss potential support options.

overview: The School for Student Leadership offers the Student Equity Fund to ensure the Year 9 residential leadership program is affordable and accessible for all eligible students from Victorian government schools. The fund supports students whose families may otherwise face financial barriers to participation in this transformative leadership program.
description: The Student Equity Fund is administered to support students attending the School for Student Leadership's unique residential Year 9 program across four Victorian campuses (Alpine, Snowy River, Gnurad-Gundidj, and Don Valley). The fund enables people who share the vision of transformative education to contribute to the program's accessibility. Students selected for the program may be eligible for financial assistance if their families demonstrate financial need. The exact financial benefit amount, application process details, and specific eligibility requirements are determined on a case-by-case basis through the Victorian Department of Education and Training. Selection for the School for Student Leadership itself requires formal written application and interview approval from home schools and regional allocations.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 9 students attending Victorian government schools; Selected through formal written application and interview process; Home school staff recommendation required; Evidence of financial need; Student must be accepted into the School for Student Leadership program itself
special_conditions: Funds are administered by the Victorian Department of Education and Training. Applicants must first be selected for the School for Student Leadership residential program. Assistance is available to ensure accessibility for students regardless of socio-economic background. School liaisons and regional coordinators assist with applications and community learning project development.

overview: The Bank Australia SSL Annual Scholarship is offered to Year 9 students participating in the School for Student Leadership program. This scholarship recognises student achievement and participation in the transformative leadership education experience provided across the school's residential campuses.
description: The Bank Australia SSL Annual Scholarship is an annual award supporting students enrolled in the School for Student Leadership Year 9 residential program. Bank Australia partners with the program to provide financial support for participating students. The scholarship supports the residential educational experience which includes accommodation, meals, leadership training, and personal development activities over one term (approximately 9 weeks). The selection process involves assessment of student leadership potential, academic progress, and commitment to the program's community learning projects. Recipients contribute to the school community and engage in team-based leadership development activities throughout their residential experience.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled Year 9 students at School for Student Leadership; Selected for participation in the residential program; Demonstrated leadership potential; Academic satisfactory standing; Participation in community learning project activities
special_conditions: Awarded annually. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic performance and conduct throughout the program. Scholarship is renewable for the duration of participation in the School for Student Leadership residential term. Community learning projects must be completed as part of program requirements.

school_name: School for Student Leadership-Don Valley Campus
School for Student Leadership-Snowy River Campus
overview: The School for Student Leadership offers the Student Equity Fund to enable students from all backgrounds to access this unique residential leadership program. The Fund helps ensure the program is affordable and accessible for all students in the public education system, regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Student Equity Fund is a financial assistance initiative that supports students who may face financial barriers to attending the School for Student Leadership program. The Fund enables donations from individuals who share the vision of transformative education to contribute toward scholarships and bursaries for eligible students. The program is designed to ensure accessibility for all students in Victoria's public education system. Recipients may receive financial support towards program fees and associated costs. The Fund operates continuously to identify and support students with demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 students attending Victorian government schools; Must be enrolled in a Victorian public school; Students with demonstrated financial need; No specific academic threshold mentioned
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to financial assessment and verification of need. Recipients must remain enrolled at their home school during their participation in the program.

overview: Sandringham College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program for students who demonstrate high intellectual potential and exceptional abilities across their studies. The program allows gifted students to progress through advanced coursework at an accelerated pace, completing Years 7-10 in three years instead of four.
description: The SEAL program at Sandringham College is designed for students of high intellectual potential who display a range of exceptional abilities across their studies. Participation in SEAL is non-monetary—no financial cost associated with program participation. Students complete Years 7-10 in three years through an accelerated curriculum, allowing additional time to explore extension subjects or complete VCE early. Selection is based on performance in the SEAL entrance examination, which assesses verbal reasoning, numerical reasoning, reading comprehension, mathematics, and written expression. To be eligible, students must have been offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College and must list the school as their first preference in their DET placement application. Applications are coordinated through the school's Publications and Events pages, with exam dates typically held in August.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; Must have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Must list Sandringham College as first preference in DET placement application; Must complete required SEAL entrance examination; Display high intellectual potential and exceptional academic abilities
special_conditions: Students wishing to enrol into this program must sit the SEAL exam. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Program eligibility is determined by DET Enrolment Policy. Students typically complete advanced work and may undertake Year 8 curriculum in Year 7.

overview: Sandringham College offers instrumental music scholarships to Year 7 students who demonstrate high level potential in music. The scholarship provides free instrumental lessons on an allocated instrument, recognising and supporting students with exceptional musical ability.
description: Sandringham College offers 25 instrumental music scholarships annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high level potential in music. Recipients receive free instrumental lessons on their allocated instrument for 2 years, with no cost to the student. The scholarship is suitable for beginners to advanced musicians. Selection is based on a musical listening test that assesses musicality and musical potential. Eligible instruments vary by year but may include strings, woodwind, brass, and percussion instruments. Students must submit an application and pass the listening assessment to be considered. The program is part of the college's commitment to building a strong instrumental and ensemble music program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must be currently enrolled in Grade 6 or offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College; Must demonstrate high level potential in music; Suitable for both beginners and advanced musicians; Listening test to assess musicality required
special_conditions: Scholarship students will receive FREE instrumental lessons on their allocated instrument for 2 years. Specific instruments offered vary by year. Applications typically open in early August. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply for scholarships.

overview: Sandringham College's Academy of Performing Arts Dance (SCAPA) is a pre-professional dance training program for talented students aged 12 to 18 years. The program provides specialist dance training including classical ballet, jazz, contemporary, hip hop, and pilates classes integrated with the full academic curriculum.
description: The Sandringham College Academy of Performing Arts (Dance Academy) is a selective pre-professional dance training program designed for talented dancers aged 12 to 18 years. The program has no associated tuition cost. Students participate in weekly classical ballet, jazz, contemporary, hip hop, and pilates classes alongside their core academic subjects. Additional workshops and excursions throughout the year expose students to a wide variety of dance and performing arts experiences. Selection is based on audition performance demonstrating dance ability and potential. Only students who have been offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Applications open in June and close in July, with auditions held in late July. The program is designed to develop students' dancing abilities through a specialised and integrated curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or current students aged 12-18; Must have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Must demonstrate dance ability through audition; Passion for dance and commitment to development required
special_conditions: Auditions for 2026 entry held on Monday 28th July 2025 after school. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply for academy programs. Students must be aged 12-18 years and demonstrate talent and commitment to dance.

overview: The Sandringham College Sports Academy is a selective program that develops students' sporting abilities through specialised and accelerated curriculum in sports including football, soccer, rugby, netball, and basketball. The program integrates intensive sport training with full academic studies.
description: The Sandringham College Sports Academy is a specialised program designed to develop students' sporting abilities through an accelerated curriculum in football, soccer, rugby, netball, and basketball. The program has no financial cost. Students receive specialist coaching and training integrated with their core academic subjects. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting ability assessed through written application and practical trials. To be eligible, applicants must obtain and attach their Grade 6 Semester 1 report, Grade 5 NAPLAN report, and a letter of recommendation from a sports coach or teacher. Applications open in July and close later that month, with trials held in late July. Only students who have been offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Must provide Grade 6 Semester 1 report; Must provide Grade 5 NAPLAN report; Must provide letter of recommendation from sports coach or teacher; Demonstrated sporting ability required
special_conditions: Trials held on Thursday 31st July 2025. Applications close 25th July 2025. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply for academy programs. Students must demonstrate commitment to sport and willingness to participate in intensive training alongside academic studies.

overview: The Art Academy at Sandringham College is a selective program for students with a passion for art who want to further develop their artistic talents. The program provides specialist visual arts training integrated with the full academic curriculum.
description: Sandringham College's Art Academy is a selective program designed for students with demonstrated passion and talent in visual arts. The program has no associated financial cost. Students develop their artistic abilities through specialist training while maintaining their full academic curriculum. The college has an excellent reputation for its programs in performing and visual arts, with students having access to professional studio facilities. Selection is based on demonstrated artistic ability and passion for art. Only students who have been offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Applications for 2026 entry close on 5th August 2025. The program encourages students to further develop their visual arts talents within a supportive and creative school environment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Must demonstrate passion for art; Artistic ability and commitment to development required
special_conditions: Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply for academy programs. Students must demonstrate genuine passion and commitment to visual arts development. Selection based on artistic ability demonstrated through application materials.

school_name: Seaford Primary School-Seaford Special Assistance Unit

overview: Scotch College awards Academic Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate academic excellence across a broad range of disciplines. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional achievement in academic testing and supporting documentation, with a commitment to learning and contribution to school life.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are awarded based on the results of an academic test and supporting documentation provided at the time of application, as well as an interview with the Director of Extension Studies or nominated delegate. Applicants sit the scholarship test at Scotch College, which covers a range of disciplines including General Reasoning and Problem Solving, Mathematics Achievement and Reasoning, Reading Comprehension and Written Expression. Shortlisted applicants are invited to an interview. Scholarship recipients receive a reduction in their tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch. Both means-tested and non-means-tested categories are available. The exact financial benefit will be determined at the time of offer, ranging from partial to full fee remission based on merit and financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be either a new student entering Years 7, 9 or 10, or a current Scotch College Year 5 student applying for a Year 7 entry scholarship; Attend and complete the academic test, which will be held at Scotch; Current students of Scotch College Senior School are NOT eligible to apply
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients receive reductions in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch. Both means-tested and non-means-tested categories are available for each scholarship type. Terms and conditions apply to all successful applicants.

overview: Scotch College offers General Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate a commitment to academic improvement and very high achievement in one or more of the following categories: performing arts (music, drama), visual arts, sport, leadership or community service. The scholarship recognises all-round excellence across multiple disciplines.
description: General Excellence Scholarship applicants must demonstrate their capability, achievements and potential in their respective field of endeavour, and outline how the school community will benefit from their scholarship. Applicants may be required to attend an additional audition or practical day depending on their chosen category. The scholarship selection process involves academic testing covering General Reasoning and Problem Solving, Mathematics Achievement and Reasoning, Reading Comprehension and Written Expression. Shortlisted candidates proceed to interview with the Principal, Director of Extension Studies and/or nominated delegates. Scholarship recipients receive reductions in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch. Both means-tested and non-means-tested categories are available.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be either a new student entering Years 7, 9 or 10, or a current Scotch College Year 5 student applying for a Year 7 entry scholarship; Attend and complete the academic test, which will be held at Scotch; General Excellence Scholarship applicants may be required to also attend a Practical Audition Day; Current students of Scotch College Senior School are NOT eligible to apply
special_conditions: Applicants must be able to demonstrate their capability, achievements and potential in their respective field of endeavour. They may also need to attend an additional audition day. Interviews are held either in person or via video conference, depending on applicant location. Scholarship recipients must uphold the school's values and maintain academic improvement.

overview: Scotch College awards Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate high-level musical ability and a passion for music. The scholarship recognises students who meet specific instrumental standards and show commitment to the school's music program.
description: Music Scholarships are offered to students demonstrating very high achievement in musical performance. Applicants for a Year 7 Music Scholarship should be of at least AMEB Grade 6 standard for orchestral instruments, and Grade 8 for pianists, if piano is their first instrument. Those applying for a Year 9 or Year 10 Music Scholarship should be of AMEB Grade 7 standard for orchestral instruments, and Grade 8 for pianists, if piano is their first instrument. As part of the audition process, candidates will be asked to perform two contrasting works (or contrasting movements from the same work) on their main instrument, and one piece on the second instrument if applicable. Scholarship selection involves academic testing, audition, portfolio submission and interview. Scholarship recipients receive reductions in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be either a new student entering Years 7, 9 or 10, or a current Scotch College Year 5 student applying for a Year 7 entry scholarship; Attend and complete the academic test; For Year 7: AMEB Grade 6 standard minimum for orchestral instruments, Grade 8 for piano; For Year 9-10: AMEB Grade 7 standard minimum for orchestral instruments, Grade 8 for piano; Current students of Scotch College Senior School are NOT eligible to apply
special_conditions: Music Scholarship applicants must undergo academic testing, audition, and interview. Applicants must be able to demonstrate their musical capability, achievements and potential, and outline how the school community will benefit from their scholarship. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interview.

overview: Scotch College offers Sport Scholarships to students who demonstrate elite-level athletic achievement. The scholarship recognises students competing at State or National level in sporting activities and show commitment to contributing to school life.
description: Sport Scholarships are part of Scotch College's General Excellence Scholarship program and are awarded to students who demonstrate a commitment to academic improvement and very high achievement in sport. Applicants for a Sport Scholarship must be competing at a State or National level in at least one sporting activity (team or individual). The scholarship selection process involves academic testing covering General Reasoning and Problem Solving, Mathematics Achievement and Reasoning, Reading Comprehension and Written Expression, followed by interview with school leadership. Applicants must demonstrate their sporting capability, achievements and potential, and outline how the school community will benefit from their scholarship. Scholarship recipients receive reductions in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be either a new student entering Years 7, 9 or 10, or a current Scotch College Year 5 student applying for a Year 7 entry scholarship; Attend and complete the academic test, which will be held at Scotch; Must be competing at a State or National level in at least one sporting activity (team or individual); Current students of Scotch College Senior School are NOT eligible to apply
special_conditions: Applicants must be competing at State or National level in sport. Academic testing is required alongside sporting achievement assessment. Interviews are held either in person or via video conference depending on applicant location. Scholarship recipients must contribute to school sporting culture and maintain academic improvement.

overview: Scotch College awards Boarding Scholarships to students seeking a boarding experience at the school's on-campus facilities. Preference is given to students from rural and regional Australia who demonstrate academic improvement and contribution to school life.
description: Boarding Scholarships offer boys the opportunity to live and learn within the contemporary boarding facilities at the heart of Scotch College's Hawthorn campus. Preference for Boarding Scholarships is given to boys from rural or regional Australia. The scholarship selection process involves academic testing covering a range of disciplines including General Reasoning and Problem Solving, Mathematics Achievement and Reasoning, Reading Comprehension and Written Expression. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated commitment to academic improvement and contribution to at least one aspect of school life, such as performing arts (music and drama), visual arts or sport. Applicants shortlisted for interview will be required to supply evidence of their interests and accomplishments, and reasons for wanting to attend Scotch College. Shortlisted applicants are invited to an interview. Scholarship recipients receive reductions in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of their time at Scotch.
eligibility_criteria: Be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Be either a new student entering Years 7, 9 or 10, or a current Scotch College Year 5 student applying for a Year 7 entry scholarship; Attend and complete the academic test, which will be held at Scotch; Demonstrated commitment to academic improvement and contribution to at least one aspect of school life, such as performing arts, visual arts or sport; Demonstrated contribution to the candidate's current school community and passion and potential for contributing to Scotch College; Current students of Scotch College Senior School are NOT eligible to apply; Preference given to boys from rural or regional Australia
special_conditions: Preference given to boys from rural or regional Australia. Applicants shortlisted for interview will be required to supply evidence of their interests and accomplishments, and reasons for wanting to attend Scotch College. Boarding scholarships provide reduction in tuition and/or boarding fees for the duration of enrolment. Both means-tested and non-means-tested categories available.

overview: Scotch College awards First Nations Scholarships to boys of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage who demonstrate the potential to lead their community and embrace the opportunities Scotch offers. The scholarship supports Indigenous students throughout their secondary education.
description: First Nations Scholarships are awarded to boys of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage who demonstrate the potential to lead their community and embrace the opportunities Scotch College offers. First Nations Scholarships are available for new students to Scotch College entering Years 9 or 10. First Nations Scholarships are valued up to 100% of tuition and boarding (if applicable) fees, and cover tuition fees and boarding fees (if applicable). Should families exceed the household income threshold, parents or guardians may be required to contribute a small amount towards tuition fees each year. As part of the selection process, applicants are required to complete an application form, provide their latest school reports and any letters of recommendation, and have an interview with the Director of the First Nations Program (or delegate). Scotch College provides a welcoming, supportive and culturally safe environment for First Nations students, with staff assisting students to connect with the local First Nations community.
eligibility_criteria: Be a First Nations Australian (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage); Be a new Scotch College student entering Years 9 or 10; Be eligible for receipt of ABSTUDY; Demonstrate potential to lead their community; Show commitment to academic improvement and involvement in school life; Applicants must complete application form, provide latest school reports and letters of recommendation
special_conditions: Applications for First Nations Scholarships are accepted at any time. Applicants are required to complete an application form, provide their latest school reports and any letters of recommendation, and have an interview with the Director of the First Nations Program (or delegate). Should families exceed the household income threshold, parents or guardians may be required to contribute a small amount towards tuition fees each year. Scotch College provides a welcoming, supportive and culturally safe environment for First Nations students.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children by providing temporary financial support.
description: Bursary Assistance is available to families of current SEDA College students who are experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Families should contact SEDA College Finance for more information on available support. The bursary is designed to provide temporary relief for families facing financial hardship and is discretionary in nature. Applicants are considered on an individual basis and must demonstrate genuine financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Consideration on an individual basis
special_conditions: Limited funds available. Not all applicants will be successful. Applications are assessed on an individual basis. Support is intended for severe short-term financial difficulties only.

overview: SEDA College offers a Concession Discount on student fees to students who have demonstrated financial need in the form of low family income. Students must hold a valid means-tested concession card and be eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF) to access this discount.
description: A Concession Discount on student fees is offered to students who have demonstrated financial need in the form of low family income. To be eligible, students must hold a valid means-tested concession card with the student's name on it and must be eligible for the Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF) program. The concession card must be valid on 27 January (for 2026 entry) to access this discount. The Concession Discount is dependent on the Victorian Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. The discount is not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the student's enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Student has financial need in the form of low family income; Valid means-tested concession card in student's name required; Eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF); Concession card must be valid on 27 January 2026; Cards must be financially means-tested
special_conditions: Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to commencement. The discount is dependent upon the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. Only valid means-tested concession cards are accepted. Cards that are not financially means-tested (such as Indigenous cards) are not accepted.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children by providing financial support when circumstances change.
description: Bursary Assistance is available to families of current students who are experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties and may have difficulty meeting their financial obligations to the College. The specific financial benefit amount is not publicly stated and is determined on a case-by-case basis. The program is administered by SEDA College Finance and families must contact the College directly to discuss their circumstances and eligibility. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. The selection process involves contacting the Finance office and providing information about the family's financial situation. The bursary is reviewed on an ongoing basis for continued eligibility and support.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrolment at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Family circumstances make it difficult to meet financial obligations in the short term
special_conditions: Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Applications are considered on an individual basis. Families must contact SEDA College Finance on 1800 11 7332 or via email at [email protected] for more information.

overview: SEDA College offers a Concession Discount on student fees to students with financial need in the form of low family income. Students with a valid means-tested concession card are eligible for this discount on their tuition fees.
description: A Concession Discount is offered to students who demonstrate financial need in the form of a low family income. To be eligible for a Concession Discount, students must provide a valid means-tested concession card with the student's name detailed on it. The discount is applied to the student fees structure and the specific percentage reduction is determined by the Department of Education and Training's Camps, Sports and Excursions Funding (CSEF) eligibility. The concession card must be valid on 27 January 2026 and be eligible for CSEF to access this discount. The student must be enrolled at the College during the period the card is valid. Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the term in which the discount is required.
eligibility_criteria: Valid means-tested concession card required; Card must detail student's name; Card must be valid on 27 January 2026; Must be eligible for CSEF (Camps Sports Excursions Funding); Must be enrolled at the College at the time of application
special_conditions: Concession Discount is dependent upon the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. The College may change the basis upon which the Concession Discount is offered if funding changes. The Concession Discount is not to be combined with other concessions or scholarships. Cards that are not financially means-tested are not accepted.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The college has a genuine desire to help families afford quality education for their children and provides financial support to eligible families on a case-by-case basis.
description: SEDA College understands that circumstances change for some families, making it difficult for them to meet financial obligations in the short term. The college offers Bursary Assistance with financial support to eligible families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Families of current students who would like to be considered for Bursary Assistance should contact SEDA College Finance on 1800 11 7332 or via email at [email protected] for more information. The exact financial benefit varies on a case-by-case basis following assessment of individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current student at SEDA College; Family experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Applications assessed on case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Bursary Assistance is only available to current students. Applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis by SEDA College Finance. Limited funds are available and not all applicants will be successful.

overview: SEDA College offers a Concession Discount to students from families with low family income who hold a valid means-tested concession card. This discount applies to the tuition portion of student fees for eligible families.
description: A Concession Discount on student fees is offered to students who have a financial need demonstrated through low family income. To be eligible, a valid means-tested concession card detailing the student's name is required. The concession card must be valid on 27 January 2026 and be eligible for the Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF). The discount applies only to the tuition portion of the student fees and is pro-rated over the entire year. Concession Discounts are dependent on the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders and must be applied for prior to commencement of school.
eligibility_criteria: Valid means-tested concession card detailing student's name; Concession card must be valid on 27 January 2026; Student must be eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF); Enrollment at SEDA College required; Concession card holders must meet DET financially means-tested requirements
special_conditions: Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to commencement. Discount dependent on Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding. Discount applies only to tuition portion of fees, not to other student fee components. Parents must provide copy of completed CSEF Application Form and copy of eligible concession card.

overview: SEDA College offers early payment discounts to families who pay their annual student fees upfront. Families receive either a 3% or 5% discount depending on the payment date.
description: Families who pay student fees upfront receive discounts on their annual student fees. Upfront annual payments receive either a 3% discount for later payment dates or 5% discount for earlier payment dates. This financial assistance option allows families to reduce their overall costs by paying fees in full rather than through installment plans. The college also offers flexible payment options including fortnightly and monthly installment plans as alternatives to the upfront payment discount option.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at SEDA College; Annual fees must be paid in full upfront; Discount amount depends on payment date
special_conditions: Discount applied as percentage of upfront annual payment. Discount amount depends on payment date. Flexible payment options including fortnightly and monthly instalments are available as alternatives.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children and provides financial support to assist families who are facing unexpected financial hardship.
description: SEDA College understands that circumstances change for some families, making it difficult for them to meet financial obligations in the short term. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children and therefore families of current students who are experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties may be offered Bursary Assistance. Financial assistance is provided on a case-by-case basis, though due to limited funds available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Families should contact SEDA College Finance to discuss their circumstances and submit a formal application for consideration. The specific financial value of bursary support varies depending on individual circumstances and the extent of financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Application subject to financial assessment; Bursary awarded at sole discretion of the College
special_conditions: Limited funds being available for bursaries means not all students who apply will be successful. Bursary Assistance is offered at the sole discretion of the College Principal. Families of current students who would like to be considered for Bursary Assistance should contact SEDA College Finance on 1800 11 7332 or via email at finance@scv.vic.edu.au for more information.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to current students and families experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. This program is designed to help families afford quality senior secondary education at SEDA College when circumstances change and they struggle to meet financial obligations in the short term.
description: Bursary Assistance is available to families of current students who are experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties and may otherwise be unable to continue their education at SEDA College. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children. Due to limited funds available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Applications are considered on an individual and case-by-case basis. The specific financial value of bursary support is determined individually based on family circumstances and available funding. Students must contact the SEDA College Finance department to discuss their situation and application process. The program is subject to annual review and approval at the discretion of the College administration.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrolment at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Families only (not prospective students); Case-by-case assessment of circumstances
special_conditions: Application available to current students only, not prospective students. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply will be successful. Families of current students who would like to be considered for Bursary Assistance should contact SEDA College Finance directly.

overview: Sale College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program for Years 7-9 at the Guthridge Campus. The program is designed for academically talented students identified as being in the top 20% of their age group who require work presented at a significantly higher level and faster pace than the majority of students.
description: The SEAL Program allows gifted students to undertake a compacted curriculum across five of the eight Key Learning Areas, enabling them to complete Years 7-10 in three years. The program is non-fee based (no financial award). Students are selected through a competitive process involving parent evaluation, student portfolio ('The Famous You'), review of Grade 5 NAPLAN results, written examination, and interview. This accelerated pathway provides access to broader high-level subject choices and allows for diverse pathways including three-year VCE study with additional university enhancement courses, completion of VCE in two years, or combined academic and vocational pathways. Selection occurs annually with applications due in mid-October and SEAL Examination held in late October, followed by interviews from early November.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Grade 6 may apply; Must be identified as academically talented in approximately top 20% of students their age; Must demonstrate motivation, commitment and ability to complete set tasks; Must demonstrate creative thinking, fluency and flexibility; Must have strong and positive self-concept; Must have supportive parents/guardians; NAPLAN results from Grade 5 required; Must still complete standard Year 7 enrolment process regardless of SEAL status
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee of $30 due at time of application. Late applications incur additional $50 fee. Students are expected to participate in normal school activities including sports, music, and student leadership programs. Participation in academic competitions (Australian Maths Competition, Science Competitions) is expected. Successful applicants must still complete standard Year 7 enrolment process regardless of SEAL status. Students entering the program may not necessarily be currently achieving outstanding academic results.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children by providing financial support during temporary hardship periods.
description: Bursary Assistance is available to families of current students who are experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties and may have trouble meeting financial obligations. The College understands that circumstances change for some families, making it difficult to meet payments in the short term. However, due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Families interested in this support should contact SEDA College Finance to discuss their circumstances and eligibility. The amount of bursary support varies based on individual family circumstances and available funds.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Application considered on individual basis
special_conditions: Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Families of current students who would like to be considered for Bursary Assistance should contact SEDA College Finance on 1800 117 332 or via email at [email protected] for more information.

overview: SEDA College offers a Concession Discount to students from families with low income who hold a valid means-tested concession card. This discount reduces student fees for eligible families participating in the Camps, Sports and Excursions Funding (CSEF) scheme.
description: A Concession Discount on student fees is offered to students who have financial need in the form of a low family income. To be eligible, a valid means-tested concession card detailing the student's name is required. The concession card must be valid on 27 January 2026 and be eligible for the Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF) scheme. The concession discount is dependent upon the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. The exact discount amount varies but is applied directly to the student's annual fees. Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of studies.
eligibility_criteria: Hold valid means-tested concession card detailing student's name; Card must be valid on 27 January 2026; Eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF); Victorian Department of Education and Training funding must be available
special_conditions: Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to commencement. The discount is dependent upon the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. The College may change the basis upon which the Concession Discount is offered if required. Only financially means-tested concession cards are accepted; cards that are not financially means tested are not eligible.

overview: SEDA College offers the AFL Victoria Sports Development Program, which allows students to complete Years 11 and 12 in a hands-on learning environment connected to the AFL industry. Students of all ability levels may apply and work alongside AFL Victoria and AFL Club professionals.
description: The AFL Victoria Sports Development Program provides a unique learning experience enabling students to complete their Years 11 and 12 whilst developing knowledge through real projects, events and activities with AFL Victoria and AFL Clubs. Students work with experts in the AFL industry and learn from senior management, football operations, coaching, recruiting, high performance, marketing, communications, and community engagement professionals. The program combines VCE VM (Victorian Certificate of Education - Vocational Major) delivery with VET Qualification SIS30115 Certificate III in Sport and Recreation. Selection into the program is based on an online application and interview process. The program is not dependent on sporting skill, and students of all ability levels may apply. No specific financial scholarship is listed for this program beyond standard fees.
eligibility_criteria: Successfully completed Year 10 or equivalent; Born between 01/01/07 – 30/04/09 (for 2025 entry); Apply the year before intended commencement; Selection not dependent on sporting skills
special_conditions: Selection into SEDA College's programs is based on an online application and interview process. Students intending to commence in Year 11 begin their application while in Year 10. No trials conducted for AFL Victoria SDP (unlike HPP program).

overview: SEDA College offers the AFL Victoria High Performance Program for students to complete secondary education in a high-performance training environment endorsed by AFL Victoria. The program is designed for elite players who meet specific playing ability criteria and wish to complement their sporting pathways with senior secondary education.
description: The AFL Victoria High Performance Program combines the completion of Senior Secondary qualifications (VCE VM) with high performance AFL training. Students develop elite training habits through specialised training sessions and individualised strength and conditioning programs. The program includes VET Qualification SIS30115 Certificate III in Sport and Recreation, and is designed to complement a student's involvement in their elite sporting pathways whilst supporting the completion of their Senior Secondary Education. Students will be monitored and tracked through an Athlete Management System to ensure their wellbeing remains at the forefront and allow training loads to be customised through individualised programs. Selection is based on an online application and interview process, with trials conducted for successful candidates. Applicants must demonstrate high level of playing ability and meet specific criteria to automatically qualify.
eligibility_criteria: Successfully completed Year 10 or equivalent; Born between 01/01/07 – 30/04/09 (for 2025 entry); Demonstrate high level of playing ability; Meet one of: Currently on Under 18's Coates Talent League Club list OR Currently in Under 16 National Trials for Vic Metro or Vic Country OR Currently in Under 15 or 16 Coates Talent League Development squad; Subject to trials for interview stage
special_conditions: Applicants who meet the primary criteria automatically qualify for a position. Those meeting other criteria may be subject to a waitlist. Students must provide documentation at interview detailing their involvement in specified playing levels. Trials are required after interview stage (except for AFL Victoria HPP). Students must demonstrate commitment to elite training habits and standards in preparation, approach to training, games, and recovery.

overview: Sandringham College offers the Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program for students of high intellectual potential who display exceptional abilities across their studies. The program is designed to accelerate and extend learning for gifted students entering Year 7.
description: The SEAL Program addresses the needs of students who are of high intellectual potential and display a range of exceptional abilities across their studies. Students undergo a competitive examination after receiving an offer of enrolment at Sandringham College. The program requires a student statement indicating why the student wishes to join SEAL and what makes them a good candidate. A letter of recommendation from the student's current Grade 6 teacher, School Principal or Leader of Community activity is also required. Students must have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College for the year of entry. The examination assesses academic excellence and potential. Selection is based on test performance combined with the application materials. There is no financial benefit listed for this program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Open to Year 7 entry only; Nominate Sandringham College as first preference school in the statewide Application for Year 7 Placement form; Must submit student statement and letter of recommendation; Must sit the SEAL entry examination after receiving offer of enrolment
special_conditions: In accordance with the Department of Education Placement Policy, student tests or entry interviews must not occur prior to Year 7 placement offers. All students offered enrolment at Sandringham College for Year 7 will be invited to register for the SEAL examination.

overview: Sandringham College offers Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate high level potential in music. Up to 25 scholarships are available to Year 7 students entering the school.
description: Sandringham College offers 25 Music Scholarships annually to students who demonstrate high level potential in music. The program is suitable for beginners to advanced musicians. Applicants must submit an application form and undertake a listening test that assesses musicality and musical potential. Students selected for scholarships receive instrumental music tuition. The exact financial benefit is not specified but appears to include tuition support. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Must be offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Open to Year 7 entry only; Must demonstrate high level potential in music; Applicants from beginner to advanced level welcome; Must submit application form and sit listening test
special_conditions: Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Music Scholarships are offered to support instrumental tuition.

overview: Sandringham College offers the Dance Academy, also known as Sandringham College Academy of Performing Arts (SCAPA), as a specialist performing arts program for talented dancers. This pre-professional dance training program is designed for students aged 12 to 18 years.
description: The Dance Academy (SCAPA) is a pre-professional dance training program for talented students aged 12 to 18 years. Students participate in weekly classes including classical ballet, jazz, contemporary, hip hop and pilates alongside their core academic subjects. The program includes additional workshops and excursions throughout the year to expose students to a wide variety of dance and performing arts experiences. Applicants must attend auditions where dance ability and potential are assessed. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. The program requires students to demonstrate significant commitment to dance and maintain academic standards.
eligibility_criteria: Must be offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Open to Years 7-12; Must be aged 12-18 years old; Must demonstrate dance ability and potential at audition; Only enrolled students can apply; Must maintain attendance and academic performance
