special_conditions: Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Students participate in weekly classical ballet, jazz, contemporary, hip hop and pilates classes. The program is held at both Bluff Road and Holloway Road campuses.

overview: Sandringham College offers the Sports Academy program designed to develop students' sporting abilities through a specialised and accelerated curriculum. The program focuses on individual needs and athletic development in sports including football, soccer, rugby, netball and basketball.
description: The Sandringham College Sports Academy program aims to develop students in sport and personal fitness through a specialised and accelerated curriculum. The program offers tailored training and coaching in football, soccer, rugby, netball and basketball. Applicants must obtain and attach their Grade 6 Semester 1 report, Grade 5 NAPLAN report and a letter of recommendation from a sports coach or teacher to support their application. Selection trials are held where sporting ability, fitness and potential are assessed. Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. The program focuses on developing strong athletes with excellent movement skills, speed, agility and proper technique.
eligibility_criteria: Must be offered enrolment at Sandringham College; Open to Year 7 entry; Must submit Grade 6 Semester 1 school report; Must submit Grade 5 NAPLAN report; Must provide letter of recommendation from sports coach or teacher; Must participate in selection trials; Demonstrated sporting ability required
special_conditions: Only students who have been offered enrolment at Sandringham College are eligible to apply. Sports Academy trials are held after the application deadline. The program includes specialised and accelerated curriculum focusing on individual athletic development.

overview: Sandringham College offers the Art Academy for students with a passion for art who wish to further develop their artistic talents. The program provides opportunities to work with visiting artists across a range of mediums and learn from experienced art teachers.
description: The Art Academy is designed to enhance students' artistic skills, inspire creativity and provide hands-on experience in various art forms. The program is committed to a holistic vision of the arts that acknowledges the importance of community learning and practice within cultural industries. Art Academy workshops are scheduled for Wednesday after school to avoid disruptions in students' classroom learning. The program provides opportunities for students to engage with their peers, teachers and established local artists throughout the three-year program. It offers a unique chance to work with visiting artists across a range of mediums and learn from two experienced art teachers. Applicants must submit a portfolio or sample of their artistic work. Only students offered Year 7 enrolment are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Must be offered Year 7 enrolment at Sandringham College; Open to Year 7 entry; Must have a passion for art and demonstrated artistic interest; Portfolio or sample artwork required; Applications evaluated based on artistic potential and commitment
special_conditions: Only students offered Year 7 enrolment are eligible to apply. Art Academy workshops held Wednesday after school. Program provides hands-on experience with visiting artists and access to various art mediums.

overview: SEDA College Victoria offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The program provides financial support to help eligible families meet their educational fee obligations during periods of hardship.

SEDA College understands that circumstances can change for families, making it difficult to meet financial obligations in the short term. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. Applications are assessed on an individual basis. Applicants must be current students enrolled at the College. The College will consider each case based on the specific circumstances presented.

overview: SEDA College Victoria provides a Concession Discount on student fees for families with a demonstrated financial need in the form of low family income. Students must hold a valid means-tested concession card to access this discount.
description: The Concession Discount Program offers a discount on student tuition fees to families demonstrating financial need through a valid means-tested concession card. Students must hold a valid concession card (such as JSP or other eligible means-tested cards) on 27 January 2026 to be eligible for the 2026 academic year. The student must also be eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF) to access this discount. The concession discount applies only to the Tuition portion of the Student Fees and is pro-rated over the entire year. The discount is dependent on the Victorian Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the academic year.

special_conditions: Concession card must be valid on entry date and throughout the year. If the card becomes invalid, the discount is removed. The discount applies only to the Tuition portion of fees, not to other student fee components such as uniforms, student insurance, excursions, or technology. Department of Education and Training must continue funding for this program to remain available.

The College works with families individually to establish payment arrangements that suit their circumstances.

overview: SEDA College offers an Early Payment Discount to families who pay their annual student fees in full by specified dates. This financial incentive rewards families who pay fees promptly and reduces overall annual costs.
description: A discount is available for early payment of Student Fees. For all Student Fees paid in full by 18 November the discount is 5% of the total Student Fees. For all Student Fees paid in full by 18 January the discount is 3% of the total Student Fees. Students in Years 11 and 12 for 2026 are eligible for this discount. Upfront annual payments receive either a 3% or 5% discount depending on the payment date. This discount applies to the total Student Fees charged for the school year and must be paid by the specified date to qualify. The discount is automatically applied when fees are paid in full by the deadline.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at SEDA College; Payment of total annual Student Fees in full; Payment by 18 November for 5% discount OR payment by 18 January for 3% discount
special_conditions: 5% discount applies only if fees paid in full by 18 November. 3% discount applies if fees paid in full by 18 January. Discount applies to total Student Fees. No application required - discount automatically applied upon payment. Families must have confirmed enrolment prior to paying fees.

overview: SEDA College provides a Sibling Discount to families with multiple children enrolled at the college. The discount applies to the second and subsequent students once fees for the first family member are paid in full.
description: A Sibling Discount is available for families with multiple students enrolled at SEDA College. The discount will be applied as a credit to the accounts of the second and subsequent family members only once all Student Fees owing for the first family member have been paid in full. Payment Plans will be set up for all family members on the basis of the total Student Fees owing and adjusted for the Sibling Discount in the final payment made on the Plan. The discount applies to each sibling attending the College and helps reduce the overall family cost of education. The exact percentage of the sibling discount is determined annually and communicated to families through the Fees Schedule documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more family members enrolled at SEDA College; All fees for the first student must be paid in full before discount applies to siblings; Siblings must be enrolled in same or different year levels
special_conditions: Discount only applies to second and subsequent family members. First family member must have all fees paid in full before discount applies. Payment Plans include adjustment for sibling discount in final payment. Families with multiple children attending simultaneously are eligible. No application required.

school_name: SEDA College (Victoria) - AFL Victoria & Cricket Australia Sports Development Program - Strathmore
overview: SEDA College offers the AFL Victoria Sports Development Program for students completing Years 11 and 12. This program provides direct industry experience in AFL, combining senior secondary education with hands-on learning opportunities with AFL Victoria and AFL Clubs.
description: The AFL Victoria Sports Development Program allows students to complete their Years 11 and 12 at SEDA College while gaining direct exposure to the AFL industry. Students learn from AFL industry experts and participate in real projects, events and activities with staff from AFL Victoria and AFL Clubs. The program is delivered through the Victorian Certificate of Education - Vocational Major (VCE VM) alongside VET qualifications in Sport and Recreation. Students develop both theoretical knowledge and practical skills in AFL, including participation in performance training, skill development and fitness training sessions specific to football. Students also have opportunities to work on their own game while developing broader industry and life skills. Selection is based on an online application and interview process. The program includes specialist coaching opportunities and all-inclusive fees that encompass tuition, uniforms, technology including notebook computers, and compulsory educational excursions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Must have successfully completed Year 10 or equivalent; Born between 01/01/07 – 30/04/09 (Entry 2025) or 01/01/08 – 30/04/10 (Entry 2026); Selection based on online application and interview process
special_conditions: No direct scholarship or fee reduction stated for this program; however, students may be eligible for Bursary Assistance if experiencing financial hardship. All students must complete an online application form and attend an information session, followed by an interview. Documentation required at interview includes recent school report, written references and resume.

school_name: SEDA College (Victoria) - AFL Victoria & Cricket Australia Sports Development Program - Strathmore
overview: SEDA College offers the AFL Victoria High Performance Program for elite AFL players completing Years 11 and 12. This program combines senior secondary education with high-performance sports training endorsed by AFL Victoria, designed for players demonstrating exceptional playing ability.
description: The AFL Victoria High Performance Program is an elite program for students who demonstrate a high level of AFL playing ability. Students complete their secondary education in a high-performance training environment endorsed by AFL Victoria, combining senior secondary education completion with elite sports training. The program develops elite training habits through specialised training sessions and individualised strength and conditioning programs. Students develop their High-Performance Plan designed to improve their football, with support from leading professionals in the sport. The program integrates the Victorian Certificate of Education - Vocational Major (VCE VM) with VET qualifications in Sport and Recreation. Students are monitored and tracked through an Athlete Management System to ensure wellbeing remains paramount and training loads are customised. Selection into the program is based on an online application and interview process; however, trials are not conducted for the AFL Victoria High Performance Program. Students demonstrating high playing ability meeting one of the specific criteria automatically qualify for a position.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate high level of playing ability and meet one of the following: Currently on the list of an Under 18's Coates Talent League Club; Currently in the Under 16 National Trials for Vic Metro or Vic Country; Currently in the Under 15 or 16 Coates Talent League Development squad; Born between 01/01/07 – 30/04/09 (Entry 2025) or 01/01/08 – 30/04/10 (Entry 2026); Must have successfully completed Year 10 or equivalent; May be subject to waitlist depending on playing level; Applicants must provide documentation at interview detailing involvement in qualifying criteria
special_conditions: Elite program for high-performing AFL players only; Automatic qualification for students meeting playing ability criteria; No trials conducted for AFL Victoria High Performance Program; Students must demonstrate documentation of playing level; Subject to SEDA College general admissions eligibility requirements. All students must complete online application form and attend information session and interview.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The program demonstrates the College's commitment to helping families afford a quality education for their children by providing need-based financial support.
description: SEDA College understands that circumstances change for some families, making it difficult for them to meet financial obligations in the short term. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children and therefore families of current students who are experiencing severe short term financial difficulties may be offered Bursary Assistance. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. The specific financial value of bursaries is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual circumstances. Applicants must be current students at SEDA College and demonstrate genuine financial hardship. Selection is based on an assessment of individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Current student at SEDA College; Experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Case-by-case assessment of circumstances required
special_conditions: Families of current students who would like to be considered for Bursary Assistance should contact SEDA College Finance on 1800 11 7332 or via email at finance@scv.vic.edu.au for more information. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply will be successful. Applications are considered on an individual basis.

school_name: SEDA College (Victoria) - Ryans Reserve Tennis & Netball Centre
overview: SEDA College offers a Concession Discount on student fees for families with demonstrated financial need in the form of low family income. Students with a valid means-tested concession card are eligible to receive a discount on their tuition fees.
description: A Concession Discount on fees is offered to students who have a financial need in the form of a low family income. A valid means-tested concession card detailing the student's name is required for the student to be eligible for a Concession Discount. The concession card needs to be valid on 27 January 2026 to be eligible for the Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF) to access this discount. The Concession Discount applies only to the Tuition portion of the Student Fees and is pro-rated over the entire school year. Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the school year. The concession discount is dependent on the continuation of funding by the Victorian Department of Education and Training.
eligibility_criteria: Valid means-tested concession card required detailing student's name; Card must be valid on or before 27 January 2026; Must be eligible for Camps Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF); Low family income threshold; Eligible for Victorian Department of Education and Training concession funding
special_conditions: Concession Discount only applies to tuition fees, not to uniform, notebook, or other costs. Discount is pro-rated over the entire school year. Dependent upon Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. College may change the basis upon which the Concession Discount is offered if funding arrangements change. Please note that Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the school year. Parents applying for a Concession Discount must send a copy of the completed CSEF Application Form along with a copy of the eligible concession card to SEDA College Finance.

overview: Sholem Aleichem College offers the Mentshlekhkayt Scholarship to recognise and support primary school children who demonstrate exceptional character and values. The scholarship celebrates qualities such as kindness, empathy, moral responsibility, and a commitment to creating a caring school community.
description: The Mentshlekhkayt Scholarship is awarded to children who show kindness, curiosity and a love of learning, and who help make the school a happy, caring place. 'Mentshlekhkayt' means humanity and refers to qualities such as kindness, empathy, moral responsibility, and acting with decency. Scholarship recipients should demonstrate respect and pride in their connection to Jewish culture, history and traditions; be friendly and include others by treating everyone kindly and fairly; enjoy learning languages and about culture and identity; show creativity, curiosity and a desire to learn new things; and help make the school a happy and caring place for everyone. The scholarship may cover all tuition fees. Recipients are expected to keep showing a strong commitment to learning and being positive members of the College community for the duration of their enrolment. All external applicants must have attended a tour at the College prior to applying. If shortlisted, families may be invited to meet with the Principal for an interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to primary school children up to the end of Year 6; All external applicants must have attended a College tour prior to application; Must demonstrate kindness, curiosity and love of learning; Must help make the school a happy, caring place; Shortlisted applicants may be invited for interview with Principal
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided by the Principal and the number may change each year. Recipients must keep showing a strong commitment to learning and being positive members of the College community. Terms and Conditions are made available with the enquiry form.

overview: Sholem Aleichem College offers the Tsuzamen Scholarship to celebrate and support families committed to learning together and active participation in College life. The scholarship recognises collaboration, inclusion, and a commitment to being active members of the school community.
description: Tsuzamen, which means 'together' in Yiddish, celebrates learning together, collaboration, including others and being active in College life. This scholarship aims to support families who wish to learn together with the College and participate actively in the school community. The scholarship may cover all tuition fees and supports families who demonstrate commitment to collaborative learning and community engagement. Recipients should keep showing a strong commitment to learning and being positive members of the College community throughout their enrolment. All external applicants must have attended a tour at the College prior to applying for the scholarship. If shortlisted, families may be invited to meet with the Principal for an interview, and the College may ask for additional documentation at that time.
eligibility_criteria: Open to primary school children up to the end of Year 6; All external applicants must have attended a College tour prior to application; Must demonstrate commitment to learning together and collaboration; Must show intention to be active in College life; Shortlisted applicants may be invited for interview with Principal
special_conditions: Scholarships are decided by the Principal and the number may change each year. Recipients must keep showing a strong commitment to learning and being positive members of the College community. Terms and Conditions are made available with the enquiry form. Applications for entry in 2026 and 2027 are open.

overview: Sholem Aleichem College recognises that families may face financial difficulties and offers Fee Assistance to ensure all children can access a quality Jewish education. The College provides flexible financial support to eligible families experiencing limited income or temporary financial circumstances.
description: Sholem Aleichem College recognises that some families will be unable to afford College fees as a result of either their limited income and/or temporary financial circumstances. The College is able to provide Fee Assistance to eligible families to ensure that all Jewish children can access a quality Jewish education. The amount of assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and may cover partial or full fee support. The application process involves completion of a Fee Assistance Application Form and a Fee Assistance Agreement Form. A member of the Fee Assistance Committee works with each family, with compassion and discretion, to ensure that they receive the appropriate level of assistance. This is an ongoing program available throughout the year for families experiencing financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial difficulty; Limited income or temporary financial circumstances; Application requires completion of Fee Assistance Application Form and Agreement Form; Discretionary assessment by Fee Assistance Committee
special_conditions: Confidential assessment process. Fee Assistance Committee determines appropriate level of assistance on a case-by-case basis. All families can request a confidential discussion about fee relief options.

overview: SEDA College offers Bursary Assistance to families of current students experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties. The program is designed to help families afford quality education when unexpected circumstances affect their ability to meet financial obligations.
description: SEDA College understands that circumstances change for some families, making it difficult for them to meet financial obligations in the short term. The College has a genuine desire to help families afford a quality education for their children and therefore families of current students who are experiencing severe short term financial difficulties may be offered Bursary Assistance. Due to limited funds being available for bursaries, not all students who apply for Bursary Assistance will be successful. The financial value and extent of assistance varies based on individual circumstances and available funding. Families must contact SEDA College Finance directly to discuss their specific situation and apply for consideration. The assistance is provided on a case-by-case basis and applications are assessed individually.
eligibility_criteria: Families of current students; Must be experiencing severe short-term financial difficulties; Individual assessment required
special_conditions: Applications must be made directly to SEDA College Finance. Limited funds are available, and not all applicants will be successful. Assessment is conducted on a case-by-case basis.

overview: SEDA College provides a Concession Discount on student fees for families with demonstrated financial need. Students must hold a valid means-tested Concession Card to access this discount on their tuition fees.
description: A Concession Discount on student fees is offered to students who have a financial need in the form of low family income. A valid means-tested concession card detailing the student's name is required for eligibility. The Concession Card must be valid on 27 January 2026 to access this discount and the student must be eligible for the Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF). The discount applies only to the tuition portion of the student fees and is pro-rated over the entire school year. The amount of the discount varies depending on the card type and government funding continuation. Concession Discounts are not backdated and must be applied for prior to the commencement of the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Valid means-tested Concession Card required; Card must be in student's name; Card must be valid on 27 January 2026; Eligible for Camps Sports Excursions Funding (CSEF); Student must be enrolled at the College
special_conditions: Concession Discount applies only to the tuition portion of student fees. The discount is dependent upon the Department of Education and Training continuing to provide funding for concession card holders. The College reserves the right to change the basis upon which the Concession Discount is offered if government funding changes. Valid concession card types include Commonwealth Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, and Veterans Gold Card.

overview: Simonds Catholic College offers the Altar Server Tuition Scholarship to students who become altar servers at St Patrick's Cathedral. The scholarship recognises the contribution of students who actively participate in their faith community through service at the cathedral.
description: Simonds Catholic College provides a tuition scholarship of $430 per year to students who become altar servers at St Patrick's Cathedral. This scholarship is awarded to eligible students as recognition for their religious service and commitment to the Catholic faith. The scholarship provides a direct reduction of $430 annually from the student's tuition fees. Recipients must maintain their position as altar servers at St Patrick's Cathedral to continue receiving the benefit. The scholarship is available for the duration of a student's enrolment at the college, subject to continued service requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective Simonds Catholic College students; Must become and maintain status as an altar server at St Patrick's Cathedral; Must be enrolled at Simonds Catholic College; Must continue service as altar server for duration of scholarship tenure
special_conditions: Scholarship contingent upon becoming and maintaining position as altar server at St Patrick's Cathedral. Recipient must continue service for the scholarship to remain active.

overview: Sirius College offers Academic Scholarships to reward high achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The scholarship recognises students based on their academic results during their study at the College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results.
description: The Academic Scholarship at Sirius College aims to support and recognise students who display outstanding academic achievement. Scholarships are based on students' academic results during their study at the College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results. These scholarships aim to reward high achieving students and provide financial support to further their education. The specific financial value, number of awards, and detailed selection criteria are not publicly disclosed on the school website; families are advised to contact the school office directly or refer to the Scholarship and Award Policy for complete details.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students only; Year 7 to Year 12; Based on academic performance during study at the College and/or entrance exam results for prospective students; Selection based on entrance examination performance and academic merit.
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship based on entrance exam results for prospective students or academic results during study at the College. All applications within the Open Period are charged a $75 non-refundable application fee.

overview: Sirius College offers VCE Awards to recognise students who display excellence in their VCE studies. The awards are available for students who receive a significantly high ATAR or achieve a perfect score in one of their studies.
description: VCE Awards are available for Year 11 and Year 12 students who display excellence by receiving a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies. These awards recognise exceptional achievement in VCE examinations and acknowledge student excellence across diverse subject areas including Physics, Chemistry, Business Management, English, Further Mathematics, Mathematical Methods, Computing, and other subjects. The specific financial value of the awards, number of recipients, and detailed selection criteria are not publicly disclosed on the school website and families should contact the school office directly for complete details including renewal conditions and special requirements.
eligibility_criteria: VCE students (Year 11 and Year 12); Achievement of significantly high ATAR score; OR achievement of perfect score (50) in one VCE study; Selection based on VCAA examination results.
special_conditions: Award based on VCE examination results. Recognition is automatic upon achievement of significantly high ATAR or perfect study score in one subject. No separate application required; students are identified through VCAA results.

overview: Sirius College offers Special Talents Scholarships to support students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes. The scholarship recognises achievement in four distinct areas: sports, creative/performing arts, music and other special abilities.
description: Sirius College supports students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes through Special Talents Scholarships. There are four categories of scholarships in the area of special talents including sports, creative/performing arts, music and other abilities. These scholarships aim to encourage and recognise students in their pursuit of excellence across co-curricular activities and special talents beyond academics. The specific financial value, number of awards per category, and detailed selection criteria are not publicly disclosed on the school website. Families should contact the school office or refer to the Scholarship and Award Policy for complete details about application processes, auditions, portfolios, or other assessment requirements for each talent category.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students; Open to students demonstrating excellence in one of four talent areas: sports, creative/performing arts, music, or other special abilities; Selection based on demonstrated talent and performance in chosen area; May require audition, portfolio, or competition results depending on talent category.
special_conditions: Selection criteria vary by talent category (sports, performing arts, music, or other abilities). Applicants may be required to provide evidence of achievement such as competition results, portfolios, auditions, or performance demonstrations. All applications within the Open Period are charged a $75 non-refundable application fee.

overview: Sirius College offers the Community Service Scholarship to recognise socially responsible individuals who make significant contributions to their community. The scholarship acknowledges students motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony.
description: Sirius College supports socially responsible individuals through the Community Service Scholarship. The scholarship recognises and acknowledges students who are making significant contributions to their community, demonstrating a commitment to social responsibility, moral values, and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. This scholarship is designed to encourage and reward students who actively participate in community service, volunteer work, or demonstrate leadership in fostering social responsibility. The specific financial value, number of awards, and detailed selection criteria are not publicly disclosed on the school website. Families should contact the school office or refer to the Scholarship and Award Policy for information about application procedures, evidence requirements for community service, and renewal conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students; Demonstrated significant contributions to their community; Evidence of community service or volunteer work; Exemplify moral values and belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony; Selection based on demonstrated commitment to social responsibility and community engagement.
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated community service and social responsibility. Applicants may be required to provide evidence of community contributions such as volunteer hours, letters of reference, or examples of community engagement. All applications within the Open Period are charged a $75 non-refundable application fee.

overview: Sirius College offers the Financial Difficulty Scholarship to support families experiencing financial hardship. The scholarship is available to families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College due to loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control.
description: Sirius College recognises that families can experience both short term and long term financial hardship as a result of the loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. The Financial Difficulty Scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance to families experiencing genuine difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College. This means-tested financial assistance program aims to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent students from accessing education at Sirius College. The specific financial value, assessment methodology, and detailed eligibility criteria are not publicly disclosed on the school website. Families should contact the school office or refer to the Scholarship and Award Policy for information about the application process, financial assessment procedures, and available support levels.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students; Family experiencing financial hardship; Loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control; Financial assessment required to determine eligibility; May include special provisions for refugee families (only eligible for subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866 visa holders).
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to financial assessment and review of circumstances. May require supporting documentation such as financial statements, income verification, or letters explaining circumstances of hardship. Special provisions available for refugee families with eligible visa subclasses. Applications must demonstrate genuine financial hardship beyond normal family circumstances.

overview: Sirius College offers sibling discounts to support families with multiple children attending the school. The discount provides reductions to tuition fees for the second, third, and fourth siblings, with the child in the highest grade treated as the first child.
description: In addition to scholarship programs, Sirius College offers the following sibling discounts on tuition fees to support families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The second sibling receives 20% off tuition fees, the third sibling receives 30% off tuition fees, and the fourth sibling receives 40% off tuition fees. This financial assistance program is ongoing for the duration of enrolment as long as siblings remain concurrently enrolled at the school. The child in the highest grade is always treated as the first child for discount calculation purposes, and the second highest grade is treated as the second child, continuing in the same manner. Sibling discounts may vary for each campus and families should refer to campus fee schedules for eligible discounts applicable to their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple children from same family enrolled at Sirius College; Must be full-time students attending Sirius concurrently; Siblings must be living predominantly at the same address; Child in highest grade treated as first child; Discounts apply to tuition fees only; Sibling discounts may vary by campus.
special_conditions: Automatic discount applied to second sibling (20% off tuition), third sibling (30% off tuition), and fourth sibling (40% off tuition). Child in highest grade always treated as first child. Discount applies for duration of concurrent enrolment. Varies by campus - refer to campus fee schedules for specific discounts. Not applicable to application fees, bond, building levy, or other non-tuition charges.

overview: Sirius College offers Academic Scholarships to reward high achieving students. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through their entrance exam results or academic results during their study at the College.
description: Academic Scholarships aim to reward high achieving students based on their academic results during their study at the College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results. The financial value and specific duration have not been publicly detailed and families are directed to contact the school office for comprehensive information. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence and entrance examination performance. These scholarships form part of the College's commitment to supporting student excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students applying for entry; Currently enrolled students demonstrating high academic results; Performance based on entrance exam results or College academic achievement
special_conditions: For further details including the value of scholarships and selection criteria, contact the school office or check the Scholarship and Award Policy. From 2027 onwards, applications will be assessed using the two most recent semester reports showing cumulative grade of A or A+.

overview: Sirius College offers VCE Awards to students who display excellence in their VCE studies. The awards recognise students who achieve a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies.
description: VCE Awards are available for students who display excellence by receiving a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their VCE studies. These awards recognise outstanding achievement at the highest level of Victorian secondary education. The specific monetary value and duration of these awards have not been publicly detailed. Students must contact the school office for complete information regarding award criteria, selection process, and financial benefits.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Significantly high ATAR achievement; Perfect score in one or more VCE subjects
special_conditions: Available only to students who achieve significantly high ATAR or perfect VCE study scores. Specific eligibility thresholds should be confirmed with the school office.

overview: Sirius College offers Sports Scholarships to support students with special talents in sports. The scholarships recognise and reward students who demonstrate elite sporting ability and commitment to their sport.
description: Sports Scholarships are part of Sirius College's support for students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes. The College offers scholarships in the area of special talents including sports, along with creative/performing arts and music. The specific financial value, selection criteria, application process, and number of scholarships available have not been publicly detailed in accessible sources. Families interested in sports scholarships should contact the school office directly for comprehensive information including audition requirements, financial benefits, and eligibility criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated elite sporting ability; Selection based on talent assessment; Commitment to a recognised sport
special_conditions: For further details including the value of scholarships and selection criteria, contact the school office or check the Scholarship and Award Policy.

overview: Sirius College offers Music Scholarships to support students with special talents in music. The scholarships recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to their musical pursuits.
description: Music Scholarships are part of Sirius College's recognition of students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes. The College offers scholarships in the area of special talents including music performance and instrumental study. The specific financial value, duration, audition requirements, and number of scholarships awarded annually have not been publicly detailed. Students interested in music scholarships should contact the school office to obtain complete information about selection criteria, audition procedures, and financial benefits available.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability; Performance-based selection; Commitment to musical study
special_conditions: For further details including the value of scholarships and selection criteria, contact the school office or check the Scholarship and Award Policy. Selection based on music auditions and demonstrated talent.

overview: Sirius College offers Creative and Performing Arts Scholarships to support students with special talents in the arts. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional ability in drama, dance, and other performing arts disciplines.
description: Creative and Performing Arts Scholarships are available to students who demonstrate special talents in performing arts, including drama, dance, and theatrical performance. The College recognises students with a wide range of skills and interests, supporting those pursuing excellence in the creative and performing arts. Specific information about the financial value, duration of scholarships, number awarded annually, and detailed selection procedures is not publicly available in accessible sources. Interested students and families should contact the school office directly for comprehensive details about audition requirements, eligibility criteria, and financial benefits.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated creative or performing arts ability; Performance-based or portfolio assessment; Commitment to artistic pursuits
special_conditions: For further details including the value of scholarships and selection criteria, contact the school office or check the Scholarship and Award Policy.

overview: Sirius College offers Community Service Scholarships to recognise students who are making significant contributions to their community. The scholarships reward socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values, tolerance, diversity and harmony.
description: The Community Service Scholarship is awarded to students who are making significant contributions to their community and demonstrate commitment to social responsibility and ethical values. Sirius College supports socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. The specific financial value, number of scholarships awarded annually, and detailed application procedures have not been publicly disclosed. Students should contact the school office for complete information about eligibility criteria, the selection process, and the financial benefits of this scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Significant community service contributions; Demonstrated social responsibility; Commitment to moral values, tolerance, diversity and harmony
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated community service and values-based contributions. For further details including the value of scholarships and selection criteria, contact the school office or check the Scholarship and Award Policy.

overview: Sirius College offers Financial Difficulty Scholarships to support families experiencing financial hardship. The scholarships recognise that families can experience both short term and long term financial difficulties and aim to help them meet the College's fee requirements.
description: Sirius College recognises that families can experience both short term and long term financial hardship as a result of loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. Families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College can apply for financial difficulty scholarships. The specific financial benefit amount and selection criteria are not publicly detailed. Applicants must submit required supporting documentation through the online application form. The College does not accept hard copies or documents sent via email. For 2026 applications, families should review the required supporting documents on the College website before applying.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship or difficulty meeting fee requirements; Circumstances beyond family control such as illness or loss of income; Complete submission of all required supporting documents through online form
special_conditions: Requires submission of supporting documentation through online application form only. Hard copies and email submissions are not accepted. Missing or separately submitted documents will result in applications not being considered. Applicants must carefully review and provide all required supporting documents as listed on the school website.

overview: Sirius College offers Financial Difficulty Scholarships specifically for refugee families. This scholarship is available to families who hold eligible refugee or humanitarian visa subclasses and are experiencing financial hardship.
description: Sirius College recognises the particular circumstances of refugee and humanitarian visa holders and offers targeted financial difficulty scholarships for eligible families. This scholarship is specifically available only to holders of subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866 visas who are experiencing difficulty meeting the College's fee requirements. The specific financial value and detailed selection criteria have not been publicly specified. Eligible families should contact the school office directly for complete information about application procedures, required documentation, and the level of financial assistance available.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold Australian visa subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 or 866; Experiencing financial hardship or difficulty meeting fee requirements; Complete submission of required supporting documents
special_conditions: Only available to holders of specific Australian visa subclasses: 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866. Requires submission of supporting documentation through online application form only.

overview: Sirius College offers Academic Scholarships to reward high-achieving students. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through their results during enrolment at the College and through entrance exam performance for prospective students.
description: The Academic Scholarship recognises high achievement in academic performance based on students' academic results during their study at Sirius College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results. In addition to academic scholarships, VCE awards are available for students who display excellence by receiving a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies. Selection is based on academic merit and entrance exam performance. The exact value of the scholarship and specific renewal conditions are detailed in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy, which should be reviewed for complete information on financial benefits and duration.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary year levels (Year 7 to Year 12 only); Based on academic results or entrance exam results; Prospective and current students eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 remain open until the end of Term 3 holiday. Scholarships are awarded based on academic excellence criteria. From 2027 onwards, applications will be assessed using the two most recent semester reports available at the time of application, with both reports required to show a cumulative grade of A or A+, recognising consistent achievement.

program_name: Special Talents Scholarships - Sports, Music, Creative and Performing Arts
overview: Sirius College offers Special Talents Scholarships across four categories to support students with exceptional skills and abilities. The College recognises students who demonstrate excellence in sports, music, creative and performing arts, and other special abilities.
description: Sirius College supports students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes through Special Talents Scholarships. There are four categories of scholarships in the area of special talents: sports, creative and performing arts, music, and other abilities. These scholarships aim to recognise and reward students who display excellence in their chosen area beyond the classroom. Selection is based on demonstrated talent and achievement in the specific field. The College recognises that exceptional ability extends across multiple disciplines and commits to supporting talented students to develop their potential. Specific financial values and selection criteria are detailed in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated skills and achievements in sports; Students with demonstrated excellence in creative or performing arts; Students with demonstrated excellence in music; Students with other special abilities or talents
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 remain open until the end of Term 3 holiday. Late applications will not be accepted. Selection is based on demonstrated talent in the relevant field.

overview: Sirius College offers the Community Service Scholarship to recognise and support students who make significant contributions to their community. The scholarship acknowledges students who demonstrate values of social responsibility, tolerance, diversity and harmony.
description: Sirius College supports socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. Students who are making significant contributions to their community are acknowledged and recognised through the Community Service Scholarship. The College values students who demonstrate commitment to serving others and contributing positively to their school and wider community. Recipients are selected based on their demonstrated community involvement and service. The financial value and specific selection criteria are available in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy, which provides comprehensive details on this award.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated significant contributions to community service; Evidence of social responsibility; Commitment to values of tolerance, diversity and harmony
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 remain open until the end of Term 3 holiday. Late applications will not be accepted. Selection is based on demonstrated community service and commitment to social responsibility.

overview: Sirius College offers the Financial Difficulty Scholarship to support families experiencing short or long-term financial hardship. The scholarship recognises that families may face unexpected financial challenges due to circumstances beyond their control.
description: Sirius College recognises that families can experience both short-term and long-term financial hardship as a result of loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. To this extent, families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College can apply for the Financial Difficulty Scholarship. This bursary program is designed to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent capable students from accessing education at Sirius College. Applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis, with consideration given to individual family circumstances. The financial value of support provided and full eligibility requirements are detailed in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Income loss due to illness or circumstances beyond control; Difficulty meeting school fee requirements
special_conditions: Applications assessed on a case-by-case basis considering individual family circumstances. A separate Financial Difficulty Scholarship (Refugee) exists exclusively for eligible visa holders (subclasses 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866).

overview: Sirius College offers a specialised Financial Difficulty Scholarship for refugee families. This program is specifically designed to support refugee students whose families hold eligible Australian visa subclasses.
description: Sirius College offers a dedicated Financial Difficulty Scholarship for refugee families, recognising the unique circumstances and support needs of students holding eligible Australian visa subclasses. This program demonstrates the College's commitment to supporting disadvantaged student populations and ensuring equitable access to education. Eligibility is strictly limited to holders of specified visa subclasses, ensuring support reaches those most in need. The financial value and specific application procedures are available through the College's Scholarship and Award Policy and enrolment information resources.
eligibility_criteria: Only eligible for subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866 visa holders; Refugee or humanitarian visa status required
special_conditions: Exclusively for eligible refugee visa holders. Visa subclasses: 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866 only. Separate from the general Financial Difficulty Scholarship.

overview: Sirius College recognises outstanding VCE achievement through VCE Awards. These awards acknowledge students who display excellence in their VCE studies by achieving significantly high ATAR results or perfect study scores in individual subjects.
description: In addition to academic scholarships, VCE awards are available for students who display excellence in their VCE studies. These awards recognise students who receive significantly high ATAR results or achieve a perfect score in one of their studies. The VCE Awards acknowledge the exceptional achievement of senior students and celebrate their dedication to academic excellence. Recipients are selected based on their performance in the Victorian Certificate of Education, with particular recognition given to those achieving outstanding results. The specific financial value and selection criteria are outlined in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy.
eligibility_criteria: VCE students only; Significantly high ATAR result OR perfect score in one subject; Demonstrated excellence in Victorian Certificate of Education
special_conditions: Automatic recognition of exceptional VCE achievement. Awards recognise significant ATAR results and perfect subject scores. Selection is based on assessed performance in VCE studies.

overview: Sirius College offers Academic Scholarships to reward high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. These scholarships are awarded to both current students based on their academic results during their studies at the College, and to prospective students according to their entrance exam results.
description: Academic Scholarships are designed to recognise and reward students pursuing excellence in their studies at Sirius College. Awards are based on students' academic results during their time at the College as well as entrance exam results for prospective students entering the College. Secondary scholarships are available for Year 7 through Year 12 students. In primary school, scholarships are awarded based on a scholarship examination administered at the end of Year 6. The financial value of scholarships and specific selection criteria are available from the school office or in the Scholarship and Award Policy document. Selection involves completion of entrance examination and subsequent interview assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students in Year 7-Year 12; Based on academic results during study at the College or entrance exam results for prospective students; Must meet entrance examination threshold; Interview required for shortlisted applicants
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their enrolment. From 2027 onwards, applications will be assessed using the two most recent semester reports available at the time of application, with both reports required to show a cumulative grade of A or A+.

overview: Sirius College recognises outstanding VCE achievement through VCE Awards. These awards are available for students who display excellence by achieving a significantly high ATAR score or a perfect score in one of their VCE studies.
description: VCE Awards acknowledge exceptional achievement at the Victorian Certificate of Education level. Students who demonstrate excellence through significantly high ATAR (Australian Tertiary Admission Rank) scores or achieve a perfect score in any of their VCE studies are recognised through this award program. These awards celebrate the culmination of secondary education and recognize those who have achieved at the highest level. The specific financial benefits and selection criteria are detailed in the College's Scholarship and Award Policy. The program acknowledges both individual subject excellence and overall tertiary admission achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; Achieved significantly high ATAR or perfect score in VCE study; Must be completing VCE at Sirius College
special_conditions: Awards based on VCE examination results and ATAR performance as released by the Victorian Curriculum and Assessment Authority (VCAA). Recognition is automatic upon achievement of qualifying criteria.

overview: Sirius College recognises students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes through Special Talents Scholarships. These scholarships reward students who demonstrate excellence in sports, creative and performing arts, music, and other special abilities.
description: Sirius College offers Special Talents Scholarships across four distinct categories to recognise diverse student excellence. The College supports students demonstrating exceptional abilities in sports, creative and performing arts (including drama, dance, and visual arts), music (instrumental, vocal, and ensemble performance), and other special talents. These scholarships acknowledge that achievement extends beyond academics and celebrate students who develop their talents to a high level. Selection is based on auditions, portfolios, or demonstrations of ability as appropriate to the particular discipline. From the 2026 policy changes, scholarships granted to Year 9 students no longer continue automatically until graduation but are subject to annual review. Financial values and detailed selection criteria are available in the Scholarship and Award Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in all year levels; Demonstrated excellence in sports, performing arts, music, or other special abilities; Must participate in trial audition, portfolio submission, or ability demonstration as relevant to the talent category; Australian-resident students eligible
special_conditions: Selection based on auditions, trials, or portfolio demonstrations depending on the talent discipline. Previously awarded scholarships to Year 9 students continued through graduation with annual review; from 2026 onwards all Special Talents Scholarships are subject to annual review. Current achievements must be maintained for scholarship continuation.

overview: Sirius College acknowledges students demonstrating exceptional community service and social responsibility through Community Service Scholarships. These scholarships recognise students motivated by moral values and a commitment to tolerance, diversity, and harmony.
description: The Community Service Scholarship program recognises students who are making significant contributions to their community and society. Sirius College supports socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. Students applying for this scholarship must demonstrate evidence of sustained community service, volunteer work, social leadership, or contributions that reflect commitment to social responsibility. Selection criteria focus on the nature, scope, and impact of community contributions, and the student's motivation for social service. The specific financial value and detailed assessment criteria are available from the school office or the Scholarship and Award Policy document. Applications require documentation of community service activities and references supporting the applicant's commitment to social responsibility.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated significant contributions to community; Evidence of sustained volunteer work or social service; Motivated by moral values and commitment to diversity and tolerance; Strong character references required
special_conditions: Selection based on documentation of community service activities, character references, and demonstrated commitment to social responsibility. Applicants must provide evidence of sustained involvement in community-based activities beyond school.

overview: Sirius College offers Financial Difficulty Scholarships to support families experiencing genuine financial hardship. These scholarships help eligible families meet the fee requirements of the College and reduce the burden during periods of economic difficulty.
description: Recognising that families can experience both short-term and long-term financial hardship due to loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control, Sirius College provides Financial Difficulty Scholarships. Families experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College can apply for this means-tested assistance program. Applications require comprehensive financial documentation and evidence of hardship. All supporting documents must be uploaded through the online application form; the school does not accept hard copies or documents sent via email. Applications with missing documentation will not be considered. The program is designed to help qualifying families access the College's education while managing temporary or ongoing financial challenges. Financial values are determined on a case-by-case basis based on demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Loss of income due to illness or unemployment; Difficulty meeting tuition fee requirements; Must provide comprehensive financial documentation; All required supporting documents must be submitted online; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Assessment based on financial hardship documentation. Applicants must carefully review the list of required supporting documents. All supporting documents must be uploaded through the online application form. Hard copies or documents sent via email will not be accepted. Applications with missing or separately submitted documents will not be considered. Special visa eligibility exists for refugee visa holders (subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866).

overview: Siena College offers several General Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate general excellence across their studies, involvement in community life and cocurricular activities, leadership and character. These scholarships recognise achievement, enable success and assume a commitment to the values of Siena College.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is valued at between 25% to 50% of tuition fees for the duration of enrolment. All students applying for a General Excellence Scholarship must sit the scholarship test, which is run by EduTest. The selection process includes the written EduTest examination in February, submission of a scholarship portfolio with supporting documentation, and an interview with the College Principal and a member of the Leadership Team for shortlisted candidates. The scholarship is awarded based on demonstrated general excellence across academic studies, community involvement, cocurricular activities, leadership and character, with renewal subject to continued satisfactory performance.
eligibility_criteria: Current or new Year 7 students; Current or new Year 9 students; Must demonstrate general excellence across studies and community involvement; Commitment to Siena College values
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend an interview with the College Principal and a member of the Leadership Team. Scholarship Portfolio must be submitted at least one week prior to Academic Testing Day and must include: a letter from the student to the Principal outlining why they would like to attend Siena College; copy of birth certificate (and passport/citizenship certificate if born overseas); copies of two most recent school reports; copy of most recent NAPLAN summary if available; supporting certificates, awards, photos of trophies or examples of outstanding work.

overview: Siena College is committed to supporting families in need and offers a range of Equity Scholarships to assist with tuition fees. These scholarships are offered to students at all year levels and provide strong educational opportunities for girls from socially and economically diverse backgrounds.
description: Equity Scholarships contribute 25% to 50% of tuition fees and are awarded for the duration of their time at school if needed, and subject to funding availability. The Equity Scholarship program is designed to provide strong educational opportunities for girls from socially and economically diverse backgrounds, removing financial barriers to a Siena education. Scholarships are need-based and assessed on individual family circumstances. The program aims to ensure that the benefits reach far beyond each recipient's Siena education, empowering young women from disadvantaged backgrounds to achieve their potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at all year levels; Available at all year levels (Year 7 to Year 12); Families must demonstrate financial need; Assessed on individual family circumstances
special_conditions: Awarded for the duration of enrolment if needed and subject to funding availability. Annual review of circumstances may be required. Families in need should contact the College directly for application details and assessment process.

overview: Siena College offers an Indigenous Scholarship as part of its Equity Scholarship Program to support Indigenous students seeking a quality education. This scholarship is designed for students who identify as Indigenous Australians and provides financial assistance to access a Siena College education.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship is offered as a category within the Equity Scholarship Program and contributes 25% to 50% of tuition fees. Indigenous students are eligible to apply for this scholarship at all year levels and are awarded support for the duration of their time at school if needed, subject to funding availability. The scholarship recognises the value of supporting Indigenous girls from diverse backgrounds and empowering them to achieve their educational and life goals. Applications should be directed to the College and assessed based on demonstrated need and Indigenous status.
eligibility_criteria: Must identify as Indigenous Australian; Open to students seeking entry at any year level; Demonstrated Indigenous connection required; Families must demonstrate financial need
special_conditions: Offered as part of the Equity Scholarship Program. Awarded for the duration of enrolment if needed and subject to funding availability. Applicants must demonstrate Indigenous status. Contact the College directly for application details.

overview: Siena College offers a Refugee Scholarship as part of its Equity Scholarship Program to support refugee students from displaced backgrounds seeking educational opportunities. This scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance and access to quality education for refugee girls.
description: The Refugee Scholarship is offered as a category within the Equity Scholarship Program and contributes 25% to 50% of tuition fees. Refugee students are eligible to apply at all year levels and are awarded support for the duration of their time at school if needed, subject to funding availability. The scholarship recognises Siena College's commitment to supporting families in need, particularly those facing hardship due to refugee status or displacement. Applications should be directed to the College and assessed based on demonstrated need and refugee status.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold refugee status or be from a refugee background; Open to students seeking entry at any year level; Demonstrated refugee status or humanitarian circumstances required; Families must demonstrate financial need
special_conditions: Offered as part of the Equity Scholarship Program. Awarded for the duration of enrolment if needed and subject to funding availability. Applicants must demonstrate refugee status or humanitarian circumstances. Contact the College directly for application details and verification requirements.

overview: Siena College offers an Alumnae Scholarship as part of its Equity Scholarship Program to support daughters and granddaughters of Siena College alumnae. This scholarship recognises and supports the next generation of the Siena community.
description: The Alumnae Scholarship is offered as a category within the Equity Scholarship Program and contributes 25% to 50% of tuition fees for eligible applicants. This scholarship is designed for daughters and granddaughters of Siena College alumnae and is awarded at all year levels for the duration of enrolment if needed, subject to funding availability. The scholarship celebrates the legacy of Siena alumnae and supports the continuation of the College's educational mission across generations by providing financial assistance to qualifying family members seeking admission to Siena College.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a daughter or granddaughter of a Siena College alumna; Open to students seeking entry at any year level; Family connection to Siena College as alumna required; May include demonstration of financial need
special_conditions: Offered as part of the Equity Scholarship Program. Awarded for the duration of enrolment if needed and subject to funding availability. Applicants must provide evidence of family connection to a Siena College alumna. Contact the College directly for application details and family verification requirements.

overview: Sophia Mundi Steiner School offers scholarships to recognise and celebrate the capabilities of talented students. These scholarships seek to widen access to the exceptional International Baccalaureate Diploma Program and foster diversity within the school community.
description: Sophia Mundi's general scholarships recognise academic excellence and aspiration while widening access to the school's distinctive Steiner education integrated with the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program. Scholarships can be awarded for 1 to 2 years depending on the year of entry and range from 50% to 100% of tuition fees. Selection is based on an aptitude test, interview, school reports indicating academic and behavioural standards, and demonstrated commitment to the school's values. Scholarship applications are assessed based on merit, with limited spaces available each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or Year 11; Current or new students; Demonstrated excellence in academics; Strong behavioural standards
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships can be awarded for 1 to 2 years depending on the year of entry. The scholarship test will be conducted in Term 4, 2025 at Sophia Mundi. Limited scholarship spaces available. Scholarship applications for 2025 have now closed; 2026 enquiries are now open.

overview: Sophia Mundi offers Music Scholarships to current and new students entering Year 10 or 11 who demonstrate excellence in music knowledge and musical performance. These scholarships recognise and support talented musicians integrating performance excellence with the school's holistic educational philosophy.
description: The Music Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating excellence in both musical performance and music theory. Applicants must have achieved a minimum AMEB Level Grade 6 for instrumental/vocal performance and Grade 4 music theory at the time of application. Scholarships are valued at 100% of tuition fees. Selection is based on musical excellence combined with student merit as demonstrated through an aptitude test, audition, interview, community and cultural involvement, and academic and behavioural standards from school reports. Shortlisted students are invited to audition at Sophia Mundi where they will perform two contrasting pieces, undertake sight reading and aural tests, and may be asked to demonstrate technical work.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and new students entering Year 10 or Year 11; Minimum AMEB Grade 6 instrumental/vocal performance at application; Minimum AMEB Grade 4 music theory at application; Excellence in musical performance and knowledge demonstrated
special_conditions: Students applying for this scholarship must complete the Scholarship Application Form, pay the registration fee of $275, and sit the scholarship aptitude test. Shortlisted students are invited to audition at Sophia Mundi. The number of scholarship spaces is limited. Audition includes two contrasting pieces, sight reading tests, aural tests, and technical work demonstration.

overview: Sorrento Primary School Year 6 students may receive the Sorrento Rotary Club Scholarship, awarded annually to assist with secondary school education costs. The scholarship recognises promising students selected through an interview process conducted by Rotary Club members.
description: The Sorrento Rotary Club awards the Sorrento Scholarship each December to one Year 6 student from Sorrento Primary School. The award is decided through an interview process conducted by members of the Rotary Club. The scholarship is designed to assist secondary school education costs for the recipient. In addition to the main scholarship, Rotary provides three other monetary awards for Year 6 students to assist with uniform or books for secondary school. The scholarship money is paid directly to the student's account at the school to assist with educational expenses. Selection is based on demonstrated promise and student interview performance.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 student at Sorrento Primary School; Selection through interview process
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded each December. Money is paid directly to the student's school account. Awards are intended for secondary school expenses including tuition, uniforms, and books.

program_name: Rotary Club Year 6 Uniform and Books Awards
overview: Sorrento Primary School Year 6 students are eligible for three additional monetary awards provided by the Sorrento Rotary Club. These awards assist with the cost of uniforms or books for secondary school education.
description: The Sorrento Rotary Club provides three additional monetary awards specifically for Year 6 students at Sorrento Primary School to assist with secondary school expenses. These awards contribute toward the purchase of uniforms or books required for secondary school. The awards are administered in conjunction with the main Sorrento Scholarship and recognise the transition needs of students moving to secondary education. The monetary amounts are paid directly to the student's school account to support educational expenses for the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 student at Sorrento Primary School; Selection through community interview process
special_conditions: Awards are provided to assist with uniform or books for secondary school transition. Money is paid directly to the student's school account. These are community-based awards administered by Sorrento Rotary Club.

overview: Sirius College offers Academic Scholarships to reward high achieving secondary students. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement based on their entrance examination results and academic performance during their studies at the College.
description: Academic Scholarships aim to reward high achieving students through financial support to further their education. They are based on students' academic results during their study at the College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results. The selection process involves entrance examination and academic assessment. These scholarships recognise students who achieve consistently high academic results and are available for secondary students transitioning into the school system.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary year levels only; Year 7 to Year 12; Must perform well on entrance exam results; Prospective students must meet specified academic threshold; Current students must maintain academic performance standards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: For scholarships commencing in 2026, an overall A or A+ grade is required at the end of Term 3 in the application year. From 2027 onwards, applications will be assessed using the two most recent semester reports available at the time of application, with both reports showing a cumulative grade of A or A+. Annual review required for continuation.

overview: Sirius College recognises VCE students who display excellence in their final years of secondary education. VCE Awards are available to students who achieve a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies.
description: VCE Awards are available for students who display excellence by receiving a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies. These awards recognise outstanding achievement in the final years of secondary schooling. The selection process involves evaluation of VCE results and ATAR scores. These awards acknowledge students who achieve exceptional academic results at the tertiary entrance level.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; Must achieve significantly high ATAR score or perfect score in one subject; Demonstrated VCE excellence
special_conditions: Awarded based on VCE results and achievement of high ATAR or perfect subject score. Recognition is automatic upon achievement of specified criteria.
