overview: Sirius College supports students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes through Special Talents Scholarships. These scholarships recognise excellence in four categories: sports, creative/performing arts, music and other abilities.
description: Special Talents Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating excellence in four categories of special talents: sports, creative/performing arts, music and other abilities. The selection process involves auditions, portfolios, or practical demonstrations depending on the category of talent. Students must demonstrate exceptional ability in their chosen area of special talent. From 2026, students must reapply each year for assessment rather than receiving automatic continuation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students in year levels as specified; Must demonstrate exceptional ability in sports, creative/performing arts, music, or other abilities; Prospective and current students eligible; Annual reapplication required from 2026 onwards
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Category areas include: sports, creative/performing arts (drama, dance, musical theatre), music, and other abilities. From 2026, students must reapply each year for assessment. Previous policy allowed continuation until graduation with annual review, but this has changed.

overview: Sirius College recognises and rewards socially responsible students through the Community Service Scholarship. This scholarship acknowledges students who are making significant contributions to their community.
description: The Community Service Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate significant contributions to their community and are motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. The selection process involves assessment of community service activities and contributions. Students must demonstrate a genuine commitment to social responsibility and community engagement. This scholarship recognises the importance of developing students who are active and caring members of society.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students; Must demonstrate significant contributions to community; Motivated by moral values and belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony; Prospective or current students eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applicants must evidence significant community service contributions. Selection based on demonstrated moral values and commitment to tolerance, diversity and harmony.

overview: Sirius College recognises that families may experience financial hardship due to loss of income from illness or other circumstances beyond their control. The Financial Difficulty Scholarship provides support to families experiencing difficulty in meeting school fee requirements.
description: The Financial Difficulty Scholarship is designed to support families experiencing both short term and long term financial hardship. Sirius College recognises that circumstances beyond a family's control, such as loss of income through illness or other hardships, may create challenges in meeting fee requirements. The selection process involves financial assessment and evaluation of circumstances. Families must demonstrate genuine financial need. Scholarship value varies based on individual circumstances and financial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students of all year levels; Family experiencing financial hardship; Loss of income through illness or circumstances beyond control; Need-based assessment required; Must be able to meet College's Terms and Conditions of Enrolment
special_conditions: Available to families experiencing short or long-term financial hardship due to loss of income or illness. Scholarship value is determined by individual financial assessment. Families must contact the school office to discuss eligibility and application process.

overview: Sirius College offers targeted financial support for refugee families through the Financial Difficulty Scholarship (Refugee). This scholarship is specifically designed for families holding eligible refugee or humanitarian visa subclasses.
description: The Financial Difficulty Scholarship (Refugee) provides financial assistance to families who hold eligible Australian visa subclasses. This scholarship specifically supports refugee and humanitarian visa holders in accessing quality education at Sirius College. The selection process involves verification of visa status and financial assessment. Eligible visa subclasses include 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866. The scholarship value varies based on individual circumstances and assessed financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold Australian visa subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 or 866; Family experiencing financial difficulty; Open to all year levels; Must provide visa documentation and proof of status
special_conditions: Only eligible for holders of specific visa subclasses: 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866. Visa status verification required. Scholarship value determined by financial assessment. Families must contact school office to apply.

overview: Sirius College awards scholarships to prospective Year 7 students based on performance in the school's scholarship examination conducted at the end of Year 6. These awards recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential.
description: The Year 7 Scholarship Examination Award is based on performance on the scholarship examination administered at the end of Year 6. This examination assesses academic ability and potential in core subjects. The scholarship examination is the primary selection method for Year 7 entry scholarships. Students who perform well on this examination are eligible for academic recognition and financial support. These remain unchanged and continue to be awarded based on the scholarship examination at the end of Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must sit scholarship examination at end of Year 6; Performance-based selection; Prospective entrants to Year 7; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarship examination conducted at end of Year 6 for Year 7 entry. This selection method remains unchanged. Students must attend the examination to be considered for scholarships.

overview: Sirius College offers tuition fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The sibling discount structure provides graduated reductions for the second, third and fourth siblings.
description: Sirius College supports families with multiple children by offering tuition fee discounts on a tiered basis. The discount structure provides 20% off tuition fees for the second sibling, 30% off for the third sibling, and 40% off for the fourth sibling. The child in the highest grade is always treated as the first child for discount purposes. Sibling discounts are applied to tuition fees for full-time students attending the school concurrently where siblings or step-siblings live predominantly at the same address.
eligibility_criteria: Siblings or step-siblings living predominantly at the same address; Full-time students attending Sirius College concurrently; Child in highest grade treated as first child; Applies to tuition fees only
special_conditions: Tiered structure: Second sibling 20% off tuition fees; Third sibling 30% off tuition fees; Fourth sibling 40% off tuition fees. Child in highest grade always treated as first child. Discounts vary by campus and must be verified with campus fee schedules.

overview: Southern Cross Grammar offers the Academic Scholarship to academically gifted students who are ambitious in their learning and leadership. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability and nurtures students' potential and drive for success through their secondary schooling and beyond.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic excellence and leadership potential. Recipients are required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination as part of the selection process. Following examination and review of results, shortlisted candidates are invited for an interview with the Principal. Successful candidates receive a scholarship offer, with the value determined at the Principal's discretion. The scholarship is offered in the year prior to entry and is available for the duration of enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Must be a Citizen or Permanent Resident of Australia at the time of application; Prospective families must submit an Application for Enrolment prior to applying; Must sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination
special_conditions: Candidates required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination on Saturday 7 February 2026. Prospective families pay $206 total ($100 enrolment application fee plus $106 Edutest fee). Current families pay $106 Edutest registration fee only. Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal. Number of scholarships offered is at the Principal's discretion. Shortlisted students invited for interview with Principal.

overview: Southern Cross Grammar awards the General Excellence Scholarship to students who demonstrate excellence in their academic pursuits and leadership in school life and beyond in the wider community. The scholarship nurtures future leaders' potential, work ethic, and drive to achieve great things.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who combine strong academic performance with demonstrated leadership and community engagement. Candidates are required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination and submit a written application demonstrating their academic record and co-curricular involvement. Shortlisted applicants are invited for an interview with the Principal. The scholarship is offered in the year prior to entry and is available for the duration of enrolment. The value of the scholarship is determined at the Principal's discretion based on individual merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Must demonstrate excellence in academic pursuits; Must show leadership in school life and community; Must be a Citizen or Permanent Resident of Australia; Prospective families must submit an Application for Enrolment prior to applying; Must sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination
special_conditions: Candidates required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination on Saturday 7 February 2026. Supporting documents required including academic record and co-curricular information. Prospective families pay $206 total ($100 enrolment application fee plus $106 Edutest fee). Current families pay $106 Edutest registration fee only. Shortlisted students invited for interview with Principal.

overview: Southern Cross Grammar encourages students' passion for and excellence in music through the Music Scholarship. The scholarship is awarded to students with exceptional musical ability to nurture their talent and support high-performance music development.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises students with demonstrated exceptional musical ability and commitment to musical excellence. Unlike other scholarships, Music Scholarship candidates are not required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination. Instead, selection is based on audition performance and evidence of musical achievement. Auditions are held on campus before the Selection Panel in the week commencing Monday 23 February 2026. Candidates must demonstrate a minimum standard equivalent to AMEB Grade 4 or equivalent in their chosen instrument. The scholarship is offered in the year prior to entry and supports recipients for the duration of their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Must demonstrate exceptional musical ability; Must provide evidence of minimum AMEB Grade 4 (or equivalent such as ANZCA or Trinity of London) in chosen instrument; Must be a Citizen or Permanent Resident of Australia; Prospective families must submit an Application for Enrolment prior to applying
special_conditions: Music Scholarship auditions held on campus before Selection Panel in the week commencing Monday 23 February 2026. Students not required to sit Edutest examination. Families will be contacted beforehand with audition date and time. Minimum AMEB Grade 4 standard required in chosen instrument. Audition performance is the basis of selection. Prospective families pay $100 enrolment application fee only. Shortlisted students invited for interview with Principal.

overview: Southern Cross Grammar offers Indigenous Student Scholarships to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to attend the school at a significant discount. The scholarship demonstrates the school's commitment to improving education outcomes for Indigenous students at secondary schooling level.
description: The Indigenous Student Scholarship provides Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students with a significant fee discount to enable access to Southern Cross Grammar's educational programs. Candidates are required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination as part of the selection process. The scholarship is part of the school's commitment to help improve education outcomes for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students pursuing secondary education. Recipients receive support for the duration of their enrolment. Following examination and shortlisting, candidates are invited for an interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Must be a Citizen or Permanent Resident of Australia at the time of application; Prospective families must submit an Application for Enrolment prior to applying; Must sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination
special_conditions: Candidates required to sit the Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination on Saturday 7 February 2026. Scholarships provide significant discount (specific percentage not disclosed on public page). Prospective families pay $206 total ($100 enrolment application fee plus $106 Edutest fee). Current families pay $106 Edutest registration fee only. Shortlisted students invited for interview with Principal. Number of scholarships awarded at Principal's discretion.

school_name: Spensley Street Primary School-Glen Forbes Camp
Springvale Rise Primary School-Springvale Road Campus
St Albans East Primary School-St Albans East Deaf Facility

program_name: The Smith Family Learning for Life
overview: St Albans East Primary School partners with The Smith Family to deliver the Learning for Life program, which provides financial assistance to low-income families. The program helps vulnerable students remain engaged in their education and achieve their potential through support for school essentials and educational initiatives.
description: The Smith Family's Learning for Life program provides financial assistance to students from low-income families, helping with the cost of school essentials including uniforms, books, excursions, and other educational materials. The program supports students from all year levels at St Albans East Primary School and includes initiatives aimed at improving learning, wellbeing, achievement, and post-school engagement. The program also provides educational support to help students keep up with their peers. Funding is non-monetary in nature, providing direct support with school costs rather than cash payments. Students must meet financial eligibility criteria and attend school regularly (80% and above attendance required). The school works with The Smith Family Coordinator to identify eligible students.
eligibility_criteria: Students from low-income families; Parents holding a Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; School attendance requirement of 80% and above; Open to students in all year levels (Prep-Year 6)
special_conditions: School attendance of 80% minimum is required. Eligible families must hold a current Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card. The Smith Family provides support including assistance with school costs, support to improve attendance, and educational programs. Students are identified through The Smith Family Coordinator at the school.

school_name: St Angela of the Cross Primary School

overview: St Andrew's School Werribee offers concessional school fees to eligible families experiencing financial hardship. This program is administered through the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy and recognises that Catholic education should be accessible to all Catholic families regardless of financial capacity.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy provides fee remission or exemption for families experiencing financial hardship. Eligible families receive a fixed annual concessional fee set by MACS that covers all standard school fees and levies. The concessional fee amount is determined annually and covers tuition fees and the individual student levy. Approved applicants must agree to either direct debit or Centrepay payment arrangements. If a family becomes eligible during the school year, pro-rata concessions are provided. Families are required to notify the school of any changes to financial circumstances that affect their eligibility, and applications must be resubmitted annually.
eligibility_criteria: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Families holding a valid Department of Human Services Health Care Card and eligible for CSEF; Families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card; Families experiencing severe financial hardship; Families identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard; Home school students attending part-time; Families must agree to direct debit or Centrepay payment arrangements; Application must be submitted on school-approved form available from school office
special_conditions: Concessional fee covers tuition fees and levies only; Camp levies may be charged separately; Annual statutory declaration may be required to confirm eligibility; Fee concession is subject to maintenance of payment arrangements; Applications assessed by Principal according to demonstrated financial need; Dignity and privacy of applicants is respected in accordance with school's Privacy Policy. Successful applicants will be invoiced for full fees and levies with a fee remission shown on the invoice so the total reflects the concessional school fee amount.

overview: St Albans Secondary College awards the Academic Excellence Scholarship to Year 6 students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and aptitude. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic potential and provides recipients with opportunities to experience engaging curriculum and learning programs.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate significant academic achievement. Recipients are recognised for their considerable achievements and provided with opportunities to experience exciting curriculum opportunities through a broad range of engaging programs delivered by the school's dedicated teachers. Applicants are required to sit the Edutest Entrance Exam as part of the selection process. The financial value of this scholarship is not specified on the school's website, though it recognises and supports the student's academic journey through Year 7 enrolment. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis with continuation subject to ongoing academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrate significant academic achievement and aptitude; Must register and sit the Edutest Entrance Exam; Open to all students seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Applicants must register and sit the Edutest Entrance Exam. Recipients of the Academic Excellence Scholarship will be placed in the SEAL Program. Not all students accepted to the SEAL Program will receive an Academic Excellence Scholarship. Academic excellence scholarships are granted at the discretion of the school.

overview: St Albans Secondary College awards the Sporting Excellence Scholarship to Year 6 students who demonstrate significant sporting achievement and aptitude. The scholarship recognises outstanding athletic ability and provides recipients with opportunities to pursue their sporting interests within the school community.
description: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability, achievement and commitment to their chosen sport or sports. Recipients are recognised for their considerable achievements and provided with opportunities to experience engaging programs and support through the school's dedicated sporting facilities and coaching. Applicants are asked to attend an interview at the College accompanied by their parents or guardians. The financial value of this scholarship is not specified on the school's website. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis with continuation subject to ongoing sporting performance and school conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrate significant sporting achievement and aptitude; Willingness to participate in school sporting programs; Must attend an interview at the College with parent/s or guardian/s; Open to all students seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Applicants must attend an interview at the College, accompanied by their parent/s or guardian/s. Scholarship recipients are expected to contribute actively to the school's sporting programs and maintain appropriate conduct and attendance standards.

overview: St Albans Secondary College awards the Leadership Scholarship to Year 6 students who demonstrate significant leadership qualities and potential. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional ability to lead, inspire and contribute to their community.
description: The Leadership Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities, potential and commitment to contributing positively to the school community. Recipients are recognised for their considerable achievements in demonstrating leadership and provided with opportunities to experience engaging programs and develop their leadership skills further through the school's student leadership initiatives. Applicants are asked to attend an interview at the College accompanied by their parents or guardians. The financial value of this scholarship is not specified on the school's website. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis with continuation subject to ongoing demonstration of leadership and positive contribution to the school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrate significant leadership qualities and potential; Evidence of contribution to school or community leadership; Must attend an interview at the College with parent/s or guardian/s; Open to all students seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Applicants must attend an interview at the College, accompanied by their parent/s or guardian/s. Scholarship recipients are expected to actively participate in school leadership programs and maintain appropriate conduct and attendance standards.

overview: St Albans Secondary College awards the Citizenship Scholarship to Year 6 students who demonstrate significant commitment to citizenship and community contribution. The scholarship recognises students who exemplify positive values and active participation in their community.
description: The Citizenship Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional citizenship qualities, commitment to community service and contribution to the wider community. Recipients are recognised for their considerable achievements in demonstrating citizenship values and provided with opportunities to experience engaging programs and further develop their commitment to positive community participation. Applicants are asked to attend an interview at the College accompanied by their parents or guardians. The financial value of this scholarship is not specified on the school's website. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis with continuation subject to ongoing demonstration of positive citizenship values and community contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrate significant commitment to citizenship and community service; Evidence of positive contribution to community; Must attend an interview at the College with parent/s or guardian/s; Open to all students seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Applicants must attend an interview at the College, accompanied by their parent/s or guardian/s. Scholarship recipients are expected to demonstrate ongoing commitment to positive citizenship values and community contribution.

program_name: Visual and Performing Arts Excellence Scholarship
overview: St Albans Secondary College awards the Visual and Performing Arts Excellence Scholarship to Year 6 students who demonstrate significant artistic achievement and talent. The scholarship recognises exceptional ability in visual arts, music, drama, dance or other performing arts disciplines.
description: The Visual and Performing Arts Excellence Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional artistic achievement, talent and commitment to visual and performing arts. Recipients are recognised for their considerable artistic achievements and provided with opportunities to experience engaging arts programs and develop their artistic skills through the school's performing arts centre and arts facilities. Applicants are asked to attend an interview at the College accompanied by their parents or guardians, and should provide evidence of artistic achievement such as portfolios or performance recordings. The financial value of this scholarship is not specified on the school's website. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis with continuation subject to ongoing engagement in arts programs and school conduct standards.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrate significant artistic achievement and talent in visual or performing arts; Evidence of artistic accomplishment in disciplines such as music, drama, dance or visual arts; Must attend an interview at the College with parent/s or guardian/s; Open to all students seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Applicants must attend an interview at the College, accompanied by their parent/s or guardian/s. Supporting documentation such as portfolios, performance videos or letters of recommendation from arts teachers is required. Scholarship recipients are expected to actively participate in school arts programs and maintain appropriate conduct and attendance standards.

overview: The SEAL Program is an accelerated learning program for gifted and talented Year 6 students entering Year 7. It offers high-achieving students a rigorous and diverse curriculum in a stimulating and challenging learning environment designed to maximise their learning potential.
description: The Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program at St Albans Secondary College supports students capable of learning at a faster pace and in greater depth than students of the same age. The program offers high potential students a rigorous and diverse curriculum in a stimulating and challenging learning environment. Students in the SEAL Program can complete most of the Year 7-10 curriculum in three years, with an enriched program across English, Integrated Studies, Languages Other Than English, Mathematics, Science, Health & Physical Education, Music, Arts and Technology. Applicants must register and sit the Edutest Entrance Exam. The program does not provide fee remission but offers enriched academic content and accelerated learning opportunities. Students continue in the program for Years 7-10 (or longer) subject to maintaining appropriate academic standards and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Enrolled at St Albans Secondary College for Year 7; Demonstrate high academic achievement and potential; Must register and sit the Edutest Entrance Exam; Identified as a gifted learner or high achiever; Open to all qualifying students regardless of background
special_conditions: Students must register for the Edutest Entrance Exam. Students accepted to the SEAL Program are required to meet certain expectations in order to continue advancing within the program. Students who fail to meet these expectations may have their place in the program reviewed through consultation with relevant staff members including Learning Manager, SEAL Coordinator and Sub-School coordinators. Continuation is conditional upon maintaining appropriate academic progress and conduct standards.

overview: St Anthony's School offers the MACS Family Fee Assistance Scheme to assist families who cannot afford to pay full school fees. This program ensures no student is excluded from a Catholic primary school education due to financial hardship.
description: Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) offers a Family Fee Assistance Scheme through funding to assist families to send their children to Catholic primary schools. Catholic schools have always welcomed families of all financial means and no family will be denied a Catholic education due to their financial situation. Families with a valid Health Care Card may be eligible for assistance. The extent of assistance varies based on individual family financial circumstances, and suitable fee structures are negotiated with the Principal. Financial assistance decisions are made on a case-by-case basis to ensure accessibility of Catholic primary education to all families regardless of income level.
eligibility_criteria: Families with demonstrated financial need; Families with a valid Health Care Card may be eligible; All families attending St Anthony's School are welcome to apply; No family will be excluded based on inability to pay fees
special_conditions: In cases where families experience financial hardship, a suitable fee structure will be negotiated with the Principal. Applications for assistance should be made directly to the school office. No student will be excluded from St Anthony's because of an inability to pay fees.

overview: St Anthony's School offers a 10% discount on school fees for families who pay their annual or term fees in full by the designated early payment date. This program provides financial relief to families who are able to pay fees upfront.
description: St Anthony's School provides a 10% discount on school fees for families who pay their fees in full by the March deadline (March 14, 2025 for that year). This early payment incentive reduces the overall cost of education and allows families to save money through upfront payment. Fee statements are issued each term. For families who prefer to pay in installments, fees can be paid weekly, fortnightly, monthly, termly, or half-yearly by setting up a Direct Debit arrangement. This flexible payment structure ensures accessibility while encouraging early payment discounts for those who can afford them.
eligibility_criteria: All families enrolled at St Anthony's School; Payment of full fees required by March 14 to receive discount; Applies to annual or term fee payments
special_conditions: Discount applies only when fees are paid in full by March 14 of each year. Families choosing installment payments must set up Direct Debit arrangements. Fee statements are issued each term to track payment status.

overview: St Anne's Catholic Primary School offers a School Fee Concession program to assist families experiencing financial hardship in accessing Catholic primary education. The program provides reduced annual concessional school fees that cover all standard school fees and levies for eligible families.
description: The MACS School Fee Concession Program is designed to support families who would otherwise be unable to afford Catholic education due to financial difficulty. Approved applicants receive a reduced annual concessional fee set by Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) that covers tuition fees and levies including student levies and swimming fees. The concession amount is fixed annually by MACS and is applied from the time the application is approved. Pro-rata concessions are provided if families become eligible mid-year. Applicants are assessed based on demonstrated financial hardship and may include families holding means-tested concession cards (Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card) or those experiencing severe financial hardship. The school office maintains dignity and privacy for all applicants throughout the process. No specific exam or selection test is required; assessment is based on financial information provided. The program is available to all year levels from Prep to Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families holding eligible means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander families; Families of veterans or former ADF members with permanent incapacity; All student year levels from Prep to Year 6; Open to all students enrolled at St Anne's
special_conditions: Concessional fee is fixed annually by Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS). Approved applicants are charged only the annual concessional fee which covers all standard school fees and levies. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. If applicant becomes eligible mid-year, pro-rata concession is granted from the time the application is made. All applicants are treated with dignity and privacy. School office staff can advise applicants of the specific concessional fee to be charged. Applications can be submitted to the school office and are assessed on a case-by-case basis.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy - Financial Hardship Assistance
overview: St Bernadette's Catholic Primary School, operating under Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS), provides fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The school is committed to ensuring that financial difficulty does not prevent Catholic education access for eligible families.
description: MACS primary schools provide fee concessions (remission or exemption from school fees) because of financial hardship under the Concessional Fee Policy. The policy acknowledges the need for families to meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay, while providing a consistent approach to school fee concessions across all MACS primary schools. This support is based on Catholic social teaching principles including the dignity of the human person, preferential option for the poor, and the common good. The policy ensures that families experiencing financial difficulty are supported to access Catholic education. Fee concession amounts vary based on individual family circumstances and assessed financial need. The application process and eligibility assessment are managed at the school level. No specific dollar amount or percentage remission is specified in the policy, as it is tailored to individual family need.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated inability to pay full school fees; Assessment of family financial circumstances required; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Policy applies to MACS primary schools only, not secondary schools. Assessment is based on demonstrated financial hardship and family capacity to pay. Applications are managed through the school office.

school_name: St Bede's College - Bentleigh East Campus
overview: St Bede's College offers the BEAM (Beda Enrichment and Academic Mentorship) program to nurture, develop and expand the abilities of gifted and talented students. The program is designed to empower students to unleash their unlimited potential and explore themselves as learners while developing their unique gifts across academic and non-academic disciplines.
description: The BEAM program is an enrichment and mentoring initiative tailored specifically to gifted and talented learners at St Bede's College across Years 7 to 12. The program provides students with more opportunities to experience joy and success in their learning both inside and outside the classroom. Students benefit from staff mentorship and academic guidance designed to support their development across many types of facets, disciplines, and passions without borders. The program aims to provide these exceptional students with strategic development of their unique gifts and expanded holistic growth. Enrolment is available to identified gifted and talented students across the entire college community.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as gifted and talented learner; Open to students in Years 7 to 12
special_conditions: Selection is based on identification of giftedness and talent. The program provides enrichment opportunities and academic mentorship but does not provide financial assistance or fee reductions.

school_name: St Bede's College - Bentleigh East Campus
overview: The Finian Foundation provides financial assistance through bursaries to St Bede's College families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty. The Foundation raises funds specifically to support families within the St Bede's community, ensuring their children are not disadvantaged due to financial circumstances.
description: The Finian Foundation is a dedicated fundraising initiative that supports families at St Bede's College who are experiencing financial difficulties. The Foundation aims to provide financial assistance through bursaries to help ensure that no student is disadvantaged due to family financial circumstances. Applications for bursary support can be submitted by families in financial hardship. The Foundation raises funds through various activities and donations, with all proceeds dedicated to providing school-related fee assistance. Bursary applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis, and awards are determined based on demonstrated financial need and family circumstances. The Foundation operates transparently with all donations of $2 or more being fully tax deductible.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Must be enrolled at St Bede's College or applying for enrolment; Application required
special_conditions: Bursary applications must be submitted and assessed on a case-by-case basis. Awards are discretionary and based on demonstrated financial need. Families must complete and submit an application form with supporting documentation. The Foundation guarantees that recipients will not be at a disadvantage financially.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy for Primary Schools
overview: St Anthony's School Glen Huntly operates under the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy. This policy provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing genuine financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. The school ensures that no baptised Catholic student is denied a Catholic education because of a family's inability to pay school fees.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy for Primary Schools provides fee remission or exemption from school fees for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Eligible families include those of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, holders of Department of Human Services Health Care Cards, holders of Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Cards, families experiencing severe financial hardship, and refugees. The policy applies to tuition fees and levies. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility. Applications must be made on the school's approved form and are assessed by the Principal, who has delegated authority to approve concessional school fees. All information provided is treated with strict confidentiality in accordance with the school's Privacy Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families of students experiencing genuine financial hardship; Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Holders of Department of Human Services Health Care Cards; Holders of Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Cards; Families experiencing severe financial hardship; Refugees holding an ImmiCard; Annual statutory declaration required to confirm eligibility; Applies to tuition fees and levies
special_conditions: Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm eligibility. Principals may apply this policy from the date of approval. All schools are required to comply with this policy prior to setting fees. No student will be denied a Catholic education because of a family's inability to pay school fees. All information provided in support of concessional school fees must be treated in the strictest confidence, in accordance with the School's Privacy Policy.

overview: St Bernard's College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 7 through to 12. The scholarship is awarded based on performance in the ACER examination, which assesses writing, critical thinking, problem-solving, comprehension and reasoning skills. The scholarship covers a percentage of the tuition fee.
description: Academic scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 through to 12 at St Bernard's College. All applicants must sit the ACER examination, which is the major criterion for selection. The ACER examination is designed to assess skills the student has been developing during their schooling including writing, ability to interpret and think critically, problem-solving skills, comprehension skills, and reasoning skills. The scholarship will cover a percentage of the tuition fee. Students who are already enrolled at the College, as well as external applicants, are eligible to apply. All applicants will receive a copy of their child's report approximately three weeks after the testing date.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 through to 12; Must sit the ACER examination; Open to both external applicants and students already enrolled at the College; Can only sit the ACER test once per year per student
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applicants must complete the ACER examination which will be administered at specified dates throughout the year. A student can only sit the ACER test once per year. Results and scholarship outcome notifications are sent approximately three weeks after the testing date.

overview: St Bernard's College, together with the St Bernard's College Foundation, is committed to offering bursary support for students who demonstrate financial need. Bursaries are designed to make a Catholic education accessible to families regardless of their financial circumstances, reflecting the Edmund Rice tradition. Bursary support covers a percentage of the academic tuition fee.
description: Bursaries at St Bernard's College are provided in support of the Edmund Rice vision that schools should be open to all people, irrespective of their financial means. The College, together with the St Bernard's College Foundation, is committed to offering bursary support to students with demonstrated financial need. The bursary will cover a percentage of the academic tuition fee. The number of bursaries and the percentage of bursary support offered to students depends on the bursary application, financial assessment, and funds available for that particular year. Bursaries are only for one year and are reviewed annually based on all circumstances. Final approval for bursary support is at the absolute discretion of the College, which may require an interview with either the Principal or Business Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Must demonstrate genuine financial need; Requires submission of financial assistance application with supporting documentation; Subject to annual review; Interview may be required with Principal or Business Manager
special_conditions: Bursaries are only for one year and are reviewed annually based on all circumstances. The College reserves the right to withdraw any bursary award in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress or conduct. Final approval for bursary support is at the absolute discretion of the College. An interview with either the Principal or Business Manager may be required. The amount of support depends on funds available in that particular year.

overview: St Bernard's College established the Mark Rose Indigenous Scholarship in 2023 to create a learning environment that values Indigenous culture and promotes a sense of belonging for Indigenous students. The scholarship is named after Professor Mark Rose, a respected Old Collegian and Indigenous leader. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous students attending the College.
description: The Mark Rose Indigenous Scholarship was announced in 2023 by the St Bernard's College Foundation with the aim of creating a learning environment that values Indigenous culture and promotes a sense of belonging for Indigenous students in the College. The scholarship is named after Professor Mark Rose (SBOC 73), a highly respected Old Collegian who is Deakin University's Pro Vice-Chancellor for Indigenous Strategy and Innovation. Mark Rose is traditionally linked to the Gunditjmara Nation of western Victoria and has had a long career in education contributing to educational settings within the State, nationally and internationally. The scholarship reflects the College's commitment to supporting Indigenous student access and success within the Edmund Rice tradition.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students; Currently enrolled or seeking enrolment at St Bernard's College; Identified as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded for first time in 2023. The scholarship aims to support Indigenous cultural engagement and sense of belonging within the College community.

school_name: St Bernard's College - Resurrection House Campus
overview: St Bernard's College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 7 through to 12. The scholarship is based on demonstrated exceptional academic excellence and performance in the ACER examination, recognising students who excel in critical thinking, problem-solving, comprehension and reasoning skills.
description: Academic scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 through to 12. All applicants must sit the ACER examination which is the major criteria for selection. The scholarship will cover a percentage of the tuition fee. The ACER examination is designed to assess skills the student has been developing during their schooling including writing, ability to interpret and think critically, problem-solving skills, comprehension skills, and reasoning skills. Students who are already enrolled at the College as well as external applicants are eligible to apply. Both current students and prospective students can apply for this merit-based award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Current students at St Bernard's College eligible; External applicants eligible; Must demonstrate high level of academic excellence and achievement; Must be willing to participate fully in the life of the College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: ACER examination will be held on Saturday, 3 May 2025, 9:00 am to 12:00 pm at St Bernard's College Essendon Campus. Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, 23 April 2025. All applicants will receive a copy of their child's report approximately three weeks after the testing date. The College will contact families after results have been released with the outcome of the application.

school_name: St Bernard's College - Resurrection House Campus
overview: St Bernard's College offers High Excellence Scholarships in addition to its Academic Scholarship program, recognising students who demonstrate exceptional achievement. These scholarships are available through the ACER examination to both current and prospective students in Years 7-11.
description: High Excellence Scholarships are offered by St Bernard's College as part of its scholarship offering via the ACER Scholarship Test. These scholarships are available to current Years 7-11 students and prospective Year 8-11 students wishing to study at St Bernard's College, if places are available. All applicants must demonstrate a high level of academic excellence and achievement. The scholarship will cover a percentage of the tuition fee. The successful applicants will need to demonstrate a willingness to participate fully in the life of the College. The ACER examination is the major criterion for selection.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11 students; Open to prospective Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11 students; Must demonstrate high level of academic excellence and achievement; Must be willing to participate fully in the life of the College; Must sit ACER examination
special_conditions: ACER examination held on Saturday, 3 May 2025, 9:00 am to 12:00 pm at St Bernard's College Essendon Campus. Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, 23 April 2025. All applicants receive a copy of their child's report approximately three weeks after the testing date.

school_name: St Bernard's College - Resurrection House Campus
overview: St Bernard's College, together with the St Bernard's College Foundation, is committed to offering bursary support for students. Following the vision of Blessed Edmund Rice who wanted schools to be open to all people irrespective of their financial means, the College offers need-based bursaries to support families facing financial difficulties.
description: Bursaries are offered by St Bernard's College to provide financial assistance to families facing financial hardship. The number of bursaries and the percentage of bursary support offered to students depends on the bursary application, financial application and funds available for that particular year. Bursaries are only for one year and are reviewed annually based on all circumstances. The bursary will cover a percentage of the academic tuition fee. The assessment process requires the submission of an application for financial assistance, including supporting documentation. Final approval for bursary support will be at the absolute discretion of the College, which may require an interview with either the Principal or Business Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Demonstrated financial need required; Requires submission of financial assistance application; Must provide supporting financial documentation; Subject to availability of funds
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded for one year only and are reviewed annually based on all circumstances. Percentage of support varies depending on financial need and funds available. Assessment process requires submission of application for financial assistance including supporting documentation. Final approval at absolute discretion of College and may require interview with Principal or Business Manager.

school_name: St Bernard's College - Resurrection House Campus
overview: St Bernard's College Foundation established the Mark Rose Indigenous Scholarship in 2023 to support Indigenous students. The scholarship recognises the College's desire to create a learning environment that values Indigenous culture and promotes a sense of belonging for Indigenous students at the College.
description: The Mark Rose Indigenous Scholarship was established in 2023 by the St Bernard's College Foundation to support Indigenous students and create a learning environment that values Indigenous culture and promotes a sense of belonging. The scholarship is named after Professor Mark Rose, a highly respected Old Collegian (Class of 1973) who is Deakin University's Pro Vice-Chancellor for Indigenous Strategy and Innovation. Professor Rose is traditionally linked to the Gunditjmara Nation of western Victoria and has had a distinguished career in education. The College is committed to fostering an inclusive environment where Indigenous students feel supported and valued throughout their academic journey.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students; Must be enrolled or seeking enrollment at St Bernard's College; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background required
special_conditions: First awarded in 2023. Designed specifically to create a learning environment valuing Indigenous culture and promoting sense of belonging for Indigenous students. Donations to support this scholarship are tax-deductible.

school_name: St Bernard's College - Resurrection House Campus
overview: St Bernard's College offers Music Bursaries to students with strong existing or emerging music ability. The bursary provides support for music lessons in preparation for semester study, recognising the importance of music education in student development.
description: St Bernard's College offers Music Bursaries to students with strong existing or emerging music ability. Students are invited to apply for a music bursary in preparation for semester music lessons. The assessment process involves an audition where applicants prepare one piece of music, which could be something students have been working on in music classes or through music tutorial lessons. The bursary provides financial support to help students access music education opportunities at the College. Music is recognised as an integral part of the St Bernard's College community, and the College believes that every student should have access to a comprehensive and holistic co-curricular music education program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Must have strong existing or emerging music ability; Applicants must complete an application form; Must prepare and perform one piece of music for audition
special_conditions: Application form must be completed and submitted via email to mpuglisi@sbc.vic.edu.au. Applicants prepare one piece of music for audition purposes. Auditions held at times determined by the College. Music lessons must be paid in full before lessons commence, separate from School Fees.

overview: St Brendan's School participates in the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy, which provides fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. This program ensures that no baptised Catholic student is denied a Catholic education due to financial inability to pay school fees.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy provides fee concessions for families experiencing genuine financial hardship, ensuring that school fees are charged according to the family's capacity to pay. The concessional fee is a fixed annual amount set by MACS and covers all standard school fees and levies. Families eligible for concessions include those holding a valid Health Care Card or Pension Card, Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander families, families eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) support, Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) card holders, and those experiencing severe financial hardship. Applicants must complete the MACS Concessional Fee Application Form and submit a copy of a valid card, along with CSEF application form where applicable. Pro-rata concessions are available for part-year attendance. All recipients must agree to either direct debit or Centrepay deduction arrangements for fee payments.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Health Care Card or Pension Card holders; Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) eligible families; Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card or Health Card holders; Their widow(er)s and dependants of eligible veterans; Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander families; Non-receipt of school fees should not impede Catholic education access
special_conditions: Approved applicants will be charged only the annual concessional fee set by MACS. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance, with standard fees charged for portions of the year when the applicant was not yet eligible. Families must notify the school if their financial circumstances change. Any offer of concession is subject to the applicant entering into a direct debit or Centrepay deduction arrangement. Default in payment may result in the concession being withdrawn. The dignity and privacy of those seeking a fee concession will be respected at all times.

program_name: Sister Brigid Arthur Scholarship for Excellence
overview: St Brigid's College offers the Sister Brigid Arthur Scholarship for Excellence to recognise significant achievement and outstanding ability across multiple disciplines. The scholarship is open to students entering Years 7 to 11 and rewards those who demonstrate commitment to academic excellence alongside participation in co-curricular programs.
description: The Sister Brigid Arthur Scholarship for Excellence recognises students with significant achievement and outstanding ability across multiple areas including academics, sports, music, performing arts, and visual arts. Students must demonstrate ability in at least three of the five recognised areas and maintain a strong academic standard. The scholarship offers up to 50% remission on tuition fees. Recipients must commit to the school and co-curricular programs, with progress reviewed annually. Selection involves submission of school reports and NAPLAN results, with shortlisted students attending an interview with the Principal who makes the final decision.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 7 to 11 in 2025; Strong academic standard required; Must demonstrate ability in at least three of five areas (academics, sports, music, performing arts, visual arts); Currently enrolled in Year 6 or below for entry-year applicants; No disciplinary issues
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients will be required to commit to the school and the co-curricular programs. The progress of recipients will be reviewed annually. Academic testing is not required; school reports and NAPLAN reports must be submitted.

overview: St Brigid's College offers the Diocese Scholarship as part of its commitment to making Catholic education accessible. The scholarship recognises academic merit and is available to eligible students at the secondary level.
description: The Diocese Scholarship is offered through the Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Limited (DOBCEL) and is available to students at St Brigid's College. This scholarship programme is designed to support students demonstrating academic merit while advancing the college's commitment to inclusive Catholic education. The college holds the belief that a student desiring Catholic education should not be denied access based on financial considerations. Further details regarding specific eligibility criteria, financial value, selection process, and application dates are available on the Diocese Scholarship page. Applications for this scholarship are currently closed.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary students eligible; Academic merit required; Must demonstrate commitment to Catholic education; Application status currently closed
special_conditions: Applications for this scholarship are currently closed. Further information and application details can be found on the Diocese Scholarship page.

overview: St Brigid's College offers the Margaret Simonds Bursary to provide financial support to students demonstrating the qualities and attributes that Margaret Simonds valued, including faith and leadership. The bursary recognises her legacy in literacy and community support.
description: The Margaret Simonds Bursary honours the late Margaret Simonds, founder of On Track Learning in the late 1980s, who established a network of local literacy coordinators around the region to provide volunteer tutors to literacy students. The bursary provides tuition fee support valued at $1,000 for students enrolled at St Brigid's College. The bursary is available to any currently enrolled student from Year 7 through to Year 11. It is designed to support students who embody the qualities and attributes that Margaret Simonds possessed, including faith and leadership. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated alignment with her values and commitment to service. The application process and selection criteria are available through the Margaret Simonds Bursary page.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 7 through to Year 11; Must embody qualities valued by Margaret Simonds including faith and leadership; Demonstrated commitment to community values; Applications currently closed
special_conditions: Available to currently enrolled students only; students who have previously received this bursary may have additional conditions; applications are currently closed

school_name: St Catherine of Siena Catholic Primary School

overview: St Brendan's Catholic Primary School Coragulac offers the Family Assistance Scheme to support families experiencing financial hardship. The scheme provides fee relief mechanisms for families unable to meet the full costs of education.
description: The Family Assistance Scheme was introduced by the Catholic Education Office to make Catholic Education more accessible to families in times of financial need. As a Catholic school system, St Brendan's ensures fee relief mechanisms are available to support families who are unable to meet their commitment to the full costs of education. Families experiencing difficulty with fee payments are encouraged to discuss their circumstances with the school on enrolment or as needs emerge. The school works with families individually to determine appropriate support. Fee relief is provided through flexible payment arrangements and assistance mechanisms, with specific support determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Unable to meet full education costs; Open to all students; Discussion required with school leadership
special_conditions: Fee relief discussions are encouraged on enrolment or as needs emerge. Support is determined on a case-by-case basis. Families must contact the Principal or school office to discuss circumstances. No formal application process specified on website.

program_name: Health Care Card Holder Fee Concession
overview: St Carlo Borromeo School provides fee concessions to families holding eligible Health Care Cards. The level of concession is determined on a case-by-case basis by the Principal, acknowledging the school's commitment to supporting families experiencing financial hardship.
description: St Carlo Borromeo School, as part of Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS), offers fee concessions to eligible Health Care Card (HCC) holders. Eligible recipients may request a discount in their school fees, with the level of fee concession determined on a case-by-case basis by the Principal. This financial assistance program is designed to support families who would otherwise be unable to afford the full school fees. The concession applies to annual school fees, with families required to contact the Principal confidentially to discuss their circumstances and arrange appropriate fee assistance. The program reflects the school's commitment to Catholic social teaching principles, ensuring that no family is prevented from accessing a Catholic education due to financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold a valid Health Care Card; Application must be made directly to the Principal; Eligibility assessed on case-by-case basis; Family experiencing financial hardship
special_conditions: Fee concession level is determined on a case-by-case basis by the Principal. Families should contact the Principal confidentially to discuss their circumstances. Assessment based on demonstrated financial hardship and family capacity to pay school fees.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy - General Financial Hardship Support
overview: St Carlo Borromeo School, as a MACS primary school, provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be unable to receive a Catholic education. The school implements Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools' Concessional Fee Policy to support equitable access to education.
description: As a Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) primary school, St Carlo Borromeo School provides fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship. The MACS Concessional Fee Policy ensures families can meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay, while receiving consistent support across all MACS primary schools. This policy is grounded in Catholic social teaching principles, particularly the 'dignity of the human person', 'preferential option for the poor', and 'the common good'. Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the Principal confidentially to discuss available support options. The level and type of concession is determined individually based on each family's circumstances, and the school works to ensure that financial barriers do not prevent access to Catholic education. All discussions and assessments are handled confidentially by school leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Student enrolled or seeking enrolment at St Carlo Borromeo School; Australian Government financial assistance may be considered; Must be able to demonstrate capacity to pay according to family circumstances
special_conditions: Concessions available to families unable to meet school fee obligations due to financial hardship. Discussions with the Principal are confidential. The school may offer remission or exemption from fees depending on family circumstances. Policy applies to MACS primary schools and does not extend to secondary schools or colleges.

overview: St Colman's School offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme to assist families who are experiencing financial hardship. The scheme is available through the Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Office and helps eligible families afford Catholic primary education.
description: The Diocese of Ballarat, through the Catholic Education Office, offers a Family Fee Assistance Scheme to assist families to send their children to Catholic primary schools. The scheme is available to families eligible for a means tested Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card or Veteran Affairs Pensioner Card. This scheme reduces the cost of the Family Fee to a maximum of $520 per year. The school is committed to ensuring no student is excluded because of an inability to pay fees, and alternative fee structures can be negotiated directly with the Principal. Catholic schools have always welcomed families of all financial means.
eligibility_criteria: Families must be eligible for a Health Care Card or Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card or Veteran Affairs Pensioner Card; Means-tested assessment required; No student will be excluded based on inability to pay
value_aud: Up to $520 per year fee reduction
special_conditions: It is the expectation that all families make every effort to meet their financial commitments. Fees may be paid weekly, fortnightly, monthly, termly, half-yearly or annually. Fee accounts are issued once per term. In cases of inability to pay fees, a suitable fee structure will be negotiated with the Principal.

overview: St Columba's School Ballarat offers means-tested financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The program provides support to eligible families to enable access to Catholic primary education at the school.
description: St Columba's School offers a Financial Assistance program through which families meeting financial need criteria may receive support with tuition fees. The assistance is means-tested and based on a confidential assessment of family financial circumstances. Families wishing to apply must complete a Financial Assistance Application Form disclosing details of income and assets along with all relevant documentary evidence. The amount of support varies depending on individual family circumstances and school funding availability. Applications for enrollment in the following academic year should be submitted before the end of the year, approximately 8 months prior to entry. The program is reviewed annually, and recipients must maintain satisfactory academic standards and continue to demonstrate financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial hardship; Applicants must meet school entry requirements; Australian residents; Applications to be made in advance of intended entry point; Means-tested based on family income and assets
special_conditions: Means-tested assessment required. Awards shown as fee deductions on annual fee note. No cash payments made to parents. Annual review of awards by school committee. Recipient must continue to demonstrate financial need and maintain satisfactory academic performance.

overview: St Columba's Primary School offers means-tested bursaries to eligible families in Year 4 and Year 5. The school is committed to ensuring that a Catholic education remains accessible to all families through flexible payment options and financial assistance.
description: St Columba's Primary School provides bursary support for families demonstrating financial need in Year 4 and Year 5. While specific fee reduction percentages are not publicly detailed on the website, the school indicates it can offer financial assistance to make education accessible. The application process involves confidential discussion with the school office, where conversations are handled with care and confidentiality. Flexible payment plans are available and can be arranged through the school office. Bursary awards are determined on a case-by-case basis following confidential financial assessment. The school is committed to ensuring affordability remains among the most accessible for Catholic and independent primary schools in the local area.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 4 and Year 5 students; Must demonstrate financial need; Confidential financial assessment required; Currently enrolled or entering students eligible
special_conditions: All conversations regarding school fees, concessional arrangements or flexible payment options are handled with care and confidentiality. Bursary awards are reviewed on a case-by-case basis following submission of confidential financial documentation.

overview: St Dominic's School Melton participates in the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy to provide fee concessions to families of students experiencing financial hardship. This policy ensures no child is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy for primary schools provides fee concessions to families experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be prevented from accessing a Catholic primary education. The program is designed to ensure that financial circumstances do not impede enrolment and continuation at the school. Families holding a current Health Care Card or Pension Card are eligible to apply for fee concession rebates. Additionally, families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage and any family experiencing severe financial hardship are strongly encouraged to apply. Applications must be submitted to the school with supporting financial documentation. The level of fee concession granted depends on the assessed level of financial hardship and available funds. Concessional fees are offered on an ongoing basis subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families holding Health Care Card or Pension Card; Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; No income threshold specified; Assessment of genuine financial need required
special_conditions: Concessional fees are based on assessed level of financial hardship. Fees are reviewed annually. Families must provide documented supporting evidence of financial hardship. The policy is consistent with Catholic social teaching principles of human dignity and preferential option for the poor.

program_name: Financial Assistance Policy - No Child Refused
overview: St Dominic's Catholic Primary School offers financial assistance to families experiencing genuine hardship. The school maintains a commitment that no child will be refused admission on the grounds of an inability to pay all or part of school fees.
description: St Dominic's operates a financial assistance policy ensuring equitable access to Catholic primary education. The school explicitly states that no child will be refused admission due to inability to pay school fees in full or in part. Families experiencing genuine financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school office to discuss confidential arrangements. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis through discussions with the Parish Priest and Principal. The school charges fees according to the number of children enrolled (lower rates for multiple siblings) and expects all parents to contribute to the School Building Fund as part of their commitment to the school and parish. Information regarding specific assistance amounts or application processes is available by direct contact with the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to pay all or part of school fees; All year levels Prep through Year 6 eligible
special_conditions: Families must contact the school office to discuss financial hardship circumstances. Confidential discussions are available with the Parish Priest or Principal on an as-needed basis. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis. Sibling discounts apply to families with multiple children enrolled.

overview: St Columba's College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in prospective Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and are committed to excellence in their studies and college life.
description: The Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarship covers full or partial school fees for the student's six years at the College as outlined in the St Columba's Scholarship Guidelines. Students must be a Year 5 student at the time of the scholarship examination and must provide a Catholic baptismal certificate. Selection is based on performance in the scholarship examination. The scholarship is conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College. All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 students at time of scholarship exam; Must provide Catholic baptismal certificate; Must be attending an Australian Primary School at time of registration and testing; Must be either an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Sound academic progress required; Active participation in extra-curricular life required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually. College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College.

overview: St Columba's College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to Year 9 entry to maintain and improve high academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and commitment to excellence.
description: The Year 9 Academic Excellence Scholarship provides full or partial school fees for the student's remaining four years at the College as outlined in the St Columba's Scholarship Guidelines. Students must be a current Year 8 student at the time of the scholarship examination and must be attending an Australian Secondary School. Selection is based on performance in the scholarship examination and interview. The scholarship is conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College. All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 8 students at time of scholarship exam; Must be attending an Australian Secondary School at time of registration and testing; Must be either an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Sound academic progress required; Active participation in extra-curricular life required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually. College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College.

overview: St Columba's College offers Academic Excellence Scholarships to Year 11 entry to maintain and improve high academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and commitment to excellence.
description: The Year 11 Academic Excellence Scholarship provides full or partial school fees for the student's remaining two years at the College as outlined in the St Columba's Scholarship Guidelines. Students must be a current Year 10 student at the time of the scholarship examination and must be attending an Australian Secondary School. Selection is based on performance in the scholarship examination and interview. The scholarship is conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College. All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 10 students at time of scholarship exam; Must be attending an Australian Secondary School at time of registration and testing; Must be either an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Sound academic progress required; Active participation in extra-curricular life required
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually. College reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the extra-curricular life of the College.
