overview: St Columba's College offers supported entry to Catholic, Christian and Eastern Orthodox families from the local area who may not consider a Catholic education due to the costs associated with secondary education. The program recognises that cost can be a barrier to accessing quality Catholic education.
description: St Columba's College offers supported entry to Catholic/Christian/Eastern Orthodox families from the local area who may not consider a Catholic education due to costs. The College accepts recommendations from Parish Priests and Principals of Priority Parish Primary schools, as well as from the Edmund Rice Community, Refugee Services and MacKillop Family Services. The College provides financial assistance in the form of fee reductions or support. Families recommended by partner organisations may receive support towards tuition fees to enable access to Catholic education. This program recognises the College's mission to provide education regardless of financial circumstances and operates in conjunction with the Scholarship Guidelines.
eligibility_criteria: Catholic, Christian or Eastern Orthodox faith; From local Essendon area; Recommended by Parish Priest, Primary School Principal, Edmund Rice Community, Refugee Services or MacKillop Family Services; Meet entrance requirements for the Senior School
special_conditions: Families must be from local area and recommended by eligible organisations. Financial support is provided where families meet entrance requirements and demonstrate commitment to Catholic education.

school_name: St Francis Catholic College - Cobblebank Campus
St Francis de Sales Catholic Primary School
St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School
St Francis of the Fields School
overview: St Francis of the Fields offers financial hardship assistance to families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. The school policy clearly states that no child will be excluded from school based on financial circumstances. Families can make confidential arrangements with the Principal to support their enrolment.
description: The school operates a formal financial hardship policy that ensures no child is excluded due to genuine financial difficulty. Families experiencing financial hardship can contact the Principal at any time to arrange a mutually fair and suitable arrangement. These arrangements are strictly confidential, and parents experiencing difficulty in meeting school fee payments are invited to make an appointment with Principal Tim Moloney. The school recognises that families may face varying financial circumstances and is committed to working with families to find appropriate solutions. This is a flexible, need-based support program available to all families currently enrolled or enrolling at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled or enrolling families; Must be experiencing genuine financial difficulty; Families must contact the Principal to arrange support
special_conditions: Arrangements are strictly confidential. Parents who may be experiencing difficulty in meeting school fee payments are asked to please make an appointment for a confidential meeting with the Principal. Alternative arrangements can be made to suit individual family circumstances.

school_name: St Francis of the Fields School
overview: St Francis of the Fields offers an optional maintenance fee of $70.00 that families can pay in lieu of performing weekend maintenance tasks. This provides an alternative payment option for families who prefer to contribute financially rather than participate in school maintenance activities.
description: The school implements a flexible maintenance fee arrangement that allows families to choose between two options: paying a $70.00 maintenance fee or volunteering 2-3 hours on a weekend during the year for school maintenance tasks. This optional fee arrangement provides families with flexibility in how they contribute to the school's operations and maintenance. The fee is only payable if families elect to pay in lieu of performing the maintenance tasks. This arrangement demonstrates the school's commitment to inclusive community participation and recognition that families have varying capacity to volunteer time. The program acknowledges different family circumstances and allows alternative forms of contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Choice between payment or volunteer contribution; Families may select either option based on their circumstances
special_conditions: Fee is only payable if families elect to pay in lieu of performing weekend maintenance tasks for 2-3 hours one weekend during the year. Families may choose to volunteer instead of paying the fee.

school_name: St Francis Xavier College - Berwick Campus
St Francis Xavier College - Officer Campus
overview: St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus provides financial assistance to families who hold a current eligible Health Care Card. This program makes education more accessible to eligible families by offering substantial fee reductions on the annual Education Fee.
description: St Francis Xavier College offers a 70% discount against the Education Fee charged per student for families who carry a current eligible Health Care Card (HCC). This is a non-means tested program that automatically applies to eligible families. The discount applies to the annual Education Fee, which covers the Digital Learning Program, incursions, excursions and compulsory camps. Families can contact the finance department at fees@sfx.vic.edu.au or on 03 9707 3111 to confirm eligibility and apply this discount. The program is ongoing for as long as families maintain an eligible Health Care Card.
eligibility_criteria: Current eligible Health Care Card holder; Enrolled or enrolling at St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Applicants must carry a current eligible Health Care Card. Families must contact the College finance department to apply the discount to their account.

school_name: St Francis Xavier College - Officer Campus
overview: St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus offers flexible payment plans for eligible families to make tuition fees more manageable. The college works directly with families on individual payment arrangements based on their financial circumstances.
description: St Francis Xavier College recognises that families have different financial circumstances and offers flexible payment plan options to support enrolment accessibility. Rather than a fixed discount, families can discuss customised payment arrangements with the College's Finance Manager. Fees can be paid by direct debit, EFTPOS, credit cards, cheques, BPay or Centrepay. Families may pay fees in instalments, with each term's fees payable by the end of that term. For families experiencing difficulties making payments by the due dates, the College encourages confidential discussion with the Finance Manager to explore available options. Contact fees@sfx.vic.edu.au or 03 9707 3111 to discuss payment arrangements.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled or enrolling at St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus; Open to all families experiencing financial challenges; No specific income threshold
special_conditions: Families must contact the Finance Manager to arrange flexible payment plans. Plans are customised based on individual circumstances. The College reserves the right to decline or modify arrangements based on family circumstances. Early payment discounts are available if the annual tuition fee is paid in full by 31 January.

school_name: St Francis Xavier College - Officer Campus
overview: St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus offers fee discounts to families with multiple children enrolled at the College. This program reduces the overall education costs for larger families with siblings attending the school.
description: The College offers families with multiple children enrolled a discount on their fees. This is a sibling discount program designed to make education more affordable for larger families. The specific discount percentage is not detailed on the website, and families should contact the Finance Manager to learn the exact discount amount applicable to their situation. The discount applies to the annual Education Fee component of school fees. The discount can be combined with other payment options including direct debit and flexible payment plans. Families are encouraged to contact the finance department at fees@sfx.vic.edu.au or 03 9707 3111 to clarify the exact discount applicable to their family.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus simultaneously; Open to all families with multiple siblings at the College
special_conditions: Applicable only to families with two or more children simultaneously enrolled at any St Francis Xavier College campus (Officer, Berwick, or Beaconsfield).

school_name: St Francis Xavier College - Officer Campus
overview: St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus offers a Travel Conveyance Allowance to eligible students to assist with the cost of transport to and from school. This financial support program helps families meet the cost of commuting to the Officer campus.
description: Some students may be eligible for a Travel Conveyance Allowance, a financial support program specifically aimed at assisting with the cost of transport to the College. This allowance is designed to support student access to the Officer campus by helping families cover commuting costs. The allowance is particularly relevant given the Officer campus location in the outer south-eastern suburbs. Eligible students should contact the College's Finance Manager to discuss eligibility and application. The specific eligibility criteria, allowance amount, and application process should be confirmed directly with the finance department at fees@sfx.vic.edu.au or 03 9707 3111. This is a means-tested or circumstance-based program.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled at St Francis Xavier College Officer Campus; Demonstrated financial need or transport cost hardship; Geographic eligibility may apply; Families should contact the College for specific criteria
special_conditions: Eligibility criteria not fully detailed on the website. Families must contact the Finance Manager directly to discuss eligibility and the application process for the Travel Conveyance Allowance.

overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School Frankston offers concessional fee assistance to families experiencing financial hardship or meeting specific eligibility criteria. The school is committed to ensuring that financial circumstances are not a barrier to Catholic education.
description: St Francis Xavier Primary School provides fee concessions through a structured concessional fees policy aligned with MACS (Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools) guidelines. Eligible families can access reduced school fees based on their financial circumstances. In 2025, concessional fee rates are $15 per child per week for families with one child ($780 per annum), $23 per week for two children ($1,196 per annum), and $30 per week for three or more children. Families must apply for concessional fee status and provide evidence of their financial circumstances. The school assesses applications based on criteria including Services Australia Health Care Card status, Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card status, refugee status, Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, or genuine financial hardship. Concessional fees remain in effect for the duration of enrolment subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Services Australia Health Care Card holder; Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card holder; Department of Home Affairs ImmiCard holder; Refugee status where child has attended Australian school for less than five years; Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Experiencing genuine financial hardship
special_conditions: Annual statutory declaration required to confirm continued eligibility. Families must apply and provide evidence of financial circumstances. The school follows MACS Concessional Fee Policy procedures. Approved concessions are subject to annual review.

program_name: MACS Fee Relief / Concessional Fee Policy
overview: St Christopher's School offers fee relief support to families experiencing financial hardship through the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy. Eligible families with Healthcare Cards can access reduced school fees to ensure no Catholic child is denied education due to inability to pay.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy is designed to support families who have a demonstrated, means-tested inability to afford school fees at St Christopher's. The policy acknowledges the Catholic Church's preferential option for the poor and ensures that no baptised Catholic student is denied a Catholic education because of a family's inability to pay school fees. Families holding a current Healthcare Card (HCC) are entitled to apply for fee relief, which can significantly reduce annual tuition and levy fees. The concessional fee covers school tuition fees and levies and is processed at the beginning of each school year. Families are required to complete application forms and provide documentation of their current Healthcare Card. The financial assistance available can vary based on individual family circumstances and is assessed on a case-by-case basis by the school principal.
eligibility_criteria: Families holding a current Healthcare Card (HCC); Families experiencing financial hardship; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families; Families receiving Centrelink support; Application forms available from the school office at the beginning of each school year
special_conditions: Concessional fees must be paid by direct debit arrangement. Application forms are available from the school office at the beginning of each school year. Families must hold a current Healthcare Card to be eligible. This policy aligns with MACS principles based on Catholic social teaching and the dignity of the human person.

overview: St Christopher's School supports eligible families by providing access to the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports & Excursion Fund (CSEF). This government program provides financial support to families holding Healthcare Cards to offset costs of camps, sports activities, and excursions.
description: The Camps, Sports & Excursion Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative that provides annual funding to eligible families to help cover the cost of school camps, sports activities including swimming, and excursions. Families holding a current Healthcare Card (HCC) are eligible to apply for this support. The CSEF payment is made directly to St Christopher's School and is allocated by the school towards the costs of camps, sports and excursion activities for the eligible student. This assistance helps reduce the financial burden on families with eligible concession cards. Application forms for CSEF are available from the school office at the beginning of each school year, and families are encouraged to apply early to ensure they are not disadvantaged.
eligibility_criteria: Family holds a current Healthcare Card (HCC); Student is enrolled at the school; Application completed and submitted to school office; Families receiving Centrelink payments or holding temporary foster care orders may be eligible
special_conditions: The CSEF is an annual payment that must be applied for each school year. Payments are made directly to the school and allocated towards eligible camp, sports and excursion costs. CSEF payments cannot be claimed retrospectively for prior years. Families should apply as soon as possible at the beginning of the school year to avoid missing out on support.

school_name: St Francis of Assisi Primary School

overview: St James Primary School offers financial assistance to families experiencing hardship who are unable to meet full school fees. The school is committed to ensuring no student is disadvantaged due to financial circumstances, and families are encouraged to discuss their situation confidentially with school leadership.
description: St James Primary School provides assistance to reduce school fees for families experiencing financial hardship. Parents holding a Pension or Health Care card are eligible to apply. Eligibility is assessed on a case-by-case basis, and the school works with the Diocese of Sale Catholic Education Limited to apply for assistance to reduce school fees. The financial benefit is determined based on individual circumstances and eligibility assessment. The school emphasizes that no student will be refused enrolment or disadvantaged because of an inability to meet financial requirements. Families should contact the Principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial hardship; Holding a Pension or Health Care card; Open to all students at the school; Assessment of eligibility required
special_conditions: Applications are assessed confidentially. Eligibility determination is based on financial circumstances. The school works in consultation with Diocese of Sale Catholic Education Limited (DOSCEL) to approve assistance applications. Applicants must provide relevant documentation such as Pension or Health Care card evidence.

overview: St Helena Secondary College offers the Accelerated Curriculum and Enrichment (ACE) Program for gifted and talented students in Years 7-9. The program caters to highly motivated, self-directed learners who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and are committed to excellence and school involvement.
description: The ACE Program is designed for highly motivated students in Years 7-9 who seek academic challenges that align with their abilities. Students in the ACE Program learn in a supportive and collaborative environment alongside peers with similar interests and aspirations. The program emphasises higher-order thinking skills, problem-solving, and collaborative project-based learning, with a focus on personal growth and community engagement. ACE students complete the Years 7-10 curriculum in three years, followed by an enriched VCE over the next three years. Opportunities to undertake a university subject, such as Higher Education VCE Studies (HEVS), are available in Year 12. The program is non-monetary in nature and does not provide fee reduction or financial assistance. Eligibility is determined through university-developed achievement tests in Reading Comprehension, Mathematical Reasoning, and Abstract Thinking, teacher assessments from primary school, and demonstrated organisational skills, literacy, and numeracy.
eligibility_criteria: Self-directed gifted and talented students; Committed to excellence and college involvement; Strong organisational skills; Well-developed literacy and numeracy; Broad general knowledge base; Years 7-9 entry only; Must demonstrate exceptional skills in one or more curriculum areas
special_conditions: ACE Program operates one class of 26 students, commencing in Year 7, with students remaining together through Years 7 to 9. Students follow the same core curriculum as mainstream classmates but have access to additional resources, enrichment programs, and differentiated learning materials that promote deeper analysis and encourage advanced thinking skills.

overview: St Helena Secondary College offers the Kwong Lee Dow Scholarship to recognise outstanding Year 10 students. The scholarship provides recognition and future opportunities for high-achieving students who demonstrate academic excellence.
description: Two Year 10 students each year are recognised with the University of Melbourne's Kwong Lee Dow Scholarship. This presents an exciting opportunity of experiencing university life and taking part in a range of academic events and activities to help students achieve their best and get a head start on their future studies and career. Students who are recognised with the Kwong Lee Dow Scholarship, once having completed their secondary studies, could be offered a guaranteed place in an undergraduate degree, financial assistance to move to Melbourne, and an overseas scholarship during their studies. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional academic potential and achievement. Selection is determined by the University of Melbourne based on their criteria and nomination by St Helena Secondary College.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students at St Helena Secondary College; Demonstrated academic excellence; Exceptional academic potential; Nominated by St Helena Secondary College
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded by the University of Melbourne; Recipients gain access to university life and academic events; Guaranteed undergraduate place upon completion of secondary studies (conditional); Financial assistance for relocation to Melbourne and overseas scholarship during tertiary studies available upon completion of secondary studies.

overview: St Helena Secondary College provides access to State Schools Relief for families experiencing financial hardship. This program offers confidential support to eligible families who require assistance with school costs.
description: St Helena Secondary College understands that some families may experience financial difficulty and offers confidential support through State Schools Relief. Students and families experiencing hardship can access this program to help cover costs associated with school attendance. A social worker at the college coordinates access to this support and provides confidential discussions about hardship relief. The program provides flexible financial assistance based on individual family circumstances and need assessment. There is no application fee and support is tailored to each family's situation. The exact financial value varies depending on individual family circumstances and approval of the State Schools Relief assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated financial need; Current students at St Helena Secondary College; Confidentiality maintained
special_conditions: Confidential discussions available; Application and assessment process managed by school social worker; Support is individualised based on family circumstances; No stigma or public disclosure of hardship support.

school_name: St Georges Road Primary School Shepparton
St Georges Road Primary School Shepparton-Shepparton English Language Centre

overview: St James the Apostle School offers concessional school fees to eligible families experiencing financial barriers to Catholic education. The program is designed to support families who meet specific criteria including Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, Health Care Card holders eligible for CSEF, those experiencing severe financial hardship, DVA Gold Card holders, and identified refugees with ImmiCards.
description: The Concessional Fee Program provides reduced school fees and levies to qualifying families. Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; families holding a Health Care Card and eligible for Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF); families experiencing severe financial hardship; families holding a DVA Gold Card; and families identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard are strongly encouraged to apply. The school administers this program in accordance with Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy. The concessional fee covers both tuition fees and the education levy (normally $340 per child for supplies, sporting events and excursions). Applications are assessed on eligibility criteria and individual circumstances. The program aims to ensure that no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial hardship, in alignment with Catholic social teaching and the principle of preferential option for the poor.
eligibility_criteria: Family of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Holding a Health Care Card and eligible for CSEF; Experiencing severe financial hardship; Holding a DVA Gold Card; Identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard
special_conditions: Application forms must be completed through the MACS Concessional Fee Application Form available from the school office. Payment by direct debit is required for concessional fee arrangements. The concessional fee does not apply to camp fees, which are payable in addition to the reduced tuition fees. Families must maintain current account status to retain concessional fee status. All information submitted is treated in strict confidence.

school_name: St John the Apostle Catholic Primary School

overview: St John's Primary School Dennington offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme to support families holding current Pension or Health Care Cards. The scheme provides a tuition fee concession designed to reduce the financial burden of school fees for eligible families.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme is available to families who hold a current Health Care Card, Pension Card, or Centrelink concession card. Eligible families pay $520 per annum for primary school tuition fees, covering tuition and capital fees only. The discounted fee does not cover additional charges such as camps, student fees, and excursions. The scheme is applied once verification of concession cards is received by the school office. Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the office staff to discuss eligibility and application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Family must hold a valid current Health Care Card, Pension Card, or Centrelink/Veteran Affairs concession card; Student holders of Health Care Cards are not eligible; Application requires presentation of valid concession card
special_conditions: Discounted fee covers tuition and capital fees only and does not include additional charges such as camps, student fees, and excursions. Credit is applied to school fee statement in the same year of application once concession card details are verified. Families must hold current valid concession card.

overview: St John's Primary School Dennington assists Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families through Opening The Doors Foundation grants. These grants provide financial support to assist with the extra costs of schooling for eligible Indigenous students.
description: Opening The Doors Foundation provides grants to families to assist with the extra costs of schooling for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. For first-time applicants, families must provide a Confirmation of Aboriginality Certificate with the application. If unable to obtain this documentation, the school can provide contact information for alternative verification processes. The school staff are available to assist families throughout the application process. Annual application is required to access grant funding for the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to all year levels; First-time applicants must provide Confirmation of Aboriginality Certificate; School can assist with verification if certificate cannot be obtained
special_conditions: Annual application required. First-time applicants must provide Confirmation of Aboriginality Certificate or alternative verification acceptable to Opening The Doors Foundation. School staff available to assist families with application process and documentation requirements.

overview: St John's School Heidelberg participates in the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy to assist families experiencing financial hardship. This policy provides fee remissions or exemptions for eligible families, ensuring Catholic education remains accessible to all.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy is available to St John's School families who meet specific eligibility criteria. Families holding a valid Health Care Card and eligible for Camps, Sports, Excursion Fund (CSEF) are eligible to apply. Additionally, families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, families experiencing genuine financial hardship, families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) Gold Card, and families identified as refugees holding an ImmiCard are encouraged to apply. The policy provides a remission or exemption from school fees payable because of financial hardship. Applications must be resubmitted each year. The fee concession covers both tuition fees and levies. When an application is approved, an automated payment plan is established. Families are invoiced school fees and levies in full, with a fee remission shown on the invoice to reflect the concessional fee amount.
eligibility_criteria: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Families holding a valid Department of Human Services Health Care Card and eligible for CSEF; Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Families holding a Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card; Families identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard; Open to all year levels Prep-6
special_conditions: Application must be submitted each year. Families must provide a valid Health Care Card or evidence of eligibility. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. The Principal has delegated authority to approve concessional school fees based on family circumstances. This policy applies specifically to MACS primary schools and does not apply to secondary schools or colleges. Full-fee paying overseas students (FFPOS) or full-time home schooled students (HSS) are ineligible for the concession.

overview: St John's College Preston offers the General Excellence Scholarship to well-rounded new students who demonstrate a high level of academic performance and proven achievements in at least two of the following categories: the Arts, sport, music, leadership and community service. The scholarship recognises students who excel across multiple disciplines and contribute meaningfully to the school community.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to new students entering Year 7, Year 9 and Year 11 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement alongside excellence in at least two co-curricular areas including arts, sport, music, leadership or community service. Selection is based on completion of a General Excellence Scholarship Application Form, written academic assessment via Edutest examination, and an interview with the Principal. The scholarship provides partial fee remission, with the exact percentage determined at the Principal's discretion. The scholarship is awarded annually and recipients are expected to maintain active participation in school co-curricular programs. A non-refundable $50 application fee is required for General Excellence Scholarship applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; High level of academic performance required; Demonstrated achievement in at least two of the following: Arts, Sport, Music, Leadership, Community Service; Completion of General Excellence Scholarship Application Form; Successful performance on Edutest examination; Australian citizen or permanent resident preferred
special_conditions: Non-refundable $50 application fee applies. Scholarship awarded at the sole discretion of the Principal. Recipients must maintain active participation in school co-curricular programs and demonstrate commitment to the school community.

overview: St John's College Preston offers the Academic Scholarship to students who demonstrate a high calibre of academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional academic ability and is designed to make quality education more accessible to outstanding students.
description: Academic Scholarships at St John's College Preston are awarded to students who demonstrate a high calibre of academic achievement. Selection is based on assessment criteria including current academic results, Australian Council for Education Research (ACER) test results, and an interview with the College Principal. The scholarship provides up to 50% fee reduction, with the exact percentage determined by the Principal based on individual merit and circumstances. Recipients must maintain strong academic performance and active engagement in school life. Applications are invited from students entering Years 7, 9, and 11.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; High academic achievement required; Current academic results in top percentile; Strong performance on ACER assessment; Interview with Principal; Australian citizen or permanent resident preferred
special_conditions: Exact percentage of fee reduction determined by Principal discretion. Scholarship conditional upon maintaining strong academic progress and active participation in school community. Annual review of scholarship status may occur.

school_name: St Josephine Bakhita Catholic Primary School
St Joseph's College Geelong - Westcourt Campus
St Joseph's College Mildura - Mercy Campus
St Joseph's College Mildura - VCAL Precinct Campus
St Lawrence of Brindisi Catholic Primary School
St Leo the Great Primary School

overview: St Malachy's School Edenhope provides access to the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursion Funding scheme. This program assists eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities for students at the school.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursion Funding (CSEF) is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. Families who hold a valid means-tested Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, Veteran Affairs Pensioner Gold Card or are temporary foster parents may be eligible for CSEF assistance. The school coordinates applications on behalf of eligible families. Assistance is provided as financial support for specific excursion, camp and sporting activity costs during the school year. Families should contact the school office to obtain a CSEF application form or download one from the Victorian Government website.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested Health Care Card; OR hold a Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card; OR hold a Veteran Affairs Pensioner Gold Card; OR be a temporary foster parent
special_conditions: Application form must be obtained from school office. Victorian Government determines eligibility based on means-tested concession card status.

overview: St Malachy's School Edenhope offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme for families who qualify for Camps, Sports and Excursion Funding. This scheme provides significantly reduced tuition fees for eligible families experiencing financial difficulty.
description: Families that qualify for the Family Fee Assistance Scheme will pay a reduced annual tuition fee of $520 per annum, which equates to $10 per week for primary school tuition fees and the capital levy. This represents a substantial reduction from standard school fees and is available to families who meet the eligibility criteria for CSEF support. The scheme provides ongoing fee reduction throughout the student's enrollment at the school. Families must contact the school office to obtain a Family Fee Assistance Scheme application form and meet the income and family circumstance requirements set by the Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Limited.
eligibility_criteria: Families eligible for Camps, Sports and Excursion Funding; Demonstrate financial need; Contact school office for application details
special_conditions: Families eligible for CSEF are automatically eligible to apply. Provides significant fee reduction for primary school tuition fees and capital levy. Ongoing support throughout student enrollment subject to continued eligibility.

school_name: St Marianne Cope Catholic Primary School
program_name: Concessional Fee Assistance for Financial Hardship
overview: St Marianne Cope Catholic Primary School provides fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. This support is consistent with Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools policy and ensures no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial circumstances.
description: Under the MACS Concessional Fee Policy for Primary Schools, St Marianne Cope Catholic Primary School provides fee concessions and fee remissions to families experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. The policy acknowledges the need for families to meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay. Eligible families may receive partial or full fee concessions depending on their circumstances. The school recognises government assistance programs including Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card holders, Department of Veteran Affairs Health Card holders, and families on relevant government assistance. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the Office Manager or Principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially and arrange appropriate support.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating genuine financial hardship; Families with students enrolled or seeking enrolment at the school; Eligible holders of Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card or DVA Health Card (All Conditions or Totally & Permanently Incapacitated); Families receiving relevant Australian Government financial assistance; Application and assessment required
special_conditions: Families experiencing financial difficulties should contact the school's Office Manager to make an appointment with the Principal for a confidential discussion. The school operates under the MACS Concessional Fee Policy which provides a consistent approach to school fee concessions across all MACS primary schools. Assessment is based on demonstrated financial hardship and capacity to pay.

overview: St Margaret's Primary School participates in the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) Concessional Fee Policy. This policy assists families of students experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be unable to afford a Catholic education. The school provides fee concessions to eligible families to ensure educational access.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy provides fee assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Eligible families include those holding Health Care Cards, pensioner concession cards, DVA Gold Cards, families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, refugees holding ImmiCards, and those experiencing severe financial hardship. Approved applicants receive a fixed concessional annual fee set by MACS that covers all standard school fees and levies. Pro-rata concessions are provided for part-year attendance. Applications must be accompanied by statutory declarations confirming eligibility, and families must agree to direct debit or Centrepay payment arrangements. Annual review is required to maintain the concession.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Families holding valid concession cards (Health Care Card, Pensioner Card); Families with DVA Gold Card eligibility; Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Families identified as refugees with ImmiCard; Willingness to enter direct debit or Centrepay arrangement; Annual statutory declaration required to confirm ongoing eligibility
special_conditions: Concessional fee is fixed annually by MACS. Approved applicants must maintain direct debit or Centrepay payment arrangements. All families must notify the school of significant changes in financial circumstances. Applications are assessed confidentially in accordance with the School's Privacy Policy. Concession may be withdrawn if payment arrangements are defaulted. Pro-rata concession applies for students attending part of the year.

overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar offers art and design scholarships to recognize talented students who demonstrate exceptional creative expression and originality in visual arts. The scholarship supports students who wish to progress with their artistic practice into VCE level studies.
description: Art and Design Scholarships consist of 50% remission of annual Tuition Fees. Candidates are expected to contribute to the SMBG Art and Design program and have a desire to progress with their practice into VCE. All candidates must submit 2 most recent school reports, latest NAPLAN results, and a digital portfolio in a single PDF document that includes up to 5 examples of work from any medium or art form. Candidates' portfolio work will be assessed based on the originality of their work and their creative expression. Assessment interviews are held between 9.00 am and 4.00 pm on scheduled dates at the Berwick Campus. Short-listed candidates will be invited to attend an interview with the Principal or relevant Head of School. The scholarship is offered on an annual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated creative excellence; Originality and creative expression in artistic work; Commitment to continuing art and design studies to VCE level; Submission of portfolio with recent school reports and NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Candidates expected to contribute to SMBG Art and Design program. Students must remain at St Margaret's Berwick Grammar until the end of Year 12 in accordance with the terms of the Scholarship Agreement. If a student leaves prior to completion of Year 12, the tuition fees discounted during the period of the scholarship must be repaid.

overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar's Bursary Scheme is designed to provide the broadest possible access to the school for families by understanding individual circumstances and offering places where there are no waiting lists. The scheme provides financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship.
description: The Bursary Scheme is designed to provide financial assistance based on individual family circumstances. Applications are reviewed and assessed by Edstart, a financial assessment partner, who evaluates family financial information confidentially. The school reviews bursary applications considering place availability, alumni status, siblings, student interview performance, and student academic performance. The school determines a bursary value indication based on Edstart's assessment and family circumstances. Bursaries are reviewed in full every 3 years or when there is a significant change in family circumstance such as a sibling joining the school. Families must pay school fees via Edstart, which provides various payment plan options including weekly, fortnightly, or monthly payments. The application process requires completion of a Bursary Application Form with a $150 assessment fee.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolment application must be accepted by school first; Demonstrated financial hardship; Student interview and performance assessment; Family financial circumstances assessment by Edstart
special_conditions: Applications are open to enrolled and current families. Bursaries are reviewed in full every 3 years or when there is a significant change in family circumstance e.g. a sibling joins the school. Financial information provided is assessed by Edstart and never seen by the school, ensuring financial privacy. Scholarship or Bursary discounts apply to the Tuition Fee component only. Students must remain at school until end of Year 12; if they leave early, discounted tuition fees must be repaid.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 5 to Year 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through comprehensive assessment.
description: The Academic Scholarship provides up to 50% remission of annual tuition fees and is offered until the end of Year 12. Consolidated charges and additional tuition fees such as music, drama, and tennis remain at full charge. Selection is based on a rigorous examination assessing Abstract Reasoning and Problem Solving, Mathematics Achievement and Reasoning, and Reading Comprehension. Candidates who reach a pre-determined level of academic performance in the initial examination are shortlisted and invited for an interview with the Principal or relevant Head of School. The scholarship applies to the Tuition Fee component only and recipients must remain at the school until the completion of Year 12, with any fees discounted during the period of scholarship required to be repaid if the student leaves prior to completion.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Pre-determined level of academic performance in examination required; Open to external and internal candidates; Must remain at school until end of Year 12
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Students must remain at St Margaret's Berwick Grammar until the end of Year 12 in accordance with the terms of the Scholarship Agreement. If a student leaves the School prior to the completion of Year 12, the persons responsible for the payment of the student's fees will be obliged to pay to the School any tuition fees discounted during the period of the scholarship.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar offers the Music Scholarship to talented musicians entering Year 5 to Year 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to the performing arts program at the school.
description: The Music Scholarship provides up to 50% remission of annual tuition fees and 100% remission of music tuition fees for tuition undertaken at the school in one instrument, which includes voice. The cost of any additional music tuition, including music theory lessons, books, and AMEB examinations will be the responsibility of the parent and does not form part of the scholarship terms. Selection is based on academic performance, interview, and an audition that includes a performance of two contrasting pieces, sight-reading, and aural testing. Music scholars are required to undertake elective Music studies to VCE level and must also participate in the co-curricular music programs offered at St Margaret's Berwick Grammar, including participation in the relevant Junior or Senior Choir and instrumental ensembles. Students must have achieved minimum AMEB Grade 2 or equivalent for instruments such as Violin, piano, percussion, and voice.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5 to Year 11; Minimum AMEB Grade 2 or equivalent or higher depending on instrument; Commitment to participate in co-curricular music programs; Willingness to undertake elective Music studies to VCE level; Preference given to string, brass, and woodwind players and those willing to combine keyboard/vocal skills
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Music scholars are required to undertake elective Music studies to VCE level and must also participate in the co-curricular music programs offered at St Margaret's Berwick Grammar. This includes participation in the relevant Junior or Senior Choir and instrumental ensembles. The cost of any additional music tuition, including music theory lessons, books, and AMEB examinations will be the responsibility of the parent and does not form part of the Scholarship terms.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar offers Art and Design Scholarships to talented visual artists entering Year 9, 10 and 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional creative ability and commitment to developing their artistic practice.
description: The Art and Design Scholarship provides 50% remission of annual tuition fees. Selection is based on an entrance assessment process including portfolio review and audition/interview. Candidates must provide 2 most recent school reports, latest NAPLAN results, and a digital portfolio including up to 5 examples of work from any medium or art form. The portfolio should also include a statement of up to 500 words outlining reasons for applying, and may include copies of achievements, certificates and references. Only shortlisted candidates are invited to attend an assessment interview. Candidates will be expected to contribute to the SMBG Art and Design program and have a desire to progress with their practice into VCE. Assessment interviews are held between 9:00 am and 4:00 pm on designated dates at the Berwick Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 9, 10, or 11 in 2027; Digital portfolio with up to 5 examples of work required; Most recent school reports and latest NAPLAN results required; Statement of 500 words outlining motivation for applying; Existing students of SMBG can apply for all scholarships; Commitment to contribute to Art and Design program and progress into VCE
special_conditions: Candidates will be expected to contribute to the SMBG Art and Design program and have a desire to progress with their practice into VCE. Assessment interviews held between 9:00 am and 4:00 pm on Wednesday 21 May 2025 at the Berwick Campus. Scholarship or Bursary discounts apply to the Tuition Fee component only.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar Bursary Scheme is designed to provide the broadest possible access to the school for families by understanding individual circumstances and offering support where there are no waiting lists. The scheme provides financial assistance to enrolled families based on assessed need.
description: The Bursary Scheme provides variable financial assistance based on individual family circumstances. Applications are assessed through a partnership with Edstart, an education payments company backed by National Australia Bank (NAB). Families submit financial information to the secure portal, and Edstart provides a bursary value indication based on their assessment while maintaining financial privacy. The school reviews bursary applications considering multiple factors including place availability, alumni status, siblings, student interview and student performance. Bursaries are reviewed in full every 3 years or when there is a significant change in family circumstance such as a sibling joining the school. Applications are open to enrolled and current families only, with applicants able to access the Bursary Scheme Portal.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolment application must be accepted by school first; Applications open to enrolled and current families only; Not available to children in the ELC; Assessment fee of $150 includes Edstart review; Considered on individual family circumstances; Factors include place availability, alumni status, siblings, student interview and performance
year_levels: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: It is recommended that applicants apply for a Bursary the year prior to a child commencing with the school. Bursaries are reviewed in full every 3 years or when there is a significant change in family circumstance. If successful and you accept a Bursary to the school, the assessment fee will be returned to you via your school fees schedule. Families must maintain enrolment and satisfactory attendance.

school_name: St Mary MacKillop Catholic Primary School

overview: St Mary MacKillop College offers the Betty Currie Scholarship to provide fee assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The scholarship has been accessed by many families over the years and is designed to ensure no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees.
description: St Mary MacKillop College has a fee assistance scholarship through the Betty Currie Scholarship program, which many families have accessed over the years. Families who hold a means tested Health Care Card qualify automatically for the scheme, however, applicants must complete the Betty Currie Scholarship Application Form. If families are not eligible for the Health Care Card discount, the college can explore confidential financial support arrangements to enable ongoing education at the school. This reflects a long-established policy in the Diocese of Ballarat that no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees. The application form became available from 22 June 2025.
eligibility_criteria: Families holding a means-tested Health Care Card qualify automatically; Financial need assessment required; Completion of Betty Currie Scholarship Application Form required; Open to all year levels; Families not eligible for Health Care Card may also apply for confidential financial support arrangements
special_conditions: All applications are confidential. There is a diocesan policy that no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees. Financial information must be supplied with application paperwork.

school_name: St Mary MacKillop College - The Technology Centre
overview: St Mary MacKillop College offers the Betty Currie Scholarship as a fee assistance program for families experiencing financial hardship. Families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card automatically qualify, while other families may explore confidential financial support arrangements to enable their child's ongoing education at the college.
description: The Betty Currie Scholarship is a fee assistance program available to families experiencing financial hardship who wish to access a Catholic education at St Mary MacKillop College. Families holding a means-tested Health Care Card automatically qualify for the scheme; however, completion of the Betty Currie Scholarship Application Form is required. For families not eligible for the Health Care Card discount, the college can explore confidential financial support arrangements to enable the child's ongoing education. The Diocese of Ballarat maintains a long-established policy that no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees. The application form becomes available on 22 June each year, and applications require submission of financial information. All applications are treated as confidential. The specific financial benefit amount is not publicly disclosed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Must hold a means-tested Health Care Card OR be eligible for confidential financial support assessment; Open to all year levels Year 7-12; Requires completion of Betty Currie Scholarship Application Form
special_conditions: Families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card qualify automatically for the scheme but must complete the application form. All applications are confidential. The Diocese of Ballarat maintains a policy that no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees. For families not eligible for the Health Care Card discount, confidential financial support arrangements can be explored to enable the child's ongoing education.

school_name: St Mary of the Cross Catholic Primary School
St Mary of the Cross MacKillop Catholic Parish Primary School
St Mary of the Angels College
overview: St Mary of the Angels College offers Academic Scholarships to reward exceptional academic achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate strong academic ability and commitment to the College, supporting their continued excellence throughout secondary education.
description: The Academic Scholarship is offered to Year 7 students enrolling and Year 10 students in 2026. This Scholarship will have a life of three years. The scholarships are offered to students on the condition that academic performance continues to be of a high standard in ensuing years together with a commitment to the College. Scholarship paperwork is available from the College Office. Applications must be submitted through the College. The selection process involves academic assessment and review. Scholarships are renewable subject to satisfactory academic progress and commitment to the College throughout the duration of the three-year award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Open to students continuing in Year 10; Academic performance must be of a high standard; Commitment to the College required; Open to all students regardless of background or residence
special_conditions: Scholarships have a life of three years. Academic performance must continue to be of a high standard in ensuing years. Recipients must maintain commitment to the College throughout their scholarship tenure. Late applications cannot be accepted.

school_name: St Mary of the Angels College
overview: St Mary of the Angels College offers Instrumental Tuition Scholarships to support talented young musicians. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional musical ability and commitment to instrumental study, providing assistance with the cost of private lessons.
description: Instrumental Tuition Scholarships are available each year to support students undertaking private instrumental lessons at the College. Scholarships are offered to one junior (Years 7-9) and one senior (Years 10-12) student annually. These scholarships recognise students with demonstrated musical talent and commitment to instrumental music study. The scholarship provides support towards the cost of instrumental lessons, which are offered as a complement to the classroom music curriculum. Recipients are selected based on musical ability and commitment to their instrumental studies. The scholarship is renewable annually subject to continued musical excellence and participation in College music programs.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be undertaking private instrumental lessons at the College; Must demonstrate musical ability; Open to junior students (Years 7-9) or senior students (Years 10-12); Must show commitment to instrumental music study; Open to all students regardless of instrument choice
special_conditions: Two scholarships awarded annually - one for a junior student and one for a senior student. Recipients must continue to demonstrate musical excellence and participate in College music programs.

overview: St Leonard's College offers the Leonardian Scholarship to support students facing significant life challenges and difficult financial circumstances. The scholarship is designed to enable worthy students to access an independent school education who would otherwise not have the opportunity.
description: Leonardian Scholarships are awarded to prospective students entering Years 7 to 11 who are overcoming significant life challenges and offering them the chance to reach their full potential against all odds. These ASPIRE scholarships are awarded to students who face ongoing difficult financial, family, or other life circumstances which have prevented them from achieving their best. Applications should include a written statement, relevant references, two most recent school reports, the results of any other education-related testing, and proof of Australian citizenship or permanent residency. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for interview by the Principal, where additional supporting documentation may be required including references from qualified professional persons/organisations and financial statements such as tax returns and income/asset statements. The College provides life-changing opportunities for worthy students through this program.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Years 7 to 11; Australian citizens or permanent residents; Facing ongoing difficult financial, family, or other life circumstances; Sound academic ability; Strong commitment to completion of secondary studies; Desire to contribute to College community
special_conditions: Means-test required. Combined taxable income assessment applies. Renewal subject to satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated need. Recipients must support College community.

overview: St Leonard's College offers the Global Citizen Scholarship to support young people who have immigrated to Australia as refugees in search of a brighter future. These ASPIRE scholarships provide opportunities for prospective students seeking entry who have recently arrived on refugee visas.
description: Global Citizen Scholarships provide support for prospective students seeking entry into Years 7 to 11 who have immigrated to Australia on a refugee category visa in the past 24 months. These scholarships are designed for young people who have journeyed to Australia as refugees in search of a brighter future. The College has a close relationship with the Noble Park English Language Centre but welcomes applications from all eligible families. Applications should include a written statement, two most recent school reports, results of any other education-related testing, and proof of Australian permanent residency. Applicants are encouraged to include references from local community leaders or organisations. Where shortlisted for interview by the Principal, supporting documentation must be provided including references from qualified professional persons/organisations and financial statements such as tax returns and income/asset statements.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Years 7 to 11; Immigrated to Australia on refugee category visa in past 24 months; Australian permanent residents; Sound academic ability; Strong commitment to completion of secondary studies; Desire to contribute to College community
special_conditions: Refugee category visa required, obtained within past 24 months. Means-test required. Renewal subject to satisfactory academic progress and continued demonstrated need.

overview: St Leonard's College offers Indigenous Scholarships to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students seeking secondary education. The College is committed to increasing the number and ensuring the success of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students enrolled in secondary education.
description: Indigenous Scholarships may be awarded to Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander prospective students entering Years 7 to 11. These scholarships are designed for students overcoming significant life challenges, offering them the chance to reach their full potential. The College has close relationships with the Warruwi community and the Melbourne Indigenous Transition School but welcomes applications from all eligible families. Applicants must demonstrate sound academic ability, strong commitment to completion of secondary studies, and the desire to contribute to the College community. To be considered for an Indigenous Scholarship, the combined taxable income of all parties responsible for school fees must be at or below $99,000 and assets and liabilities must satisfy the College's means test. Applications should include a written statement, relevant references, most recent school reports, results of any other education-related testing, and confirmation of Indigenous heritage.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander prospective students; Entering Years 7 to 11; Sound academic ability; Strong commitment to completion of secondary studies; Desire to contribute to College community; Combined taxable income at or below $99,000; Meet College means test requirements
special_conditions: Indigenous heritage confirmation required. Combined taxable income must not exceed $99,000. Means-test required. Renewal subject to satisfactory academic progress.

overview: St Leonard's College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement and merit. These scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are offered to prospective students demonstrating outstanding academic performance and potential. Applications are administered by Edutest on behalf of the College. The scholarship selection process involves academic testing and assessment of student achievement. Recipients are recognised for their exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, with the scholarship providing financial support to help access an independent school education. The exact financial value of the scholarship is available in the College's official prospectus and requires contact with the admissions office for current details.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic achievement; Exceptional academic ability; Strong commitment to learning; Application through Edutest assessment process
special_conditions: Applications administered by Edutest. Renewal subject to maintaining academic standards.

overview: St Leonard's College offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise well-rounded students demonstrating excellence across multiple areas. These scholarships reward students who show balanced achievement in academics and co-curricular pursuits.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to prospective students demonstrating well-rounded achievement and excellence across academic and co-curricular pursuits. Applications are administered by Edutest on behalf of the College. The scholarship recognises students who balance strong academic performance with meaningful participation in sports, arts, music, leadership, and community service. The exact financial value and specific selection criteria are available through the College's official prospectus and admissions office. Recipients are expected to continue their well-rounded contributions to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Strong academic performance; Demonstrated excellence in co-curricular activities; Well-rounded student profile; Participation in sports, arts, music, and/or community service; Application through Edutest assessment process
special_conditions: Applications administered by Edutest. Renewal subject to maintaining academic standards and participation in co-curricular activities.

overview: St Leonard's College offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and commitment. These scholarships are designed for accomplished musicians who wish to pursue their passion while attending the College.
description: Music Scholarships are awarded to prospective students demonstrating excellence and outstanding potential in music. Applications are administered by Edutest on behalf of the College. The scholarship selection process involves auditions and assessment of musical ability, technical skill, and performance experience. Recipients are expected to contribute significantly to the musical life of the College through participation in ensembles, performances, and co-curricular music activities. The College is renowned for its extensive music program and performance opportunities at the Leonardian Centre. The exact financial value is available through the College's official prospectus and admissions office.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated excellence or outstanding potential in music; Sound technical ability in chosen instrument(s) or voice; Musicality and interpretation; Strong commitment to musical performance; Application through Edutest audition process
special_conditions: Audition required. Recipients expected to participate in College music ensembles and performances. Renewal subject to maintaining music participation and academic standards.

overview: St Leonard's College offers Drama Scholarships to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional dramatic ability and commitment to theatre. These scholarships reward talented performers who will contribute to the College's renowned Hart Theatre Company program.
description: Drama Scholarships are awarded to prospective students demonstrating excellence and outstanding potential in drama. Applications are administered by Edutest on behalf of the College. The scholarship selection process involves auditions and assessment of dramatic ability, performance skills, and stage presence. Recipients are expected to contribute significantly to the dramatic life of the College, including participation in the Hart Theatre Company productions and other theatrical performances. The College hosts five major productions annually and has state-of-the-art theatre facilities including the Leonardian Centre. The exact financial value is available through the College's official prospectus and admissions office.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated excellence or outstanding potential in drama; Sound understanding and knowledge of creating and presenting dramatic works; Ability to perform with confidence; Strong stage presence and communication skills; Strong commitment to theatrical participation; Application through Edutest audition process
special_conditions: Audition required. Recipients expected to participate in College drama productions and Hart Theatre Company. Renewal subject to maintaining drama participation and academic standards.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Dandenong Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Equity Scholarship to support families experiencing financial hardship who would find it difficult to access primary or secondary education at the college. The scholarship assists with the cost associated with tuition and is offered as a result of generous bequests made to the College.
description: St Mary's College is committed to supporting families in need of a St Mary's education through the Equity Scholarship, which assists with the cost associated with tuition. The scholarships cover between 50% and 90% of tuition fees depending on family income. Equity scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. Equity Scholarships are offered to students at all year levels. Eligibility requires students with a moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss and either from a family experiencing financial hardship, or families who hold a means tested Health Care Card (HCC) and are eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) qualify automatically for the MACS Fee Concession Program. Combined family income for non-Health Care Card Holders (HCC) is capped at $100,000.
eligibility_criteria: Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Enrolled at St Mary's College; Family experiencing financial hardship or holding means tested Health Care Card (HCC); Combined family income capped at $100,000 for non-HCC holders; Available to students at all year levels
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. The College offers scholarships throughout the year but submissions should be received no later than the closing dates specified. Awarded as a result of generous bequests made to the College.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Dandenong Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Foundation/Prep Dominican Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate commitment to the shared values of the school and the Four Pillars of Dominican life—community, service, study, and prayer. This scholarship supports primary level students entering Foundation or Prep.
description: The Foundation/Prep Dominican Scholarship is awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life, being community, service, study, and prayer. The scholarship covers 80% of tuition fees for primary students. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated values alignment and commitment to Dominican principles. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement. Applicants should complete the application form with supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Foundation or Prep year level; Demonstrated commitment to shared values of the school; Demonstrated commitment to the Four Pillars of Dominican life (community, service, study, prayer); Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Application submitted by September 18th prior to entry
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement. Selection based on commitment to shared school values and the Four Pillars of Dominican life.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Dandenong Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Year 7 Dominican Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate commitment to the shared values of the school and the Four Pillars of Dominican life—community, service, study, and prayer. This scholarship supports secondary students entering Year 7.
description: The Year 7 Dominican Scholarship is awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life, being community, service, study, and prayer. The scholarship covers 33% of tuition fees for secondary students. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated values alignment and commitment to Dominican principles. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Applicants should complete the application form with supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Demonstrated commitment to shared values of the school; Demonstrated commitment to the Four Pillars of Dominican life (community, service, study, prayer); Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Application submitted by September 18th prior to entry
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Selection based on commitment to shared school values and the Four Pillars of Dominican life.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Dandenong Campus
program_name: Teacher of the Deaf Master's Degree Bursary
overview: St Mary's College offers a bursary to support teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. This program aims to enhance the number and quality of Teachers of the Deaf.
description: St Mary's College offers a bursary to support teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. The purpose of this offering is to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications who are available to work at St Mary's College as well as the wider Deaf Education sector. The bursary provides financial assistance to eligible teachers pursuing this specialisation. This program supports the school's commitment to building capacity in the Deaf Education field and developing specialist educators.
eligibility_criteria: Completed Australian Catholic University or other recognised teaching course; Seeking to undertake Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education; Committed to working in Deaf Education sector
special_conditions: Applicants must have completed an Australian Catholic University or other recognised teaching course prior to application. Designed to support teachers entering the specialised field of Deaf Education.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Ringwood Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Equity Scholarship to support families experiencing financial hardship who would not ordinarily be able to access an education at the College. The scholarship assists with tuition costs and recognises the importance of ensuring equitable access to education for all deaf and hard of hearing students.
description: The Equity Scholarship covers between 50% and 90% of tuition fees, with the exact amount depending on family income and individual circumstances. Scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. The application process involves submission of financial documentation and assessment by the College. Families who hold a means tested Health Care Card (HCC) and are eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) qualify automatically for the MACS Fee Concession Program. Combined family income for non-Health Care Card Holders is capped at $100,000.
eligibility_criteria: Students with a moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; From a family experiencing financial hardship or holding a Health Care Card; Available to students at all year levels; Open to students enrolled at any St Mary's campus
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and maybe extended for a further 3 years if needed. Equity Scholarships are offered as a result of generous bequests made to the College.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Ringwood Campus
overview: St Mary's College awards the Dominican Scholarship to Foundation and Prep students in recognition of commitment to the shared values of the school and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. The scholarship supports families seeking to provide their children with a Catholic education grounded in Dominican principles of community, service, study, and prayer.
description: The Foundation/Prep Dominican Scholarship covers 80% of tuition fees and is awarded for a period of 3 years. Selection is based on commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life. Applicants must complete the application form demonstrating how they embody these values. The scholarship recognises students whose families align with the Dominican tradition and who will actively contribute to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Foundation or Prep year; Commitment to shared values of the school and Four Pillars of Dominican life; Enrolled in Catholic education; Demonstrated commitment to community, service, study and prayer
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Foundation or Prep commencement.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Ringwood Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Year 7 Dominican Scholarship to secondary students entering Year 7, recognising commitment to the shared values of the College and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. The scholarship supports Catholic education aligned with Dominican principles of community, service, study, and prayer.
description: The Year 7 Dominican Scholarship covers 33% of tuition fees for secondary students and is awarded for a period of 3 years. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life, which include community, service, study, and prayer. Applicants must complete the scholarship application form demonstrating alignment with these values and their intention to contribute to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; Commitment to shared values of the school and Four Pillars of Dominican life; Enrolled in Catholic education; Demonstrated commitment to community, service, study and prayer; In Year 6 at time of application
