special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Scholarship subject to satisfactory completion of application process.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Ringwood Campus
overview: The Clare Winter Bursary has been established by the Dominican Sisters to enable students to gain access to a St Mary's College education where this may have been prevented as a result of financial hardship. The bursary recognises the friendship and links between the Dominican Sisters and Clare Winter.
description: The Clare Winter Bursary is awarded to provide financial assistance to students experiencing hardship that would otherwise prevent them from accessing education at St Mary's College. The bursary is discretionary and awarded at the gift of the Principal, meaning awards are determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual circumstances and demonstrated need. The value and duration of assistance varies depending on individual family circumstances and the Principal's assessment of need.
eligibility_criteria: Students experiencing financial hardship; Where financial hardship would prevent access to St Mary's College education; All year levels; Open to all campuses
special_conditions: The bursary, known as the Clare Winter Bursary, is in the gift of the Principal. Awards are determined at the Principal's discretion based on individual circumstances.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Ringwood Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the John Patterson Bursary to support teachers undertaking the Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education. The bursary is funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest and aims to enhance the number and quality of Teacher of the Deaf qualifications available in the sector.
description: The John Patterson Bursary is a financial support program for teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University teaching course or other teaching qualification and wish to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. The bursary's purpose is to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications who are available to work at St Mary's College as well as the wider Deaf Education sector. The bursary is funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest and provides financial assistance to eligible teachers pursuing this specialisation.
eligibility_criteria: Teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course; Intending to undertake Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education; Committed to working in Deaf Education sector
special_conditions: Funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest. The bursary is designed to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications available to work at St Mary's College and the wider Deaf Education sector. Eligible applicants must be committed to pursuing the Master of Learning Intervention specialisation.

overview: St Mary's Coptic Orthodox College offers The Principal's Award as its highest possible award, recognising outstanding achievement by VCE students. The award honours exceptional academic performance and commitment to excellence at the tertiary level.
description: The Principal's Award initiative was introduced in 2021 to recognise the highest level of academic achievement. Recipients are awarded a MacBook Pro valued at up to $2,000 for achieving an ATAR of 95 and above. Additionally, $500 is awarded for each study score of 40 or above achieved in a Units 3 & 4 VCE subject. The award recognises sustained excellence across multiple subjects and provides tangible recognition of student achievement at the conclusion of secondary education. This is an annual award with multiple possible recipients based on merit thresholds.
eligibility_criteria: ATAR of 95 and above in VCE; Unit 3 & 4 subject study scores of 40 or above
special_conditions: Award is based on final VCE results. Students must achieve the specified ATAR threshold or subject study scores to be eligible. Each additional 40+ study score in a Units 3 & 4 subject results in a separate $500 award.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in prospective Year 7-12 students. The scholarship is awarded based on performance in the ACER entrance examination and recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic merit and commitment to learning.
description: The Academic Scholarship at St Mary's College provides students with significant financial assistance to recognise academic excellence. The maximum scholarship value is 50% of academic tuition fees for two years for students applying in Years 7-11, and 1 year for students applying at Year 12. Students must sit the designated ACER Scholarship Exam (Level 1 for Years 7-8, Level 2 for Years 9-10, Level 3 for Years 11-12). Successful applicants are required to maintain a high standard of academic achievement, and academic performance is reviewed at the end of each year. As a condition of accepting the scholarship, students must remain at St Mary's College until the completion of the scholarship terms. Any student who leaves before completion must refund the credits applied to their fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12 in 2026; Must not currently be receiving any other discounted fees through bursary or scholarship at St Mary's College; Must perform strongly in the ACER Scholarship Exam; Parent/guardian contact required prior to scholarship application for non-enrolled students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered for two years for Years 7-11 entrants and one year for Year 12 entrants. Academic performance is reviewed annually. As a condition of acceptance, students must remain at the College until completion of scholarship terms. If a student leaves prior to completion, any credits applied to fees must be refunded. Students must demonstrate willingness to participate fully in college life and maintain a high academic standard. The scholarship agreement is entered into that is beneficial to the student, family and College.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers Drama Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to the performing arts. The scholarship recognises students with outstanding talent in drama and supports their continued participation in the college's vibrant performing arts programs.
description: The Drama Scholarship program at St Mary's College supports students with exceptional drama abilities. Students applying for drama scholarships are required to audition, with the audition process conducted by the St Mary's College Drama Department. The amount and length of the scholarship are determined upon completion of the audition assessment. Drama scholars are expected to play a full part in the musical and dramatic life of the school, prioritising performing arts activities and contributing to dramatic and musical ensembles. Scholarships are awarded for the successful candidate's entire time at the College, and the College reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if scholars cease to participate in drama without authorisation.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrates outstanding ability in drama; Must be willing to participate fully in the dramatic life of the College; Current St Mary's College students receiving other discounted fees by bursary or scholarship are ineligible; Students applying from outside the College must contact the Registrar to ensure places are available prior to application
special_conditions: Students must audition with the St Mary's College Drama Department. The amount and length of scholarship are determined upon completion of audition. Drama scholars are expected to prioritise performing arts activities and contribute to theatrical productions. Scholarships are awarded for the student's entire time at College and may be withdrawn if participation ceases without authorisation. Efforts and behaviour must remain consistent with the standard expected of scholars.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and talent. The scholarship recognises and supports students with outstanding achievement in music and encourages their participation in the college's comprehensive music program.
description: The Music Scholarship program at St Mary's College supports students with exceptional musical abilities and talent. Students applying for music scholarships are required to audition with the St Mary's College Music Department. The amount and length of the scholarship are determined upon completion of the audition assessment. Music scholars are expected to play a full part in the musical life of the school, which means prioritising musical activities over other pursuits and contributing to instrumental and vocal ensembles. Scholarships are awarded for the successful candidate's entire time at the College, and the College reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if scholars cease to participate in music without authorisation.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrates outstanding ability and talent in music; Must be willing to participate fully in the musical life of the College; Current St Mary's College students receiving other discounted fees by bursary or scholarship are ineligible; Students applying from outside the College must contact the Registrar to ensure places are available prior to application
special_conditions: Students must audition with the St Mary's College Music Department. The amount and length of scholarship are determined upon completion of audition. Music scholars are expected to prioritise musical activities and contribute to instrumental and vocal ensembles. Scholarships are awarded for the student's entire time at College and may be withdrawn if participation ceases without authorisation. Efforts and behaviour must remain consistent with the standard expected of scholars.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers Sports Scholarships to students who demonstrate elite sporting ability and have been selected to represent either state or national teams. The scholarship recognises and supports outstanding athletes who will contribute to the college's sporting programs.
description: The Sports Scholarship program at St Mary's College supports students who have achieved elite sporting status at state or national level. To be eligible, students must have been selected to represent either a State team or National team. Sports scholarship applications must be submitted in the same year the student is selected for the State or National team, and past representation will not be considered. Sports scholarships are awarded for one year only, and they apply only to the current year of study. Should a student be selected for the State or National Team in following years, parents must notify the College and provide a letter of selection. Sports scholars are expected to play a full part in sporting activities within the College, including extra-curricular activities at lunchtimes, after school and on Saturday mornings, with representing the school regarded as an honour and obligation.
eligibility_criteria: Must have been selected to represent either a State team or National team; Application must be submitted in the year of selection; Past representation will not be considered; Must be currently attending or seeking to attend St Mary's College; Must be willing to represent the College in sporting competitions; Current St Mary's College students receiving other discounted fees by bursary or scholarship are ineligible
special_conditions: Sports scholarship applications must be submitted in the same year student is selected for State or National team. Past representation will not be considered. Scholarship applies to current year of study only. If student is selected for State or National Team in subsequent years, parents must notify College with letter of selection. Sports scholars must prioritise sporting participation at College including extra-curricular activities at lunchtimes, after school and Saturday mornings. Representing the school must be regarded as an honour and obligation.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers fee concessions for families holding a valid Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC) or Pensioner Concession Card (PCC). The program provides tuition fee relief to support families experiencing financial hardship.
description: St Mary's College is committed to ensuring that a Catholic education is accessible to every child, regardless of financial circumstances. Families who hold a valid Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC) or Pensioner Concession Card (PCC) may be eligible for a concession on tuition fees under the Health Care Card Concession Scheme. This non-means-tested program provides fee relief based on government welfare card eligibility. Interested families should contact the College directly for further information about eligibility requirements, application process and the specific amount of fee relief available. The concession provides flexible financial assistance to support families facing economic hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Family holds a valid Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC); OR Family holds a valid Pensioner Concession Card (PCC); Open to all students at St Mary's College
special_conditions: Requires proof of valid Centrelink Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. Application must be made directly to the College. Families should contact the College for further information regarding eligibility and application process.

school_name: St Mary’s College Melbourne - Edmund Rice Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers a sibling discount program for families with multiple children concurrently enrolled at the College. This fee reduction supports families managing education costs for two or more students simultaneously.
description: St Mary's College recognises that managing education costs for multiple children can be challenging and provides fee relief through a sibling discount program. A sibling discount applies to families with second and subsequent students concurrently enrolled at the College. This means families with two or more children attending St Mary's College at the same time receive a discount on tuition fees. The exact discount percentage and application process should be confirmed directly with the College. This program is designed to make Catholic education more accessible and affordable for larger families or families with multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Family has two or more children concurrently enrolled at St Mary's College; Both/all siblings must be active students during the same period; Open to all fee-paying families
special_conditions: Discount applies to second and subsequent students concurrently enrolled. Families should contact the College for specific discount amounts and how to apply.

school_name: St Mary's College for the Deaf - Tarneit Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Equity Scholarship to support families experiencing financial hardship who would otherwise be unable to access a St Mary's education. The scholarship is funded through generous bequests made to the College and assists with the cost of tuition fees.
description: The Equity Scholarship supports families in financial need who wish to access St Mary's College education. Scholarships cover between 50% and 90% of tuition fees depending on family income. Equity scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. The scholarship is available to all year levels. Students must have a moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss and either hold a means tested Health Care Card (HCC) and be eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF), or have a combined family income for non-HCC holders capped at $100,000. No specific selection test is required; assessment is based on submitted application documentation and financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Students with moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; from a family experiencing financial hardship; Health Care Card holders eligible for CSEF qualify automatically for MACS Fee Concession Program; combined family income for non-HCC holders capped at $100,000; students at all year levels eligible
special_conditions: Scholarships awarded for 3 years and may be extended for further 3 years if needed. Scholarship subject to continued eligibility and financial circumstances review.

school_name: St Mary's College for the Deaf - Tarneit Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Foundation/Prep Dominican Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate commitment to the shared values of St Mary's and the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer. This scholarship is available to students entering Foundation or Prep level.
description: The Dominican Scholarship recognises commitment to the shared values of St Mary's and the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer. For primary level students, the scholarship covers 80% of tuition fees. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications are strictly required by September 18th in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement. Selection is based on the applicant's demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and school community engagement. Applicants must submit application forms with supporting documentation demonstrating alignment with the school's ethos and values.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Foundation or Prep level; demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and Four Pillars of Dominican life; application submission by September 18th in year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded for 3 years. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to Dominican ethos and values. Selection based on application forms demonstrating alignment with school's shared values.

school_name: St Mary's College for the Deaf - Tarneit Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Year 7 Dominican Scholarship to recognise students demonstrating commitment to the shared values of St Mary's and the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer. This scholarship supports students transitioning into Year 7.
description: The Year 7 Dominican Scholarship recognises commitment to the shared values of St Mary's School and the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer. For secondary level students, the scholarship covers 33% of tuition fees. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications must be strictly closed by September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and evidence of community engagement and service. Applicants must submit completed application forms with supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and Four Pillars of Dominican life; application submission by September 18th in year prior to Year 7 commencement
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded for 3 years. Recipients must maintain commitment to Dominican ethos and values. Selection based on submitted application forms demonstrating alignment with school's Four Pillars.

school_name: St Mary's College for the Deaf - Tarneit Campus
program_name: Teacher of the Deaf Master's Degree Bursary
overview: St Mary's College offers a bursary to support teachers undertaking the Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education. The bursary is designed to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications available to work at St Mary's College and the wider Deaf Education sector.
description: This bursary is available to teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other recognised teaching course and wish to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. The bursary supports professional development and qualification in Deaf Education, addressing the critical need for qualified Teachers of the Deaf in specialist education. The purpose of this offering is to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications who are available to work at St Mary's College as well as the wider Deaf Education sector. The bursary provides financial support for course fees and professional development. Specific financial benefit amount not publicly specified; assessed on individual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Completed Australian Catholic University or other recognised teaching qualification; committed to undertaking Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education; seeking to work in Deaf Education field
special_conditions: Applicant must be a current or prospective teacher with teaching qualification. Bursary conditional on commitment to Deaf Education sector. Applications accepted on rolling basis.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – South Morang Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Equity Scholarship to support families in need of a St Mary's education through financial assistance with tuition costs. This scholarship is available to students at all year levels and recognises families who would find it difficult to access primary or secondary education without financial support.
description: The Equity Scholarship assists families experiencing financial hardship with the cost associated with tuition. Scholarships cover between 50% and 90% of tuition fees depending on family income. Equity scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. Eligibility requires students with moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss, and either from a family experiencing financial hardship (families holding a means-tested Health Care Card and eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund qualify automatically for MACS Fee Concession Program), or families with combined income capped at $100,000 for non-Health Care Card holders. These scholarships are offered as a result of generous bequests made to the College.
eligibility_criteria: Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; From a family experiencing financial hardship; Health Care Card holders with CSEF eligibility automatically qualify for MACS Fee Concession Program; Combined family income capped at $100,000 for non-HCC holders; Open to students at all year levels; Must have potential to benefit from St Mary's program
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed depending on individual circumstances. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and remain enrolled at the school. Annual review is conducted to assess ongoing eligibility and need.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – South Morang Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Dominican Scholarship to Foundation/Prep students in recognition of commitment to the shared values of the school and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. This scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating commitment to community, service, study, and prayer.
description: The Foundation/Prep Dominican Scholarship covers 80% of tuition fees and is awarded for a period of 3 years. Dominican scholarships are awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and the Four Pillars of Dominican life, being community, service, study, and prayer. Eligibility requires moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss and alignment with the Dominican values and ethos of the school. Selection is based on the applicant demonstrating these values through their application materials and demonstrated conduct. Applications must be submitted by the specified deadline in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement.
eligibility_criteria: Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Demonstrating commitment to Dominican values and Four Pillars (community, service, study, prayer); Foundation/Prep entry level students; Must benefit from St Mary's program; Applicants must complete application demonstrating alignment with school values
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and ethos. Please see application forms for further details.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – South Morang Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Year 7 Dominican Scholarship to students entering secondary education who demonstrate commitment to the shared values of the school and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. The scholarship recognises students aligned with the Dominican tradition of community, service, study, and prayer.
description: The Year 7 Dominican Scholarship covers 33% of tuition fees (for secondary students) and is awarded for a period of 3 years. Dominican scholarships are awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of St Mary's College and the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer. Eligibility requires moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss and demonstrated alignment with the Catholic Dominican ethos and values of the school. Selection is based on the applicant demonstrating these values through their application materials and evidence of commitment. Applications must be submitted by the specified deadline in the year prior to Year 7 commencement.
eligibility_criteria: Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Demonstrating commitment to Dominican values and Four Pillars (community, service, study, prayer); Year 7 entry level students; Must benefit from St Mary's program; Applicants must complete application demonstrating alignment with school values; Currently enrolled in Year 6
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and ethos. Please see application forms for further details.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – South Morang Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Clare Winter Bursary to support students experiencing financial hardship. This bursary is in the gift of the Principal and enables students to access a St Mary's College education when prevented by financial difficulties.
description: A bursary has been set up at St Mary's College by the Dominican Sisters in recognition of the great friendship and links between the Dominican Sisters and Clare Winter, to enable a student to gain access to a St Mary's College education where this may have been prevented as a result of financial hardship. The bursary is in the gift of the Principal, meaning the Principal has discretion in allocating support. Eligible students must have moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss and demonstrate financial need or hardship that would otherwise prevent school access. The value and duration of support are determined by the Principal based on individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; Experiencing financial hardship that would prevent school access; Open to students at all year levels; Merit determined by Principal assessment
special_conditions: This bursary is in the gift of the Principal. Enquiries regarding allocation should be directed to the Principal. Award amount and duration are determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual circumstances and demonstrable financial need.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – South Morang Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the John Patterson Bursary (funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest) to support teachers undertaking specialist Deaf Education qualifications. This bursary supports teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education.
description: St Mary's College offers a bursary, funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest, to support teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. The purpose of this offering is to enhance the number and quality of teachers with Teacher of the Deaf qualifications who are available to work at St Mary's College as well as the wider Deaf Education sector. This is a professional development bursary aimed at teachers seeking to develop specialist expertise in Deaf Education. The value and duration of support are determined based on individual circumstances and the costs of the Master of Learning Intervention program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a qualified teacher; Must have completed Australian Catholic University or other teaching course; Must be accepted into or enrolled in Master of Learning Intervention program specialising in Deaf Education; Commitment to work in Deaf Education field; Financial need for postgraduate studies
special_conditions: This bursary is available to teachers seeking professional development in Deaf Education. Enquiries regarding the bursary should be directed to the Principal. Award amount varies based on program costs and individual circumstances. The bursary is designed to enhance teacher qualifications in the Deaf Education sector.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Sunbury Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Equity Scholarship to support families experiencing financial hardship in accessing a St Mary's College education. The scholarship is available to all year levels and is awarded based on family income to assist with tuition costs.
description: St Mary's College is committed to supporting families in need of a St Mary's education through the Equity Scholarship which assists with the cost associated with tuition. The scholarships cover between 50% and 90% of tuition fees depending on family income. Equity Scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. Applications are assessed based on family income and financial hardship circumstances, with families holding a means-tested Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for Camps, Sport and Excursion Fund (CSEF) qualifying automatically for the MACS Fee Concession Program. Non-Health Care Card holders must have a combined family income capped at $100,000. Submission requirements are clearly outlined in the application form.
eligibility_criteria: Students with a moderate to profound bilateral hearing loss; From a family experiencing financial hardship; Families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) and eligible for CSEF qualify automatically; Non-Health Care Card holders with combined family income capped at $100,000; Available to students at all year levels; Open to all campuses
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years and may be extended for a further 3 years if needed. Equity Scholarships are offered as a result of generous bequests made to the College. The College offers scholarships throughout the year but submissions should be received no later than November 28th in the year prior to year 7 commencement.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Sunbury Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Dominican Scholarship to Prep students in recognition of commitment to Dominican values and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. The scholarship recognises students who exemplify community, service, study, and prayer.
description: St Mary's College offers the Dominican Scholarship to Prep students who are awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of the school and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life, being community, service, study, and prayer. The scholarship covers 80% of tuition fees for primary students. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applicants are assessed on their demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and their personal qualities aligned with the school's spiritual and community ethos. Selection involves completion of an application form with supporting documentation as specified in the application guidelines. The scholarship recognises both academic merit and personal character.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Foundation or Prep; Demonstrated commitment to Dominican shared values; Demonstrated commitment to the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer; Must complete application form with supporting documentation
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Foundation/Prep commencement. Please see application forms for further details regarding assessment criteria and required documentation.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Sunbury Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Dominican Scholarship to Year 7 students in recognition of commitment to Dominican values and the Four Pillars of Dominican life. The scholarship recognises students who exemplify community, service, study, and prayer.
description: St Mary's College offers the Dominican Scholarship to Year 7 students who are awarded in recognition of commitment to the shared values of the school and/or the Four Pillars of Dominican life, being community, service, study, and prayer. The scholarship covers 33% of tuition fees for secondary students. Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applicants are assessed on their demonstrated commitment to Dominican values and their personal qualities aligned with the school's spiritual and community ethos. Selection involves completion of an application form with supporting documentation as specified in the application guidelines. The scholarship recognises both academic merit and personal character.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; Demonstrated commitment to Dominican shared values; Demonstrated commitment to the Four Pillars of Dominican life: community, service, study, and prayer; Must complete application form with supporting documentation; Applications accepted up until September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement
special_conditions: Dominican scholarships are awarded for a period of 3 years. Applications strictly close September 18th in the year prior to Year 7 commencement. Please see application forms for further details regarding assessment criteria and required documentation.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Sunbury Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the Clare Winter Bursary to enable students to gain access to a St Mary's College education where this may have been prevented as a result of financial hardship. The bursary is funded by a generous bequest from the Dominican Sisters.
description: Recognising the great friendship and links between the Dominican Sisters and Clare Winter, a bursary has been set up at St Mary's College by the Sisters to enable a student to gain access to a St Mary's College education where this may have been prevented as a result of financial hardship. The bursary is in the gift of the Principal, meaning it is awarded at the discretion of the school's leadership. The financial value and specific terms of the bursary are determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual circumstances and financial need. Recipients must meet the school's standard enrolment requirements and demonstrate genuine financial hardship that would otherwise prevent access to education.
eligibility_criteria: Students at any year level; Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must otherwise meet standard school enrolment requirements
special_conditions: The bursary is in the gift of the Principal. Enquiries regarding bursaries should be directed to the Principal.

school_name: St Mary’s College for the Deaf – Sunbury Campus
overview: St Mary's College offers the John Patterson Bursary to support teachers undertaking postgraduate qualifications in Deaf Education. Funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest, this bursary supports professional development in the field of Deaf Education.
description: St Mary's College offers the John Patterson Bursary, funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest, to support teachers who have completed an Australian Catholic University or other teaching course to undertake the Master of Learning Intervention, specialising in Deaf Education. The bursary is designed to facilitate professional development and advancement in the critical field of Deaf Education. This program demonstrates the school's commitment to building capacity in the deaf education sector by supporting qualified teachers to obtain specialised postgraduate qualifications. The financial value and specific terms are determined based on the applicant's circumstances and the scope of their postgraduate studies.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be qualified teachers; Must have completed an Australian Catholic University or other recognised teaching course; Seeking to undertake Master of Learning Intervention specialising in Deaf Education; Demonstrated commitment to career in Deaf Education
special_conditions: Funded from the Marjorie Oman Power Bequest. Enquiries regarding bursaries should be directed to the Principal.

overview: St Mary's Primary School offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme to support families experiencing financial need. The scheme provides a reduced fee contribution for eligible families who hold a current Health Care Card, enabling access to Catholic education.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme is administered through the Catholic Education Office in the Diocese of Ballarat and is available to families who hold a current Health Care Card. Eligible families pay a maximum of $520 per annum ($10 per week) towards school fees, covering the Family (School) Fee and the Capital Fee only. The Student Book Levy is charged separately. This scheme recognises that at times, some families are unable to meet their commitment to the full costs of education. As a Catholic school system, St Mary's ensures fee relief mechanisms are available to support families in time of need. The school encourages families to discuss their circumstances on enrolment or as needs emerge. Application forms and information brochures are available from the school office.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a current Health Care Card; Families who demonstrate genuine financial need; Open to Catholic and non-Catholic families
special_conditions: Families must hold a current Health Care Card. The $520 maximum annual contribution covers the Family (School) Fee and Capital Fee only; Student Book Levy is additional. Fee accounts are sent home at the beginning of Term 1. Flexible payment options available: weekly, fortnightly, monthly, per term, or annually via Cash, Direct Credit or Direct Debit. Direct Debit is the school's preferred payment option and allows for negotiated payment arrangements.

overview: St Mary's Primary School provides flexible payment options to help families manage school fees and levies. The school recognises that financial circumstances may vary and works with families to ensure fees are affordable and accessible.
description: St Mary's Primary School offers flexible fee payment options to support families in meeting their financial obligations. Fees can be paid weekly, fortnightly, monthly, per term, or annually through various payment methods including Cash, Direct Credit, or Direct Debit. Direct Debit is the school's preferred payment option as it allows for negotiated arrangements to be made. The school states that financial circumstances are never a barrier to enrolment and encourages families to discuss their needs on enrolment or as they emerge. Through flexible payment structures and negotiated payment arrangements, the school aims to make Catholic education accessible to all families in the community who support its ethos and values.
eligibility_criteria: All families enrolling at the school; Open to families seeking flexible payment arrangements; Available on an ongoing basis
special_conditions: Direct Debit is the school's preferred and usual payment option. Families may negotiate payment arrangements that suit their individual circumstances. Fee accounts are issued at the beginning of Term 1 and subsequently at the end of each term. All families are expected to make every effort to meet their financial commitments, with the understanding that no student will be excluded due to inability to pay.

program_name: Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited Health Care Card Fee Relief
overview: St Mary's School, as part of Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited (CESL), provides Health Care Card fee relief to eligible families. This government-supported financial assistance program reduces annual school fee contributions for families holding a valid Health Care Card.
description: Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited (CESL) offers Health Care Card fee relief to primary school families (Foundation to Year 6) holding a valid Health Care Card (HCC). The fee reduction significantly reduces annual school fee contributions, with eligible families reducing their annual fee contributions to a maximum of $540. The discount is automatically applied to school fee accounts once families provide a copy of their valid Health Care Card to the school office, complete a CSEF form (Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund), and set up a direct debit arrangement for fee payments. This is an ongoing annual program reviewed each year by CESL based on changes to government eligibility income thresholds. Families must maintain their direct debit arrangement to continue receiving this assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Health Care Card (HCC); Must be enrolled in Foundation to Year 6; Must provide copy of HCC to school office; Must complete CSEF eligibility form; Must establish and maintain direct debit arrangement for school fees; Annual review and reconfirmation of eligibility required
special_conditions: Automatic application upon provision of Health Care Card documentation and CSEF form completion. Discount automatically applied to direct debit school fee accounts. Annual review required to maintain eligibility. All fee concession details are treated in strictest confidence by the school. Eligible families may also access Victorian Government Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) providing $400 annual support.

overview: St Mary's Parish Primary School Ararat, as part of the Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education system, offers fee relief mechanisms to support families experiencing financial hardship. The Family Fee Assistance Scheme provides assistance to families who hold Health Care Cards and may be unable to meet the full cost of school fees.
description: The Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Limited (DOBCEL) ensures fee relief mechanisms are available to support families in time of need. Families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card are eligible to access the Family Fee Assistance Scheme and are asked to make only a small contribution to school fees. The scheme is part of a long-established diocesan policy that no family should be denied a Catholic education due to an inability to pay school fees. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to confidentially discuss their situation with school principals on enrolment or as needs emerge, so that appropriate fee arrangements can be put in place. The value and duration of assistance are assessed on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family circumstances and demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a means-tested Health Care Card; Families experiencing financial hardship; Genuine inability to meet full school fees; Open to all enrolled students at St Mary's Primary School
special_conditions: Families must provide evidence of Health Care Card eligibility or demonstrate genuine financial hardship. Assistance is reviewed regularly and adjusted as family circumstances change. Fee arrangements are confidential and handled on a case-by-case basis by the school principal. Sibling discounts are also available to families with multiple children enrolled in DOBCEL schools.

overview: St Mary's Catholic Primary School Colac offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme to provide fee relief for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The scheme is designed to ensure that Catholic education remains accessible to all families in the school community, regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme is available through Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Limited (DOBCEL) and provides concessional school fees to eligible families holding a valid Health Care Card. Families who qualify for the scheme pay a significantly reduced tuition fee (typically around $520 per annum or $10 per week for primary school tuition fees and capital levy, with curriculum levy, technology levy and excursion/camp costs added separately). The scheme is ongoing for the duration of enrolment, subject to annual review. Families experiencing difficulty meeting fee payments are encouraged to contact the school as soon as financial issues arise to discuss arrangements. Applications require completion of the Family Fee Assistance Scheme form and provision of evidence of Health Care Card eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid Health Care Card; Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Open to all year levels P-6; Must complete the Family Fee Assistance Scheme application form; Annual review of eligibility required
special_conditions: Scheme is for families holding a valid Health Care Card and in genuine financial hardship. Families should contact the school immediately if experiencing difficulty meeting fee payments. The school offers multiple payment methods including fortnightly, monthly, term-by-term and annual payment options to assist financial planning. Additional fees for optional extra-curricular activities such as private music lessons must still be paid separately.

overview: St Mary's School Echuca offers fee concessions through the Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited framework to support families facing financial hardship. Any family experiencing financial difficulties is strongly encouraged to apply for a fee concession to assist with school fee obligations.
description: St Mary's School participates in the Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited fee concession program, which provides financial assistance to families facing temporary or ongoing financial hardship. The concession process is transparent, fair and just, consistent with Catholic Social Teaching principles. Families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) or eligible for the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) are strongly encouraged to apply. The school operates under a fee structure that includes a family fee (paid once annually per family regardless of number of children), curriculum levy (covering books, stationary, ICT leases and maintenance), learning experience levy, and capital maintenance levy. Any family facing financial hardship can apply for a fee concession to support access to Catholic education. The approval of fee concessions is determined according to the Catholic Education Sandhurst Limited Delegation Policy, with processes designed to support access and equity priorities.
eligibility_criteria: Any family facing financial hardship; Families holding a Health Care Card (HCC) eligible for CSEF; Income assessment required; Application process available at time of enrolment or during schooling
special_conditions: All families are expected to meet their school fee obligations. Where parents have the financial means but refuse to pay school fees, the school will make every effort to help them understand their obligations and the impact of non-payment. Fee concessions must not be provided for Fulltime Fee-Paying International Students (FFPOS). The parental contribution to fees is assessed based on financial circumstances. All information provided by families through the application process is held in strict confidence.

overview: St Mary's School offers an Enrolment Scholarship to all new students joining the school. This scholarship provides financial assistance to families enrolling their children and helps make a Catholic education more accessible.
description: The Enrolment Scholarship provides new students with a $500 credit towards their school fees. This one-time scholarship is awarded to all families upon enrolment of a new student at St Mary's School, Clarkes Hill. The scholarship is applied directly to the student's account and reduces the annual school fees payable. Eligibility requires the student to be new to the school. The scholarship is processed automatically upon enrolment without requiring a separate application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students enrolling at St Mary's School Clarkes Hill; must be enrolling for the first time at the school
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded once per new enrolment and is non-refundable. It applies only to school tuition fees and cannot be transferred to other students.

overview: St Mary's School provides Family Fee Assistance to families holding a current Health Care Card or Pension Card. This program ensures no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial hardship.
description: Family Fee Assistance is available to families who hold a current Health Care Card or Pension Card. The school recognizes that families may experience financial difficulty and offers fee relief as part of its commitment to providing accessible Catholic education. Eligible families automatically qualify for assistance upon presentation of their current concession card. The level of assistance is determined by the school based on individual family circumstances. This program complements other payment arrangements and financial support options available through the school.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold a current Health Care Card or Pension Card; card must be in the name of a parent or guardian; card must be current and valid
special_conditions: Families must present current Health Care Card or Pension Card. Fee assistance level varies based on family circumstances and school resources. Annual review required to confirm continued eligibility. School offers fee relief for families experiencing financial hardship beyond concession card holders, determined on a case-by-case basis.

overview: The Cathedral Choral Scholarship is offered by St Patrick's Cathedral, Ballarat to students with musical interest and aptitude. This scholarship supports students who wish to participate in the Cathedral's Cantate Domino Choir and develop their musical talents.
description: The Cathedral Choral Scholarship provides an annual contribution of $1,000 towards the student's education and/or musical tuition. Students selected for this scholarship join the Cantate Domino Choir, which performs a broad range of musical repertoire including traditional sacred music and contemporary hymns. The scholarship includes opportunities for musical, vocal, and personal development in a supportive environment. Successful applicants are required to participate in weekly choir rehearsals and perform at two Masses per month during school term time. Auditions are held in Term 3, and awards are presented based on demonstrated musical aptitude.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 3-6; must demonstrate musical interest and aptitude; audition required; students from Catholic schools eligible
special_conditions: Successful applicants must commit to weekly rehearsals and perform at two Masses per month during term time. Scholarship is annual and subject to ongoing participation and musical commitment. Contact Cantate Domino Choir Director Mr Vincent Sully at vincenzo101923@gmail.com or 0407 310 115 for application forms and further information.

overview: St Mary's School awards the Peg Collery Memorial Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate school values in their words and actions. The scholarship honours students who exemplify respect, doing their best, and helping others to succeed.
description: The Peg Collery Memorial Scholarship is awarded to students at St Mary's School Hamilton who consistently demonstrate the school's core values of Respect, Do Your Best, and Help Others to Succeed in their daily interactions and conduct. Recipients are selected based on their exemplary character and positive contribution to the school community throughout the year. The scholarship recognises all-rounder students who lead by example and embody the Catholic values of the school. Multiple recipients are awarded annually, as evidenced by recent winners including students across different year levels. The scholarship value and specific financial benefit have not been publicly detailed on the school's website, though it represents recognition of exceptional personal and community values.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled at St Mary's School; Consistent demonstration of school values in words and actions; Positive contribution to school community; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Award presented at school assemblies. Recipients are selected by the school community based on demonstrated values alignment throughout the school year.

overview: St Mary's School offers the Family Fee Assistance Scheme to support families experiencing financial hardship. Families who hold a Health Care Card or Pension Card qualify for significantly reduced tuition fees, ensuring that no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial circumstances.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme is available to families at St Mary's School Hamilton who qualify based on holding a means-tested Health Care Card or Pension Card. Families enrolled in this scheme pay $520 per annum for primary school tuition fees, which covers tuition and capital fees only. This represents substantial savings compared to the standard family fee of $1,315 plus additional student and capital fees. The scheme is part of a long-established Diocese of Ballarat policy ensuring that no family is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay school fees. The application process requires completion of the Family Fee Assistance Application Form. Families not eligible for the Health Care Card discount may be able to explore confidential financial support arrangements on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a means-tested Health Care Card or Pension Card; Families experiencing financial hardship; Open to all enrolled students and prospective students
special_conditions: Qualification requires holding a Health Care Card or Pension Card. Discounted fee covers tuition and capital fees only, and does not cover additional charges such as camps, student fees, and excursions. Applications must be submitted before the school fee due date of 30 June. Regular payment plans may be negotiated.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy - Financial Hardship Assistance
overview: St Mary's School offers financial hardship assistance through the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) concessional fee policy. This program provides fee concessions to families experiencing genuine financial difficulty who would otherwise be unable to access a Catholic education at St Mary's School.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy for primary schools provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing financial hardship. Families who face genuine financial difficulty and would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education can apply for support. The policy recognises the dignity of the human person and the preferential option for the poor, ensuring no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial incapacity. Fee remissions vary based on individual family circumstances and are assessed confidentially. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school principal for a confidential discussion about available support options. The specific amount of assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis following a financial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to meet full school fee obligations; Application assessed on individual circumstances; Confidential financial assessment process required
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted confidentially through the school principal. Eligible families meet the financial hardship criteria as defined by MACS policy. Assessment is confidential between school and family. Fee concessions are reviewed and may vary year to year depending on family circumstances.

school_name: St Michael and John's Primary School
St Monica's College - St Stephen’s/Gaudete Campus
St Monica's College - Dalton Road Campus
overview: St Monica's College offers the HORIZONS Program as an accelerated learning pathway for gifted and talented students. The program is designed to cater to students who are highly able in their learning and encourages studying at an accelerated pace. HORIZONS students complete Years 7 to 10 in three years and may commence VCE one year early.
description: The HORIZONS Program is an accelerated pathway available to all Year 7 students at St Monica's College who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. From 2026 onwards, selection into the Year 7 HORIZONS Program takes place as part of the Grade 6 Testing Day, held in June each year. Students will complete this testing while they are in Grade 6, ensuring that all prospective students are considered through the standard testing process prior to Year 7. The program establishes a structured learning environment that allows highly able students to work with others of similar abilities at an accelerated pace. HORIZONS students complete Years 7 to 10 in three years and may commence VCE one year early, creating a myriad of options for students to maximise their performance in Year 12. A VCE mentor is provided to support students throughout their senior studies. No specific fee reduction is mentioned in available documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students applying for Year 7 at St Monica's College; Must be identified as highly able through Grade 6 testing; Selection occurs in June of Grade 6; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability
special_conditions: Selection into the Year 7 HORIZONS Program takes place as part of the Grade 6 Testing Day held in June each year. Students must commit to working at an accelerated pace. VCE mentor support is provided throughout senior studies. Scholarship is subject to maintenance of academic performance standards.

school_name: St Padre Pio Catholic Primary School

overview: St Monica's School offers significant fee discounts to families who hold a valid Health Care Card. This financial assistance program recognises families experiencing financial hardship and provides support to improve accessibility to Catholic education.
description: St Monica's Primary School provides substantial discounts on school fees for families who hold a current Health Care Card. To access these discounts, eligible families are required to provide proof of their Health Care Card status during the enrolment process or when contacting the school office. The assistance applies to tuition fees and helps reduce the financial burden on disadvantaged families. The school recognises the importance of removing financial barriers to education and offers this support as part of its commitment to serving the broader community. Families must present their current Health Care Card documentation to the school office to activate the discount. This program operates on an ongoing basis throughout the school year and may be reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Family holds a current Health Care Card; Students enrolled or enrolling at St Monica's School; Application of discount reviewed during enrolment or upon request
special_conditions: Families must provide evidence of a current Health Care Card. The discount amount is determined by the school based on individual family circumstances and may vary. Families are encouraged to contact the school office on 03 5447 7832 for specific information regarding discount amounts and application procedures.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School offers the MACS Concessional Fee Policy to provide reduced fees for eligible families facing financial barriers to Catholic education. The policy recognises families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent, families with government support cards, and families experiencing severe financial hardship.
description: The MACS Concessional Fee Policy, released in late 2021, provides reduced fees for three categories of families: those of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent, families with Health Care or DVA Cards, and families facing severe financial hardship. The concessional fee must be paid by direct debit and is not retrospective, with any existing debt standing in full. Camp fees are charged separately in addition to the concessional fee. Families must be entitled to the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) payment to be eligible for the concessional fee, with an April eligibility cutoff date. Interested families should contact the Finance Department early to request documentation and apply as soon as possible to avoid missing out on the program.
eligibility_criteria: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Families with Health Care Card or DVA Gold Card; Families facing severe financial hardship; Must be entitled to CSEF Payment with eligibility cut-off in April; Payment by direct debit required
special_conditions: Concessional fee must be paid by direct debit. Concessional fee is not retrospective and any existing debt stands in full. Camp fees do not qualify for concessional fee reduction and must be paid in addition to concessional fees. Eligibility for CSEF is essential; families who do not apply for CSEF cannot access concessional fee support.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School encourages eligible families to apply for the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), which provides government payments towards school fees to offset the cost of camps, sports activities and excursions. This program supports families with government support cards to access enrichment activities.
description: The CSEF is a State Government program that provides eligible families with a payment towards school fees to offset the cost of camps and excursions. Parents/carers/guardians who hold a Health Card, Aged Pension Card, DVA Gold Card or equivalent support card are encouraged to apply. The 2024 CSEF Application Form is available on the school website. The school emphasises that families must apply for CSEF as soon as possible to avoid missing out. CSEF eligibility is also a prerequisite for access to the school's Concessional Fee Policy. The application process is straightforward and handled through the Finance Department.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/carer/guardian holds a Health Card; Parent/carer/guardian holds Aged Pension Card; Parent/carer/guardian holds DVA Gold Card or equivalent support card; Application must be made before April eligibility cutoff date
special_conditions: Application deadline is typically April each year. Families who do not apply for CSEF will not be eligible for Concessional Fee support. CSEF payment is made towards school fees specifically to offset camps and excursions costs.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School participates in the State Government's Conveyance Allowance program, which provides families in regional areas with financial support to cover the cost of transporting children to school. This program assists families living beyond the prescribed distance threshold who choose St Patrick's as their closest Catholic primary school.
description: The State Government's Conveyance Allowance program provides families with financial support to offset the cost of transporting children to school in regional areas. Families living more than 4.8 kilometres from St Patrick's Primary School and for whom St Patrick's is the closest Catholic primary school may be eligible for this program. For families using car travel, the assistance is paid directly to the school against school fees. For families using bus travel to Assumption College, the payment is made to Assumption College against bus fees. This program recognises the regional location of the school and supports equitable access to Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Family lives more than 4.8km from St Patrick's Primary School; St Patrick's must be the closest Catholic primary school to the family's residence; Family is using either car or bus transport to school
special_conditions: Assistance is paid to the school against school fees for car travel, or to Assumption College against bus fees for bus travel. Applicants must verify that St Patrick's is their closest Catholic primary school. Program is administered by the State Government.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School offers Hardship Fee Assistance to families experiencing temporary or unexpected financial difficulties. The school recognises that families may face financial challenges at various times and provides confidential support to ensure cost is not a barrier to Catholic education.
description: St Patrick's Primary School operates a Hardship Fee Assistance program as part of its commitment to ensuring that financial hardship does not prevent families from accessing Catholic education. The school provides flexible payment plans and fee assistance as part of a confidential support process. Families facing financial challenges are encouraged to contact the school early to discuss their situation. All information is treated with strict confidentiality, with the least number of people involved as possible. The school works directly with families to develop individualised solutions that may include negotiated payment plans, alternative payment arrangements, or direct fee assistance. This assistance can be provided in conjunction with government assistance, concessional fees, or as a standalone support mechanism.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Willingness to contact school early for discussion; All families welcome to apply regardless of income level
special_conditions: All information is treated with strict confidentiality. School encourages families to contact early to have a discussion. Payment arrangements must typically be by direct debit where possible. The school may work with families on alternative arrangements case-by-case. School operates on the principle that cost should not be a barrier to Catholic education for baptised students.

program_name: St Patrick's Primary Koroit Scholarship Awards
overview: St Patrick's Primary Koroit offers six scholarship awards totaling $2,000 per year to support students transitioning to secondary school. These scholarships recognize exceptional students completing their primary education and provide financial assistance toward school fees and costs in their chosen secondary school.
description: St Patrick's Primary Koroit offers six scholarship awards annually with a combined value of $2,000 per year, comprising two scholarships worth $500 each and four scholarships worth $250 each. The awards are designed to support Grade 6 students as they transition to secondary education. Each successful recipient will have the scholarship award transferred directly to their chosen secondary school of enrolment to support school fees and costs. Application forms are distributed to Grade 6 students, with additional copies available by email request. The scholarships assist families with the financial burden of transitioning to secondary school.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Grade 6 student at St Patrick's Primary Koroit; application form must be submitted by specified deadline; award is transferred to chosen secondary school of enrolment for 2025
special_conditions: Applications are sent home with Grade 6 students. Electronic copies can be requested by emailing kdobson@spkoroit.catholic.edu.au. Awards total $2,000 per year distributed as: 2 scholarships at $500 each and 4 scholarships at $250 each. Award is transferred to the student's chosen secondary school of enrolment.

overview: St Patrick's School Gordon provides financial assistance to eligible families through the Catholic Education Office's Family Fee Assistance Scheme. This scheme offers fee relief to families holding a valid means-tested Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card. The program recognises families experiencing financial difficulty and helps make Catholic education more accessible.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme is a diocesan program administered through the Catholic Education Office that provides eligible families with a credit towards their school fees. Families who hold a valid means-tested Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card are eligible to apply. For the 2025 school year, eligible families received a credit of $1,050.00 against the combined Family Fee and Capital Fee of $1,570.00, reducing their balance to $350.00. Additionally, the State Government provides a Camp, Sports and Excursion Fund credit of $150.00. The application process involves providing proof of eligibility through your relevant concession card. No specific annual deadline is published, but families are encouraged to contact the school office confidentially to discuss their financial circumstances. There are no financial thresholds or academic requirements; eligibility is based solely on holding appropriate concession documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of valid means-tested Health Care Card; OR Holder of Pensioner Concession Card; OR Holder of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card; Contact school office for application process
special_conditions: Families must hold current, valid concession card documentation. Credits are applied annually and subject to ongoing eligibility verification. Victorian Government Camp, Sports and Excursion Fund of $150.00 may also apply. Application can be made at any time; families are encouraged to discuss financial difficulties confidentially with the school principal.

overview: St Patrick's School Gordon students from eligible families can access the Victorian Government's Camp, Sports and Excursion Fund. This program provides financial support specifically for camps, sports participation, and school excursion costs, helping remove financial barriers to student participation in co-curricular activities.
description: The Camp, Sports and Excursion Fund is a Victorian Government initiative that provides funding for eligible families to support their children's participation in school camps, sports activities, and excursions. For the 2025 school year, eligible families receive a credit of $150.00 towards these activities. This fund complements the Family Fee Assistance Scheme and is available to families holding a valid means-tested Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card. The purpose is to ensure that financial hardship does not prevent students from engaging in enriching educational experiences and developing their full potential through participation in co-curricular activities. Families should contact the school to claim this benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of valid means-tested Health Care Card; OR Holder of Pensioner Concession Card; OR Holder of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Card; Application through school office or Victorian Government portal
special_conditions: Funds are specifically designated for camps, sports and excursion costs only. Families must hold current concession documentation. Credit is applied annually and subject to ongoing eligibility verification. School office can provide advice on claiming and using this benefit for specific activities.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School Tongala provides significant fee reduction support for families holding a valid Health Care Card. The reduction is automatically applied to the school fee account once families provide proof of their HCC, complete a CSEF form, and establish a direct debit arrangement.
description: Families holding a valid Health Care Card (HCC) are eligible for a significant reduction in school fees for the upcoming year including all fees and levies. The discount will be automatically applied to the school fee account once families provide a copy of the HCC to the school office, complete a CSEF form, and set up a direct debit arrangement. If families have already submitted their HCC, completed the CSEF form, and have a direct debit in place, no further action is needed as the discount will be applied automatically. This ongoing assistance ensures that families experiencing financial hardship can access quality Catholic education without additional burden.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Health Care Card (HCC); Must provide a copy of HCC to school office; Must complete a CSEF form; Must set up direct debit arrangement; Applies to all year levels at the school
special_conditions: Discount is applied automatically once required documentation is submitted and direct debit is established. Families experiencing financial hardship should contact the Principal to discuss availability of further assistance.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School recognises that financial hardship may arise for families and offers discretionary support to assist those experiencing genuine financial difficulties. Families are encouraged to contact the Principal to discuss their circumstances and work toward a resolution.
description: St Patrick's Primary School Tongala provides discretionary financial assistance for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The school understands that financial circumstances can change and impact families' ability to contribute to school fees. Families who face financial difficulties are encouraged to promptly make contact with the Principal to work toward a resolution and discuss the availability of further assistance. The school operates in partnership with families to ensure that no child's education is compromised by their family's financial situation. Support may include flexible payment arrangements or additional fee relief beyond the standard HCC program.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; All year levels; Must contact Principal to discuss circumstances; No specific income threshold stated but assessed on individual family circumstances
special_conditions: Families must contact the Principal promptly to discuss financial hardship. Support is provided at the Principal's discretion on a case-by-case basis. The school emphasises working with families toward a resolution rather than refusing enrolment based on financial inability to pay fees.

school_name: St Paul the Apostle Catholic Primary School
St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding achievement in students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The scholarship supports students who are intentional about their learning and strive to extend their minds.
description: Academic Scholarships at St Paul's are offered to students who achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations. Scholarship recipients must have a genuine commitment to their learning and be hard-working students of remarkable character keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. The financial award is subject to annual review and scholarships may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of a student's primary or secondary education. Selection is based on the applicant's performance in the external Edutest examination, which serves as the primary assessment tool for identifying academically exceptional students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students new to St Paul's for entry from Years 3 to 11; Current students may apply for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10; Must achieve excellent results in external Edutest scholarship examinations; Must have genuine commitment to learning and be of remarkable character; Must be keen to make valuable contribution to school community
special_conditions: Scholarship holders are expected to remain at St Paul's for the duration of the scholarship term. Scholarships are subject to annual review. The amount of the scholarship remains confidential between the family and the school. Scholarship holders may apply for more than one type of scholarship but can only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time. The school reserves the right not to offer a scholarship if the standard of the candidate does not warrant it.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's recognises and empowers students with exceptional passion and a commitment to pursuing their purpose through Personal Excellence Scholarships. These scholarships reward students who demonstrate potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen interest or discipline fields such as sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness.
description: Personal Excellence Scholarships are offered to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen interest/discipline fields including sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness. These are hard-working students of excellent character who are keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. For Full Performing Arts Scholarships, recipients also receive 32 free instrumental/drama lessons (30 minutes each) per year in addition to the tuition fee reduction. Scholarships are subject to annual review and may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of a student's primary or secondary education. Selection is based on demonstration of passion, talent and commitment in the chosen discipline.
