eligibility_criteria: Open to students new to St Paul's for entry from Years 7 to 11; Current students may apply for entry into Year 7; Must demonstrate potential to be outstanding contributor in chosen field; Must demonstrate genuine commitment and/or considerable accomplishment in discipline area; Must be hard-working student of excellent character; Must have desire to participate in environment where learning is valued
special_conditions: Scholarships are held whilst the student is actively involved in the relevant activity. Scholarship holders are expected to remain at St Paul's for the duration of the scholarship term. Scholarships are subject to annual review. The amount of the scholarship remains confidential between the family and the school. Scholarship holders may apply for more than one type of scholarship but can only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time. Performance requirements and commitment expectations vary by discipline. The school reserves the right to withdraw a scholarship if the student is no longer actively involved in the relevant activity.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's offers VCE Scholarships to recognise and support high-achieving students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 at the secondary level. These scholarships reward students demonstrating exceptional academic potential and commitment to excellence in VCE study.
description: VCE Scholarships are available to students new to St Paul's for entry in Years 10, 11 or 12. These scholarships are offered to students who achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations and demonstrate a genuine commitment to their learning. They support hard-working students of remarkable character who are keen to make valuable contributions to the school community. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. VCE Scholarship awards are subject to annual review and may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of the student's secondary education. Selection is based on performance in the external Edutest examination.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only for entry in Years 10, 11 or 12; Must achieve excellent results in external Edutest scholarship examinations; Must have genuine commitment to learning and be of remarkable character; Must be keen to make valuable contribution to school community; Applicable only to Traralgon Campus VCE program
special_conditions: Scholarship holders are expected to remain at St Paul's for the duration of the scholarship term. Scholarships are subject to annual review. The amount of the scholarship remains confidential between the family and the school. Only new students are eligible for VCE Scholarships. Students can only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time. The school reserves the right not to offer a scholarship if the standard of the candidate does not warrant it.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's is pleased to offer Foundation Scholarships to new students for entry in Years 8 to 11 in 2026 as part of its Education Support Program. These scholarships are offered under the School's Education Support Program and provide places to students continuing through to Year 12 at the Traralgon Campus.
description: Foundation Scholarships are offered to dedicated and capable students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but for financial or other circumstances may be unable to do so without assistance. These scholarships are offered under the School's Education Support Program exclusively for the Traralgon Campus and for entry in Years 8 to 11 in 2026. Recipients are expected to continue through to Year 12. Such students must have a genuine desire to achieve their potential, be committed to their studies, be a positive influence on those around them and have the desire to participate in an environment where learning is valued and respected. The financial value and specific benefits of Foundation Scholarships are determined on a case-by-case basis in accordance with assessed need and circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students for entry in Years 8, 9, 10, 11 in 2026 only; Must demonstrate genuine desire to achieve potential; Must be committed to studies and positive influence in school community; Must wish to continue through to Year 12 at Traralgon Campus; Must be capable student who would benefit from St Paul's education; Financial or other circumstances must make assistance necessary
special_conditions: Offered for Traralgon Campus only. Recipients expected to continue to Year 12. Application involves assessment of financial circumstances and capability. Personal interview or meetings with school leadership likely required. Confidentiality applies to scholarship amounts. Award contingent on continued demonstrated commitment and positive contribution.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Academic Scholarships to reward students who achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and a genuine commitment to their learning.
description: The Academic Scholarship is offered to students who achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations. Scholarship recipients must have a genuine commitment to their learning and be a hard-working student of remarkable character who is keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. New students may apply for Academic Scholarships for entry from Years 3 to 11, and current students may apply for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10. Scholarships may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of a student's primary or secondary education. All scholarships are subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 3 to 11 or current students entering Years 5, 7, 10; Must achieve excellent results in external Edutest examination; Genuine commitment to learning; Hard-working student of remarkable character; Keen to make valuable contribution to school community
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress and take an active part in co-curricular activities. Behaviour must be consistent with the ethos and aims of the school. Junior Academic Scholarships are for the duration of the recipient's Junior School education; applicants must apply separately for a Secondary Academic Scholarship when in Year 6. Students may only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Personal Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen interest or discipline fields. These scholarships recognise excellence across sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness.
description: The Personal Excellence Scholarship is offered to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen interest/discipline fields such as sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness. Applicants are hard-working students of excellent character who are keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. New students may apply for Personal Excellence scholarships for entry from Years 7 to 11, and current students may also apply for entry into Years 7 and 10. All scholarships are subject to annual review and recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress and take active roles in relevant co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 7 to 11 or current students entering Years 7 and 10; Demonstration of potential in chosen discipline field (sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership, or environmental awareness); Hard-working student of excellent character; Keen to make valuable contribution to school community
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress and take active parts in co-curricular activities relevant to their area of excellence. Behaviour must be consistent with the ethos and aims of the school. Assessment includes portfolio, audition, or interview as appropriate to the discipline. Students may only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: St Paul's offers VCE Scholarships for secondary students entering Years 10, 11 or 12. These scholarships are available only to new students and recognise academic excellence at the senior secondary level.
description: The VCE Scholarship is available only to new students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 at St Paul's. Applicants must achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations and demonstrate a genuine commitment to their learning. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees and may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of a student's secondary education. All VCE Scholarships are subject to annual review. Recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress, take an active part in various co-curricular activities offered at the school, and demonstrate behaviour consistent with the school's ethos and aims.
eligibility_criteria: New students only for entry into Years 10, 11 or 12; Must achieve excellent results in external Edutest examination; Genuine commitment to learning; Hard-working student of remarkable character; Keen to make valuable contribution to school community
special_conditions: Available only to new students. VCE Scholarships are only available for entry in Years 10, 11 or 12. Recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress and take an active part in co-curricular activities. All scholarships are subject to annual review. Students may only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Performing Arts Scholarships to students who demonstrate excellence in music, drama, or dance. These scholarships provide both financial assistance and complimentary instrumental or drama lessons.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship is available to students demonstrating excellence in performing arts disciplines such as music, drama or dance. Full Performing Arts Scholarships include 32 free instrumental or drama lessons of 30 minutes duration per year and up to 50% reduction off the annual tuition fees. Students must demonstrate genuine commitment to their chosen performing arts discipline and be hard-working individuals of excellent character. The scholarship may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of a student's primary or secondary education. All scholarships are subject to annual review and recipients are expected to maintain high academic progress while taking active roles in performing arts activities.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstration of excellence in music, drama or dance; Hard-working student of excellent character; Genuine commitment to chosen performing arts discipline; Keen to make valuable contribution to school community through their arts participation
special_conditions: Full Performing Arts Scholarships include 32 free instrumental or drama lessons of 30 minutes duration per year in addition to fee reduction. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing excellence and commitment to their performing arts discipline. All scholarships are subject to annual review. Students may only hold one St Paul's scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Foundation Scholarships at the Traralgon Campus to new students entering Years 8 to 11 in 2026. These scholarships are offered under the school's Education Support Program to support students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but may face financial or other circumstances that would otherwise prevent them from accessing the school.
description: The Foundation Scholarship is offered under the School's Education Support Program and is available only at the Traralgon Campus for new students entering Years 8 to 11 in 2026. The scholarship provides places to students continuing through to Year 12 at the Traralgon Campus. This program is designed to invite applications from dedicated and capable students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but, for financial or other circumstances, may be unable to do so without assistance. Applicants should have a genuine desire to achieve their potential, be committed to their studies, be a positive influence on those around them, and have the desire to participate in an environment where learning is valued and respected. The specific financial value will be discussed with successful applicants.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 8 to 11 for the Traralgon Campus in 2026; Must intend to continue through to Year 12; Genuine desire to achieve potential; Committed to studies; Positive influence on those around them; Desire to participate in learning-focused environment; Students who would benefit from attending but face financial or other circumstances preventing access
special_conditions: Foundation Scholarships are available only at the Traralgon Campus. Students must be committed to continuing through to Year 12 at that campus. Applications are assessed through the Education Support Program process. Contact the school's Scholarships Officer on 5623 5833 for more details and to complete an application form.

overview: St Patrick's School Camperdown offers the Family Assistance Scheme (FAS) to make Catholic education more accessible for families in financial need. This scheme, introduced by the Catholic Education Office, provides eligible families with significant fee reductions based on demonstrated financial hardship and government support eligibility.
description: The Family Assistance Scheme is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship in accessing Catholic primary education. Eligible families holding a Commonwealth Health Care Card can access reduced tuition fees of $10 per week or $520 per year, which covers tuition and capital fees. This represents a substantial reduction from standard student fees ranging from $400-$500 annually. To be eligible, applicants must be either a parent or guardian of a primary or secondary school student up to age sixteen, and must be an eligible beneficiary of a Centrelink pension, allowance or benefit (within the meaning of the State Concessions Act 2004), or be a Veterans Affairs (TPI) pensioner, or be foster parents. Applications must be submitted to the school by the due date (typically end of February), and families must provide a photocopy of their Commonwealth Health Care Card. Renewal is automatic for families who received the scheme in the previous year.
eligibility_criteria: Be either a parent or guardian of a primary or secondary school student, up to age sixteen; Be an eligible beneficiary of a Centrelink pension, allowance or benefit under the State Concessions Act 2004 or be a Veterans Affairs (TPI) pensioner or be foster parents; Hold a current Commonwealth Health Care Card; Submit application by the school deadline; Applications are processed annually with automatic renewal for returning recipients
special_conditions: Families must provide a photocopy of their Commonwealth Health Care Card to the school office. Automatic renewal applies for returning recipients; new applications required annually for new families. No late applications are accepted. This scheme is administered by the Catholic Education Office (Diocese of Ballarat Catholic Education Limited) and is available across all Catholic primary schools in the Diocese.

overview: St Patrick's School Camperdown assists families with transportation costs through the Conveyance Allowance program. This government-supported scheme reimburses families for costs incurred when transporting children to and from school, particularly for rural and remote access.
description: The Conveyance Allowance is a government assistance program that helps families with the cost of transporting their children to and from school. Families whose private vehicle travels more than 4.8km to pick up or drop off students at a Contract or MRC bus pick-up point can apply for reimbursement through the Private Car Conveyance Allowance application. For students who travel on St Patrick's School or Mercy Regional College owned or chartered buses, the school makes a claim to the government for Conveyance Allowance on behalf of parents, which helps offset some of the cost of chartering buses. The specific financial benefit varies depending on the distance traveled and government reimbursement rates, which may change annually. Parents should contact the school office to determine their eligibility and the applicable reimbursement amount for their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Live more than 4.8km from school or designated bus pick-up points; Reside within the catchment area served by St Patrick's School or Mercy Regional College buses; Be eligible for government Conveyance Allowance under current legislation
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted to the school office. Families driving more than 4.8km to bus pick-up points must complete the Private Car Conveyance Allowance application. Families using school or MRC owned/chartered buses do not need to apply; the school makes the government claim on their behalf. Reimbursement amounts are determined by government rates and distance traveled.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Traralgon Campus
overview: St Paul's offers the Academic Scholarship to students who achieve excellent results in the external Edutest scholarship examinations. The scholarship recognises students with genuine commitment to their learning and hard work who are keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to new and current students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance through Edutest examination results. Successful applicants will receive up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. The selection process includes completion of the external Edutest scholarship examinations, which must be sat at either the Warragul or Traralgon Campus on the published test date. New students may apply for entry from Years 3 to 11, while current students may apply for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10. Scholarships are subject to annual review and may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Achieve excellent results in external Edutest scholarship examinations; Demonstrate genuine commitment to learning; Be a hard-working student of remarkable character; Keen to contribute to school community; New students eligible for Years 3-11 entry; Current students eligible for Years 5, 7, 10 entry; All applicants must sit Edutest examination; No alternate test dates offered
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review. Scholarship recipients are expected to consent to photo permission for school promotion. Minimum attendance expectations and satisfactory academic progress required. While students may apply for multiple scholarships, they can only hold one scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Traralgon Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Personal Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen field of interest. These scholarships recognise excellence in sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness.
description: Personal Excellence Scholarships are designed for applicants who demonstrate potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen discipline such as sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership, or environmental awareness. Successful applicants receive up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. The selection process requires completion of Edutest registration (fee: $110, non-refundable) and submission of supporting documentation via online portal. Full Performing Arts Scholarships include 32 free instrumental or drama lessons (30 minutes each) per year plus fee reduction. New students may apply for Personal Excellence Scholarships for entry from Years 7 to 11. Scholarships are subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate potential in chosen field of sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership, or environmental awareness; Be a hard-working student of excellent character; Keen to contribute to school community; New students eligible for Years 7-11 entry; All applicants must sit Edutest examination; Performing arts applicants must submit audition recording; No alternate test dates offered
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review. Recipients must maintain involvement in and achievement level in the field for which scholarship was awarded. Full Performing Arts Scholarships include 32 free instrumental/drama lessons per year. Performing Arts applicants must submit audition recording. School offers either Full or Co-curricular performing arts scholarships at discretion. Successful Performing Arts applicants may be invited to join St Paul's Academy of Music. While students may apply for multiple scholarships, they can only hold one scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Traralgon Campus
overview: St Paul's offers VCE Scholarships to new students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 who demonstrate exceptional academic potential. These scholarships support students to excel in their VCE studies at the Traralgon Campus.
description: VCE Scholarships are available exclusively to new students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 and provide up to 50% reduction of annual tuition fees. The selection process requires completion of Edutest registration (fee: $110, non-refundable) and submission of supporting documentation via online portal. All applicants must sit the external Edutest examination at either Warragul or Traralgon Campus on the published test date. No alternate examination dates are offered. These scholarships are particularly designed for the Traralgon Campus as it expands its VCE offering. Scholarships are subject to annual review and may be offered for a specified number of years or until the end of secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Eligible for entry into Years 10, 11 or 12; Achieve excellent results in external Edutest scholarship examinations; Demonstrate genuine commitment to learning; Be a hard-working student of remarkable character; Keen to contribute to school community; All applicants must sit Edutest examination; No alternate test dates offered
special_conditions: Scholarships are subject to annual review. VCE Scholarships available to new students only for entry into Years 10, 11 or 12. Recipients are expected to consent to photo permission for school promotion. Satisfactory academic progress required. While students may apply for multiple scholarships, they can only hold one scholarship at any given time.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Traralgon Campus
overview: St Paul's offers Foundation Scholarships under its Education Support Program to students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but may be unable to do so without financial or other assistance. These scholarships are offered to new students entering Years 8 to 11 in 2026 at the Traralgon Campus, with continuation through to Year 12.
description: Foundation Scholarships are offered under the School's Education Support Program to dedicated and capable students who would otherwise be unable to access a St Paul's education due to financial or other circumstances. These scholarships provide places for students continuing through to Year 12 at the Traralgon Campus. Students must be genuinely committed to achieving their potential, dedicated to their studies, be a positive influence on those around them, and have a desire to participate in an environment where learning is valued and respected. The selection process involves application through the Education Support Program. Scholarships are subject to annual review. For specific details on financial benefit and application process, contact the Scholarships Officer.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 8 to 11 in 2026; Applicable to Traralgon Campus only; Would benefit from attending St Paul's but unable to do so without financial or other assistance; Genuine desire to achieve their potential; Committed to their studies; Positive influence on those around them; Desire to participate in learning-focused environment; Must continue through to Year 12
special_conditions: Limited to Traralgon Campus. Students must continue through to Year 12. Scholarships subject to annual review. Contact school for specific application details and financial benefit information.

program_name: MACS Concessional Fee Policy for Financial Hardship
overview: St Peter's School Bentleigh East provides fee concessions to families of students experiencing financial hardship under the Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools Concessional Fee Policy. This policy ensures that families can meet school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay, consistent with Catholic social teaching principles of dignity and preferential option for the poor.
description: Under the MACS Concessional Fee Policy, St Peter's School Bentleigh East offers fee remission or exemption to families experiencing genuine financial hardship who would otherwise be impeded from receiving a Catholic education. The policy recognises that families must meet their school fee obligations according to their capacity to pay. Eligible families may receive fee concessions including remission or exemption from school fees. The assessment considers income, assets, savings, and necessary outgoings. Eligible card holders include holders of Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card, DVA Health Card - All Conditions, or Totally & Permanently Incapacitated status. Families receiving the Coronavirus Supplement or other Australian Government financial assistance may also be considered. Fee concessions are determined on a case-by-case basis and administered confidentially by the school principal in accordance with MACS policy.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated inability to pay school fees in full; Australian Government financial assistance recipients; Department of Veterans' Affairs Gold Card or Health Card holders; Families with limited income and assets; Part-time home school students also eligible for pro-rata concessions
special_conditions: Fee concessions are based on genuine financial hardship assessment. Applications are handled confidentially by the school principal. Pro-rata concession is provided for part-year attendance. Annual statutory declaration may be required. School reserves right to adjust concession levels if family financial circumstances change. Policy applies to all MACS primary schools consistently.

school_name: St Peter's College - Clyde North Campus

overview: St Richard's Primary School provides concessional school fees to eligible families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The policy outlines mechanisms for fee remission and exemption based on financial need and circumstances.
description: St Richard's Primary School operates a formal Concessional Fee Policy (A54, updated 21.10.21) to assist families who may experience genuine financial hardship in meeting school fees. The policy provides fee concessions and remissions for eligible families. As a Melbourne Archdiocese Catholic Schools (MACS) member school, the policy aligns with MACS standards for financial assistance. Eligible applicants may qualify for partial or full fee remission. The principal has delegated authority to approve concessional fee applications. Annual statutory declarations are typically required to confirm ongoing eligibility. Pro-rata concessions are provided for part-year attendance where applicable. Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the school's office directly.
eligibility_criteria: Genuine financial hardship; Families unable to meet full school fees; Submission of statutory declaration confirming financial circumstances; Ongoing eligibility confirmation required annually
special_conditions: Applications must be made on the official St Richard's Primary School Concessional Fee Application Form. An annual statutory declaration is required to confirm continued eligibility for the concession. Automated payment plans are established when applications are approved. Families must maintain confidentiality regarding their concessional fee status.

overview: St Scholastica's offers the Maltby Scholarship to assist students who need support with school fees, uniforms or books. The scholarship was established by Fr. Rom Murphy in honour of Fr. Maltby and recognises students requiring financial assistance who demonstrate commitment to parish involvement.
description: The Maltby Scholarship was set up to assist in the payment of school fees at St Scholastica's School or payment for school uniform or books for a student who needed support. In return, recipients are required to show their support back through involvement in the parish. This scholarship provides financial relief for families experiencing difficulties meeting the costs of Catholic education, including tuition, uniforms, and educational materials. The scholarship is restricted to Year 5 students who are progressing to Year 6 the following year, ensuring it targets students at a specific transition point in their primary education journey.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 students going into Year 6 the following year; Must demonstrate financial need; Must show commitment to parish involvement
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must demonstrate support back to the parish through involvement. Financial support extends to school fees, uniform costs, or books as determined by the school.

overview: St Scholastica's provides fee reductions for families who hold a valid Australian Healthcare Card. This concession recognises families experiencing financial hardship and makes Catholic education more accessible to disadvantaged families.
description: St Scholastica's School offers a reduction in school fees for families who hold a valid Healthcare Card or concession card, subject to eligibility verification. The school recognises that some families face financial barriers to accessing education and is committed to ensuring these families are not excluded based on affordability concerns. Families are encouraged to discuss their financial situation with the Principal, and the school commits to treating all fee concession requests with dignity and privacy. This program operates in conjunction with the school's flexible payment options policy, which allows families to arrange payment schedules suited to their circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold a valid Australian Healthcare Card or concession card; Subject to eligibility verification; All year levels welcome to apply
special_conditions: Families will not be excluded from school if there is concern about affordability. Applicants must discuss directly with the Principal. At all times, the dignity and privacy of those seeking a fee concession will be respected. Flexible payment options are available to support family situations.

overview: St Scholastica's commits to ensuring families experiencing financial hardship are not excluded from enrolment. The school offers flexible payment arrangements and fee reductions to families who discuss their circumstances with the Principal.
description: St Scholastica's School operates a financial hardship support program for families experiencing affordability concerns. The school's explicit commitment states that families will not be excluded from enrolment due to financial concerns. Parents are encouraged to speak with the Principal confidentially to discuss flexible payment options, fee reductions, or other arrangements tailored to their family's financial situation. The school also accepts multiple payment methods including cash, credit card, and direct deposit to accommodate different family preferences. This holistic approach to financial support recognises that some families may require assistance across multiple years of their child's education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Must demonstrate financial need or concern about affordability; Must contact the Principal to discuss arrangements
special_conditions: Families must initiate discussion with the Principal regarding financial concerns. Flexible payment options available tailored to family circumstances. Dignity and privacy of all families seeking assistance is guaranteed. Arrangements are flexible and individualised based on need.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School awards the Year 5 Effort and Curriculum Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate consistent effort across all areas of the curriculum throughout the school year. This scholarship acknowledges academic commitment and dedication to learning.
description: This scholarship is awarded annually to a Year 5 student who has shown consistent effort across all areas of the curriculum throughout the school year. The scholarship recognises sustained engagement with learning in literacy, numeracy, science and all other curriculum areas. The scholarship appears to be awarded without specific financial details mentioned in available school publications. Recipients are announced during school assemblies and are recognised in the school newsletter. Selection is based on demonstrated effort and commitment across the entire curriculum rather than achievement in specific subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled in Year 5; Must demonstrate consistent effort across all curriculum areas throughout the school year
special_conditions: Recipients are announced at end-of-year school assembly. Scholarship is honour-based recognition of effort rather than financial support.

overview: St Patrick's Primary School acknowledges support from the Port Fairy Community Bank, which provides scholarships to eligible students through an application process. The school recognises the generosity of this community organisation in supporting student achievement.
description: The Port Fairy Community Bank provides scholarships to students at St Patrick's Primary School through a formal application process. Recipients are selected based on applications submitted to the school. The specific financial value of these scholarships is not detailed in available school publications. Recipients are announced and presented with scholarships during school events, with Community Bank representatives participating in the presentation. This is an external scholarship programme managed by the Port Fairy Community Bank in partnership with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at St Patrick's Primary School; Must submit a formal application; Selection criteria determined by Port Fairy Community Bank
special_conditions: Administered by Port Fairy Community Bank in partnership with the school. Presented by Community Bank Board representatives during school events.

program_name: Port Fairy Community Bank Writing Award
overview: St Patrick's Primary School awards the Port Fairy Community Bank Writing Award to students who demonstrate academic excellence and consistent effort in writing throughout the year. This award recognises achievement in the writing subject area.
description: The Port Fairy Community Bank Writing Award is presented to students who have shown academic excellence and consistent effort throughout the year in writing. Selection is based on demonstrated writing skills, effort and application. The award is presented during school assemblies and is sponsored by the Port Fairy Community Bank. Specific financial value is not detailed in school publications. This award recognises achievement in writing as a core curriculum area and is aimed at encouraging continued excellence in literacy.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at St Patrick's Primary School; Demonstrated academic excellence in writing; Shown consistent effort in writing throughout the year
special_conditions: Sponsored by Port Fairy Community Bank and Port Fairy Folk Festival Committee. Presented at school assembly.

school_name: St Teresa of Kolkata Catholic Primary School

overview: St Peter's School offers concessional school fees to eligible families experiencing financial hardship or meeting specific criteria. The program provides fee reduction assistance to ensure access to Catholic education for disadvantaged families.
description: St Peter's School Concessional Fee Policy assists families who meet specific eligibility criteria and are unable to pay full school fees. Eligible families receive a concessional fee reduction on annual school fees of $1,410 per family. The concession applies to the tuition fee only and does not include outdoor education, swimming, excursions, incursions, sacraments, interschool sport, or graduation charges. Any offer of concession is subject to families entering into a Direct Debit Service Agreement or Centrepay Deduction arrangement. Default in payment may result in the concession being withdrawn. Applications are processed by the school office and families must meet one or more eligibility criteria including Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage, holding a Health Care Card, experiencing severe financial hardship, holding a DVA Gold Card, or being identified as a refugee with an ImmiCard.
eligibility_criteria: Of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Holding a Health Care Card and eligible for CSEF; Experiencing severe financial hardship; Holding a DVA Gold Card; Identified as refugees and holding an ImmiCard
special_conditions: Concessional fee does not include the cost of Outdoor Education, Swimming, Excursions/Incursions, Sacraments, Interschool Sport or Graduation Charges. Any offer of a concession is subject to the application entering into a Direct Debit Service Agreement or Centrepay Deduction arrangement. Any default in payment may result in the concession being withdrawn.

overview: St Peter's School provides an automatic $150 per child reduction on annual school fees for families holding a valid Health Care Card. This is a simple, direct financial assistance benefit available at the point of fee payment.
description: Families holding a valid Health Care Card are eligible to receive an automatic reduction of $150 per child off the annual school fees of $1,410 per family. This reduction must be claimed by bringing the Health Care Card to the school office at the time of fee payment. The reduction applies directly to school tuition fees and represents approximately 10.6% fee reduction per child. This program operates as part of the school's commitment to supporting families experiencing financial hardship. The reduction is subject to the same payment arrangements as regular school fees and can be paid in full or in three instalments of $470.00 per term.
eligibility_criteria: Holding a valid Health Care Card; Must present card to school office to claim reduction
special_conditions: Health Care Card must be presented to school office to claim the reduction. Reduction applies per child and must be applied at time of fee payment. Reduction only applies to school tuition fees, not to additional charges such as excursions, swimming, or sacraments.

overview: St Thomas' School Terang participates in the DOBCEL Family Fee Assistance Scheme, which supports families experiencing financial hardship. The scheme reduces school tuition fees for families who hold a valid Health Care Card, allowing them to access quality Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Family Fee Assistance Scheme operates across all DOBCEL Catholic Primary Schools, including St Thomas' School Terang. Families who qualify for the scheme will pay $520 ($10 per week) for Primary School tuition fees, representing a significant reduction from standard fees. The program is means-tested and targets families experiencing genuine financial hardship. Approximately 20% of families across DOBCEL primary schools hold Health Care Cards and access this support. The scheme ensures no child is denied a Catholic education due to inability to pay. Families should contact the school office directly to discuss their individual circumstances and explore available assistance options.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid Health Care Card or equivalent means-tested concession card; Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment at St Thomas' School Terang
special_conditions: The scheme is means-tested and subject to verification of financial hardship. Families should contact the principal to discuss their circumstances confidentially. The school will work with families to establish payment plans and explore all available support options. Existing students from families in genuine financial need may also access additional assistance with uniforms and school supplies beyond fee relief.

school_name: St Thomas the Apostle Catholic Primary School
overview: St Thomas the Apostle Catholic Primary School offers the School Fee Concession Program to provide financial assistance to families experiencing hardship in meeting school fees. The program is designed to ensure that families who want to access Catholic education are not excluded due to lack of means to pay fees.
description: The School Fee Concession Program is available to families who experience personal financial hardship in meeting school fees at any stage of their child's enrolment. The program provides fee relief or reduction based on demonstrated financial need. Families holding a means-tested Health Care Card are eligible to apply, and special consideration is also available for families without a Health Care Card. Families should contact the School Bursar to discuss their situation and determine the level of assistance available. The program operates in accordance with the Diocese of Sale Catholic Education Ltd policy to assist any family facing financial difficulty.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing personal financial hardship in meeting school fees; May hold a means-tested Health Care Card or apply for special consideration without a Health Care Card; Current or prospective enrolled students at the school
special_conditions: Applications should be made to the School Bursar. The school and Diocese of Sale Catholic Education Ltd are committed to assisting families who experience financial difficulty. This is expressed policy and practice of the school.

overview: Star of the Sea College offers a music scholarship program to Year 7 students each year. This program provides successful applicants with the opportunity to have group instrumental lessons free of charge for one year. Scholarships are open to all students regardless of previous musical experience.
description: The Year 7 Music Scholarship Program provides full remission of instrumental lesson fees for one year to incoming Year 7 students. Successful scholarship recipients receive free group instrumental lessons on selected instruments including strings, woodwind, brass, and percussion. The scholarship allows students to become members of school musical ensembles including orchestras, bands, and choirs. The selection process involves audition or demonstration of musical interest. The scholarship covers the duration of Year 7, after which students may continue with paid lessons or join school ensembles at no additional cost.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Enrolled as Year 7 student at Star of the Sea College; No previous musical experience required; Open to all skill levels
special_conditions: This is an internal annual scholarship program available to current Year 7 students. Scholarship recipients are able to become members of school ensembles. Further information can be obtained by contacting Ms Marcella Crowley, Head of Music, on (03) 9595 9595.

overview: Star of the Sea College offers bursaries and financial scholarships to eligible families facing financial hardship. The College's Bursary Fund, established in 2017, provides support to families who would otherwise be unable to afford fees. Both short-term bursaries and longer-term financial scholarships are available based on demonstrated need.
description: Star of the Sea College provides a limited number of bursaries for families suffering short-term financial hardship and a small number of financial scholarships for families in severe financial hardship. The Bursary Fund was established in 2017 with a Bursary Committee to determine eligibility criteria. Families seeking assistance must undergo interviews and be prepared to document their financial status. The College offers short-term fee relief through bursaries and longer-term fee relief through financial scholarships. Support is awarded on a needs-basis and amounts vary depending on individual circumstances. Families are encouraged to contact the Principal or Business Manager to discuss any difficulty in meeting fee obligations.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Star of the Sea College or applying for enrolment; Demonstrated financial hardship; Willingness to undergo financial assessment and provide documentation of financial circumstances; Families in severe financial hardship eligible for longer-term scholarships
special_conditions: Application process involves meeting with Principal, Business Manager, or Bursary Committee. Financial status must be fully documented. Short-term bursaries are available for temporary hardship; longer-term scholarships available for families in severe financial hardship. The College community is invited to contribute to the Bursary Fund through tax-deductible donations to support eligible students.

overview: St Thomas Aquinas College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward students with exceptional academic achievement across multiple disciplines. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate consistent excellence in three or more subjects and show potential to continue achieving outstanding academic results.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students with a proven record of exceptional academic achievement in three or more disciplines, with consistent achievement in other disciplines, and the potential to continue to achieve outstanding academic results. Scholarships are valued between 25 – 75 per cent reduction in tuition fees for the duration of the student's time at the College, from the year of the award until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress. Selection involves submission of an application form with supporting documentation, followed by shortlisting and invitation to attend an interview to discuss achievements. Student progress is assessed on an ongoing basis and formally reviewed at the end of Year 6 and Year 9.
eligibility_criteria: Proven record of exceptional academic achievement in three or more disciplines; Consistent achievement in other disciplines; Demonstrated potential to continue achieving outstanding academic results; Open to current and non-current students; Subject to satisfactory progress
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered from the year of the award until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress. Student progress is assessed on an ongoing basis and formally reviewed at the end of Year 6 and Year 9. Scholarship students will be assigned a mentor to support their learning journey at the School. The number and value of scholarships determined by applicants' ability to meet or exceed scholarship criteria and availability of places.

overview: St Thomas Aquinas College offers the All-Rounder Scholarship to recognise students who have demonstrated excellence across a broad range of activities and disciplines. The scholarship rewards well-rounded students who show strengths in leadership, sports, performing and visual arts, academic achievement, and community service.
description: The All-Rounder Scholarship is awarded to students who have demonstrated strengths, interests and achievements in a broad range of activities that may include, but are not limited to leadership, sport, the performing and visual arts, academic performance or community service. Recipients may show a combination of consistent academic achievement, sporting prowess, musical ability, school leadership, extra-curricular pursuits and community involvement. Scholarships are valued between 25 – 75 per cent reduction in tuition fees for the duration of the student's time at the College, from the year of the award until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress. Selection involves completion of an application form, participation in College events and activities, shortlisting, and attendance at an interview.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated strengths and achievements in a broad range of activities; Evidence of leadership, sporting prowess, musical ability, school leadership, extra-curricular pursuits or community involvement; Strong academic performance; Open to current and non-current students; Subject to satisfactory progress; Active participation in College events and activities
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered from the year of the award until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress. Student progress is assessed on an ongoing basis and formally reviewed at the end of Year 6 and Year 9. Scholarship students will be assigned a mentor to support their learning journey at the School. The number and value of scholarships determined by applicants' ability to meet or exceed scholarship criteria and availability of places.

overview: St Thomas Aquinas College offers Financial Assistance as part of its scholarship application process to provide additional support for families with demonstrated financial need. The program seeks to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent access to a traditional Catholic education.
description: Financial Assistance may be applied for as part of the College's scholarship application process. The financial assistance component of the scholarship program seeks to provide additional support for families with demonstrated financial need and relevant personal circumstances seeking the opportunity of a traditional Catholic education. Applicants can combine their scholarship application with a request for financial assistance, which is assessed separately. This program aims to provide opportunities for students who otherwise may not be able to start, or continue, their education at the College. The College evaluates financial circumstances and personal circumstances to determine eligibility and the level of support provided.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial need; Relevant personal circumstances; Application as part of scholarship process; Families seeking access to traditional Catholic education; Open to current and non-current students
special_conditions: Financial assistance is assessed as part of the scholarship application. The program is intended to provide opportunities for students who otherwise may not be able to start or continue their education at the College. Assessment considers financial need and relevant personal circumstances.

overview: Staughton College offers the Year 7 Leadership Scholarship to recognise and reward students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to personal excellence. The scholarship acknowledges students who show outstanding potential to contribute positively to the school community.
description: Staughton College provides scholarships for Outstanding Leadership to Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional leadership capabilities and a commitment to making a positive contribution to the school community. The scholarship is designed to recognise and support emerging student leaders who show dedication to developing their leadership skills and supporting their peers. The selection process involves an application and expression of interest from prospective Year 7 students. The scholarship is offered to multiple recipients annually, though the exact number is not publicly specified. Students are expected to actively participate in student leadership programs, contribute to school events, and maintain high standards of behaviour and attendance throughout their secondary schooling.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities; Must show commitment to positive community contribution; Must maintain acceptable behaviour and attendance standards
special_conditions: Students receiving the scholarship are expected to actively participate in student leadership programs and contribute to school initiatives. Scholarship status is contingent upon maintaining acceptable behaviour and attendance standards throughout their enrolment at the school.

overview: Staughton College offers the SEAL Program to gifted, talented and high-achieving students entering Year 7. The program is designed to provide curriculum acceleration and enrichment opportunities for students with exceptional academic abilities who seek a more challenging learning environment.
description: The TAASS Select Entry Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program is an accredited program at Staughton College designed to cater for gifted, talented and high-achieving students. The program operates on the principles of Curriculum Acceleration and Enrichment, enabling students to commence areas of work earlier and move through curriculum content more quickly, providing a more challenging learning environment. SEAL students complete a three-year program (Years 7-9) covering Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities, Music, French, Food, Health, PE and Art. After Year 9, students meet with the Careers Coordinator and High Abilities Learning Coordinator to agree on a personalised pathway for Years 10-12. The program includes academic study along with enrichment activities and community focused events. Admission requires sitting the EduTest, which costs $99.00. Registration opens September 9 annually, with testing typically held in October. Candidates are notified of results by November 1, followed by interviews. Final placement decisions are made by November 18.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Grade 6 seeking entry to Year 7; Must register and pay EduTest fee of $99.00; Must sit the EduTest assessment; Must pass selection interview
special_conditions: Registration and payment must be completed before applications close. All correspondence regarding the EduTest comes via email from test administrators. After Year 9, students have pathway options: consolidate with mainstream Year 10 in all subjects, Year 10 with accelerated options in one VCE Year 11 subject, or alternative pathways. All conversation around pathways considers available information to support decisions by parents, students, High Abilities Learning Coordinator and Course Counsellor. School is currently reviewing SEAL program structure in line with DET High-Ability initiative.

overview: Staughton College will assist parents experiencing financial difficulties by facilitating applications to State Schools' Relief, a Victorian government support scheme. This program provides financial assistance to families who face hardship in meeting school costs.
description: Staughton College provides support to families facing financial difficulties by facilitating applications to State Schools' Relief, an external Victorian government financial assistance program. The college recognises that some families may struggle to meet school costs due to financial hardship or unexpected circumstances. Rather than providing direct scholarships, Staughton College acts as an intermediary to help eligible families access state-funded financial assistance through the official State Schools' Relief program. Parents who are experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the school to discuss what support may be available. The college will work with families to complete applications to State Schools' Relief and provide any required documentation or information to support the application process. This program is designed for families experiencing genuine financial hardship and is administered through the Victorian government education system.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial difficulties; Must contact school to discuss support options; Eligible for Victorian government financial assistance programs
special_conditions: Applications are facilitated by the school. Staughton College will assist parents under financial difficulties by connecting them with State Schools' Relief. Contact the school directly to discuss individual circumstances. All applications are assessed on a case-by-case basis according to Victorian government guidelines.

school_name: Sunbury And Macedon Ranges Specialist School
Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Primary Campus
overview: Strathcona offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in prospective students. The scholarship recognises girls who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, based on independent testing through EduTest.
description: Academic Scholarships may be offered to a select number of girls on the basis of their academic results after an academic examination with independent provider EduTest. Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees. The selection process involves submission of an Academic Scholarship application form to EduTest, a Scholarship Portfolio to the Registrar including a letter from the student outlining their interest in Strathcona, school reports, and NAPLAN results. Shortlisted students are invited to attend an interview with the Principal of Strathcona Girls Grammar. Scholarships are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 3 applying for Year 5 entry; Currently in Year 4 applying for Year 6 entry; Currently in Year 5 applying for Year 7 entry; Currently in Year 6 applying for Year 8 entry; Currently in Year 7 applying for Year 9 entry; Application with EduTest required
special_conditions: Scholarships are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted. Unsuccessful applicants will be notified via email.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Primary Campus
overview: Strathcona offers Instrumental Music Scholarships to students with the potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life of the school. The scholarship recognises excellence in instrumental music performance, with particular encouragement for low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists.
description: Instrumental Music Scholarships may be offered to candidates with the potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life at Strathcona. Low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists are particularly encouraged to audition. Scholarships may be awarded to girls based on their performance auditions, academic results and an interview with the Principal. Music Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees. All Music scholarship candidates must register and sit for the EduTest examination and forward School Reports and NAPLAN results. Music Scholarship applicants must also submit documents supporting the student's level of achievement pertaining to their instrument(s) of choice, including performance pieces contrasting in style and tempo and technical examples demonstrating facility, control, coordination and tonal qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Must have potential for outstanding musical contribution to school; Low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists particularly encouraged; Must meet Music Prerequisites listed in Scholarship Policy; Must also participate in Academic Scholarship process; Currently in Year 3 applying for Year 5 entry; Currently in Year 4 applying for Year 6 entry; Currently in Year 5 applying for Year 7 entry; Currently in Year 6 applying for Year 8 entry; Currently in Year 7 applying for Year 9 entry
special_conditions: All Music Scholarship candidates must apply for and participate in the Academic Scholarship process. Music Scholarships are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Performance time limits apply: single instrumentalists up to 8 minutes total; dual instrumentalists up to 10 minutes total.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Primary Campus
overview: The Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship was established to support girls with significant hearing impairment who would benefit from an education at Strathcona. The scholarship recognises the school's commitment to inclusive education and accessibility for students with disability.
description: The Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship was established by a former student of Strathcona, the late Mrs Beth Smallwood (nee MacLaren). The recipient will be a girl with significant hearing impairment who would benefit from an education at Strathcona. The recipient of the Scholarship will be determined by interview and, dependent on financial circumstances, will be valued at up to 80% of the tuition fees in any one year. The Scholarship shall be available to each scholar for the period of her enrolment, subject to satisfactory conduct and progress. Applications for the Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship may be submitted at any time of year, with notification of scholarship potentially provided in the year preceding entry.
eligibility_criteria: Must have significant hearing impairment; Must demonstrate that education at Strathcona would be beneficial; Means-tested based on financial circumstances; Must maintain satisfactory conduct and progress for continued receipt
special_conditions: Scholarship is means-tested and dependent on financial circumstances. Scholarship available for period of enrolment subject to satisfactory conduct and progress. Applications may be submitted at any time of year. Notification of scholarship may be provided in the year preceding entry.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Primary Campus
overview: The Strathcona Centenary Scholarship was established following the school's centenary celebration in 2024 to provide full financial support to deserving students. The scholarship offers up to 100% tuition coverage to students who would not normally be able to afford a Strathcona education.
description: After celebrating the school's Centenary in 2024, Strathcona Girls Grammar, due to the generosity of donors, can offer a Scholarship to the value of up to 100% tuition to a student who would not normally be able to afford a Strathcona education. The scholarship is means-tested and applicants must provide copies of two most recent ATO tax statements at the time of applying. The selection process includes an application form, a letter from the student to the Principal outlining why they would like to attend Strathcona (preferably handwritten in the student's own words), the student's Birth Certificate, and supporting certificates, awards or examples of outstanding work in leadership, community involvement, sport, and culture. Applications may be submitted at any time. Shortlisted students will be invited to attend an interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective Senior School students; Entry from Year 7 to Year 11; Current Strathcona Students are not eligible; Must demonstrate financial hardship; Means-tested based on ATO tax statements; Must provide evidence of student's interests and achievements
special_conditions: Centenary Scholarship is means-tested. Applicants must provide two most recent ATO tax statements at time of applying. Current Strathcona Students are not eligible. Scholarship represents school's first step towards extending similar opportunities to deserving young women in years to come. Applications have now closed for 2026.

overview: Stawell Secondary College students are eligible for this community-based scholarship offered through Youthrive Victoria. The scholarship provides significant financial support and mentoring to support worthy students in realising their educational and leadership potential.
description: The Cunningham Scholarship is a full scholarship that includes financial support of $30,000 over three years, plus mentoring and leadership development. The scholarship is available to students from the Stawell community who demonstrate outstanding potential and leadership qualities. This award recognises students who show commitment to their studies and community engagement. The scholarship aims to provide transformational support to help deserving students achieve their educational goals while developing leadership skills.
eligibility_criteria: Students from Stawell area; Demonstrated outstanding potential; Commitment to leadership and community engagement; Attending Stawell Secondary College
special_conditions: The scholarship is administered by Youthrive Victoria and includes mentoring and leadership development components alongside financial support.

school_name: Sunbury And Macedon Ranges Specialist School-Bullengarook Senior Campus

overview: Strathcona offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement in incoming students. The scholarship is awarded based on strong academic performance demonstrated through independent EduTest examinations and previous school results. Recipients are expected to maintain consistent academic progress and full participation in school life.
description: Academic Scholarships may be offered to a select number of girls on the basis of their academic results after an academic examination with independent provider EduTest. The scholarship carries partial remission of tuition fees and is conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Candidates must submit an Application for Academic Scholarship form with EduTest along with a Scholarship Portfolio including a letter from the student to the Principal, birth certificate, and school reports. The scholarship provides partial fee remission for the duration of enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, or Year 9; Previous academic performance required; Must submit application through EduTest; Current Strathcona students not eligible; Australian citizenship or permanent residency documentation required
special_conditions: Scholarships are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Scholarship Portfolio must be submitted at least one week prior to the Academic Testing Day. Recipients must accept the scholarship offer within two business days and sign a Scholarship Agreement detailing concession conditions.

overview: Strathcona offers Instrumental Music Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional musical talent and potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life of the school. Scholarships are awarded based on audition performance, academic results, and interview with the Principal. Particular interest is shown in low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists.
description: Instrumental Music Scholarships may be offered to candidates with the potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life at Strathcona. Low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists are particularly encouraged to audition. Scholarships may be awarded to girls based on their performance auditions, academic results and an interview with the Principal. Music Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees and are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. All Music scholarship candidates must register and sit for the EduTest examination and forward school reports and NAPLAN results. An audition is required consisting of two contrasting pieces (no more than 10 minutes total), technical work, and aural tests.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, or Year 9; Must demonstrate advanced instrumental music ability; Proficiency in low brass, low woodwind or strings preferred; Must sit EduTest examination; Must submit school reports and NAPLAN results; Must attend audition; Australian citizenship or permanent residency required
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and active modelling of Strathcona values. Successful applicants who audition on more than one instrument will only be awarded a Music Scholarship on one instrument. Recipients must accept the scholarship offer within two business days and sign a Scholarship Agreement. Audition repertoire must include works demonstrating facility, control, coordination and tonal qualities in contrasting styles and key tonalities.

program_name: Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship for the Hearing Impaired
overview: The Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship is a legacy scholarship established by a former Strathcona student to support talented girls with significant hearing impairment. The scholarship recognises the potential of hearing-impaired students and provides financial support to enable them to access a Strathcona education. Selection is based on interview and financial circumstances.
description: The Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship was established by a former student of Strathcona, the late Mrs Beth Smallwood (nee MacLaren). The recipient will be a girl with significant hearing impairment who would benefit from an education at Strathcona. The recipient of the Scholarship will be determined by interview and, dependent on financial circumstances, will be valued at up to 80% of the tuition fees in any one year. The Scholarship shall be available to each scholar for the period of her enrolment, subject to satisfactory conduct and progress. Applications for the Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship may be submitted at any time of year. Notification of scholarship may be provided in the year preceding entry.
eligibility_criteria: Must have significant hearing impairment; Open to prospective students at all year levels; Applications accepted year-round; Dependent on financial circumstances for valuation; Must demonstrate capacity to benefit from Strathcona education; Subject to satisfactory conduct and progress; Australian residency required
special_conditions: Scholarship available for the period of enrolment, subject to satisfactory conduct and progress. Recipient must be a girl with significant hearing impairment. Financial circumstances determine the final value of the scholarship. Applications can be submitted at any time, with notification potentially provided in the year preceding entry. Submission includes an outline of the nature and extent of hearing impairment with supporting audiology and medical reports.

overview: Established to celebrate Strathcona's Centenary in 2024, the Centenary Scholarship provides transformative financial support to deserving girls who would otherwise be unable to afford a Strathcona education. This is a fully means-tested scholarship offering up to 100% tuition remission, recognising talented students from disadvantaged backgrounds. It reflects the school's commitment to inclusion and equal access.
description: After celebrating the Centenary in 2024, Strathcona Girls Grammar, due to the generosity of donors, can offer a Scholarship to the value of up to 100% tuition to a student who would not normally be able to afford a Strathcona education. The Centenary Scholarship is a fully means-tested scholarship awarded annually to a girl or girls demonstrating an unstoppable spirit and showing promise across a range of interests and areas. Recipients are selected through interviews with the Principal based on demonstrated potential and financial need. The scholarship is funded by the generosity of the Strathcona community. Applications are processed on an ongoing basis when open, and candidates must complete the Centenary Scholarship Application Form and submit supporting documentation including a letter from the student and evidence of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 11; Fully means-tested based on family financial circumstances; Must demonstrate exceptional potential across academics, culture, leadership or sport; Must exemplify the Strathcona spirit; Current Strathcona students not eligible for entry-level application; Australian residency required
special_conditions: Currently closed for 2026 applications. Shortlisted students invited to attend interview with Principal. Feedback on individual submissions not provided. Late or incomplete submissions not accepted. Unsuccessful applicants notified via email. Recipients must support the Strathcona ethos and demonstrate ongoing commitment to the school values. Scholarship is a community-funded initiative, dependent on fundraising.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Hawthorn Campus
overview: Strathcona offers Academic Scholarships to a select number of girls on the basis of their academic results after an academic examination. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to high-quality learning at Strathcona.
description: Academic Scholarships may be offered to a select number of girls on the basis of their academic results after an academic examination with independent provider EduTest. Academic Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees and are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Candidates must first submit an Application for Academic Scholarship form with EduTest and also submit a Scholarship Portfolio to the Registrar. The selection process includes academic examination with EduTest, review of academic results and school reports, portfolio assessment including a letter from the student explaining why they wish to attend Strathcona, and may include an interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9; Year 3 in 2025 may apply for Year 5 2027 entry; Year 4 in 2025 may apply for Year 6 2027 entry; Year 5 in 2025 may apply for Year 7 2027 entry; Year 6 in 2025 may apply for Year 8 2027 entry; Year 7 in 2025 may apply for Year 9 2027 entry; Must submit application form and supporting documents to registrar; Australian or New Zealand born students require birth certificate; Overseas-born students require birth certificate, Australian passport, citizenship certificate or permanent resident visa
special_conditions: Conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Scholarships recognise achievement, enable success and assume a commitment to the school. For more information about what to expect from the testing day and review process, applicants should download the Scholarship Guide.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Hawthorn Campus
overview: Strathcona offers Instrumental Music Scholarships to students with the potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life at the school. The scholarship recognises exceptional musical ability and commitment to performance.
description: Instrumental Music Scholarships may be offered to candidates with the potential to make an outstanding contribution to the musical life at Strathcona. Low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists are particularly encouraged to audition. Scholarships may be awarded to girls based on their performance auditions, academic results and an interview with the Principal. Music Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees and are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. All Music scholarship candidates must register and sit for the EduTest examination and forward school reports and NAPLAN results. Candidates must also meet the Music Prerequisites and complete the Academic Scholarship Registration.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9; Music prerequisite requirements must be met; Must complete Academic Scholarship Registration; Must sit for EduTest examination; Must provide school reports and NAPLAN results; Low brass, low woodwind and string instrumentalists particularly encouraged; Must submit application form and supporting documents to registrar
special_conditions: Conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Audition requirements include two contrasting pieces (up to 8 minutes for single instrument, up to 10 minutes for double instrument), technical examples in scales and arpeggios in at least 3 different key tonalities, and aural tests appropriate to the level of study.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Hawthorn Campus
overview: Strathcona offers Special Scholarships annually to recognise excellence across multiple disciplines. The scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in leadership, community involvement, sport, and cultural pursuits.
description: Strathcona offers a limited number of Special Scholarships on an annual basis to recognise achievement across multiple areas. Students are invited to showcase supporting certificates, awards, photos of trophies or examples of outstanding work in leadership, community involvement, sport, and culture. The selection process includes portfolio review and may include an interview with the Principal.
