Special Scholarships carry partial remission of tuition fees and are conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Candidates must submit supporting documentation including birth certificate and a letter from the student outlining why they would like to attend Strathcona.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9; Demonstrated excellence in leadership, community involvement, sport or cultural pursuits; Must submit application form and supporting documentation to registrar; Australian or New Zealand born students require birth certificate; Overseas-born students require birth certificate, Australian passport, citizenship certificate or permanent resident visa; Certificates, awards and photographic evidence of achievements required
special_conditions: Conditional upon consistent academic progress, full participation in the co-curricular life of Strathcona and the active modelling of Strathcona values. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted. Unsuccessful applicants will be notified via email. Do not include original documents or display folders as they cannot be returned.

school_name: Strathcona Baptist Girls' Grammar - Hawthorn Campus
overview: Strathcona offers the Centenary Scholarship to a student who would not normally be able to afford a Strathcona education. The scholarship was established following the school's centenary in 2024 and represents the school's commitment to extending opportunities to deserving young women.
description: After celebrating its Centenary in 2024, Strathcona Girls Grammar can offer a Scholarship to the value of up to 100% tuition to a student who would not normally be able to afford a Strathcona education. The Centenary Scholarship is designed to provide life-changing opportunities for talented young women whose family situations would otherwise prevent them from experiencing the transformative education at Strathcona. Candidates must complete and submit the Centenary Scholarship Application form and provide copies of birth certificate, Australian passport/citizenship certificate/permanent resident visa if overseas-born, and showcase certificates, awards, or examples of outstanding work in leadership, community involvement, sport, and culture. The selection process includes portfolio review and shortlisted students are invited to attend an interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11; Offered for Senior School entry from Year 7 to Year 11; Must demonstrate financial need; Current Strathcona students not eligible to apply; Demonstrated excellence in leadership, community involvement, sport or cultural pursuits; Must submit application form and supporting documentation to registrar; Overseas-born students must provide birth certificate, Australian passport, citizenship certificate or permanent resident visa
special_conditions: Applications have closed for 2026. Applications may be submitted at any time throughout the year. Notification of scholarship may be provided in the year preceding entry. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted. Unsuccessful applicants will be notified via email. Shortlisted students invited to interview with Principal. Do not include original documents or display folders as they cannot be returned.

overview: Strathcona offers the Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship to girls with significant hearing impairment who would benefit from an education at Strathcona. The scholarship was established by a former student and supports access to education for hearing-impaired students.
description: The Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship was established by a former student of Strathcona, the late Mrs Beth Smallwood (nee MacLaren). The recipient will be a girl with significant hearing impairment who would benefit from an education at Strathcona. The recipient of the Scholarship will be determined by interview and, dependent on financial circumstances, will be valued at up to 80% of the tuition fees in any one year. The Scholarship shall be available to each scholar for the period of her enrolment, subject to satisfactory conduct and progress. Applications for the Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship may be submitted at any time of year, and notification of scholarship may be provided in the year preceding entry. Candidates must complete and submit the Beth MacLaren Smallwood Scholarship Application form, an Application for Enrolment form, and supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to girls with significant hearing impairment; All year levels eligible; Must demonstrate that hearing impairment would benefit from Strathcona education; Applications may be submitted at any time throughout the year; Must submit application form and supporting documentation; Must provide letter from student to Principal; Australian or New Zealand born students require birth certificate; Overseas-born students require birth certificate, Australian passport, citizenship certificate or permanent resident visa; Must provide outline of nature and extent of hearing impairment with supporting audiology/medical reports

Dependent on financial circumstances, valued at up to 80% of tuition fees in any one year. Applications may be submitted at any time. Notification may be provided in year preceding entry.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and commitment to their learning. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 and continuing through to Year 12, with automatic consideration for eligible applicants.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship at Sunbury Downs College recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and learning commitment. Students in the Accelerated Curriculum and Enrichment (ACE) Program receive automatic consideration for this scholarship. The scholarship acknowledges outstanding achievement in core academic subjects including English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence and performance throughout the student's primary school years. The scholarship is non-monetary and recognises achievement through various college opportunities and programs. Recipients are eligible to continue receiving recognition through Year 12. The scholarship supports students who wish to pursue an academic pathway and are committed to sustained high achievement throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated outstanding academic performance in primary school; Commitment to academic learning; Automatic consideration for ACE Program participants; Open to all students regardless of sector; Continue eligibility through Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Automatic consideration for students in ACE Program. Scholarship recognition continues through secondary schooling. Recipients are expected to maintain commitment to academic excellence and contribute positively to college community.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers an Instrumental Music Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding aptitude and commitment in instrumental music. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 and continues through to Year 12.
description: The Instrumental Music Scholarship at Sunbury Downs College recognises students who have demonstrated exceptional musical ability and sustained commitment to instrumental music study. The college offers a comprehensive instrumental music program with tuition from industry professionals. Students selected for this scholarship demonstrate outstanding performance and dedication to their chosen instrument. The college supports instrumental music through a structured program with band rehearsals and lessons offered throughout the year. Selection is based on demonstrated musical excellence, audition performance, and evidence of serious musical study. The scholarship provides recognition of musical achievement and supports students' continued participation in the college's vibrant music program. Recipients are expected to participate actively in college musical events and performances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated outstanding aptitude and commitment in instrumental music; Evidence of sustained music study; Participation in music ensembles; Open to all school sectors; Continue eligibility through Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on audition and demonstrated musical ability. Recipients expected to participate in college musical productions and ensemble performances. Participation in instrumental music program contributes to college musical culture and extracurricular life.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers a Sporting Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and commitment to sport. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 and continues through to Year 12.
description: The Sporting Excellence Scholarship at Sunbury Downs College recognises students who have demonstrated exceptional athletic ability and dedication to sporting pursuits. The college offers a comprehensive sporting program with interschool competitions in athletics, swimming, Australian Rules Football, Basketball, Netball, Soccer, Volleyball, Badminton, Baseball, and Softball. Students selected for this scholarship demonstrate outstanding sporting performance, athleticism, and commitment to representing the college. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence, athletic ability, and participation in competitive sport. The scholarship provides recognition of sporting achievement and supports students' continued participation in the college's strong sporting culture. Recipients are expected to represent the college at local and interschool sporting events and demonstrate positive sporting behaviour and teamwork.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated outstanding sporting ability and achievement; Commitment to competitive sport; Representation at local or regional level; Open to all school sectors; Continue eligibility through Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated sporting ability and competitive achievement. Recipients expected to represent college at interschool sports and maintain positive participation. Sportsmanship and teamwork are essential qualities for scholarship holders.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers a Citizenship and Leadership Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding leadership qualities and commitment to community service. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 and continues through to Year 12.
description: The Citizenship and Leadership Scholarship at Sunbury Downs College recognises students who have demonstrated exceptional leadership abilities and commitment to positive community contribution. The college values student leadership through its well-established Student Voice program, House system, and Captaincy team that provide opportunities for students to develop leadership skills. Students selected for this scholarship demonstrate outstanding character, positive behaviour, initiative in community service, and ability to inspire peers. Selection is based on demonstrated leadership in primary school, evidence of citizenship, commitment to community, and character references. The scholarship provides recognition of leadership excellence and supports students' continued development as leaders throughout secondary school. Recipients are expected to take active roles in student leadership positions and contribute positively to college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated leadership skills and initiatives; Evidence of community service; Positive behaviour and character; Commitment to broader community engagement; Open to all school sectors; Continue eligibility through Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated leadership qualities and community contribution. Recipients expected to take active roles in student leadership programs and House activities. Character references and evidence of citizenship activities required for consideration.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers the Accelerated Curriculum and Enrichment (ACE) Program to cater for academically gifted and talented students who demonstrate strong work ethics. The program operates on principles of curriculum acceleration and enrichment for Years 7-9.
description: The ACE Program at Sunbury Downs College is a selective entry course designed for high-ability students who wish to experience a more challenging and accelerated curriculum. The program is offered in Years 7-9 and provides students with opportunities to progress through the Victorian Curriculum at a faster pace while receiving enrichment activities. Students in the ACE Program receive differentiated teaching through an Individualised Learning Plan (ILP) developed by teachers and parents to support students at their point of need. The program includes additional interdisciplinary subjects, outdoor education challenges, and extended learning opportunities. ACE students progress through core subjects of English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities while accessing enriched content. The program operates with one dedicated ACE homegroup per year level at Middle Years. Students receive support from a dedicated homegroup teacher and access to specialized student services. Upon completion of Year 9, successful ACE students are invited to apply for the Scholars Program for Years 10-11, which provides early commencement of VCE and advanced academic opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-ability students; Strong work ethic required; Select entry course with enrolment from feeder primary schools; Students in Years 7-9; Academic performance assessment required; Open to all school sectors; New enrolments welcome with suitability assessment
special_conditions: ACE students are grouped in dedicated homegroups with same-age peers. All ACE students participate in year level assemblies and general college activities. All ACE students in Year 9 are automatically invited to apply for Scholars Program for Years 10-11. Students must demonstrate sustained academic commitment. Scholarships also available for ACE program participants: Academic Excellence, Instrumental Music, Sporting Excellence, and Citizenship and Leadership.

overview: Sunbury Downs College offers the Scholars Program for Years 10-11 to academically talented students who have demonstrated passion and commitment to their learning. The program is designed to develop and extend individual student strengths while providing greater opportunities through wider curriculum offerings.
description: The Scholars Program at Sunbury Downs College is a selective program for Years 10 and 11 that supports academically talented students pursuing an academic pathway after VCE. Students who are successful members of the Year 9 ACE Program are automatically offered a place in the Scholars Program. The program has been designed to develop and extend individual student strengths while presenting greater opportunities through provision of wider curriculum offerings and advanced academic support. Students may be offered placement through identification via semester reports, GPAs, and recommendations from classroom teachers. External students seeking entry must submit evidence of academic achievement and attend an interview to determine suitability for program placement. The Scholars Program is focussed on supporting students in early commencement of VCE, developing independent academic skills, and preparing students for university pathways. Participants in the program are required to pay an additional fee to cover various costs of the program. The program provides access to specialist teaching, advanced subject offerings, and university preparation support.
eligibility_criteria: Automatic offer for Year 9 ACE Program members; Currently enrolled students identified as requiring academic extension; Evidence of academic achievement in semester reports and GPA; High-achieving students in Years 10-11; Interview required for external applicants; Demonstrated commitment to academic learning; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Additional program fees apply to cover costs. Automatic placement for Year 9 ACE students who meet expectations. External applicants must demonstrate evidence of academic achievement and attend interview. Program focuses on early VCE commencement and university preparation. Students must maintain commitment to academic excellence.

overview: Swan Hill College Scholarship Foundation provides financial support to eligible students in Years 10-12 who demonstrate exceptional skills in academic or artistic areas, demonstrate financial need, and possess strong potential and character. The foundation was established by former students to assist current and future students who may face financial barriers to pursuing their education.
description: The Swan Hill College Scholarship Foundation provides $1,000 to successful applicants to be spent on nominated approved school expenses, including computers, books, fees, approved camps and other items with foundation approval. The foundation commits to fund at least two scholarships annually for students in Years 10, 11, and 12. These scholarships provide comprehensive three-year funding packages from Year 10 through Year 12. Selection is based on skill (academic or artistic), need, potential, character, and community references. Applicants must be Year 9 students progressing to Year 10 at Swan Hill College. The selection process involves an initial written or video application addressing the specified criteria, followed by potential interviews with parents and a panel appointed by the foundation. Successful applicants are expected to report regularly to the foundation on school progress and engage with the community to promote the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 9 at Swan Hill College; Progressing to Year 10; Demonstrate exceptional skills in academic or artistic areas; Demonstrate financial need; Show potential to reach educational goals; Demonstrate good character and active community involvement; Provide at least one teacher reference and one community member reference
special_conditions: Scholarships provide a comprehensive three-year funding package supporting students from Year 10 through Year 12. Successful applicants must report regularly to the foundation on their school progress and studies. Applicants must engage with the community to promote the scholarship. The foundation is a registered charity. Donations are tax deductible.

program_name: Swan Hill College Scholarship Foundation Scholarship
overview: Swan Hill College offers the Swan Hill College Scholarship Foundation Scholarship to support students facing financial barriers to education. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional skills in academics or arts, combined with demonstrated need and potential for growth. Recipients are selected based on skill, need, potential, character and community involvement.
description: The Swan Hill College Scholarship Foundation Scholarship provides $1,000 to successful applicants to be spent on nominated approved school expenses, including computers, books, fees, approved camps and other items with foundation approval. The foundation commits to funding at least two scholarships each year for Years 10, 11, and 12 at Swan Hill College. Recipients are selected based on: Skill (academic or artistic); Need (financial support enabling students to reach their potential); Potential (demonstrated ability to reach goals); Character (good character and active school/community membership); and References (at least one teacher reference and one community reference). Initial applications are made in writing or by video to the foundation, with shortlisted applicants attending a panel meeting with parents.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled in Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12 at Swan Hill College; Students in Year 9 who will be completing Year 10 are eligible to make initial applications; Must demonstrate exceptional skills in academics or arts; Must demonstrate financial need; Must demonstrate potential to reach goals through education; Must be of good character and active members of school and local community; Must provide at least one teacher reference and one community reference
special_conditions: Successful applicants are expected to report regularly to the foundation on their school progress and studies. Recipients are required to engage with the community to promote the scholarship. The scholarship funds must be spent on nominated approved school expenses only.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarship to incoming students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students with an exemplary learning record showing outstanding aptitude, achievement and growth in Mathematics and English, alongside strong performance in general studies.
description: The Year 7 Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate outstanding academic performance across core subjects. Recipients receive school books and a uniform pack as part of their scholarship award. The selection process is based on interview assessment (not entry exam), allowing the college to evaluate candidates' academic achievements, character, and potential. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship. The scholarship is open to all students entering Year 7 and recognises those with strong foundational knowledge and commitment to academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Exemplary learning record demonstrating outstanding aptitude and achievement in Mathematics and English; Strong performance in general studies; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack. Students may apply for more than one class of Scholarship.

overview: Sunshine College provides the Year 7 Leadership Scholarship to recognise students demonstrating leadership potential and community engagement. The scholarship is available to students with solid academic records who have shown leadership skills in both school and community settings.
description: The Year 7 Leadership Scholarship supports students entering Year 7 who have demonstrated solid academic achievement combined with proven leadership capabilities. Recipients receive school books and a uniform pack as scholarship benefits. Candidates are selected through interview assessment rather than written examination, allowing the college to evaluate their leadership potential, character development, and contribution to community activities. The scholarship recognises students who have shown leadership in a variety of settings including school and community involvement. This scholarship reflects the college's commitment to developing well-rounded leaders.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Solid academic record; Demonstrated leadership skills and potential; Participation in school and community activities; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack. Students may apply for more than one class of Scholarship. Leadership demonstrated through school and community activities.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Year 7 Community Service Scholarship to recognise students who have demonstrated outstanding commitment to supporting fellow students. The scholarship celebrates those who have fully participated in the life of their school and made meaningful contributions to their school community.
description: The Year 7 Community Service Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who have shown exceptional dedication to supporting their peers and actively contributing to school life. Recipients receive school books and a uniform pack as part of their scholarship benefit. The selection process utilises interviews rather than formal examinations, enabling the college to assess candidates' character, community mindedness, and interpersonal skills. This scholarship reflects the college's values of community engagement and collective responsibility. Students who have demonstrated outstanding effort in helping fellow students and full participation in school activities are encouraged to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Outstanding effort in supporting fellow students; Full participation in school life and activities; Demonstrated commitment to school community; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack. Students may apply for more than one class of Scholarship.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Year 7 Specialist Scholarship to recognise students with exceptional expertise in a particular area. The scholarship is available to students demonstrating outstanding work ethic and dedication to personal growth in their chosen field of specialisation.
description: The Year 7 Specialist Scholarship supports students entering Year 7 who have shown outstanding commitment and excellence in a particular area of expertise, whether academic, sporting, artistic, or technical. Recipients receive school books and a uniform pack as part of their scholarship package. Selection is conducted through interview assessment, allowing the college to evaluate the depth of students' commitment, achievement in their specialist area, and overall potential for continued excellence. This scholarship recognises that students excel in different areas and celebrates diverse forms of achievement beyond traditional academic pathways. The college values students who pursue personal growth in their chosen field with dedication and enthusiasm.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated outstanding work ethic and application in a particular area of expertise; Strongly focused on personal growth in chosen field; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack. Students may apply for more than one class of Scholarship. Specialist area of expertise can include academic, sporting, artistic or technical pursuits.

program_name: Year 11 SHS Alumni VCE Support Scholarship
overview: Sunshine College offers the Year 11 SHS Alumni VCE Support Scholarship in partnership with the SHS Alumni community. This scholarship provides direct financial support to students entering Year 11 to assist them through their VCE studies.
description: The Year 11 SHS Alumni VCE Support Scholarship is specifically designed to support students entering Year 11 as they commence their Victorian Certificate of Education (VCE) studies. Two scholarships are awarded annually through this program, which was developed in collaboration with the SHS Alumni community. The scholarship provides financial assistance dedicated to supporting students through their VCE for books and essential study materials. This targeted support recognises the substantial costs associated with final-year secondary education and aims to ensure that capable students are not disadvantaged by financial constraints. Recipients benefit from alumni community involvement and mentoring opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; Enrolled in VCE program; Selection based on demonstrated academic commitment and need
special_conditions: Scholarship provided to two students entering Year 11. Developed with SHS Alumni community. Support specifically allocated for VCE books and materials.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Year 7 Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students transitioning to secondary school. The scholarship supports students with exemplary learning records demonstrating outstanding aptitude, achievement and growth in Mathematics and English alongside strong general studies performance.
description: The Year 7 Scholarship at Sunshine College is available to students entering Year 7 who have demonstrated exceptional academic performance. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack as part of the financial benefit. Selection is by interview - not entry exam. These scholarships are available to students with an exemplary learning record demonstrating outstanding aptitude, achievement and growth in Mathematics and English, alongside a strong performance in general studies. Scholars will be selected based on their demonstrated academic excellence and commitment to learning throughout their primary school years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exemplary learning record; Outstanding aptitude and achievement in Mathematics and English; Strong performance in general studies; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Scholarships include school books and a uniform pack.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students with an exemplary learning record showing outstanding aptitude, achievement and growth in Mathematics and English alongside strong general studies performance.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to students who have maintained an exemplary learning record demonstrating outstanding aptitude, achievement and growth in Mathematics and English, alongside a strong performance in general studies. Selection is by interview rather than entry examination. This scholarship supports Sunshine College's educational philosophy that all students can learn, progress and excel. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship, allowing them to pursue multiple opportunities based on their strengths and achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Exemplary learning record; Outstanding aptitude and achievement in Mathematics and English; Strong performance in general studies; May apply for multiple scholarship classes; Selection by interview
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Leadership Scholarship to students who have demonstrated outstanding effort in supporting their peers and have fully participated in the life of the school. This scholarship recognises students who show commitment to community and positive engagement with the school.
description: The Leadership Scholarship at Sunshine College is available to students who have demonstrated outstanding effort in supporting fellow students and have fully participated in the life of their school. Selection is by interview rather than entry examination. These scholarships recognise students who exhibit exceptional leadership qualities and dedication to supporting their school community. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship, providing opportunities to be considered across different merit categories based on their individual strengths and achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding effort in supporting fellow students; Full participation in school life; Demonstrated leadership qualities; Commitment to school community; Selection by interview; May apply for multiple scholarship classes
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship.

overview: Sunshine College offers the Specialist Scholarship to students who have demonstrated outstanding work ethic and application in school with strong focus on personal growth in a particular area of expertise. This scholarship recognises students pursuing excellence in a specialised field.
description: Specialist Scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated an outstanding work ethic and application at school who are strongly focused on personal growth in a particular area of expertise. These scholarships recognise students who are pursuing excellence within a specialised discipline or area of focused study. Selection is by interview rather than entry examination. The scholarship supports Sunshine College's philosophy that all students can learn, progress and excel in a particular field of endeavour, and students may apply for more than one class of scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding work ethic and application at school; Strong focus on personal growth in area of expertise; Specialisation in particular discipline; Selection by interview; May apply for multiple scholarship classes
special_conditions: Selection is by interview - not entry exam. Students may apply for more than one class of scholarship.

overview: Sunshine College offers the SHS Alumni Scholarship in partnership with Sunshine High School alumni. This scholarship provides support to two Year 11 students to assist them through their VCE studies. The scholarship was developed with the SHS Alumni and is designed to support academic achievement in the final years of secondary education.
description: Developed with the SHS Alumni, this scholarship is provided to two students entering Year 11 and is designed to support them through their VCE for books and materials. The scholarship recognises students with strong academic merit and achievement. Students cannot apply for this scholarship; instead, they are selected by teachers and Principals based on academic merit and achievements. This ensures the most deserving students receive support during their critical VCE year to enable them to purchase necessary study materials and resources.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 11; Selected by teachers and Principals; Based on academic merit and achievements; Open to all students regardless of background
special_conditions: Students cannot apply; instead are selected by teachers and Principals based on academic merit and achievements. Scholarship is provided to exactly two students entering Year 11. Support is for books and materials during VCE studies.

school_name: Swinburne Senior Secondary College-Camberwell Road Campus
Sydney Road Community School-Coburg Teaching Unit
Sydney Road Community School-The Brunswick Learning Space
Sunshine College-Sunshine Western Region Deaf Facility

overview: The Templeton Dux is awarded to the student with the highest academic results at the end of Grade 6. The award recognises outstanding achievement across core academic subjects and is presented at Grade 6 Graduation.
description: The Templeton Dux Award is presented annually to a single Grade 6 student who demonstrates the highest academic results at the end of the school year. The recipient is judged according to performance in English, Mathematics, History, Geography and Science. This prestigious award reflects exceptional academic achievement across multiple disciplines and is presented at the Grade 6 Graduation ceremony by the Principal. The award recognises students who have excelled throughout their primary school journey with a strong commitment to learning excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students; Must achieve highest academic results across English, Mathematics, History, Geography and Science; Open to all students enrolled at Templeton Primary School
special_conditions: Award is presented at Grade 6 Graduation. Winners are determined based on academic performance across the specified subjects at the end of Grade 6.

overview: Templeton Primary School offers uniform cost assistance to parents who require support meeting the expense. Parents experiencing financial difficulty can contact the Principal or Business Manager to discuss their situation and arrange alternative payment arrangements.
description: The school recognises that uniform costs may create financial hardship for some families and provides assistance to help parents meet these expenses. Families in need can contact the Principal or Business Manager to discuss their individual circumstances and arrange a customised payment plan. This program ensures that cost of uniforms does not create a barrier to students participating fully in school life. The assistance is available at the discretion of the Principal and is implemented flexibly based on individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Parents experiencing financial difficulty with uniform costs; Open to all enrolled students; Requires discussion with Principal or Business Manager
special_conditions: Assistance is provided at the discretion of the Principal. Parents must contact the Principal or Business Manager directly to discuss their situation and arrange payment plans. Implemented under the Uniform Policy.

overview: Taylors Lakes Secondary College recognises the achievements of selected students with applications to a Western Chances Scholarship. These scholarships are awarded to talented and motivated young people in Melbourne's West that experience financial hardship.
description: Each year, the College recognises the achievements of selected students with applications to a Western Chances Scholarship. These scholarships are awarded to talented and motivated young people in Melbourne's West that experience financial hardship. Successful applicants are able to receive grants of up to $2,000 to support their education. The application process involves submitting an application to Western Chances with evidence of both talent/achievement and financial need. Students are selected based on their ability to benefit from financial assistance while demonstrating motivation and promise in their studies.
eligibility_criteria: Young people from Melbourne's West; Experience financial hardship; Demonstrate talent and motivation; Be enrolled at the college or applying to enrol
special_conditions: Demonstrated financial need required. Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: The Learning Enhancement and Advancement Program (LEAP) is a program specifically designed to engage high ability students. This program takes in a cohort of 25 students each year and is designed for students who need an additional academic challenge. The program is strongly grounded in research on supporting gifted and talented students.
description: Taylors Lakes Secondary College offers the Learning Enhancement and Advancement Program (LEAP) to high-ability students entering Year 7. The program accepts a cohort of 25 students annually and focuses on challenging students at their point of need rather than using a one-size-fits-all acceleration model. The application process includes completing an application form (sections completed by student, family, and primary school teacher), taking ACER HAST testing, and providing copies of school reports and Grade 5 NAPLAN data. Applications generally open early in each year and students must be in Grade 6 at the time of application. The program provides personalized academic challenges tailored to individual strengths and focuses on both academic advancement and the wellbeing of highly able learners.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Grade 6; High academic ability; Perform in top tier on ACER HAST testing; Strong school reports and NAPLAN results; Open to all students regardless of gender; Enrol at Taylors Lakes Secondary College for Year 7
special_conditions: All staff working within the program receive training on meeting the academic and wellbeing needs of highly able students. The program acknowledges individual differences and focuses on challenging students at their point of need rather than uniform acceleration across all curriculum areas. Acceleration opportunities are also available in Years 10, 11 and 12 for individual subjects where appropriate.

overview: Taylors Lakes Secondary College operates a Football Academy that integrates intensive training in Australian Football League (AFL) and soccer with academic studies. The academy caters to both male and female students from Years 7 to 12 and is designed to blend passion for the sport with a robust education that provides the basis for further tertiary studies and future employment.
description: The Football Academy at Taylors Lakes Secondary College is designed to cater for students in the middle years (Years 7-9) and eventually the later years (Years 10-12) through VCE VET Sport and Recreation with a focus on soccer and AFL. The program is unique in its structure, aiming to establish a pathway that combines intensive football training with core curriculum studies. The program operates within the curriculum structure of the college and includes contributions from the local football community and governing bodies. Selection involves assessment by the Football Academy Co-ordinator. The academy provides separate specialized soccer and AFL streams within the Physical Education program and opportunities for students to compete at elevated levels, including interstate matches. The program integrates co-curricular football activities with VET pathways in senior years.
eligibility_criteria: Interested in soccer or AFL; Demonstrated ability in the chosen sport; Willing to participate in both academic and football components; Open to all year levels from Year 7 to Year 12; Selection based on assessment by Football Academy Co-ordinator
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: The Football Academy co-exists within the current curriculum structure of the college. Separate specialized soccer and AFL streams are provided within Physical Education classes. Students may progress through VCE VET Sport and Recreation in senior years with a focus on soccer and football. The program includes facilitation from the local football community and governing bodies of the respective codes.

overview: Taylors Lakes Secondary College offers an extensive and vibrant Instrumental Music and Dance Program with more than 250 students currently participating. Open to all students in Year 7-12, this ever-growing program offers a broad range of instruments and a range of dance styles, with many opportunities to perform at College concerts across the year and in the local community.
description: The Instrumental Music and Dance Program at Taylors Lakes Secondary College is one of the college's flagship programs with more than 250 students participating. The program is open to all students from Year 7 to Year 12 and offers instruction in a broad range of instruments including strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, and keyboard instruments, as well as various dance styles. Lessons and instrument hire are affordably priced. Students participate in multiple performing groups including vocal choir, guitar ensembles (junior and senior), keyboard ensembles (junior and senior), stage band, woodwind ensemble, and various dance style groups. The program provides performance opportunities at college concerts throughout the year and at community events. The performance program is integral to the musical life of the college, allowing students to develop their skills, build confidence, and facilitate peer connectedness through shared musical and dance interests.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in Year 7 to Year 12; No prior experience required; Students must be enrolled at Taylors Lakes Secondary College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Lessons and instrument hire are affordably priced. All students involved in the program derive great enjoyment and benefit from the performance opportunities. The performing groups provide a social aspect to music and dance activities, with students learning and performing together with others who share their interests. The program operates within the college's co-curricular framework with strong community and college concert opportunities throughout the year.

overview: Each year, Taylors Lakes Secondary College provides students in Years 7-12 the opportunity to audition for and participate in the College Production. The production is performed in front of families, friends, staff, peers and members of the wider community, allowing students to develop their skills and bolster their confidence.
description: The College Production at Taylors Lakes Secondary College is an annual performing arts opportunity open to all students from Year 7 to Year 12. The production is performed in front of families, friends, staff, peers, and members of the wider community. Students can participate in various capacities including lead roles, ensemble roles, makeup assistance, and backstage crew. The program allows students to develop their performance and theatrical skills, bolster their confidence, and facilitate peer connectedness through collaborative artistic work. The experience encompasses all aspects of theatrical production and offers something for students with diverse interests and abilities, from lead performers to technical crew members.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 7 to Year 12; Interest in performing arts or theatrical production; Willingness to audition or participate in various roles (performing or technical); Enrolled at Taylors Lakes Secondary College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: The production is performed annually in front of the wider school and community. Roles available include lead performers, ensemble members, makeup assistants, and backstage crew. The program emphasizes collaborative work and provides opportunities for students of all abilities and interests to contribute to the production.

overview: Templestowe College offers the Elite Sports Development Program for basketball to students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to the sport. The program provides intensive training under elite coaches for students performing at representative level or aspiring to elite competition.
description: The Elite Sports Development Program (ESDP) focuses on developing individual skills, understanding of game concepts, and team play necessary to compete at elite junior level. The program includes skill development sessions, shooting sessions, game play, general fitness, and decision-making training in 1-on-1, 2-on-2, and 3-on-3 play. Students compete in Basketball Victoria tournaments including the Victorian College Championships, Champions Cup, and 3-on-3 Challenge, as well as Secondary School Sport Victoria Regional competitions. Templestowe College holds tier one status with Basketball Victoria as an official partner in elite basketball development pathways. The program is designed for students currently performing at representative level or equivalent who aspire to elite competition. Continuation requires students to maintain high academic progress and commitment to studies alongside sport participation.
eligibility_criteria: Performing at representative level or equivalent; Aspiring to elite level basketball competition; Currently enrolled at Templestowe College; Must demonstrate commitment to academic studies; Must maintain high level of academic progress
special_conditions: The ESDP is available to existing students at TC. Students within the ESDP are full members of the TC Community and must be committed to the whole TC philosophy, not just participation in the basketball program. Applicants must demonstrate commitment to studies as well as their chosen sport. Continuation in ESDP requires students to maintain high level of academic progress.

overview: Templestowe College offers the Montessori Adolescent Program (MAP) for students aged 12-15 based on the teachings of Dr Maria Montessori. MAP provides an alternative educational pathway for younger students seeking a student-centered, self-directed learning approach within the Montessori philosophy.
description: The Montessori Adolescent Program (MAP) has been offered at Templestowe College since 2012 as an alternative program aimed at students in Years 7 to 9 age range, teaching students in the third plane of the Montessori approach to education. The program serves approximately 50 students and represents one of several educational styles offered at TC. If enrolled in MAP, students also undertake electives alongside the Montessori curriculum. The program is designed for students seeking self-directed learning and personal agency in their education. Entry requires attendance at MAP Expo Sessions to understand both the TC school philosophy and the Montessori Program before application.
eligibility_criteria: Age 12-15 or Years 7-9; Interest in Montessori-based education philosophy; Must attend TC school tour first; Must attend MAP Expo Session before applying
special_conditions: Prospective students must attend a TC school tour first before attending a MAP Expo Session to understand the full school philosophy. MAP Expo Sessions are offered every term with new sessions added regularly. Applicants must demonstrate commitment to Montessori principles and self-directed learning.

overview: Templestowe College offers the VCE Vocational Major for Years 11-12 students seeking an applied learning pathway. This program replaces the previous VCAL and prepares students for apprenticeships, traineeships, further education, or workforce entry.
description: The VCE Vocational Major (VCE VM) is a two-year vocational and applied learning program delivered over Years 11 and 12. It replaces the previous Intermediate and Senior VCAL and takes an applied learning approach where theoretical information connects to real-world context relevant to students' futures. The VCE VM is delivered Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday at TC, with Vocational Education and Training (VET) on Wednesday and Structured Workplace Learning (SWL) on Friday. Students do not receive external examinations, study scores, or Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR), but those completing satisfactory requirements receive a Victorian Certificate of Education marked 'Vocational Major'. Entry requires application, interview, and panel review to assess suitability. All applicants and parents/guardians are invited to interview; if not suitable for VCE VM at that time, a pathways meeting with a TC Careers Advisor identifies alternatives. Places may be limited.
eligibility_criteria: Years 11-12 students; Looking at apprenticeship, TAFE or employment pathway; Clear career pathway that does not require ATAR; Mature enough to manage complex timetable; Must submit VCE VM and VET application before closing date; Must attend VCE VM interview
special_conditions: Places in VCE VM may be limited. All VCE VM applicants and parents/guardians must attend interview for assessment of suitability. Panel review determines provisional place offer. If not suitable at time of application, pathways meeting scheduled with TC Careers Advisor. Submission of VCE VM and VET applications before closing dates and attendance at interview provide best chance of securing place.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Academic Scholarships to reward students of exceptional academic potential who demonstrate outstanding achievement in entrance assessments. The scholarship recognises students who excel in standardised testing and show strong academic ability.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through performance on the EduTest exam, a third-party educational assessment. The EduTest examination assesses verbal reasoning, numerical reasoning, reading comprehension, mathematics and written expression. These scholarships support students who achieve outstanding results on the standardised assessment. The selection process is merit-based, with results determining scholarship eligibility. The financial value and duration of awards vary by individual assessment outcomes and are subject to renewal conditions based on continued academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must sit the EduTest exam; Demonstrates exceptional academic ability; Open to co-educational applicants
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are closed. 2027 Applications will open November 2025. Applicants are required to sit the EduTest exam hosted by a third-party educational assessment organisation.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Ivy Scholarships to students in Year 4, 5 and 6 who demonstrate meritorious achievement, a passion for continuous learning and strong character strengths. The scholarship recognises well-rounded and curious learners who display a genuine interest in the world around them.
description: Ivy Scholarships at the primary level are awarded for meritorious achievement, a passion for continuous learning and strong character strengths. The College seeks applicants entering Year 4, 5 or 6 who are well-rounded and curious about learning and who display an interest in the world around them. Selection is based on ability in both academic and co-curricular areas, school reports and the results of interviews. The scholarship recognises all-round excellence rather than academic merit alone, supporting students who demonstrate strong character and a commitment to learning. The financial value and duration are subject to individual assessment and renewal based on maintaining scholarship conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, 5 or 6; Demonstrates meritorious achievement; Shows passion for continuous learning; Displays strong character strengths; Well-rounded and curious learners; Shows interest in the world around them
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are closed. 2027 Applications will open November 2025. Selection is based on school reports, interviews and demonstrated ability in academic and co-curricular areas.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Ivy Scholarships for boarding students in Years 9–12 who wish to join the boarding community. The scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate meritorious achievement and strong character, recognising the value of boarding access to quality education for those who would otherwise be unable to access it.
description: Ivy Scholarships for boarding students (Years 9–12) are available to students who would like to join the boarding house community and demonstrate the required academic and personal qualities. These scholarships have the same requirements as the general excellence application. The College recognises that the tyranny of distance can limit access to quality education and offers boarding scholarships to provide opportunities for regional, rural and remote students. Selection is based on ability in both academic and co-curricular areas, school reports and interviews. The financial value, including potential accommodation support, varies by individual circumstances. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to boarding and College values for the duration of their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Interested in boarding; Demonstrates academic and co-curricular ability; Meets general excellence application requirements; From regional, rural or remote areas
special_conditions: Requirements for a boarding scholarship are the same as those for the general excellence application. Ivy Scholarships are available for students who would like to join the Boarding House community.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Tower Scholarships to students in Years 9–12 who aspire to and prioritise receiving a quality, independent education. The scholarship is designed to support worthy students who are committed to excellence across academic and co-curricular pursuits.
description: Tower Scholarships are offered by The Hamilton and Alexandra College Foundation to students in Years 9–12 who aspire and prioritise a quality, independent education. These scholarships support students who demonstrate commitment to the College's values and educational mission. The scholarship recognises students who show potential for all-round excellence and contribution to the College community. Selection considers academic ability, co-curricular involvement and character. The financial value and specific conditions of Tower Scholarships vary by individual award and are subject to renewal based on maintaining academic progress and College expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Aspires to quality independent education; Prioritises education commitment; Demonstrates College values; Open to co-educational applicants
special_conditions: Tower Scholarships are offered by The Hamilton and Alexandra College Foundation. Recipients must maintain commitment to the College and demonstrate continued dedication to learning and College values.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Year 7 and 8 Boarding Bursaries to support access to boarding education. For Year 7 students, the bursary is available for all boarders. For Year 8 students, eligibility requires residence more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300.
description: The Year 7 and 8 Boarding Bursaries provide a 100% reduction of boarding fees for one year to eligible students. This bursary is available to all Year 7 boarding students for one year, and for Year 8 students, the bursary is applicable for one year if they live more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300. The bursary is open to both new and current College families. The bursary covers boarding fees only and does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees. Recipients are required to remain boarding at The Hamilton and Alexandra College through to the end of Year 12. If a student leaves before this time, the bursary must be repaid in full. Applications for 2025/2026 are open year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students: Open to all Year 7 boarding students; Year 8 students: Must live more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300; Open to both new and current College families; Must commit to boarding through end of Year 12
special_conditions: All students (Year 7 and 8) that receive this bursary are required to remain boarding at The Hamilton and Alexandra College through to the end of Year 12. If you leave before this time, you will be obligated to pay this bursary back in full. The bursary does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees. Applications for 2025/2026 are open.

overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Equity Scholarships to support families who require financial assistance and who understand and prioritise the benefit of receiving a quality, independent education. The scholarship is designed to enable worthy students to access the College's educational opportunities regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Equity Scholarships are designed to support families who require financial assistance and who understand and prioritise the benefit of receiving a quality, independent education. The College is passionate about supporting worthy students who cannot afford the full tuition fees to benefit from the extraordinary education offered at the College. These scholarships recognise that financial circumstances should not prevent students from accessing quality independent education. Applications are open year-round, allowing families to apply at any time throughout the year. The selection process considers financial need and the student's potential to thrive in the College environment. The financial value of Equity Scholarships varies based on individual family circumstances and assessed need.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrates financial need; Family requires financial assistance; Values quality independent education; Student demonstrates potential to succeed; Open to all year levels and backgrounds
special_conditions: Applications are open year-round. We are passionate about supporting worthy students, who cannot afford the full tuition fees, to benefit from the extraordinary education offered at our College.

overview: The Grange P-12 College offers the High Achievers Program to enrich and extend students' learning. The program is designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. It develops skills required for university-level study and entry to the professions.
description: The High Achievers Program is available to students from Year 7 through to Year 12. Students who aspire to enter the program are required to provide a copy of their most recent NAPLAN results, semester reports, a written statement stating why they would like to be considered, and a Year 6 Teacher support statement. The program allows students to move through school with their peers, while challenging them with a curriculum and extra-curricular activities appropriate for their learning style and ability. Students in the High Achievers Program participate in a range of enrichment activities including access to Deakin University, RMIT and Australian Catholic University programs such as mentoring, pathways planning and visits to campus. They have access to the University of Melbourne 'Kwong Lee Dow Young Scholars' Program, an academic enrichment program designed to support high-achieving Victorian students. Students also participate in a wide range of competitions including Premier's Reading Challenge, Dorothea MacKellar poetry competition, Australian Maths competition and Computational and Algorithmic Thinking competitions. Students in the High Achievers Program are also offered an additional opportunity to study Hindi as a language.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Most recent NAPLAN results in top tier; Semester reports demonstrating strong academic performance; Written statement of intent; Year 6 Teacher support statement required; Continuation available through Year 12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Application forms provided to students upon acceptance to the College. Program aims to develop deep understanding and critical thinking skills, enhance problem-solving abilities and extend analytic and creative skills.

overview: The Sports Science Academy at The Grange P-12 College is a select entry program that pursues the sporting ability of students through an academic lens. The program builds the capacities of students as they participate in and around sporting environments in a variety of different roles. Student athletes receive specialised physical and theoretical training in state of the art facilities.
description: The Sports Science Academy is a selective entry program available to students from Grade 4 through to Year 12, with key entry points at Grade 4, Year 7 and Year 11. The program focuses on five pillar sports: Rugby, Soccer, Volleyball, Basketball and Netball. Students participate in before and after school sports training with specialised coaches alongside formal academic studies in Mathematics and English. The program requires students to complete formal academic studies while developing sporting skills. Students gain specialised physical and theoretical training working alongside industry professionals. The program offers a pathway through VCE, VCE Vocational Major (VM), and VET to ensure students complete Year 12 studies in an appropriate yet challenging learning environment. There is high demand from currently enrolled students so early contact with the College is important. No financial assistance is provided.
eligibility_criteria: Strong sporting ability and academic skills; Commitment to aspiring to leadership roles within the College; Interest in career within sports related industries; Desire to enrol in sports courses at University or TAFE; Key entry points are Grade 4, Year 7 and Year 11; Other year level enrolments considered on case-by-case basis; Assessment via further testing; Positions not guaranteed based on previous involvement
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students are required to undertake further testing for Sports Science Academy enrollment. All students must work within the context of College Values of Respect, Learning and Working Together as well as values supporting integrity within sport: Strong, Proud and Humble. The Athlete Development Program is offered with varying training and academic regimes at different year levels. Rugby stream has partnerships with Victorian Rugby Union and Victorian Rugby League; Soccer stream has partnerships with Football Federation Victoria and Futsal Victoria; Volleyball stream has partnership with Volleyball Victoria.

overview: The SALT Program (Student Advanced Learning Tutorials) at The Grange P-12 College provides individualised and additional small group instruction for students who are very advanced with Literacy and Numeracy skills. The program is designed to improve learning outcomes for advanced students through specialised instruction.
description: The SALT Program provides individualised and additional small group 'Advanced Learning Teams' for students demonstrating advanced Literacy and Numeracy skills. Groups of 5-10 students are provided with a specialised program and a dedicated Teacher tutor. Students are withdrawn from their regular class for up to two hours per week to engage in this program. Educators and parents who have had children participate in these small learning teams report a range of benefits including enhanced student learning, motivation, accomplishment and self-esteem. The program provides academic challenge and progression at a faster rate that is more closely aligned with the student's ability and interests. The College is committed to improving learning outcomes for all students and uses assessment and learning growth data to identify students appropriate for the SALT Program.
eligibility_criteria: Advanced Literacy and Numeracy skills identified through assessment; Selection based on student learning data and assessments; Students identified as very advanced in their year level; Available from Prep through secondary years
special_conditions: Students withdrawn from regular class for up to two hours per week. Group size of 5-10 students per tutor. Selection based on teacher recommendations and assessment data. Program focuses on accelerated learning aligned to student ability.

overview: The Grange P-12 College offers a specialist Gymnastics Program for Foundation through Year 6 students who engage in weekly sessions designed to stimulate fine motor skills, coordination and perceptual motor skills to enhance learning. The program is unique to the Callistemon P-6 Campus.
description: The Grange P-12 College has developed specialist gymnastics classes for Foundation to Year 6 students who engage in weekly programs designed to stimulate fine motor skills, coordination and perceptual motor skills to enhance their learning. The Callistemon P-6 Campus is the only school providing this unique Gymnasium Program to promote learning. The program of gymnastics, physical movement, coordination and fitness is designed to better assist students' learning in the early years of schooling. Research shows positive associations between children's physical activity participation and academic achievement. Short bouts of exercise benefit executive control and function. The program consists of sessions in the gymnasium with their classroom teacher, a Physical Education Teacher and, at times, a specialist gymnast for students who wish to participate in training for competitive level gymnastics. The program is available to all students enrolled at the Callistemon P-6 Campus.
eligibility_criteria: Enrollment at Callistemon Campus (Prep-Year 6); Open to all students at Prep-Year 6 level; Weekly participation in gymnasium sessions
year_levels: Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Specialist gymnast available for students wishing to train for competitive level gymnastics. Sessions run weekly with classroom teachers and Physical Education Teachers. Program includes development of fine motor skills, coordination and perceptual motor skills to support academic learning.

overview: The Grange P-12 College offers a Hindi Language Program encompassing Indian language and culture from Prep through secondary years. The program is designed to develop listening, speaking, reading and writing skills in Hindi.
description: The Grange P-12 College offers a language program encompassing Hindi culture at Years Prep through 6 and expanding to secondary year levels. Students in Prep and Year 1 participate in language lessons (Hindi) each week alongside Physical Education and Visual Art. In Years 7 and 8, the Hindi course introduces students to Indian language and culture. It aims at developing listening, speaking, reading and writing skills and helps students develop their ability to converse in Hindi. Students in the High Achievers Program from Year 7 onwards are offered an additional opportunity to study Hindi as a language, reflecting the enrichment focus of that program. The program integrates cultural knowledge alongside language development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Prep through Year 12; Students in Prep and Year 1 have weekly Hindi language lessons; Years 7-8 students may elect Hindi course; Students in High Achievers Program (Year 7 onwards) offered additional Hindi study opportunity
year_levels: Prep, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8
special_conditions: Prep and Year 1 students participate in weekly Hindi language lessons as part of their curriculum. The program develops listening, speaking, reading, writing and conversational skills in Hindi. Available as additional enrichment opportunity for High Achievers students.

school_name: The King David School - Junior School

overview: The Knox School offers a limited number of Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in students entering Years 7, 9, and 10. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential, with selection based on performance in a specialised examination and comprehensive portfolio assessment.
description: The Knox School offers Academic Scholarships for entry into Years 7, 9, and 10 in 2027. The amount offered with each scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students must complete an EduTest academic scholarship examination as well as submit a comprehensive portfolio including school reports. The selection process involves shortlisting by a scholarship committee based on examination results and portfolios. Shortlisted applicants are invited to meet with the Principal. The scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. Selection criteria includes demonstrated academic achievement and potential, commitment to The Knox School Values, excellence in academic areas or STEM/Arts/Humanities, engagement in curriculum and co-curricular activities, leadership evidence, and positive contribution to school activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 10 in 2027; Open to external, current, and future TKS students; Must demonstrate academic achievement and potential; Must show commitment to The Knox School Values; Students on student or temporary resident visas are not eligible to apply; Must submit school reports and portfolio; Scholarship commitment binding until Year 12
special_conditions: The commitment is binding and continues until completion of Year 12. Exam results remain the property of The Knox School and are not provided to parents. Shortlisted applicants are invited to meet the Principal and parents. All supporting documents must be submitted in PDF format to registrar@knox.vic.edu.au.

overview: The Knox School offers General Excellence Scholarships recognising outstanding achievement across multiple disciplines and pathways. These scholarships are designed to attract well-rounded students who excel in sport, entrepreneurship, service, or other areas of excellence beyond purely academic achievement.
description: The Knox School offers General Excellence Scholarships available in multiple pathways including Sport, Entrepreneurship, Service, and other areas of excellence. The amount offered with each scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. This program recognises students who demonstrate excellence across their chosen pathway while maintaining commitment to The Knox School Values. Applications require submission of portfolios and evidence of achievement in the selected pathway. The selection process involves shortlisting by a scholarship committee and invited meetings with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 9, or 10; Must demonstrate excellence in at least one pathway: Sport, Entrepreneurship, Service, or other areas; Must show commitment to The Knox School Values; Must be willing to contribute to school community; Students on student or temporary resident visas are not eligible; Scholarship commitment binding until Year 12
special_conditions: The commitment is binding and continues until completion of Year 12. Application forms must be completed and supporting documents submitted in PDF format. Selection based on demonstrated excellence in chosen pathway and commitment to school values.

overview: The Knox School offers Young Entrepreneurs Scholarships to support students who demonstrate entrepreneurial spirit, creativity, and innovation. The scholarship provides 50% tuition fee reduction and includes mentoring and specialised support for developing entrepreneurial ideas from concept to implementation.
description: The Knox School offers Young Entrepreneurs Scholarships for entry into Years 7, 9, or 10 in 2023 and potentially subsequent years. The scholarship includes 50% off tuition fees. The scholarship recognises and supports students who are entrepreneurial-minded, creative, inventive, and courageous. Recipients benefit from a customised school program combining strong academics with co-curricular activities focused on developing entrepreneurial ideas. The scholarship includes adult mentoring and support to help students bring their entrepreneurial visions to reality. The program develops skills in creativity, collaboration, communication, critical thinking, and analytical skills. Recipients are positioned to develop sophisticated business and employment prospects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 10; Must demonstrate entrepreneurial interest, drive, and creative ideas; Must be willing to engage in both academic and co-curricular programs; Must show evidence of creative and critical thinking; Must be willing to develop entrepreneurial ideas from concept to reality; Demonstration of resilience and growth mindset required
special_conditions: Scholarship includes 50% off tuition fees and personalised school program support. Adult mentoring provided to develop entrepreneurial ideas. Recipients participate in challenging and engaging entrepreneurial pursuits designed to shape creative and critical thinking skills.

overview: The Knox School offers Alumni Scholarships to students with family connections to the school who demonstrate strong character and commitment to The Knox School Values. This scholarship recognises and supports the next generation of Knox School community members.
description: The Knox School offers Alumni Scholarships to support students who have family connections to the school through parents, siblings, or other relatives who are Knox School alumni (Falconians). The amount offered with each scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. This program recognises students who maintain the values and traditions of The Knox School community. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to The Knox School Values, potential for academic and personal growth, and ability to contribute positively to the school community. The application process involves submission of supporting documentation and portfolio review.
eligibility_criteria: Student must have family member(s) who are Knox School alumni (Falconians); Open to students entering appropriate year levels; Must demonstrate commitment to The Knox School Values; Must show potential to contribute positively to school community; Scholarship commitment binding until Year 12; Students on student or temporary resident visas are not eligible
special_conditions: Requires demonstration of family connection to The Knox School through alumni status. Commitment binding until completion of Year 12. Selection considers potential to benefit from school program and contribute to school community.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers the Ivy Scholarship to reward meritorious achievement in entering Year 4, 5 or 6 students. The scholarship recognises well-rounded and curious learners who display strong character strengths and a passion for continuous learning.
description: Ivy Scholarships are awarded based on ability in both academic and co-curricular areas, school reports and the results of interviews. Applicants are sought who are well-rounded and curious about learning and who display an interest in the world around them. The selection process includes interviews and consideration of school reports alongside academic and co-curricular assessment. The exact financial benefit and duration are not specified on the scholarships page, though scholarships are offered for continuing education through the College. Recipients are expected to support the College's values and educational mission.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, 5 or 6; Well-rounded and curious about learning; Display an interest in the world around them; Applications for 2026 are closed; 2027 applications will open November 2025
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are closed. 2027 Applications will open November 2025.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers the Ivy Scholarship with a boarding component for students in Years 9 to 12. This scholarship recognises meritorious achievement and supports students from regional and rural areas who wish to access the boarding community.
description: Ivy Scholarships are available for students who would like to join the Boarding House community. The requirements for a boarding scholarship are the same as those for the general excellence application, which includes assessment of academic ability, co-curricular involvement, school reports and interview results.
