The scholarship is designed to support families affected by the tyranny of distance in accessing quality education. Boarding requirements must be maintained throughout enrolment at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 9, 10, 11, 12; Interested in joining the Boarding House community; Academic and co-curricular merit required; Interview required
special_conditions: Families are welcome to apply for Boarding Scholarships when their children are in Years 9-12 through either the Ivy or Tower Scholarship programs.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Tower Scholarship is available through The Hamilton and Alexandra College for students in Years 9 to 12. The scholarship recognises meritorious achievement and strong character strengths in secondary students.
description: Tower Scholarships are offered for Years 9-12 students and are available for both day and boarding students. The scholarship programme recognises well-rounded students with strong academic achievement and character strengths. The exact selection criteria, financial benefit, and duration are not detailed on the College's scholarships page. Families are welcome to apply for boarding scholarships through the Tower Scholarship program when their children are in Years 9-12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 9, 10, 11, 12; Meritorious achievement required; Strong character strengths required
special_conditions: Families are welcome to apply for Boarding Scholarships when their children are in Years 9-12 through either the Ivy or Tower Scholarship programs.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Academic Scholarships to reward exceptional academic ability. Applicants are assessed through the EduTest examination, a standardised educational assessment tool used to identify gifted and talented students.
description: For Academic Scholarships, applicants are required to sit the EduTest exam hosted by a third-party educational assessment organisation. This standardised test is used to identify students with exceptional academic ability and potential. The specific year levels eligible, financial value, and duration of this scholarship are not detailed on the published scholarships page. Applicants should contact the College directly for comprehensive details about the Academic Scholarship program.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the EduTest exam; Exceptional academic ability required; Specific year level eligibility not specified on webpage
special_conditions: Applicants are required to sit the EduTest exam hosted by a third-party educational assessment organisation.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Year 7 and 8 Boarding Bursaries to support new and current College families with boarding costs. This bursary provides a full reduction of boarding fees for one year to eligible students.
description: This bursary is a 100% reduction of boarding fees for one year. The Year 7 and 8 Boarding Bursaries are open to both new and current College families. For Year 7 students, the bursary is available for one year. For Year 8 students, this bursary is applicable for one year if you live more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300. The bursary does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees. All students who receive this bursary are required to remain boarding at the College through to the end of Year 12, and must repay the full amount if they leave before completing Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 and Year 8 students; Year 7: available to all boarding students; Year 8: must live more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300; Open to both new and current College families; Must remain at College through Year 12
special_conditions: This bursary is a 100% reduction of boarding fees for one year only. The bursary does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees. All students (Year 7 and 8) that receive this bursary are required to remain boarding at The Hamilton and Alexandra College through to the end of Year 12. If you leave before this time, you will be obligated to pay this bursary back in full.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Hamilton and Alexandra College offers Equity Scholarships to support families requiring financial assistance to access a quality independent education. These scholarships recognise worthy students who cannot otherwise afford full tuition fees.
description: Equity Scholarships are designed to support families who require financial assistance and who understand and prioritise the benefit of receiving a quality, independent education. The College is passionate about supporting worthy students who cannot afford the full tuition fees to benefit from the extraordinary education offered. Applications are open year-round, allowing families to apply at any time when financial circumstances necessitate support. The exact financial benefit and amount of bursaries available are not specified on the published page. Applicants should contact the Registrar for detailed information about available support.
eligibility_criteria: Families require financial assistance; Genuine commitment to quality independent education; Students demonstrated to be worthy of support; Year level eligibility not specified
special_conditions: Applications are open year-round. Equity Scholarships are designed to support families who require financial assistance.

overview: Undera Primary School receives the Schools Plus grant, which enables the school to provide all students with free education. This program covers essential educational expenses including stationery, digital devices, excursions, uniforms, and annual swimming programs.
description: The Schools Plus grant is a Victorian government initiative that enables Undera Primary School to provide free education to all enrolled students. The grant covers a comprehensive range of educational costs and learning resources without direct cost to families. Specifically, it provides access to stationery, iPads and laptops for student use, school camps and excursions, school uniforms, and participation in the annual swimming program. The grant supports all students currently enrolled at the school and is available for the duration of their enrolment. This is a government-funded program with no application process required—all students automatically benefit from the funding. The program continues to support educational equity and access for rural school communities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students currently enrolled at Undera Primary School; Must be enrolled in a Victorian government primary school; Covers Foundation to Year 6 students
special_conditions: This is an automatic government grant with no application required. The benefit is provided to all students as part of the school's standard funding model. Duration extends for the period the student remains enrolled at the school.

school_name: University High School-Elizabeth Blackburn School of Sciences
overview: University High School offers the Acceleration Program to gifted and talented students entering Year 7. The program is designed for academically capable students who can potentially complete their full secondary education in just five years, rather than the traditional six, by condensing the standard Years 7-10 curriculum into an intensive three-year timeframe.
description: The Acceleration Program is a special program for gifted and talented students at University High School. Admission is at Year 7 level only, and selection is based on aptitude tests administered through Edutest and interviews. Students in the program benefit from an extended curriculum across Key Learning Areas including English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities, Languages, The Arts, Physical Education and Technology, where their higher cognitive skills allow rapid advancement. The program is cohort-based, providing supportive peer-group environments. After completing Years 8 and 9, students may select from a range of programs where they can complete their secondary education in either five or six years. Students may take extra VCE subjects, with the school offering approximately 130 units in 39 different VCE subjects in a typical year. Participants invariably achieve excellent VCE results and are admitted to tertiary courses of their preference. There is a registration fee payable to Edutest which is non-refundable. All students offered a confirmed place in Year 7 at University High School are offered the opportunity to apply to the Year 7 Acceleration Program.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Offered confirmed placement at University High School for Year 7; Must register and complete aptitude tests through Edutest; Australian citizen or holder of an eligible resident's visa; Pass aptitude tests and interview assessment
special_conditions: Admission to this program is at Year 7 level only. Applications after registrations have closed are not permitted. There is a non-refundable registration fee payable to Edutest. Edutest registrations strictly close on the specified date. The aptitude tests are conducted by Edutest and must be completed on the scheduled examination date.

school_name: University High School-Elizabeth Blackburn School of Sciences
overview: Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences (EBS) is a branch of University High School designed for Years 11 and 12 students with a passion and potential for success in science and mathematics. The program provides exposure to cutting-edge innovations and excellence in contemporary science and mathematics education through a unique partnership with the University of Melbourne and the Bio21 Institute.
description: Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences is a specialist Year 11 and 12 program cater for 200 high-performing science students from across Victoria who have a genuine passion for pursuing careers in science or mathematics. The entrance process involves two stages: first, a formal assessment administered by Edutest covering abilities and aptitudes in mathematics, science reasoning, verbal reasoning, and reading comprehension (this is not a test of subject-specific knowledge); second, students who proceed past the written exam are invited to attend an interview and group activities with EBS staff. Students do not have to reside within the school zone to apply. Students must be Australian citizens or holders of eligible resident's visas at the time of application. EBS has been designed in partnership with the Victorian Department of Education, University of Melbourne and Bio21 and provides a rigorous academic program focused on sciences and mathematics in a supportive and engaging environment. The program includes university-style lecture theatres, laboratories, collaborative study areas and its own resource centre within a purpose-built facility opened in 2014. There is a $150 non-refundable registration fee payable to Edutest for the entrance examination.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 10 or Year 11 students; Genuine passion and intense interest in pursuing a career in sciences or mathematics; Talent and potential for high performance in sciences, mathematics and related fields; Ability to thrive and engage in an innovative learning environment while preserving University High School values and traditions; Australian citizen or holder of an eligible resident's visa at time of application; Must not be required to reside within school zone; Diverse backgrounds and life experiences welcome; Must register and sit formal entrance examination; Late applications will not be accepted
special_conditions: Maximum capacity of 100 Year 11 and 100 Year 12 students total. No sharing or transfer of results to or from other schools. Late registrations cannot be accepted. The Edutest assessment is not a test of subject-specific knowledge. Specific information about the entrance examination will not be provided by the school to ensure equity for all applicants. Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for interview stage. EBS is limited to a maximum number of students from each school.

overview: University High School offers the Acceleration Program to gifted students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential. Students enter the program and complete Years 8 and 9 before selecting from senior pathway programs for Years 10 to 12, with opportunities to accelerate into VCE subjects early.
description: The Acceleration Program is designed for gifted students entering Year 7 at University High School. Students within this program complete Years 8 and 9 and then have the opportunity to select from a range of Senior Pathways programs for Years 10 to 12. High-achieving students can apply to accelerate into a VCE subject at Year 10, with the expectation of completing a full VCE program in Year 11 (6 subjects) and Year 12 (5 subjects). The school offers over 40 different VCE subjects. Additionally, students can participate in the University of Melbourne's Extension Program, taking first-year degree subjects while still studying for their VCE. Admission involves aptitude testing conducted by Edutest and interviews with shortlisted applicants. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence, aptitude test results, and potential for accelerated learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at a Victorian school or equivalent; Students must pass the Edutest aptitude test administered by an external provider; Australian citizen or holder of eligible resident's visa; All students offered confirmed Year 7 placement at University High School are eligible to apply
special_conditions: Registration for Edutest is non-refundable. Registration link emailed with 2026 Year 7 confirmation of placement. Interviews conducted with shortlisted applicants at start of Term 4. Students and parents informed of outcomes in Term 4. Acceleration Program falls under school's Enrolment Management Plan.

overview: University High School offers the Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences program for Years 11 and 12 students with passion and potential in Science and Mathematics. This selective-entry campus provides a rigorous academic program combining VCE study with university-level research mentorship from the University of Melbourne and Bio21 Institute.
description: Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences (EBS) is a campus of University High School designed for students in Years 11 and 12 who have a passion and potential for success in Science and Mathematics. The program exposes students to the latest innovations and excellence in contemporary science and mathematics teaching through partnerships with the University of Melbourne and the Bio21 Institute. The selective entry program admits up to 100 Year 11 and 100 Year 12 students. All EBS students complete a rigorous VCE program including English Studies, Mathematical Methods, Chemistry and Extended Investigation. Year 11 students conduct university-level research projects under the close guidance of University of Melbourne and/or Bio21 research mentors, gaining valuable technical and analytical skills. All research is presented in a scientific symposium at year-end. Year 12 students can pursue different innovative curriculum pathways in STEM fields and have opportunities to study university extension subjects alongside Year 12 subjects. Admission requires an Edutest entrance examination assessing scientific aptitude, communication, mathematics and English skills, followed by interviews with shortlisted applicants. No residency requirement - students may apply from anywhere in Victoria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 and 11 students for Year 11 and 12 entry respectively; Australian citizen or holder of eligible resident's visa at time of application; Must sit mandatory Edutest entrance examination; No requirement to reside within school zone; Selection based on aptitude test results and interview performance; School seeks students from diverse backgrounds with passion and potential in Science and Mathematics
number_awarded: Up to 100 annually per year level
special_conditions: Entrance examination is specific to the school; No sharing or transfer of results to/from other schools; Capacity of 100 Year 11 and 100 Year 12 students annually; Only one intake per year; Students required to maintain commitment to Science and Mathematics focus; University of Melbourne and Bio21 research mentorship provided for Year 11 students; Extended Investigation subject includes university-style research presentation.

overview: The Acceleration Program at University High School is a selective entry program for gifted and talented students entering Year 7. The program allows students to complete the Years 7-10 curriculum in three years, progressing through an accelerated pathway that allows earlier entry into VCE and senior pathways programs.
description: The Acceleration Program is designed for high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential. Students who gain entry complete Years 7-10 curriculum content in three years rather than four, with approximately one year of curriculum covered every three terms. This accelerated pathway allows students to begin VCE studies earlier and select from a range of Senior Pathways programs for Years 10-12. Students may accelerate into individual VCE subjects at Year 10 or apply to participate in the University of Melbourne Extension Program, where they take first-year degree subjects while studying for their VCE. As a state government school, University High School does not charge tuition fees, though parents are asked to pay a modest annual levy. The program is not means-tested and selection is based purely on academic merit and aptitude.
eligibility_criteria: Entry at Year 7 only; Must pass aptitude tests administered through Edutest; Must complete an Expression of Interest form; Must attend an interview at the school; Australian citizen or eligible resident visa holder; Confirmed placement at University High School in Year 7
special_conditions: Admission is at Year 7 level only. There is a non-refundable registration fee payable to Edutest for the aptitude test. The program falls under the school's Enrolment Management Plan, which means all students offered a confirmed place in Year 7 at University High School are offered the opportunity to apply. Upon completion of Year 9, students select from a range of Senior Pathways programs for Years 10-12. Participants typically achieve excellent VCE results and are admitted to tertiary courses of their preference.

overview: Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences is a specialist selective entry program for Years 11 and 12 students with a passion for science and mathematics. The program provides an advanced academic pathway with partnership access to the University of Melbourne and Bio21 Institute research mentors, offering students exposure to laboratory research environments and cutting-edge STEM education.
description: Elizabeth Blackburn Sciences (EBS) is a branch campus of University High School designed to cater to and engage young people in Years 11 and 12 who have demonstrated passion and high potential for success in science and mathematics. The program features university-style lecture theatres, specialized laboratories, collaborative study areas and its own resource centre opened in 2014. Year 11 students conduct supervised research projects under the guidance of University of Melbourne and Bio21 Institute research mentors in disciplines of their choosing, developing technical and analytical skills alongside understanding of scientific processes. Research is presented in an end-of-year scientific symposium. The program does not charge tuition fees as it is part of a Victorian government school, though annual levies apply. EBS has a capacity of 100 Year 11 and 100 Year 12 students per year and does not require students to reside within the school zone to apply. Selection is based on demonstrated passion for science/mathematics careers, aptitude and potential for high performance, and ability to thrive in an innovative learning environment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students entering Year 11; Open to Year 11 students entering Year 12; Must be an Australian citizen or eligible resident visa holder; Must pass Edutest entrance examination; Must complete interview and written/group tasks at school; Demonstrated genuine passion and interest in pursuing a career in sciences or mathematics; Capacity limited with selection from each school capped
special_conditions: Applications open in Term 2. Late applications will not be accepted. EBS is limited to a maximum number of students from each school. Students shortlisted after Stage 1 (Edutest) proceed to Stage 2 interview day which includes individual interviews and written and group tasks. Specific information about Stage 2 tasks is not provided in advance to ensure equity. Students do not need to reside within the University High School zone. All research undertaken by EBS students is presented at a scientific symposium. EBS students typically demonstrate the ability to achieve high ATARs; within the program, 25% of students attain ATARs of 95 or higher with a mean ATAR of 85.2.

overview: Victoria University Secondary College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise students with outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is available to students with exemplary academic records who have demonstrated outstanding application and achievement in English, Humanities, Mathematics or Science.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is valued at $500 per year and is redeemable against College expenses including devices, uniform and textbooks. These scholarships are available to students with an exemplary academic record who have demonstrated outstanding application and achievement in one or more of English, Humanities, Mathematics or Science along with a strong performance in general studies and technology. Students will display a passion for learning, outstanding work ethic and a preparedness to participate fully in the life of Victoria University Secondary College. Selection is based on a written application and interview, with students required to present their latest Semester Report and NAPLAN results together with a portfolio of their work.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry students; Exemplary academic record in English, Humanities, Mathematics or Science; Strong performance in general studies and technology; Demonstrated outstanding application and achievement; Passion for learning and outstanding work ethic; Preparedness to participate fully in the life of the College
special_conditions: Scholarships are valued at $500 and are redeemable against College expenses including devices, uniform and textbooks. Applicants are not required to sit an entry exam. Selection is based on a written application and interview. Students are also required to present their latest Semester Report and NAPLAN results together with a portfolio of their work which best supports the scholarship they are applying for. Parents/carers should follow the normal Year 7 enrolment process through their child's primary school. Scholarships are now closed for Year 7 entry in 2026.

overview: Victoria University Secondary College offers the Leadership Scholarship to recognise students who have demonstrated leadership skills and commitment to their community. The scholarship is available to students who have demonstrated leadership skills in Primary School as well as citizenship and a commitment to the broader community.
description: The Leadership Scholarship is valued at $500 per year and is redeemable against College expenses including devices, uniform and textbooks. These scholarships are available to students who have demonstrated leadership skills in Primary School as well as citizenship and a commitment to the broader community. Students will be encouraging and supportive of their peers, and display a strong academic performance, outstanding work ethic and positive behaviours, together with a preparedness to participate fully in the life of Victoria University Secondary College. Selection is based on a written application and interview, with students required to present their latest Semester Report and NAPLAN results together with a portfolio of their work supporting their leadership qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry students; Demonstrated leadership skills in Primary School; Demonstrated citizenship and commitment to broader community; Strong academic performance; Outstanding work ethic and positive behaviours; Encouraging and supportive of peers; Preparedness to participate fully in the life of the College
special_conditions: Scholarships are valued at $500 and are redeemable against College expenses including devices, uniform and textbooks. Applicants are not required to sit an entry exam. Selection is based on a written application and interview. Students are also required to present their latest Semester Report and NAPLAN results together with a portfolio of their work which best supports the scholarship they are applying for. Parents/carers should follow the normal Year 7 enrolment process through their child's primary school. Scholarships are now closed for Year 7 entry in 2026.

overview: Victoria University Secondary College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognise students with outstanding sporting skills and commitment to excellence both on and off the field. The scholarship is available to keen sports persons who have demonstrated outstanding sporting skills, positive behaviours and a commitment to doing their best in both sporting and academic development.
description: The Sports Scholarship is valued at $500 per year and is redeemable against College expenses including devices, uniform and textbooks. overview: Wantirna Primary School recognises that some families may experience financial difficulty and offers a range of support options to ensure all students can participate fully in school programs. Financial assistance is available to help with costs of camps, sporting activities, excursions, educational items and extra-curricular activities. The school operates a flexible parent payment policy where families experiencing hardship can discuss alternative payment arrangements with the school. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis in accordance with the Department of Education Victoria's Financial Help for Families policy. Families should contact the school office to discuss their circumstances and available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial hardship; Parents or carers receiving valid Centrelink income support payments; Holders of valid Health Care Card, Low Income Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Youth Allowance recipients
special_conditions: Applications for CSEF must be submitted at the beginning of each school year. Support is subject to verification of eligibility through Centrelink. program_name: Select Entry and Accelerated Learning (SEAL) Program
overview: Wangaratta High School offers the Select Entry and Accelerated Learning (SEAL) program for highly able students. The program provides accelerated curriculum delivery and selective entry for students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential and capability to perform above the expected level for their age.
description: The SEAL Program is an innovative program for highly able students that has been running successfully at Wangaratta High School for nearly two decades. The program has attracted some of the brightest regional students who move through sections of the Year 7-9 curriculum at a faster pace in a separate class group. The SEAL Program provides these students with the opportunity to work with like-minded peers and prepares them for broader, more intensive Victorian Certificate of Education (VCE) studies and University Extension units in their senior years. Selection involves application responses, external testing through EduTest, teacher recommendation, analysis of student data and student interview. There is no fee charged for the program itself as it is part of the government school's regular offerings.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry; Students must register with EduTest; Must be capable of performing above expected level for age; Options to transfer from mainstream classes into SEAL for current Year 8 and 9 High School students if places available; Enrolments open for new students from other schools or those moving to the area
special_conditions: 2025 testing date for 2026 enrolment: Saturday 9th August at Wangaratta High School 8.45am to 12.15pm. You must have registered with EduTest in order to attend the testing day. Interview bookings open following the test date. Students must bring pens, pencils, eraser, water and morning tea to the testing session. The interview is holistic in nature and gives students an opportunity to talk about their strengths and achievements. Students are invited to bring something they are proud of to discuss at interview.

overview: Wangaratta High School students are eligible for the Harding Miller Education Foundation Scholarship. This scholarship is designed to support girls from low-income households or those experiencing significant financial barriers to accessing education who demonstrate high academic potential.
description: The Harding Miller Education Foundation Scholarship is valued at $20,000 and provides recipients with comprehensive support over four years of high school from Year 9 to Year 12. The scholarship includes a high-quality laptop and internet access, IT support throughout the scholarship period, personal 1:1 tutoring, prepaid cards to assist with school essentials, access to online homework help, a dedicated personal coach and mentor, and invitations to special future success enrichment activities that provide development and learning opportunities. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence (consistently achieving outstanding or high results across Maths, Science and English with NAPLAN results in Exceeding or Strong bands), evidence of financial barriers/hardship, and demonstrated motivation to succeed at school and pursue tertiary education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be female student; Currently in Year 8 entering Year 9 in 2026; Enrolled at a public/government high school; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident; Consistently achieving outstanding or high academic results across Maths, Science and English; NAPLAN results in Exceeding or Strong bands; Demonstrated motivation to achieve academically and professionally; Intention to undertake tertiary studies; Experiencing low socioeconomic circumstances or significant financial barriers; Interest in extracurricular, community and leadership activities considered favourably
special_conditions: Scholarship is provided as a toolkit over 4 years. Students must apply independently and willingly—applications should not be submitted under pressure or obligation. Recipients must provide evidence of successful progress through annual online surveys and uploading recent reports. If a student fails to submit the annual survey, the Foundation reserves the right to discontinue support. Principal reference required on school letterhead. If student completes Year 12 and provides ATAR result, the laptop is theirs to keep. Can hold multiple scholarships simultaneously if meeting criteria.

overview: Wangaratta High School awards the Mikal Rhys Stanimiouch Memorial Scholarship to recognise outstanding student achievement. The scholarship provides recipients with an international student exchange program opportunity to develop leadership and life skills.
description: The Mikal Rhys Stanimiouch Memorial Scholarship is presented by Inechange Inc. to Wangaratta High School to award one student every year demonstrating the most outstanding achievement. The scholarship provides recipients with either a 5 or 10 month student exchange program overseas, offering a unique opportunity to develop interpersonal skills, cultural awareness and personal growth through international experience. The award recognises students who have made an exceptional contribution to the school community and demonstrated outstanding personal development. Inechange Inc. is dedicated to assisting young people of the world to realise their potential through international exchange programs that foster global citizenship and cross-cultural understanding.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Selected based on most outstanding achievement; Demonstrated exceptional contribution to school community; Demonstrated outstanding personal development; Selection at school's discretion
special_conditions: Awarded to one student every year. Selection based on nomination and school assessment of outstanding achievement. International exchange program duration is either 5 or 10 months.

overview: Warrandyte Primary School presents an annual scholarship at Year 6 Graduation to a student who continually strives to do their best and demonstrates exemplary values. This award recognises outstanding character and commitment to excellence.

The scholarship recipient is selected from Year 6 students who have participated in the special Rats of Tobruk program, which sees students visit the Rats of Tobruk Hall to meet servicemen and their families, learn about their experiences, and create reflective writing. The scholarship recognises a student who continually strives to do their best and demonstrates exemplary values throughout their time at the school. The award is presented by representatives of the Rats of Tobruk. The scholarship value and specific financial benefits are not publicly disclosed on the school website. Recipients are selected through their demonstrated commitment to character development, academic effort, and alignment with the school's core values.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 6 student; Must have participated in the Rats of Tobruk program; Must demonstrate continual effort to do their best; Must demonstrate exemplary values
special_conditions: Award is presented at Year 6 Graduation ceremony. Selection is made by school representatives in consultation with Rats of Tobruk representatives. Specific selection criteria beyond demonstrated exemplary values are not publicly detailed.

overview: Warrnambool College offers the Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise students who have shown a positive commitment to the arts. This scholarship celebrates excellence in public speaking, debating, theatrical performance, vocal and dance.

Successful applicants will be expected to participate fully in the Performing Arts program at the school. The scholarship supports students who wish to develop their artistic talents while engaging in the school's vibrant performing arts community. Recipients will have opportunities to showcase their abilities through the annual school musical and other cultural performances.

overview: Wayi School supports eligible families through the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund. This program helps lower-income families access camps, sports activities and excursions, ensuring all students can participate in school experiences regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian Government initiative that has been providing financial assistance to government and non-government students from lower-income families since 2020. The program helps ensure that more than 220,000 students each year can participate in camps, sports and excursions. Wayi School families may be eligible to receive this funding. The program provides vouchers or direct payments to support participation costs. Eligibility is means-tested and based on family income thresholds. Families experiencing financial hardship should contact the school for detailed eligibility criteria and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Student enrolled at Wayi School; Family meets Victorian Government income thresholds for CSEF; Lower-income families eligible
special_conditions: Eligibility criteria set by Victorian Government. Families should contact school for current income thresholds and application deadlines. Program provides support for camps, sports and excursions costs specifically.

overview: Wayi School assists families experiencing financial hardship through the State School's Relief program, which provides support with school uniforms and essential school items. This ensures all students can attend school feeling valued and included.
description: State School's Relief is a support program that helps families experiencing financial hardship access uniforms and other essential school items. Wayi School can assist eligible families to apply for this support, which provides direct assistance with uniform costs and other critical school necessities. The program is designed to ensure that financial difficulties do not prevent students from attending school or participating fully. Families experiencing hardship should contact the school confidentially to discuss their situation and eligibility for support.
eligibility_criteria: Student enrolled at Wayi School; Family experiencing financial hardship; Requires assistance with school uniforms or essential items
special_conditions: Application is confidential and discussed directly with school leadership. The program is designed to support families in hardship who need assistance with uniforms and essential school items.

program_name: Schools Saving Bonus - Uniforms and Activities Support
overview: As part of the 2024/25 Victorian Budget, Wayi School families receive support through the Schools Saving Bonus, which provides $400 per eligible student to help cover school uniforms and activities costs.
description: The Schools Saving Bonus is a one-off support payment provided by the Victorian Government as part of the 2024/25 budget. Every eligible student enrolled in a Victorian government school in 2025 receives $400 in support. The funds are provided as credits on school accounts to help families meet the costs of activities and uniforms. This support is in addition to existing means-tested supports for camps, sports, excursions and uniforms. The bonus helps reduce financial barriers to participation in school activities and ensures students have access to necessary uniform and activity costs.
eligibility_criteria: Student enrolled at Wayi School in 2025; All families eligible for the one-off $400 bonus
special_conditions: Available to all families with children enrolled in Victorian government schools in 2025. Credits are applied to school accounts. Provided as part of Victorian Government 2024/25 budget initiative.

overview: Waverley Christian College offers Christian Character Scholarships to students who demonstrate a strong commitment to the Christian Faith and who are interested in a Christian education to further their faith development and service. These scholarships recognise students with exceptional Christian character and the potential to contribute positively to the college community.
description: The Christian Character Scholarship is available to new students undertaking studies in Year 10, 11 or 12. Applicants must demonstrate strong Christian commitment and active faith participation. Scholarship recipients will receive a tuition fee rebate of up to 80% for the remainder of their secondary schooling up to Year 12. The value of the scholarship will be determined based on an assessment of the applicant at interview and the family's financial circumstances. Applicants are required to complete and submit a financial assessment form. The scholarship is conditional on adherence to the College's policies and rules, and recipients are expected to maintain strong engagement and commitment to the College.
eligibility_criteria: New students; Australian citizens or permanent residents; studying full-time; demonstrating strong commitment to the Christian Faith; interested in Christian education for faith development and service; undertaking studies in Year 10, 11 or 12
special_conditions: The scholarship is conditional on the continued adherence to the College's policies and rules. The student is expected to contribute to the life of the College. The College reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if the student's engagement, commitment or behaviour is less than the expected level of the scholarship. Where the student withdraws from the College prior to the completion of Year 12, families will be required to pay the equivalent tuition fees that have been discounted for the period enrolled.

overview: Waverley Christian College offers scholarships to new students who demonstrate strong Christian character and/or Christian leadership and who would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education. The scholarship value and terms are determined on an individual basis following assessment of the applicant's circumstances and interview. The college aims to provide Christian families with the opportunity to have their children educated from a Christ-centred, Biblical perspective. Financial circumstances are considered as part of the application process to support families with genuine financial need.

overview: Wellington Secondary College offers Year 7 Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward high-achieving students from feeder primary schools. The scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 and is based on academic merit and performance at entry level assessment.
description: One year scholarships are open to all students at feeder primary schools. Each scholarship is valued at a total of $400, comprising $195 for approved expenses and a waiver of the $205 Year 7 voluntary contribution. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on standardised assessment and academic performance. Students must demonstrate strong academic ability to be considered for this award.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6 at feeder primary schools; Academic merit required; Good behaviour and conduct expected
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded on a one-year basis. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic performance to continue into the second year. A review of performance after one year will determine continued access to the scholarship.

overview: Wellington Secondary College offers Instrumental Music Scholarships to recognise musical talent and commitment. Two one-year scholarships are available annually for Year 7 students with demonstrated instrumental music ability. Recipients receive personal tuition from highly qualified music staff.
description: Two one-year Instrumental Scholarships are valued at a total of $500 each year. The value comprises up to $195 for approved expenses, a waiver of the 2011 and 2012 voluntary contributions, and music instrument tuition fees. Students receive personal tuition from highly qualified Brass, Strings, Woodwind and Percussion Instrumental Music staff at the college. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated musical ability and audition performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Musical ability and instrumental experience required; Must pass music audition; Good behaviour and conduct expected; Commitment to instrumental music program required
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded on a one-year basis. Recipients must maintain satisfactory performance in instrumental music to continue into the second year. A review of performance after one year will determine continued access. Students must actively participate in ensemble rehearsals and performances.

Students will receive personal tuition from highly qualified college Sports and Physical Education staff. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence, athletic ability, and commitment to sport.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated sporting ability and athletic achievement required; Must pass sports assessment or tryout; Good behaviour and conduct expected; Commitment to school sports programs required
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded on a one-year basis. Recipients must maintain satisfactory athletic performance and participation in school sports to continue into the second year. A review of performance after one year will determine continued access.

overview: Wellington Secondary College recognises high-achieving students by offering the High Flyers Program in Years 7-9. This accelerated learning program provides challenging learning opportunities through core subjects for students with exceptional academic ability.
description: The High Flyers Program is available to high-achieving students in Years 7, 8, and 9 at Wellington Secondary College. This program offers challenging learning opportunities through core academic subjects, allowing academically gifted students to work with like-minded peers of similar abilities. The program ensures these students are appropriately challenged and supported in their academic development. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and aptitude testing. No direct monetary scholarship is provided, but the accelerated curriculum represents enrichment of educational experience.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high-achieving students in Years 7-9; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Must pass academic assessment or entrance examination; Recommendation from primary school or prior school performance; Good behaviour and conduct expected
special_conditions: The program requires students to maintain high academic standards and good behaviour. Students work alongside other high-achieving students in a challenging curriculum designed to extend their learning beyond standard year level expectations.

overview: Westall Secondary College offers a range of scholarships to local students entering Year 7. The scholarship program recognises academic excellence and supports talented students to access secondary education at the college.
description: Westall Secondary College provides scholarships for local students entering Year 7. The specific details regarding financial value, selection criteria, application processes, and special conditions are not publicly detailed on the accessible scholarship page. Prospective applicants are encouraged to contact the college directly on 03 9546 3233 or via westall.sc@education.vic.gov.au for comprehensive information about the scholarship programs available, including eligibility requirements, application timelines, and award amounts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be local students; Specific academic thresholds not publicly stated
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to local students only. Specific terms and conditions are not publicly available on the website.

overview: Westbourne Grammar School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning. This scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 7, 10 and 11 who achieve excellence in the classroom and maintain the highest academic standards throughout their schooling.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship celebrates students who strive for excellence in their academic pursuits. Recipients are expected to maintain high academic standards and demonstrate sustained commitment to learning. The scholarship provides fee remission (exact percentage varies annually at the Principal's discretion). Selection is based on performance in the Edutest scholarship examination (approximately 2.5 hours), interview, and supporting documentation. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that students will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12, and must continue to thrive academically and act with integrity throughout their enrolment. Recipients are subject to annual review and must engage fully in the life of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current domestic students; Entering Year 7, 10 or 11; Must sit the Edutest scholarship test; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident; Minimum academic threshold determined by competitive selection; Expected to complete Year 12 at Westbourne; Must maintain high academic standards and good conduct; Scholarships reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal and scholarship holders are subject to all terms and conditions of enrolment. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave to attend another school prior to completing Year 12, the fees foregone by discount are required to be repaid to the school. Scholarship recipients must continue to thrive academically, act with integrity and engage fully in the life of the school. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Westbourne Grammar School offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas including academics, sports, arts and community engagement. This scholarship celebrates well-rounded students who actively contribute to school life and inspire others through their dedication and enthusiasm.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who display excellence not just academically but across multiple dimensions of school life. Recipients are actively involved in school life, demonstrating involvement in sports, co-curricular activities, community service and school leadership. The scholarship provides fee remission (exact percentage varies annually at the Principal's discretion). Selection involves the Edutest scholarship test (approximately 2.5 hours), interview, and assessment of broader contributions to school life. Scholars must remain at Westbourne completing Year 12 and engage fully in school activities. Annual reviews ensure ongoing engagement and achievement across all areas.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current domestic students; Entering Year 7, 10 or 11; Must sit the Edutest scholarship test; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident; Demonstrate engagement in sport, co-curricular programs, community service and school contribution; Expected to complete Year 12 at Westbourne; Must maintain good academic and conduct standards; Scholarships reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal and scholarship holders are subject to all terms and conditions of enrolment. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave to attend another school prior to completing Year 12, the fees foregone by discount are required to be repaid to the school. General Excellence scholars are actively involved in school life, inspiring others through their dedication and enthusiasm. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Westbourne Grammar School offers Music Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional musical talent and achievement. This scholarship celebrates musicians who show dedication to their craft and commitment to contributing to the school's musical life.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises students with exceptional ability in music at any year level in the Senior School. Applicants are invited to audition before a panel including the Director of Music and Performing Arts, where they perform prepared pieces and demonstrate their musical skills. A portfolio of achievements and references from music instructors is required. The scholarship provides fee remission on tuition fees (exact percentage varies annually at the Principal's discretion) and may include coverage of private music lessons and instrument rental. Recipients must remain at Westbourne completing Year 12 and actively contribute to the school's musical programs. Annual reviews ensure ongoing musical engagement and achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current domestic students; Entering Year 7, 10 or 11; Exceptional musical ability required; Must audition before panel including Director of Music and Performing Arts; Must provide portfolio of musical achievements; Must provide references from music instructors; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident; Expected to complete Year 12 at Westbourne; Must engage fully in school musical life; Scholarships reviewed annually
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal and scholarship holders are subject to all terms and conditions of enrolment. Music applicants will be invited to audition before a panel including the Director of Music and Performing Arts. A portfolio of achievements and references from music instructors is required. Scholarships may include coverage of private music lessons and instrument rental. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

Applicants will be invited to an interview where they can share their aspirations, approach to learning, and involvement in school life. The school looks for students with a positive attitude towards learning who enjoy being part of the school community. Scholarship recipients are role models within the school. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave prior to completing Year 12, fees foregone are required to be repaid. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Werribee Secondary College offers a general academic scholarship to Year 7 students from feeder primary schools. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate strong academic achievement and commitment to learning.
description: This one-year scholarship is valued at a total of $400, comprising $195 for approved expenses and a waiver of the $205 Year 7 voluntary contribution. The scholarship is open to all students at feeder primary schools. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on academic performance and merit. Students must maintain satisfactory academic progress to renew their scholarship for a second year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Currently enrolled in feeder primary schools; Academic merit based on school performance
special_conditions: Scholarship valued at $400 total: $195 for approved expenses and $205 waiver of Year 7 voluntary contribution. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship.

overview: Werribee Secondary College offers instrumental music scholarships to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding aptitude in music performance. The scholarship supports students pursuing instrumental music study with qualified college staff.
description: Two one-year instrumental scholarships are valued at a total of $500 each year, comprising up to $195 for approved expenses, a waiver of the 2011 and 2012 voluntary contributions, and music instrument tuition fees. Students receive personal tuition from highly qualified Brass, Strings, Woodwind and Percussion Instrumental Music staff at the college. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated musical ability and commitment to instrumental music study.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated musical aptitude and commitment to instrumental music; Ability to learn a musical instrument; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Two scholarships awarded annually. Valued at $500 total per award: up to $195 for approved expenses, waiver of 2011 and 2012 voluntary contributions, and music instrument tuition fees. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship.

overview: Werribee Secondary College offers sports scholarships to Year 7 students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to athletic development. The scholarship provides support and coaching from qualified college sports staff.
description: Two one-year sports scholarships are each valued at a total of $400, comprising $195 for approved expenses and a waiver of 2011 Year 7 voluntary contribution of $205. Students will receive personal tuition from highly qualified college Sports and Physical Education staff. The scholarship recognises outstanding sporting performance and commitment. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated excellence in sports or athletics; Commitment to sporting development; Currently enrolled in feeder primary schools; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Two scholarships awarded annually. Valued at $400 total per award: $195 for approved expenses and $205 waiver of Year 7 voluntary contribution. Students will receive personal tuition from highly qualified Sports and Physical Education staff. A review of performance after one year will determine access to the second year of the scholarship.

overview: Werribee Secondary College offers the Select Entry Learning Program (SELP) to academically talented students from Year 7 to Year 10. The program provides an accelerated and enriched curriculum designed for high-achieving students committed to academic excellence.
description: SELP is an accelerated learning program featuring a challenging academic curriculum with full participation in arts, sports and technology. Students undertake accelerated curriculum in Mathematics, Science and Humanities, and extension curriculum in English, Music, Arts and Technology. Students are selected on the basis of their performance on the EduTest and additional transcripts. For Year 7 entry, students must complete the EduTest in September, and selection is based on test performance and academic records. A testing fee applies for the EduTest. Entrance to SELP in Years 8 to 10 is available only to enrolled Werribee Secondary College students based on their performance and ability to succeed in the program, with assessment based on Grade Point Average, NAPLAN performance, PAT-R and PAT-M results, and Semester 1 report.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10; Must reside within school zone; Year 7 applicants must accept enrolment prior to expressing interest in SELP; Students must demonstrate high academic achievement; For Years 8-10: existing Werribee Secondary College students only; Must demonstrate high GPA and academic potential
special_conditions: Students must be enrolled at Werribee Secondary College to participate in SELP. Year 7 students must accept their enrolment prior to expressing interest in SELP during Term 3 of Grade 6. Late enrolments to the college are ineligible for Year 7 SELP. Testing fee applies for EduTest. Places in the program are limited. Additional paid practice tests are available through EduTest online.

overview: The Department of Education's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) provides financial assistance to eligible families at Werribee Secondary College. The fund helps support student participation in school activities.
description: CSEF provides payments for eligible students to attend activities including camps, sports and excursions. The CSEF funding amount for Secondary School students is $225.00 (subject to change). Year 7 students who receive CSEF funding may also be eligible to receive support for school uniforms via the State Schools' Relief program. CSEF funding cannot be used towards books, stationery, before/after school care, music lessons, or formals/graduations. Applications are made through the Department of Education website and schools verify eligibility based on Centrelink income support or Youth Allowance receipt.
eligibility_criteria: Families receiving Centrelink income support payment; Students receiving Youth Allowance; Financial need as primary criterion; Open to all students at the college; Secondary school students eligible for $225.00 annually
special_conditions: Families must receive Centrelink income support payment or student must receive Youth Allowance. CSEF funding cannot be used towards books, stationery, before/after school care, music lessons, or formals/graduations. Year 7 recipients may also access State Schools' Relief program for uniform support. Funding amount subject to change.

overview: The State Schools' Relief program provides support to families experiencing severe financial hardship at Werribee Secondary College. The program assists with obtaining necessary items to commence school.
description: Financial assistance is available for families experiencing severe financial hardship making it difficult to obtain necessary school items. Students who receive CSEF funding may be eligible to receive support for school uniforms via the State Schools' Relief program. Families can contact the college Wellbeing department for further assistance and application information. The program is designed to support students in accessing essential school resources and uniforms when families face financial barriers.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing severe financial hardship; Difficulty obtaining necessary school items; Students receiving CSEF funding (for uniform support); Open to all year levels; Must contact college Wellbeing department
special_conditions: Application requires contact with college Wellbeing department. Program is for families experiencing severe financial hardship. Particularly available to Year 7 students receiving CSEF funding for uniform support. Non-refundable application fee may apply depending on assessment.

overview: Westbourne Grammar offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who strive for excellence in the classroom. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and maintain high academic standards throughout their schooling.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to new and current domestic students entering Years 7, 10 and 11. Recipients must sit the Edutest scholarship test (approximately 2.5 hours), which evaluates academic skills across multiple subject areas. Scholarships cover tuition fees and most school-related costs, including subject fees, excursions, IT support, and outdoor education, though private music lessons, books, stationery, and bus fares are not covered. Recipients are expected to maintain high academic standards and remain at Westbourne completing Year 12, as leaving early requires repayment of all fees foregone by the scholarship discount. Scholarships are reviewed annually and may be withdrawn if recipients do not meet academic or behavioural expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must be new or current domestic students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Must sit the Edutest scholarship test; Must maintain high academic standards throughout enrolment; Must complete Year 12 at school or repay fees
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave to attend another school prior to completing Year 12, the fees foregone by discount are required to be repaid to the school. Scholarship recipients must continue to thrive academically, act with integrity and engage fully in the life of the school. Scholarships are reviewed annually. Academic Excellence scholars are expected to maintain high academic standards.

overview: Westbourne Grammar offers the General Excellence Scholarship to students who demonstrate dedication, creativity and leadership across multiple areas of school life. This scholarship recognises students who excel not just academically but in the stage, field, or community engagement.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is available to new and current domestic students entering Years 7, 10 and 11 who show excellence across multiple disciplines including academics, music, sports, and leadership. Applicants must sit the Edutest scholarship test and participate in an interview where they can share their aspirations, approach to learning, and involvement in school life. Recipients must have a positive attitude towards learning and enjoy being part of a school community. Scholarships cover tuition fees and most school-related costs, including subject fees, excursions, IT support, and outdoor education. Recipients are expected to remain at Westbourne completing Year 12, and scholarships are reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must be new or current domestic students; Must demonstrate excellence in multiple areas (academic, music, sports, leadership); Must sit the Edutest scholarship test; Must participate in interview; Must have positive attitude to learning; Must complete Year 12 at school or repay fees
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are role models within the school. General Excellence scholars are actively involved in school life, inspiring others through their dedication and enthusiasm. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave prior to completing Year 12, fees foregone must be repaid. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Westbourne Grammar offers Music Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional musical talent. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement in music performance on instruments or through vocal performance across all year levels in the Senior School.
description: The Music Scholarship is available to students entering Years 7, 10 and 11 who are exceptional in musical performance. Music applicants do not sit the standard scholarship test but instead undergo auditions to demonstrate their skills on their preferred instrument or through vocal performance. A portfolio of achievements and references from music instructors is required to support the application. The scholarships include coverage of private music lessons and instrument rentals, along with tuition fees and most school-related costs. Recipients must maintain high musical standards and remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Music scholarships are reviewed annually and may be adjusted or withdrawn if recipients do not meet expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate exceptional musical talent; Must participate in audition; Must provide portfolio of musical achievements; Must provide references from music instructors; Must complete Year 12 at school or repay fees
special_conditions: Music applicants do not sit the scholarship test but must audition. Scholarships cover private music lessons and instrument rentals in addition to tuition fees. Recipients must remain at Westbourne completing Year 12 or repay fees foregone by the scholarship discount. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Westbourne Grammar offers Sport Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional talent in sports. The scholarship is awarded to talented athletes entering Years 7, 10 and 11 who show dedication to their chosen sport and commitment to school life.
description: The Sport Scholarship is available to students demonstrating exceptional sporting talent across various disciplines. Sporting applicants must sit the Edutest scholarship test and meet with a panel including the Director of Sport to discuss their achievements and future goals. A portfolio of accomplishments and references from coaches or sporting associations is required to support the application. Scholarships cover tuition fees and most school-related costs including subject fees, excursions, IT support, and outdoor education. Recipients are expected to serve as role models in their sport and engage fully in school life. Scholarships are reviewed annually and recipients must maintain high standards throughout their time at Westbourne.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting talent; Must sit the Edutest scholarship test; Must participate in interview with Director of Sport; Must provide portfolio of sporting accomplishments; Must provide references from coaches or sporting associations; Must complete Year 12 at school or repay fees
special_conditions: Sporting applicants must sit the Edutest scholarship test and meet with a panel including the Director of Sport. Portfolio of accomplishments and coach references required. Scholarship recipients are role models within the school. Scholarships are awarded on the basis that the student will remain at Westbourne completing Year 12. Should a student leave prior to completing Year 12, fees foregone must be repaid. Scholarships are reviewed annually.

overview: Westbourne Grammar offers First Nations Scholarships to celebrate and support Indigenous students who strive for excellence. The scholarship recognises talented First Nations students and supports their educational pathway at Westbourne.
description: The First Nations Scholarship is available to Indigenous students entering Years 7, 10 and 11. This program represents Westbourne Grammar's commitment to supporting First Nations students and recognising their unique talents and contributions to the school community. While specific application requirements and financial details vary, the scholarship is designed to provide meaningful support to Indigenous learners. Applicants may be required to participate in interviews to discuss their goals and aspirations. Scholarships cover tuition fees and most school-related costs. Recipients must remain at Westbourne completing Year 12, and scholarships are reviewed annually to ensure recipients continue to meet expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous/First Nations students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must be new or current domestic students; Must demonstrate academic potential and leadership qualities; Must complete Year 12 at school or repay fees
special_conditions: First Nations Scholarship supports Indigenous students in their educational journey at Westbourne. Recipients must remain at Westbourne completing Year 12 or repay fees foregone by the scholarship. Scholarships are reviewed annually. Specific conditions and application requirements should be confirmed directly with the school.

school_name: Western English Language School-Manor Lakes Campus
Western English Language School-St Albans Campus
Western English Language School-Wyndham Secondary Campus
Wesley College - St Kilda Road Junior Campus

overview: Wheelers Hill Secondary College partners with Community Bank Pinewood to offer a scholarship valued at $1,500 to a Year 11 student. The scholarship recognises financial need and is intended to support eligible students with educational costs by the end of Term 1.
description: The Community Bank Pinewood Scholarship provides $1,500 to one Year 11 student per year at Wheelers Hill Secondary College. The scholarship is valued at $1,500.00 per year for 1 year and is available to one successful recipient, subject to meeting the academic performance criteria. The award is administered by Community Bank Pinewood, which is part of Bendigo Bank. Funds must be used by the end of Term 1 in the award year. The selection process involves assessing academic performance and meeting specified eligibility requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 students; Must demonstrate academic performance meeting specified criteria; One award per year
special_conditions: Funds must be used by the end of Term 1 in the award year. Scholarship subject to meeting academic performance criteria successfully.

overview: Wheelers Hill Secondary College offers the ACE Program, an enrichment initiative for high-ability students entering Year 7. The program provides an extension curriculum across all subject areas, recognising students with demonstrated academic excellence and high learning capacity.
description: The ACE Program is an enrichment program designed for high-achieving students entering Year 7 at Wheelers Hill Secondary College. This unique extension program provides high-ability students with a stimulating alternate curriculum across all subject areas, offered alongside robust co-curricular opportunities available to all college students. The program operates continuously from Year 7 onwards and involves a special application and selection process. Selection involves assessing academic ability and learning potential through an application process. The program combines accelerated academic content with enrichment activities designed to challenge and engage gifted learners.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate high academic ability; Application process required; Must meet program entry criteria based on academic performance
special_conditions: Application process applies. Contact the General Office for details regarding the selection process and application requirements.

overview: Western Heights College offers scholarships to Year 6 students who demonstrate a dedication to learning and commitment to the College values of Community, Integrity and Courage. These scholarships recognise exceptional commitment to academic achievement and alignment with college values.
description: Western Heights College awards scholarships to Year 6 students entering Year 7 who demonstrate dedication to learning and commitment to the college's core values of Community, Integrity and Courage. Prospective students who understand and align their behaviours with these values are invited to apply. Applications are sent out with Year 7 acceptance packages to students who have been successfully placed in the college. The specific financial value of these scholarships, selection process, and renewal conditions are not publicly detailed on the college website and applicants are directed to contact the college directly for further information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students; Must demonstrate dedication to learning; Must show commitment to College values of Community, Integrity and Courage; Prospective students must align their behaviours with college values
special_conditions: Scholarship applications are sent out with Year 7 2024 acceptance packages. Students must be accepted into Western Heights College before scholarship information is provided. Contact Sophie Trezise at sophie.trezise2@education.vic.gov.au for further details regarding scholarship applications.

overview: Whitefriars College awards Full Fee Bursaries to incoming Year 7 students based on recommendations from Parish Priests. These bursaries provide complete financial assistance to eligible families experiencing exceptional financial circumstances, ensuring deserving students are not denied a Catholic education at the College.
description: The Principal may award up to three (3) Full Fee Bursaries annually to incoming Year 7 students. The value of each bursary is Full Fee Remission, covering the entire tuition fee for the student's duration at the College. Selection is based on recommendations from Parish Priests within the College Priority Parishes. Applications are assessed through the Fee Collection Policy process, with consideration given to families in exceptional financial circumstances. Bursary recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct standards, and recipients remain subject to annual review. The College requires a statutory declaration and supporting evidence of financial hardship as part of the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 students; Recommended by a Parish Priest; Families in exceptional financial circumstances; Must be from a College Priority Parish; Financial need assessment required; Good conduct and willingness to support the College ethos expected
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded based on recommendations from Parish Priests. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct. The College retains the right to review bursaries annually. Bursaries are subject to the conditions outlined in the Fee Collection Policy. Recipients are expected to support the College's Catholic and Carmelite ethos. Non-compulsory activities may be restricted for bursary recipients with outstanding fees.

overview: Western Heights College offers scholarships to Year 6 students who demonstrate a dedication to learning and commitment to the College values of Community, Integrity and Courage. The scholarship recognises students with strong all-round potential who align with the school's values.
description: Western Heights College awards scholarships to Year 6 students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional dedication to learning and align with the College's core values of Community, Integrity and Courage. Prospective students are invited to apply if they understand and embody these values. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not specified on the school website. The selection process appears to involve application and demonstration of alignment with school values. Specific details regarding test requirements, interview procedures, duration of the award, and renewal conditions are not publicly disclosed on the college's accessible web pages.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must demonstrate dedication to learning; Must demonstrate commitment to College values of Community, Integrity and Courage; Must understand and align behaviours with college values
special_conditions: Prospective students who understand and align their behaviours with college values are invited to apply. Contact Sophie Trezise (sophie.trezise2@education.vic.gov.au) for scholarship enquiries.

overview: Western Port Secondary College offers the Youth Excellence Stream (YES) program as a selective entry class for students who strive for academic excellence. The program is designed to promote excellence through a differentiated curriculum that challenges thinking and develops independent learning habits, problem-solving skills and advanced academic capabilities.
description: The Youth Excellence Stream is a special program class within Western Port Secondary College for highly capable Year 7-8 students who demonstrate high levels of task commitment and academic achievement. Students within the YES Program are encouraged to further develop their independent thinking skills, learning habits and problem-solving skills. The curriculum is designed to challenge thinking and provide a differentiated learning experience that caters to individual needs. The program also develops skills that prepare students for early entry into VCE subjects.
