Students apply through a formal application process which includes a written application, personal recommendation, and an interview. Selection also involves compulsory Literacy and Numeracy testing.
eligibility_criteria: Application through formal process in 2025 Year 7 enrolment pack; Compulsory Literacy and Numeracy testing required; Interview required; Personal recommendation required; Students must demonstrate high levels of task commitment; Students must demonstrate high levels of academic achievement
special_conditions: The YES Program is a Select Entry Program. No financial assistance or fee remission is provided; this is an educational acceleration program only.

overview: Whittlesea Secondary College offers Year 7 Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement and all-round excellence. The scholarship is available to Grade 6 students entering Year 7 and recognises those who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and excellent potential to contribute to the life of the College.
description: Year 7 Academic Scholarships at Whittlesea Secondary College are awarded to Grade 6 students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and excellent all-round potential. The scholarship provides $1,000 in value as a credit note to be used for relevant expenses including Curriculum Materials and Services Charges, Year 7 camp, musical instrumental tuition and hire of instruments, and books. The scholarship is tenable over Years 7 and 8, with continuation into Year 8 dependent upon a successful review conducted by the Assistant Principal at the end of Year 7. Selection is based on the completed scholarship application, a reference from the student's primary school, and specific individual criteria addressed during the application and interview process.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Grade 6; Must be entering Year 7 at Whittlesea Secondary College in 2025; Must be academically sound with excellent all-round potential; Must demonstrate capacity to benefit from and contribute to the life of the College
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded on a two-year basis covering Years 7 and 8. Continuation into Year 8 is conditional upon a successful review at the end of Year 7, conducted by the Assistant Principal. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and adhere to school expectations.

overview: William Ruthven Primary School participates in the Victorian Government's Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) to assist eligible families with the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. The fund provides financial support for students whose families hold a valid means-tested concession card or are temporary foster parents.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is provided by the Victorian Government to assist eligible families to cover the costs of school trips, camps and sporting activities. Eligible families can use the CSEF to cover costs of participating in school-based camps, excursions, and sports activities. The financial benefit varies by family eligibility and the specific costs of activities. Families must hold a valid means-tested concession card or be temporary foster parents to qualify. Eligible families should contact the school office to obtain a CSEF application form.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid means-tested concession card; OR be a temporary foster parent; Application submitted to school
special_conditions: Families must hold valid documentation (means-tested concession card) to apply. The program is subject to Victorian Government eligibility criteria and funding availability.

program_name: State School Relief Uniform Assistance Program
overview: William Ruthven Primary School partners with State School Relief to provide uniform assistance to eligible families. Students whose families hold a health care card can receive support in purchasing school uniforms, including black school shoes.
description: William Ruthven Primary School provides access to State School Relief assistance for families in financial hardship. Families who hold a health care card can receive assistance in purchasing black school shoes and other uniform items. This program recognizes that uniform costs can present a barrier to school attendance and aims to support families facing financial difficulties. Interested families should contact the school office for information about how to access this assistance through State School Relief.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid health care card; Contact school office (Lee Phillipson or administration); Primary school student enrolled at William Ruthven
special_conditions: Families must hold a valid health care card to be eligible. Contact school office for specific details and to arrange assistance. The program is subject to State School Relief availability and funding.

overview: Wild Cherry School offers concession tuition fees to families who hold valid concession cards. The school is committed to providing Waldorf/Steiner education accessible to children from all socio-economic backgrounds and situations.
description: Wild Cherry School provides concession tuition fees for eligible families holding valid concession cards. Concession cards must be presented at the beginning of each term to validate the discount. The school endeavours to keep all fees and charges at an affordable level for the high-quality education provided. All fees and charges are used solely for the provision of education-related services. The concession fee applies to the tuition component only and is not available in combination with the early payment discount option. Concession tuition fees are reviewed annually as deemed necessary by the Board.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold valid concession card; Must present concession card at beginning of each term; Applicable to any year level
special_conditions: Concession cards must be presented at the beginning of each term to validate discount. The early payment discount option is not available for concession tuition fees.

program_name: Emergency and Short-term Financial Hardship Assistance
overview: Wild Cherry School makes provision in its budget for financial assistance in cases of emergency or short-term financial hardship which may occur during the course of a child's enrolment. Families experiencing unexpected financial difficulties are invited to discuss assistance options with the School Leader or Principal.
description: Wild Cherry School recognises that families may experience unexpected financial hardship during their child's enrolment. The school makes budgetary provision specifically for emergency and short-term financial assistance to support affected families. This assistance is designed to help families navigate temporary financial difficulties without requiring their child's withdrawal from the school. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the School Leader or Principal to discuss available assistance options. The amount and nature of assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual circumstances and school resources.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing emergency or short-term financial hardship; Child currently enrolled at Wild Cherry School; Family must contact School Leader or Principal to discuss eligibility
special_conditions: Families must contact School Leader or Principal directly to discuss and apply for assistance. Assistance is evaluated on case-by-case basis considering individual circumstances.

overview: Wild Cherry School offers a 5% discount on tuition fees for families who pay the full annual tuition fee in advance by February 28. This incentive encourages early payment commitment and assists with school cash flow planning.
description: An early payment discount of 5% is applied to tuition fees only when the full tuition fee is paid annually in advance by February 28. This discount is available for standard and concessional tuition fees where applicable. The discount provides families with an opportunity to reduce annual education costs through advance payment planning. The early payment option is not available for concession tuition fees in circumstances where families are already receiving fee assistance. This discount is applied automatically when fees are paid according to the specified payment schedule and timing.
eligibility_criteria: Full annual tuition fee must be paid by February 28; Discount applies to tuition fees only; Not available for concession tuition fees
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only when full annual fee is paid by February 28. Not available for concessional tuition fees. Payment must be completed in advance to receive discount benefit.

overview: Wild Cherry School offers sibling discounts on tuition fees when more than one child from the same family is enrolled at the school. This program recognises the additional financial commitment of families with multiple children in the school.
description: Sibling discounts are applied to tuition fees only where more than one child is enrolled at Wild Cherry School at the same time. The discount applies to the tuition component of fees and is designed to reduce the financial burden on families with multiple children attending the school. The specific discount percentage or amount is determined according to the school's current fee policy and is applied automatically upon verification of sibling enrolment. Sibling discounts are reviewed annually as part of the school's fee structure review process. Families are advised to confirm current discount rates with the school office.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child from the same family must be enrolled at Wild Cherry School; Discount applies to tuition fees only; Must be currently enrolled students
special_conditions: Applies to tuition fees only. Discount is automatically applied upon verification of multiple children enrolled. Discount amount is subject to annual review by the school board.

school_name: Willmott Park Primary School-Willmott Park Deaf Facility

overview: William Ruthven Secondary College offers the Learning Enhancement Academic Program (LEAP) to highly able students in Years 7 and 8. The program is designed to challenge and enhance knowledge and understanding, and create higher order skills in literacy and numeracy.
description: The Learning Enhancement Academic Program (LEAP) is offered to highly able students and is designed to challenge and enhance knowledge and understanding, and to create higher order skills in literacy and numeracy. Students are selected for LEAP according to test results and teacher recommendations. This program runs during Years 7 and 8 and provides differentiated instruction tailored to the needs of gifted and talented learners. No monetary value is stated as this is an accelerated educational program rather than a scholarship with financial benefits. The program aims to develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and advanced academic skills.
eligibility_criteria: Open to highly able students in Years 7 and 8; Selection based on test results and teacher recommendations
special_conditions: Selection is based on test results and teacher recommendations. Students must demonstrate high ability in literacy and numeracy.

overview: William Ruthven Secondary College offers Student Leadership roles to students from all year levels. Students can nominate for leadership positions at the beginning of each year and are given the opportunity to develop their public speaking, leadership and teamwork skills.
description: The Student Leadership Program is open to all students at William Ruthven Secondary College who wish to develop their leadership capabilities. Students can apply for leadership positions at the start of each year, showcasing previous experience, current leadership involvement, and their ideas for making improvements to the college. Student leaders from every year level aim to represent the views and interests of all students and demonstrate the school values of Respect, Excellence, Acceptance, and Leadership. Students selected as leaders develop their academic CV while gaining practical experience in public speaking, teamwork, and decision-making. No financial award is attached to this program. Student leaders have the opportunity to attend school committees such as the School Council, where their opinions and concerns have a formal platform.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students at William Ruthven Secondary College; Students can nominate for leadership positions at the beginning of each year; Selection based on demonstrated leadership skills and ideas for school improvement
special_conditions: Students must demonstrate commitment to the school values of Respect, Excellence, Acceptance and Leadership. Student leaders attend school committee meetings including the School Council.

program_name: State School Relief (SSR) Financial Hardship Support
overview: William Ruthven Secondary College assists students experiencing financial hardship through State School Relief (SSR), a not-for-profit organisation that supports Victorian students whose families are experiencing financial difficulty. SSR provides uniform items and financial vouchers to help families meet essential school costs.
description: State School Relief (SSR) is a not-for-profit organisation that supports the needs of Victorian students whose families are experiencing financial hardship. SSR provides some uniform items directly and also makes financial contributions via vouchers for other uniform items, helping to reduce the burden of school uniform costs on families in difficulty. Students and families should contact the Student Wellbeing Coordinators at the school to discuss applying for an SSR voucher. Due to demands on their service, SSR cannot guarantee they can meet all requests, but they work to support as many families as possible. This support is non-monetary in the form of goods and vouchers rather than cash assistance. No specific financial amounts are stated for this assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students whose families are experiencing financial hardship; Must contact Student Wellbeing Coordinators to apply
special_conditions: SSR cannot guarantee they can meet all requests due to demands on their service. Application must be initiated through contact with Student Wellbeing Coordinators. Support is provided in the form of uniform items and vouchers rather than direct financial payments.

program_name: Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEALP) - Year 7
overview: Williamstown High School provides a Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program for students who have exceptional academic ability. The program offers an accelerated curriculum for gifted students to progress more quickly through secondary school and undertake challenging learning experiences tailored to meet the needs of students capable of outstanding academic achievement.
description: The Select Entry Accelerated Learning Program (SEALP) is available at Year 7 entry and provides an enriched and accelerated curriculum. Students in the SEAL class undertake studies in English, Mathematics, Science, Humanities, Physical Education, Visual and Performing Arts, Technology-based subjects and Homegroup. The program compacts a normal Year 7-10 curriculum into a 3-year period from Year 7-9. The program provides enrichment by broadening content, understanding, experience and skills, and includes extension through in-depth study and integration of knowledge at a higher level. Students undertake the Edutest entrance examination and those with results indicating potential are invited for an interview. There is a non-refundable application fee. Final enrolment decisions take into account all information available, and parents are advised of outcomes by end of October.
eligibility_criteria: Students must have confirmed placement at Year 7 at Williamstown High School; Students must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Competitive testing via Edutest required; Sustainability interview required; Application fee is non-refundable
special_conditions: Program acceptance is non-binding for entry; Year 8 and Year 9 SEAL classes currently have no vacancies. Places may become available and will be advertised on the school website. Participation in testing does not guarantee a place in the SEAL Program. Students complete the Year 7-10 curriculum in a 3-year period with greater emphasis on higher order thinking, independent learning and research.

overview: Williamstown High School Ex-Students Association presents scholarships annually to Year 9 to Year 11 students. The association offers six scholarships each year to recognise outstanding academic achievement and contributions to the school community.
description: The Ex-Students Association of Williamstown High School administers a scholarship fund and presents six scholarships annually to students in Years 9 through 11. The scholarships are awarded in recognition of outstanding academic results and contributions to the school. Recipients are announced and celebrated at school assemblies and special events. The association holds regular reunions at the school and continues to develop the scholarship fund through ongoing administration. The specific financial value of individual scholarships is not publicly detailed, though the program demonstrates ongoing institutional support for student excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 to Year 11 students; Outstanding academic results required; Contribution to school community expected; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Scholarships are nominated and awarded by the Ex-Students Association. Recipients are recognised at school events and assemblies. Regular reunions are held at the school to celebrate the scholarship program and alumni community.

overview: Western Chances awards scholarships to students at Williamstown High School who demonstrate hard work, talent, motivation and commitment to their studies. The scholarship provides financial support and access to additional learning opportunities and leadership development programs.
description: Western Chances is an external organisation that awards scholarships to eligible students at Williamstown High School. Recipients are recognised for their hard work, talent, motivation and commitment to their studies. In addition to providing financial scholarship payments, Western Chances offers scholarship recipients access to their Links program, which includes free mathematics tutoring, leadership camps, VCE masterclasses and access to full residential or non-residential scholarships with college partners. As of recent reports, Williamstown High School has eleven scholarship recipients from Western Chances, with four new recipients awarded in 2025. The scholarship provides comprehensive support beyond financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated hard work and talent; Motivation and commitment to studies required; Students nominated by school staff; Financial need may be a consideration; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Recipients recognised at awards ceremony. Scholarships provide both financial support and access to comprehensive support programs including free maths tutoring, leadership camps and VCE masterclasses. College partnership opportunities available for residential/non-residential scholarships.

overview: The 60 Years On Scholarship is awarded annually to a Year 12 student at Williamstown High School in recognition of outstanding humanitarian endeavour. The award celebrates students who have made significant contributions through community service and social responsibility.
description: The 60 Years On Scholarship is an annual award presented to a Year 12 student who has demonstrated outstanding work in the form of humanitarian endeavour. This award recognises students who have made meaningful contributions to the wider community through volunteer work, social justice initiatives or charitable service. Recipients are celebrated at graduation events and Year 12 assemblies. The award acknowledges the school's values of community engagement and social responsibility, with recent recipients being recognised for their humanitarian contributions both within the school and in the broader community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Outstanding humanitarian endeavour required; Community service or social justice work expected; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Award presented annually at Year 12 graduation or end-of-year event. Recognition is based on demonstrated humanitarian work and community contributions throughout the student's time at the school.

overview: The Melissa Horne Book Award is presented annually to a Year 12 student at Williamstown High School in recognition of efforts towards social justice and contributions to the local community. The award honours students who have demonstrated commitment to positive social change.
description: The Melissa Horne Book Award is an annual award given to a Year 12 student who has shown outstanding effort towards social justice initiatives and made significant contributions to the local community. Recipients are recognised at graduation events and Year 12 assemblies for their advocacy work and community engagement. This award reflects the school's commitment to developing socially conscious and engaged citizens who contribute positively to their communities. The award is presented as a book prize to honour the recipient's achievements in social justice work.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Efforts towards social justice required; Local community contributions expected; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Award presented as a book prize at Year 12 graduation or end-of-year event. Recognition is based on demonstrated social justice efforts and community contributions.

overview: The Tim Watts Book Prize is presented annually to a Year 12 student at Williamstown High School in recognition of a high standard of academic achievement throughout the year. The award celebrates consistent academic excellence.
description: The Tim Watts Book Prize is an annual award given to a Year 12 student who has achieved a high standard of academic performance throughout the school year. Recipients are recognised at graduation events and Year 12 assemblies for their sustained academic commitment and achievement. The award is presented as a book prize to honour the recipient's academic excellence. This award reflects the school's commitment to recognising and celebrating academic achievement among its senior students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; High standard of academic achievement required; Consistent performance throughout the year expected; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Award presented as a book prize at Year 12 graduation or end-of-year event. Recognition is based on demonstrated academic achievement throughout the school year.

overview: The Bayview Hold Fast Award is presented to a Year 12 student from the Bayview Campus in recognition of fantastic contributions to the school. The award celebrates well-rounded students who have made significant positive impacts on school life.
description: The Bayview Hold Fast Award is an annual award presented to a Year 12 student in recognition of fantastic contributions to Williamstown High School. The award references the school's motto 'Hold Fast' and celebrates students who have made outstanding contributions across various areas of school life. Recipients are recognised at graduation events and assemblies for their commitment to school values and community. The award demonstrates the school's appreciation for students who exemplify the 'Hold Fast' values of respect, responsibility and resilience in their actions and contributions to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students from Bayview Campus; Fantastic contributions to the school required; Alignment with school values and motto expected; Current enrolment at Williamstown High School required
special_conditions: Award presented at Year 12 graduation or end-of-year event. Recognition is based on demonstrated contributions to school life and alignment with the school's 'Hold Fast' values of respect, responsibility and resilience.

school_name: Wimmera Lutheran College - Nhill Lutheran School
overview: Wimmera Lutheran College offers Academic and General Excellence Scholarships to new and returning students demonstrating exceptional achievement. The scholarship recognises students with outstanding academic performance and excellence in co-curricular pursuits.
description: Academic and general excellence scholarships are available to new and returning students entering Year 7 up to Year 12. These scholarships are based on academic performance (ACER Scholarship Test for Academic Scholarship only and previous academic results), application to studies, leadership and service capabilities, and excellence in fields beyond academics. Scholarships awarded to successful students will provide a remission on tuition fees (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) ranging from 25% to 50%, plus a one-off bursary to support the purchase of additional uniform items. The scholarship is awarded annually and based on demonstrated merit across the selection criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 up to Year 12; New and returning students; Academic performance in top range nationally; Demonstrated leadership and service capabilities; Demonstrated excellence in fields beyond academics
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships include tuition fee remission ranging from 25% to 50% (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) plus a one-off bursary for uniform items. A $50 processing fee is charged upon submission of an enrolment application. Selection based on ACER Scholarship Test results combined with previous academic results, application to studies, leadership and service capabilities.

overview: Wimmera Lutheran College - Horsham Campus offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to excellence through rigorous assessment.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to new and returning students entering Year 7 up to Year 12. This scholarship is based on academic performance as demonstrated through both the ACER Scholarship Test and previous academic results from the student's current school. Successful scholarship recipients will receive a remission on tuition fees (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) ranging from 25% to 50%, plus a one-off bursary to support the purchase of additional uniform items. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic achievement and commitment to learning.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; New and returning students eligible; Academic performance in top tier based on ACER Scholarship Test results; Previous academic results required; Must be currently enrolled or gaining admission to the school
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships provide remission on tuition fees excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs. A one-off bursary is provided to support purchase of additional uniform items. Scholarship subject to continuation of satisfactory academic performance.

overview: Wimmera Lutheran College - Horsham Campus offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students demonstrating excellence in academic studies, leadership, service, and co-curricular achievements. The scholarship supports well-rounded students who contribute significantly to school life.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is available to new and returning students entering Year 7 up to Year 12. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate high application to studies, strong leadership and service capabilities, and excellence in fields beyond academics. The selection is based on academic performance through ACER Scholarship Test and previous academic results, combined with evidence of broader achievements and contributions. Successful scholarship recipients will receive a remission on tuition fees (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) ranging from 25% to 50%, plus a one-off bursary to support the purchase of additional uniform items.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; New and returning students eligible; Demonstrated application to studies; Evidence of leadership and service capabilities; Excellence in fields beyond academics such as sports, music, cultural or community involvement; Strong overall academic performance required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships provide remission on tuition fees excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs. A one-off bursary is provided to support purchase of additional uniform items. Selection based on interview, ACER results, school report, and evidence of co-curricular excellence. Scholarship subject to continued demonstration of excellence across academic and co-curricular areas.

school_name: Woady Yaloak Primary School-Ross Creek Campus

overview: Wimmera Lutheran College offers the Academic Scholarship to new and returning students entering Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and the potential to benefit from the school's outstanding academic and co-curricular programs.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 to Year 12 based on academic performance demonstrated through the ACER Scholarship Test and previous academic results. Scholarships awarded to successful students will provide a remission on tuition fees (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) ranging from 25% to 50%, plus a one-off bursary to support the purchase of additional uniform items. The scholarship is evaluated based on the candidate's academic excellence and capacity to benefit from the college's comprehensive academic program. Scholarships are available free of means test and renewable subject to satisfactory academic progress and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and returning students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Academic performance demonstrated through ACER Scholarship Test and previous academic results; Open to all applicants; No means test requirement
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are available free of means test. Scholarships awarded are based on results of ACER Scholarship Test and previous academic results. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct. Scholarships provide tuition fee remission only and do not apply to levies, excursions or other tuition costs.

overview: Wimmera Lutheran College offers General Excellence Scholarships to new and returning students entering Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence beyond academics, including application to studies, leadership and service capabilities, and excellence in fields beyond academics.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to new and returning students entering Year 7 to Year 12 based on multiple criteria including application to studies, leadership and service capabilities, and excellence in fields beyond academics such as sports, arts, cultural activities, and community service. Scholarships awarded to successful students will provide a remission on tuition fees (excluding levies, excursions and other tuition costs) ranging from 25% to 50%, plus a one-off bursary to support the purchase of additional uniform items. Selection is based on demonstrated achievements across multiple dimensions beyond academic performance alone.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and returning students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Demonstrated application to studies; Leadership and service capabilities; Excellence in fields beyond academics; No means test requirement
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are available free of means test. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in application to studies, leadership and service capabilities, and achievement in fields beyond academics. Scholarships provide tuition fee remission only and do not apply to levies, excursions or other tuition costs. Recipients must maintain satisfactory progress and conduct.

school_name: Woady Yaloak Primary School-Snake Valley Campus
Wodonga Middle Years College-Flying Fruit Fly Circus School
overview: The Flying Fruit Fly Circus School offers the Neil McDermott Scholarship Fund to support deserving students who face financial hardship. This scholarship recognises talented young performers and removes financial barriers to attending the school.
description: The Neil McDermott Scholarship Fund provides a 50% contribution towards the fees of a deserving student who may otherwise struggle to attend the Flying Fruit Fly Circus. The scholarship was created to honour the memory of Neil McDermott, a valued teacher and passionate supporter of the Circus. This fund is available to students enrolled or applying to the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School who demonstrate financial need and exceptional talent in circus arts. The scholarship can help cover tuition and training costs, allowing students to focus on their craft and academic studies.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled or applying to Flying Fruit Fly Circus School; Demonstrated financial hardship; Exceptional circus arts talent; Australian resident or international student
special_conditions: Application and eligibility determined by Flying Fruit Fly Circus. Contact (02) 6043 0777 for application details. Scholarship provides a 50% contribution towards fees only.

school_name: Wodonga Middle Years College-Flying Fruit Fly Circus School
overview: The Flying Fruit Fly Circus School offers the Noel Tovey Scholarship Fund to ensure there is no financial barrier to enrolment. This scholarship supports talented young artists and removes financial obstacles for students of all backgrounds.
description: The Noel Tovey Scholarship Fund helps to ensure there is no financial barrier to enrolment at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus. Being a young artist at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus is a full-time commitment, with students attending school for 30 hours per week and training between 9 and 20 hours per week. A gift to the Noel Tovey Scholarship Fund supports students to attend this unique creative learning environment and removes the financial barrier so that children of all backgrounds can join the circus and aspire to bright, creative futures. Over 30 students have so far received a Noel Tovey Scholarship, allowing them to learn and flourish with one of the world's leading youth arts companies.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled or applying to Flying Fruit Fly Circus School; Demonstrated financial need; Exceptional circus arts talent; From all socioeconomic backgrounds
special_conditions: Application determined by Flying Fruit Fly Circus based on financial need and artistic merit. Contact (02) 6043 0777 for details. Over 30 students have received this scholarship to date.

overview: Wodonga Middle Years College offers the Academic Advancement Program to support high-achieving students with strong academic results and high educational aspirations. The program is designed to provide students with accelerated learning in a supportive yet challenging environment with like-minded peers.
description: The Academic Advancement Program is an accelerated learning program offered to Year 7 students at Wodonga Middle Years College. Students selected for the program participate in enriched classes across core subjects including English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities. The program aims to develop independent learner qualities through self-driven tasks and build leadership skills through group work and leadership activities. AAP students are expected to maintain a GPA above 2.8 every six weeks and demonstrate excellent learner qualities reflecting school values of Respect, Resilience and Aspiration. Students have access to exclusive opportunities including guest speaker talks, university trips, debating team participation, and other leadership activities. The program caters to competent and committed students with limited places available each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students; Must have strong academic results; Must be highly motivated; Must submit an Expression of Interest (EOI); Must complete an aptitude test assessing Literacy, Numeracy and Critical thinking; Must participate in a 15-minute interview with a Curriculum Leader; GPA requirement of above 2.8 to remain in program
special_conditions: Limited number of places available. Students are expected to show excellent learner qualities reflecting school values. Teachers understand that gifted or talented students may excel in particular areas and will teach to point of need. No financial assistance provided; this is an accelerated learning pathway only.

overview: The Young Carer Bursary Program is a state-based support initiative that assists young carers to continue with their education. The program offers financial bursaries to eligible students to help with education needs and resources.
description: The Young Carer Bursary Program provides direct financial assistance to students who are identified as young carers. The program offers bursaries of $3,000 each year to assist with education needs and resources, with up to 1,000 bursaries available annually across Victoria. Young carers are students who provide unpaid care or support to family members due to illness, disability, or other circumstances. This program recognizes the additional challenges these students face and provides targeted financial support to help them continue their education and achieve their learning goals.
eligibility_criteria: Must be identified as a young carer; Providing unpaid care or support to a family member; Must be enrolled at Wodonga Middle Years College; Demonstrated financial need or education resource barriers due to caring responsibilities
special_conditions: Program subject to annual availability of funding. Young carers must be formally identified and assessed. Bursary is offered annually and students may reapply each year based on continued eligibility.

overview: Wodonga Senior Secondary College offers scholarships through NESPA (North East School of Performance & Art) to support students interested in pursuing performing arts and music education. The scholarships assist eligible students with the voluntary financial contributions required for the NESPA program.
description: NESPA scholarships are available to support students participating in the North East School of Performance & Art program, which offers intensive arts-based training in music, drama, visual arts and dance. Students enrolled in NESPA are required to complete a voluntary-noncompulsory financial payment for the program. The college makes scholarships available to assist eligible families with these contributions, enabling broader access to the arts program. The scholarship supports students' engagement with the four main disciplines of theatre, music, art and dance, and provides industry-standard training and mentoring. Duration is for the length of enrollment in the NESPA program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at either Wodonga Middle Years College or Wodonga Senior Secondary College; Must be participating in NESPA program; Must select a minimum of two arts disciplines to focus on
special_conditions: Scholarships are specifically designed to support the voluntary financial contributions required for NESPA participation. Recipients must maintain active engagement with the NESPA program and complete coursework in selected arts disciplines. The college states that scholarships are available to support this process, though specific terms and renewal conditions are not publicly detailed.

school_name: Wodonga Senior Secondary College-Benalla Flexible Learning Centre
Wodonga Senior Secondary College-Seymour Flexible Learning Centre

overview: The Young Carer Bursary Program supports young carers to continue with their education. Wodonga Middle Years College promotes awareness of this program to eligible students who provide unpaid care to family members.
description: The Young Carer Bursary Program is a national initiative that offers financial assistance to young carers aged 12-25 who balance their education with caring responsibilities. The program provides up to $3,000 per year to assist with education needs and resources including school uniforms, books, laptops, transport to school, and school fees. This is a competitive bursary program with 1,000 places available nationally each year. Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents living in Australia, provide unpaid care to a family member or friend with a disability, chronic illness, mental illness, or who is aged or frail, and must not be receiving another scholarship worth more than $500. Applications typically open in July each year with selection based on demonstrated financial need and caring responsibilities.
eligibility_criteria: Aged between 12 and 25 years; Providing unpaid care to a family member or friend with disability, chronic illness, mental illness, or who is frail and aged; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Living in Australia; Currently enrolled at secondary school; Not receiving another bursary or scholarship valued over $500
special_conditions: This is a national program managed by Carers Australia, not by the college itself. The college promotes awareness and supports eligible students with the application process. Recipients must maintain enrolment as a full-time student. Bursary funds should be used for education-related expenses.

overview: Wodonga Primary School administers the Camps, Sports and Excursion Fund (CSEF) to support eligible students. This annual State Government payment assists families with the cost of school camps, sports, and excursion expenses for the benefit of eligible students.
description: The CSEF is an annual State Government payment made to the school which is used towards camps, sports and/or excursion expenses for the benefit of the eligible student. Primary school funding is $154 per year. To be eligible for this fund, a parent or legal guardian of a student attending a registered Government School must be a holder of a current Veterans Affairs Gold Card or be an eligible Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC) or Pensioner Concession Card (PCC), or be a temporary foster parent. Application forms are available at the school office and can be submitted to the school by 4th July. As part of the Victorian Government's Affordable Uniform Program, every Foundation government school student who is a recipient of the CSEF automatically qualifies for a uniform pack voucher.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or legal guardian holds current Veterans Affairs Gold Card; OR holds eligible Centrelink Health Care Card (HCC); OR holds Pensioner Concession Card (PCC); OR is a temporary foster parent; Student must be attending Wodonga Primary School
special_conditions: Vouchers have an expiry date and must be used before the expiry date. Foundation government school students who are recipients of CSEF automatically qualify for a uniform pack voucher valued at $85 for winter uniform items.

overview: Wodonga Primary School offers support through the State Schools Relief Fund (SSR) to assist families experiencing financial hardship. SSR has received government funding to help families needing financial assistance and ensure no one is left behind.
description: The State Schools Relief Fund (SSR) provides financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. SSR will provide a winter uniform voucher valued at $85 to subsidise the cost of school tops, dresses, windcheater, and bomber jacket. The program assists families who are struggling financially to access essential school uniforms and materials. Families experiencing financial hardship are advised to contact the Business Manager to discuss support options. The school provides application forms available at the school office, and SSR has received government funding specifically to help more families needing financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Student enrolled at Wodonga Primary School; Application through school Business Manager
special_conditions: Vouchers have an expiry date and must be used before the expiry date. PE items are not included as part of this package. Shoes must be requested in UK sizes. Families who have already purchased uniforms can select items of a larger size from the retailer.

overview: Wodonga Primary School provides direct support for families experiencing financial hardship through the school Business Manager. Families can discuss support options and develop payment plans for school fees and costs.
description: Families experiencing financial hardship can contact the Business Manager at Wodonga Primary School to discuss support options. The school recognises that some families may struggle financially and offers flexible payment arrangements, including the option to devise payment plans to pay school fees throughout the year rather than upfront. This support ensures that financial difficulties do not prevent students from accessing the full range of school programs and activities. The school actively works with families to find solutions that meet their circumstances and support continued engagement in school life.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Student enrolled at Wodonga Primary School; Requires contact with Business Manager to discuss individual circumstances
special_conditions: Support is individual and case-specific. Families should contact the Business Manager to discuss their particular circumstances and available support options including flexible payment plans.

school_name: Wodonga Senior Secondary College-Wodonga Flexible Learning Centre
Wodonga Senior Secondary College-Shepparton Flexible Learning Centre
overview: NESPA (North East School of Performance & Art) offers scholarships to support students interested in pursuing performing and visual arts. The scholarship supports students' voluntary financial contributions for enrolment in the NESPA program, which requires students to engage in weekly instrumental or vocal music lessons and select multiple arts disciplines.
description: NESPA makes scholarships available to support students in completing the voluntary-noncompulsory financial payment required for program enrolment. Students are enrolled in a weekly instrument music lesson of their choice, or vocal lessons including singing and expression, and must select a minimum of two arts disciplines to focus on. The scholarship provides financial support to assist families with program costs. Students participate in performance ensembles, extra-curricular classes after school hours, industry workshops, and public performances throughout the year. The program integrates academic subjects with arts practice and includes opportunities for excursions and camps. Scholarships support access to this comprehensive performing arts pathway within the Wodonga Senior Secondary College structure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolling in NESPA program; Must demonstrate interest in performing and/or visual arts; Interested in instrumental music or vocal training; Willing to participate in weekly music lessons and select minimum two arts disciplines; Must be eligible to enrol at Wodonga Senior Secondary College
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to support completion of voluntary-noncompulsory financial payments. Students must engage with weekly instrumental or vocal music lessons as part of the program. Participation in extra-curricular classes and school performances is expected. Other subjects typically offered in middle and senior school experience are also available to NESPA students.

overview: The Science Academy at Woodmans Hill is a select entry program for high achieving students with a passion for and excellent ability in science. This program provides students access to unique learning experiences both within the school and through community and specialist links. Specialist science classes build and extend students in critical scientific areas of understanding and investigation.
description: The Science Academy at Woodmans Hill is a select entry program designed for high achieving students with a demonstrated passion for and excellent ability in science. Students gain access to unique learning experiences both within the school and through community and specialist links. The program supports students through Years 7 to 9, after which they will accelerate their learning into a VCE Science subject at Year 10. Specialist science classes build and extend students in the critical scientific areas of understanding and investigation. Students will spend time immersed within each of the scientific disciplines including biology, chemistry, physics and earth sciences, and undertake higher-level experiments. They will design and undertake detailed and well researched scientific investigations. The program aims to extend students' scientific knowledge and understanding in a range of scientific disciplines, provide a range of scientific learning opportunities both within and outside the classroom, and develop practical scientific investigation skills from experimental design to scientific report writing.
eligibility_criteria: Select entry program; High achieving students; Demonstrated passion for science; Excellent ability in science
special_conditions: Students will accelerate their learning into a VCE Science subject at Year 10. Entry is based on demonstrated excellence and passion for science.

overview: The Visual Arts Academy at Woodmans Hill is a select entry program for high achieving students with a passion for and excellent ability in the visual arts. This program provides students with access to unique learning experiences and opportunities to develop their artistic skills and understanding.
description: The Visual Arts Academy at Woodmans Hill is a select entry program designed for high achieving students with a demonstrated passion for and excellent ability in the visual arts. This selective program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional artistic ability and commitment to visual arts learning. The program provides students with access to unique learning experiences both within the school and through community and specialist links. Students in the Visual Arts Academy benefit from specialist teaching, access to advanced art facilities, and opportunities to participate in exhibitions and community art experiences. The program is designed to extend students' artistic knowledge, understanding, and practical skills across a range of visual arts disciplines and mediums.
eligibility_criteria: Select entry program; High achieving students; Demonstrated passion for visual arts; Excellent ability in visual arts
special_conditions: Entry is based on demonstrated excellence and passion for visual arts. Students must submit a portfolio or provide evidence of visual arts ability.

overview: Woodleigh School values intellectual curiosity, academic rigour, and a passion for learning. The Woodleigh Academic Excellence Scholarship recognises students who inspire others through their dedication and achievement – students whose commitment to scholarly growth stretches across a broad range of subjects and interest areas.
description: The Woodleigh Academic Excellence Scholarship is open to students entering Years 4 through 10 who consistently strive for excellence in their studies and are eager to contribute to the academic life of the school. Recipients typically engage deeply with their learning and seek out new educational challenges and opportunities. Successful applicants will receive part remission of tuition fees through to the end of Year 12, typically between 25-50%. The scholarship is subject to annual review by the Principal, and scholarship status may be withdrawn if the student's work or behaviour falls below standard.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Currently striving for excellence in academic studies; Eager to contribute to academic life of the school; Subject to annual review and withdrawal if performance is unsatisfactory
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Recipients are awarded with the intention that the successful candidate will continue through to the end of Year 12. Scholarships are subject to annual review by the Principal. If work or behaviour is below standard, the Principal reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship with reasonable notice.

overview: Woodleigh's approach to teaching and learning promotes holistic education – engagement across the full range of school programs. The Woodleigh Community and Culture Scholarship seeks to reward students who are broadly involved in school life – young leaders who thrive on opportunity and are wanting to throw themselves into everything that a Woodleigh education offers.
description: The Woodleigh Community and Culture Scholarship is open to students entering Years 4 through 10 who are broadly involved in school life and display leadership qualities. Recipients typically take on leadership roles on the student-led 'Woodleigh Committee' and support and lead Service Learning initiatives. Successful applicants will receive part remission of tuition fees through the end of Year 12, typically between 25-33%. The scholarship recognises students who are engaged across the full range of school programs and demonstrates commitment to community service.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Broadly involved in school life; Display leadership qualities; Engaged across full range of school programs; Commitment to community and service
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Recipients are awarded with the intention that the successful candidate will continue through to the end of Year 12. Scholarships are subject to annual review by the Principal. Recipients expected to continue leadership roles and Service Learning involvement.

overview: Respect for the Environment is one of the core tenets of Woodleigh School. The Henderson Sustainability and Regenerative Scholarship is Victoria's first scholarship awarded on Sustainable and Regenerative grounds and is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10. Named after Brian Henderson, who led the Senior Campus and pioneered the school's landscaping and land regeneration efforts, it recognises the positive, solutions-focused impact and advocacy young people can bring to the world they will inherit.
description: The Henderson Sustainability and Regenerative Scholarship recognises committed individuals eager to contribute meaningfully to Woodleigh's Environmental and Regenerative programs. The scholarship is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10 and is Victoria's first scholarship awarded specifically on Sustainable and Regenerative grounds. Successful applicants will receive part remission of tuition fees through the end of Year 12, typically between 25-33%. The scholarship honours students who demonstrate commitment to environmental action, sustainability, and regenerative practices.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Committed to contributing to Environmental and Regenerative programs; Demonstrate solutions-focused impact and environmental advocacy; Eager to engage with sustainability initiatives
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Recipients are awarded with the intention that the successful candidate will continue through to the end of Year 12. Scholarships are subject to annual review by the Principal. Named after Brian Henderson (Senior Campus leader 1975-2007) who pioneered school's landscaping and land regeneration.

overview: Woodleigh School has long celebrated the transformative power of the Performing Arts. The Jago Performing Arts Scholarship is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10 and recognises students with a passion for performance and production and a desire to contribute meaningfully to the cultural life of the school.
description: The Jago Performing Arts Scholarship is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10 who have a passion for performance and production, including music, drama, dance, and theatre production. Successful applicants will receive part remission of tuition fees through the end of Year 12, typically between 25-33%. An allowance for music lessons will also be included for Music scholars. The scholarship recognises committed individuals eager to grow as artists and performers within Woodleigh's vibrant Performing Arts community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Passion for performance and production; Demonstrated involvement in music, drama, dance, or theatre production; Desire to contribute meaningfully to cultural life of school
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Recipients are awarded with the intention that the successful candidate will continue through to the end of Year 12. Scholarships are subject to annual review by the Principal. For Music Scholars, the scholarship includes an allowance for music lessons. If a student withdraws from studying their specified instrument, the Principal reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship with reasonable notice.

overview: Woodleigh School recognises the importance of The Arts to its culture and school community. The Woodleigh Visual Arts Scholarship is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10 and offers awardees the chance to grow and excel through their immersion in the school's broad Visual Arts program.
description: The Woodleigh Visual Arts Scholarship is offered to students entering Years 4 through 10 and recognises outstanding achievement and passion in visual arts disciplines. Woodleigh's history of Visual Arts excellence stretches back fifty years to the foundation of Senior Campus. Successful applicants will receive part remission of tuition fees through the end of Year 12, typically between 25-33%. The scholarship provides opportunities for students to grow and excel through immersion in the school's comprehensive Visual Arts program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10; Demonstrated excellence or passion in visual arts; Samples of artwork required; Commitment to contributing to school's visual arts culture
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Recipients are awarded with the intention that the successful candidate will continue through to the end of Year 12. Scholarships are subject to annual review by the Principal. Applicants should submit portfolio of visual arts work demonstrating their achievement and potential.

overview: Woodleigh School is committed to providing an environment that is free from discrimination and to offering opportunities for members of the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander community. The First Nations Scholarship is offered for entry into Year 7 2027 and is available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who do not currently and would otherwise be unable to attend Woodleigh School.
description: The First Nations Scholarship is available for entry into Year 7 in 2027 for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who would otherwise be unable to attend Woodleigh School. This scholarship represents Woodleigh's deep commitment to reconciliation and providing life-changing educational opportunities to First Nations students. Unlike other Woodleigh scholarships, the First Nations Scholarship offers 100% remission of all tuition and consolidated charges. The school welcomes applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who feel that a Woodleigh education might be transformative for both them and their families.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander student; Entering Year 7 in 2027; Do not currently attend Woodleigh School; Would otherwise be unable to attend Woodleigh School due to financial circumstances; Seeking life-changing educational opportunity
special_conditions: Exclusively for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Offers 100% remission of all tuition and consolidated charges. Designed for students who would otherwise be unable to attend. Recipients are awarded with the intention of continuing through to the end of Year 12. Part of Woodleigh's commitment to reconciliation and two-way learning with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander community.

overview: Wyndham Vale Primary School supports eligible families through the Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF), a Victorian government program. This scheme provides direct financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship to ensure all students can participate in school camps, sports, and excursion programs.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian state government assistance program administered through the school. Parents who hold a valid Centrelink Health Care Card or Pension Card are eligible for a $125 annual payment from the State Government specifically to use towards camps, sports activities, and school excursions. This is non-means-tested and automatically available to eligible cardholders. There is no application process requiring academic achievement or selection criteria—eligibility is determined solely by Centrelink card status. Families are required to notify the school office if they hold an eligible card to claim this entitlement. The funding is designed to remove financial barriers preventing student participation in co-curricular activities and camps.
eligibility_criteria: Hold a valid Centrelink Health Care Card or Pension Card; Parent or guardian applies to the school; Student must be enrolled at Wyndham Vale Primary School
special_conditions: Funding is managed by the State Government and distributed directly through the school. Families with a sibling who started at the school in a given year must complete a separate form. Families transferring from another school must also complete a new application form with Wyndham Vale Primary School office.

overview: Wyndham Vale Primary School recognises that some families may experience financial difficulty and provides support through flexible payment arrangements and alternative options. The school works confidentially with families to ensure no child misses out on essential school activities and experiences.
description: The school understands that some families may experience financial difficulty and has established support arrangements to assist. Wyndham Vale Primary School offers flexible payment plans and alternative arrangements for families unable to pay for essential items and excursions on the standard timeline. Parents facing financial challenges are encouraged to contact the school administration office on (03) 8754 0888 or mark 'CONFIDENTIAL' in communications sent to wyndham.vale.ps@education.vic.gov.au to discuss alternative payment options and hardship support. The school operates a confidential system for families to discuss financial concerns, ensuring student access to educational programs and experiences is not compromised. All school contributions are voluntary, and families cannot be required to pay for fundamental educational items or curriculum delivery.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial difficulty or inability to pay for school items or excursions; Currently enrolled at Wyndham Vale Primary School; Parent or guardian contact required
special_conditions: All communications regarding financial hardship should be marked 'CONFIDENTIAL' in the subject line when emailing. Families are encouraged to have confidential discussions with the school office about payment plans and alternative arrangements. The school applies the Victorian Government's Parent Payment Policy and Financial Help for Families Policy. Refunds for school payments are assessed on a case-by-case basis and provided where the school deems it reasonable and fair.

overview: Wycheproof P-12 College awards musical scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in instrumental music performance. The scholarships are provided to encourage students who demonstrate exceptional musical talent and commitment to the college's instrumental program.
description: Wycheproof P-12 College has awarded musical scholarships to several students over the past few years as a source of encouragement and recognition of musical excellence. The college maintains a strong instrumental music program with both junior and senior concert bands. Students in Years 4 and above are placed into musical groups based on their instrument type and ability level. The specific financial value, duration, selection criteria, and application process for these scholarships are not detailed on the school website and prospective applicants should contact the school directly for complete information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional instrumental music ability; Commitment to the college's musical program required; Specific year level not specified
special_conditions: Specific conditions and renewal requirements are not stated on the school website. Interested students should contact the school directly for detailed information about scholarship terms, duration, and any ongoing obligations.

overview: Xavier College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic ability among incoming students. The scholarship supports gifted students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in their academic studies and commitment to learning excellence.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship covers a percentage of the academic tuition fee. Students currently attending Xavier College are eligible to apply, as well as external applicants entering Years 7, 9, and 11. A demonstrated high level of excellence and achievement in a field of endeavour related to school life, such as performing arts, creative arts or sport may also be seen as an advantage. The scholarship selection process involves a competitive academic examination conducted in association with ACER (Australian Council for Educational Research). Successful applicants must demonstrate a willingness to participate fully in the life of the College. The performance and conduct of all scholarship recipients are reviewed annually or as required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Demonstrated outstanding academic ability; Open to both external and internal students; Must demonstrate willingness to participate fully in College life; Competitive ACER examination required
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all scholarship recipients are reviewed annually or as required. Recipients must make a worthy contribution to the life of the College, and their effort in academic studies, as well as their overall conduct, participation and character, is of a high standard at all times. For students who have received a Scholarship, it is expected that the recipient is entirely involved in their nominated field of award, that their contribution is significant and that their performance is of a commensurate standard.

overview: Xavier College awards the Music Excellence Scholarship to students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and achievement in their principal instrument. The scholarship recognises gifted musicians who are committed to contributing to the College's diverse and vibrant music program.
description: The Music Excellence Scholarship covers a percentage of the academic tuition and/or boarding fee and/or the cost of the main instrumental tuition fee taught at the College. The instrumental music dispensation allows for 30 lessons per year. These scholarships are available to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11. All applicants will sit for a competitive academic examination, although an audition will be the major criterion for selection. Successful applicants will have demonstrated excellence in music in their main instrument and must provide documented evidence of achievements. As a guideline, applicants for Year 7 should have completed a minimum standard of AMEB Grade 5 or equivalent; for Year 9, Grade 7 or equivalent; and for Year 11, Grade 8 or equivalent. Sight-reading will be heard during the audition, and some musical excerpts may be sent to candidates to learn.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11; Demonstrated excellence in music with minimum AMEB grading (Grade 5 for Year 7, Grade 7 for Year 9, Grade 8 for Year 11); Must provide documented evidence of musical achievements; Open to current Xavier College students and external applicants; Must participate actively in wider co-curricular music program
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all scholarship recipients are reviewed annually or as required. Recipients must make a worthy contribution to the life of the College and demonstrate a willingness to participate fully, particularly in the music program. The successful applicants must be active, ongoing participants in the wider co-curricular music program during each of their years as students at Xavier College. The Head of Music will play an active role in guiding and instructing students' involvement in the music program as well as their aims and ambitions for their principal instrument.

overview: Xavier College provides the General Excellence Award to recognise students who display outstanding achievement across various endeavours in school life. The award is offered to Catholic students who demonstrate excellence in academic, service-oriented, or co-curricular pursuits including performing arts, creative arts, and sport.
description: These awards offer part but not total financial support to students based on application to the College. The College provides financial assistance for a number of enrolment opportunities each year for Catholic students who display excellence across various endeavours applicable to College life, with the underpinning requirement of both faith and personal character. Such endeavour might be academic, service-oriented, or be associated with the College's diverse cocurricular program that includes performing and creative arts and sport. The level of fee support ranges from 40% to 70%. Some financial information may be required to determine the level of tuition fee support, as will supporting documentation to validate the level of excellence in the applicant's field/s of endeavour. The performance and conduct of all Excellence Award recipients are reviewed annually or as required.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Catholic students; Must display excellence across various endeavours in College life; Must demonstrate faith and personal character; Open to students entering multiple year levels; Financial information may be required (means tested)
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all Excellence Award recipients are reviewed annually or as required. It is expected that every recipient makes a significant contribution to the life of the College in their nominated field/s of award, and that their effort in academic studies, as well as their overall conduct, participation and character, are of a high standard at all times. Recipients must display excellence that justifies the award based on application to the College.

overview: Xavier College provides the Kostka Exhibition Award using proceeds from the sale of the former Kostka Hall Campus. The award supports students who wish to access a Xavier education but face financial or personal challenges that make access difficult, while not qualifying for a full bursary.
description: Funding set aside from the proceeds from the former Kostka Hall Campus of Xavier College enables the College to provide part-tuition fee support to families who may wish to attend Xavier but whose personal or financial circumstance would not enable them to access a Xavier education. The Exhibitions are offered to students who would not be eligible for a bursary. Accordingly, financial information is required to support the integrity of the application. This is handled with strict confidentiality and discretion to respect the particular circumstances and privacy of the applicant. The level of tuition fee support ranges from 20% to 70%. The Exhibitions are designed to provide access to metropolitan communities of significance to Xavier. Noting the relevance of Kostka Hall to these opportunities, the historical catchment area of Kostka Hall will be an area of priority.
eligibility_criteria: Must display strong commitment to faith and service; Must demonstrate academic growth and human (personal) excellence; Must demonstrate willingness to contribute to the full life of the College; Not eligible for a full bursary; Financial information required; Preference given to historical Kostka Hall catchment area
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all Kostka Exhibition Award recipients are reviewed annually or as required. Financial information is required to support the integrity of the application and is handled with strict confidentiality and discretion. Applicants must display a strong commitment to faith and service, academic growth, human (personal) excellence and demonstrate a willingness to contribute to the full life of the College.

overview: Xavier College provides Bursaries to offer financial assistance to families who would otherwise not be in a position to afford enrolment of their child at the College. Bursaries are financially needs-based and are not awarded on academic, musical or sporting prowess.
description: The College Foundation, in conjunction with generous donors, provides funds for a number of named and general bursaries. A bursary provides financial assistance to a family who would otherwise not be in a position to afford the enrolment of their child at the College. Accordingly, financial information is required to support the integrity of the application. This is handled with strict confidentiality and discretion to respect the particular circumstances and privacy of the applicant. In most cases, these awards provide part but not total financial support. The level of fee support ranges from 25% to 100%. Families external to the College are welcome to apply for a bursary at any time, although typically they are awarded in one period at the beginning of a school year for a commencement the following academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Families facing financial hardship and unable to afford Xavier fees; Financial information required and confidentially assessed; Open to students entering any year level; Open to new and current students; Not based on academic or sporting merit
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all bursary recipients are reviewed annually or as required. Financial information is required to support the integrity of the application and is handled with strict confidentiality and discretion. Recipients must make a worthy contribution to the life of the College, and their effort in academic studies, as well as their overall conduct, participation and character, is of a high standard at all times. Families external to the College are typically awarded in one period at the beginning of a school year for commencement the following academic year.

overview: Xavier College provides Cura Personalis Support to families experiencing unforeseen circumstances, tragedies or hardships during their journey with the College. This support embodies the Jesuit principle of caring for the whole person and supporting families during difficult times.
description: Unforeseen occurrences, circumstances and tragedies can befall families during the course of their journey within the Xavier Family. The College will consider these on their merits and will do all that it can to support its families during these difficult times. This support might include financial assistance in the payment of school fees (such as reduction or deferment). The College recognises that not all circumstances can be anticipated and provides discretionary support to families facing unexpected hardship. This support is considered individually and may include temporary fee reduction, deferment of payment, or other tailored financial assistance. The College's commitment to Cura Personalis—care for the whole person—ensures that no family is abandoned during times of crisis.
eligibility_criteria: Facing unforeseen circumstances, personal tragedy or financial hardship; Student and family currently enrolled at Xavier College; Application considered on individual merits; No specific eligibility threshold
special_conditions: The performance and conduct of all Cura Personalis Support recipients are reviewed annually or as required. Each case is considered individually on its merits. Support may include fee reduction, deferment of payment, or other forms of financial assistance. Recipients must maintain a worthy contribution to the life of the College with high standards of conduct and character. This is discretionary support intended for families facing genuine hardship.

overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers the Academic Scholarship to Year 7 students who have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School. The scholarship rewards exceptional academic qualities and recognises students with strong academic achievement.
description: These scholarships are awarded to students with exceptional academic qualities. Applicants will be required to undertake an academic test (EduTest), as well as an interview, at a time arranged by the school. Registration for testing must be completed online prior to the set closing date. Information regarding the selection process will be sent out once a full application has been received. The specific financial benefit and duration of the scholarship are not detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College
