overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers the Arts Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in Performing Arts or Visual Arts. The scholarship recognises exceptional achievement in arts disciplines both within and outside of school.
description: These scholarships are awarded to students who are able to display outstanding qualities in the area of Performing Arts or Visual Arts – either at school, outside of school or both. A portfolio of work will be required to be presented at an interview, at a time arranged by the school. The portfolio should showcase the student's artistic abilities and achievements. The specific financial benefit and duration of the scholarship are not detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in Performing Arts or Visual Arts; Portfolio of work required; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College

overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers the Sporting Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in sport. The scholarship recognises exceptional sporting achievement and excellence in athletics both within and outside of school.
description: These scholarships are awarded to students who are able to display outstanding qualities in the area of sport – either at school, outside of school or both. A presentation of achievements will be required to be presented at an interview, at a time arranged by the school. The presentation should demonstrate the student's sporting accomplishments and potential. The specific financial benefit and duration of the scholarship are not detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in sport; Presentation of achievements required; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College

overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers the Leadership Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in Leadership. The scholarship recognises exceptional leadership potential and experience in community and school leadership contexts.
description: These scholarships are awarded to students who are able to display outstanding qualities in the area of Leadership – either at school, outside of school or both. Selection involves an interview at a time arranged by the school where leadership achievements and potential are assessed. The interview process will evaluate the applicant's demonstrated leadership qualities in various contexts. The specific financial benefit and duration of the scholarship are not detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in Leadership; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College

school_name: Yarra Hills Secondary College-Mount Evelyn Campus
overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers Year 7 Academic Scholarships to students who have displayed outstanding attributes whilst at Primary School and possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College. These scholarships are awarded to students with exceptional academic qualities and recognition of their intellectual achievement.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is designed to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic abilities. Applicants are required to undertake an academic test administered by EduTest, as well as attend an interview at a time arranged by the school. The selection process assesses outstanding academic qualities through standardised testing and individual interviews. Registration for the academic testing must be completed online prior to the set closing date, with information regarding the complete process sent once a full application has been received. The scholarship recognises students who have demonstrated exceptional academic achievement at primary school level and possess the personal qualities to contribute meaningfully to the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent primary education; Must have demonstrated exceptional academic qualities; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College
special_conditions: Applicants will be required to undertake an academic test (EduTest) as well as an interview at a time arranged by the school. Registration for testing must be completed online prior to the set closing date.

school_name: Yarra Hills Secondary College-Mount Evelyn Campus
overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers Year 7 Arts Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in Performing Arts or Visual Arts. These scholarships recognise excellence in the arts demonstrated either at school, outside of school or both, and are designed for students who possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College.
description: The Year 7 Arts Scholarship recognises students who display outstanding qualities in the area of Performing Arts (including music, drama, and dance) or Visual Arts (including painting, sculpture, design, and other visual mediums). Applicants must present a portfolio of their artistic work at an interview scheduled at a time arranged by the school. The portfolio should showcase the student's best work demonstrating their artistic ability, whether developed through school programs, private tuition, or independent practice. The selection process evaluates both the quality of the portfolio and the student's potential to contribute to the college's vibrant arts programs. This scholarship supports students who have demonstrated excellence in the arts and possess the personal qualities to thrive in the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent primary education; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in Performing Arts or Visual Arts; Can demonstrate skills developed at school, outside school or both; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College
special_conditions: A portfolio of work will be required to be presented at an interview at a time arranged by the school. The portfolio should demonstrate the student's artistic work in Performing Arts or Visual Arts.

school_name: Yarra Hills Secondary College-Mount Evelyn Campus
overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers Year 7 Sporting Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in sport. These scholarships recognise sporting excellence demonstrated either at school, outside of school or both, and are designed for students who possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College.
description: The Year 7 Sporting Scholarship recognises students who display outstanding qualities in the area of sport at any level. Applicants must present a detailed account of their sporting achievements and participation history at an interview scheduled at a time arranged by the school. The presentation of achievements should include documentation of competitive results, team participation, levels of competition achieved, and evidence of sporting commitment and development. The selection process evaluates both the student's sporting accomplishments and their potential to contribute to the college's extensive sporting programs. This scholarship supports students who have demonstrated excellence in sport and possess the personal qualities to thrive in the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent primary education; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in sport; Can demonstrate skills developed at school, outside school or both; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College
special_conditions: A presentation of achievements will be required to be presented at an interview at a time arranged by the school. The presentation should document sporting achievements and participation history.

school_name: Yarra Hills Secondary College-Mount Evelyn Campus
overview: Yarra Hills Secondary College offers Year 7 Leadership Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding qualities in leadership. These scholarships recognise students who display exceptional leadership capabilities demonstrated either at school, outside of school or both, and are designed for students who possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College.
description: The Year 7 Leadership Scholarship recognises students who display outstanding qualities in the area of leadership. Applicants must attend an interview at a time arranged by the school where they can demonstrate their leadership qualities and potential. Leadership at this level may be demonstrated through various forms including student leadership roles, community service, mentoring of peers, organisational skills, or initiative in group settings. The selection process evaluates the student's demonstrated leadership abilities, character, and potential to contribute positively to the college community. This scholarship supports students who have shown evidence of leadership capacity and possess the personal qualities and values that will enhance the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent primary education; Must demonstrate outstanding qualities in Leadership; Can demonstrate leadership at school, outside school or both; Must possess personal qualities and values that will contribute to the life of the College
special_conditions: An interview at a time arranged by the school is required to assess leadership qualities and potential. Students should be prepared to discuss their leadership experiences and demonstrate their commitment to contributing positively to the college community.

Recipients are expected to maintain high academic standards and are reviewed annually.

special_conditions: Applications must be complete and all requested documents submitted for review. This support reflects the College's commitment to the Jesuit principle of caring for the whole person and provides financial assistance when families experience distress.

The College considers these on their merits and does all it can to support families during difficult times. This support might include financial assistance in the payment of school fees such as reduction or deferment. The College recognises that sometimes families need support beyond standard financial assistance programs and addresses these situations with compassion. Support is determined on a case-by-case basis and takes into account the particular circumstances of each family.
eligibility_criteria: Experience unforeseen occurrences, circumstances or tragedies; Families within Xavier community; Assessment on individual merits; Support may include fee reduction or deferment; Case-by-case consideration; Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship from specified circumstances
special_conditions: Unforeseen occurrences, circumstances and tragedies considered on merits. College will do all it can to support families during difficult times. Support might include financial assistance in payment of school fees (such as reduction or deferment). Assessed on individual basis. Not a standard program but discretionary support. Performance and conduct of all College Enrolment Support students reviewed annually or as required.

overview: Yarra Valley Grammar offers the Carter Family Music Bursary to support students undertaking music studies. This bursary assists students in Years 9-12 who would otherwise be unable to afford to participate in the school's music program and represents the school's commitment to ensuring music is accessible to all talented students.
description: The Carter Family Music Bursary is available annually thanks to the generosity of the Carter family. Two bursaries are made available each year to support students in Years 9-12 who otherwise could not afford to participate in the YVG Music School. The bursary provides financial assistance specifically for music tuition and study. The intention of this bursary is to help students access professional music education and development. If no applications have been received in October of any given year, Awards of Merit will be given from the Fund to the top student(s) meeting the criteria. Students should contact the Music Department for further information and application details.
eligibility_criteria: Years 9-12 enrollment; Financial need for music participation; Demonstrated commitment to music study; Current enrolment at Yarra Valley Grammar
special_conditions: If no applications have been received in October of any given year, Awards of Merit will be given from the Fund to the top student(s) meeting the criteria.

program_name: James Simpson Performing Arts Scholarship Fund
overview: Yarra Valley Grammar offers the James Simpson Performing Arts Scholarship Fund to recognise and support VCE students with exceptional aptitude in performing arts. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate strong commitment to drama, dance, music, and related theatrical disciplines.
description: Each year, the James Simpson Scholarship Fund supports one or more VCE students with a strong aptitude and ongoing commitment to the performing arts. This prize is designed to enrich students' learning in areas such as drama, dance, voice, music, lighting, and sound. The prize may be awarded in the form of professional tuition, masterclasses, theatrical experiences, or other resources tailored to the recipient's development. The scholarship recognises excellence across the breadth of performing arts disciplines and provides tangible support for students to further develop their artistic skills and experiences.
eligibility_criteria: VCE (Year 11 and Year 12) enrollment; Demonstrated aptitude in performing arts; Ongoing commitment to performing arts; Current enrolment at Yarra Valley Grammar
special_conditions: Prize awarded in the form of professional tuition, masterclasses, theatrical experiences, or other resources tailored to the recipient's development. Award determined annually based on student applications and demonstrated commitment to performing arts.

overview: Yarra Valley Grammar's Hearing Unit Scholarship Fund supports students to access the school's world-class integrated hearing facility. This fund assists students who require specialist hearing support and would benefit from the school's internationally acclaimed hearing services.
description: The Hearing Unit Scholarship Fund is administered through the Yarra Foundation and is designed to support students attending Yarra Valley Grammar's world-class integrated hearing facility. The fund provides financial assistance to enable students to access specialist hearing services and support. The Yarra Foundation actively fundraises to support this scholarship fund, which assists students who otherwise might not be able to access these specialised services. Donations made to the YVG Hearing Unit Scholarship Fund are tax deductible. The fund reflects the school's commitment to supporting students with hearing needs and ensuring they can fully participate in all aspects of school life.
eligibility_criteria: Current enrollment at Yarra Valley Grammar; Diagnosed hearing needs or attending Hearing Unit; Financial need for hearing services; Assessed suitability for Hearing Unit programs
special_conditions: Fund operated through Yarra Foundation. Donations to the Hearing Unit Scholarship Fund are tax deductible. Scholarships awarded at discretion of the school based on demonstrated need and Hearing Unit assessment.

overview: Yarrunga Primary School offers the Wangaratta Chronicle Scholarship to recognise Grade 5 and Grade 6 students who demonstrate passions and well-rounded achievements. The scholarship supports students in pursuing their interests and preparing for transition to secondary school.
description: The Wangaratta Chronicle Scholarship recognises students who excel across multiple areas including sports, creative pursuits, and personal development. Students are invited to apply by discussing their passions and how the scholarship will benefit their education. The scholarship has been awarded annually for nine years, with the 2025 recipients using their award to assist with costs associated with starting secondary school. The selection process involves written applications where students articulate their achievements and educational goals. Recipients are selected based on their demonstrated resilience, well-rounded character, and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 5 students; Grade 6 students; Must demonstrate passions across multiple areas; Must submit written application
special_conditions: Scholarships have been awarded annually for nine years. The Wangaratta Chronicle provides ongoing support and contribution toward this award.

program_name: Wangaratta Rotary Club Jean McDonald Perpetual Scholarship for All Round Student
overview: Yarrunga Primary School offers the Wangaratta Rotary Club Jean McDonald Perpetual Scholarship to recognise Grade 6 students who demonstrate all-round excellence. The scholarship rewards students who excel across academic, co-curricular, and personal development areas.
description: The Jean McDonald Perpetual Scholarship from Wangaratta Rotary Club is presented to Grade 6 students who have demonstrated well-rounded achievement and excellence across school life. The award recognises students who balance strong academic performance with active participation in school activities and community engagement. Recipients are selected through an assessment process that considers overall contribution to the school community. This perpetual award, named after Jean McDonald, continues the Rotary Club's commitment to supporting young people.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students; Demonstrated all-round excellence; Must show strong school involvement and academic achievement
special_conditions: Perpetual scholarship awarded annually. Award honours the legacy of Jean McDonald.

program_name: Brian Bosse Scholarship for Personal Growth
overview: Yarrunga Primary School presents the Brian Bosse Scholarship for Personal Growth to Grade 6 students who demonstrate significant personal development and growth. The scholarship recognises students who have shown resilience and positive change throughout their schooling.
description: The Brian Bosse Scholarship for Personal Growth is awarded to Grade 6 students who have demonstrated notable personal growth, resilience, and positive development during their time at school. The scholarship acknowledges students who have overcome challenges and shown commitment to their personal development journey. Recipients are selected based on their character development and commitment to positive change. This award celebrates the importance of personal growth alongside academic achievement, reflecting Brian Bosse's values.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 6 students; Demonstrated personal growth and development; Evidence of resilience and positive character change
special_conditions: Award recognises personal growth and development. Named in honour of Brian Bosse.

overview: Yarrunga Primary School presents School Council Awards for Excellence to students in Grades 5 and 6 who demonstrate exceptional leadership, outstanding academic performance, and significant school involvement. These awards recognise students who contribute positively to the school community.
description: The School Council Awards for Excellence recognise Grade 5 and Grade 6 students who have distinguished themselves through excellent leadership, strong academic achievement, and active school involvement. Recipients are selected by School Council for their demonstrated commitment to the school community and their positive influence on peers. The awards acknowledge students who balance academic excellence with meaningful participation in school life and leadership opportunities. Selection is based on overall contribution to school culture and demonstrated leadership qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 5 students; Grade 6 students; Demonstrated excellent leadership; Strong academic achievement; Significant school involvement
special_conditions: Awards presented by School Council. Recipients recognised for excellence across academic and school involvement dimensions.

program_name: Kids Council Award for Volunteering Excellence
overview: Yarrunga Primary School presents the Kids Council Award for Volunteering Excellence to Grade 4 students who demonstrate outstanding volunteer qualities and community service. The award recognises students who show exceptional commitment to helping others and supporting the school community.
description: The Kids Council Award for Volunteering Excellence is presented to Grade 4 students who demonstrate exceptional qualities as volunteers and show genuine commitment to community service. This award recognises students who contribute meaningfully to the school and wider community through volunteer work and helping others. Recipients are selected for their demonstrated reliability, enthusiasm, and positive impact as volunteers. The award celebrates the importance of community service and volunteer contribution at primary school level.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 4 students; Demonstrated volunteer qualities; Evidence of community service contribution; Positive community impact
special_conditions: Awarded by Kids Council. Recognises volunteer contribution and community service qualities.

overview: Yarrunga Primary School presents the Zoe Buttigieg Sports Award to Grade 5 students who excel in sports. The award recognises students who demonstrate exceptional athletic ability, commitment, and passion for physical activity.
description: The Zoe Buttigieg Sports Award is presented annually to a Grade 5 student who demonstrates excellence in sports and athletic pursuits. The award recognises students who show strong commitment to physical activity, whether in competitive sports or recreational endeavours. Recipients are selected based on their athletic achievement, dedication to improvement, and positive sporting attitude. This award honours Zoe Buttigieg's legacy and celebrates sporting achievement among primary school students.
eligibility_criteria: Grade 5 students; Demonstrated excellence in sports; Strong athletic ability and commitment
special_conditions: Award named in honour of Zoe Buttigieg. Recognises sporting excellence among Grade 5 students.
